license: Open Canon readings — open-witness-derived sources only (see _meta.sources per witness); public-domain / CC-BY witnesses (WLC/OpenScriptures, SBLGNT/morphgnt) underlie every kept reading. Merged from the builders' latest full sweep (readable-fresh.jsonl) and the small hand-gated/John-1 set (readable-bounded.jsonl), with gated readings taking precedence over ungated ones verse-for-verse.

1ch.1.text: Adam, Seth, Enosh;
Kenan, Mahalalel, Jared;
Enoch, Methuselah, Lamech;
Noah, Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
The sons of Japheth: Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshech, and Tiras.
The sons of Gomer: Ashkenaz, Diphath, and Togarmah.
The sons of Javan: Elishah, Tarshish, Kittim, and Rodanim.
The sons of Ham: Cush, Egypt, Put, and Canaan.
The sons of Cush: Seba, Havilah, Sabta, Raama, and Sabteca. The sons of Raamah: Sheba and Dedan.
And Cush fathered Nimrod; he was the first to become a mighty one on the earth.
Egypt fathered the Ludim, the Anamim, the Lehabim, the Naphtuhim,
the Pathrusim, the Casluhim — from whom the Philistines came — and the Caphtorim.
Canaan fathered Sidon his firstborn, and Heth,
and the Jebusite, the Amorite, the Girgashite,
the Hivite, the Arkite, the Sinite,
the Arvadite, the Zemarite, and the Hamathite.
The sons of Shem: Elam, Asshur, Arpachshad, Lud, and Aram; and Uz, Hul, Gether, and Meshech.
Arpachshad fathered Shelah, and Shelah fathered Eber.
To Eber were born two sons: the name of the one was Peleg, for in his days the earth was divided; and his brother's name was Joktan.
Joktan fathered Almodad, Sheleph, Hazarmaveth, Jerah,
Hadoram, Uzal, Diklah,
Ebal, Abimael, Sheba,
Ophir, Havilah, and Jobab. All these were the sons of Joktan.
Shem, Arpachshad, Shelah;
Eber, Peleg, Reu;
Serug, Nahor, Terah;
Abram — that is, Abraham.
The sons of Abraham: Isaac and Ishmael.
These are their generations: the firstborn of Ishmael, Nebaioth; then Kedar, Adbeel, Mibsam,
Mishma, Dumah, Massa, Hadad, Tema,
Jetur, Naphish, and Kedemah. These were the sons of Ishmael.
The sons of Keturah, Abraham's concubine: she bore Zimran, Jokshan, Medan, Midian, Ishbak, and Shuah. The sons of Jokshan: Sheba and Dedan.
The sons of Midian: Ephah, Epher, Hanoch, Abida, and Eldaah. All these were the sons of Keturah.
And Abraham fathered Isaac. The sons of Isaac: Esau and Israel.
The sons of Esau: Eliphaz, Reuel, Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.
The sons of Eliphaz: Teman, Omar, Zephi, Gatam, Kenaz; and by Timna, Amalek.
The sons of Reuel: Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, and Mizzah.
The sons of Seir: Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah, Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan.
The sons of Lotan: Hori and Homam; and Lotan's sister was Timna.
The sons of Shobal: Alian, Manahath, Ebal, Shephi, and Onam. The sons of Zibeon: Aiah and Anah.
The son of Anah: Dishon. The sons of Dishon: Hamran, Eshban, Ithran, and Cheran.
The sons of Ezer: Bilhan, Zaavan, and Jaakan. The sons of Dishan: Uz and Aran.
These are the kings who reigned in the land of Edom before any king reigned over the sons of Israel: Bela son of Beor, and the name of his city was Dinhabah.
Bela died, and Jobab son of Zerah of Bozrah reigned in his place.
Jobab died, and Husham of the land of the Temanites reigned in his place.
Husham died, and Hadad son of Bedad, who struck down Midian in the field of Moab, reigned in his place; and the name of his city was Avith.
Hadad died, and Samlah of Masrekah reigned in his place.
Samlah died, and Shaul of Rehoboth on the River reigned in his place.
Shaul died, and Baal-hanan son of Achbor reigned in his place.
Baal-hanan died, and Hadad reigned in his place; the name of his city was Pai, and his wife's name was Mehetabel daughter of Matred, daughter of Mezahab.
And Hadad died. The chiefs of Edom were: chief Timna, chief Aliah, chief Jetheth,
chief Oholibamah, chief Elah, chief Pinon,
chief Kenaz, chief Teman, chief Mibzar,
chief Magdiel, chief Iram. These were the chiefs of Edom.

1ch.21.text: And an adversary rose up against Israel, and incited David to number Israel.
And David said to Joab and to the commanders of the people, "Go, count Israel from Beersheba to Dan, and bring me word, that I may know their number."

And Joab said, "May YHWH add to his people a hundred times what they are! My lord the king, are they not all my lord's servants? Why does my lord seek this thing? Why should it bring guilt upon Israel?"
But the king's word prevailed against Joab. So Joab went out and passed through all Israel, and came back to Jerusalem.

And Joab gave the number of the muster of the people to David: all Israel was one million one hundred thousand men who drew the sword, and Judah four hundred and seventy thousand men who drew the sword.
But Levi and Benjamin he did not count among them, for the king's word was abhorrent to Joab.
And this thing was evil in the eyes of God, and he struck Israel.
And David said to God, "I have sinned greatly in that I have done this thing. And now, take away, I beg you, the guilt of your servant — for I have acted like a great fool."
And YHWH spoke to Gad, David's seer, saying,
"Go and speak to David, saying: Thus says YHWH — three things I hold out against you; choose one of them, and I will do it to you."
So Gad came to David and said to him, "Thus says YHWH: Take your choice —

either three years of famine, or three months of being swept away before your foes while the sword of your enemies overtakes you, or three days of the sword of YHWH — plague in the land, the angel of YHWH destroying through all the territory of Israel. Now then, decide what answer I shall bring back to him who sent me."
And David said to Gad, "I am in deep distress. Let me fall into the hand of YHWH, for his mercies are very great — but into the hand of man let me not fall."
So YHWH sent a plague upon Israel, and seventy thousand men of Israel fell.

And God sent an angel to Jerusalem to destroy it; but as he was destroying, YHWH looked, and he relented of the evil, and said to the destroying angel, "Enough. Now stay your hand." And the angel of YHWH was standing by the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite.

And David lifted his eyes and saw the angel of YHWH standing between earth and heaven, his sword drawn in his hand, stretched out over Jerusalem. And David and the elders, covered in sackcloth, fell on their faces.

And David said to God, "Was it not I who gave the order to count the people? I am the one who sinned — I have done great evil. But these sheep, what have they done? YHWH my God, let your hand be against me and against my father's house, but not against your people, for a plague."
Then the angel of YHWH told Gad to say to David that David should go up and raise an altar to YHWH on the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite.
And David went up at the word of Gad, which he had spoken in the name of YHWH.
Now Ornan turned and saw the angel, and his four sons with him hid themselves — and Ornan was threshing wheat.
And David came to Ornan; and Ornan looked and saw David, and went out from the threshing floor and bowed to David with his face to the ground.
And David said to Ornan, "Give me the site of the threshing floor, that I may build on it an altar to YHWH — give it to me for the full price — that the plague may be held back from the people."

And Ornan said to David, "Take it for yourself, and let my lord the king do what is good in his eyes. See, I give the oxen for burnt offerings, the threshing sledges for wood, and the wheat for the grain offering — I give it all."
But King David said to Ornan, "No — I will surely buy it for the full price. I will not take for YHWH what is yours, nor offer up a burnt offering that cost me nothing."
So David gave Ornan for the site six hundred shekels of gold by weight.
And David built there an altar to YHWH and offered up burnt offerings and peace offerings, and he called on YHWH; and he answered him with fire from heaven upon the altar of burnt offering.
Then YHWH spoke to the angel, and he returned his sword to its sheath.
At that time, when David saw that YHWH had answered him at the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite, he sacrificed there.
For the tabernacle of YHWH, which Moses had made in the wilderness, and the altar of burnt offering were at that time on the high place at Gibeon;
but David could not go before it to seek God, for he was terrified before the sword of the angel of YHWH.

1ch.22.text: Then David said, "This is the house of YHWH God, and this is the altar of burnt offering for Israel."
And David gave orders to gather the strangers who were in the land of Israel, and he set stonecutters to hew dressed stones for building the house of God.
And David prepared iron in abundance for the nails of the doors of the gates and for the clamps, and bronze in abundance beyond weighing,
and cedar timbers past counting — for the Sidonians and the Tyrians brought cedar timbers in abundance to David.

And David said, "Solomon my son is young and tender, and the house to be built for YHWH must be exceedingly magnificent, for fame and for glory through all lands. Let me make preparation for it." So David prepared abundantly before his death.
Then he called for Solomon his son and charged him to build a house for YHWH, the God of Israel.
And David said to Solomon, "My son, it was in my heart to build a house for the name of YHWH my God.
But the word of YHWH came to me, saying: You have shed blood in abundance and waged great wars. You shall not build a house for my name, for you have shed much blood on the earth before me.

Behold, a son shall be born to you; he shall be a man of rest, and I will give him rest from all his enemies on every side. For Solomon shall be his name, and peace and quiet I will give to Israel in his days.
He shall build a house for my name; he shall be to me a son, and I will be to him a father, and I will establish the throne of his kingdom over Israel forever.
Now, my son, may YHWH be with you, that you may prosper and build the house of YHWH your God, as he has spoken concerning you.
Only may YHWH give you insight and understanding, and set you in charge over Israel, that you may keep the law of YHWH your God.
Then you will prosper — if you are careful to do the statutes and the judgments that YHWH commanded Moses for Israel. Be strong and courageous; do not fear, and do not be dismayed.

And behold, in my hardship I have prepared for the house of YHWH a hundred thousand talents of gold and a million talents of silver, and bronze and iron beyond weighing, for it is in such abundance; timber and stone too I have prepared — and to these you shall add.
And with you in abundance are workmen: stonecutters, masons and carpenters, and every man skilled in every kind of work —
of gold, of silver, of bronze, and of iron there is no counting. Rise and work, and may YHWH be with you."
And David commanded all the leaders of Israel to help Solomon his son:
"Is not YHWH your God with you? Has he not given you rest on every side? For he has given the inhabitants of the land into my hand, and the land is subdued before YHWH and before his people.

Now set your heart and your soul to seek YHWH your God. Rise and build the sanctuary of YHWH God, to bring the ark of the covenant of YHWH and the holy vessels of God into the house that is to be built for the name of YHWH."

1jn.1.text: That which was from the beginning, which we have heard, which we have seen with our eyes, which we looked upon and our hands have handled, concerning the Word of life —
and the life was made manifest, and we have seen it, and we bear witness, and we declare to you the eternal life which was with the Father and was made manifest to us —
that which we have seen and heard we declare also to you, so that you too may have fellowship with us; and our fellowship is with the Father and with his Son, Jesus Christ.
And these things we write, that our joy may be made full.
And this is the message we have heard from him and announce to you: God is light, and in him there is no darkness at all.
If we say we have fellowship with him and yet walk in the darkness, we lie, and we do not do the truth.
But if we walk in the light, as he is in the light, we have fellowship with one another, and the blood of Jesus his Son cleanses us from all sin.
If we say we have no sin, we deceive ourselves, and the truth is not in us.
If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just, to forgive us our sins and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness.
If we say we have not sinned, we make him a liar, and his word is not in us.

1jn.2.text: My little children, I write these things to you so that you may not sin. And if anyone does sin, we have an advocate with the Father — Jesus Christ the righteous.
And he is the atoning sacrifice for our sins — and not for ours only, but also for the sins of the whole world.
And by this we know that we have known him: if we keep his commandments.
Whoever says, "I have known him," and does not keep his commandments, is a liar, and the truth is not in him.
But whoever keeps his word — in him, truly, the love of God has been made perfect. By this we know that we are in him:
whoever says he abides in him ought himself to walk just as he walked.
Beloved, I write you no new commandment, but an old commandment, which you have had from the beginning; the old commandment is the word you heard.
Yet again, a new commandment I write to you — a thing true in him and in you — for the darkness is passing away, and the true light already shines.
Whoever says he is in the light and hates his brother is in the darkness still.
Whoever loves his brother abides in the light, and in him there is no cause of stumbling.
But whoever hates his brother is in the darkness, and walks in the darkness, and does not know where he is going, because the darkness has blinded his eyes.
I write to you, little children, because your sins are forgiven for his name's sake.
I write to you, fathers, because you have known him who is from the beginning. I write to you, young men, because you have overcome the evil one.

I have written to you, children, because you have known the Father. I have written to you, fathers, because you have known him who is from the beginning. I have written to you, young men, because you are strong, and the word of God abides in you, and you have overcome the evil one.
Do not love the world, nor the things in the world. If anyone loves the world, the love of the Father is not in him.
For all that is in the world — the desire of the flesh, and the desire of the eyes, and the pride of possessions — is not from the Father, but is from the world.
And the world is passing away, and its desire with it; but whoever does the will of God abides forever.
Children, it is the last hour; and as you heard that antichrist is coming, so now many antichrists have arisen — by this we know that it is the last hour.
They went out from us, but they were not of us; for if they had been of us, they would have remained with us. But they went out, so that it might be laid bare that none of them are of us.
And you have an anointing from the Holy One, and you all know.
I have not written to you because you do not know the truth, but because you know it, and because no lie is of the truth.
Who is the liar, if not the one who denies that Jesus is the Christ? This is the antichrist: the one who denies the Father and the Son.
Everyone who denies the Son does not have the Father either; whoever confesses the Son has the Father also.
As for you — let what you heard from the beginning abide in you. If what you heard from the beginning abides in you, you also will abide in the Son and in the Father.
And this is the promise that he himself promised us: eternal life.
These things I have written to you concerning those who would lead you astray.

And as for you, the anointing you received from him abides in you, and you have no need for anyone to teach you; but as his anointing teaches you concerning all things — and it is true, and is no lie — just as it has taught you, abide in him.
And now, little children, abide in him, so that when he appears we may have boldness, and not shrink from him in shame at his coming.
If you know that he is righteous, you know that everyone who does righteousness has been born of him.

1jn.3.text: See what manner of love the Father has given us, that we should be called children of God — and so we are. For this reason the world does not know us: because it did not know him.
Beloved, now we are children of God, and what we shall be has not yet been made manifest. We know that when he appears we shall be like him, for we shall see him as he is.
And everyone who has this hope set on him purifies himself, just as he is pure.
Everyone who commits sin commits lawlessness also; and sin is lawlessness.
And you know that he was made manifest to take away sins, and in him there is no sin.
No one who abides in him keeps on sinning; no one who keeps on sinning has seen him or known him.
Little children, let no one lead you astray: whoever does righteousness is righteous, just as he is righteous.
Whoever commits sin is of the devil, for the devil has sinned from the beginning. For this the Son of God was made manifest: to undo the works of the devil.
No one born of God commits sin, for God's seed abides in him; and he cannot keep on sinning, because he has been born of God.
By this the children of God and the children of the devil are laid bare: whoever does not do righteousness is not of God — nor is the one who does not love his brother.
For this is the message you heard from the beginning: that we should love one another —
not as Cain, who was of the evil one and slaughtered his brother. And why did he slaughter him? Because his own works were evil, and his brother's righteous.
Do not marvel, brothers, if the world hates you.
We know that we have crossed over from death into life, because we love the brothers. Whoever does not love remains in death.
Everyone who hates his brother is a murderer, and you know that no murderer has eternal life abiding in him.
By this we have come to know love: that he laid down his life for us. And we ought to lay down our lives for the brothers.
But whoever has the world's goods, and sees his brother in need, and shuts his heart against him — how does the love of God abide in him?
Little children, let us not love in word or in tongue, but in deed and in truth.
By this we shall know that we are of the truth, and shall set our heart at rest before him:
for whenever our heart condemns us, God is greater than our heart, and he knows all things.
Beloved, if our heart does not condemn us, we have boldness before God,
and whatever we ask, we receive from him, because we keep his commandments and do what is pleasing in his sight.
And this is his commandment: that we believe in the name of his Son Jesus Christ, and love one another, just as he commanded us.
And whoever keeps his commandments abides in him, and he in him; and by this we know that he abides in us — by the Spirit he has given us.

1pe.1.text: Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ, to the chosen ones, strangers scattered through Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia,

chosen according to the foreknowledge of God the Father, in the sanctifying work of the Spirit, for obedience and for the sprinkling of the blood of Jesus Christ: grace to you, and peace be multiplied.
Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who in his great mercy has begotten us anew to a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead,
to an inheritance imperishable, undefiled, and unfading, kept in the heavens for you,
who by the power of God are guarded through faith for a salvation ready to be unveiled in the last time.
In this you rejoice, though now for a little while, if it must be so, you have been grieved by trials of many kinds,
so that the proving of your faith — more precious than gold that perishes though it is tested by fire — may be found to bring praise and glory and honor at the unveiling of Jesus Christ.
Him you love, though you have not seen him; in him, though you do not now see him, you believe, and you rejoice with joy unspeakable and full of glory,
receiving the goal of your faith, the salvation of your souls.
Concerning this salvation the prophets searched and inquired, who prophesied of the grace that was coming to you,
searching what person or what time the Spirit of Christ within them was pointing to, when he testified beforehand to the sufferings of Christ and the glories that would follow.

To them it was revealed that they were serving not themselves but you in these things — things now announced to you through those who preached the good news to you by the Holy Spirit sent from heaven, things into which angels long to look.
Therefore gird up the loins of your mind; be fully sober; set your hope on the grace that is being brought to you at the unveiling of Jesus Christ.
As children of obedience, do not be shaped by the desires of your former ignorance,
but as he who called you is holy, so be holy yourselves in all your conduct,
for it is written: "You shall be holy, for I am holy."
And if you call on him as Father who judges without partiality, each one according to his work, then walk in fear through the time of your exile,
knowing that it was not with perishable things, with silver or gold, that you were ransomed from the empty way of life handed down from your fathers,
but with the precious blood of Christ, as of a lamb without blemish and without spot.
He was known before the foundation of the world, but was made manifest at the last of the times for your sake —
you who through him are faithful toward God, who raised him from the dead and gave him glory, so that your faith and hope are in God.
Having purified your souls by obedience to the truth for a brotherly love without pretense, love one another earnestly from the heart,
for you have been born anew, not of perishable seed but of imperishable, through the living and abiding word of God.
For all flesh is like grass, and all its glory like the flower of the grass: the grass withers, and the flower falls,
but the word of the Lord endures forever. And this is the word that was preached as good news to you.

1ti.1.text: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the command of God our Savior and of Christ Jesus our hope,
to Timothy, my true child in the faith: grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.
As I urged you when I set out for Macedonia, stay on in Ephesus, so that you may charge certain men to teach no other doctrine,
and to give no heed to myths and endless genealogies, which breed speculation rather than the stewardship of God that is by faith —
for the aim of the charge is love, out of a pure heart and a good conscience and a faith without pretense.
Some have missed that mark, and have turned aside into empty talk,
wanting to be teachers of the law, though they understand neither what they are saying nor the things they insist on so boldly.
But we know that the law is good, if one uses it lawfully,

knowing this: that law is not laid down for the righteous, but for the lawless and the unruly, the ungodly and sinners, the unholy and the profane, for those who strike down father or mother, for murderers,
for the sexually immoral, for men who bed men, for slave-traders, liars, perjurers — and whatever else stands against sound teaching,
according to the gospel of the glory of the blessed God, which was entrusted to me.
I thank Christ Jesus our Lord, who has given me strength, because he judged me faithful and appointed me to his service —
though I was formerly a blasphemer, a persecutor, a man of violence. But I received mercy, because I acted in ignorance, in unbelief,
and the grace of our Lord overflowed beyond all measure, with the faith and love that are in Christ Jesus.
The saying is trustworthy, and worthy of full welcome: Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners — and of them I am the first.
But for this very reason I received mercy: that in me first Christ Jesus might display the whole breadth of his patience, as a pattern for those who would come to believe in him for eternal life.
Now to the King of the ages, imperishable, invisible, the only God, be honor and glory forever and ever. Amen.
This charge I lay before you, Timothy my child, in keeping with the prophecies once spoken over you, that by them you may wage the good warfare,
holding faith and a good conscience — which some have thrust aside, and made shipwreck of the faith.
Among them are Hymenaeus and Alexander, whom I have handed over to Satan, that they may be taught not to blaspheme.

2ch.25.text: Amaziah was twenty-five years old when he became king, and he reigned twenty-nine years in Jerusalem. His mother's name was Jehoaddan, of Jerusalem.
And he did what was right in the eyes of YHWH — yet not with a whole heart.
And when the kingdom was firm in his hand, he killed the servants who had struck down the king his father.

But their children he did not put to death, for it is written in the Law, in the book of Moses, where YHWH commanded, "Fathers shall not die for their children, and children shall not die for their fathers; each shall die for his own sin."

Then Amaziah gathered Judah and set them by fathers' houses under commanders of thousands and commanders of hundreds, for all Judah and Benjamin. He mustered them from twenty years old and upward and found them three hundred thousand picked men, fit for war, who could handle spear and shield.
He also hired from Israel a hundred thousand mighty warriors for a hundred talents of silver.
But a man of God came to him and said, "O king, do not let the army of Israel go with you, for YHWH is not with Israel — not with any of the sons of Ephraim.
But go yourself; act; be strong for the battle. Otherwise God will throw you down before the enemy — for God has power to help and power to throw down."
And Amaziah said to the man of God, "But what about the hundred talents I gave to the troop of Israel?" And the man of God said, "YHWH is able to give you far more than that."
So Amaziah dismissed the troop that had come to him from Ephraim, to go back home. And their anger burned hot against Judah, and they went home in raging fury.
But Amaziah took courage and led out his people, and went to the Valley of Salt, and struck down ten thousand of the men of Seir.
And the men of Judah captured another ten thousand alive, brought them to the top of the rock, and threw them down from the top of the rock, and every one of them was dashed to pieces.

But the men of the troop Amaziah had sent back, refusing to let them go with him to battle, raided the cities of Judah from Samaria to Beth-horon. They struck down three thousand there and carried off a great plunder.
Now after Amaziah came back from striking down the Edomites, he brought the gods of the men of Seir and set them up as his own gods — he bowed down before them and burned offerings to them.
And the anger of YHWH burned against Amaziah, and he sent him a prophet, who said to him, "Why have you sought the gods of a people who could not deliver their own people from your hand?"

But while he was still speaking, the king said to him, "Have we made you a counselor to the king? Stop — why should you be struck down?" So the prophet stopped, but he said, "I know that God has determined to destroy you, because you have done this and have not listened to my counsel."
Then Amaziah king of Judah took counsel and sent to Joash son of Jehoahaz, son of Jehu, king of Israel, saying, "Come, let us look each other in the face."

And Joash king of Israel sent back to Amaziah king of Judah, "The thistle in Lebanon sent to the cedar in Lebanon, saying, 'Give your daughter to my son as a wife.' And a wild beast of Lebanon passed by and trampled the thistle down.
You say, 'Look, I have struck down Edom,' and your heart has lifted you up to boast. Now stay at home. Why provoke disaster, so that you fall — you, and Judah with you?"
But Amaziah would not listen — for it was from God, to give them into the enemy's hand, because they had sought the gods of Edom.
So Joash king of Israel came up, and he and Amaziah king of Judah looked each other in the face at Beth-shemesh, which belongs to Judah.
And Judah was routed before Israel, and every man fled to his own tent.

And Joash king of Israel captured Amaziah king of Judah, son of Joash, son of Jehoahaz, at Beth-shemesh, and brought him to Jerusalem, and broke down the wall of Jerusalem from the Ephraim Gate to the Corner Gate — four hundred cubits.
He took all the gold and silver and all the vessels found in the house of God with Obed-edom, and the treasures of the king's house, and hostages besides, and returned to Samaria.
Amaziah son of Joash, king of Judah, lived fifteen years after the death of Joash son of Jehoahaz, king of Israel.
Now the rest of the deeds of Amaziah, from first to last — are they not written in the book of the kings of Judah and Israel?
From the time Amaziah turned away from following YHWH, they formed a conspiracy against him in Jerusalem, and he fled to Lachish. But they sent men after him to Lachish and put him to death there.
And they carried him back on horses and buried him with his fathers in the city of Judah.

2jn.1.text: The elder, to the elect lady and her children, whom I love in truth — and not I only, but also all who have come to know the truth —
because of the truth that dwells in us, and will be with us forever:
Grace, mercy, and peace will be with us from God the Father and from Jesus Christ, the Son of the Father, in truth and love.
I rejoiced greatly to find some of your children walking in truth, just as we received commandment from the Father.
And now I ask you, lady — not as though I were writing you a new commandment, but the one we have had from the beginning — that we love one another.
And this is love: that we walk according to his commandments. This is the commandment, just as you heard from the beginning, that you should walk in it.
For many deceivers have gone out into the world, who do not confess Jesus Christ coming in the flesh. This is the deceiver and the antichrist.
Watch yourselves, so that you do not lose what we have worked for, but that you may receive a full reward.
Everyone who runs ahead and does not remain in the teaching of Christ does not have God. Whoever remains in the teaching has both the Father and the Son.
If anyone comes to you and does not bring this teaching, do not receive him into your house, and do not say to him, "Greetings."
For whoever says "Greetings" to him shares in his evil works.
Though I have much to write to you, I did not want to do it with paper and ink; but I hope to come to you and speak face to face, so that our joy may be made full.
The children of your elect sister greet you.

2ki.6.text: And the sons of the prophets said to Elisha, "Look now, the place where we sit before you is too cramped for us.
Let us go to the Jordan, and let each man take a beam from there, and let us make ourselves a place there to live in." And he said, "Go."
And one of them said, "Be willing, please, and go with your servants." And he said, "I will go."
So he went with them; and they came to the Jordan and cut down trees.
And as one of them was felling a beam, the iron axhead fell into the water; and he cried out and said, "Ah, my lord — and it was borrowed!"
And the man of God said, "Where did it fall?" And he showed him the place. And he cut a stick and threw it in there, and the iron floated.
And he said, "Lift it out for yourself." So he reached out his hand and took it.
Now the king of Aram was at war with Israel, and he took counsel with his servants, saying, "At such and such a place shall be my camp."
And the man of God sent word to the king of Israel, saying, "Take care not to pass by that place, for the Arameans are coming down there."
And the king of Israel sent to the place the man of God had told him of and warned him about, and he kept guard there — not once and not twice.
And the heart of the king of Aram stormed over this thing; and he called his servants and said to them, "Will you not tell me which of us is for the king of Israel?"
And one of his servants said, "No one, my lord the king; but Elisha, the prophet who is in Israel, tells the king of Israel the words you speak in your own bedchamber."
And he said, "Go and see where he is, that I may send and take him." And it was told him, "Look — he is in Dothan."
So he sent horses there, and chariots, and a heavy force; and they came by night and ringed the city around.
And the servant of the man of God rose early and went out, and there was an army surrounding the city, with horses and chariots. And his young man said to him, "Ah, my lord, what shall we do?"
And he said, "Do not be afraid, for those who are with us are more than those who are with them."

And Elisha prayed and said, "YHWH, open his eyes, that he may see." And YHWH opened the young man's eyes, and he saw — and look, the mountain was full of horses and chariots of fire all around Elisha.
And when they came down toward him, Elisha prayed to YHWH and said, "Strike this nation, I pray, with blindness." And he struck them with blindness, according to the word of Elisha.
And Elisha said to them, "This is not the way, and this is not the city. Follow me, and I will bring you to the man you are seeking." And he led them to Samaria.
And when they had come into Samaria, Elisha said, "YHWH, open the eyes of these men, that they may see." And YHWH opened their eyes, and they saw — and look, they were in the middle of Samaria.
And the king of Israel said to Elisha when he saw them, "Shall I strike them down? Shall I strike them down, my father?"

And he said, "You shall not strike them. Would you strike down men you had taken captive with your own sword and your own bow? Set bread and water before them, that they may eat and drink and go back to their master."
So he spread a great feast for them; and they ate and drank, and he sent them away, and they went back to their master. And the raiding bands of Aram came no more into the land of Israel.
And it came to pass after this that Ben-hadad king of Aram gathered all his army, and went up and laid siege to Samaria.
And there was a great famine in Samaria; and they pressed the siege against it until a donkey's head sold for eighty pieces of silver, and a quarter-kab of dove's dung for five pieces of silver.
And as the king of Israel was passing along the wall, a woman cried out to him, saying, "Save me, my lord the king!"
And he said, "If YHWH does not save you, from where shall I save you? From the threshing floor, or from the winepress?"
And the king said to her, "What troubles you?" And she said, "This woman said to me, 'Give up your son, and we will eat him today, and we will eat my son tomorrow.'
So we boiled my son and ate him. And I said to her the next day, 'Give up your son, and we will eat him' — and she has hidden her son."
And when the king heard the woman's words, he tore his clothes — and he was passing along the wall, and the people looked, and there was sackcloth against his flesh underneath.
And he said, "So may God do to me, and more also, if the head of Elisha son of Shaphat stays on him this day."

Now Elisha was sitting in his house, and the elders were sitting with him. And the king sent a man ahead of him; but before the messenger reached him, Elisha said to the elders, "Do you see how this son of a murderer has sent to take off my head? Watch: when the messenger comes, shut the door and hold him fast against the door. Is not the sound of his master's feet behind him?"
While he was still speaking with them, there was the messenger coming down to him. And the king said, "Look, this evil is from YHWH — why should I wait for YHWH any longer?"

2ki.7.text: And Elisha said, Hear the word of YHWH. Thus says YHWH: By this time tomorrow, a measure of fine flour will sell for a shekel, and two measures of barley for a shekel, in the gate of Samaria.

Then the officer on whose arm the king leaned answered the man of God and said, Look—even if YHWH made windows in the heavens, could such a thing be? And he said, You will see it with your own eyes, but you will not eat of it.
Now four men with a skin disease were at the entrance of the gate, and they said to one another, Why do we sit here until we die?

If we say, We will go into the city—the famine is in the city, and we will die there. And if we sit here, we die. So come, let us go over to the camp of Aram. If they spare us, we live; and if they kill us, we only die.
So they rose in the twilight to go to the camp of Aram; and when they came to the edge of the camp of Aram, behold, there was no one there.

For the Lord had made the camp of Aram hear a sound of chariots, a sound of horses, the sound of a great army; and they said to one another, Look, the king of Israel has hired the kings of the Hittites and the kings of Egypt against us, to come upon us.
So they rose and fled in the twilight, and left their tents and their horses and their donkeys—the camp just as it was—and fled for their lives.

And when these men with the skin disease came to the edge of the camp, they went into one tent and ate and drank, and carried off silver and gold and clothing, and went and hid them; then they came back and entered another tent, and carried off from there too, and went and hid it.

Then they said to one another, What we are doing is not right. This day is a day of good news, and we are keeping silent. If we wait until morning light, guilt will find us. Come now, let us go and tell the king's household.

So they came and called to the gatekeepers of the city, and told them, We went to the camp of Aram, and behold, no one was there—not a human voice—only the horses tied and the donkeys tied, and the tents just as they were.
And the gatekeepers called it out, and word was told within the king's household.

And the king rose in the night and said to his servants, Let me tell you what Aram has done to us. They know we are starving, so they have gone out of the camp to hide in the field, thinking, When they come out of the city we will take them alive and get into the city.

And one of his servants answered and said, Let some men take five of the horses that remain, that are left in the city—look, they are like all the multitude of Israel left in it; look, they are like all the multitude of Israel that has already perished—and let us send and see.
So they took two chariots with horses, and the king sent them after the camp of Aram, saying, Go and see.

And they went after them as far as the Jordan, and behold, all the way was strewn with clothing and gear that the Arameans had thrown off in their headlong flight. And the messengers returned and told the king.
Then the people went out and plundered the camp of Aram; and a measure of fine flour went for a shekel, and two measures of barley for a shekel, according to the word of YHWH.

Now the king had set the officer on whose arm he leaned in charge of the gate, and the people trampled him in the gate, and he died—just as the man of God had said, who spoke when the king came down to him.
For it happened as the man of God had spoken to the king, saying, Two measures of barley for a shekel, and a measure of fine flour for a shekel, by this time tomorrow in the gate of Samaria.

And the officer had answered the man of God and said, Look—even if YHWH made windows in the heavens, could such a thing be? And he had said, You will see it with your own eyes, but you will not eat of it.
And so it happened to him: the people trampled him in the gate, and he died.

2ki.8.text: And Elisha spoke to the woman whose son he had brought back to life, saying, "Rise up and go, you and your household, and sojourn wherever you can sojourn; for YHWH has called for a famine, and it is coming upon the land for seven years."
And the woman rose and did according to the word of the man of God; she went, she and her household, and sojourned in the land of the Philistines seven years.
And it happened, at the end of seven years, that the woman returned from the land of the Philistines, and she went out to cry to the king for her house and for her field.
Now the king was speaking with Gehazi, servant of the man of God, saying, "Tell me, I pray, all the great things that Elisha has done."

And as he was telling the king how Elisha had brought the dead back to life, behold, the very woman whose son he had raised was crying out to the king for her house and for her field. And Gehazi said, "My lord the king, this is the woman, and this is her son, whom Elisha brought back to life."

So the king asked the woman, and she told him her story. And the king appointed for her a certain officer, saying, "Restore to her all that was hers, and all the produce of the field, from the day she left the land until now."
Then Elisha came to Damascus, and Ben-Hadad king of Aram was sick; and it was told him, saying, "The man of God has come here."
And the king said to Hazael, "Take a gift in your hand, and go meet the man of God, and inquire of YHWH through him, saying, 'Shall I recover from this sickness?'"

So Hazael went to meet him, taking a gift in his hand, and all the good things of Damascus, forty camel-loads' worth; and he came and stood before him and said, "Your son Ben-Hadad king of Aram has sent me to you, saying, 'Shall I recover from this sickness?'"
And Elisha said to him, "Go, say to him, 'You shall surely recover' — but YHWH has shown me that he shall surely die."
And he fixed his gaze steadily until Hazael was ashamed, and the man of God wept.

And Hazael said, "Why does my lord weep?" And he answered, "Because I know the evil you will do to the children of Israel: their fortresses you will set on fire, their young men you will slay with the sword, their little ones you will dash to pieces, and their pregnant women you will rip open."
And Hazael said, "But what is your servant, a mere dog, that he should do this great thing?" And Elisha said, "YHWH has shown me you as king over Aram."
So he departed from Elisha and came to his master, who said to him, "What did Elisha say to you?" And he said, "He told me you would surely recover."
But the next day he took the coverlet and dipped it in water and spread it over his face, so that he died; and Hazael reigned in his place.
In the fifth year of Joram son of Ahab king of Israel, Jehoshaphat then being king of Judah, Jehoram son of Jehoshaphat began to reign as king of Judah.
He was thirty-two years old when he began to reign, and he reigned eight years in Jerusalem.
And he walked in the way of the kings of Israel, just as the house of Ahab had done, for the daughter of Ahab was his wife; and he did evil in the eyes of YHWH.
Yet YHWH was not willing to destroy Judah, for the sake of David his servant, since he had promised to give him a lamp for his sons, all their days.
In his days Edom revolted from under the hand of Judah, and set a king over themselves.

So Joram crossed over to Zair, and all his chariots with him; and he rose by night and struck down the Edomites who had surrounded him, along with the commanders of the chariots, and the people fled to their tents.
So Edom has been in revolt from under the hand of Judah to this day. Libnah revolted at that same time.
And the rest of the acts of Joram, and all that he did, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah?
And Joram slept with his fathers, and was buried with his fathers in the city of David; and Ahaziah his son reigned in his place.
In the twelfth year of Joram son of Ahab king of Israel, Ahaziah son of Jehoram began to reign as king of Judah.
Ahaziah was twenty-two years old when he began to reign, and he reigned one year in Jerusalem; and his mother's name was Athaliah, daughter of Omri king of Israel.
And he walked in the way of the house of Ahab, and did evil in the eyes of YHWH, like the house of Ahab, for he was a son-in-law of the house of Ahab.
And he went with Joram son of Ahab to war against Hazael king of Aram at Ramoth-gilead; and the Arameans struck Joram down.

And Joram the king returned to Jezreel to be healed of the wounds the Arameans had dealt him at Ramah, when he fought against Hazael king of Aram; and Ahaziah son of Jehoram king of Judah went down to see Joram son of Ahab in Jezreel, because he was sick.

2ki.9.text: Now Elisha the prophet called one of the sons of the prophets and said to him: Gird up your loins, take this flask of oil in your hand, and go to Ramoth-gilead.
When you arrive there, look for Jehu son of Jehoshaphat, son of Nimshi. Go in, make him rise from among his brothers, and lead him to an inner chamber.
Then take the flask of oil and pour it on his head, and say: Thus says YHWH, I have anointed you king over Israel. Then open the door and flee — do not linger.
So the young man, the young prophet, went to Ramoth-gilead.
When he arrived, the commanders of the army were sitting together, and he said, I have a word for you, commander. Jehu said, For which of us? And he said, For you, commander.
So he rose and went into the house, and poured the oil on his head, and said to him: Thus says YHWH, God of Israel, I have anointed you king over the people of YHWH, over Israel.
You shall strike down the house of Ahab your lord, that I may avenge the blood of my servants the prophets, and the blood of all the servants of YHWH, at the hand of Jezebel.
The whole house of Ahab shall perish; I will cut off from Ahab every male, both bond and free in Israel.
I will make the house of Ahab like the house of Jeroboam son of Nebat, and like the house of Baasha son of Ahijah.
And the dogs shall eat Jezebel in the plot of ground at Jezreel, with none to bury her. Then he opened the door and fled.
Then Jehu came out to the servants of his lord, and they said to him: Is all well? Why did this madman come to you? He said to them: You know the man and his talk.
They said, That is a lie — tell us now. So he said: Thus and thus he spoke to me, saying, Thus says YHWH, I have anointed you king over Israel.
Then they hurried, each man taking his garment, and spread them under him on the bare steps. They blew the trumpet and cried: Jehu is king!
So Jehu son of Jehoshaphat, son of Nimshi, conspired against Joram. Now Joram, with all Israel, had been guarding Ramoth-gilead against Hazael king of Aram.

But King Joram had gone back to Jezreel to be healed of the wounds the Arameans had given him when he fought against Hazael king of Aram. Jehu said: If this is your will, let no one slip out of the city to go and tell it in Jezreel.
Then Jehu mounted his chariot and went to Jezreel, for Joram lay there, and Ahaziah king of Judah had come down to see Joram.

Now the watchman stood on the tower in Jezreel, and he saw the company of Jehu as he came, and said: I see a company. Joram said: Take a horseman and send him to meet them, and let him ask, Is it peace?

So the horseman went to meet him and said, Thus says the king: Is it peace? Jehu said: What have you to do with peace? Turn in behind me. The watchman reported: The messenger reached them, but he is not coming back.
So he sent out a second horseman, who came to them and said, Thus says the king: Is it peace? Jehu answered: What have you to do with peace? Turn in behind me.
The watchman reported: He reached them, but he is not coming back either. And the driving is like the driving of Jehu son of Nimshi — he drives like a madman.

Joram said: Harness the chariot. So they harnessed his chariot, and Joram king of Israel and Ahaziah king of Judah went out, each in his own chariot, to meet Jehu; and they met him on the plot of ground of Naboth the Jezreelite.
When Joram saw Jehu, he said: Is it peace, Jehu? And he answered: What peace, as long as the whorings of your mother Jezebel and her many sorceries abound?
Then Joram wheeled his hands about and fled, crying to Ahaziah: Treachery, Ahaziah!
But Jehu drew his bow with full strength and struck Joram between the shoulders, and the arrow went out through his heart, and he sank down in his chariot.

Jehu said to Bidkar his officer: Take him up and throw him on the plot of ground of Naboth the Jezreelite; for remember, you and I were riding together behind Ahab his father when YHWH laid this burden upon him.

Surely I saw yesterday the blood of Naboth and the blood of his sons, says YHWH, and I will repay you on this very plot, says YHWH. Now therefore take him up and throw him on the plot, according to the word of YHWH.

When Ahaziah king of Judah saw this, he fled by the way of the garden house. Jehu pursued him and cried: Strike him down too! And they struck him in his chariot at the ascent of Gur, near Ibleam. He fled to Megiddo and died there.
His servants carried him in a chariot to Jerusalem and buried him in his tomb with his fathers, in the city of David.
In the eleventh year of Joram son of Ahab, Ahaziah had become king over Judah.
Then Jehu came to Jezreel. When Jezebel heard of it, she painted her eyes with kohl and adorned her head, and looked out of the window.
As Jehu entered the gate, she said: Is it peace, Zimri, murderer of his master?
He lifted his face to the window and said: Who is on my side, who? And two or three eunuchs looked down at him.
He said: Throw her down. So they threw her down, and some of her blood splattered on the wall and on the horses, and he trampled her underfoot.
Then he went in and ate and drank, and said: See now to this cursed woman and bury her, for she is a king's daughter.
So they went to bury her, but they found nothing of her except the skull, the feet, and the palms of her hands.

They came back and told him, and he said: This is the word of YHWH, which he spoke by his servant Elijah the Tishbite, saying: In the plot of ground at Jezreel the dogs shall eat the flesh of Jezebel.
And the corpse of Jezebel shall be as dung upon the face of the field, in the plot of ground at Jezreel, so that none shall say, This is Jezebel.

2pe.1.text: Simeon Peter, a servant and apostle of Jesus Christ, to those who have received a faith of equal honor with ours, in the righteousness of our God and Savior Jesus Christ:
Grace to you, and peace be multiplied, in the knowledge of God and of Jesus our Lord.
His divine power has given us everything that belongs to life and godliness, through the knowledge of him who called us by his own glory and excellence,

and through these he has given us his precious and very great promises, so that through them you might become sharers in the divine nature, having escaped the corruption that is in the world through craving.
For this very reason, bring all diligence to bear, and supply in your faith excellence; and in excellence, knowledge;
and in knowledge, self-mastery; and in self-mastery, endurance; and in endurance, godliness;
and in godliness, love of the brothers; and in love of the brothers, love itself.
For if these things are yours and overflowing, they will keep you from being idle or fruitless in the knowledge of our Lord Jesus Christ.
But whoever lacks them is blind, seeing only what is near, having forgotten the cleansing of his old sins.
Therefore, brothers, be all the more eager to make your calling and election firm; for if you do these things, you will never stumble.
For so there will be richly supplied to you an entrance into the everlasting kingdom of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ.
So I intend always to remind you of these things, though you know them, and stand fixed in the truth you now hold.
And I think it right, as long as I live in this tent of a body, to stir you awake by reminding you,
knowing that the laying down of my tent comes soon, as our Lord Jesus Christ has made plain to me.
And I will do my utmost, so that after my departure you may be able, at every turn, to call these things to mind.
For it was not by following cleverly crafted myths that we made known to you the power and coming of our Lord Jesus Christ — we were eyewitnesses of his majesty.
For he received honor and glory from God the Father when a voice was carried to him from the Majestic Glory: "This is my Son, my beloved; in him I delight."
And we heard this voice carried out of heaven, for we were with him on the holy mountain.
And we hold the prophetic word made more sure, and you do well to attend to it, as to a lamp shining in a murky place, until the day dawns and the morning star rises in your hearts —
knowing this first, that no prophecy of scripture comes from one's own unraveling;
for prophecy was never carried by the will of man, but men spoke from God, borne along by the Holy Spirit.

2pe.2.text: But there were false prophets also among the people, as there will be false teachers among you, who will smuggle in destructive heresies, denying even the Master who bought them — and bringing swift destruction on themselves.
And many will follow their shameless ways, and because of them the way of truth will be reviled;
and in their greed they will make merchandise of you with fabricated words. Their judgment, pronounced long ago, is not idle, and their destruction does not sleep.
For if God did not spare angels when they sinned, but cast them into Tartarus, handing them over in chains of gloom to be kept for judgment;
and did not spare the ancient world, but preserved Noah, a herald of righteousness, with seven others, when he brought the flood upon a world of the ungodly;
and condemned the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah to ruin, burning them to ash, setting them as an example of what is coming to the ungodly;
and rescued righteous Lot, worn down by the shameless conduct of lawless men —
for that righteous man, living among them, was tormented day after day in his righteous soul by the lawless deeds he saw and heard —
then the Lord knows how to rescue the godly from trial, and to keep the unrighteous under punishment for the day of judgment,
and above all those who go after the flesh in defiling craving, and despise authority. Bold and self-willed, they do not tremble to revile the glorious ones,
whereas angels, though greater in strength and power, do not bring against them a reviling judgment.
But these people, like unreasoning beasts born by nature to be caught and destroyed, reviling what they do not understand, will be destroyed in the same destruction with them,
suffering wrong as the wage of wrongdoing. They count it pleasure to revel in broad daylight — stains and blemishes, feasting with you while they delight in their deceptions;
eyes full of adultery, never resting from sin; luring unsteady souls; hearts trained in greed — children of the curse!
Forsaking the straight road, they have gone astray, following the way of Balaam son of Bosor, who loved the wages of wrongdoing
but was rebuked for his own lawbreaking: a mute beast of burden spoke with a human voice and restrained the prophet's madness.
These men are springs without water, mists driven by a storm; for them the gloom of darkness has been kept.
For mouthing swollen and empty words, they lure with the cravings of the flesh, with shameless acts, those who are barely escaping from the people who live in error —
promising them freedom while they themselves are slaves of corruption; for whatever masters a man, to that he is enslaved.

For if, having escaped the defilements of the world through the knowledge of the Lord and Savior Jesus Christ, they are again entangled in them and mastered, their last state has become worse than their first.
For it would have been better for them never to have known the way of righteousness than, having known it, to turn back from the holy commandment handed on to them.
What the true proverb says has happened to them: "A dog returns to its own vomit," and, "A sow, once washed, to wallowing in the mud."

2pe.3.text: This, beloved, is now the second letter I write to you; in both of them I stir your pure mind awake by way of reminder,
that you remember the words spoken beforehand by the holy prophets, and the commandment of the Lord and Savior given through your apostles —
knowing this first, that in the last days mockers will come with their mocking, walking after their own cravings
and saying, "Where is the promise of his coming? For since the fathers fell asleep, everything goes on as it has from the beginning of creation."
For they willfully forget this: that the heavens existed long ago, and the earth was formed out of water and through water by the word of God,
and through these the world of that time was flooded with water and perished.
But the heavens and the earth that now are, by the same word, have been stored up for fire, kept for the day of judgment and the destruction of ungodly men.
But do not let this one thing escape you, beloved: with the Lord one day is as a thousand years, and a thousand years as one day.
The Lord is not slow about his promise, as some count slowness, but is patient toward you, not willing that any should perish, but that all should come to repentance.
But the day of the Lord will come like a thief, and in it the heavens will pass away with a roar, the elements will blaze and dissolve, and the earth and the works within it will be laid bare.
Since all these things are to be dissolved in this way, what kind of people ought you to be — in holy conduct and godliness,
waiting for and hastening the coming of the day of God, because of which the heavens, set ablaze, will dissolve, and the elements melt in the burning.
But according to his promise we wait for new heavens and a new earth, in which righteousness makes its home.
Therefore, beloved, since you wait for these things, be diligent to be found by him without spot or blemish, at peace;
and count the patience of our Lord as salvation — just as our beloved brother Paul also wrote to you, according to the wisdom given him,

as he does in all his letters, speaking in them of these things. In them are some things hard to understand, which the untaught and the unsteady twist, as they do the rest of the scriptures, to their own destruction.
You therefore, beloved, knowing this beforehand, be on your guard, so that you are not carried off by the error of the lawless and fall from your own firm footing;
but grow in the grace and knowledge of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ. To him be the glory, both now and to the day of eternity. Amen.

2sa.13.text: Now it happened after this: Absalom son of David had a beautiful sister named Tamar, and Amnon son of David fell in love with her.
Amnon was so tormented that he made himself sick over Tamar his sister, for she was a virgin, and it seemed impossible to Amnon to do anything to her.
Now Amnon had a friend named Jonadab, son of Shimeah, David's brother, and Jonadab was a very cunning man.
He said to him, Why are you, the king's son, so haggard morning after morning? Won't you tell me? And Amnon said to him, I am in love with Tamar, the sister of my brother Absalom.

Jonadab said to him, Lie down on your bed and pretend to be ill, and when your father comes to see you, say to him, Let my sister Tamar come and give me food to eat -- let her prepare the dish before my eyes, so that I may watch her, and eat from her hand.

So Amnon lay down and pretended to be ill, and when the king came to see him, Amnon said to the king, Please let my sister Tamar come and make a couple of cakes before my eyes, that I may eat from her hand.
So David sent word to Tamar at the house, saying, Go now to the house of your brother Amnon, and prepare food for him.
Tamar went to the house of her brother Amnon, where he was lying down. She took dough and kneaded it, and shaped cakes before his eyes, and cooked the cakes.
Then she took the pan and poured them out before him, but he refused to eat. And Amnon said, Send everyone out from me. So everyone went out from him.
Then Amnon said to Tamar, Bring the food into the chamber, that I may eat from your hand. So Tamar took the cakes she had made and brought them to her brother Amnon in the chamber.
And when she brought them near to him to eat, he seized her, and said to her, Come, lie with me, my sister.
But she said to him, No, my brother, do not violate me, for such a thing is not done in Israel. Do not commit this outrage.
As for me, where could I carry my shame? And you -- you would become like one of the vile fools in Israel. Please, speak to the king now, for he will not keep me from you.
But he would not listen to her voice; he overpowered her, violated her, and lay with her.
Then Amnon hated her with a very great hatred -- indeed, the hatred with which he hated her was greater than the love with which he had loved her. And Amnon said to her, Get up, go.
But she said to him, No -- this wrong of sending me away is greater than the other you have done to me. But he would not listen to her.
He called the young man who served him and said, Put this woman out of here, away from me, and bolt the door behind her.
Now she was wearing an ornamented robe, for that is how the virgin daughters of the king were dressed. So his servant put her out and bolted the door behind her.
Then Tamar put ashes on her head, and tore the ornamented robe she was wearing, and put her hand on her head, and went away crying aloud as she went.

Her brother Absalom said to her, Has Amnon your brother been with you? Now then, my sister, be silent -- he is your brother; do not take this matter to heart. So Tamar remained, desolate, in the house of her brother Absalom.
When King David heard all these things, he was very angry.
But Absalom spoke to Amnon neither good nor bad, for Absalom hated Amnon, because he had violated his sister Tamar.
Two full years later, Absalom had sheepshearers at Baal-hazor, near Ephraim, and Absalom invited all the king's sons.
Absalom came to the king and said, Look, your servant has sheepshearers; let the king and his servants please come with your servant.
But the king said to Absalom, No, my son, let us not all go, or we will be a burden to you. Though Absalom urged him, he would not go, but he blessed him.
Then Absalom said, If not, then let my brother Amnon go with us, please. The king said to him, Why should he go with you?
But Absalom urged him, so he sent Amnon with him, and all the king's sons.

Absalom commanded his servants, Watch now, when Amnon's heart is merry with wine, and I say to you, Strike Amnon down -- then kill him. Do not be afraid; is it not I who have commanded you? Be strong, be brave men.
So Absalom's servants did to Amnon just as Absalom had commanded. Then all the king's sons rose, each mounted his mule, and fled.
While they were still on the road, the report reached David: Absalom has struck down all the king's sons, and not one of them is left.
Then the king rose, tore his garments, and lay on the ground, and all his servants stood by with their garments torn.

But Jonadab, son of Shimeah, David's brother, answered, Let not my lord think that they have killed all the young men, the king's sons -- Amnon alone is dead. For this has been decided by Absalom's mouth ever since the day Amnon violated his sister Tamar.
So now, let not my lord the king take this to heart, thinking that all the king's sons are dead -- Amnon alone is dead.
Meanwhile Absalom fled. And the young man on watch lifted his eyes and looked, and behold, many people were coming along the road behind him, from the side of the hill.
Jonadab said to the king, Look, the king's sons have come; just as your servant said, so it has happened.
As soon as he finished speaking, there were the king's sons, coming in, and they lifted up their voices and wept. The king also, and all his servants, wept with very great weeping.
But Absalom fled and went to Talmai son of Ammihud, king of Geshur. And David mourned for his son every day.
So Absalom fled and went to Geshur, and he was there three years.
And King David's spirit longed to go out to Absalom, for he was consoled concerning Amnon's death.

2ti.1.text: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, for the sake of the promise of life that is in Christ Jesus,
To Timothy, my beloved child: grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.
I thank God, whom I serve with a clear conscience as my forebears did, when without ceasing I remember you in my prayers, night and day,
longing to see you, remembering your tears, so that I may be filled with joy.
I am reminded of the sincere faith that lives in you, the faith that first lived in your grandmother Lois and your mother Eunice, and I am persuaded it lives in you as well.
For this reason I remind you to fan into flame the gift of God that is within you through the laying on of my hands.
For God did not give us a spirit of timidity, but of power and love and self-discipline.
So do not be ashamed of the testimony about our Lord, nor of me his prisoner, but share in suffering for the gospel by the power of God,
who saved us and called us with a holy calling, not according to our works but according to his own purpose and grace, which was given us in Christ Jesus before the ages began,
and has now been revealed through the appearing of our Savior Jesus Christ, who abolished death and brought life and immortality to light through the gospel,
for which I was appointed a herald, an apostle, and a teacher.
That is why I suffer as I do. But I am not ashamed, for I know the one in whom I have believed, and I am convinced that he is able to guard what I have entrusted to him until that day.
Hold to the pattern of sound words that you heard from me, in the faith and love that are in Christ Jesus.
Guard the good deposit entrusted to you, through the Holy Spirit who dwells within us.
You know this: that everyone in Asia has turned away from me, including Phygelus and Hermogenes.
May the Lord grant mercy to the household of Onesiphorus, for he often refreshed me, and was not ashamed of my chains.
but when he arrived in Rome, he searched for me eagerly and found me —
may the Lord grant him to find mercy from the Lord on that day. And all the ways he served me in Ephesus, you know better than I.

2ti.2.text: You then, my child, be strong in the grace that is in Christ Jesus,
and the things you have heard from me in the presence of many witnesses, entrust these to faithful people who will be able to teach others also.
Share in hardship as a good soldier of Christ Jesus.
No soldier on campaign gets entangled in the affairs of everyday life, since his aim is to please the one who enlisted him.
And if anyone competes as an athlete, he is not crowned unless he competes according to the rules.
The hardworking farmer ought to be the first to share in the harvest.
Think over what I am saying, for the Lord will give you understanding in all things.
Remember Jesus Christ, risen from the dead, descended from David — this is my gospel,
for which I suffer hardship, even to the point of being bound in chains like a criminal. But the word of God is not bound.
Therefore I endure everything for the sake of the chosen ones, so that they too may obtain the salvation that is in Christ Jesus, with eternal glory.
This saying is trustworthy: if we have died with him, we will also live with him;
if we endure, we will also reign with him; if we deny him, he also will deny us;
if we are faithless, he remains faithful — for he cannot deny himself.
Remind them of these things, and solemnly charge them before the Lord not to fight over words — a thing that does no good, and only ruins those who listen.
Do your utmost to present yourself to God as one approved, a worker who has no need to be ashamed, rightly handling the word of truth.
But avoid irreverent, empty chatter, for it will lead people further into ungodliness,
and their talk will spread like gangrene. Among them are Hymenaeus and Philetus,
who have missed the mark concerning the truth, saying that the resurrection has already happened, and they are overturning the faith of some.
But God's firm foundation stands, bearing this seal: "The Lord knows those who are his," and, "Let everyone who names the name of the Lord depart from wickedness."
Now in a great house there are not only vessels of gold and silver, but also of wood and clay, some for honorable use, some for common use.
So if anyone cleanses himself from what is common, he will be a vessel for honorable use, set apart, useful to the master, ready for every good work.
Flee youthful passions, and pursue righteousness, faith, love, and peace, together with those who call on the Lord from a pure heart.
Have nothing to do with foolish and ignorant controversies, for you know they breed quarrels.
And the Lord's servant must not be quarrelsome, but gentle to everyone, able to teach, patient when wronged,
correcting his opponents with gentleness, in the hope that God may perhaps grant them repentance leading to the full knowledge of truth,
and that they may come to their senses and escape the snare of the devil, who has taken them captive to do his will.

2ti.3.text: But understand this: in the last days terrible times will come.
For people will be lovers of themselves, lovers of money, boastful, arrogant, abusive, disobedient to their parents, ungrateful, unholy,
without natural affection, unforgiving, slanderous, without self-control, brutal, haters of good,
treacherous, reckless, swollen with conceit, lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God,
holding a form of godliness while denying its power. Turn away from such people.
For among them are those who worm their way into households and captivate weak women weighed down with sins, led astray by all kinds of desires,
always learning and never able to arrive at the knowledge of the truth.
Just as Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses, so these also oppose the truth — people of corrupted mind, disqualified regarding the faith.
But they will not get very far, for their folly will be plain to everyone, as theirs also came to be.
But you have followed my teaching, my conduct, my purpose, my faith, my patience, my love, my endurance,
my persecutions and sufferings — what happened to me at Antioch, at Iconium, at Lystra, the persecutions I endured; yet from them all the Lord rescued me.
Indeed, all who desire to live godly lives in Christ Jesus will be persecuted,
while evil people and impostors will go from bad to worse, deceiving and being deceived.
But as for you, continue in what you have learned and become convinced of, knowing from whom you learned it,
and that from infancy you have known the sacred writings, which are able to make you wise for salvation through faith in Christ Jesus.
All Scripture is breathed out by God and profitable for teaching, for rebuke, for correction, for training in righteousness,
so that the person of God may be complete, equipped for every good work.

2ti.4.text: I charge you solemnly before God and Christ Jesus, who is going to judge the living and the dead, and by his appearing and his kingdom:
preach the word; be ready in season and out of season; rebuke, correct, and encourage, with complete patience and teaching.
For the time will come when people will not endure sound teaching, but according to their own desires they will heap up teachers for themselves to suit their own itching ears,
and they will turn their ears away from the truth and turn aside to myths.
As for you, be sober in all things, endure hardship, do the work of an evangelist, fulfill your ministry.
For I am already being poured out as a drink offering, and the time of my departure has come.
I have fought the good fight, I have finished the race, I have kept the faith.
From now on there is laid up for me the crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous judge, will award to me on that day — and not only to me, but also to all who have loved his appearing.
Do your utmost to come to me soon,
for Demas, in love with this present age, has deserted me and gone to Thessalonica; Crescens has gone to Galatia, Titus to Dalmatia.
Only Luke is with me. Pick up Mark and bring him with you, for he is useful to me for ministry.
Tychicus I have sent to Ephesus.
When you come, bring the cloak that I left with Carpus at Troas, and the scrolls, especially the parchments.
Alexander the coppersmith did me great harm — the Lord will repay him according to his deeds —
beware of him yourself, for he strongly opposed our message.
At my first defense no one stood by me; everyone deserted me. May it not be counted against them.
But the Lord stood by me and gave me strength, so that through me the message might be fully proclaimed and all the nations might hear it; and I was rescued from the lion's mouth.
The Lord will rescue me from every evil deed, and will bring me safely into his heavenly kingdom. To him be glory forever and ever. Amen.
Greet Prisca and Aquila, and the household of Onesiphorus.
Erastus remained in Corinth, and Trophimus I left ill at Miletus.
Do your utmost to come before winter. Eubulus greets you, and Pudens, and Linus, and Claudia, and all the brothers and sisters.
The Lord be with your spirit. Grace be with you all.

3jn.1.text: The elder, to Gaius the beloved, whom I love in truth.
Beloved, I pray that in all things you may prosper and be in health, even as your soul prospers.
For I rejoiced greatly when brothers came and testified to your truth, how you walk in truth.
I have no greater joy than this: to hear that my children are walking in the truth.
Beloved, you act faithfully in whatever you do for the brothers, and especially for strangers,
who have testified to your love before the church. You will do well to send them on their way in a manner worthy of God.
For they went out for the sake of the Name, taking nothing from the outsiders.
We then ought to welcome such men, so that we may become fellow workers for the truth.
I wrote something to the church, but Diotrephes, who loves to put himself first among them, will not receive us.

So if I come, I will bring up what he is doing, prating against us with malicious words. And not content with that, he himself refuses to welcome the brothers, stops those who want to, and throws them out of the church.
Beloved, do not imitate what is evil, but what is good. Whoever does good is of God; whoever does evil has not seen God.
Demetrius has a good report from everyone, and from the truth itself. We also testify for him, and you know that our testimony is true.
I had much to write to you, but I do not want to write it with ink and pen.
I hope to see you soon, and we will speak face to face.
Peace to you. The friends greet you. Greet the friends, each by name.

col.1.text: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, and Timothy our brother,
to the holy and faithful brothers and sisters in Christ at Colossae: grace to you and peace from God our Father.
We give thanks to God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, praying always for you,
for we have heard of your faith in Christ Jesus and of the love you hold for all the holy ones,
because of the hope laid up for you in the heavens — the hope you heard of before, in the word of the truth of the gospel
that has come to you. In all the world it is bearing fruit and growing, as it has among you from the day you heard it and knew the grace of God in truth,
just as you learned it from Epaphras, our beloved fellow servant, who is a faithful minister of Christ on our behalf,
and who has told us of your love in the Spirit.
And so, from the day we heard, we have not ceased praying for you, asking that you be filled with the knowledge of his will in all wisdom and spiritual understanding,
so that you may walk worthy of the Lord, pleasing him in everything — bearing fruit in every good work and growing in the knowledge of God,
strengthened with all strength according to the might of his glory, for all endurance and patience — and with joy
giving thanks to the Father, who has made you fit to share the inheritance of the holy ones in the light.
He has rescued us from the dominion of darkness and carried us over into the kingdom of the Son of his love,
in whom we have redemption, the forgiveness of sins.
He is the image of the invisible God, the firstborn of all creation,
for in him all things were created — in the heavens and on the earth, the seen and the unseen, whether thrones or dominions, rulers or powers. All things were created through him and for him,
and he is before all things, and in him all things hold together.
And he is the head of the body, the church. He is the beginning, the firstborn from the dead, so that in everything he might be first —
for in him all the fullness was pleased to dwell,
and through him to reconcile all things to himself — making peace by the blood of his cross — through him, whether things on the earth or things in the heavens.
And you, who were once estranged, enemies in your minds through evil works —
he has now reconciled in the body of his flesh through death, to present you holy and blameless and beyond accusation before him —

if indeed you remain in the faith, grounded and steady, not shifting from the hope of the gospel you heard, which has been proclaimed in all creation under heaven, and of which I, Paul, became a servant.
Now I rejoice in my sufferings for your sake, and in my flesh I fill up what remains of the afflictions of Christ, for the sake of his body, which is the church.
Of this church I became a servant, by the stewardship God gave me for you: to bring the word of God to its fullness —
the mystery hidden from the ages and from the generations, but now laid open to his holy ones.
To them God chose to make known, among the nations, the riches of the glory of this mystery — which is Christ in you, the hope of glory.
Him we proclaim, warning every person and teaching every person in all wisdom, so that we may present every person complete in Christ.
For this I labor, striving with his energy, which works in me with power.

col.2.text: For I want you to know how great a struggle I have for you, and for those in Laodicea, and for all who have never seen my face in the flesh —
that their hearts may be strengthened, knit together in love, and brought into all the riches of full assurance of understanding, into the knowledge of the mystery of God: Christ,
in whom all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge lie hidden.
I say this so that no one may deceive you with persuasive talk.
For though I am absent in the flesh, I am with you in spirit, rejoicing to see your good order and the firmness of your faith in Christ.
So then, as you received Christ Jesus the Lord, walk in him —
rooted and built up in him, made firm in the faith as you were taught, overflowing with thanksgiving.
See to it that no one carries you off through philosophy and empty deceit, following human tradition, following the elemental powers of the world, and not following Christ.
For in him all the fullness of deity dwells in bodily form,
and in him you have been made full — in him who is the head of every ruler and power.
In him you were also circumcised, with a circumcision not made by hands: the stripping off of the body of flesh, in the circumcision of Christ.
You were buried with him in baptism, and in him you were also raised with him, through faith in the working of God, who raised him from the dead.
And you, who were dead in your trespasses and in the uncircumcision of your flesh — he made you alive together with him, forgiving us all our trespasses.
He wiped out the record of debt that stood against us with its decrees; he took it out of the way, nailing it to the cross.
He stripped the rulers and powers bare and made a public spectacle of them, leading them in triumph in him.
