Therefore let no one judge you in matters of food or drink, or with regard to a festival, a new moon, or sabbaths.
These are a shadow of the things to come; the body belongs to Christ.
Let no one disqualify you — no one who delights in self-abasement and the worship of angels, going on in detail about visions, puffed up without cause by a fleshly mind,
and not holding fast to the head, from whom the whole body, supplied and knit together through its joints and ligaments, grows with a growth that is from God.
If you died with Christ to the elemental powers of the world, why, as though still living in the world, do you submit to its decrees —
“Do not handle, do not taste, do not touch”?
All these things perish with use; they follow merely human commands and teachings.
Such rules have a reputation for wisdom, with their self-made religion, their self-abasement, their harshness to the body — but they are worth nothing against the indulgence of the flesh.

col.3.text: If then you were raised with Christ, seek the things above, where Christ is, seated at the right hand of God.
Set your minds on the things above, not on the things of the earth,
for you died, and your life is hidden with Christ in God.
When Christ, who is your life, is revealed, then you also will be revealed with him in glory.
Put to death, then, what belongs to the earth in you: sexual immorality, impurity, passion, evil desire, and greed — which is idolatry.
Because of these the wrath of God is coming upon the children of disobedience.
In these you too once walked, when you lived among them.
But now, put them all away: anger, rage, malice, slander, and filthy talk from your mouth.
Do not lie to one another, for you have stripped off the old self with its practices
and put on the new — the self being renewed in knowledge after the image of its Creator.
Here there is no Greek and Jew, no circumcised and uncircumcised, no barbarian, Scythian, slave, or free — but Christ is all, and in all.
Clothe yourselves, then, as God's chosen ones, holy and beloved, with deep compassion, kindness, humility, gentleness, and patience,
bearing with one another and forgiving each other, if anyone has a grievance against another. As the Lord forgave you, so also must you forgive.
And over all these, put on love, which is the bond of completeness.
And let the peace of Christ rule in your hearts — the peace to which you were called in one body. And be thankful.
Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly. In all wisdom teach and admonish one another with psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs, singing with grace in your hearts to God.
And whatever you do, in word or in deed, do everything in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks through him to God the Father.
Wives, submit to your husbands, as is fitting in the Lord.
Husbands, love your wives and do not be harsh with them.
Children, obey your parents in everything, for this is pleasing in the Lord.
Fathers, do not provoke your children, or they will lose heart.
Slaves, obey your earthly masters in everything — not with service done for the eye, to please people, but with a single heart, fearing the Lord.
Whatever you do, work at it from the soul, as for the Lord and not for people,
knowing that from the Lord you will receive the inheritance as your reward. Serve the Lord Christ.
For the one who does wrong will be repaid for the wrong — and there is no favoritism.

col.4.text: Masters, give your slaves what is just and fair, knowing that you too have a Master in heaven.
Devote yourselves to prayer, keeping watch in it with thanksgiving.
Pray also for us, that God may open to us a door for the word, to speak the mystery of Christ — for which I am in chains —
that I may make it plain, as I ought to speak.
Walk in wisdom toward those outside, making the most of the time.
Let your speech always be gracious, seasoned with salt, so that you may know how you ought to answer each one.
Tychicus will tell you all my news — the beloved brother, faithful minister, and fellow servant in the Lord.
I have sent him to you for this very purpose: that you may know how we are, and that he may encourage your hearts.
With him comes Onesimus, the faithful and beloved brother, who is one of you. They will tell you everything that is happening here.
Aristarchus, my fellow prisoner, greets you; and Mark, the cousin of Barnabas — you have received instructions about him: if he comes to you, welcome him —
and Jesus who is called Justus. These are the only ones of the circumcision among my fellow workers for the kingdom of God, and they have been a comfort to me.
Epaphras greets you — one of your own, a servant of Christ, always wrestling for you in his prayers, that you may stand complete and fully assured in all the will of God.
For I bear him witness that he labors hard for you and for those in Laodicea and in Hierapolis.
Luke, the beloved physician, greets you, and so does Demas.
Greet the brothers and sisters in Laodicea, and Nympha and the church in her house.
And when this letter has been read among you, see that it is read also in the church of the Laodiceans, and that you in turn read the letter from Laodicea.
And say to Archippus: "See to the ministry you received in the Lord, that you fulfill it."
This greeting is in my own hand — Paul's. Remember my chains. Grace be with you.

est.1.text: It happened in the days of Ahasuerus — that Ahasuerus who reigned from India to Cush, over a hundred and twenty-seven provinces —
in those days, when King Ahasuerus sat on his royal throne in Susa the citadel,
in the third year of his reign, he made a feast for all his officials and his servants — the army of Persia and Media, the nobles and the princes of the provinces before him —
while he displayed the riches of his glorious kingdom and the splendor of his great majesty, many days — a hundred and eighty days.
And when those days were fulfilled, the king made a feast for all the people found in Susa the citadel, from the greatest to the least, seven days, in the court of the garden of the king's pavilion.

There were hangings of white cotton and violet, caught up with cords of fine linen and purple on rods of silver and pillars of marble; couches of gold and silver stood on a pavement of porphyry and marble, mother-of-pearl and dark stone.
Drink was served in vessels of gold — no vessel like another — and royal wine in abundance, by the open hand of the king.
And the drinking was by rule: no one compelled; for so the king had charged every steward of his house, to do as each man pleased.
Queen Vashti also made a feast for the women in the royal house that belonged to King Ahasuerus.
On the seventh day, when the king's heart was merry with wine, he ordered Mehuman, Biztha, Harbona, Bigtha and Abagtha, Zethar and Carkas — the seven eunuchs who attended King Ahasuerus —
to bring Queen Vashti before the king in the royal crown, to show the peoples and the princes her beauty; for she was lovely to look upon.
But Queen Vashti refused to come at the king's word carried by the eunuchs. And the king grew very angry, and his fury burned in him.
Then the king spoke to the wise men who knew the times — for such was the king's custom, to set his affairs before all who knew law and judgment —
and those nearest him were Carshena, Shethar, Admatha, Tarshish, Meres, Marsena, and Memucan, the seven princes of Persia and Media who saw the king's face and sat first in the kingdom:
"By law, what is to be done with Queen Vashti, because she has not performed the command of King Ahasuerus carried by the eunuchs?"
And Memucan said before the king and the princes, "Not against the king alone has Queen Vashti done wrong, but against all the princes and all the peoples in all the provinces of King Ahasuerus.

For word of the queen will go out to all the women, making their husbands contemptible in their eyes, when they say, 'King Ahasuerus commanded Queen Vashti to be brought before him — and she did not come.'
This very day the noblewomen of Persia and Media who have heard what the queen did will say it to all the king's princes — and there will be contempt and wrath in plenty.

If it please the king, let a royal word go out from his presence, and let it be written among the laws of Persia and Media, never to pass away: that Vashti come no more before King Ahasuerus, and that the king give her royal place to another better than she.
And when the king's decree is heard through all his kingdom — vast as it is — all the wives will give honor to their husbands, from the greatest to the least."
The word was good in the eyes of the king and the princes, and the king did as Memucan had said.

And he sent letters to all the king's provinces — to every province in its own script, and to every people in its own tongue — that every man should be master in his own house and speak the language of his own people.

gal.1.text: Paul, an apostle — not from men, nor through a man, but through Jesus Christ and God the Father, who raised him from the dead —
and all the brothers with me, to the churches of Galatia:
grace to you and peace from God the Father and our Lord Jesus Christ,
who gave himself for our sins, to tear us free from this present evil age, according to the will of our God and Father —
to him be the glory forever and ever. Amen.
I am astonished that you are turning away so quickly, from the one who called you in the grace of Christ, to a different gospel —
not that there is another one, only there are some who are troubling you and want to twist the gospel of Christ.
But even if we, or an angel from heaven, should preach to you a gospel contrary to what we preached to you, let him be accursed.
As we have said before, and now I say again: if anyone preaches to you a gospel contrary to what you received, let him be accursed.
For am I now seeking the favor of men, or of God? Or am I trying to please men? If I were still pleasing men, I would not be a servant of Christ.
For I want you to know, brothers, that the gospel I preached is no human invention.
For I did not receive it from any man, nor was I taught it — it came to me through a revelation of Jesus Christ.
For you have heard of my former life in Judaism, how I persecuted the church of God beyond all measure and tried to destroy it.
And I was advancing in Judaism beyond many of my own age among my people, being far more zealous for the traditions of my fathers.
But when he who set me apart from my mother's womb, and called me by his grace, was pleased
to reveal his Son in me, so that I might preach him among the Gentiles, I did not at once confer with flesh and blood,
nor did I go up to Jerusalem to those who were apostles before me — I went away instead into Arabia, and returned again to Damascus.
Then after three years I went up to Jerusalem to get to know Cephas, and I stayed with him fifteen days.
But I saw none of the other apostles — only James, the Lord's brother.
In what I write to you — see, before God — I do not lie.
Then I went into the regions of Syria and Cilicia.
And I was still unknown by face to the churches of Judea that are in Christ.
They only kept hearing it said, "He who once persecuted us is now preaching the very faith he once tried to destroy."
And they glorified God because of me.

gal.2.text: Then after fourteen years I went up again to Jerusalem with Barnabas, taking Titus along with me.
I went up because of a revelation, and I laid before them the gospel I preach among the Gentiles — privately, before those of repute — for fear that I might be running, or had run, in vain.
But not even Titus, who was with me, though he was a Greek, was compelled to be circumcised —
and this because of false brothers smuggled in, who slipped in to spy out the freedom we have in Christ Jesus, meaning to hale us back into slavery —
to them we did not yield in submission, not for an hour, so that the truth of the gospel might remain standing for you.
And from those who seemed to be something — whatever they were makes no difference to me; God shows no favoritism — those, I say, who seemed influential added nothing to me.
On the contrary, they saw that I had been entrusted with the gospel to the uncircumcised, just as Peter had been to the circumcised
(for he who worked in Peter for an apostleship to the circumcised worked also in me for the Gentiles),

and when James and Cephas and John, who were reputed to be pillars, recognized the grace given to me, they gave to me and Barnabas the right hand of fellowship, that we should go to the Gentiles and they to the circumcised.
Only, they asked that we remember the poor — the very thing I was eager to do.
But when Cephas came to Antioch, I opposed him to his face, because he stood condemned.
For before certain men came from James, he ate with the Gentiles; but when they came, he drew back and separated himself, fearing those of the circumcision.
And the rest of the Jews joined him in this pretense, so that even Barnabas was carried away by their hypocrisy.

But when I saw that they were not walking a straight path toward the truth of the gospel, I said to Cephas in front of them all, "If you, though a Jew, live like a Gentile and not like a Jew, how can you compel the Gentiles to live like Jews?"
We ourselves are Jews by birth, and not sinners from among the Gentiles —

yet knowing that no one is justified by works of the law but through faith in Jesus Christ, we too believed in Christ Jesus, in order to be justified by faith in Christ and not by works of the law, because by works of the law no flesh will be justified.
But if, in seeking to be justified in Christ, we ourselves were found sinners too, is Christ then a servant of sin? Let it never be!
For if I rebuild what I tore down, I prove myself a transgressor.
For through the law I died to the law, that I might live to God. I have been crucified with Christ —
and it is no longer I who live, but Christ who lives in me. And the life I now live in the flesh, I live by faith in the Son of God, who loved me and gave himself up for me.
I do not set aside the grace of God — for if righteousness came through the law, then Christ died for nothing.

gal.3.text: O foolish Galatians! Who has cast a spell on you? Before your very eyes Jesus Christ was set forth, placarded as crucified.
This alone I want to learn from you: did you receive the Spirit by works of the law, or by hearing with faith?
Are you so foolish? Having begun in the Spirit, are you now finishing in the flesh?
Did you suffer so much for nothing — if indeed it was for nothing?
So then, does he who supplies the Spirit to you and works miracles among you do it by works of the law, or by hearing with faith? —
just as Abraham believed God, and it was counted to him as righteousness.
Know then that it is those of faith who are the sons of Abraham.
And the Scripture, foreseeing that God would justify the Gentiles by faith, announced the gospel beforehand to Abraham: "In you shall all the nations be blessed."
So then, those who are of faith are blessed together with Abraham, the man of faith.
For all who rely on works of the law are under a curse, for it is written, "Cursed is everyone who does not abide by all the things written in the book of the law, to do them."
Now it is plain that no one is justified before God by the law, for "The righteous shall live by faith."
But the law is not of faith — rather, "The one who does them shall live by them."
Christ redeemed us from the curse of the law by becoming a curse for us — for it is written, "Cursed is everyone who hangs on a tree" —
so that in Christ Jesus the blessing of Abraham might flow to the Gentiles, and we might receive the promised Spirit through faith.
Brothers, I speak in human terms: even with a man's covenant, once it is ratified, no one sets it aside or adds to it.
Now the promises were spoken to Abraham and to his offspring. It does not say, "And to offsprings," as of many, but as of one: "And to your offspring," who is Christ.
This is what I mean: the law, which came four hundred and thirty years later, does not annul a covenant already ratified by God, so as to make the promise void.
For if the inheritance comes by the law, it no longer comes by promise — but God granted it to Abraham through a promise.
Why then the law? It was added because of transgressions, until the offspring should come to whom the promise had been made — ordained through angels, by the hand of a mediator.
Now a mediator implies more than one, but God is one.
Is the law then set against the promises of God? Let it never be! For if a law had been given that could impart life, then righteousness would indeed have come by the law.
But the Scripture shut up everything together under sin, so that the promise, by faith in Jesus Christ, might be given to those who believe.
Now before faith came, we were held in custody under the law, locked up until the coming faith should be revealed.
So then, the law became our guardian to bring us to Christ, that we might be justified by faith.
But now that faith has come, we are no longer under a guardian,
for you are all sons of God through faith in Christ Jesus.
For as many of you as were baptized into Christ have clothed yourselves with Christ.
There is no longer Jew nor Greek, there is no longer slave nor free, there is no longer male and female — for you are all one in Christ Jesus.
And if you belong to Christ, then you are Abraham's offspring, heirs according to promise.

gal.4.text: I mean that the heir, as long as he is a child, is no different from a slave, though he is owner of everything;
he is under guardians and stewards until the date set by his father.
So it was with us: while we were children, we were held as slaves under the elemental powers of the world.
But when the fullness of time had come, God sent out his Son, born of a woman, born under the law,
to redeem those under the law, so that we might receive adoption as sons.
And because you are sons, God has sent the Spirit of his Son into our hearts, crying out, "Abba! Father!"
So you are no longer a slave, but a son — and if a son, then an heir through God.
Formerly, when you did not know God, you were slaves to beings that by nature are no gods at all.
But now that you have come to know God — or rather, to be known by God — how can you turn back again to those weak and beggarly powers, wanting to be enslaved to them all over again?
You go on observing days and months and seasons and years!
I am afraid for you, that I may have labored over you for nothing.
Brothers, I beg you, become as I am, for I too have become as you are. You did me no wrong.
You know it was because of a bodily illness that I preached the gospel to you the first time,
and though my condition put you to the test, you did not scorn or spit at me — you welcomed me as an angel of God, as Christ Jesus himself.
What then has become of that blessing you felt? For I testify to you that, if it had been possible, you would have torn out your own eyes and given them to me.
Have I then become your enemy by telling you the truth?
They court you eagerly, but not for good — they want to shut you out, so that you will court them instead.
It is good to be courted always in a good cause, and not only when I am present with you,
my little children, for whom I am again in labor pains until Christ is formed in you!
How I wish I could be with you now and change my tone, for I am at my wits' end over you.
Tell me, you who want so badly to be under the law, will you not listen to what the law says?
For it is written that Abraham had two sons, one by the slave woman and one by the free woman.
But the son by the slave woman was born according to the flesh, while the son by the free woman was born through promise.
Now this is to be understood as an allegory: these women are two covenants. One is from Mount Sinai, bearing children into slavery — this is Hagar.
Now Hagar is Mount Sinai in Arabia, and she corresponds to the present Jerusalem, for she is in slavery with her children.
But the Jerusalem above is free, and she is our mother.

For it is written: "Rejoice, O barren one who bears no children; break into cry, you who feel no labor pains — for the children of the desolate woman are many more than the children of the one who has a husband."
Now you, brothers, like Isaac, are children of promise.
But just as then the one born according to the flesh persecuted the one born according to the Spirit, so it is now too.
But what does the Scripture say? "Cast out the slave woman and her son, for the son of the slave woman shall never inherit with the son of the free woman."
So then, brothers, we are not children of the slave woman, but of the free woman.

gal.5.text: For freedom Christ has set us free; stand firm, then, and do not be tangled up again in a yoke of slavery.
Look — I, Paul, tell you that if you let yourselves be circumcised, Christ will be of no benefit to you at all.
I testify again to every man who accepts circumcision that he is obligated to keep the whole law.
You have been cut off from Christ, you who would be justified by the law — you have fallen out of grace.
For we, through the Spirit, by faith, are eagerly waiting for the hope of righteousness.
For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision nor uncircumcision counts for anything — only faith working through love.
You were running well. Who cut in on you, to keep you from obeying the truth?
That persuasion did not come from the one who calls you.
A little leaven leavens the whole lump.
I am confident about you in the Lord, that you will take no other view; but the one who is troubling you will bear the judgment, whoever he is.
But if I, brothers, am still preaching circumcision, why am I still being persecuted? Then the offense of the cross has been abolished.
I wish those who are unsettling you would go all the way and cut themselves off!
For you were called to freedom, brothers — only do not turn that freedom into an opening for the flesh, but through love be servants to one another.
For the whole law is fulfilled in a single word: "You shall love your neighbor as yourself."
But if you go on biting and devouring one another, watch out, or you will be consumed by one another.
So I say: walk by the Spirit, and you will not carry out the desire of the flesh.
For the flesh desires against the Spirit, and the Spirit against the flesh — they are set against each other, to keep you from doing the very things you want to do.
But if you are led by the Spirit, you are not under the law.
Now the works of the flesh are plain: sexual immorality, impurity, debauchery,
idolatry, sorcery, hatreds, strife, jealousy, fits of rage, selfish ambitions, dissensions, factions,
envy, drunkenness, orgies, and things like these. I warn you, as I warned you before: those who do such things will not inherit the kingdom of God.
But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness,
gentleness, self-control — against such things there is no law.
And those who belong to Christ Jesus have crucified the flesh with its passions and desires.
If we live by the Spirit, let us also walk in step with the Spirit.
Let us not become conceited, provoking one another, envying one another.

gal.6.text: Brothers, if anyone is caught in any trespass, you who are spiritual should restore him in a spirit of gentleness — watching yourself, lest you too be tempted.
Carry one another's burdens, and in this way fulfill the law of Christ.
For if anyone thinks he is something, when he is nothing, he is fooling himself.
But let each one test his own work, and then he will have reason to boast in himself alone, and not in his neighbor —
for each will carry his own load.
Let the one who is taught the word share all good things with the one who teaches.
Do not be deceived — God is not mocked: whatever a man sows, that he will also reap.
The one who sows to his own flesh will from the flesh reap corruption; the one who sows to the Spirit will from the Spirit reap eternal life.
And let us not grow weary in doing good, for in due season we will reap, if we do not give up.
So then, as we have opportunity, let us work for the good of everyone, and especially for those of the household of faith.
See with what large letters I am writing to you in my own hand!
It is those who want to make a good showing in the flesh who are forcing you to be circumcised — and only so that they will not be persecuted for the cross of Christ.
For not even the circumcised themselves keep the law, but they want you circumcised so that they can boast in your flesh.
But far be it from me to boast, except in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, through which the world has been crucified to me, and I to the world.
For neither circumcision counts for anything, nor uncircumcision, but a new creation.
And as for all who will walk by this rule — peace and mercy upon them, and upon the Israel of God.
From now on let no one make trouble for me, for I carry on my body the brand-marks of Jesus.
The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit, brothers. Amen.

gen.31.text: And he heard the words of Laban's sons, saying, Jacob has taken all that was our father's; from what was our father's he has made all this wealth.
And Jacob saw Laban's face, and behold, it was not toward him as in days before.
And YHWH said to Jacob, Return to the land of your fathers and to your kindred, and I will be with you.
And Jacob sent and called Rachel and Leah to the field, to his flock,
and he said to them, I see your father's face, that it is not toward me as in days before; but the God of my father has been with me.
And you know that with all my strength I have served your father.
Yet your father has cheated me and changed my wages ten times over; but God did not let him do me harm.
If he said, The speckled shall be your wages, then all the flock bore speckled; and if he said, The striped shall be your wages, then all the flock bore striped.
So God has stripped away your father's livestock and given it to me.
And it happened at the time the flock was breeding that I lifted my eyes and saw in a dream, and behold, the he-goats mounting the flock were striped, speckled, and mottled.
And the angel of God said to me in the dream, Jacob. And I said, Here I am.
And he said, Lift up your eyes now and see: all the he-goats mounting the flock are striped, speckled, and mottled — for I have seen all that Laban is doing to you.
I am the God of Bethel, where you anointed a pillar, where you vowed a vow to me. Now rise, go out from this land, and return to the land of your kindred.
And Rachel and Leah answered and said to him, Is there still any portion or inheritance for us in our father's house?
Are we not counted by him as strangers? For he has sold us, and he has wholly devoured our silver.
For all the wealth that God has stripped from our father — it belongs to us and to our children. Now then, all that God has said to you, do.
And Jacob rose and set his sons and his wives upon the camels,
and he drove off all his livestock and all the goods he had gathered, the livestock of his own getting that he had gathered in Paddan-aram, to go to Isaac his father in the land of Canaan.
Now Laban had gone to shear his flock, and Rachel stole the household gods that were her father's.
And Jacob stole the heart of Laban the Aramean, by not telling him that he was fleeing.
So he fled, he and all that he had; he rose and crossed the River, and set his face toward the hill country of Gilead.
And on the third day it was told to Laban that Jacob had fled.
And he took his kinsmen with him and pursued after him seven days' journey, and overtook him in the hill country of Gilead.
And God came to Laban the Aramean in a dream by night and said to him, Take care that you speak to Jacob neither good nor evil.
And Laban overtook Jacob. Now Jacob had pitched his tent in the hill country, and Laban with his kinsmen camped in the hill country of Gilead.
And Laban said to Jacob, What have you done, that you stole my heart and drove off my daughters like captives of the sword?
Why did you flee in secret and steal away from me, and not tell me? I would have sent you off with gladness and with songs, with tambourine and lyre.
And you did not let me kiss my sons and my daughters. Now you have done a foolish thing.
It is in the power of my hand to do you harm; but the God of your father spoke to me last night, saying, Take care that you speak to Jacob neither good nor evil.
And now you have gone your way because you longed so fiercely for your father's house — but why did you steal my gods?
And Jacob answered and said to Laban, Because I was afraid, for I said, You might tear your daughters away from me.
With whomever you find your gods, that one shall not live. Before our kinsmen, point out what of yours is with me, and take it. For Jacob did not know that Rachel had stolen them.
And Laban went into Jacob's tent, and into Leah's tent, and into the tent of the two maidservants, and found nothing; and he came out of Leah's tent and went into Rachel's tent.
Now Rachel had taken the household gods and put them in the camel's saddle and sat upon them. And Laban felt through all the tent and found nothing.
And she said to her father, Let my lord not burn with anger that I cannot rise before you, for the way of women is upon me. So he searched, and did not find the household gods.
Then Jacob's anger burned, and he took Laban to task; and Jacob answered and said to Laban, What is my crime? What is my sin, that you have come blazing after me?
You have felt through all my goods — what have you found of all your household's goods? Set it here before my kinsmen and yours, and let them judge between the two of us.
These twenty years I have been with you: your ewes and your she-goats have not miscarried, and the rams of your flock I have not eaten.
What was torn by beasts I did not bring to you; I bore the loss myself. From my hand you demanded it, whether stolen by day or stolen by night.
By day the heat devoured me, and the frost by night, and sleep fled from my eyes.
These twenty years I have been in your house: I served you fourteen years for your two daughters and six years for your flock, and you changed my wages ten times over.

If the God of my father, the God of Abraham and the Dread of Isaac, had not been with me, surely now you would have sent me away empty-handed. God has seen my affliction and the toil of my hands, and last night he gave judgment.

And Laban answered and said to Jacob, The daughters are my daughters, and the sons are my sons, and the flock is my flock; all that you see is mine. Yet for my daughters — what can I do for them this day, or for their sons whom they have borne?
Now then, come, let us cut a covenant, I and you, and let it be a witness between me and you.
And Jacob took a stone and raised it up as a pillar.
And Jacob said to his kinsmen, Gather stones. And they took stones and made a heap, and they ate there upon the heap.
And Laban called it Jegar-sahadutha, but Jacob called it Galeed.
And Laban said, This heap is a witness between me and you this day. Therefore its name was called Galeed,
and also Mizpah, for he said, May YHWH keep watch between me and you when we are hidden from one another's sight.
If you mistreat my daughters, or if you take wives besides my daughters — though no man is with us, see, God is witness between me and you.
And Laban said to Jacob, Behold this heap, and behold the pillar that I have set between me and you.
This heap is witness, and the pillar is witness, that I will not pass beyond this heap to you, and you will not pass beyond this heap and this pillar to me, for harm.
May the God of Abraham and the God of Nahor judge between us — the God of their father. And Jacob swore by the Dread of his father Isaac.
And Jacob offered a sacrifice on the mountain and called his kinsmen to eat bread; and they ate bread and spent the night on the mountain.

gen.32.text: And Laban rose early in the morning and kissed his sons and his daughters and blessed them; and Laban went and returned to his own place.
And Jacob went on his way, and angels of God met him.
And Jacob said when he saw them, This is the camp of God. And he called the name of that place Mahanaim.
And Jacob sent messengers ahead of him to Esau his brother, to the land of Seir, the country of Edom.
And he charged them, saying, Thus you shall say to my lord, to Esau: Thus says your servant Jacob, I have sojourned with Laban and stayed until now.
I have oxen and donkeys, flocks and menservants and maidservants; and I have sent to tell my lord, that I may find favor in your eyes.
And the messengers returned to Jacob, saying, We came to your brother, to Esau — and he is already coming to meet you, and four hundred men with him.
Then Jacob was greatly afraid, and distress closed in on him; and he divided the people who were with him, and the flocks and the herds and the camels, into two camps.
And he said, If Esau comes to the one camp and strikes it, then the camp that is left will escape.
And Jacob said, God of my father Abraham and God of my father Isaac, YHWH, who said to me, Return to your land and to your kindred, and I will deal well with you:
I am too small for all the kindnesses and all the faithfulness that you have shown your servant; for with my staff alone I crossed this Jordan, and now I have become two camps.
Deliver me, I pray, from the hand of my brother, from the hand of Esau, for I fear him, lest he come and strike me down, mother together with children.
And you yourself said, I will surely deal well with you, and I will make your seed like the sand of the sea, which cannot be counted for its multitude.
And he spent that night there, and from what came to his hand he took a gift for Esau his brother:
two hundred she-goats and twenty he-goats, two hundred ewes and twenty rams,
thirty milking camels with their young, forty cows and ten bulls, twenty she-donkeys and ten he-donkeys.
And he put them into the hands of his servants, drove by drove alone, and said to his servants, Cross over ahead of me, and put a space between drove and drove.
And he charged the first, saying, When Esau my brother meets you and asks you, saying, Whose are you, and where are you going, and whose are these before you?
then you shall say, Your servant Jacob's. It is a gift sent to my lord, to Esau; and behold, he himself is behind us.
And he charged the second also, and the third, and all who followed the droves, saying, In this way you shall speak to Esau when you find him,

and you shall say, Moreover, behold, your servant Jacob is behind us. For he said, Let me cover his face with the gift that goes before me, and afterward I will see his face; perhaps he will lift up my face.
So the gift crossed over ahead of him, and he himself spent that night in the camp.
And he rose that night and took his two wives and his two maidservants and his eleven children, and crossed the ford of the Jabbok.
He took them and sent them across the stream, and sent across all that he had.
And Jacob was left alone; and a man wrestled with him until the rising of the dawn.
And when the man saw that he could not prevail against him, he touched the socket of his hip, and the socket of Jacob's hip was wrenched as he wrestled with him.
And he said, Let me go, for the dawn has risen. But he said, I will not let you go unless you bless me.
And he said to him, What is your name? And he said, Jacob.
And he said, Your name shall no longer be called Jacob, but Israel, for you have striven with God and with men, and you have prevailed.
And Jacob asked and said, Tell me your name, I pray. And he said, Why is it that you ask my name? And he blessed him there.
And Jacob called the name of the place Peniel: For I have seen God face to face, and my life has been delivered.
And the sun rose upon him as he passed Penuel, and he was limping on his hip.
Therefore the sons of Israel do not eat the sinew of the thigh that is on the hip socket, to this day, because he touched the socket of Jacob's hip at the sinew of the thigh.

jdg.1.text: After the death of Joshua, the children of Israel asked YHWH, "Who shall go up first for us against the Canaanites, to fight them?"
And YHWH said, "Judah shall go up. See, I have given the land into his hand."
And Judah said to Simeon his brother, "Come up with me into my allotted land, and let us fight the Canaanites — and I in turn will go with you into yours." So Simeon went with him.
Judah went up, and YHWH gave the Canaanites and the Perizzites into their hand, and they struck down ten thousand men at Bezek.
At Bezek they found Adoni-bezek, and they fought him, and they struck down the Canaanites and the Perizzites.
Adoni-bezek fled, but they chased him and caught him, and cut off his thumbs and his big toes.

And Adoni-bezek said, "Seventy kings with their thumbs and big toes cut off used to gather scraps under my table. As I have done, so God has repaid me." They brought him to Jerusalem, and there he died.
The men of Judah fought against Jerusalem and took it; they put it to the sword and set the city on fire.
Afterward the men of Judah went down to fight the Canaanites living in the hill country, the Negev, and the lowland.
Judah marched against the Canaanites who lived in Hebron — Hebron was formerly called Kiriath-arba — and they struck down Sheshai, Ahiman, and Talmai.
From there they marched against the inhabitants of Debir — Debir was formerly called Kiriath-sepher.
And Caleb said, "Whoever strikes Kiriath-sepher and takes it, to him I will give my daughter Achsah as wife."
Othniel son of Kenaz, Caleb's younger brother, took it; and Caleb gave him his daughter Achsah as wife.
When she came to him, she urged him to ask her father for a field. As she got down from her donkey, Caleb said to her, "What do you want?"
She said to him, "Give me a blessing. Since you have set me in the land of the Negev, give me springs of water as well." So Caleb gave her the upper springs and the lower springs.

The descendants of the Kenite, Moses' father-in-law, went up with the men of Judah from the City of Palms into the wilderness of Judah in the Negev near Arad; and they went and settled among the people.
Then Judah went with Simeon his brother, and they struck the Canaanites living in Zephath and devoted the city to destruction. So the city was named Hormah.
And Judah took Gaza with its territory, Ashkelon with its territory, and Ekron with its territory.
YHWH was with Judah, and he took possession of the hill country; but he could not drive out the people of the valley, for they had chariots of iron.
They gave Hebron to Caleb, as Moses had said, and he drove out from it the three sons of Anak.
But the men of Benjamin did not drive out the Jebusites who lived in Jerusalem; so the Jebusites live with the men of Benjamin in Jerusalem to this day.
The house of Joseph, they too went up — against Bethel — and YHWH was with them.
The house of Joseph sent men to spy out Bethel — the city was formerly called Luz.
The watchers saw a man coming out of the city, and they said to him, "Show us the way into the city, and we will deal kindly with you."
He showed them the way into the city, and they put the city to the sword; but the man and all his family they let go.
The man went to the land of the Hittites, built a city, and named it Luz — that is its name to this day.

Manasseh did not drive out Beth-shean and its villages, or Taanach and its villages, or the inhabitants of Dor and its villages, or the inhabitants of Ibleam and its villages, or the inhabitants of Megiddo and its villages; the Canaanites were determined to stay in that land.
When Israel grew strong, they put the Canaanites to forced labor, but they never drove them out entirely.
Ephraim did not drive out the Canaanites living in Gezer; so the Canaanites lived among them in Gezer.
Zebulun did not drive out the inhabitants of Kitron or the inhabitants of Nahalol; so the Canaanites lived among them and were put to forced labor.
Asher did not drive out the inhabitants of Acco or the inhabitants of Sidon, or Ahlab, Achzib, Helbah, Aphik, or Rehob;
so the Asherites lived among the Canaanites, the people of the land, for they did not drive them out.

Naphtali did not drive out the inhabitants of Beth-shemesh or the inhabitants of Beth-anath, but lived among the Canaanites, the people of the land; and the inhabitants of Beth-shemesh and Beth-anath became forced labor for them.
The Amorites pressed the men of Dan back into the hill country, for they would not let them come down into the valley.
The Amorites were determined to stay in Har-heres, in Aijalon, and in Shaalbim; but the hand of the house of Joseph lay heavy on them, and they were put to forced labor.
The border of the Amorites ran from the Ascent of Akrabbim, from Sela and upward.

jdg.16.text: Samson went to Gaza, and there he saw a prostitute, and he went in to her.

It was told to the Gazites, “Samson has come here.” So they surrounded the place and lay in wait for him all night at the gate of the city. They kept quiet all night, saying, “At the light of morning we will kill him.”

But Samson lay only until midnight, then rose at midnight, took hold of the doors of the city gate and its two posts, and pulled them up, bar and all. He put them on his shoulders and carried them up to the top of the hill that faces Hebron.
After this he fell in love with a woman in the valley of Sorek, whose name was Delilah.

The lords of the Philistines came up to her and said, “Coax him, and find out where his great strength lies, and how we may overpower him, that we may bind him to subdue him. And we will each give you eleven hundred pieces of silver.”
So Delilah said to Samson, “Tell me, please, where your great strength lies, and how you might be bound to subdue you.”
Samson said to her, “If they bind me with seven fresh bowstrings, not yet dried, then I shall become weak and be like any other man.”
So the lords of the Philistines brought up to her seven fresh bowstrings, not yet dried, and she bound him with them.

Now she had men lying in wait in the inner room. And she said to him, “The Philistines are upon you, Samson!” But he snapped the bowstrings as a strand of tow snaps when it touches fire. So his strength was not discovered.
Then Delilah said to Samson, “Look, you have mocked me and told me lies. Now please tell me how you might be bound.”
He said to her, “If they bind me tight with new ropes that have never been used, then I shall become weak and be like any other man.”
So Delilah took new ropes and bound him with them, and said to him, “The Philistines are upon you, Samson!”—the men lying in wait in the inner room—but he snapped them off his arms like thread.

Then Delilah said to Samson, “Until now you have mocked me and told me lies. Tell me how you might be bound.” He said to her, “If you weave the seven locks of my head into the web and fasten it with the pin.”
So she drove it in with the pin and said to him, “The Philistines are upon you, Samson!” But he woke from his sleep and pulled out the pin, the loom, and the web.
Then she said to him, “How can you say, ‘I love you,’ when your heart is not with me? This is the third time you have mocked me and not told me where your great strength lies.”
And because she pressed him daily with her words and nagged him until his soul was vexed to death,

he told her his whole heart and said to her, “No razor has ever touched my head, for I have been a Nazirite to God from my mother’s womb. If I am shaved, my strength will leave me, and I shall become weak and be like any other man.”

When Delilah saw that he had told her his whole heart, she sent and called the lords of the Philistines, saying, “Come up this once, for he has told me his whole heart.” So the lords of the Philistines came up to her, bringing the silver in their hands.
She made him sleep on her knees, and she called a man and had him shave off the seven locks of his head. Then she began to subdue him, and his strength left him.

Then she said, “The Philistines are upon you, Samson!” And he woke from his sleep and said, “I will go out as before, at other times, and shake myself free.” But he did not know that YHWH had left him.
So the Philistines seized him and gouged out his eyes, and brought him down to Gaza and bound him with bronze shackles, and he ground grain in the prison house.
But the hair of his head began to grow again after it had been shaved.
Now the lords of the Philistines gathered to offer a great sacrifice to Dagon their god, and to rejoice, and they said, “Our god has given Samson our enemy into our hand.”
When the people saw him, they praised their god, for they said, “Our god has given into our hand our enemy, the ravager of our land, who multiplied our slain.”
And when their hearts were merry, they said, “Call for Samson, that he may entertain us.” So they called Samson from the prison house, and he entertained them, and they stood him between the pillars.
Samson said to the young man who held his hand, “Let me go, and let me feel the pillars on which the house stands, that I may lean against them.”
Now the house was full of men and women, and all the lords of the Philistines were there, and on the roof were about three thousand men and women watching Samson entertain them.
Then Samson called to YHWH and said, “Lord YHWH, remember me, I pray, and strengthen me, I pray, only this once, God, that I may take one act of vengeance for my two eyes upon the Philistines.”
Samson took hold of the two middle pillars on which the house stood and leaned on them, one with his right hand and one with his left.

And Samson said, “Let me die with the Philistines.” Then he pushed with all his might, and the house fell upon the lords and upon all the people who were in it. So the dead whom he killed in his death were more than those he had killed in his life.

Then his brothers and all his father’s house came down and took him up, and brought him up and buried him between Zorah and Eshtaol, in the tomb of Manoah his father. He had judged Israel twenty years.

jdg.2.text: The angel of YHWH went up from Gilgal to Bochim, and said, "I brought you up out of Egypt and led you into the land I swore to your fathers. I said, 'I will never break my covenant with you,
and you shall make no covenant with the inhabitants of this land; you shall tear down their altars.' But you have not listened to my voice. What is this you have done?
So now I say: I will not drive them out before you. They will be thorns in your sides, and their gods will be a snare to you."
When the angel of YHWH had spoken these words to all the children of Israel, the people lifted up their voice and wept.
So they named that place Bochim, and there they sacrificed to YHWH.
When Joshua had dismissed the people, the children of Israel went each to his own inheritance, to take possession of the land.
And the people served YHWH all the days of Joshua, and all the days of the elders who outlived Joshua, who had seen all the great work YHWH had done for Israel.
And Joshua son of Nun, the servant of YHWH, died at the age of a hundred and ten years.
They buried him within the bounds of his inheritance at Timnath-heres, in the hill country of Ephraim, north of Mount Gaash.
And all that generation too was gathered to their fathers; and another generation rose up after them who did not know YHWH, nor the work he had done for Israel.
Then the children of Israel did what was evil in the eyes of YHWH, and they served the Baals.

They abandoned YHWH, the God of their fathers, who had brought them out of the land of Egypt, and went after other gods, the gods of the peoples around them, and bowed down to them; and they provoked YHWH to anger.
They abandoned YHWH and served Baal and the Ashtaroth.

And the anger of YHWH burned against Israel. He gave them into the hand of raiders who plundered them; he sold them into the hand of their enemies all around, and they could no longer stand before their enemies.
Wherever they marched out, the hand of YHWH was against them for harm, as YHWH had said and as YHWH had sworn to them; and they were in bitter distress.
Then YHWH raised up judges, who saved them from the hand of their plunderers.

Yet even to their judges they would not listen, for they went whoring after other gods and bowed down to them. They turned quickly from the way their fathers had walked in obeying the commandments of YHWH; they did not do as their fathers had done.

Whenever YHWH raised up a judge for them, YHWH was with the judge, and he saved them from the hand of their enemies all the days of the judge; for YHWH was moved to pity by their groaning under those who oppressed and crushed them.

But when the judge died, they would turn back and act more corruptly than their fathers, going after other gods to serve them and bow down to them. They dropped none of their practices, none of their stubborn ways.
So the anger of YHWH burned against Israel, and he said, "Because this nation has broken my covenant which I commanded their fathers, and has not listened to my voice,
I for my part will no longer drive out before them any of the nations that Joshua left when he died —
in order to test Israel by them, whether they will keep the way of YHWH and walk in it as their fathers kept it, or not."
So YHWH left those nations, not driving them out at once; and he had not given them into the hand of Joshua.

jdg.3.text: These are the nations YHWH left, to test by them all in Israel who had not known any of the wars of Canaan —
only so that the generations of the children of Israel might know war, to teach it to those who had not known it before:
the five lords of the Philistines, all the Canaanites, the Sidonians, and the Hivites who lived on Mount Lebanon, from Mount Baal-hermon to Lebo-hamath.
They were there to test Israel by them, to know whether Israel would obey the commandments of YHWH, which he had commanded their fathers by the hand of Moses.
So the children of Israel lived among the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Amorites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites.
They took their daughters as wives for themselves, and gave their own daughters to their sons, and served their gods.
The children of Israel did what was evil in the eyes of YHWH; they forgot YHWH their God and served the Baals and the Asherahs.
And the anger of YHWH burned against Israel, and he sold them into the hand of Cushan-rishathaim king of Aram-naharaim; and the children of Israel served Cushan-rishathaim eight years.
But when the children of Israel cried out to YHWH, YHWH raised up a deliverer for the children of Israel who saved them: Othniel son of Kenaz, Caleb's younger brother.
The Spirit of YHWH came upon him, and he judged Israel. He went out to war, and YHWH gave Cushan-rishathaim king of Aram into his hand, and his hand prevailed over Cushan-rishathaim.
And the land had rest forty years. Then Othniel son of Kenaz died.
Again the children of Israel did what was evil in the eyes of YHWH; and YHWH strengthened Eglon king of Moab against Israel, because they had done what was evil in the eyes of YHWH.
He gathered to himself the Ammonites and the Amalekites, and went and struck Israel, and they took possession of the City of Palms.
And the children of Israel served Eglon king of Moab eighteen years.

But when the children of Israel cried out to YHWH, YHWH raised up a deliverer for them: Ehud son of Gera, a Benjaminite, a left-handed man. The children of Israel sent tribute by his hand to Eglon king of Moab.
Ehud made himself a sword with two edges, a cubit long, and strapped it under his clothes on his right thigh.
He presented the tribute to Eglon king of Moab — and Eglon was a very fat man.
When he had finished presenting the tribute, he sent away the people who had carried it.
But he himself turned back at the carved stones near Gilgal, and said, "I have a secret word for you, O king." The king said, "Silence!" — and all his attendants went out from him.
Ehud came to him as he sat alone in his cool upper room, and Ehud said, "I have a word from God for you." And as he rose from his seat,
Ehud reached with his left hand, took the sword from his right thigh, and drove it into his belly.
Even the hilt went in after the blade, and the fat closed over the blade, for he did not draw the sword out of his belly; and the filth came out.
Then Ehud went out to the porch, shut the doors of the upper room behind him, and locked them.
After he had gone, the servants came; they looked, and there were the doors of the upper room, locked. They said, "He must be relieving himself in the cool inner chamber."
They waited until they were ashamed to wait longer — and still he did not open the doors of the upper room. So they took the key and opened them, and there lay their lord, fallen to the floor, dead.
While they lingered, Ehud escaped; he passed the carved stones and got away to Seirah.
When he arrived, he blew the trumpet in the hill country of Ephraim, and the children of Israel came down with him from the hills, and he at their head.
He said to them, "Follow me, for YHWH has given your enemies, Moab, into your hand." So they came down after him, seized the fords of the Jordan against Moab, and let no one cross.
At that time they struck down about ten thousand Moabites, all of them stout and able men; not a man escaped.
So Moab was subdued that day under the hand of Israel. And the land had rest eighty years.
After him came Shamgar son of Anath, who struck down six hundred Philistines with an ox-goad; and he too saved Israel.

jol.1.text: The word of YHWH that came to Joel son of Pethuel.
Hear this, you elders; give ear, all who live in the land. Has anything like this happened in your days, or in the days of your fathers?
Tell it to your children, and let your children tell their children, and their children the generation after.
What the cutter left, the swarmer ate; what the swarmer left, the hopper ate; and what the hopper left, the stripper ate.
Wake up, you drunkards, and weep; wail, all you drinkers of wine, for the sweet wine — it is cut off from your mouth.
For a nation has come up against my land, mighty and beyond counting; its teeth are the teeth of a lion, and it has the fangs of a lioness.
It has made my vine a waste and splintered my fig tree; it stripped it bare and flung it down; its branches are left white.
Mourn like a virgin girded in sackcloth for the husband of her youth.
Grain offering and drink offering are cut off from the house of YHWH; the priests mourn, the ministers of YHWH.
The field is ravaged, the ground is in mourning; for the grain is ravaged, the new wine has dried up, the fresh oil fails.
Stand ashamed, you farmers; wail, you vinedressers, over the wheat and the barley, for the harvest of the field is lost.
The vine has withered and the fig tree droops; pomegranate, palm, and apple — every tree of the field is dried up. Yes, joy itself has withered away from the children of men.

Gird yourselves and lament, you priests; wail, you ministers of the altar. Come, spend the night in sackcloth, you ministers of my God, for grain offering and drink offering are withheld from the house of your God.
Consecrate a fast, call a solemn assembly; gather the elders and all who live in the land to the house of YHWH your God, and cry out to YHWH.
Alas for the day! For the day of YHWH is near, and it comes like devastation from the Almighty.
Is not the food cut off before our very eyes — joy and gladness from the house of our God?
The seed shrivels under the clods, the storehouses stand desolate, the granaries are broken down, for the grain has dried up.
How the beasts groan! The herds of cattle wander bewildered, for there is no pasture for them; even the flocks of sheep bear the guilt.
To you, YHWH, I cry, for fire has devoured the pastures of the wilderness, and flame has scorched every tree of the field.
Even the beasts of the field pant for you, for the channels of water have dried up, and fire has devoured the pastures of the wilderness.

jud.1.text: Jude, a servant of Jesus Christ and brother of James, to those who are called — loved in God the Father, and kept for Jesus Christ:
Mercy to you, and peace, and love, in fullest measure.
Beloved, though I was making every effort to write to you about the salvation we share, I found I had to write and urge you to contend for the faith delivered once for all to the holy ones.
For certain men have slipped in unseen — men marked out long ago for this judgment — godless men, who twist the grace of our God into license, and deny our only Master and Lord, Jesus Christ.
Now I want to remind you, though you know it all once for all, that Jesus, having saved a people out of the land of Egypt, the second time destroyed those who did not believe;
and the angels who did not keep their own dominion, but abandoned their proper dwelling, he has kept in everlasting chains under gloom for the judgment of the great day;

as Sodom and Gomorrah and the cities around them, which in the same way as these gave themselves to fornication and went after strange flesh, are set forth as a warning, undergoing the penalty of eternal fire.
Yet in the same way these dreamers also defile the flesh, set lordship at nothing, and rail against the glorious ones.
But Michael the archangel, when he contended with the devil and disputed over the body of Moses, did not dare to bring a railing judgment against him, but said, "May the Lord rebuke you."
But these men rail against whatever they do not understand; and whatever they know by instinct, like beasts without reason — by these very things they are destroyed.
Woe to them! For they have walked the road of Cain; for wages they have poured themselves out into the error of Balaam; and in the rebellion of Korah they have perished.

These are the hidden reefs at your feasts of love, feasting beside you without fear, shepherds who feed only themselves — clouds without water, driven past by the winds; trees of late autumn without fruit, twice dead, torn up by the roots;
wild waves of the sea, foaming up their own shame; wandering stars, for whom the gloom of darkness has been kept forever.
It was of these too that Enoch, seventh from Adam, prophesied, saying: "Behold, the Lord came with his holy ten thousands,
to execute judgment upon all, and to convict all the ungodly of all their ungodly deeds done in ungodliness, and of all the hard words that ungodly sinners have spoken against him."
These are grumblers and fault-finders, walking after their own desires; their mouths speak swollen words, and they flatter faces for the sake of gain.
But you, beloved, remember the words spoken beforehand by the apostles of our Lord Jesus Christ,
how they told you: "In the last time there will be mockers, walking after their own ungodly desires."
These are the ones who draw lines of division — men of mere nature, who do not have the Spirit.
But you, beloved: build yourselves up on your most holy faith; pray in the Holy Spirit;
keep yourselves in the love of God, awaiting the mercy of our Lord Jesus Christ unto eternal life.
And on those who waver, have mercy;
others save, snatching them out of the fire; and on others have mercy with fear, hating even the tunic stained by the flesh.
Now to him who is able to keep you from stumbling, and to set you blameless before his glory with great rejoicing —
to the only God our Savior, through Jesus Christ our Lord, be glory, majesty, dominion, and authority, before all the ages, and now, and unto all the ages. Amen.

lev.1.text: And he called to Moses, and YHWH spoke to him from the tent of meeting, saying:
"Speak to the children of Israel and say to them: When any one of you brings an offering to YHWH, from the beasts — from the herd or from the flock — you shall bring your offering.
If his offering is a burnt offering from the herd, he shall bring a male without blemish; he shall bring it to the door of the tent of meeting, that it may be accepted for him before YHWH.
And he shall lay his hand upon the head of the burnt offering, and it shall be accepted for him, to make atonement for him.
And he shall slaughter the young bull before YHWH; and the sons of Aaron, the priests, shall bring the blood, and dash the blood against the altar on every side, at the door of the tent of meeting.
And he shall flay the burnt offering, and cut it into its pieces.
And the sons of Aaron the priest shall put fire on the altar, and lay wood in order upon the fire.
And the sons of Aaron, the priests, shall lay the pieces, the head and the fat, in order upon the wood that is on the fire upon the altar.
But its entrails and its legs he shall wash with water; and the priest shall burn the whole of it on the altar — a burnt offering, an offering by fire, a soothing fragrance to YHWH.
And if his offering is from the flock, from the sheep or from the goats, for a burnt offering, he shall bring a male without blemish.
And he shall slaughter it on the north side of the altar before YHWH; and the sons of Aaron, the priests, shall dash its blood against the altar on every side.
And he shall cut it into its pieces, with its head and its fat; and the priest shall lay them in order upon the wood that is on the fire upon the altar.
But the entrails and the legs he shall wash with water; and the priest shall bring the whole and burn it on the altar. It is a burnt offering, an offering by fire, a soothing fragrance to YHWH.
And if his offering to YHWH is a burnt offering of birds, he shall bring his offering from the turtledoves or from the young pigeons.
And the priest shall bring it to the altar, and wring off its head, and burn it on the altar; and its blood shall be drained out against the side of the altar.
And he shall take away its crop with its feathers, and cast it beside the altar on the east side, into the place of the ashes.

And he shall tear it open by its wings, but shall not sever it; and the priest shall burn it on the altar, upon the wood that is on the fire. It is a burnt offering, an offering by fire, a soothing fragrance to YHWH.

lev.2.text: And when a soul brings a grain offering to YHWH, his offering shall be of fine flour; and he shall pour oil upon it, and lay frankincense upon it.

And he shall bring it to the sons of Aaron, the priests; and the priest shall take from it his handful of the fine flour and of the oil, with all its frankincense, and burn it as its memorial portion on the altar — an offering by fire, a soothing fragrance to YHWH.
And what remains of the grain offering shall be for Aaron and his sons: a most holy portion of the offerings by fire to YHWH.
And when you bring a grain offering baked in the oven, it shall be of fine flour — unleavened cakes mingled with oil, or unleavened wafers anointed with oil.
And if your offering is a grain offering from the griddle, it shall be of fine flour mingled with oil, unleavened.
Break it in pieces, and pour oil upon it: it is a grain offering.
And if your offering is a grain offering from the pan, it shall be made of fine flour with oil.
And you shall bring the grain offering that is made of these things to YHWH; and it shall be presented to the priest, and he shall bring it to the altar.
And the priest shall lift from the grain offering its memorial portion, and burn it on the altar — an offering by fire, a soothing fragrance to YHWH.
And what remains of the grain offering shall be for Aaron and his sons: a most holy portion of the offerings by fire to YHWH.
No grain offering that you bring to YHWH shall be made with leaven; for no leaven and no honey shall you burn as an offering by fire to YHWH.
As an offering of firstfruits you may bring them to YHWH, but they shall not rise upon the altar as a soothing fragrance.

And every offering of your grain offering you shall season with salt; you shall not let the salt of the covenant of your God be lacking from your grain offering. With all your offerings you shall offer salt.
And if you bring a grain offering of firstfruits to YHWH, you shall bring for the grain offering of your firstfruits fresh ears roasted in the fire, crushed grain of the new harvest.
And you shall put oil upon it, and lay frankincense upon it: it is a grain offering.
And the priest shall burn its memorial portion — some of its crushed grain and of its oil, with all its frankincense — an offering by fire to YHWH.

lev.3.text: And if his offering is a sacrifice of peace offerings: if he brings it from the herd, whether male or female, he shall bring it without blemish before YHWH.
And he shall lay his hand upon the head of his offering, and slaughter it at the door of the tent of meeting; and the sons of Aaron, the priests, shall dash the blood against the altar on every side.
And from the sacrifice of peace offerings he shall bring, as an offering by fire to YHWH, the fat that covers the entrails, and all the fat that is upon the entrails,
and the two kidneys with the fat that is on them by the loins, and the lobe of the liver, which he shall remove with the kidneys.
And the sons of Aaron shall burn it on the altar, upon the burnt offering that is on the wood upon the fire — an offering by fire, a soothing fragrance to YHWH.
And if his offering for a sacrifice of peace offerings to YHWH is from the flock, male or female, he shall bring it without blemish.
If he brings a lamb for his offering, he shall bring it before YHWH,
and he shall lay his hand upon the head of his offering, and slaughter it before the tent of meeting; and the sons of Aaron shall dash its blood against the altar on every side.

And from the sacrifice of peace offerings he shall bring, as an offering by fire to YHWH, its fat: the whole fat tail, which he shall remove close by the backbone, and the fat that covers the entrails, and all the fat that is upon the entrails,
and the two kidneys with the fat that is on them by the loins, and the lobe of the liver, which he shall remove with the kidneys.
And the priest shall burn it on the altar: it is food, an offering by fire to YHWH.
And if his offering is a goat, he shall bring it before YHWH,
and he shall lay his hand upon its head, and slaughter it before the tent of meeting; and the sons of Aaron shall dash its blood against the altar on every side.
And from it he shall bring his offering, an offering by fire to YHWH: the fat that covers the entrails, and all the fat that is upon the entrails,
and the two kidneys with the fat that is on them by the loins, and the lobe of the liver, which he shall remove with the kidneys.
And the priest shall burn them on the altar: it is food, an offering by fire, for a soothing fragrance. All the fat belongs to YHWH.
It is an everlasting statute through your generations, in all your dwellings: you shall eat neither fat nor blood."

lev.4.text: And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying:
"Speak to the children of Israel, saying: When a soul sins unwittingly against any of the commandments of YHWH — things that must not be done — and does any one of them:
if the anointed priest sins, bringing guilt upon the people, then for the sin he has sinned he shall bring a young bull without blemish to YHWH as a sin offering.
And he shall bring the bull to the door of the tent of meeting before YHWH, and lay his hand upon the head of the bull, and slaughter the bull before YHWH.
And the anointed priest shall take some of the bull's blood, and bring it into the tent of meeting.
And the priest shall dip his finger in the blood, and sprinkle some of the blood seven times before YHWH, before the veil of the sanctuary.

And the priest shall put some of the blood on the horns of the altar of fragrant incense before YHWH, which is in the tent of meeting; and all the rest of the bull's blood he shall pour out at the base of the altar of burnt offering, which is at the door of the tent of meeting.
And all the fat of the bull of the sin offering he shall lift from it: the fat that covers the entrails, and all the fat that is upon the entrails,
and the two kidneys with the fat that is on them by the loins, and the lobe of the liver, which he shall remove with the kidneys —
just as it is lifted from the ox of the sacrifice of peace offerings; and the priest shall burn them on the altar of burnt offering.
But the hide of the bull and all its flesh, with its head and its legs, its entrails and its dung —
the whole bull he shall carry outside the camp to a clean place, where the ashes are poured out, and burn it on wood with fire; where the ashes are poured out it shall be burned.

And if the whole congregation of Israel goes astray, and the matter is hidden from the eyes of the assembly, and they do any one of the things that YHWH has commanded not to be done, and become guilty —
when the sin they have sinned becomes known, the assembly shall bring a young bull for a sin offering, and bring it before the tent of meeting.
And the elders of the congregation shall lay their hands upon the head of the bull before YHWH; and the bull shall be slaughtered before YHWH.
And the anointed priest shall bring some of the bull's blood into the tent of meeting.
And the priest shall dip his finger in the blood, and sprinkle it seven times before YHWH, before the veil.

And some of the blood he shall put on the horns of the altar that is before YHWH, in the tent of meeting; and all the rest of the blood he shall pour out at the base of the altar of burnt offering, which is at the door of the tent of meeting.
And all its fat he shall lift from it, and burn it on the altar.
And he shall do with the bull just as he did with the bull of the sin offering; so shall he do with it. And the priest shall make atonement for them, and they shall be forgiven.
And he shall carry the bull outside the camp and burn it, as he burned the first bull. It is the sin offering of the assembly.
When a ruler sins, and does unwittingly any one of the things that YHWH his God has commanded not to be done, and becomes guilty —
or if the sin he has sinned is made known to him — he shall bring as his offering a male goat without blemish.
And he shall lay his hand upon the head of the goat, and slaughter it in the place where the burnt offering is slaughtered before YHWH. It is a sin offering.

And the priest shall take some of the blood of the sin offering with his finger, and put it on the horns of the altar of burnt offering; and its blood he shall pour out at the base of the altar of burnt offering.
And all its fat he shall burn on the altar, like the fat of the sacrifice of peace offerings. And the priest shall make atonement for him for his sin, and he shall be forgiven.
And if any soul of the people of the land sins unwittingly, doing any one of the things that YHWH has commanded not to be done, and becomes guilty —
or if the sin he has sinned is made known to him — he shall bring as his offering a female goat without blemish, for the sin he has sinned.
And he shall lay his hand upon the head of the sin offering, and slaughter the sin offering in the place of the burnt offering.
And the priest shall take some of its blood with his finger, and put it on the horns of the altar of burnt offering; and all the rest of its blood he shall pour out at the base of the altar.

And all its fat he shall remove, as the fat is removed from the sacrifice of peace offerings; and the priest shall burn it on the altar, a soothing fragrance to YHWH. And the priest shall make atonement for him, and he shall be forgiven.
And if he brings a lamb as his offering for a sin offering, he shall bring a female without blemish.
And he shall lay his hand upon the head of the sin offering, and slaughter it as a sin offering in the place where the burnt offering is slaughtered.

And the priest shall take some of the blood of the sin offering with his finger, and put it on the horns of the altar of burnt offering; and all the rest of its blood he shall pour out at the base of the altar.

And all its fat he shall remove, as the fat of the lamb is removed from the sacrifice of peace offerings; and the priest shall burn them on the altar, upon the offerings by fire to YHWH. And the priest shall make atonement for him, for the sin he has sinned, and he shall be forgiven.

lev.5.text: And when a soul sins in that he hears the voice of a spoken curse, and he is a witness — whether he has seen or has known — if he does not tell it, then he shall bear his iniquity.

Or a soul who touches any unclean thing — the carcass of an unclean wild beast, or the carcass of unclean cattle, or the carcass of an unclean creeping thing — and it is hidden from him, and he is unclean, and guilty;
or when he touches human uncleanness, any uncleanness by which one is made unclean, and it is hidden from him, and then he knows it, and is guilty;

or when a soul swears, speaking rashly with the lips, to do evil or to do good — whatever a man may utter rashly with an oath — and it is hidden from him, and then he knows it, and is guilty in any of these:
then it shall be, when he is guilty in any of these things, that he shall confess the sin he has committed.
And he shall bring to YHWH, as his penalty for the sin he has sinned, a female from the flock — a lamb or a she-goat — as a sin offering; and the priest shall make atonement for him for his sin.

And if his hand cannot reach enough for a lamb, he shall bring to YHWH, as his penalty for the sin he has sinned, two turtledoves or two young pigeons — one for a sin offering and one for a burnt offering.
And he shall bring them to the priest, who shall offer first the one for the sin offering: he shall wring its head from its neck, but shall not sever it.
And he shall sprinkle some of the blood of the sin offering against the side of the altar, and the rest of the blood shall be drained out at the base of the altar. It is a sin offering.
And the second he shall offer as a burnt offering, according to the ordinance. And the priest shall make atonement for him, for the sin he has sinned, and he shall be forgiven.

And if his hand cannot reach two turtledoves or two young pigeons, then he shall bring as his offering for the sin he has sinned a tenth of an ephah of fine flour for a sin offering. He shall put no oil upon it, and lay no frankincense upon it, for it is a sin offering.
And he shall bring it to the priest, and the priest shall take his handful of it as its memorial portion, and burn it on the altar, upon the offerings by fire to YHWH. It is a sin offering.
And the priest shall make atonement for him, for the sin he has sinned in any of these things, and he shall be forgiven. And it shall belong to the priest, like the grain offering."
And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying:

"When a soul commits a trespass and sins unwittingly against the holy things of YHWH, he shall bring to YHWH as his penalty a ram without blemish from the flock, valued in silver shekels by the shekel of the sanctuary, for a guilt offering.

And he shall make restitution for what he has taken in sin from the holy thing, and add a fifth to it, and give it to the priest. And the priest shall make atonement for him with the ram of the guilt offering, and he shall be forgiven.
And if a soul sins, doing any one of the things that YHWH has commanded not to be done, though he did not know it, still he is guilty, and shall bear his iniquity.

And he shall bring to the priest a ram without blemish from the flock, at your valuation, for a guilt offering. And the priest shall make atonement for him for the error he committed unknowing, and he shall be forgiven.
It is a guilt offering; he is guilty indeed before YHWH."
And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying:
"When a soul sins and commits a trespass against YHWH — deceiving his neighbor in a matter of deposit or pledge, or by robbery, or by oppressing his neighbor,
or he has found what was lost and lied about it, and sworn falsely — in any of all these things a man may do and sin thereby:
then it shall be, when he has sinned and is guilty, that he shall restore what he took by robbery, or what he gained by oppression, or the deposit entrusted to him, or the lost thing he found,
or anything about which he swore falsely: he shall repay it in full, and add a fifth to it. To him to whom it belongs he shall give it, on the day of his guilt offering.
And he shall bring to YHWH his penalty: a ram without blemish from the flock, at your valuation, for a guilt offering, to the priest.
And the priest shall make atonement for him before YHWH, and he shall be forgiven for any one of all the things a man may do and incur guilt thereby."

mat.1.text: The book of the genealogy of Jesus the Messiah, son of David, son of Abraham.
Abraham was the father of Isaac, and Isaac the father of Jacob, and Jacob the father of Judah and his brothers,
and Judah the father of Perez and Zerah by Tamar, and Perez the father of Hezron, and Hezron the father of Aram,
and Aram the father of Amminadab, and Amminadab the father of Nahshon, and Nahshon the father of Salmon,
and Salmon the father of Boaz by Rahab, and Boaz the father of Obed by Ruth, and Obed the father of Jesse,
and Jesse the father of David the king. And David was the father of Solomon by the wife of Uriah,
and Solomon the father of Rehoboam, and Rehoboam the father of Abijah, and Abijah the father of Asaph,
and Asaph the father of Jehoshaphat, and Jehoshaphat the father of Joram, and Joram the father of Uzziah,
and Uzziah the father of Jotham, and Jotham the father of Ahaz, and Ahaz the father of Hezekiah,
and Hezekiah the father of Manasseh, and Manasseh the father of Amos, and Amos the father of Josiah,
and Josiah the father of Jechoniah and his brothers, at the time of the exile to Babylon.
And after the exile to Babylon, Jechoniah was the father of Shealtiel, and Shealtiel the father of Zerubbabel,
and Zerubbabel the father of Abiud, and Abiud the father of Eliakim, and Eliakim the father of Azor,
and Azor the father of Zadok, and Zadok the father of Achim, and Achim the father of Eliud,
and Eliud the father of Eleazar, and Eleazar the father of Matthan, and Matthan the father of Jacob,
and Jacob the father of Joseph, the husband of Mary, of whom was born Jesus, who is called the Messiah.

So all the generations from Abraham to David were fourteen generations; and from David to the exile to Babylon, fourteen generations; and from the exile to Babylon to the Messiah, fourteen generations.
Now the birth of Jesus the Messiah came about in this way. When his mother Mary had been betrothed to Joseph, before they came together she was found to be with child by the Holy Spirit.
And Joseph her husband, being a just man and unwilling to expose her to disgrace, resolved to divorce her quietly.

But as he pondered these things, behold, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream, saying, "Joseph, son of David, do not be afraid to take Mary as your wife, for what has been conceived in her is of the Holy Spirit.
She will bear a son, and you shall call his name Jesus, for he will save his people from their sins."
All this took place to fulfill what the Lord had spoken through the prophet, saying,
"Behold, the virgin shall conceive and bear a son, and they shall call his name Emmanuel" — which means, God with us.
And Joseph rose from sleep and did as the angel of the Lord had commanded him: he took his wife,
but he did not know her until she had borne a son. And he called his name Jesus.

mat.2.text: Now when Jesus was born in Bethlehem of Judea, in the days of Herod the king, behold, wise men from the east came to Jerusalem,
