All who are with me greet you. Greet those who love us in the faith. Grace be with you all.

zep.1.text: The word of YHWH that came to Zephaniah son of Cushi, son of Gedaliah, son of Amariah, son of Hezekiah, in the days of Josiah son of Amon, king of Judah.
"I will sweep away everything from the face of the earth," declares YHWH.
"I will sweep away man and beast; I will sweep away the birds of the sky and the fish of the sea, and the ruins with the wicked; and I will cut off mankind from the face of the earth," declares YHWH.

"And I will stretch out my hand against Judah and against all who dwell in Jerusalem, and I will cut off from this place the last remnant of Baal, the very name of the idol-priests along with the priests,
and those who bow down on the rooftops to the host of heaven, and those who bow down and swear to YHWH yet swear by Milcom,
and those who have turned back from following YHWH, who have not sought YHWH and have not inquired of him."
Hush before the Lord YHWH! For the day of YHWH is near; YHWH has prepared a sacrifice, he has consecrated his guests.
"And on the day of YHWH's sacrifice I will call to account the officials and the sons of the king, and all who dress themselves in foreign clothing.
On that day I will call to account everyone who leaps over the threshold, who fill their master's house with violence and deceit."
"And on that day," declares YHWH, "a cry will rise from the Fish Gate, a wail from the Second Quarter, a great crashing from the hills.
Wail, you who dwell in the Mortar, for all the merchant people are silenced; all who weigh out silver are cut off.
And at that time I will search Jerusalem with lamps, and I will call to account the men who sit thickening on their dregs, who say in their hearts, 'YHWH will do no good, and he will do no harm.'
Their wealth will be plunder and their houses a desolation. They will build houses and not live in them; they will plant vineyards and not drink their wine."
The great day of YHWH is near — near, and rushing fast. Listen: the day of YHWH! Bitter — there the warrior cries aloud.
That day is a day of wrath, a day of anguish and distress, a day of ruin and devastation, a day of darkness and gloom, a day of cloud and thick blackness,
a day of trumpet and battle cry against the fortified cities and against the high corner towers.
"And I will bring such distress on mankind that they will walk like the blind, because they have sinned against YHWH. Their blood will be poured out like dust, and their flesh like dung."

Neither their silver nor their gold will be able to save them on the day of YHWH's wrath; in the fire of his jealousy the whole earth will be consumed, for a full and sudden end he will make of all who dwell on the earth.

nam.2.text: Look — on the mountains, the feet of one bringing good news, proclaiming peace! Keep your feasts, O Judah; fulfill your vows. For never again shall the worthless one pass through you; he is cut off entirely.
A scatterer has come up against your face. Guard the fortress! Watch the road! Brace your loins; gather all your strength!
For YHWH is restoring the pride of Jacob, like the pride of Israel — though plunderers plundered them and ruined their vine-branches.
The shields of his warriors are dyed red, the men of war clothed in scarlet. The chariots flash with steel on the day he readies them, and the cypress spears are brandished.
Through the streets the chariots rage; they rush to and fro in the squares. They look like torches; like lightning they dart.
He summons his mighty ones — they stumble as they go; they hurry to her wall, and the siege-cover is set in place.
The gates of the rivers are thrown open, and the palace melts away.
It is decreed: she is stripped, she is carried off. Her handmaids moan like the voice of doves, beating upon their breasts.
And Nineveh — like a pool whose waters are draining away. "Stand! Stand!" — but no one turns back.
Plunder the silver! Plunder the gold! There is no end to the treasure — a weight of every precious thing.
Emptiness, desolation, devastation! Hearts melting, knees buckling, anguish in every loin, and every face drained of color.
Where now is the den of lions, the feeding-ground of the young lions — where the lion walked, the lioness, the lion's cub, with none to make them afraid?
The lion tore enough for his cubs and strangled prey for his lionesses; he filled his caves with the kill, his dens with torn flesh.

Behold, I am against you — the word of YHWH of hosts. I will burn your chariots to smoke, and the sword shall devour your young lions. I will cut off your prey from the earth, and the voice of your messengers shall be heard no more.

nam.3.text: Woe to the city of blood — all of her a lie, full of plunder, and the prey never departs!
The crack of the whip, the rumble of the wheel, the horse at the gallop, the chariot bounding —
the horseman charging, the flash of the sword, the lightning of the spear! A multitude of slain, a mass of corpses, dead bodies without end — they stumble over the dead.
All for the countless harlotries of the harlot, lovely with grace, mistress of sorceries, who sold nations by her harlotries and clans by her spells.
Behold, I am against you — the word of YHWH of hosts. I will lift your skirts over your face; I will show the nations your nakedness, and the kingdoms your shame.
I will hurl filth upon you and treat you with contempt, and make you a spectacle.
And all who see you will shrink from you and say, "Nineveh is laid waste — who will grieve for her?" Where shall I seek comforters for you?
Are you better than No-Amon, throned among the rivers, waters all around her — whose rampart was the sea, whose wall was of the waters?
Cush was her strength, and Egypt, without limit; Put and the Libyans stood at her aid.
Yet even she went into exile, into captivity. Even her infants were dashed to pieces at the head of every street; for her nobles they cast lots, and all her great ones were bound in chains.
You too shall be made drunk; you shall go into hiding. You too shall seek a refuge from the enemy.
All your fortresses are fig trees with first-ripe figs — shake them, and they fall into the eater's mouth.
Look — your troops are women in your midst. The gates of your land stand wide open to your enemies; fire has devoured your bars.
Draw water for the siege! Strengthen your fortresses! Go down into the clay and tread the mortar; take hold of the brick-mold!
There the fire will devour you, the sword will cut you down — it will devour you like the locust. Multiply like the locust! Multiply like the swarming locust!
You multiplied your merchants beyond the stars of the heavens — the locust sheds its skin and flies away.
Your guards are like the locust, your officials like swarms of swarming locusts that camp on the walls on a cold day: the sun rises, and they flee, and no one knows where they have gone.
Your shepherds slumber, O king of Assyria; your nobles lie down to rest. Your people are scattered on the mountains, and there is no one to gather them.
There is no easing your wound; your blow is fatal. All who hear the news of you clap their hands over you — for upon whom has your evil not passed, without ceasing?

zep.2.text: Gather yourselves — gather, O nation without shame,
before the decree gives birth, before the day passes like chaff, before the burning anger of YHWH comes upon you, before the day of YHWH's anger comes upon you.
Seek YHWH, all you humble of the land who have done his justice. Seek righteousness; seek humility. Perhaps you will be hidden on the day of YHWH's anger.
For Gaza shall be forsaken, and Ashkelon a desolation; Ashdod they shall drive out at noonday, and Ekron shall be uprooted.
Woe to the dwellers of the seacoast, nation of the Cherethites! The word of YHWH is against you, Canaan, land of the Philistines: I will destroy you until no inhabitant is left.
And the seacoast shall become pastures, meadows for shepherds and folds for flocks.

And the coast shall belong to the remnant of the house of Judah; upon it they shall graze. In the houses of Ashkelon they shall lie down at evening, for YHWH their God will visit them and restore their fortunes.
I have heard the taunting of Moab and the revilings of the sons of Ammon — how they taunted my people and made great boasts against their border.

Therefore, as I live — the word of YHWH of hosts, the God of Israel — Moab shall become like Sodom, and the sons of Ammon like Gomorrah: a possession of nettles, a pit of salt, a desolation forever. The remnant of my people shall plunder them; the survivors of my nation shall inherit them.
This shall they have in exchange for their pride, because they taunted and made great boasts against the people of YHWH of hosts.
Fearsome shall YHWH be against them, for he will make lean all the gods of the earth; and every man shall bow down to him from his own place — all the coastlands of the nations.
You also, O Cushites — slain by my sword are they.
And he shall stretch out his hand against the north and destroy Assyria, and make Nineveh a desolation, dry as the desert.

And herds shall lie down in her midst, every beast of the nations; even the desert owl and the porcupine shall lodge on her capitals. A voice shall sing in the window; rubble on the threshold — for the cedar-work is laid bare.

This is the exultant city that sat secure, that said in her heart, "I am, and there is none besides me." How she has become a desolation, a lair for beasts! Everyone who passes by her hisses and shakes his fist.

zep.3.text: Woe to the rebellious, the defiled — the oppressing city!
She listened to no voice; she accepted no correction. In YHWH she did not trust; to her God she did not draw near.
Her princes in her midst are roaring lions; her judges are evening wolves that leave nothing for the morning.
Her prophets are reckless, men of treachery; her priests have profaned what is holy — they have done violence to the law.
YHWH is righteous in her midst; he does no wrong. Morning by morning he brings his justice to light; it does not fail. But the wrongdoer knows no shame.
I have cut off nations; their corner-towers lie desolate. I have laid waste their streets, with none passing through. Their cities are destroyed — without a man, without an inhabitant.

I said, "Surely you will fear me; you will accept correction" — then her dwelling would not be cut off, according to all I had appointed against her. But no — they rose early and corrupted all their deeds.

Therefore wait for me — the word of YHWH — for the day I rise up as witness. For my judgment is to gather nations, to assemble kingdoms, to pour out upon them my indignation, all my burning anger; for in the fire of my jealousy all the earth shall be consumed.
For then I will change the speech of the peoples to a purified speech, that all of them may call on the name of YHWH, to serve him with one shoulder.
From beyond the rivers of Cush my worshipers, the daughter of my scattered ones, shall bring my offering.

On that day you shall not be ashamed of all the deeds by which you rebelled against me; for then I will remove from your midst your proudly exultant ones, and you shall never again be haughty on my holy mountain.
And I will leave in your midst a people humble and lowly, and they shall take refuge in the name of YHWH.
The remnant of Israel shall do no wrong and speak no lie; no tongue of deceit shall be found in their mouth. For they shall graze and lie down, and none shall make them afraid.
Sing, O daughter of Zion! Shout aloud, O Israel! Rejoice and exult with all your heart, O daughter of Jerusalem!
YHWH has taken away your judgments; he has turned aside your enemy. The King of Israel, YHWH, is in your midst; you shall fear disaster no more.
On that day it shall be said to Jerusalem: "Do not fear, O Zion; let not your hands go slack."
YHWH your God is in your midst, a mighty one who saves. He will rejoice over you with gladness; he will quiet you with his love; he will exult over you with singing.
Those who grieve for the appointed feasts I have gathered from you — they were a burden on her, a reproach.
Behold, at that time I will deal with all your oppressors. I will save the lame and gather the outcast, and I will turn their shame into praise and renown in all the earth.
At that time I will bring you in — at the time I gather you. For I will make you a name and a praise among all the peoples of the earth, when I restore your fortunes before your eyes, says YHWH.

1jn.4.text: Beloved, do not believe every spirit, but test the spirits, whether they are from God; for many false prophets have gone out into the world.
By this you know the Spirit of God: every spirit that confesses Jesus Christ come in the flesh is from God,
and every spirit that does not confess Jesus is not from God. This is the spirit of the antichrist, which you heard was coming — and now it is already in the world.
You are from God, little children, and you have overcome them; for greater is he who is in you than he who is in the world.
They are from the world; therefore they speak from the world, and the world listens to them.
We are from God. Whoever knows God listens to us; whoever is not from God does not listen to us. By this we know the spirit of truth and the spirit of error.
Beloved, let us love one another, for love is from God; and everyone who loves has been born of God and knows God.
Whoever does not love does not know God — for God is love.
In this the love of God was made manifest among us: that God sent his only Son into the world, that we might live through him.
In this is love: not that we loved God, but that he loved us and sent his Son to be the atoning sacrifice for our sins.
Beloved, if God so loved us, we also ought to love one another.
No one has ever seen God. If we love one another, God abides in us, and his love is made perfect in us.
By this we know that we abide in him and he in us: that he has given us of his Spirit.
And we have seen and bear witness that the Father has sent the Son as Savior of the world.
Whoever confesses that Jesus is the Son of God — God abides in him, and he in God.
And we have come to know and to believe the love that God has for us. God is love, and whoever abides in love abides in God, and God abides in him.
In this, love has been made perfect with us, that we may have boldness on the day of judgment; for as he is, so also are we in this world.
There is no fear in love — perfect love casts out fear, for fear has to do with punishment, and whoever fears has not been made perfect in love.
We love, because he first loved us.
If anyone says, "I love God," and hates his brother, he is a liar; for whoever does not love his brother, whom he has seen, cannot love God, whom he has not seen.
And this commandment we have from him: that whoever loves God must love his brother also.

1jn.5.text: Everyone who believes that Jesus is the Christ has been born of God; and everyone who loves the one who begot loves the one born of him.
By this we know that we love the children of God: when we love God and do his commandments.
For this is the love of God: that we keep his commandments. And his commandments are not burdensome,
for everything born of God overcomes the world. And this is the victory that has overcome the world — our faith.
And who is the one who overcomes the world but the one who believes that Jesus is the Son of God?
This is he who came through water and blood — Jesus Christ; not in the water only, but in the water and in the blood. And the Spirit is the one who bears witness, because the Spirit is the truth.
For there are three that bear witness:
the Spirit and the water and the blood — and the three are as one.
If we receive the witness of men, the witness of God is greater; for this is the witness of God, that he has borne witness concerning his Son.
Whoever believes in the Son of God has the witness within himself. Whoever does not believe God has made him a liar, because he has not believed the witness God has borne concerning his Son.
And this is the witness: that God gave us eternal life, and this life is in his Son.
Whoever has the Son has life; whoever does not have the Son of God does not have life.
These things I have written to you who believe in the name of the Son of God, that you may know that you have eternal life.
And this is the boldness we have toward him: that if we ask anything according to his will, he hears us.
And if we know that he hears us in whatever we ask, we know that we have the requests we have asked of him.

If anyone sees his brother committing a sin not leading to death, he shall ask, and God will give him life — to those sinning not unto death. There is sin that leads to death; I do not say that one should ask about that.
All wrongdoing is sin, and there is sin that does not lead to death.
We know that everyone born of God does not go on sinning, but the one born of God keeps him, and the evil one does not touch him.
We know that we are from God, and the whole world lies in the power of the evil one.

And we know that the Son of God has come and has given us understanding, that we may know him who is true; and we are in him who is true — in his Son Jesus Christ. This is the true God and eternal life.
Little children, guard yourselves from idols.

jol.2.text: Blow the shofar in Zion; sound the alarm on my holy mountain! Let all the inhabitants of the land tremble — for the day of YHWH is coming; it is near:

a day of darkness and gloom, a day of cloud and thick blackness, like dawn spread over the mountains. A people great and mighty — their like has never been from of old, and after them shall never be again, through the years of generation upon generation.
Before them a fire devours, and behind them a flame burns. Like the garden of Eden is the land before them, and behind them a desolate wilderness — and nothing at all escapes them.
Their appearance is the appearance of horses; like war-horses, so they run.
With a sound like chariots they leap over the mountaintops, like the sound of a flame of fire devouring stubble, like a mighty people arrayed for battle.
Before them the peoples writhe; every face drains of color.
They run like warriors; they scale the wall like men of war. Each marches on his own way; they do not swerve from their paths.
They do not jostle one another; each goes on his own track. They plunge through the weapons and are not cut down.
They rush upon the city; they run along the wall; they climb into the houses; through the windows they enter like a thief.
Before them the earth quakes, the heavens tremble. Sun and moon go dark, and the stars withdraw their shining.
And YHWH gives voice before his army — for his camp is very great; for mighty is he who performs his word. For great is the day of YHWH, and terrible indeed — who can endure it?
"Yet even now" — the word of YHWH — "return to me with all your heart, with fasting and weeping and mourning."
Rend your hearts and not your garments, and return to YHWH your God; for he is gracious and compassionate, slow to anger and abounding in steadfast love, and he relents from the evil.
Who knows? He may turn and relent, and leave a blessing behind him — a grain-offering and a drink-offering for YHWH your God.
Blow the shofar in Zion! Consecrate a fast; call a solemn assembly.
Gather the people; consecrate the congregation; assemble the elders; gather the children, even nursing infants. Let the bridegroom come out of his chamber, and the bride from her canopy.

Between the porch and the altar let the priests weep, the ministers of YHWH, and let them say: "Spare your people, O YHWH, and do not make your heritage a reproach, a byword among the nations. Why should they say among the peoples, 'Where is their God?'"
Then YHWH grew jealous for his land and had pity on his people.

And YHWH answered and said to his people: "Behold, I am sending you the grain, the new wine, and the oil, and you shall be satisfied with them; and I will never again make you a reproach among the nations.

And the northerner I will remove far from you and drive him into a land parched and desolate — his front toward the eastern sea, his rear toward the western sea. His stench shall rise; his foulness shall rise — for he has done great things."
Do not fear, O soil; rejoice and be glad, for YHWH has done great things!
Do not fear, you beasts of the field, for the pastures of the wilderness are green again; the tree bears its fruit; the fig tree and the vine yield their strength.
And you children of Zion, rejoice and be glad in YHWH your God; for he has given you the early rain for vindication, and he sends down showers for you — early rain and latter rain, as before.
The threshing floors shall be full of grain, and the vats shall overflow with new wine and oil.
"And I will repay you the years the locust devoured — the swarmer, the hopper, and the cutter — my great army that I sent among you.
And you shall eat, eating and being satisfied, and praise the name of YHWH your God, who has dealt wondrously with you; and my people shall never be put to shame.
And you shall know that I am in the midst of Israel — that I am YHWH your God, and there is no other; and my people shall never be put to shame."

jol.3.text: And it shall come to pass afterward: I will pour out my spirit on all flesh, and your sons and your daughters shall prophesy; your old men shall dream dreams; your young men shall see visions.
Even on the male and female servants, in those days, I will pour out my spirit.
And I will set wonders in the heavens and on the earth: blood and fire and columns of smoke.
The sun shall be turned to darkness, and the moon to blood, before the coming of the day of YHWH, great and terrible.

And it shall be that everyone who calls on the name of YHWH shall be delivered; for on Mount Zion and in Jerusalem there shall be an escape, as YHWH has said — and among the survivors, those whom YHWH calls.

jol.4.text: For behold, in those days and at that time, when I restore the fortunes of Judah and Jerusalem,

I will gather all the nations and bring them down to the valley of Jehoshaphat, and I will enter into judgment with them there on behalf of my people and my heritage Israel — whom they scattered among the nations; and they divided up my land.
And for my people they cast lots: they gave a boy for a harlot and sold a girl for wine — and drank it.

And what are you to me, Tyre and Sidon, and all the regions of Philistia? Are you paying me back for something? If you are paying me back, swiftly and speedily I will return your payment on your own head —
you who took my silver and my gold and carried my finest treasures into your temples,
and sold the children of Judah and the children of Jerusalem to the Greeks, to remove them far from their own border.
Behold, I am rousing them from the place where you sold them, and I will return your payment on your own head.
And I will sell your sons and your daughters into the hand of the children of Judah, and they shall sell them to the Sabeans, to a nation far away — for YHWH has spoken.
Proclaim this among the nations: Consecrate war! Rouse the warriors! Let all the men of war draw near and come up!
Beat your plowshares into swords, and your pruning-hooks into spears; let the weakling say, "I am a warrior."
Hurry and come, all you nations round about, and gather yourselves there. Bring down your warriors, O YHWH!
Let the nations be roused and come up to the valley of Jehoshaphat; for there I will sit to judge all the nations round about.
Swing the sickle, for the harvest is ripe. Come, tread, for the winepress is full; the vats overflow — for great is their evil.
Multitudes, multitudes in the valley of decision! For the day of YHWH is near in the valley of decision.
Sun and moon go dark, and the stars withdraw their shining.
And YHWH roars from Zion; from Jerusalem he gives his voice, and heavens and earth quake. But YHWH is a refuge for his people, a stronghold for the children of Israel.
And you shall know that I am YHWH your God, dwelling in Zion, my holy mountain. And Jerusalem shall be holy, and strangers shall pass through her no more.

And it shall come to pass on that day: the mountains shall drip sweet wine, and the hills shall flow with milk, and all the streambeds of Judah shall flow with water; and a spring shall go out from the house of YHWH and water the Valley of Shittim.
Egypt shall become a desolation, and Edom a desolate wilderness, for the violence done to the children of Judah — because they shed innocent blood in their land.
But Judah shall be inhabited forever, and Jerusalem from generation to generation.
And I will hold innocent their blood that I have not held innocent — and YHWH dwells in Zion.

1pe.2.text: So put away all malice and all deceit, and hypocrisies and envies and every slander,
and like newborn infants long for the pure milk of the word, that by it you may grow up into salvation —
if indeed you have tasted that the Lord is good.
Come to him, a living stone — rejected by men, but chosen and precious before God —
and you yourselves, like living stones, are being built into a spiritual house, a holy priesthood, to offer spiritual sacrifices acceptable to God through Jesus Christ.
For it stands in scripture: "Behold, I lay in Zion a chosen and precious cornerstone, and whoever believes in him shall not be put to shame."
To you who believe, then, the honor; but to those who do not believe: "The stone the builders rejected — this one has become the head of the corner,"
and "a stone of stumbling and a rock of offense." They stumble because they disobey the word — to which also they were appointed.

But you are a chosen race, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, a people for God's own possession, that you may proclaim the excellencies of him who called you out of darkness into his marvelous light —
you who once were no people, but now are the people of God; who had not received mercy, but now have received mercy.
Beloved, I urge you as sojourners and exiles to abstain from the desires of the flesh, which wage war against the soul.
Keep your conduct among the nations beautiful, so that where they speak against you as evildoers, they may — from watching your good works — glorify God on the day of visitation.
Submit yourselves to every human institution for the Lord's sake: whether to the king as supreme,
or to governors as those sent by him to punish evildoers and to praise those who do good.
For this is the will of God: that by doing good you silence the ignorance of foolish men —
as free people, yet not using freedom as a cover for evil, but as servants of God.
Honor everyone. Love the brotherhood. Fear God. Honor the king.
Servants, be subject to your masters with all reverence — not only to the good and gentle, but also to the crooked.
For this is grace: when, for conscience toward God, someone endures sorrows while suffering unjustly.
For what credit is it if you sin, are beaten for it, and endure? But if you do good, suffer for it, and endure — this is grace before God.
For to this you were called, because Christ also suffered for you, leaving you a pattern, that you might follow in his footsteps:
he committed no sin, nor was deceit found in his mouth;
when he was reviled, he did not revile in return; suffering, he did not threaten, but entrusted himself to the one who judges justly.
He himself bore our sins in his body upon the tree, that we, having died to sins, might live to righteousness — by his wound you were healed.
For you were straying like sheep, but now you have returned to the Shepherd and Overseer of your souls.

1pe.3.text: Likewise, wives, be subject to your own husbands, so that even if some disobey the word, they may be won without a word through the conduct of their wives,
having watched your pure conduct lived in reverence.
Let your adorning not be the outward — braiding of hair, wearing of gold, putting on of garments —
but the hidden person of the heart, in the imperishable beauty of a gentle and quiet spirit, which is precious before God.
For so once the holy women who hoped in God adorned themselves, being subject to their own husbands,
as Sarah obeyed Abraham, calling him lord. You have become her children if you do good and fear no intimidation.
Husbands, likewise, live with them according to knowledge, showing honor to the woman as the more fragile vessel, and as fellow heirs of the grace of life — so that your prayers may not be hindered.
Finally, all of you: be of one mind, sympathetic, loving the brothers, tender-hearted, humble-minded,
not returning evil for evil or insult for insult, but on the contrary blessing — for to this you were called, that you might inherit a blessing.
For "whoever would love life and see good days, let him stop his tongue from evil and his lips from speaking deceit;
let him turn from evil and do good; let him seek peace and pursue it.
For the eyes of the Lord are on the righteous, and his ears open to their plea; but the face of the Lord is against those who do evil."
And who will harm you if you become zealous for what is good?
But even if you should suffer for righteousness — blessed are you. Do not fear their fear, nor be shaken;
but sanctify Christ as Lord in your hearts, always ready with an answer for everyone who asks you the reason for the hope that is in you —
yet with gentleness and reverence, keeping a good conscience, so that when you are slandered, those who revile your good conduct in Christ may be put to shame.
For it is better to suffer for doing good, if the will of God should so will, than for doing evil.
For Christ also suffered once for sins, the righteous for the unrighteous, that he might bring you to God — put to death in the flesh, but made alive in the spirit,
in which also he went and preached to the spirits in prison,
who long ago disobeyed, when the patience of God kept waiting in the days of Noah while the ark was being built — in which a few, that is, eight souls, were brought safely through water.
And baptism, its counterpart, now saves you — not the removal of dirt from the flesh, but the appeal of a good conscience to God — through the resurrection of Jesus Christ,
who is at the right hand of God, having gone into heaven, with angels and authorities and powers made subject to him.

1pe.4.text: Since Christ, then, suffered in the flesh, arm yourselves also with the same mind — for whoever has suffered in the flesh is done with sin —
so as to live the remaining time in the flesh no longer for human desires but for the will of God.
For the time past is enough for having done what the nations willed — walking in sensuality, desires, drunkenness, carousals, drinking bouts, and lawless idolatries.
They are astonished that you do not run with them into the same flood of dissipation, and they slander you;
they will render account to him who stands ready to judge the living and the dead.
For this is why the good news was preached even to the dead: that though judged in the flesh as men are, they might live in the spirit as God does.
The end of all things has drawn near. Be sober-minded, therefore, and watchful for your prayers.
Above all, hold your love for one another at full stretch — for love covers a multitude of sins.
Be hospitable to one another without grumbling.
As each has received a gift, use it in serving one another, as good stewards of the many-colored grace of God:

if anyone speaks — as one uttering oracles of God; if anyone serves — as from the strength God supplies; so that in everything God may be glorified through Jesus Christ, to whom belong the glory and the dominion forever and ever. Amen.
Beloved, do not be astonished at the fiery ordeal among you, which comes upon you for testing, as though something strange were happening to you;
but insofar as you share the sufferings of Christ, rejoice — that at the revelation of his glory you may also rejoice, exulting.
If you are reproached for the name of Christ — blessed are you, for the Spirit of glory and of God rests upon you.
But let none of you suffer as a murderer or a thief or an evildoer, or as a meddler in others' affairs;
yet if anyone suffers as a Christian, let him not be ashamed, but glorify God in this name.
For it is time for the judgment to begin from the house of God; and if it begins first with us, what will be the end of those who disobey the good news of God?
And "if the righteous one is barely saved, where will the ungodly and the sinner appear?"
So then, let those who suffer according to the will of God entrust their souls to a faithful Creator, while doing good.

1pe.5.text: The elders among you, then, I exhort — I, a fellow elder and witness of the sufferings of Christ, and a sharer in the glory about to be revealed:
shepherd the flock of God that is among you, watching over it not under compulsion but willingly, as God would have it; not for shameful gain but eagerly;
not lording it over those in your charge, but becoming examples to the flock.
And when the Chief Shepherd appears, you will receive the unfading crown of glory.
Likewise, you who are younger, be subject to the elders. And all of you, clothe yourselves with humility toward one another — for "God opposes the proud, but gives grace to the humble."
Humble yourselves, then, under the mighty hand of God, that he may lift you up in due time,
casting all your anxiety upon him — because he cares about you.
Be sober; be watchful. Your adversary the devil prowls around like a roaring lion, seeking someone to devour.
Resist him, firm in the faith, knowing that the same sufferings are being laid upon your brotherhood throughout the world.
And the God of all grace, who called you into his eternal glory in Christ, will himself — after you have suffered a little while — restore, establish, strengthen, and settle you.
To him be the dominion forever and ever. Amen.
Through Silvanus, the faithful brother as I regard him, I have written to you briefly, exhorting and testifying that this is the true grace of God — stand firm in it.
She who is in Babylon, chosen together with you, greets you; and so does Mark, my son.
Greet one another with a kiss of love. Peace to all of you who are in Christ.

1ti.2.text: I urge, then, first of all, that petitions, prayers, intercessions, and thanksgivings be made for all people —
for kings and all who are in high position, that we may lead a tranquil and quiet life in all godliness and dignity.
This is good and acceptable before God our Savior,
who desires all people to be saved and to come to the knowledge of the truth.
For there is one God, and one mediator between God and humanity — the man Christ Jesus,
who gave himself as a ransom for all: the testimony given in its own times.
For this I was appointed a herald and an apostle — I speak the truth, I do not lie — a teacher of the nations in faith and truth.
I desire, then, that in every place the men pray, lifting up holy hands without anger or argument;
likewise also that women adorn themselves in respectable attire, with modesty and self-restraint — not with braided hair and gold or pearls or costly clothing,
but with what befits women who profess reverence for God: good works.
Let a woman learn in quietness, in all submission.
I do not permit a woman to teach or to exercise authority over a man; she is to be in quietness.
For Adam was formed first, then Eve;
and Adam was not deceived, but the woman, being deceived, came into transgression.
Yet she will be saved through the bearing of children — if they continue in faith and love and holiness, with self-restraint.

1ti.3.text: The saying is trustworthy: if anyone aspires to oversight, he desires a good work.
An overseer, then, must be above reproach, the husband of one wife, sober, self-controlled, orderly, hospitable, able to teach;
not given to wine, not violent, but gentle; not quarrelsome, not a lover of money;
managing his own household well, keeping his children in submission with all dignity —
for if someone does not know how to manage his own household, how will he care for the church of God?
Not a new convert, lest he be puffed up and fall into the judgment of the devil.
And he must also have a good testimony from those outside, lest he fall into reproach and the snare of the devil.
Deacons likewise must be dignified, not double-tongued, not devoted to much wine, not greedy for shameful gain,
holding the mystery of the faith with a clean conscience.
And let these also be tested first; then let them serve, being beyond accusation.
The women likewise must be dignified, not slanderers, sober, faithful in everything.
Let deacons be husbands of one wife, managing their children and their own households well;
for those who have served well gain for themselves a good standing and great boldness in the faith that is in Christ Jesus.
These things I write to you, hoping to come to you soon;
but if I delay — so that you may know how one must conduct oneself in the household of God, which is the church of the living God, the pillar and foundation of the truth.
And confessedly great is the mystery of godliness: He was manifested in the flesh, vindicated in the Spirit, seen by angels, proclaimed among the nations, believed on in the world, taken up in glory.

1ti.4.text: Now the Spirit says expressly that in later times some will fall away from the faith, giving heed to deceiving spirits and teachings of demons,
through the hypocrisy of liars whose own conscience has been seared,
who forbid marriage and demand abstinence from foods that God created to be received with thanksgiving by those who believe and know the truth.
For every creature of God is good, and nothing is to be rejected if received with thanksgiving;
for it is sanctified through the word of God and prayer.
By setting these things before the brothers you will be a good servant of Christ Jesus, nourished on the words of the faith and of the good teaching you have followed.
But refuse the profane myths of old women. Train yourself instead for godliness;
for bodily training is of some small profit, but godliness is profitable for everything, holding promise for the life that now is and the life to come.
The saying is trustworthy and worthy of full acceptance:
for to this end we labor and are reproached, because we have set our hope on the living God, who is the Savior of all people — especially of those who believe.
Command these things and teach them.
Let no one despise your youth; rather, become an example to the believers — in speech, in conduct, in love, in faith, in purity.
Until I come, attend to the public reading, to exhortation, to teaching.
Do not neglect the gift that is in you, which was given you through prophecy with the laying on of the hands of the council of elders.
Practice these things; live in them, so that your progress may be plain to all.
Watch yourself and your teaching; persist in them — for in doing this you will save both yourself and those who hear you.

1ti.5.text: Do not rebuke an older man harshly, but appeal to him as a father; to younger men as brothers,
to older women as mothers, to younger women as sisters — in all purity.
Honor widows who are truly widows.
But if a widow has children or grandchildren, let them learn first to show godliness to their own household and to repay their forebears — for this is acceptable before God.
Now she who is truly a widow, and left alone, has set her hope on God and continues in petitions and prayers night and day;
but she who lives for pleasure is dead while she lives.
Command these things too, that they may be above reproach.
And if anyone does not provide for his own, and especially for his own household, he has denied the faith and is worse than an unbeliever.
Let a widow be enrolled if she is not less than sixty years old, the wife of one husband,
attested by good works: if she has raised children, if she has shown hospitality, if she has washed the feet of the saints, if she has relieved the afflicted, if she has followed every good work.
But refuse younger widows; for when their desires draw them away from Christ, they want to marry,
and so bring judgment on themselves for setting aside their first pledge.
And at the same time they learn to be idle, going around from house to house — and not only idle, but gossips and busybodies, saying what they should not.
So I would have younger widows marry, bear children, manage their households, and give the adversary no occasion for reviling;
for some have already turned aside after Satan.
If any believing woman has widows in her family, let her relieve them; let the church not be burdened, so that it may relieve those who are truly widows.
Let the elders who lead well be counted worthy of double honor — especially those who labor in word and teaching.
For the scripture says, "You shall not muzzle an ox while it treads the grain," and, "The worker is worthy of his wages."
Do not receive an accusation against an elder, except on the testimony of two or three witnesses.
Those who persist in sin, rebuke before all, so that the rest also may have fear.
I solemnly charge you before God and Christ Jesus and the chosen angels: keep these things without prejudgment, doing nothing out of partiality.
Lay hands on no one hastily, and do not share in the sins of others; keep yourself pure.
No longer drink only water, but use a little wine for the sake of your stomach and your frequent illnesses.
The sins of some people are plain, going before them into judgment; but for others, they follow after.
Likewise also good works are plain — and those that are otherwise cannot stay hidden.

1ti.6.text: Let all who are under the yoke as slaves count their own masters worthy of all honor, so that the name of God and the teaching may not be blasphemed.

And those who have believing masters must not despise them because they are brothers; rather let them serve all the better, because those who benefit from their service are believers and beloved. Teach and urge these things.
If anyone teaches differently and does not agree with the sound words of our Lord Jesus Christ and the teaching that accords with godliness,
he is puffed up, understanding nothing, but sick with a craving for controversies and word-battles — from which come envy, strife, blasphemies, evil suspicions,
and constant friction among people corrupted in mind and robbed of the truth, who suppose that godliness is a means of gain.
But godliness with contentment is great gain;
for we brought nothing into the world, and neither can we carry anything out.
If we have food and covering, with these we shall be content.
But those who want to be rich fall into temptation and a snare and many senseless and harmful desires that plunge people into ruin and destruction.
For the love of money is a root of all evils; craving it, some have wandered away from the faith and pierced themselves through with many sorrows.
But you, O man of God, flee these things. Pursue righteousness, godliness, faith, love, endurance, gentleness of spirit.
Fight the good fight of the faith; take hold of the eternal life to which you were called, and for which you confessed the good confession before many witnesses.
I charge you before God, who gives life to all things, and Christ Jesus, who bore witness to the good confession before Pontius Pilate:
keep the commandment spotless and above reproach until the appearing of our Lord Jesus Christ,
which he will display in its own times — he who is the blessed and only Sovereign, the King of those who reign and Lord of those who rule,
who alone has immortality, dwelling in unapproachable light, whom no human being has seen or is able to see. To him be honor and eternal dominion. Amen.
Command those who are rich in the present age not to be high-minded, nor to set their hope on the uncertainty of riches, but on God, who richly provides us everything for our enjoyment —
to do good, to be rich in good works, to be open-handed, ready to share,
storing up for themselves a good foundation for what is coming, so that they may take hold of the life that truly is life.
O Timothy, guard the deposit entrusted to you, turning away from the profane empty chatter and contradictions of what is falsely called knowledge —
which some have professed and so missed the mark concerning the faith. Grace be with you all.

hab.1.text: The burden that Habakkuk the prophet saw.
How long, YHWH, have I cried out, and you do not hear? I shout to you, "Violence!" — and you do not save.
Why do you make me see wickedness, and look upon trouble? Ruin and violence are before me; strife arises, and contention lifts itself up.
Therefore the law grows numb, and justice never goes forth; for the wicked hem in the righteous — therefore justice goes forth twisted.
Look among the nations and see — be astonished, be astounded! For a work is being worked in your days you would not believe if it were told.
For behold, I am raising up the Chaldeans, that bitter and headlong nation, that marches across the breadth of the earth to seize dwellings not its own.
Dreadful and terrible are they; from themselves their justice and their eminence go forth.
Swifter than leopards their horses, keener than evening wolves. Their horsemen charge ahead; their horsemen come from far away — they fly like an eagle rushing to devour.
All of them come for violence, their faces set forward like the east wind; they gather up captives like sand.
They mock at kings, and rulers are their laughingstock; they laugh at every fortress — they heap up earth and take it.
Then they sweep on like the wind and pass through — and stand guilty: this one whose own strength is his god.
Are you not from of old, YHWH my God, my Holy One? We shall not die. YHWH, you have appointed them for judgment; O Rock, you have founded them for correction.
Your eyes are too pure to look on evil; you cannot gaze upon trouble. Why then do you gaze on the treacherous, and stay silent while the wicked swallows one more righteous than he?
You have made humanity like the fish of the sea, like crawling things that have no ruler.
He hauls all of them up with a hook; he drags them out with his net and gathers them in his seine — therefore he rejoices and exults.
Therefore he sacrifices to his net and burns incense to his seine — for by them his portion is fat and his food is rich.
Shall he then keep emptying his net, and go on slaying nations without mercy?

hab.2.text: I will stand at my watchpost and station myself on the rampart; I will keep watch to see what he will say to me, and what I shall answer to my complaint.
And YHWH answered me and said: Write the vision; make it plain on the tablets, so that a runner may read it.
For the vision still awaits its appointed time; it pants toward the end, and it will not lie. If it delays, wait for it — for it will surely come; it will not be late.
Behold, the puffed-up one — his soul is not upright within him; but the righteous shall live by his faithfulness.

Moreover, wine is a betrayer: an arrogant man, and he will not stay at home — who widens his appetite like Sheol; like death he is never satisfied, gathering to himself all the nations, collecting to himself all the peoples.
Shall not all these take up a taunt against him, with mocking riddles for him, saying: "Woe to him who heaps up what is not his own — for how long? — and loads himself with pledges!"
Will not your creditors suddenly arise, and those who shake you awake? — and you shall become their spoil.
Because you plundered many nations, all the remainder of the peoples shall plunder you — for human blood, and violence to the land, to the city and all who dwell in her.
Woe to him who gains evil gain for his house, to set his nest on high, to be delivered from the grasp of harm!
You have plotted shame for your own house — cutting off many peoples, and forfeiting your own soul.
For the stone will cry out from the wall, and the rafter will answer it from the woodwork.
Woe to him who builds a city with blood, and founds a town on injustice!
Behold, is it not from YHWH of hosts that peoples labor for the fire, and nations weary themselves for nothing?
For the earth shall be filled with the knowledge of the glory of YHWH as the waters cover the sea.
Woe to him who gives his neighbor drink, pouring out your wrath — yes, making them drunk, in order to gaze on their nakedness!
You are sated with shame instead of glory. Drink — you too — and be exposed! The cup of YHWH's right hand shall come around to you, and disgrace upon your glory.
For the violence done to Lebanon shall cover you, and the destruction of beasts shall terrify you — for human blood, and violence to the land, to the city and all who dwell in her.
What profit is a carved image, that its maker has carved it — a molten image, a teacher of lies — that the maker of his creation trusts in it, making dumb idols?
Woe to him who says to wood, "Awake!" — to silent stone, "Arise!" Can this teach? Behold, it is overlaid with gold and silver, and there is no breath at all within it.
But YHWH is in his holy temple — be silent before him, all the earth.

hab.3.text: A prayer of Habakkuk the prophet, upon Shigionoth.
YHWH, I have heard the report of you, and I am afraid. YHWH, revive your work in the midst of the years; in the midst of the years make it known; in wrath remember mercy.
God comes from Teman, and the Holy One from Mount Paran. Selah. His splendor covers the heavens, and his praise fills the earth.
His brightness is like the light; rays flash from his hand — and there is the hiding place of his power.
Before him goes pestilence, and plague goes forth at his feet.
He stands, and the earth shakes; he looks, and makes the nations start. The everlasting mountains are shattered; the ancient hills bow down — his are the ways of old.
I saw the tents of Cushan under affliction; the curtains of the land of Midian trembled.
Was YHWH angry with the rivers? Was your wrath against the rivers, or your fury against the sea, that you rode upon your horses, your chariots of salvation?
Your bow is stripped bare; the oaths of the tribes are a sure word. Selah. You split the earth with rivers.
The mountains saw you and writhed; the downpour of waters swept by. The deep gave its voice; it lifted its hands on high.
Sun and moon stood still in their lofty home, at the light of your arrows as they flew, at the brightness of your flashing spear.
In indignation you stride the earth; in anger you thresh the nations.
You went forth for the salvation of your people, for the salvation of your anointed. You crushed the head from the house of the wicked, laying bare foundation to neck. Selah.
You pierced with his own shafts the head of his warriors, who stormed out to scatter me, exulting as if to devour the poor in secret.
You trod the sea with your horses, the churning of many waters.

I heard, and my belly trembled; at the sound my lips quivered. Rottenness entered my bones, and beneath me I trembled — yet I will rest for the day of distress, when it comes up against the people who invade us.

Though the fig tree does not blossom, and there is no fruit on the vines; though the labor of the olive fails, and the fields yield no food; though the flock is cut off from the fold, and there is no herd in the stalls —
yet I will exult in YHWH; I will rejoice in the God of my salvation.
YHWH the Lord is my strength; he makes my feet like the deer's, and makes me tread upon my high places. To the choirmaster: with my stringed instruments.

hag.1.text: In the second year of Darius the king, in the sixth month, on the first day of the month, the word of YHWH came by the hand of Haggai the prophet to Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel, governor of Judah, and to Joshua son of Jehozadak, the high priest:
Thus says YHWH of hosts: This people says, "The time has not come — the time for the house of YHWH to be built."
And the word of YHWH came by the hand of Haggai the prophet:
Is it time for you yourselves to dwell in your paneled houses, while this house lies in ruins?
And now, thus says YHWH of hosts: Set your heart upon your ways.

You have sown much and brought in little; you eat, but there is no fullness; you drink, but no quenching; you clothe yourselves, but no one is warm; and the wage-earner earns wages into a purse full of holes.
Thus says YHWH of hosts: Set your heart upon your ways.
Go up to the hill country and bring wood and build the house, that I may take pleasure in it and be glorified, says YHWH.

You looked for much, and behold, it came to little; and when you brought it home, I blew it away. Why? — the word of YHWH of hosts. Because of my house, which lies in ruins, while each of you runs to his own house.
Therefore, because of you, the heavens have withheld the dew, and the earth has withheld its produce.
And I called for a drought upon the land and the mountains, upon the grain, the new wine, and the oil, upon what the ground brings forth, upon man and beast, and upon all the labor of your hands.

Then Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel, and Joshua son of Jehozadak, the high priest, and all the remnant of the people obeyed the voice of YHWH their God, and the words of Haggai the prophet, as YHWH their God had sent him — and the people feared before YHWH.
And Haggai, the messenger of YHWH, spoke YHWH's message to the people: "I am with you — the word of YHWH."

And YHWH stirred up the spirit of Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel, governor of Judah, and the spirit of Joshua son of Jehozadak, the high priest, and the spirit of all the remnant of the people; and they came and worked on the house of YHWH of hosts, their God,
on the twenty-fourth day of the month, in the sixth month, in the second year of Darius the king.

hag.2.text: In the seventh month, on the twenty-first of the month, the word of YHWH came by the hand of Haggai the prophet:
Say now to Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel, governor of Judah, and to Joshua son of Jehozadak, the high priest, and to the remnant of the people:
Who is left among you who saw this house in its former glory? And how do you see it now? Is it not, in your eyes, as nothing?

Yet now be strong, Zerubbabel — the word of YHWH; be strong, Joshua son of Jehozadak, high priest; be strong, all you people of the land — the word of YHWH — and work! For I am with you, the word of YHWH of hosts,
according to the word I covenanted with you when you came out of Egypt. And my Spirit stands in your midst — do not fear.
For thus says YHWH of hosts: Once more — it is a little while — and I will shake the heavens and the earth, the sea and the dry land.
And I will shake all the nations, and the treasure of all nations shall come in; and I will fill this house with glory, says YHWH of hosts.
Mine is the silver and mine the gold — the word of YHWH of hosts.
Greater shall be the glory of this latter house than of the former, says YHWH of hosts; and in this place I will give peace — the word of YHWH of hosts.
On the twenty-fourth of the ninth month, in the second year of Darius, the word of YHWH came to Haggai the prophet:
Thus says YHWH of hosts: Ask now the priests for a ruling:
If a man carries holy flesh in the fold of his garment, and with his fold touches bread or stew or wine or oil or any food — does it become holy? And the priests answered and said, "No."
And Haggai said, "If one unclean from a corpse touches any of these, does it become unclean?" And the priests answered and said, "It becomes unclean."
Then Haggai answered and said: "So is this people, and so is this nation before me — the word of YHWH — and so is every work of their hands; and what they offer there is unclean.
And now, set your heart on this: from this day onward, before stone was laid upon stone in the temple of YHWH —
since those days were, one came to a heap of twenty measures, and there were ten; one came to the wine vat to draw fifty, and there were twenty.
I struck you — all the work of your hands — with blight and mildew and hail, and still you did not turn to me — the word of YHWH.
Set your heart on this: from this day onward, from the twenty-fourth day of the ninth month, from the day the temple of YHWH was founded — set your heart:
Is the seed still in the storehouse? And the vine, the fig, the pomegranate, and the olive tree have borne nothing. From this day on, I will bless."
And the word of YHWH came a second time to Haggai, on the twenty-fourth of the month:
Say to Zerubbabel, governor of Judah: I am shaking the heavens and the earth,

and I will overturn the throne of kingdoms and destroy the strength of the kingdoms of the nations; I will overturn the chariot and its riders, and horses and their riders shall go down, each by his brother's sword.

On that day — the word of YHWH of hosts — I will take you, Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel, my servant — the word of YHWH — and I will set you like a signet ring; for I have chosen you — the word of YHWH of hosts.

mal.1.text: The burden of the word of YHWH to Israel by the hand of Malachi.
"I have loved you," says YHWH. And you say, "How have you loved us?" "Was not Esau Jacob's brother?" — the word of YHWH — "Yet I loved Jacob,
and Esau I hated; I made his mountains a desolation, and gave his heritage to the jackals of the wilderness."

Though Edom says, "We are shattered, but we will return and rebuild the ruins," thus says YHWH of hosts: They may build, but I will tear down; and they shall be called the Territory of Wickedness, the people with whom YHWH is indignant forever.
Your own eyes shall see it, and you yourselves shall say, "Great is YHWH beyond the border of Israel!"

"A son honors his father, and a servant his master. If then I am a father, where is my honor? And if I am a master, where is my fear?" says YHWH of hosts to you, O priests who despise my name. And you say, "How have we despised your name?"
By presenting defiled bread upon my altar. And you say, "How have we defiled you?" By saying, "The table of YHWH — it is contemptible."

And when you present a blind animal for sacrifice — is that not evil? And when you present the lame and the sick — is that not evil? Offer it now to your governor: will he be pleased with you? Will he lift up your face? — says YHWH of hosts.
And now, entreat the face of God, that he may be gracious to us. With such an offering from your hand, will he lift up any of your faces? — says YHWH of hosts.
Oh that someone among you would shut the doors, that you might not kindle my altar in vain! "I have no pleasure in you," says YHWH of hosts, "and I will accept no offering from your hand.

For from the rising of the sun to its setting, my name is great among the nations; and in every place incense is offered to my name, and a pure offering — for great is my name among the nations," says YHWH of hosts.
"But you profane it when you say, 'The table of the Lord is defiled, and its fruit — its food — is contemptible.'

And you say, 'Behold, what a weariness!' and you sniff at it," says YHWH of hosts. "You bring what was seized, and the lame, and the sick — this you bring as the offering! Shall I accept it from your hand?" says YHWH.
"Cursed be the cheat who has a male in his flock, and vows it, yet sacrifices to the Lord a blemished thing. For I am a great King," says YHWH of hosts, "and my name is feared among the nations."

mal.2.text: And now, O priests, this commandment is for you.

"If you will not listen, and if you will not lay it to heart to give honor to my name," says YHWH of hosts, "then I will send the curse upon you, and I will curse your blessings — indeed, I have cursed them already, because you do not lay it to heart.
Behold, I am rebuking your seed, and I will spread dung upon your faces — the dung of your feasts — and you shall be carried off with it.
And you shall know that I sent you this commandment, that my covenant with Levi might stand," says YHWH of hosts.
"My covenant with him was life and peace, and I gave them to him — and fear, and he feared me; before my name he stood in awe.
True instruction was in his mouth, and no wrong was found on his lips. In peace and uprightness he walked with me, and he turned many back from iniquity.
For the lips of a priest should guard knowledge, and instruction should be sought from his mouth — for he is the messenger of YHWH of hosts.
But you have turned aside from the way; you have made many stumble by your instruction; you have corrupted the covenant of Levi," says YHWH of hosts.
"So I in turn have made you despised and abased before all the people, inasmuch as you do not keep my ways, but show partiality in your instruction."
Have we not all one Father? Has not one God created us? Why then do we deal treacherously, each against his brother, profaning the covenant of our fathers?

Judah has dealt treacherously, and an abomination has been done in Israel and in Jerusalem; for Judah has profaned the sanctuary of YHWH, which he loves, and has married the daughter of a foreign god.
May YHWH cut off from the tents of Jacob the man who does this — witness and answerer alike — even one who brings an offering to YHWH of hosts.
And this second thing you do: covering the altar of YHWH with tears, with weeping and groaning, because he no longer regards the offering or receives it with favor from your hand.
And you say, "Why?" Because YHWH has been witness between you and the wife of your youth, against whom you have dealt treacherously — though she is your companion and the wife of your covenant.

Did he not make them one, with a portion of spirit in it? And what does the One seek? Godly offspring. So guard yourselves in your spirit, and let none deal treacherously against the wife of his youth.
"For he hates divorce," says YHWH the God of Israel, "and him who covers his garment with violence," says YHWH of hosts. "So guard yourselves in your spirit, and do not deal treacherously."

You have wearied YHWH with your words. And you say, "How have we wearied him?" By saying, "Everyone who does evil is good in the eyes of YHWH, and in them he delights" — or, "Where is the God of justice?"

mal.3.text: "Behold, I am sending my messenger, and he will clear the way before me; and suddenly the Lord whom you seek will come to his temple — the messenger of the covenant in whom you delight. Behold, he is coming," says YHWH of hosts.
But who can endure the day of his coming, and who can stand when he appears? For he is like a refiner's fire, and like the fullers' soap.
He will sit as a refiner and purifier of silver, and he will purify the sons of Levi and refine them like gold and silver — and they shall belong to YHWH, bringing offerings in righteousness.
Then the offering of Judah and Jerusalem will be pleasing to YHWH, as in the days of old, as in former years.

"And I will draw near to you for judgment, and I will be a swift witness against the sorcerers, against the adulterers, against those who swear falsely, against those who oppress the hired worker in his wages, the widow and the orphan, and those who thrust aside the sojourner — and do not fear me," says YHWH of hosts.
"For I, YHWH, do not change — and you, children of Jacob, are not consumed.
From the days of your fathers you have turned aside from my statutes and have not kept them. Return to me, and I will return to you," says YHWH of hosts. "But you say, 'In what shall we return?'
Will a man rob God? Yet you are robbing me. And you say, 'How have we robbed you?' In the tithe and the contribution.
You are cursed with the curse, and me you keep robbing — the whole nation of you.

Bring the whole tithe into the storehouse, that there may be food in my house; and test me now in this," says YHWH of hosts, "if I will not open for you the windows of heaven and pour out for you blessing until there is no more need.
And I will rebuke the devourer for you, so that it will not destroy for you the fruit of the ground, and the vine in the field will not miscarry for you," says YHWH of hosts.
"And all the nations will call you blessed, for you will be a land of delight," says YHWH of hosts.
"Your words have been hard against me," says YHWH. "And you say, 'What have we spoken against you?'
You have said, 'It is vain to serve God. And what profit is it that we have kept his charge, and that we have walked in mourning before YHWH of hosts?
And now we call the arrogant blessed: those who do wickedness are built up; they even test God and escape.'"
Then those who feared YHWH spoke with one another, and YHWH listened attentively and heard; and a book of remembrance was written before him for those who fear YHWH and treasure his name.
"And they shall be mine," says YHWH of hosts, "for the day I am making — a treasured possession; and I will spare them as a man spares his son who serves him.
Then once more you shall see the difference between the righteous and the wicked, between one who serves God and one who does not serve him.

For behold, the day is coming, burning like a furnace, when all the arrogant and every evildoer shall be stubble; and the coming day shall set them ablaze," says YHWH of hosts, "leaving them neither root nor branch.
But for you who fear my name, the sun of righteousness shall rise with healing in its wings; and you shall go out leaping like calves from the stall.
And you shall trample the wicked, for they shall be ashes under the soles of your feet, on the day I am making," says YHWH of hosts.
Remember the law of Moses my servant, which I commanded him at Horeb for all Israel — statutes and judgments.
Behold, I am sending you Elijah the prophet before the coming of the day of YHWH, great and terrible.
And he shall turn the heart of fathers to their children, and the heart of children to their fathers — lest I come and strike the land with utter destruction.

jon.1.text: And the word of YHWH came to Jonah son of Amittai:
"Arise, go to Nineveh, that great city, and cry out against her — for their evil has come up before me."

But Jonah arose to flee to Tarshish, away from the presence of YHWH. He went down to Joppa and found a ship bound for Tarshish; he paid her fare and went down into her, to go with them to Tarshish, away from the presence of YHWH.
But YHWH hurled a great wind upon the sea, and there was a great storm on the sea, so that the ship reckoned herself about to break apart.

And the sailors were afraid, and each cried out to his own god; and they hurled the cargo that was in the ship into the sea, to lighten it from upon them. But Jonah had gone down into the recesses of the vessel, and had lain down, and was fast asleep.
And the captain came to him and said to him, "What are you doing, sleeper? Arise, call on your god! Perhaps the god will give a thought to us, and we will not perish."
And they said to one another, "Come, let us cast lots, that we may know on whose account this evil is upon us." So they cast lots — and the lot fell on Jonah.
And they said to him, "Tell us now — you on whose account this evil is upon us — what is your business? Where do you come from? What is your country? And from what people are you?"
And he said to them, "I am a Hebrew, and I fear YHWH, the God of the heavens, who made the sea and the dry land."
Then the men feared a great fear, and said to him, "What is this you have done!" For the men knew that he was fleeing from the presence of YHWH, because he had told them.
And they said to him, "What shall we do with you, that the sea may quiet down for us?" — for the sea was growing more and more stormy.
And he said to them, "Lift me up and hurl me into the sea, and the sea will quiet down for you; for I know that it is on my account that this great storm is upon you."
Nevertheless the men rowed hard to return to the dry land — and they could not, for the sea grew more and more stormy against them.
Then they called out to YHWH and said, "Please, YHWH, let us not perish for this man's life, and do not lay innocent blood upon us — for you, YHWH, have done as you pleased."
And they lifted Jonah and hurled him into the sea — and the sea stood still from its raging.
Then the men feared YHWH with a great fear, and they offered a sacrifice to YHWH and made vows.

jon.2.text: And YHWH appointed a great fish to swallow Jonah; and Jonah was in the belly of the fish three days and three nights.
And Jonah prayed to YHWH his God from the belly of the fish,
and he said: "I called out of my distress to YHWH, and he answered me; from the belly of Sheol I cried for help — you heard my voice.
You cast me into the deep, into the heart of the seas, and the current surrounded me; all your breakers and your waves passed over me.
And I said, 'I am driven out from before your eyes' — yet I will look again toward your holy temple.
Waters closed in around me, up to the soul; the deep surrounded me; weeds were wrapped about my head.
To the roots of the mountains I went down; the earth — her bars closed behind me forever. Yet you brought my life up from the pit, YHWH my God.
When my soul fainted within me, I remembered YHWH — and my prayer came to you, to your holy temple.
Those who cling to worthless vapors forsake their own steadfast love.
But I, with the voice of thanksgiving, will sacrifice to you; what I have vowed I will pay. Salvation belongs to YHWH!"
And YHWH spoke to the fish — and it vomited Jonah out onto the dry land.

jon.3.text: And the word of YHWH came to Jonah a second time:
"Arise, go to Nineveh, that great city, and proclaim to her the proclamation that I tell you."
So Jonah arose and went to Nineveh, according to the word of YHWH. Now Nineveh was a city great even to God — a three days' walk across.
And Jonah began to go into the city, one day's walk, and he cried out and said, "Forty days more, and Nineveh is overthrown!"
And the people of Nineveh believed God; and they proclaimed a fast and put on sackcloth, from the greatest of them to the least of them.
And the word reached the king of Nineveh, and he rose from his throne, laid aside his robe, covered himself with sackcloth, and sat in the ashes.
And he had it proclaimed and announced in Nineveh, by decree of the king and his nobles: "Let neither man nor beast, herd nor flock, taste anything; let them not graze, and let them not drink water.
But let man and beast be covered with sackcloth, and let them call out to God with strength; and let each one turn from his evil way and from the violence that is in their hands.
Who knows? God may turn and relent, and turn from his burning anger, and we will not perish."
And God saw their deeds — that they turned from their evil way — and God relented concerning the evil he had said he would do to them, and he did not do it.

jon.4.text: But it was evil to Jonah — a great evil — and his anger burned.

And he prayed to YHWH and said, "Please, YHWH — was this not my word while I was still on my own soil? That is why I fled beforehand to Tarshish; for I knew that you are a God gracious and compassionate, slow to anger and abounding in steadfast love, and relenting from evil.
And now, YHWH, take my life from me, I pray — for my death is better than my life."
And YHWH said, "Is it good for your anger to burn?"
And Jonah went out of the city and sat down east of the city, and made himself a booth there, and sat under it in the shade — until he should see what would become of the city.
And YHWH God appointed a qiqayon plant, and it grew up over Jonah to be a shade over his head, to deliver him from his misery. And Jonah rejoiced over the plant — a great rejoicing.
But God appointed a worm at the rising of dawn the next day, and it struck the plant, and it withered.
And when the sun rose, God appointed a scorching east wind; and the sun struck Jonah's head, and he grew faint. And he asked for his life to die, and said, "My death is better than my life."
And God said to Jonah, "Is it good for your anger to burn over the plant?" And he said, "It is good for my anger to burn — even unto death."
And YHWH said, "You had pity on the plant, for which you did not labor and which you did not grow — which came up in a night and perished in a night.
And should I not have pity on Nineveh, that great city, in which there are more than a hundred and twenty thousand human beings who do not know their right hand from their left — and many animals?"

mic.1.text: The word of YHWH that came to Micah of Moresheth in the days of Jotham, Ahaz, and Hezekiah, kings of Judah — which he saw concerning Samaria and Jerusalem.
Hear, O peoples, all of you; listen, O earth and all that fills you: let the Lord YHWH be witness against you — the Lord from his holy temple.
For behold, YHWH is going out from his place; he will come down and tread upon the high places of the earth.
And the mountains will melt beneath him, and the valleys split open — like wax before the fire, like waters poured down a slope.

All this for the transgression of Jacob, and for the sins of the house of Israel. What is the transgression of Jacob — is it not Samaria? And what are the high places of Judah — are they not Jerusalem?
So I will make Samaria a heap of rubble in the field, a place for planting vineyards; I will pour her stones down into the valley and lay bare her foundations.

All her carved images shall be beaten to pieces, all her harlot's wages burned with fire, and all her idols I will lay waste; for from a harlot's wage she gathered them, and to a harlot's wage they shall return.
For this I will lament and wail; I will go barefoot and stripped. I will make lament like the jackals, and mourning like the daughters of the ostrich.
For her wound is incurable; it has come as far as Judah; it has reached the gate of my people — as far as Jerusalem.
Tell it not in Gath; weep not at all. In Beth-le-aphrah roll yourself in the dust.
Pass on your way, dweller of Shaphir, in nakedness and shame. The dweller of Zaanan does not come out; the lament of Beth-ha-ezel takes from you its standing place.
For the dweller of Maroth writhes for good — because evil has come down from YHWH to the gate of Jerusalem.
Harness the chariot to the steeds, dweller of Lachish — she was the beginning of sin to the daughter of Zion, for in you were found the transgressions of Israel.
Therefore you shall give parting gifts to Moresheth-gath; the houses of Achzib shall be a deception to the kings of Israel.
I will yet bring the dispossessor to you, dweller of Mareshah; the glory of Israel shall come to Adullam.
Make yourself bald and cut off your hair for the children of your delight; make your baldness broad as the vulture's — for they have gone from you into exile.

mic.2.text: Woe to those who devise wickedness and work evil upon their beds! In the light of morning they do it — because it is in the power of their hand.
They covet fields and seize them, houses — and carry them off; they oppress a man and his house, a person and his inheritance.
Therefore thus says YHWH: Behold, I am devising evil against this family, from which you shall not remove your necks, nor walk haughtily — for it will be an evil time.

On that day a taunt shall be raised over you, and a bitter lament lamented: "We are utterly ruined! He exchanges the portion of my people. How he removes it from me! To the apostate he apportions our fields."
Therefore you shall have no one casting the measuring line by lot in the assembly of YHWH.
"Do not preach!" — so they preach — "they should not preach of such things; disgrace will not overtake us."
Should it be said, O house of Jacob, "Is the spirit of YHWH shortened? Are these his doings?" Do not my words do good to the one who walks upright?
But of late my people has risen up as an enemy: you strip the cloak from off the garment of those who pass by trustingly, returning from war.
The women of my people you drive out from their delightful houses; from their little children you take away my splendor forever.
Arise and go — for this is no resting place; because of uncleanness it destroys, and the destruction is grievous.
If a man walking in wind and falsehood should lie, "I will preach to you of wine and strong drink" — he would be the preacher for this people!

I will surely gather all of you, O Jacob; I will surely assemble the remnant of Israel. I will set them together like sheep in a fold, like a flock in the midst of its pasture — it will be noisy with people.
The breaker goes up before them; they break through, and pass the gate, and go out by it. Their king passes on before them — and YHWH at their head.

mic.3.text: And I said: Hear now, you heads of Jacob and rulers of the house of Israel — is it not for you to know justice?
You who hate the good and love the evil, who tear the skin from off my people and the flesh from off their bones,
who eat the flesh of my people, and flay their skin from off them, and break their bones in pieces, and spread them out as in a pot — like meat inside a cauldron.
Then they will cry to YHWH, and he will not answer them; he will hide his face from them at that time, according as they have made their deeds evil.

Thus says YHWH concerning the prophets who lead my people astray — who, when they have something to bite with their teeth, proclaim "Peace," but against him who puts nothing into their mouths they consecrate war:
Therefore night shall be yours, without vision, and darkness yours, without divination. The sun shall go down on the prophets, and the day grow black over them.
The seers shall be ashamed and the diviners disgraced; they shall all cover their lips — for there is no answer from God.
But as for me — I am filled with power, with the spirit of YHWH, and with justice and might, to declare to Jacob his transgression and to Israel his sin.
Hear this now, you heads of the house of Jacob and rulers of the house of Israel, who abhor justice and twist all that is straight,
building Zion with blood, and Jerusalem with injustice.
Her heads give judgment for a bribe; her priests teach for a price; her prophets divine for silver — yet they lean on YHWH, saying, "Is not YHWH in our midst? No evil shall come upon us."
Therefore, because of you, Zion shall be plowed as a field; Jerusalem shall become heaps of ruins, and the mountain of the house a high place of forest.

mic.4.text: And it shall come to pass in the last days: the mountain of the house of YHWH shall be established at the head of the mountains, and lifted above the hills — and peoples shall stream to it.

And many nations shall go and say, "Come, let us go up to the mountain of YHWH, to the house of the God of Jacob, that he may teach us his ways, and we may walk in his paths." For out of Zion shall go forth instruction, and the word of YHWH from Jerusalem.

And he shall judge between many peoples and arbitrate for mighty nations far away; and they shall beat their swords into plowshares, and their spears into pruning-hooks. Nation shall not lift sword against nation, and they shall learn war no more.
And each shall sit under his own vine and under his own fig tree, and none shall make them afraid — for the mouth of YHWH of hosts has spoken.
For all the peoples walk each in the name of its own god — but we will walk in the name of YHWH our God forever and ever.
On that day — the word of YHWH — I will gather the lame, and assemble the outcast, and those I have afflicted;
and I will make the lame a remnant, and the far-scattered a mighty nation. And YHWH will reign over them on Mount Zion, from now on and forever.
And you, O tower of the flock, hill of the daughter of Zion — to you it shall come; the former dominion shall come, the kingdom of the daughter of Jerusalem.
Now why do you cry aloud? Is there no king in you? Has your counselor perished, that pangs have seized you like a woman in labor?
