Writhe and burst forth, daughter of Zion, like a woman in labor — for now you shall go out of the city and dwell in the field; and you shall come to Babylon. There you shall be rescued; there YHWH will redeem you from the grasp of your enemies.
And now many nations are gathered against you, saying, "Let her be defiled, and let our eyes gaze upon Zion."
But they do not know the thoughts of YHWH; they do not understand his counsel — that he has gathered them like sheaves to the threshing floor.

Arise and thresh, daughter of Zion! For I will make your horn iron and your hooves bronze, and you shall crush many peoples; and I will devote their gain to YHWH, and their wealth to the Lord of all the earth.
Now muster your troops, daughter of troops; siege is laid against us. With a rod they strike the judge of Israel on the cheek.

mic.5.text: And you, Bethlehem Ephrathah — little to be among the thousands of Judah — from you shall come forth for me one to be ruler in Israel, whose goings forth are from of old, from days everlasting.
Therefore he shall give them up until the time when she who is in labor has given birth; then the rest of his brothers shall return to the children of Israel.
And he shall stand and shepherd in the strength of YHWH, in the majesty of the name of YHWH his God. And they shall dwell secure — for now he shall be great to the ends of the earth.
And this one shall be peace. When Assyria comes into our land and treads in our citadels, we will raise against him seven shepherds and eight princes of men;
and they shall shepherd the land of Assyria with the sword, and the land of Nimrod at its gates; and he shall deliver from Assyria when he comes into our land and treads within our border.
And the remnant of Jacob shall be, in the midst of many peoples, like dew from YHWH, like showers upon the grass — which wait for no man, nor tarry for the children of men.

And the remnant of Jacob shall be among the nations, in the midst of many peoples, like a lion among the beasts of the forest, like a young lion among flocks of sheep — which, if it passes through, tramples and tears, and there is none to deliver.
Your hand shall be lifted over your adversaries, and all your enemies shall be cut off.
And on that day — the word of YHWH — I will cut off your horses from your midst and destroy your chariots;
and I will cut off the cities of your land and tear down all your fortresses;
and I will cut off sorceries from your hand, and you shall have no more soothsayers;
and I will cut off your carved images and your sacred pillars from your midst, and you shall bow down no more to the work of your hands;
and I will uproot your Asherim from your midst and demolish your cities.
And in anger and wrath I will execute vengeance on the nations that have not listened.

mic.6.text: Hear now what YHWH is saying: Arise, plead your case before the mountains, and let the hills hear your voice.
Hear, O mountains, the case of YHWH — and you enduring foundations of the earth. For YHWH has a case against his people; with Israel he will contend.
"My people, what have I done to you? And how have I wearied you? Answer me!
For I brought you up from the land of Egypt, and from the house of slaves I redeemed you; and I sent before you Moses, Aaron, and Miriam.
My people, remember now what Balak king of Moab devised, and what Balaam son of Beor answered him — from Shittim to Gilgal — that you may know the righteous acts of YHWH."
With what shall I come before YHWH, and bow myself before God on high? Shall I come before him with burnt offerings, with calves a year old?
Will YHWH be pleased with thousands of rams, with ten thousand rivers of oil? Shall I give my firstborn for my transgression — the fruit of my body for the sin of my soul?
He has told you, O human, what is good — and what does YHWH require of you but to do justice, and to love steadfast kindness, and to walk humbly with your God?
The voice of YHWH calls to the city — and sound wisdom sees your name: "Hear, O tribe — and who appointed it?
Are there still, in the house of the wicked, the treasures of wickedness — and the scant measure that is accursed?
Shall I be pure with wicked scales, and with a bag of deceitful weights?
Her rich men are full of violence; her inhabitants speak falsehood, and their tongue is deceit in their mouth.
So I also have made you sick, striking you down — laying you desolate because of your sins.
You shall eat and not be satisfied, and your emptiness shall be within you; you shall carry away and not save — and what you save I will give to the sword.
You shall sow and not reap; you shall tread the olive and not anoint yourself with oil, and the new wine — but you shall drink no wine.

For the statutes of Omri are kept, and all the works of the house of Ahab, and you walk in their counsels — so that I make you a desolation, and her inhabitants a hissing; and you shall bear the reproach of my people."

mic.7.text: Woe is me! For I have become like the gatherings of summer fruit, like the gleanings of the vintage: there is no cluster to eat, no early fig my soul desires.
The faithful one has perished from the earth, and there is no one upright among men. They all lie in wait for blood; each hunts his brother with a net.
Their hands are on evil, to do it well: the prince demands, and the judge — for payment; and the great man speaks the craving of his soul — and together they weave it.
The best of them is like a brier; the most upright, worse than a thorn hedge. The day of your watchmen — your visitation — has come; now shall be their confusion.
Put no trust in a neighbor; have no confidence in a close friend. From her who lies in your bosom, guard the doors of your mouth.
For son treats father with contempt; daughter rises up against her mother, daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law — a man's enemies are the people of his own house.
But as for me, I will watch for YHWH; I will wait for the God of my salvation. My God will hear me.
Do not rejoice over me, O my enemy. Though I have fallen, I rise; though I sit in darkness, YHWH is a light to me.
I will bear the indignation of YHWH — for I have sinned against him — until he pleads my case and executes judgment for me. He will bring me out to the light; I shall look upon his righteousness.
Then my enemy will see, and shame will cover her — she who said to me, "Where is YHWH your God?" My eyes will look upon her; now she will be trampled like the mire of the streets.
A day for building your walls! On that day the boundary shall be far extended.
On that day they shall come to you from Assyria and the cities of Egypt, and from Egypt to the River, from sea to sea and mountain to mountain.
And the earth shall be a desolation because of its inhabitants, from the fruit of their deeds.
Shepherd your people with your staff, the flock of your inheritance, who dwell alone in a forest in the midst of a garden land; let them graze in Bashan and Gilead as in the days of old.
As in the days when you came out of the land of Egypt, I will show him wonders.
The nations shall see and be ashamed of all their might; they shall lay hand on mouth; their ears shall be deaf.
They shall lick dust like the serpent, like the crawling things of the earth; they shall come trembling out of their strongholds. To YHWH our God they shall turn in dread — and they shall fear you.
Who is a God like you — bearing iniquity and passing over transgression for the remnant of his inheritance? He does not hold fast his anger forever, for he delights in steadfast love.
He will turn again and have compassion on us; he will subdue our iniquities. And you will cast all their sins into the depths of the sea.
You will give truth to Jacob, steadfast love to Abraham — as you swore to our fathers from the days of old.

amo.1.text: The words of Amos, who was among the herdsmen of Tekoa — what he saw concerning Israel in the days of Uzziah king of Judah and in the days of Jeroboam son of Joash king of Israel, two years before the earthquake.
And he said: YHWH roars from Zion; from Jerusalem he gives his voice. The pastures of the shepherds mourn, and the crown of Carmel withers.
Thus says YHWH: For three transgressions of Damascus, and for four, I will not turn it back — because they threshed Gilead with sledges of iron.
So I will send fire into the house of Hazael, and it shall devour the citadels of Ben-hadad.
And I will break the bar of Damascus, and cut off the enthroned from the Valley of Aven, and the scepter-holder from Beth-eden; and the people of Aram shall go into exile to Kir, says YHWH.
Thus says YHWH: For three transgressions of Gaza, and for four, I will not turn it back — because they carried a whole people into exile, to hand them over to Edom.
So I will send fire against the wall of Gaza, and it shall devour her citadels.
And I will cut off the enthroned from Ashdod, and the scepter-holder from Ashkelon; I will turn my hand against Ekron — and the remnant of the Philistines shall perish, says the Lord YHWH.
Thus says YHWH: For three transgressions of Tyre, and for four, I will not turn it back — because they handed over a whole people in exile to Edom, and did not remember the covenant of brothers.
So I will send fire against the wall of Tyre, and it shall devour her citadels.

Thus says YHWH: For three transgressions of Edom, and for four, I will not turn it back — because he pursued his brother with the sword and destroyed his own compassion; his anger tore perpetually, and he kept his fury forever.
So I will send fire into Teman, and it shall devour the citadels of Bozrah.
Thus says YHWH: For three transgressions of the sons of Ammon, and for four, I will not turn it back — because they ripped open the pregnant women of Gilead, to enlarge their own border.
So I will kindle fire against the wall of Rabbah, and it shall devour her citadels — with a war-cry on the day of battle, with a tempest on the day of the whirlwind.
And their king shall go into exile — he and his princes together, says YHWH.

amo.2.text: Thus says YHWH: For three transgressions of Moab, and for four, I will not turn it back — because he burned the bones of the king of Edom into lime.
So I will send fire into Moab, and it shall devour the citadels of Kerioth; and Moab shall die amid uproar, with a war-cry, with the sound of the shofar.
And I will cut off the judge from her midst, and all her princes I will slay with him, says YHWH.

Thus says YHWH: For three transgressions of Judah, and for four, I will not turn it back — because they rejected the law of YHWH and did not keep his statutes; their lies led them astray, after which their fathers walked.
So I will send fire into Judah, and it shall devour the citadels of Jerusalem.
Thus says YHWH: For three transgressions of Israel, and for four, I will not turn it back — because they sell the righteous for silver, and the needy for a pair of sandals.
They who trample the head of the poor into the dust of the earth, and turn aside the way of the lowly; a man and his father go in to the same girl, so that my holy name is profaned.
They stretch themselves out beside every altar on garments taken in pledge, and in the house of their God they drink the wine of the fined.
Yet it was I who destroyed the Amorite before them, whose height was like the height of the cedars, and who was strong as the oaks; I destroyed his fruit above and his roots beneath.
And it was I who brought you up from the land of Egypt, and led you forty years in the wilderness, to possess the land of the Amorite.
And I raised up prophets from among your sons, and Nazirites from among your young men. Is it not so, O children of Israel? — the word of YHWH.
But you made the Nazirites drink wine, and commanded the prophets, saying, "You shall not prophesy."
Behold, I am pressing you down in your place, as a cart presses down when full of sheaves.
Flight shall perish from the swift; the strong shall not summon his strength, and the warrior shall not save his life;
the archer shall not stand, and the swift of foot shall not escape, and the horseman shall not save his life;
and the stoutest-hearted among the warriors shall flee naked on that day — the word of YHWH.

amo.3.text: Hear this word that YHWH has spoken against you, O children of Israel — against the whole family that I brought up from the land of Egypt:
You only have I known of all the families of the earth; therefore I will visit upon you all your iniquities.
Do two walk together unless they have made an appointment?
Does a lion roar in the forest when he has no prey? Does a young lion give his voice from his den unless he has caught something?
Does a bird fall into a snare on the ground when there is no trap for it? Does a snare spring up from the soil and take nothing at all?
Is a shofar blown in a city, and the people do not tremble? Does evil befall a city, and YHWH has not done it?
Surely the Lord YHWH does nothing without revealing his secret counsel to his servants the prophets.
The lion has roared — who will not fear? The Lord YHWH has spoken — who will not prophesy?
Proclaim it upon the citadels in Ashdod, and upon the citadels in the land of Egypt, and say: Gather on the mountains of Samaria and see the great tumults within her, and the oppressed in her midst.
"They do not know how to do right" — the word of YHWH — "they who store up violence and devastation in their citadels."
Therefore thus says the Lord YHWH: An adversary shall surround the land; he shall bring down your strength from you, and your citadels shall be plundered.

Thus says YHWH: As the shepherd rescues from the mouth of the lion two legs or a piece of an ear, so shall the children of Israel be rescued — those who sit in Samaria on the corner of a couch, on the damask of a bed.
Hear, and testify against the house of Jacob — the word of the Lord YHWH, the God of hosts —
that on the day I visit the transgressions of Israel upon him, I will visit the altars of Bethel: the horns of the altar shall be cut off and fall to the ground.
And I will strike the winter house along with the summer house; the houses of ivory shall perish, and the great houses shall come to an end — the word of YHWH.

amo.4.text: Hear this word, you cows of Bashan on the mountain of Samaria — who oppress the poor, who crush the needy, who say to their lords, "Bring, that we may drink!"
The Lord YHWH has sworn by his holiness: behold, days are coming upon you when they shall carry you off with hooks — and the last of you with fishhooks.
And through the breaches you shall go out, each straight ahead of her, and you shall be flung out toward Harmon — the word of YHWH.
Come to Bethel — and transgress! To Gilgal — and multiply transgression! Bring your sacrifices in the morning, your tithes every three days;
burn a thank-offering of leavened bread, and proclaim freewill offerings — announce them! For so you love to do, O children of Israel — the word of the Lord YHWH.
"And I for my part gave you cleanness of teeth in all your cities, and lack of bread in all your places — yet you did not return to me" — the word of YHWH.

"And I for my part withheld the rain from you while there were still three months to the harvest; I would send rain on one city and on another city send no rain; one field would be rained on, and the field on which it did not rain would wither.
So two or three cities staggered to one city to drink water, and were not satisfied — yet you did not return to me" — the word of YHWH.
"I struck you with blight and mildew; the locust devoured your many gardens and vineyards, your fig trees and your olive trees — yet you did not return to me" — the word of YHWH.

"I sent pestilence among you in the manner of Egypt; I slew your young men with the sword, along with your captured horses; I made the stench of your camps rise into your nostrils — yet you did not return to me" — the word of YHWH.
"I overthrew some of you, as God overthrew Sodom and Gomorrah, and you were like a brand snatched from the burning — yet you did not return to me" — the word of YHWH.
Therefore thus will I do to you, O Israel — and because I will do this to you: prepare to meet your God, O Israel!

For behold: he who forms the mountains and creates the wind, who declares to man what his thought is, who makes dawn into darkness and treads on the high places of the earth — YHWH, God of hosts, is his name.

amo.5.text: Hear this word that I take up over you — a lament, O house of Israel:
Fallen, no more to rise, is the virgin Israel; forsaken on her own soil, with none to raise her up.
For thus says the Lord YHWH: The city that marched out a thousand shall have a hundred left; and the one that marched out a hundred shall have ten left — for the house of Israel.
For thus says YHWH to the house of Israel: Seek me — and live.
But do not seek Bethel; do not come to Gilgal; do not cross over to Beersheba. For Gilgal shall surely go into exile, and Bethel shall come to nothing.
Seek YHWH — and live; lest he break out like fire against the house of Joseph, and it devour, with none to quench it for Bethel.
You who turn justice to wormwood, and cast righteousness to the ground!

He who made the Pleiades and Orion, who turns deep darkness into morning and darkens day into night, who calls to the waters of the sea and pours them out upon the face of the earth — YHWH is his name —
who flashes destruction upon the strong, so that destruction comes upon the fortress.
They hate the one who reproves in the gate; they abhor the one who speaks with integrity.

Therefore, because you trample on the poor and take from him exactions of grain: you have built houses of hewn stone — but you shall not dwell in them; you have planted delightful vineyards — but you shall not drink their wine.
For I know how many are your transgressions, and how mighty your sins — you who harass the righteous, who take a bribe, who turn aside the needy in the gate.
Therefore the prudent keeps silent at such a time — for it is an evil time.
Seek good and not evil, that you may live; and so YHWH, God of hosts, will be with you — as you have said.
Hate evil and love good, and set up justice in the gate. Perhaps YHWH, God of hosts, will be gracious to the remnant of Joseph.

Therefore thus says YHWH, God of hosts, the Lord: In all the squares — wailing; in all the streets they shall say, "Alas! Alas!" They shall call the farmer to mourning, and wailing to those skilled in lamentation;
and in all the vineyards — wailing. For I will pass through your midst, says YHWH.
Woe to those who long for the day of YHWH! What is the day of YHWH to you? It is darkness, and not light —
as when a man flees from the lion, and the bear meets him; and he comes into the house and leans his hand on the wall — and the serpent bites him.
Is not the day of YHWH darkness, and not light — pitch dark, with no brightness in it?
I hate, I despise your feasts; I take no delight in your solemn assemblies.
Even if you offer me burnt offerings and your grain offerings, I will not accept them; the peace-offering of your fattened animals I will not look at.
Take away from me the noise of your songs; the melody of your harps I will not hear.
But let justice roll down like waters, and righteousness like an ever-flowing stream.
Did you bring me sacrifices and offerings the forty years in the wilderness, O house of Israel?
And you shall take up Sikkuth your king and Kiyyun your images — the star of your gods that you made for yourselves —
and I will send you into exile beyond Damascus, says YHWH — God of hosts is his name.

amo.6.text: Woe to those at ease in Zion, and to those secure on the mountain of Samaria — the notables of the first of the nations, to whom the house of Israel comes!
Cross over to Calneh and see; go from there to great Hamath; then go down to Gath of the Philistines. Are they better than these kingdoms? Is their territory greater than your territory?
You who push away the evil day — yet bring near a reign of violence!
They lie on beds of ivory and sprawl upon their couches; they eat lambs from the flock and calves from the midst of the stall;
they strum on the mouth of the harp — like David they devise for themselves instruments of song;
they drink from bowls of wine and anoint themselves with the finest oils — but they are not sickened over the ruin of Joseph.
Therefore now they shall go into exile at the head of the exiles, and the sprawlers' revelry shall pass away.
The Lord YHWH has sworn by himself — the word of YHWH, God of hosts: I abhor the pride of Jacob, and I hate his citadels; and I will deliver up the city and all that fills it.
And it shall be: if ten men are left in one house — they shall die.

And when a man's kinsman, the one who burns the dead, lifts him up to carry the bones out of the house, and says to the one in the innermost part of the house, "Is anyone still with you?" and he says, "No one" — then he shall say, "Hush! For we must not invoke the name of YHWH."
For behold, YHWH commands: the great house shall be struck into fragments, and the little house into splinters.
Do horses run on rock? Does one plow it with oxen? Yet you have turned justice into poison, and the fruit of righteousness into wormwood —
you who rejoice in Lo-debar — a nothing — who say, "Have we not taken Karnaim for ourselves by our own strength?"
For behold, I am raising up against you, O house of Israel — the word of YHWH, God of hosts — a nation; and they shall oppress you from Lebo-hamath to the wadi of the Arabah.

amo.7.text: Thus the Lord YHWH showed me: behold, he was forming locusts at the beginning of the late growth's coming up — and behold, it was the late growth after the king's mowings.
And when they had finished eating the herbage of the land, I said, "Lord YHWH, forgive, I pray! How can Jacob stand? For he is small."
YHWH relented concerning this. "It shall not be," said YHWH.
Thus the Lord YHWH showed me: behold, the Lord YHWH was calling for a contest by fire; and it devoured the great deep, and was devouring the portion of land.
Then I said, "Lord YHWH, cease, I pray! How can Jacob stand? For he is small."
YHWH relented concerning this. "This also shall not be," said the Lord YHWH.
Thus he showed me: behold, the Lord standing upon a wall of plumb, with a plumb line in his hand.
And YHWH said to me, "What do you see, Amos?" And I said, "A plumb line." And the Lord said, "Behold, I am setting a plumb line in the midst of my people Israel; I will not pass by them again.
The high places of Isaac shall be made desolate, and the sanctuaries of Israel laid waste; and I will rise against the house of Jeroboam with the sword."
Then Amaziah, the priest of Bethel, sent to Jeroboam king of Israel, saying, "Amos has conspired against you in the midst of the house of Israel; the land cannot bear all his words.
For thus Amos has said: 'Jeroboam shall die by the sword, and Israel shall surely go into exile away from his land.'"
And Amaziah said to Amos, "Seer, go — flee to the land of Judah. Eat bread there, and prophesy there.
But at Bethel you shall prophesy no more — for it is the king's sanctuary, and a house of the kingdom."
And Amos answered and said to Amaziah, "I am no prophet, nor a prophet's son — I am a herdsman, and a dresser of sycamore figs.
But YHWH took me from behind the flock, and YHWH said to me, 'Go, prophesy to my people Israel.'
And now, hear the word of YHWH. You say, 'Do not prophesy against Israel; do not preach against the house of Isaac.'

Therefore thus says YHWH: 'Your wife shall become a harlot in the city, and your sons and daughters shall fall by the sword; your land shall be divided by the measuring line, and you yourself shall die on unclean soil — and Israel shall surely go into exile away from his land.'"

amo.8.text: Thus the Lord YHWH showed me: behold, a basket of summer fruit.
And he said, "What do you see, Amos?" And I said, "A basket of summer fruit." And YHWH said to me, "The end has come upon my people Israel; I will not pass by them again.
The songs of the palace shall become wailing on that day" — the word of the Lord YHWH. "Many the corpses — flung everywhere. Hush!"
Hear this, you who trample the needy, and would bring the poor of the land to an end,
saying, "When will the new moon pass, that we may sell grain — and the sabbath, that we may open the wheat — making the ephah small and the shekel great, and bending the scales of deceit,
buying the poor for silver and the needy for a pair of sandals — and selling the sweepings of the wheat?"
YHWH has sworn by the pride of Jacob: Surely I will never forget any of their deeds.
Shall not the land tremble for this, and all who dwell in it mourn? Shall it not all rise like the Nile, be tossed about, and sink again like the Nile of Egypt?
"And it shall come to pass on that day" — the word of the Lord YHWH — "I will make the sun go down at noon, and darken the earth in broad daylight.

I will turn your feasts into mourning and all your songs into lamentation; I will bring sackcloth upon every loin and baldness upon every head. I will make it like mourning for an only son — and its end like a bitter day.
Behold, days are coming" — the word of the Lord YHWH — "when I will send a famine on the land: not a famine of bread, nor a thirst for water, but of hearing the words of YHWH.
And they shall wander from sea to sea, and from north to east; they shall roam to and fro seeking the word of YHWH — and they shall not find it.
On that day the beautiful young women and the young men shall faint from thirst —
those who swear by the Guilt of Samaria and say, 'As your god lives, O Dan,' and 'As the way of Beersheba lives' — they shall fall, and never rise again."

amo.9.text: I saw the Lord standing upon the altar, and he said: "Strike the capital, so that the thresholds shake — and break them on the heads of all of them; and their last remnant I will slay with the sword. Not one of them fleeing shall flee away; not one of their fugitives shall escape.
Though they dig into Sheol, from there my hand shall take them; though they climb up to the heavens, from there I will bring them down.

Though they hide on the summit of Carmel, there I will search them out and take them; though they conceal themselves from before my eyes on the floor of the sea, there I will command the serpent, and it shall bite them.
Though they go into captivity before their enemies, there I will command the sword, and it shall slay them. And I will set my eye upon them — for evil, and not for good."
And the Lord YHWH of hosts — he who touches the earth and it melts, and all who dwell in it mourn; and it all rises like the Nile and sinks like the Nile of Egypt;
who builds his upper chambers in the heavens and founds his vault upon the earth; who calls to the waters of the sea and pours them out upon the face of the earth — YHWH is his name.

"Are you not like the children of the Cushites to me, O children of Israel?" — the word of YHWH. "Did I not bring Israel up from the land of Egypt — and the Philistines from Caphtor, and Aram from Kir?
Behold, the eyes of the Lord YHWH are upon the sinful kingdom, and I will destroy it from the face of the earth — except that I will not utterly destroy the house of Jacob" — the word of YHWH.
"For behold, I am commanding, and I will shake the house of Israel among all the nations as one shakes with a sieve — and not a pebble falls to the ground.
By the sword shall die all the sinners of my people who say, 'The evil shall not draw near or confront us.'
On that day I will raise up the fallen booth of David; I will wall up its breaches and raise up his ruins, and I will build it as in the days of old,
so that they may possess the remnant of Edom and all the nations over whom my name is called" — the word of YHWH, who does this.

"Behold, days are coming" — the word of YHWH — "when the plowman shall overtake the reaper, and the treader of grapes the one who sows the seed; the mountains shall drip sweet wine, and all the hills shall melt with it.

And I will restore the fortunes of my people Israel: they shall rebuild the desolate cities and dwell in them; they shall plant vineyards and drink their wine; they shall make gardens and eat their fruit.
And I will plant them upon their own soil, and they shall never again be uprooted from their soil that I have given them," says YHWH your God.

hos.1.text: The word of YHWH that came to Hosea son of Beeri, in the days of Uzziah, Jotham, Ahaz, and Hezekiah, kings of Judah, and in the days of Jeroboam son of Joash, king of Israel.
The beginning of YHWH's speaking through Hosea. And YHWH said to Hosea: "Go, take for yourself a wife of harlotries, and children of harlotries — for the land commits great harlotry, away from YHWH."
So he went and took Gomer, daughter of Diblaim; and she conceived and bore him a son.
And YHWH said to him: "Call his name Jezreel — for yet a little while, and I will visit the blood of Jezreel upon the house of Jehu, and I will put an end to the kingdom of the house of Israel.
And on that day I will break the bow of Israel in the Valley of Jezreel."
And she conceived again and bore a daughter. And he said to him: "Call her name Lo-ruhamah — Not-Pitied — for I will no longer have pity on the house of Israel, that I should forgive them at all.
But on the house of Judah I will have pity, and I will save them by YHWH their God; I will not save them by bow or sword or battle, by horses or horsemen."
And when she had weaned Lo-ruhamah, she conceived and bore a son.
And he said: "Call his name Lo-ammi — Not-My-People — for you are not my people, and I am not yours."

hos.2.text: Yet the number of the children of Israel shall be like the sand of the sea, which cannot be measured or counted; and in the place where it was said to them, "You are not my people," it shall be said to them, "Children of the living God."
And the children of Judah and the children of Israel shall be gathered together, and they shall appoint for themselves one head, and go up out of the land — for great shall be the day of Jezreel.
Say to your brothers, "Ammi" — My People — and to your sisters, "Ruhamah" — Pitied.
Contend with your mother — contend! For she is not my wife, and I am not her husband. Let her put away her harlotries from her face, and her adulteries from between her breasts,
lest I strip her naked and set her out as on the day she was born; lest I make her like the wilderness, and set her like a parched land, and kill her with thirst.
And on her children I will have no pity, for they are children of harlotries.

For their mother has played the harlot; she who conceived them has acted shamefully. For she said, "I will go after my lovers, who give me my bread and my water, my wool and my flax, my oil and my drink."
Therefore, behold, I am hedging her way with thorns; I will wall up her wall, and she shall not find her paths.
She shall pursue her lovers and not overtake them; she shall seek them and not find them. Then she shall say, "I will go and return to my first husband, for it was better for me then than now."
But she did not know that it was I who gave her the grain, the new wine, and the oil — I who multiplied her silver, and the gold they made into the Baal.
Therefore I will turn and take back my grain in its time and my new wine in its season; and I will snatch away my wool and my flax, given to cover her nakedness.
And now I will uncover her shame before the eyes of her lovers, and no one shall deliver her from my hand.
And I will put an end to all her rejoicing — her feast, her new moon, her sabbath, and all her appointed seasons.
And I will lay waste her vine and her fig tree, of which she said, "These are my wages, which my lovers gave me." I will make them a thicket, and the beasts of the field shall devour them.

And I will visit upon her the days of the Baals, when she burned incense to them — when she decked herself with her ring and her jewelry and went after her lovers; but me she forgot — the word of YHWH.
Therefore, behold, I am going to woo her: I will lead her into the wilderness and speak to her heart.

And from there I will give her back her vineyards, and make the Valley of Achor — of Trouble — a door of hope. And there she shall answer as in the days of her youth, as on the day she came up from the land of Egypt.
"And on that day" — the word of YHWH — "you shall call me 'my husband,' and no longer call me 'my Baal.'
For I will remove the names of the Baals from her mouth, and they shall be remembered by their name no more.

And on that day I will cut for them a covenant with the beasts of the field, with the birds of the heavens and the creeping things of the ground; bow and sword and war I will break from the earth, and I will make them lie down in safety.
And I will betroth you to me forever; I will betroth you to me in righteousness and in justice, in steadfast love and in compassion;
I will betroth you to me in faithfulness — and you shall know YHWH.
"And on that day I will answer" — the word of YHWH — "I will answer the heavens, and they shall answer the earth,
and the earth shall answer the grain, the new wine, and the oil — and they shall answer Jezreel.
And I will sow her for myself in the land, and I will have pity on Not-Pitied; and I will say to Not-My-People, 'You are my people' — and he shall say, 'My God.'"

hos.3.text: And YHWH said to me: "Go again, love a woman loved by another and an adulteress — as YHWH loves the children of Israel, though they turn to other gods and love raisin-cakes."
So I bought her for myself for fifteen pieces of silver, and a homer of barley, and a half-homer of barley.
And I said to her: "You shall sit still for me many days. You shall not play the harlot, and you shall not belong to a man — and I likewise toward you."
For the children of Israel shall sit many days without king and without prince, without sacrifice and without pillar, without ephod or teraphim.
Afterward the children of Israel shall return and seek YHWH their God and David their king; and they shall come in dread to YHWH and to his goodness, in the last days.

hos.4.text: Hear the word of YHWH, O children of Israel — for YHWH has a case against the inhabitants of the land: there is no truth, no steadfast love, no knowledge of God in the land.
Swearing and lying, murdering and stealing and committing adultery — they break out, and blood touches blood.
Therefore the land mourns, and all who dwell in it languish — with the beasts of the field and the birds of the heavens; even the fish of the sea are swept away.
Yet let no one contend, and let none reprove — for your people are like those who contend with a priest.
You shall stumble by day, and the prophet shall stumble with you by night — and I will destroy your mother.

My people are destroyed for lack of knowledge. Because you have rejected knowledge, I reject you from serving me as priest; since you have forgotten the law of your God, I also will forget your children.
The more they multiplied, the more they sinned against me; I will exchange their glory for shame.
They feed on the sin of my people; they set their appetite on their iniquity.
And it shall be: like people, like priest. I will visit his ways upon him and repay him his deeds.
They shall eat and not be satisfied; they shall play the harlot and not multiply — because they have forsaken YHWH, to keep
harlotry. Wine and new wine take away the heart.
My people inquires of its piece of wood, and its staff gives it oracles! For a spirit of harlotry has led them astray, and they have played the harlot away from their God.

On the tops of the mountains they sacrifice, and on the hills they burn incense — under oak and poplar and terebinth, because its shade is good. Therefore your daughters play the harlot, and your daughters-in-law commit adultery.

I will not punish your daughters when they play the harlot, nor your daughters-in-law when they commit adultery — for the men themselves go aside with harlots and sacrifice with temple-women; and a people without understanding comes to ruin.
Though you play the harlot, O Israel, let not Judah become guilty. Do not come to Gilgal; do not go up to Beth-aven; and do not swear, "As YHWH lives."
For like a stubborn heifer, Israel is stubborn. Can YHWH now feed them like a lamb in a broad pasture?
Ephraim is bound to idols — let him alone.
Their drink turns rebel; they play the harlot continually; her shields dearly love disgrace.
A wind has wrapped her in its wings, and they shall be ashamed of their sacrifices.

hos.5.text: Hear this, O priests! Attend, O house of Israel! Give ear, O house of the king! For the judgment is yours: you have been a snare at Mizpah, and a net spread out upon Tabor.
The revolters have gone deep into slaughter — and I am a rebuke to them all.
I know Ephraim, and Israel is not hidden from me; for now, Ephraim, you have played the harlot; Israel is defiled.
Their deeds do not permit them to return to their God — for a spirit of harlotry is within them, and YHWH they do not know.
The pride of Israel testifies to his face; Israel and Ephraim stumble in their iniquity — Judah also stumbles with them.
With their flocks and their herds they shall go to seek YHWH — and they shall not find him; he has withdrawn from them.
They have dealt treacherously with YHWH, for they have borne strange children. Now the new moon shall devour them with their portions.
Blow the shofar in Gibeah, the trumpet in Ramah! Sound the alarm at Beth-aven — behind you, O Benjamin!
Ephraim shall become a desolation on the day of rebuke; among the tribes of Israel I make known what is sure.
The princes of Judah have become like movers of a boundary-stone; upon them I will pour out my fury like water.
Ephraim is oppressed, crushed in judgment — because he was determined to walk after filth.
And I am like a moth to Ephraim, and like rot to the house of Judah.
When Ephraim saw his sickness, and Judah his sore, Ephraim went to Assyria and sent to the great king — but he cannot heal you, nor cure you of your sore.
For I am like a lion to Ephraim, and like a young lion to the house of Judah. I — I myself — will tear and go away; I will carry off, and none shall deliver.
I will go away and return to my place, until they bear their guilt and seek my face. In their distress they will seek me early.

hos.6.text: "Come, let us return to YHWH — for he has torn, and he will heal us; he has struck, and he will bind us up.
After two days he will revive us; on the third day he will raise us up, and we shall live before him.
Let us know — let us press on to know YHWH. His going forth is sure as the dawn; and he will come to us like the rain, like the latter rain that waters the earth."
What shall I do with you, Ephraim? What shall I do with you, Judah? Your steadfast love is like a morning cloud, like the dew that goes away early.
Therefore I have hewn them by the prophets; I have slain them by the words of my mouth — and my judgment goes forth like light.
For steadfast love I desire, and not sacrifice — the knowledge of God rather than burnt offerings.
But they, like Adam, transgressed the covenant; there they dealt treacherously with me.
Gilead is a city of workers of wickedness, tracked with blood.
And like bandits lying in wait for a man, a company of priests: they murder on the road to Shechem — yes, they have committed depravity.
In the house of Israel I have seen a horror: there is Ephraim's harlotry; Israel is defiled.
For you also, O Judah, a harvest is appointed — when I restore the fortunes of my people.

hos.7.text: When I would heal Israel, the iniquity of Ephraim is uncovered, and the evils of Samaria — for they practice falsehood; the thief comes in, and the raiding band strips outside.
And they do not say in their hearts that I remember all their evil. Now their deeds surround them; they are before my face.
By their evil they make the king glad, and the princes by their lies.
They are all adulterers — like an oven heated by the baker, who ceases stirring from the kneading of the dough until it is leavened.
On the day of our king, the princes became sick with the heat of wine; he stretched out his hand with the scoffers.
For like an oven their hearts draw near in their ambush; all night their baker sleeps — in the morning it burns like a blazing fire.
They are all hot as an oven, and they devour their judges; all their kings have fallen — and none among them calls to me.
Ephraim — he mixes himself with the peoples; Ephraim has become a cake not turned.
Strangers have devoured his strength, and he does not know it; gray hairs are sprinkled upon him, and he does not know it.
The pride of Israel testifies to his face — yet they have not returned to YHWH their God, nor sought him, for all this.
And Ephraim has become like a dove, easily deceived, without sense: they call to Egypt; they go to Assyria.
As they go, I will spread my net over them; like the birds of the heavens I will bring them down; I will discipline them according to the report to their assembly.
Woe to them, for they have strayed from me! Devastation upon them, for they have rebelled against me! And I — I would redeem them, but they speak lies against me.
They do not cry to me from their heart when they wail upon their beds. For grain and new wine they gash themselves — they turn away from me.
And it was I who trained and strengthened their arms — yet they devise evil against me.
They turn — but not upward; they are like a slack bow. Their princes shall fall by the sword for the insolence of their tongue — this shall be their derision in the land of Egypt.

hos.8.text: Set the shofar to your lips! Like an eagle over the house of YHWH — because they have transgressed my covenant and rebelled against my law.
To me they cry, "My God — we, Israel, know you!"
Israel has spurned the good; an enemy shall pursue him.
They made kings — but not from me; they set up princes — and I did not know it. With their silver and their gold they made themselves idols — so that it may be cut off.
Your calf is spurned, O Samaria! My anger burns against them. How long will they be incapable of innocence?
For it is from Israel — a craftsman made it, and it is no god. The calf of Samaria shall be broken to splinters.
For they sow the wind, and they shall reap the whirlwind. Standing grain it has none; the sprout yields no flour — and if it should yield, strangers would swallow it.
Israel is swallowed up; now they are among the nations like a vessel no one wants.
For they have gone up to Assyria — a wild donkey alone by itself; Ephraim has hired lovers.
Though they hire among the nations, now I will gather them up; and they shall begin to diminish under the burden of the king of princes.
Because Ephraim multiplied altars for sinning, they became for him altars for sinning.
Were I to write for him my law by the ten thousands, they would be regarded as something strange.
As sacrifices of my offerings they sacrifice flesh and eat it — YHWH does not accept them. Now he will remember their iniquity and visit their sins: they shall return to Egypt.
For Israel has forgotten his Maker and built palaces, and Judah has multiplied fortified cities; but I will send fire upon his cities, and it shall devour her citadels.

hos.9.text: Do not rejoice, O Israel, with exultation like the peoples! For you have played the harlot away from your God; you have loved a harlot's wage on every threshing floor of grain.
Threshing floor and wine vat shall not feed them, and the new wine shall fail her.
They shall not dwell in the land of YHWH; Ephraim shall return to Egypt, and in Assyria they shall eat what is unclean.

They shall not pour out wine to YHWH, and their sacrifices shall not please him. Theirs shall be like mourners' bread: all who eat it become unclean. For their bread is for their own hunger — it shall not come into the house of YHWH.
What will you do on the day of the appointed feast, on the day of the festival of YHWH?
For behold, they have gone from destruction — Egypt shall gather them; Memphis shall bury them. Nettles shall possess their treasures of silver; thorns shall be in their tents.

The days of visitation have come; the days of recompense have come — let Israel know it! "The prophet is a fool; the man of the spirit is mad" — because of the greatness of your iniquity, and the great hostility.
The watchman of Ephraim stands with my God — yet the prophet finds a fowler's snare on all his ways, hostility in the house of his God.
They have gone deep in corruption, as in the days of Gibeah. He will remember their iniquity; he will visit their sins.

Like grapes in the wilderness I found Israel; like the first fruit on a fig tree in its first season I saw your fathers. But they came to Baal-peor and consecrated themselves to Shame — and became detestable, like the thing they loved.
Ephraim — their glory shall fly away like a bird: no birth, no womb, no conception.
Even if they raise their children, I will bereave them till none is left. Yes, woe to them indeed when I turn away from them!
Ephraim, as I saw, was like Tyre, planted in a meadow — but Ephraim must lead out his children to the slayer.
Give them, YHWH — what will you give? Give them a miscarrying womb and shriveled breasts.
All their evil is at Gilgal; there indeed I came to hate them. For the evil of their deeds I will drive them from my house. I will love them no more; all their princes are rebels.
Ephraim is stricken; their root is dried up; they shall bear no fruit. Even if they give birth, I will slay the darlings of their womb.
My God will reject them because they have not listened to him — and they shall be wanderers among the nations.

hos.10.text: Israel is a luxuriant vine that yields its fruit. The more his fruit, the more altars he made; the better his land, the better they made the pillars.
Their heart is divided; now they shall bear their guilt. He will break the necks of their altars; he will destroy their pillars.
For now they will say: "We have no king — for we have not feared YHWH; and the king, what could he do for us?"
They speak mere words — swearing falsely, cutting covenants; so judgment springs up like poison-weed in the furrows of the field.
For the calves of Beth-aven the inhabitants of Samaria tremble; indeed its people mourn over it, and its idol-priests wail over it — over its glory, because it has gone into exile from it.
It too shall be carried to Assyria as tribute to the great king. Ephraim shall be seized with shame; Israel shall be ashamed of his own counsel.
Samaria's king shall be cut off, like a twig on the face of the waters.
The high places of Aven — the sin of Israel — shall be destroyed; thorn and thistle shall climb their altars. And they shall say to the mountains, "Cover us!" and to the hills, "Fall upon us!"
Since the days of Gibeah you have sinned, O Israel; there they took their stand. Shall not war overtake them in Gibeah, against the sons of injustice?
When I desire it, I will discipline them; and peoples shall be gathered against them, when they are bound for their double iniquity.
And Ephraim is a trained heifer that loves to thresh; and I myself passed over her fair neck — I will make Ephraim pull; Judah shall plow; Jacob shall harrow for himself.
Sow for yourselves righteousness; reap according to steadfast love; break up your fallow ground. For it is time to seek YHWH — until he comes and rains righteousness upon you.
You have plowed wickedness; you have reaped injustice; you have eaten the fruit of lies. Because you trusted in your own way, in the multitude of your warriors,

the tumult of war shall rise against your people, and all your fortresses shall be devastated — as Shalman devastated Beth-arbel on the day of battle, when mothers were dashed to pieces upon their children.
So shall it be done to you, O Bethel, because of your utter evil. At dawn the king of Israel shall be utterly cut off.

hos.11.text: When Israel was a child, I loved him — and out of Egypt I called my son.
The more they were called, the more they went away from them: they kept sacrificing to the Baals and burning incense to carved images.
Yet it was I who taught Ephraim to walk — taking them up in his arms; but they did not know that I healed them.
With human cords I drew them, with bands of love; and I became to them like those who lift the yoke from their jaws — and I bent down to him and fed him.
He shall not return to the land of Egypt — but Assyria, he shall be his king, because they refused to return.
The sword shall whirl in his cities and consume his bars and devour — because of their own counsels.
My people hang suspended on their turning from me; though they call him upward, none at all will rise.
How can I give you up, Ephraim? How can I hand you over, Israel? How can I make you like Admah — treat you like Zeboiim? My heart turns over within me; all my compassions grow hot together.
I will not execute my burning anger; I will not turn to destroy Ephraim. For I am God and not a man — the Holy One in your midst — and I will not come in wrath.
They shall walk after YHWH; like a lion he will roar. When he roars, then children shall come trembling from the west;
they shall come trembling like a bird from Egypt, like a dove from the land of Assyria — and I will settle them in their houses, the word of YHWH.

hos.12.text: Ephraim has surrounded me with lies, and the house of Israel with deceit; but Judah still walks with God, and is faithful with the Holy One.
Ephraim herds the wind and chases the east wind all day long; he multiplies lies and violence. They cut a covenant with Assyria, and oil is carried to Egypt.
And YHWH has a case against Judah; he will visit upon Jacob according to his ways, and repay him according to his deeds.
In the womb he grasped his brother's heel, and in his strength he strove with God.
He strove with the angel and prevailed; he wept and sought his favor. At Bethel he found him, and there he spoke with us —
YHWH, the God of hosts: YHWH is his name of remembrance.
And you — by your God you shall return. Keep steadfast love and justice, and wait for your God continually.
A trader — in his hand are scales of deceit; he loves to defraud.
And Ephraim says, "Surely I have grown rich; I have found wealth for myself. In all my gains they will find in me no iniquity that is sin."
But I am YHWH your God from the land of Egypt; I will again make you dwell in tents, as in the days of the appointed feast.
And I spoke to the prophets — it was I who multiplied vision, and through the prophets I gave parables.
If Gilead is wickedness, surely they have become worthless. In Gilgal they sacrifice bulls; their altars too shall be like heaps of stones on the furrows of the field.
And Jacob fled to the country of Aram; Israel served for a wife — for a wife he kept watch.
And by a prophet YHWH brought Israel up from Egypt, and by a prophet he was kept.
Ephraim has provoked bitter anger; his Lord will leave his bloodguilt upon him, and repay him his reproach.

hos.13.text: When Ephraim spoke, there was trembling; he was exalted in Israel — but he incurred guilt through Baal, and died.

And now they go on sinning: they make for themselves molten images from their silver — idols by their own skill, all of it the work of craftsmen. Of them they say: "Those who sacrifice — men! — kiss calves."
Therefore they shall be like a morning cloud, like the dew that goes away early, like chaff storm-driven from the threshing floor, like smoke from the window.
But I am YHWH your God from the land of Egypt; you know no God but me, and there is no savior besides me.
It was I who knew you in the wilderness, in the land of burning drought.
When they had their pasture, they were filled; they were filled, and their heart was lifted up — therefore they forgot me.
So I have become like a lion to them; like a leopard by the way I will lurk.
I will fall upon them like a bear robbed of her cubs; I will tear open the casing of their heart; there I will devour them like a lioness — the beasts of the field shall rip them open.
It has destroyed you, O Israel — that you are against me, against your helper.
Where now is your king, that he may save you in all your cities — and your judges, of whom you said, "Give me a king and princes"?
I give you a king in my anger — and I take him away in my wrath.
The iniquity of Ephraim is bound up; his sin is stored away.
The pangs of a woman in labor come upon him. He is an unwise son — for at the right time he does not present himself at the breaking forth of children.
From the hand of Sheol shall I ransom them? From death shall I redeem them? Where are your plagues, O Death? Where is your destruction, O Sheol? Compassion is hidden from my eyes.

Though he flourishes among his brothers, an east wind shall come — a wind of YHWH rising from the wilderness; his fountain shall be ashamed and his spring dried up. It shall plunder the treasury of every precious vessel.

hos.14.text: Samaria shall bear her guilt, for she has rebelled against her God. They shall fall by the sword; their infants shall be dashed to pieces, and their pregnant women ripped open.
Return, O Israel, to YHWH your God — for you have stumbled in your iniquity.
Take words with you and return to YHWH. Say to him: "Forgive all iniquity, and accept what is good — and we will pay with the bulls of our lips.
Assyria shall not save us; upon horses we will not ride; and we will say no more 'Our God' to the work of our hands — for in you the orphan finds mercy."
I will heal their turning away; I will love them freely — for my anger has turned from him.
I will be like the dew to Israel: he shall blossom like the lily, and strike his roots like Lebanon.
His shoots shall spread out; his splendor shall be like the olive tree, and his fragrance like Lebanon.
Those who dwell in his shade shall return; they shall revive like the grain, and blossom like the vine — his remembrance like the wine of Lebanon.
Ephraim — what have I still to do with idols? It is I who answer and look after him. I am like a luxuriant cypress: from me your fruit is found.
Whoever is wise, let him understand these things; whoever is discerning, let him know them. For the ways of YHWH are straight: the righteous walk in them — but transgressors stumble in them.

zec.1.text: In the eighth month, in the second year of Darius, the word of YHWH came to Zechariah son of Berechiah, son of Iddo, the prophet:
YHWH was angry with your fathers — angry indeed.
So say to them: Thus says YHWH of hosts: Return to me — the word of YHWH of hosts — and I will return to you, says YHWH of hosts.

Do not be like your fathers, to whom the former prophets called, saying, "Thus says YHWH of hosts: Turn now from your evil ways and your evil deeds" — but they did not listen, and they paid no heed to me — the word of YHWH.
Your fathers — where are they? And the prophets — do they live forever?

But my words and my statutes, which I commanded my servants the prophets — did they not overtake your fathers? And they turned and said: "As YHWH of hosts planned to do to us, according to our ways and our deeds, so he has done with us."
On the twenty-fourth day of the eleventh month — the month of Shebat — in the second year of Darius, the word of YHWH came to Zechariah son of Berechiah, son of Iddo, the prophet:
I saw in the night — and behold, a man riding on a red horse, standing among the myrtles in the hollow; and behind him horses — red, sorrel, and white.
And I said, "What are these, my lord?" And the angel who spoke with me said to me, "I will show you what these are."
And the man standing among the myrtles answered and said, "These are they whom YHWH has sent to walk to and fro through the earth."
And they answered the angel of YHWH standing among the myrtles and said, "We have walked to and fro through the earth — and behold, all the earth sits still and is at rest."
Then the angel of YHWH answered and said, "YHWH of hosts, how long will you have no compassion on Jerusalem and the cities of Judah, against which you have been indignant these seventy years?"
And YHWH answered the angel who spoke with me with good words — comforting words.
And the angel who spoke with me said to me: "Proclaim: Thus says YHWH of hosts: I am jealous for Jerusalem and for Zion with a great jealousy;
and with great anger I am angry at the nations at ease — for I was angry but a little, and they helped toward evil.
Therefore thus says YHWH: I have returned to Jerusalem with compassion; my house shall be built in her — the word of YHWH of hosts — and a measuring line shall be stretched out over Jerusalem.
Proclaim again: Thus says YHWH of hosts: My cities shall again overflow with good; and YHWH will again comfort Zion and again choose Jerusalem."

zec.2.text: And I lifted my eyes and saw — and behold, four horns.
And I said to the angel who spoke with me, "What are these?" And he said to me, "These are the horns that scattered Judah, Israel, and Jerusalem."
Then YHWH showed me four craftsmen.

And I said, "What are these coming to do?" And he spoke, saying, "Those are the horns that scattered Judah so that no man lifted his head; and these have come to terrify them — to cast down the horns of the nations that lifted a horn against the land of Judah to scatter her."
And I lifted my eyes and saw — and behold, a man with a measuring line in his hand.
And I said, "Where are you going?" And he said to me, "To measure Jerusalem — to see how wide and how long she is."
And behold, the angel who spoke with me was going out, and another angel was going out to meet him,
and he said to him: "Run, speak to that young man: Jerusalem shall be inhabited as unwalled villages, because of the multitude of people and beasts within her.
And I myself will be to her — the word of YHWH — a wall of fire all around, and I will be the glory within her."
Up! Up! Flee from the land of the north — the word of YHWH — for like the four winds of the heavens I have spread you abroad — the word of YHWH.
Up, O Zion! Escape, you who dwell with the daughter of Babylon!
For thus says YHWH of hosts — after glory he sent me — concerning the nations that plunder you: whoever touches you touches the apple of his eye.
For behold, I am waving my hand over them, and they shall become plunder for their own servants — and you shall know that YHWH of hosts has sent me.
Sing and rejoice, O daughter of Zion! For behold, I am coming, and I will dwell in your midst — the word of YHWH.
And many nations shall join themselves to YHWH on that day, and they shall be my people; and I will dwell in your midst — and you shall know that YHWH of hosts has sent me to you.
And YHWH shall inherit Judah as his portion on the holy soil, and shall again choose Jerusalem.
Be silent, all flesh, before YHWH — for he has roused himself from his holy dwelling.

zec.3.text: And he showed me Joshua the high priest standing before the angel of YHWH — and the Accuser standing at his right hand to accuse him.
And YHWH said to the Accuser: "YHWH rebuke you, O Accuser! YHWH, who has chosen Jerusalem, rebuke you! Is this not a brand snatched from the fire?"
Now Joshua was clothed in filthy garments as he stood before the angel.

And he answered and said to those standing before him: "Remove the filthy garments from him." And to him he said: "See, I have taken your iniquity away from you, and I will clothe you in festal robes."
And I said, "Let them set a clean turban on his head." So they set the clean turban on his head and clothed him in garments — and the angel of YHWH stood by.
And the angel of YHWH charged Joshua:
"Thus says YHWH of hosts: If you walk in my ways and keep my charge, then you shall also judge my house and keep my courts — and I will give you passage among these who stand here.
Hear now, Joshua the high priest — you and your companions who sit before you, for they are men of portent: behold, I am bringing my servant, the Branch.
For behold, the stone I have set before Joshua — on one stone, seven eyes. Behold, I am engraving its engraving — the word of YHWH of hosts — and I will remove the iniquity of that land in one day.
On that day — the word of YHWH of hosts — you shall invite one another under the vine and under the fig tree."

zec.4.text: And the angel who spoke with me returned and roused me, like a man roused from his sleep.

And he said to me, "What do you see?" And I said, "I see — and behold, a lampstand all of gold, with its bowl on top of it, and its seven lamps upon it, with seven pipes to each of the lamps on its top;
and two olive trees beside it, one to the right of the bowl and one to its left."
And I answered and said to the angel who spoke with me, "What are these, my lord?"
And the angel who spoke with me answered and said to me, "Do you not know what these are?" And I said, "No, my lord."
Then he answered and said to me: "This is the word of YHWH to Zerubbabel: Not by might, and not by power, but by my Spirit, says YHWH of hosts.
Who are you, O great mountain? Before Zerubbabel — a plain! And he shall bring out the capstone amid shouts of 'Grace, grace to it!'"
And the word of YHWH came to me:
"The hands of Zerubbabel have founded this house, and his hands shall complete it — and you shall know that YHWH of hosts has sent me to you.
For who has despised the day of small things? They shall rejoice when they see the plumb stone in the hand of Zerubbabel. These seven — they are the eyes of YHWH, ranging through all the earth."
And I answered and said to him, "What are these two olive trees on the right of the lampstand and on its left?"
And I answered a second time and said to him, "What are the two olive branches beside the two golden pipes that pour out the gold from themselves?"
And he said to me, "Do you not know what these are?" And I said, "No, my lord."
And he said, "These are the two sons of fresh oil, who stand by the Lord of all the earth."

zec.5.text: And again I lifted my eyes and saw — and behold, a flying scroll.
And he said to me, "What do you see?" And I said, "I see a flying scroll — its length twenty cubits and its width ten cubits."

And he said to me: "This is the curse going out over the face of the whole land. For everyone who steals shall be purged away according to it on this side, and everyone who swears falsely shall be purged away according to it on that side.

I have sent it out — the word of YHWH of hosts — and it shall enter the house of the thief and the house of the one who swears falsely by my name; and it shall lodge within his house and consume it, both its timbers and its stones."
And the angel who spoke with me came forward and said to me, "Lift up your eyes now and see: what is this that is going out?"
And I said, "What is it?" And he said, "This is the ephah-basket going out." And he said, "This is their eye in all the land."
And behold, a round leaden cover was lifted — and there was a woman sitting inside the ephah.
And he said, "This is Wickedness." And he thrust her back into the ephah, and thrust the leaden weight onto its mouth.
And I lifted my eyes and saw — and behold, two women coming forward with the wind in their wings (they had wings like the wings of the stork), and they lifted the ephah between earth and heaven.
And I said to the angel who spoke with me, "Where are they taking the ephah?"
And he said to me, "To build her a house in the land of Shinar; and when it is prepared, she shall be set there on her own base."

zec.6.text: And again I lifted my eyes and saw — and behold, four chariots coming out from between two mountains; and the mountains were mountains of bronze.
With the first chariot were red horses; with the second chariot, black horses;
with the third chariot, white horses; and with the fourth chariot, dappled horses — strong ones.
And I answered and said to the angel who spoke with me, "What are these, my lord?"
And the angel answered and said to me: "These are the four winds of the heavens, going out after presenting themselves before the Lord of all the earth.
The one with the black horses goes out to the land of the north; the white go out after them; and the dappled go out to the land of the south."
And the strong ones went out, straining to go and range through the earth. And he said, "Go, range through the earth." And they ranged through the earth.
Then he cried out to me and spoke to me, saying: "See — those going out to the land of the north have set my Spirit at rest in the land of the north."
And the word of YHWH came to me:
"Take from the exiles — from Heldai, from Tobijah, and from Jedaiah — and come the same day and enter the house of Josiah son of Zephaniah, to which they have come from Babylon.
Take silver and gold and make crowns, and set one on the head of Joshua son of Jehozadak, the high priest,
and say to him: Thus says YHWH of hosts: Behold, a man whose name is Branch — and from his place he shall branch out, and he shall build the temple of YHWH.

It is he who shall build the temple of YHWH; and he shall bear majesty, and sit and rule on his throne. And there shall be a priest by his throne — and the counsel of peace shall be between the two of them.
And the crowns shall be for Helem, Tobijah, Jedaiah, and Hen son of Zephaniah, as a memorial in the temple of YHWH.
And those far off shall come and build in the temple of YHWH — and you shall know that YHWH of hosts has sent me to you. And it shall come to pass, if you truly obey the voice of YHWH your God."

zec.7.text: And in the fourth year of Darius the king, the word of YHWH came to Zechariah, on the fourth of the ninth month, in Chislev.
Now Bethel had sent Sharezer and Regem-melech and his men to entreat the face of YHWH,
asking the priests of the house of YHWH of hosts and the prophets: "Shall I weep in the fifth month, abstaining, as I have done these many years?"
And the word of YHWH of hosts came to me:
"Say to all the people of the land and to the priests: When you fasted and mourned in the fifth month and the seventh, these seventy years — was it for me that you fasted, for me?
And when you eat and when you drink — is it not you who eat, and you who drink?
Are these not the words YHWH proclaimed by the former prophets, when Jerusalem sat inhabited and at ease, with her cities around her, and the Negev and the lowland inhabited?"
And the word of YHWH came to Zechariah:
"Thus says YHWH of hosts: Judge true judgment, and show steadfast love and compassion, each to his brother.
Do not oppress the widow or the orphan, the sojourner or the poor; and let none of you devise evil in your heart against his brother."
But they refused to pay heed; they turned a stubborn shoulder and made their ears heavy against hearing.
They made their hearts adamant against hearing the law and the words that YHWH of hosts sent by his Spirit through the former prophets — and great anger came from YHWH of hosts.
"And just as he called and they did not hear, so they shall call and I will not hear," says YHWH of hosts.
"And I stormed them away over all the nations they had not known, and the land was left desolate behind them, with none passing through or returning — for they made the pleasant land a desolation."

zec.8.text: And the word of YHWH of hosts came:
"Thus says YHWH of hosts: I am jealous for Zion with a great jealousy; with great wrath I am jealous for her.
Thus says YHWH: I have returned to Zion, and I will dwell in the midst of Jerusalem; and Jerusalem shall be called the City of Truth, and the mountain of YHWH of hosts the Holy Mountain.
Thus says YHWH of hosts: Old men and old women shall again sit in the squares of Jerusalem, each with staff in hand for great age;
and the squares of the city shall be full of boys and girls playing in her squares.
Thus says YHWH of hosts: If it is marvelous in the eyes of the remnant of this people in those days, shall it also be marvelous in my eyes? — the word of YHWH of hosts.
Thus says YHWH of hosts: Behold, I am saving my people from the land of the east and from the land of the setting sun;
and I will bring them in, and they shall dwell in the midst of Jerusalem. And they shall be my people, and I will be their God — in truth and in righteousness.

Thus says YHWH of hosts: Let your hands be strong, you who in these days hear these words from the mouth of the prophets who were there on the day the house of YHWH of hosts was founded — the temple, that it might be built.
For before those days there was no wage for man and no wage for beast, and no peace from the adversary for him who went out or came in — for I had set every man against his neighbor.
But now I am not as in the former days to the remnant of this people — the word of YHWH of hosts.

For there shall be a sowing of peace: the vine shall give its fruit, the earth shall give its produce, and the heavens shall give their dew — and I will make the remnant of this people inherit all these things.
And as you were a curse among the nations, O house of Judah and house of Israel, so I will save you, and you shall be a blessing. Do not fear; let your hands be strong.
For thus says YHWH of hosts: As I planned to do evil to you when your fathers provoked me to anger — says YHWH of hosts — and I did not relent,
so again I have planned in these days to do good to Jerusalem and to the house of Judah. Do not fear.
These are the things you shall do: Speak truth, each to his neighbor; judge with truth and the judgment of peace in your gates;
let none of you devise evil in your hearts against his neighbor, and love no false oath — for all these are what I hate — the word of YHWH."
And the word of YHWH of hosts came to me:

"Thus says YHWH of hosts: The fast of the fourth month, the fast of the fifth, the fast of the seventh, and the fast of the tenth shall become for the house of Judah joy and gladness and good festivals — so love truth and peace.
Thus says YHWH of hosts: Peoples shall yet come, and the inhabitants of many cities;
and the inhabitants of one shall go to another, saying, 'Let us go at once to entreat the face of YHWH, and to seek YHWH of hosts — I too will go.'
And many peoples and mighty nations shall come to seek YHWH of hosts in Jerusalem, and to entreat the face of YHWH.

Thus says YHWH of hosts: In those days ten men from all the tongues of the nations shall take hold — they shall take hold of the robe of a Jew, saying, 'Let us go with you, for we have heard that God is with you.'"

zec.9.text: A burden: the word of YHWH is against the land of Hadrach, and Damascus is its resting place — for to YHWH belongs the eye of man and all the tribes of Israel —
and Hamath too, which borders on it; Tyre and Sidon, though she is very wise.
Tyre built herself a stronghold; she heaped up silver like dust, and fine gold like the mire of the streets.
Behold, the Lord will dispossess her; he will strike her wealth into the sea — and she shall be devoured by fire.
Ashkelon shall see it and fear; Gaza too, and writhe in anguish; and Ekron — for her hope is put to shame. The king shall perish from Gaza; Ashkelon shall not be inhabited;
a mongrel people shall dwell in Ashdod — and I will cut off the pride of the Philistines.
And I will remove his blood from his mouth and his abominations from between his teeth; and he too shall be left for our God — he shall be like a clan-chief in Judah, and Ekron like the Jebusite.
And I will encamp at my house as a garrison, against any who march past or return; no oppressor shall pass over them again — for now I have seen with my own eyes.

Rejoice greatly, O daughter of Zion! Shout aloud, O daughter of Jerusalem! Behold, your king comes to you: righteous and saved is he, humble and riding on a donkey — on a colt, the foal of a she-donkey.

And I will cut off the chariot from Ephraim and the horse from Jerusalem; the battle bow shall be cut off, and he shall speak peace to the nations. His dominion shall be from sea to sea, and from the River to the ends of the earth.
As for you also — because of the blood of your covenant, I have set your prisoners free from the waterless pit.
Return to the stronghold, O prisoners of hope! This very day I declare: I will restore to you double.
For I have bent Judah as my bow; I have filled it with Ephraim. I will rouse your sons, O Zion, against your sons, O Greece, and make you like a warrior's sword.
And YHWH shall appear over them, and his arrow go forth like lightning. The Lord YHWH shall blow the shofar, and march in the storm-winds of the south.

YHWH of hosts shall shield them: they shall devour, and subdue the sling-stones; they shall drink and roar as with wine; they shall be full like the sacrificial bowl, drenched like the corners of the altar.
And YHWH their God shall save them on that day, as the flock of his people — for like jewels of a crown they shall shine upon his soil.
For how great is its goodness, and how great its beauty! Grain shall make the young men flourish, and new wine the young women.

zec.10.text: Ask rain from YHWH in the season of the latter rain — YHWH who makes the storm clouds; and he will give them showers of rain, to each one grass in the field.

For the teraphim have spoken emptiness, and the diviners have seen a lie; they tell false dreams and give vain comfort. Therefore the people wander like sheep; they are afflicted for lack of a shepherd.
Against the shepherds my anger burns, and I will punish the he-goats — for YHWH of hosts has visited his flock, the house of Judah, and will make them like his splendid horse in battle.
From him the cornerstone, from him the tent-peg, from him the battle bow — from him every ruler together.
And they shall be like warriors trampling the mire of the streets in battle. They shall fight, for YHWH is with them — and the riders on horses shall be put to shame.

And I will make the house of Judah mighty, and the house of Joseph I will save. I will bring them back, for I have compassion on them, and they shall be as though I had never rejected them — for I am YHWH their God, and I will answer them.
And Ephraim shall be like a warrior, and their heart shall rejoice as with wine; their children shall see it and rejoice — their heart shall exult in YHWH.
I will whistle for them and gather them in, for I have redeemed them — and they shall multiply as they once multiplied.
Though I sow them among the peoples, in far countries they shall remember me; they shall live with their children — and return.
And I will bring them back from the land of Egypt, and from Assyria I will gather them; to the land of Gilead and Lebanon I will bring them — and room shall not be found for them.

And he shall pass through the sea of distress and strike the waves of the sea, and all the depths of the Nile shall dry up; the pride of Assyria shall be brought down, and the scepter of Egypt shall depart.
And I will make them mighty in YHWH, and in his name they shall walk — the word of YHWH.

zec.11.text: Open your doors, O Lebanon, that fire may devour your cedars!
Wail, O cypress, for the cedar has fallen, for the mighty are despoiled! Wail, O oaks of Bashan, for the impenetrable forest has come down!
The sound of the shepherds' wailing — for their splendor is despoiled! The sound of the lions' roaring — for the pride of the Jordan is despoiled!
Thus said YHWH my God: "Shepherd the flock marked for slaughter —
whose buyers slay them and go unpunished, whose sellers say, 'Blessed be YHWH — I have grown rich!' and whose own shepherds have no pity on them.

For I will no longer have pity on the inhabitants of the land — the word of YHWH. Behold, I am delivering every man into the hand of his neighbor and into the hand of his king; and they shall crush the land, and I will not deliver from their hand."
So I shepherded the flock marked for slaughter — truly the poorest of the flock. And I took for myself two staffs: one I called Favor, and the other I called Bonds — and I shepherded the flock.
And I did away with the three shepherds in one month. Then my soul grew short with them, and their soul also loathed me.
And I said: "I will not shepherd you. Let what is dying die; let what is being cut off be cut off; and let those left devour each other's flesh."
And I took my staff Favor and broke it — annulling the covenant I had cut with all the peoples.
And it was annulled on that day; and so the poorest of the flock, who were watching me, knew that it was the word of YHWH.
And I said to them: "If it is good in your eyes, give me my wages; and if not, keep them." And they weighed out my wages — thirty pieces of silver.
And YHWH said to me: "Throw it to the potter" — this magnificent price at which I was valued by them! So I took the thirty pieces of silver and threw them into the house of YHWH, to the potter.
Then I broke my second staff, Bonds — annulling the brotherhood between Judah and Israel.
And YHWH said to me: "Take once more the gear of a foolish shepherd.

For behold, I am raising up a shepherd in the land who will not attend to those being cut off, nor seek the young, nor heal the broken, nor sustain the one still standing — but he will eat the flesh of the fat ones and tear off even their hooves.
Woe to the worthless shepherd who abandons the flock! A sword upon his arm and upon his right eye! His arm shall wither utterly, and his right eye go utterly dark."

zec.12.text: A burden: the word of YHWH concerning Israel — the word of YHWH, who stretches out the heavens and founds the earth and forms the spirit of man within him:
Behold, I am making Jerusalem a cup of reeling for all the peoples around; and against Judah too it shall be, in the siege against Jerusalem.
And on that day I will make Jerusalem a burdensome stone for all the peoples: all who lift her shall grievously gash themselves — and all the nations of the earth shall gather against her.

On that day — the word of YHWH — I will strike every horse with panic and its rider with madness. But upon the house of Judah I will open my eyes, while I strike every horse of the peoples with blindness.
Then the clan-chiefs of Judah shall say in their hearts: "The inhabitants of Jerusalem are strength to me, through YHWH of hosts their God."

On that day I will make the clan-chiefs of Judah like a firepot among wood, like a flaming torch among sheaves; and they shall devour, right and left, all the peoples around — and Jerusalem shall dwell again in her own place, in Jerusalem.
And YHWH shall save the tents of Judah first, so that the glory of the house of David and the glory of the inhabitants of Jerusalem may not exalt itself over Judah.
On that day YHWH will shield the inhabitants of Jerusalem: the one who stumbles among them on that day shall be like David, and the house of David like God — like the angel of YHWH before them.
And on that day I will seek to destroy all the nations that come against Jerusalem.

And I will pour out on the house of David and on the inhabitants of Jerusalem a spirit of grace and supplication; and they shall look to me concerning him whom they have pierced, and they shall mourn for him as one mourns for an only child, and weep bitterly over him as one weeps over a firstborn.
On that day the mourning in Jerusalem shall be as great as the mourning of Hadad-rimmon in the plain of Megiddo.
And the land shall mourn, family by family apart: the family of the house of David apart, and their women apart; the family of the house of Nathan apart, and their women apart;
the family of the house of Levi apart, and their women apart; the family of the Shimeites apart, and their women apart —
all the remaining families, family by family apart, and their women apart.
