Thus says the Lord, YHWH of hosts: Go, enter to that steward, to Shebna, who is over the household, and say:
What have you here, and whom have you here, that you have carved out a tomb here — hewing your grave on the height, cutting a resting place for yourself in the rock?
Look — YHWH is about to hurl you away violently, O man, and seize hold of you.
He will roll you up tightly like a ball and hurl you into a vast land; there you will die, and there your splendid chariots will lie, O disgrace of your master's house.
I will thrust you from your office; from your station you will be torn down.
On that day I will call my servant Eliakim son of Hilkiah,
and I will clothe him with your robe and bind him with your sash, and hand your authority into his hand; he will be a father to the inhabitants of Jerusalem and to the house of Judah.
I will lay the key of the house of David on his shoulder; he will open, and none will shut; he will shut, and none will open.
I will drive him like a peg in a sure place, and he will become a throne of glory to his father's house.
And they will hang on him all the glory of his father's house, the offspring and the offshoots, every small vessel, from the bowls to all the jars.
On that day, declares YHWH of hosts, the peg driven in the sure place will give way; it will be cut down and fall, and the load hanging on it will be cut off — for YHWH has spoken.

isa.23.text: The burden of Tyre. Wail, ships of Tarshish, for she is laid waste, without house, without harbor; from the land of Cyprus it is revealed to them.
Be silent, you inhabitants of the coast, you merchants of Sidon; your envoys crossed the sea and filled you.
On the great waters the grain of Shihor, the harvest of the Nile, was her revenue; she was the marketplace of the nations.
Be ashamed, Sidon, for the sea has spoken, the stronghold of the sea, saying: I have not labored nor given birth, I have not reared young men nor raised up virgins.
When the report reaches Egypt, they will writhe at the report of Tyre.
Cross over to Tarshish; wail, you inhabitants of the coast.
Is this your exultant city, whose origin is from days of old, whose own feet carried her to settle far away?
Who has purposed this against Tyre, the bestower of crowns, whose merchants were princes, whose traders were the honored of the earth?
YHWH of hosts has purposed it, to defile the pride of all splendor, to bring low all the honored of the earth.
Flood over your land like the Nile, daughter of Tarshish; there is no harbor anymore.
He has stretched out his hand over the sea; he has made kingdoms tremble. YHWH has commanded concerning Canaan to destroy her strongholds.
He said: You will exult no more, O oppressed one, virgin daughter of Sidon. Rise, cross over to Cyprus; even there you will find no rest.
Look at the land of the Chaldeans — this is the people that was not; Assyria founded her for wild beasts. They raised their siege towers, they stripped her palaces bare, they made her a ruin.
Wail, ships of Tarshish, for your stronghold is laid waste.
On that day Tyre will be forgotten seventy years, like the days of one king; at the end of seventy years it will happen to Tyre as in the song of the harlot:
Take a harp, go about the city, forgotten harlot; play sweetly, sing many songs, that you may be remembered.
At the end of seventy years YHWH will visit Tyre, and she will return to her hire, and prostitute herself with all the kingdoms of the world on the face of the earth.
But her profit and her hire will be holy to YHWH; it will not be hoarded or stored, for her profit will go to those who dwell before YHWH, to eat their fill and clothe themselves in fine array.

isa.24.text: Look — YHWH lays the earth waste and makes it empty; he twists its face and scatters its inhabitants.

And it will be, as with the people, so with the priest; as with the servant, so with his master; as with the maid, so with her mistress; as with the buyer, so with the seller; as with the lender, so with the borrower; as with the creditor, so with the debtor.
The earth will be utterly laid waste, utterly plundered; for YHWH has spoken this word.
The earth mourns and withers, the world languishes and withers; the heights of the earth's people languish.
The earth is defiled beneath its inhabitants, for they have transgressed the laws, violated the statute, broken the everlasting covenant.
Therefore a curse devours the earth, and those who dwell in it bear their guilt; therefore the inhabitants of the earth are scorched, and few are left.
The new wine mourns, the vine languishes; all the merry-hearted sigh.
The joy of the tambourines is stilled, the din of the revelers has ceased, the joy of the harp is stilled.
No more do they drink wine with song; strong drink is bitter to those who drink it.
The city of chaos is broken; every house is shut, barred against entry.
There is an outcry in the streets for wine; all gladness has darkened, the joy of the earth is banished.
Desolation is left in the city; the gate is battered to ruins.
For so it will be in the midst of the earth, among the peoples, as when an olive tree is beaten, as when gleanings are left after the grape harvest.
They lift up their voices, they sing for joy; over the majesty of YHWH they cry aloud from the sea.
Therefore in the east give glory to YHWH, in the coastlands of the sea the name of YHWH, the God of Israel.
From the ends of the earth we hear songs: Glory to the Righteous One! But I said: I waste away, I waste away, woe is me! The treacherous betray, with treachery the treacherous betray.
Terror and pit and snare are upon you, O inhabitant of the earth.

Whoever flees from the sound of terror will fall into the pit, and whoever climbs from the pit will be caught in the snare; for the windows of heaven are opened, and the foundations of the earth quake.
The earth is utterly broken, the earth is torn apart, the earth is violently shaken.
The earth staggers like a drunkard, it sways like a hut; its transgression lies heavy upon it, and it falls, never to rise again.
On that day YHWH will punish the host of heaven in the heavens, and the kings of the earth on the earth.
They will be gathered together like prisoners in a pit, shut up in a dungeon, and after many days they will be punished.
Then the moon will be abashed and the sun ashamed, for YHWH of hosts will reign on Mount Zion and in Jerusalem, and before his elders in glory.

isa.25.text: YHWH, you are my God; I will exalt you, I will praise your name; for you have done wonders, plans formed long ago, faithful and sure.
For you have made the city a heap, the fortified town a ruin, the palace of foreigners no longer a city; it will never be rebuilt.
Therefore strong peoples will honor you; the city of ruthless nations will fear you.
For you have been a stronghold to the poor, a stronghold to the needy in his distress, a refuge from the storm, a shade from the heat; for the blast of the ruthless is like a storm against a wall.
Like heat in a drought you subdue the uproar of foreigners; like heat by the shadow of a cloud, the song of the ruthless is silenced.
On this mountain YHWH of hosts will make for all peoples a feast of rich food, a feast of aged wine, of rich food full of marrow, of aged wine well refined.
On this mountain he will swallow up the shroud that shrouds all peoples, the veil that veils all nations.
He will swallow up death forever; the Lord YHWH will wipe away the tears from all faces, and the reproach of his people he will take away from all the earth — for YHWH has spoken.
On that day it will be said: Look — this is our God; we waited for him, and he saved us. This is YHWH; we waited for him; let us rejoice and be glad in his salvation.
For the hand of YHWH will rest on this mountain, and Moab will be trodden down under him as straw is trodden down in the dung pit.
He will spread out his hands in it as a swimmer spreads his hands to swim, but YHWH will bring down his pride together with the cunning of his hands.
The high fortress of your walls he will bring down, lay low, cast to the ground, into the dust.

isa.26.text: On that day this song will be sung in the land of Judah: We have a strong city; salvation he sets as walls and rampart.
Open the gates, that a righteous nation may enter, one that keeps faith.
You will keep in perfect peace the mind that is stayed on you, because it trusts in you.
Trust in YHWH forever, for in YAH, YHWH, is an everlasting Rock.
For he has brought low those who dwell on high, the lofty city; he lays it low, lays it low to the ground, brings it down to the dust.
The foot tramples it — the feet of the poor, the steps of the needy.
The path of the righteous is level; you make smooth the way of the righteous.
Yes, in the way of your judgments, YHWH, we wait for you; your name and your renown are the soul's desire.
My soul yearns for you in the night; my spirit within me seeks you at dawn; for when your judgments are in the earth, the inhabitants of the world learn righteousness.
Though the wicked is shown grace, he does not learn righteousness; in the land of uprightness he deals falsely and does not see the majesty of YHWH.
YHWH, your hand is lifted high, but they do not see it. Let them see your zeal for your people and be ashamed; let the fire meant for your enemies consume them.
YHWH, you will ordain peace for us, for you have accomplished all our works for us.
YHWH our God, other lords besides you have ruled over us, but your name alone we invoke.
The dead will not live, the shades will not rise; therefore you have punished and destroyed them, and wiped out all remembrance of them.
You have enlarged the nation, YHWH, you have enlarged the nation; you are glorified; you have extended all the borders of the land.
YHWH, in distress they sought you; they poured out a whispered prayer when your chastening was upon them.
Like a woman with child, near her time of birth, writhing and crying out in her pangs, so were we before you, YHWH.
We were with child, we writhed, but we gave birth only to wind; we brought no salvation to the earth, and no inhabitants of the world were born.
Your dead will live; my corpses will rise. Awake and sing for joy, you who dwell in the dust! For your dew is a dew of light, and the earth will give birth to the shades.
Come, my people, enter your chambers and shut your doors behind you; hide yourselves a little while until the wrath has passed.
For look — YHWH is coming out from his place to punish the inhabitants of the earth for their guilt; the earth will disclose its blood and no longer conceal its slain.

isa.27.text: On that day YHWH with his hard and great and mighty sword will punish Leviathan the fleeing serpent, Leviathan the twisting serpent, and he will slay the dragon that is in the sea.
On that day sing of it — a vineyard of delight!
I, YHWH, am its keeper; every moment I water it; lest any harm it, night and day I guard it.
I have no wrath. If only there were briers and thorns to war against me! I would march against them, I would burn them all together.
Or let them take hold of my refuge, let them make peace with me — peace let them make with me.
In days to come Jacob will take root, Israel will blossom and put forth buds, and fill the face of the world with fruit.
Has he struck them as he struck those who struck them? Or were they slain as their slayers were slain?
By exile, by driving her away, you contended with her; he removed her with his fierce blast in the day of the east wind.

Therefore by this the guilt of Jacob will be atoned, and this is the full fruit of removing his sin: when he makes all the altar stones like crushed stones of chalk, no sacred poles or incense stands will stand again.
For the fortified city stands alone, a homestead forsaken and abandoned like the wilderness; there the calf grazes, there it lies down and strips its branches bare.

When its boughs are withered, they are broken off; women come and set them ablaze. For this is a people without understanding; therefore their Maker has no compassion on them, and their Creator shows them no grace.
On that day YHWH will thresh out the grain from the channel of the River to the Wadi of Egypt, and you will be gleaned one by one, O children of Israel.
On that day a great trumpet will be blown, and those lost in the land of Assyria and those driven into the land of Egypt will come and worship YHWH on the holy mountain in Jerusalem.

isa.40.text: Comfort, comfort my people, says your God.
Speak to the heart of Jerusalem, and cry out to her that her hard service is ended, that her guilt is paid, that she has received from YHWH's hand double for all her sins.
A voice cries out: In the wilderness clear the way of YHWH; make straight in the desert a highway for our God.
Every valley shall be lifted up, and every mountain and hill made low; the crooked ground shall become level, and the rugged places a plain.
And the glory of YHWH shall be revealed, and all flesh together shall see it; for the mouth of YHWH has spoken.
A voice says, "Cry out!" And I said, "What shall I cry?" All flesh is grass, and all its beauty like the flower of the field.
The grass withers, the flower fades, when the breath of YHWH blows upon it; surely the people are grass.
The grass withers, the flower fades, but the word of our God stands forever.
Climb a high mountain, O herald of good news to Zion; lift up your voice with strength, O herald of good news to Jerusalem; lift it up, do not fear; say to the cities of Judah, "Here is your God!"
Here comes the Lord YHWH in power, and his arm rules for him; here is his reward with him, and his recompense before him.
Like a shepherd he tends his flock; in his arm he gathers the lambs and carries them in his bosom, and gently leads the nursing ewes.
Who has measured the waters in the hollow of his hand, and marked off the heavens with a span, held the dust of the earth in a measure, and weighed the mountains in scales and the hills in a balance?
Who has directed the spirit of YHWH, or as his counselor has instructed him?
Whom did he consult for understanding, who taught him the path of justice, and taught him knowledge, and showed him the way of insight?
Behold, the nations are like a drop from a bucket, counted as dust on the scales; behold, he lifts up the coastlands like fine powder.
Lebanon is not enough for fuel, nor its beasts enough for a burnt offering.
All the nations are as nothing before him; they are counted by him as less than nothing and emptiness.
To whom then will you liken God? What likeness will you set beside him?
An idol! A craftsman casts it, and a goldsmith overlays it with gold and forges silver chains for it.
One too poor for such an offering chooses wood that will not rot; he seeks out a skilled craftsman to set up an idol that will not totter.
Do you not know? Do you not hear? Has it not been told you from the beginning? Have you not understood since the earth was founded?
He sits enthroned above the circle of the earth, and its inhabitants are like grasshoppers; he stretches out the heavens like a curtain and spreads them like a tent to dwell in.
He brings princes to nothing, and makes the rulers of the earth as emptiness.
Scarcely are they planted, scarcely sown, scarcely has their stem taken root in the earth, when he blows upon them and they wither, and the storm carries them off like straw.
To whom then will you liken me, that I should be his equal? says the Holy One.
Lift up your eyes on high and see: who created these? He who brings out their host by number, calling them each by name; because of his great might and mighty power, not one is missing.
Why do you say, O Jacob, and speak, O Israel, "My way is hidden from YHWH, and my cause is passed over by my God"?
Do you not know? Have you not heard? YHWH is the everlasting God, Creator of the ends of the earth; he does not faint or grow weary; his understanding is beyond searching.
He gives power to the faint, and to the strengthless he multiplies might.
Even youths grow faint and weary, and young men stumble and fall;
but those who wait for YHWH shall renew their strength; they shall mount up with wings like eagles; they shall run and not grow weary; they shall walk and not faint.

isa.41.text: Be silent before me, O coastlands, and let the peoples renew their strength; let them draw near, then let them speak; let us come together for judgment.
Who stirred up from the east one whom victory meets at every step? He gives up nations before him and tramples down kings; he makes them like dust with his sword, like driven straw with his bow.
He pursues them and passes on unscathed, by a path his feet have never trod.
Who has done and accomplished this, calling forth the generations from the beginning? I, YHWH, the first, and with the last I am he.
The coastlands see it and are afraid; the ends of the earth tremble; they draw near and come.
Each one helps his neighbor, and says to his brother, "Take courage!"
The craftsman heartens the goldsmith, and he who smooths with the hammer heartens him who strikes the anvil, saying of the soldering, "It is good"; and he fastens it with nails so it will not totter.
But you, Israel, my servant, Jacob whom I have chosen, seed of Abraham my friend,
you whom I took from the ends of the earth and called from its farthest corners, saying to you, "You are my servant; I have chosen you and not cast you off":
Do not fear, for I am with you; do not be dismayed, for I am your God. I strengthen you, yes, I help you; I uphold you with my righteous right hand.
Behold, all who rage against you shall be ashamed and disgraced; those who strive with you shall be as nothing and perish.
You will seek them and not find them, those who contend with you; those who war against you shall be as nothing at all.
For I am YHWH your God, who takes hold of your right hand, who says to you, "Do not fear; I will help you."
Do not fear, you worm Jacob, you men of Israel; I am the one who helps you, declares YHWH; your Redeemer is the Holy One of Israel.
Behold, I make of you a threshing sledge, sharp and new, with double teeth; you shall thresh the mountains and crush them, and make the hills like chaff.
You shall winnow them, and the wind shall carry them away, and the storm shall scatter them; but you shall rejoice in YHWH, and glory in the Holy One of Israel.
The poor and the needy seek water, and there is none; their tongue is parched with thirst. I, YHWH, will answer them; I, the God of Israel, will not forsake them.
I will open rivers on the bare heights, and springs in the midst of the valleys; I will turn the desert into pools of water, and the parched land into springs of water.
I will put in the wilderness the cedar, the acacia, the myrtle, and the olive; I will set in the desert the cypress, the pine, and the box tree together,
that they may see and know, may consider and understand together, that the hand of YHWH has done this, that the Holy One of Israel has created it.
Present your case, says YHWH; bring forward your proofs, says the King of Jacob.
Let them bring them forward and tell us what is to happen. Tell us the former things, what they were, that we may consider them and know their outcome; or declare to us the things to come.
Tell us what is to come hereafter, that we may know you are gods; do good, or do harm, that we may be dismayed and look on in fear together.
Behold, you are nothing, and your work is nothing at all; whoever chooses you is an abomination.
I stirred up one from the north, and he has come; from the rising of the sun he calls on my name; he tramples rulers like mortar, like a potter treading clay.
Who declared it from the beginning, that we might know, and beforehand, that we might say, "He is right"? There was no one who declared it, no one who proclaimed it, no one who heard your words.
First to Zion I say, "Behold, here they are!" and I give to Jerusalem a herald of good news.
But when I look, there is no one; among these there is no counselor who, when I ask, gives an answer.
Behold, they are all a delusion; their works are nothing; their molten images are wind and emptiness.

isa.42.text: Here is my servant, whom I uphold, my chosen, in whom my soul delights; I have put my Spirit upon him; he will bring forth justice to the nations.
He will not cry out or lift up his voice, or make it heard in the street.
A bruised reed he will not break, and a dimly burning wick he will not quench; in faithfulness he will bring forth justice.
He will not grow dim or be crushed until he has established justice in the earth, and the coastlands wait for his teaching.
Thus says God, YHWH, who created the heavens and stretched them out, who spread out the earth and all that comes from it, who gives breath to the people upon it and spirit to those who walk on it:
I, YHWH, have called you in righteousness; I will take hold of your hand and keep you; I will make you a covenant for the people, a light for the nations,
to open the eyes that are blind, to bring out the prisoner from the dungeon, and those who sit in darkness from the house of confinement.
I am YHWH; that is my name; my glory I will not give to another, nor my praise to idols.
Behold, the former things have come to pass, and new things I now declare; before they spring forth I tell you of them.
Sing to YHWH a new song, his praise from the ends of the earth, you who go down to the sea and all that fills it, the coastlands and their inhabitants.
Let the desert and its cities lift up their voice, the villages that Kedar inhabits; let the dwellers of Sela sing for joy, let them shout from the tops of the mountains.
Let them give glory to YHWH, and declare his praise in the coastlands.
YHWH goes forth like a warrior, like a man of battle he stirs up his fury; he raises the war cry, he roars aloud, he prevails against his enemies.
For a long time I have held my peace; I have kept still and restrained myself. Now like a woman in labor I will cry out; I will gasp and pant at once.
I will lay waste the mountains and hills, and dry up all their vegetation; I will turn the rivers into islands and dry up the pools.

I will lead the blind by a way they do not know; along paths they have not known I will guide them; I will turn the darkness before them into light, and the rough places into level ground. These are the things I will do, and I will not forsake them.
They shall be turned back, utterly put to shame, who trust in idols, who say to molten images, "You are our gods."
Hear, you deaf; and look, you blind, that you may see!
Who is blind but my servant, or deaf like the messenger I send? Who is blind like the one at peace with me, blind like the servant of YHWH?
You have seen many things, but do not observe them; your ears are open, but you do not hear.
It pleased YHWH, for the sake of his righteousness, to make his teaching great and glorious.
But this is a people plundered and stripped, all of them trapped in holes and hidden away in prisons; they have become plunder with none to rescue, spoil with none to say, "Give it back!"
Who among you will give ear to this, will listen and heed for the time to come?
Who gave up Jacob to the spoiler, and Israel to the plunderers? Was it not YHWH, against whom we have sinned, in whose ways they would not walk, and whose teaching they would not obey?
So he poured out on them the heat of his anger and the fury of war; it blazed around them, but they did not understand; it burned them, but they took it not to heart.

isa.43.text: But now, thus says YHWH, he who created you, O Jacob, he who formed you, O Israel: Do not fear, for I have redeemed you; I have called you by name; you are mine.
When you pass through the waters, I am with you; and through the rivers, they shall not overwhelm you; when you walk through fire, you shall not be scorched, and the flame shall not consume you.
For I am YHWH your God, the Holy One of Israel, your Savior; I gave Egypt as your ransom, Cush and Seba in exchange for you.
Because you are precious in my eyes, and honored, and I love you, I give men in exchange for you, and peoples in exchange for your life.
Do not fear, for I am with you; from the east I will bring your offspring, and from the west I will gather you.
I will say to the north, "Give them up," and to the south, "Do not hold them back"; bring my sons from far away, and my daughters from the ends of the earth,
everyone who is called by my name, whom I created for my glory, whom I formed and made.
Bring out the people who are blind, yet have eyes, who are deaf, yet have ears.

Let all the nations gather together, and let the peoples assemble. Who among them can declare this and proclaim to us the former things? Let them bring their witnesses to prove them right, that others may hear and say, "It is true."
You are my witnesses, declares YHWH, and my servant whom I have chosen, that you may know and believe me and understand that I am he. Before me no god was formed, and after me there shall be none.
I, I am YHWH, and besides me there is no savior.
I declared and saved and proclaimed, and there was no strange god among you; you are my witnesses, declares YHWH, and I am God.
Yes, from this day I am he; there is none who can deliver from my hand; I act, and who can reverse it?
Thus says YHWH, your Redeemer, the Holy One of Israel: For your sake I send to Babylon, and I bring them all down as fugitives, even the Chaldeans, in the ships of their rejoicing.
I am YHWH, your Holy One, the Creator of Israel, your King.
Thus says YHWH, who makes a way through the sea, a path through the mighty waters,
who brings out chariot and horse, army and warrior; together they lie down, never to rise; they are snuffed out, quenched like a wick.
Do not remember the former things, or consider the things of old.
Behold, I am doing a new thing; now it springs forth; do you not perceive it? I will make a way in the wilderness, and rivers in the desert.
The wild beasts will honor me, the jackals and the ostriches, for I give water in the wilderness, rivers in the desert, to give drink to my chosen people,
the people I formed for myself, that they might declare my praise.
Yet you did not call upon me, O Jacob; you grew weary of me, O Israel.
You have not brought me the sheep of your burnt offerings, or honored me with your sacrifices. I did not burden you with grain offerings, or weary you with frankincense.
You did not buy me sweet cane with money, or satisfy me with the fat of your sacrifices. Instead, you have burdened me with your sins; you have wearied me with your iniquities.
I, I am he who blots out your transgressions for my own sake, and I will not remember your sins.
Put me in remembrance; let us argue it out together; set forth your case, that you may be proved right.
Your first father sinned, and your spokesmen rebelled against me.
So I profaned the officials of the sanctuary; I gave Jacob over to destruction, and Israel to reviling.

isa.44.text: But now hear, O Jacob my servant, Israel whom I have chosen.
Thus says YHWH who made you, who formed you from the womb and will help you: Do not fear, O Jacob my servant, Jeshurun whom I have chosen.
For I will pour water on the thirsty land, and streams on the dry ground; I will pour my Spirit upon your offspring, and my blessing upon your descendants.
They shall spring up like grass among the waters, like willows by flowing streams.
This one will say, "I am YHWH's," and that one will call himself by the name of Jacob, and another will write on his hand, "YHWH's," and take the name of Israel.
Thus says YHWH, the King of Israel and his Redeemer, YHWH of hosts: I am the first and I am the last, and besides me there is no god.
Who is like me? Let him proclaim it, let him declare and set it before me, since I established an ancient people. Let them tell what is to come, and what will yet happen.
Do not tremble, do not be afraid. Have I not told you from of old and declared it? You are my witnesses. Is there a god besides me? There is no Rock; I know of none.
All who fashion idols are nothing, and the things they treasure are worthless; their witnesses neither see nor know, that they may be put to shame.
Who fashions a god or casts an idol that is good for nothing?
Behold, all its devotees shall be put to shame, and its craftsmen are only men. Let them all gather and take their stand; they shall be terrified and put to shame together.
The blacksmith takes a tool and works it over the coals; he shapes it with hammers and forges it with his strong arm; yet he grows hungry and his strength fails; he drinks no water and is faint.
The carpenter stretches a line, marks it with a stylus, works it with planes, and marks it with the compass; he shapes it in the form of a man, in human beauty, to dwell in a house.
He cuts down cedars, or takes a holm tree or an oak, letting it grow strong among the trees of the forest; he plants a fir, and the rain makes it grow.
It becomes fuel for a man; he takes part of it and warms himself; he kindles a fire and bakes bread; and he makes a god and worships it, fashions an idol and bows down to it.
Half of it he burns in the fire; over that half he roasts meat, eats his fill, and is satisfied; he warms himself and says, "Ah, I am warm, I have seen the fire!"
And the rest of it he makes into a god, his idol; he bows down to it and worships, and prays to it, saying, "Save me, for you are my god!"
They do not know, they do not understand; their eyes are shut so they cannot see, their hearts closed so they cannot grasp.

No one takes it to heart; none has the knowledge or the sense to say, "Half of it I burned in the fire; I baked bread on its coals; I roasted meat and ate. Shall I make the rest of it an abomination? Shall I bow down to a block of wood?"
He feeds on ashes; a deluded heart has led him astray; he cannot save himself or say, "Is there not a lie in my right hand?"
Remember these things, O Jacob, and Israel, for you are my servant; I formed you, you are my servant; O Israel, you will not be forgotten by me.
I have swept away your transgressions like a cloud, and your sins like mist; return to me, for I have redeemed you.
Sing, O heavens, for YHWH has done it; shout aloud, O depths of the earth; break into song, O mountains, O forest and every tree in it; for YHWH has redeemed Jacob, and will be glorified in Israel.
Thus says YHWH, your Redeemer, who formed you from the womb: I am YHWH, who made all things, who alone stretched out the heavens, who spread out the earth by myself,
who frustrates the signs of the diviners and makes fools of the soothsayers, who turns the wise back and makes their knowledge folly,

who confirms the word of his servant and fulfills the counsel of his messengers, who says of Jerusalem, "She shall be inhabited," and of the cities of Judah, "They shall be rebuilt, and I will raise up their ruins,"
who says to the deep, "Be dry; I will dry up your rivers,"
who says of Cyrus, "He is my shepherd, and he shall fulfill all my purpose," saying of Jerusalem, "She shall be rebuilt," and of the temple, "Your foundation shall be laid."

isa.45.text: Thus says YHWH to his anointed, to Cyrus, whose right hand I have grasped to subdue nations before him and strip kings of their armor, to open doors before him so that gates will not be shut:
I will go before you and level the mountains; I will shatter the doors of bronze and cut through the bars of iron.
I will give you the treasures of darkness and hoards in secret places, that you may know that it is I, YHWH, the God of Israel, who calls you by your name.
For the sake of Jacob my servant, and Israel my chosen, I call you by your name; I give you a title of honor, though you do not know me.
I am YHWH, and there is no other; besides me there is no god. I gird you, though you do not know me,
that they may know, from the rising of the sun to its setting, that there is none besides me. I am YHWH, and there is no other.
I form light and create darkness, I make peace and create calamity; I, YHWH, do all these things.
Rain down, O heavens, from above, and let the skies pour down righteousness; let the earth open, that salvation may bear fruit, and let righteousness spring up with it; I, YHWH, have created it.
Woe to him who quarrels with his Maker, a potsherd among the potsherds of the earth! Shall the clay say to the one who shapes it, "What are you making?" or your work say, "He has no hands"?
Woe to him who says to a father, "What are you begetting?" or to a woman, "What are you bringing to birth?"
Thus says YHWH, the Holy One of Israel and its Maker: Will you question me about my children, or command me concerning the work of my hands?
I made the earth and created man upon it; it was my hands that stretched out the heavens, and I marshaled all their host.
I have stirred him up in righteousness, and I will make all his ways straight; he shall rebuild my city and set my exiles free, not for a price and not for a bribe, says YHWH of hosts.

Thus says YHWH: The wealth of Egypt and the merchandise of Cush, and the Sabeans, tall of stature, shall come over to you and be yours; they shall follow you, they shall come over in chains; they shall bow down to you and plead with you, saying, "Surely God is with you, and there is no other; there is no other god."
Truly you are a God who hides himself, O God of Israel, the Savior.
They shall all be put to shame and disgraced; the makers of idols shall go off in disgrace together.
But Israel is saved by YHWH with an everlasting salvation; you shall not be put to shame or disgraced to all eternity.

For thus says YHWH, who created the heavens (he is God!), who formed the earth and made it (he established it; he did not create it a wasteland, but formed it to be inhabited): I am YHWH, and there is no other.
I did not speak in secret, in a land of darkness; I did not say to the offspring of Jacob, "Seek me in the void." I, YHWH, speak the truth; I declare what is right.
Gather together and come; draw near, you survivors of the nations! They have no knowledge, those who carry about their wooden idols and pray to a god that cannot save.

Declare it and present your case; yes, let them take counsel together. Who foretold this long ago, who declared it from of old? Was it not I, YHWH? There is no other god besides me, a righteous God and a Savior; there is none but me.
Turn to me and be saved, all the ends of the earth; for I am God, and there is no other.
By myself I have sworn; from my mouth has gone forth in righteousness a word that shall not return: To me every knee shall bow, every tongue shall swear allegiance.
"Only in YHWH," they shall say of me, "are righteousness and strength." To him shall come, put to shame, all who raged against him.
In YHWH all the offspring of Israel shall be justified and shall glory.

isa.46.text: Bel bows down, Nebo stoops; their idols are loaded on beasts and cattle; the things you carried are now a burden, a load for the weary animal.
They stoop, they bow down together; they cannot rescue the burden, and they themselves go into captivity.
Listen to me, O house of Jacob, all the remnant of the house of Israel, who have been carried by me from the belly, borne from the womb:
Even to your old age I am he, and to your gray hairs I will carry you. I have made you, and I will bear you; I will carry you and I will save you.
To whom will you liken me and make me equal, and compare me, as though we were alike?
Those who lavish gold from the purse and weigh out silver on the scales hire a goldsmith to make it into a god; then they bow down and worship it.
They lift it to their shoulders and carry it; they set it in its place, and there it stands, unable to move from its spot. If one cries to it, it does not answer or save him from his distress.
Remember this and stand firm; take it to heart, you rebels.
Remember the former things of old; for I am God, and there is no other; I am God, and there is none like me.
I declare the end from the beginning, and from ancient times what is not yet done, saying, "My purpose shall stand, and I will accomplish all that I please,"
calling a bird of prey from the east, from a far country the man of my purpose. What I have spoken I will bring to pass; what I have planned I will do.
Listen to me, you stubborn of heart, you who are far from righteousness:
I bring my righteousness near; it is not far off, and my salvation will not delay; I will grant salvation in Zion, my glory to Israel.

isa.47.text: Come down and sit in the dust, O virgin daughter of Babylon; sit on the ground without a throne, O daughter of the Chaldeans! No more will they call you tender and delicate.
Take the millstones and grind flour; remove your veil, strip off your skirt, bare your legs, wade through the rivers.
Your nakedness shall be uncovered, and your shame shall be seen. I will take vengeance, and I will spare no one.
Our Redeemer—YHWH of hosts is his name—is the Holy One of Israel.
Sit in silence, and go into the darkness, O daughter of the Chaldeans; no more will they call you the queen of kingdoms.
I was angry with my people; I profaned my heritage and gave them into your hand; you showed them no mercy; on the aged you laid your yoke very heavily.
You said, "I shall be queen forever," so that you did not take these things to heart or remember their outcome.
Now hear this, you lover of pleasures, who sit secure, who say in your heart, "I am, and there is none besides me; I shall not sit as a widow or know the loss of children."
These two things shall come upon you in a moment, in a single day: loss of children and widowhood shall come upon you in full measure, for all your many sorceries and the great power of your spells.
You felt secure in your wickedness; you said, "No one sees me." Your wisdom and your knowledge led you astray, and you said in your heart, "I am, and there is none besides me."
But disaster shall come upon you, which you cannot charm away; ruin shall fall upon you, which you cannot ward off; and devastation shall come upon you suddenly, before you know it.
Stand fast now with your spells and your many sorceries, at which you have toiled from your youth; perhaps you can succeed, perhaps you can strike terror.
You are wearied with your many counsels; let them stand up and save you, those who divide the heavens, who gaze at the stars, who announce at each new moon what shall come upon you.
Behold, they are like stubble; the fire consumes them; they cannot save themselves from the power of the flame. This is no coal to warm by, no fire to sit before!
Such to you are those for whom you toiled, those who trafficked with you from your youth; each wanders off his own way, and there is no one to save you.

isa.48.text: Hear this, O house of Jacob, you who are called by the name of Israel and who came from the waters of Judah, who swear by the name of YHWH and invoke the God of Israel—but not in truth or in righteousness.
For they call themselves after the holy city, and lean on the God of Israel—YHWH of hosts is his name.
The former things I declared long ago; they went forth from my mouth, and I proclaimed them; then suddenly I acted, and they came to pass.
Because I knew that you are stubborn, that your neck is an iron sinew and your forehead bronze,
I declared it to you long ago; before it came to pass I proclaimed it to you, lest you should say, "My idol did it; my carved and molten image commanded it."
You have heard; now look at all of it. Will you not admit it? From now on I make you hear new things, hidden things you have not known.
They are created now, not long ago; before today you have not heard of them, lest you say, "Behold, I knew them already."
You have not heard, you have not known, and from of old your ear has not been open; for I knew that you would deal treacherously, that from birth you were called a rebel.
For my own name's sake I restrain my anger; for the sake of my praise I hold back from you, so as not to cut you off.
Behold, I have refined you, but not as silver; I have tested you in the furnace of affliction.
For my own sake, for my own sake, I do it; for how should my name be profaned? My glory I will not give to another.
Listen to me, O Jacob, and Israel whom I called: I am he; I am the first, and I am the last.
My own hand laid the foundation of the earth, and my right hand spread out the heavens; when I summon them, they stand forth together.
Assemble, all of you, and listen! Who among them has declared these things? YHWH loves him; he shall carry out his purpose against Babylon, and his arm shall be against the Chaldeans.
I, even I, have spoken; yes, I have called him; I have brought him, and he shall prosper in his way.
Draw near to me, hear this: from the beginning I have not spoken in secret; from the time it came to be, I was there. And now the Lord YHWH has sent me, and his Spirit.
Thus says YHWH, your Redeemer, the Holy One of Israel: I am YHWH your God, who teaches you for your good, who leads you in the way you should go.
If only you had heeded my commandments! Then your peace would have been like a river, and your righteousness like the waves of the sea.
Your offspring would have been like the sand, and the children of your body like its grains; their name would never be cut off or destroyed before me.
Go out from Babylon, flee from the Chaldeans! With a shout of joy proclaim this, declare it, send it out to the ends of the earth; say, "YHWH has redeemed his servant Jacob!"
They did not thirst when he led them through the deserts; he made water flow for them from the rock; he split the rock, and water gushed out.
"There is no peace," says YHWH, "for the wicked."

isa.49.text: Listen to me, O coastlands, and attend, you peoples far off: YHWH called me from the womb; from my mother's body he named my name.
He made my mouth like a sharpened sword; in the shadow of his hand he hid me. He made me a polished arrow; in his quiver he kept me hidden.
And he said to me, "You are my servant, Israel, in whom I will be glorified."
But I said, "I have labored for nothing; I have spent my strength for emptiness and breath. Yet surely my justice is with YHWH, and my reward with my God."

And now YHWH speaks — he who formed me from the womb to be his servant, to bring Jacob back to him, that Israel might be gathered to him; for I am honored in the eyes of YHWH, and my God has become my strength —

he says: "It is too small a thing that you should be my servant to raise up the tribes of Jacob and restore the survivors of Israel; I will make you a light to the nations, that my salvation may reach to the end of the earth."

Thus says YHWH, the Redeemer of Israel, his Holy One, to the one despised in soul, abhorred by the nations, the slave of rulers: "Kings shall see and rise, princes shall bow down, because of YHWH, who is faithful, the Holy One of Israel, who has chosen you."

Thus says YHWH: "In the time of favor I answered you, and in the day of salvation I helped you; I will keep you and give you as a covenant to the people, to raise up the land, to apportion the desolate inheritances,
saying to the prisoners, 'Come out,' and to those in darkness, 'Show yourselves.' Along the roads they shall graze, and on all the bare heights their pasture shall be.
They shall not hunger or thirst, neither scorching heat nor sun shall strike them; for he who has mercy on them will lead them, and by springs of water he will guide them.
And I will make all my mountains into a road, and my highways shall be raised high.
Look — these shall come from far away; and look — these from the north and from the sea, and these from the land of Sinim."
Sing, O heavens, and rejoice, O earth; break out, O mountains, into song! For YHWH has comforted his people, and on his afflicted he has mercy.
But Zion said, "YHWH has forsaken me; my Lord has forgotten me."
Can a woman forget her nursing child, and not have compassion on the son of her womb? Even these may forget, yet I will not forget you.
See, I have carved you on the palms of my hands; your walls are ever before me.
Your builders hurry back; those who tore you down and laid you waste go out from you.
Lift up your eyes and look around: they all gather, they come to you. As I live, declares YHWH, you shall put them all on like an ornament, and bind them on like a bride.
For your ruins and your desolate places, your devastated land — surely now you will be too cramped for the inhabitants, and those who swallowed you will be far away.
The children of your bereavement shall yet say in your ears, "This place is too narrow for me; make room for me to settle."
Then you will say in your heart, "Who bore me these? I was bereaved and barren, exiled and cast aside — who raised these? I was left all alone; where then have these come from?"
Thus says the Lord YHWH: "See, I will lift up my hand to the nations, and to the peoples raise my banner; and they shall bring your sons in their arms, and your daughters carried on their shoulders.

Kings shall be your foster fathers, and their queens your nursing mothers. With faces to the ground they shall bow before you, and lick the dust of your feet. Then you will know that I am YHWH; those who wait for me shall not be put to shame."
Can the prey be taken from the mighty, or the captive of a tyrant be rescued?

For thus says YHWH: "Even the captive of the mighty shall be taken, and the prey of the tyrant rescued; for I myself will contend with those who contend with you, and I myself will save your children.

I will make your oppressors eat their own flesh, and they shall be drunk on their own blood as with new wine. Then all flesh shall know that I, YHWH, am your Savior, your Redeemer, the Mighty One of Jacob."

isa.50.text: Thus says YHWH: "Where is your mother's certificate of divorce, with which I sent her away? Or to which of my creditors did I sell you? See, for your iniquities you were sold, and for your transgressions your mother was sent away.

Why, when I came, was there no one? When I called, why did no one answer? Is my hand too short to redeem, or have I no strength to rescue? Look — by my rebuke I dry up the sea, I turn rivers to desert; their fish stink for lack of water and die of thirst.
I clothe the heavens in blackness, and make sackcloth their covering."
The Lord YHWH has given me the tongue of the learned, to know how to sustain the weary with a word. Morning by morning he wakens — he wakens my ear to hear as those who are taught.
The Lord YHWH has opened my ear, and I did not rebel; I did not turn back.
I gave my back to those who struck me, and my cheeks to those who tore out the beard; I did not hide my face from insult and spitting.
For the Lord YHWH helps me; therefore I am not disgraced; therefore I have set my face like flint, and I know I shall not be put to shame.
He who vindicates me is near. Who will contend with me? Let us stand together. Who is my accuser? Let him come to me.
See, the Lord YHWH helps me; who is he that will condemn me? Look — they will all wear out like a garment; the moth will devour them.
Who among you fears YHWH and obeys the voice of his servant? Whoever walks in darkness with no light for him — let him trust in the name of YHWH and lean on his God.

But look, all you who kindle fire, who gird yourselves with firebrands: walk in the flame of your own fire, in the brands you have set ablaze! This is what you shall have from my hand — you shall lie down in torment.

isa.51.text: Listen to me, you who pursue righteousness, who seek YHWH: look to the rock from which you were hewn, to the quarry from which you were dug.
Look to Abraham your father, and to Sarah who bore you; for I called him when he was but one, and I blessed him and made him many.
For YHWH comforts Zion; he comforts all her ruins. He makes her wilderness like Eden, her desert like the garden of YHWH; joy and gladness shall be found in her, thanksgiving and the sound of song.
Attend to me, my people; give ear to me, my nation. For instruction goes out from me, and my justice as a light to the peoples.
My righteousness draws near, my salvation goes forth, and my arms will rule the peoples; the coastlands wait for me, and for my arm they hope.

Lift up your eyes to the heavens, and look at the earth beneath: the heavens will vanish like smoke, the earth will wear out like a garment, and its dwellers will die like gnats — but my salvation will last forever, and my righteousness will never be shattered.
Listen to me, you who know righteousness, the people in whose heart is my instruction: do not fear the reproach of mere men, and do not be shattered by their reviling.
For the moth will eat them like a garment, and the worm will devour them like wool — but my righteousness will last forever, and my salvation to all generations.
Awake, awake, put on strength, O arm of YHWH! Awake as in days of old, in generations long ago. Was it not you who cut Rahab in pieces, who pierced the dragon?
Was it not you who dried up the sea, the waters of the great deep, who made the depths of the sea a road for the redeemed to cross over?
The ransomed of YHWH shall return; they shall come to Zion with singing, everlasting joy upon their heads. Gladness and joy shall overtake them, and sorrow and sighing shall flee away.
I, I am he who comforts you. Who are you, that you fear mortal man, a son of man who is given up like grass —

that you forget YHWH your Maker, who stretched out the heavens and laid the foundations of the earth, and dread continually, all day long, the fury of the oppressor as he sets himself to destroy? And where now is the fury of the oppressor?
The captive bent low will soon be set free; he will not die in the pit, nor will his bread run out.
For I am YHWH your God, who stirs the sea so its waves roar — YHWH of hosts is his name.
I have put my words in your mouth and covered you in the shadow of my hand, to plant the heavens and lay the foundations of the earth, and to say to Zion, "You are my people."
Rouse yourself, rouse yourself, stand up, O Jerusalem, you who have drunk from the hand of YHWH the cup of his fury, who have drained to the dregs the goblet, the cup of reeling.
There is no one to guide her among all the children she has borne; there is no one to take her by the hand among all the children she has raised.
These two things have befallen you — who will grieve for you? — devastation and ruin, famine and sword. Who will comfort you?
Your children have fainted; they lie at the head of every street like an antelope in a net, filled with the fury of YHWH, the rebuke of your God.
Therefore hear this, you afflicted one, drunk but not with wine.
Thus says your Lord, YHWH, your God who defends his people: "See, I have taken from your hand the cup of reeling, the goblet, the cup of my fury; you shall never drink it again.
I will put it into the hand of your tormentors, who said to your soul, 'Bow down, that we may walk over you' — and you made your back like the ground, like a street for them to cross."

isa.52.text: Awake, awake, put on your strength, O Zion; put on your garments of splendor, O Jerusalem, the holy city. For never again shall the uncircumcised and the unclean enter you.
Shake yourself from the dust; arise, be seated, O Jerusalem. Loose the chains from your neck, O captive daughter of Zion.
For thus says YHWH: "You were sold for nothing, and you shall be redeemed without money."
For thus says the Lord YHWH: "At the first my people went down to Egypt to sojourn there, and Assyria oppressed them for nothing.
And now what have I here, declares YHWH, that my people are taken away for nothing? Their rulers wail, declares YHWH, and continually, all day long, my name is despised.
Therefore my people shall know my name; therefore in that day they shall know that it is I who speak — here I am."
How beautiful upon the mountains are the feet of the herald who brings good news, who proclaims peace, who brings tidings of good, who proclaims salvation, who says to Zion, "Your God reigns!"
Listen! Your watchmen lift up their voices; together they shout for joy; for with their own eyes they see the return of YHWH to Zion.
Break out together into singing, you ruins of Jerusalem! For YHWH has comforted his people, he has redeemed Jerusalem.
YHWH has bared his holy arm before the eyes of all the nations, and all the ends of the earth shall see the salvation of our God.
Depart, depart, go out from there; touch nothing unclean! Go out from her midst; purify yourselves, you who bear the vessels of YHWH.
For you shall not go out in haste, nor go in flight; for YHWH goes before you, and the God of Israel is your rear guard.
See, my servant shall prosper; he shall be raised and lifted up and highly exalted.
Just as many were appalled at you — so marred was his appearance, beyond that of any man, his form beyond the sons of men —
so shall he startle many nations; kings shall shut their mouths because of him. For what was never told them they shall see, and what they never heard they shall understand.

isa.53.text: Who has believed our report? And to whom has the arm of YHWH been revealed?
He grew up before him like a young shoot, like a root out of dry ground. He had no form or splendor that we should look at him, no beauty that we should desire him.
He was despised and rejected by men, a man of sorrows and acquainted with grief; like one from whom men hide their faces, he was despised, and we held him of no account.
Yet surely it was our griefs he bore, and our sorrows he carried; but we counted him stricken, struck down by God, and afflicted.
But he was pierced for our transgressions, crushed for our iniquities; the punishment that brought us peace was upon him, and by his wounds we are healed.
We all like sheep have strayed; each of us has turned to his own way; and YHWH has laid on him the iniquity of us all.
He was oppressed and afflicted, yet he did not open his mouth; like a lamb led to the slaughter, like a ewe silent before her shearers, so he did not open his mouth.
By oppression and judgment he was taken away; and who of his generation gave it a thought — that he was cut off from the land of the living, stricken for the transgression of my people?
They made his grave with the wicked, and with a rich man in his death, though he had done no violence, and there was no deceit in his mouth.
Yet it was the will of YHWH to crush him and lay him low. When you make his life an offering for guilt, he shall see offspring and prolong his days; and the will of YHWH shall prosper in his hand.
Out of the anguish of his soul he shall see and be satisfied; by his knowledge my righteous servant shall justify the many, and their iniquities he himself shall bear.

Therefore I will give him a portion among the great, and he shall divide the spoil with the strong; because he poured out his soul to death and was numbered with the transgressors, yet he bore the sin of many and made intercession for the transgressors.

isa.54.text: Sing, O barren one who never bore; break out into singing and cry aloud, you who were never in labor. For the children of the desolate are more than the children of her that is married, says YHWH.
Enlarge the place of your tent; let the curtains of your dwellings stretch wide; do not hold back. Lengthen your cords and strengthen your stakes.
For you shall spread out to the right and to the left; your offspring shall dispossess nations and resettle the desolate cities.
Do not fear, for you shall not be ashamed; do not be disgraced, for you shall not be humiliated. You will forget the shame of your youth, and remember no more the reproach of your widowhood.
For your Maker is your husband — YHWH of hosts is his name — and the Holy One of Israel is your Redeemer; the God of all the earth he is called.
For YHWH has called you back like a wife forsaken and grieved in spirit, like a wife of one's youth when she is cast off, says your God.
For a brief moment I forsook you, but with great compassion I will gather you.
In a flood of wrath I hid my face from you for a moment, but with everlasting love I will have compassion on you, says YHWH your Redeemer.
For this is to me like the days of Noah, when I swore that the waters of Noah would never again cover the earth; so I have sworn not to be angry with you and not to rebuke you.
Though the mountains may depart and the hills be shaken, my love shall not depart from you, and my covenant of peace shall not be shaken, says YHWH, who has compassion on you.
O afflicted one, storm-tossed and uncomforted: see, I will set your stones in antimony, and lay your foundations with sapphires.
I will make your battlements of rubies, your gates of sparkling jewels, and all your walls of precious stones.
All your children shall be taught by YHWH, and great shall be the peace of your children.
In righteousness you shall be established; you shall be far from oppression, for you shall not fear, and from terror, for it shall not come near you.
If anyone stirs up strife, it is not from me; whoever attacks you shall fall because of you.
See, I have created the smith who blows the coals in the fire and forges a weapon fit for its work; and I have created the destroyer to lay waste.

No weapon forged against you shall prosper, and every tongue that rises against you in judgment you shall condemn. This is the heritage of the servants of YHWH, and their vindication from me, declares YHWH.

isa.55.text: Come, all you who thirst, come to the waters; and you who have no money, come, buy and eat! Come, buy wine and milk without money and without price.
Why spend money on what is not bread, and your labor on what does not satisfy? Listen carefully to me, and eat what is good, and let your soul delight in rich fare.
Incline your ear and come to me; listen, that your soul may live. And I will make with you an everlasting covenant, my sure mercies promised to David.
See, I made him a witness to the peoples, a leader and commander of the peoples.
See, you shall summon a nation you do not know, and nations that do not know you shall run to you, because of YHWH your God, the Holy One of Israel, for he has glorified you.
Seek YHWH while he may be found; call on him while he is near.
Let the wicked forsake his way, and the man of iniquity his thoughts; let him return to YHWH, and he will have mercy on him, and to our God, for he will abundantly pardon.
For my thoughts are not your thoughts, nor are your ways my ways, declares YHWH.
For as the heavens are higher than the earth, so are my ways higher than your ways, and my thoughts than your thoughts.
For as the rain and the snow come down from heaven and do not return there, but water the earth, making it bring forth and sprout, giving seed to the sower and bread to the eater,
so shall my word be that goes out from my mouth: it shall not return to me empty, but shall accomplish what I desire and prosper in the thing for which I sent it.
For you shall go out in joy and be led forth in peace; the mountains and the hills shall break out before you into singing, and all the trees of the field shall clap their hands.
Instead of the thornbush the cypress shall rise, and instead of the briar the myrtle shall rise; and it shall be to YHWH a name, an everlasting sign that shall never be cut off.

isa.56.text: Thus says YHWH: Keep justice, and do what is right, for my salvation is near to come, and my deliverance to be revealed.
Blessed is the man who does this, the child of dust who holds it fast: who keeps the Sabbath without profaning it, and keeps his hand from doing any evil.
Let no foreigner who has joined himself to YHWH say, "YHWH will surely cut me off from his people." And let the eunuch not say, "Look, I am a dry tree."
For thus says YHWH: To the eunuchs who keep my Sabbaths, who choose what pleases me and hold fast to my covenant —
to them I will give within my house and within my walls a monument and a name better than sons and daughters; I will give them an everlasting name that shall never be cut off.
And the foreigners who join themselves to YHWH, to serve him and to love the name of YHWH, to be his servants — everyone who keeps the Sabbath without profaning it, and holds fast to my covenant —

these I will bring to my holy mountain, and make them joyful in my house of prayer; their burnt offerings and their sacrifices shall be accepted upon my altar, for my house shall be called a house of prayer for all peoples.
Thus declares the Lord YHWH, who gathers the outcasts of Israel: I will gather still more to him, beyond those already gathered.
All you beasts of the field, come to devour — all you beasts in the forest.
His watchmen are blind, all of them; they know nothing. They are all mute dogs that cannot bark, dreaming, lying down, in love with sleep.
Greedy dogs that never have enough — and these are the shepherds who cannot understand. They have all turned to their own way, each to his own gain, every last one of them.
"Come," they say, "let me fetch wine, and let us swill strong drink; and tomorrow shall be like today, great beyond all measure."

isa.57.text: The righteous man perishes, and no one lays it to heart; the faithful are swept away, and no one understands that the righteous is taken away from the face of the evil to come.
He enters into peace; they rest upon their beds, each who walked in his uprightness.
But you — come here, you sons of a sorceress, offspring of an adulterer and a whore.
At whom are you sneering? At whom do you open wide the mouth and stick out the tongue? Are you not children of rebellion, offspring of a lie —
you who burn with lust among the oaks, under every green tree, who slaughter your children in the wadis, under the clefts of the rocks?
Among the smooth stones of the wadi is your portion; they, they are your lot. To them you have poured out a drink offering, you have brought up a grain offering. Shall I relent over these?
On a high and lofty mountain you have set your bed; there too you went up to offer sacrifice.

Behind the door and the doorpost you have set your emblem; forsaking me, you stripped yourself and went up, you made your bed wide and cut a bargain with them; you loved their bed, you gazed on their nakedness.
You journeyed to the king with oil and heaped up your perfumes; you sent your envoys far off and sent them down even to Sheol.
You wore yourself out with the length of your road, yet you never said, "It is hopeless." You found the life of your hand renewed, and so you did not grow weak.
Whom did you dread and fear, that you dealt so falsely, and did not remember me, nor lay it to your heart? Is it not because I have kept silent so long that you do not fear me?
I will expose your righteousness — and your works — and they will do you no good.

When you cry out, let your hoard of idols deliver you! The wind will carry them all off, a breath will take them away. But he who takes refuge in me shall inherit the land and possess my holy mountain.
And it shall be said: Build up, build up, clear the road, lift every stumbling block out of the way of my people.

For thus says the high and lofty One who dwells in eternity, whose name is Holy: I dwell in the high and holy place, yet also with the crushed and lowly of spirit, to revive the spirit of the lowly, to revive the heart of the crushed.
For I will not contend forever, nor will I always be angry; for the spirit would faint before me, and the souls of breath that I myself have made.
For the guilt of his greed I was angry and struck him; I hid my face and was angry, and he went on wayward in the way of his own heart.
I have seen his ways, and I will heal him; I will lead him and restore comfort to him and to his mourners,
creating the fruit of the lips: Peace, peace, to the far and to the near, says YHWH; and I will heal him.
But the wicked are like the tossing sea that cannot be still, whose waters churn up mire and mud.
There is no peace, says my God, for the wicked.

isa.58.text: Cry out with full throat, do not hold back; lift up your voice like a trumpet, and declare to my people their rebellion, to the house of Jacob their sins.

Yet day after day they seek me and delight to know my ways, like a nation that has done what is right and has not forsaken the justice of its God; they ask of me righteous judgments, they delight to draw near to God.
"Why have we fasted, and you do not see? Why have we humbled ourselves, and you take no notice?" Look — on the day of your fast you pursue your own desire, and drive hard all your laborers.
Look — you fast only for strife and quarreling, to strike with a wicked fist. You do not fast this day so as to make your voice heard on high.
Is this the fast I choose, a day for a man to humble himself — to bow down his head like a reed and spread out sackcloth and ashes? Is this what you call a fast, a day acceptable to YHWH?
Is not this the fast I choose: to loosen the chains of wickedness, to untie the cords of the yoke, to set the crushed free, and to break every yoke?
Is it not to share your bread with the hungry, and to bring the homeless poor into your house; when you see the naked, to cover him, and not to hide yourself from your own flesh?
Then your light shall break forth like the dawn, and your healing shall spring up quickly; your righteousness shall go before you, and the glory of YHWH shall be your rear guard.
Then you shall call, and YHWH will answer; you shall cry, and he will say, "Here I am." If you remove the yoke from your midst, the pointing finger and the malicious word,
if you pour yourself out for the hungry and satisfy the afflicted soul, then your light shall rise in the darkness, and your gloom shall be as the noonday.
And YHWH will guide you continually and satisfy your soul in scorched places, and make strong your bones; you shall be like a watered garden, like a spring of water whose waters never fail.
Your people shall rebuild the ancient ruins; you shall raise up the foundations of many generations; you shall be called the Repairer of the Breach, the Restorer of Streets to dwell in.

If you turn back your foot from the Sabbath, from pursuing your own desire on my holy day, and call the Sabbath a delight, the holy day of YHWH honorable; if you honor it, not going your own ways, not seeking your own desire or speaking your own words —
then you shall delight yourself in YHWH, and I will make you ride upon the heights of the earth, and feed you with the heritage of Jacob your father; for the mouth of YHWH has spoken.

isa.59.text: Look — the hand of YHWH is not too short to save, nor his ear too dull to hear.
It is your iniquities that have made a division between you and your God, and your sins that have hidden his face from you, so that he does not hear.
For your palms are defiled with blood, and your fingers with guilt; your lips have spoken lies, your tongue mutters malice.
No one calls out for justice, none pleads a case in truth; they trust in emptiness and speak worthless words, they conceive trouble and give birth to evil.
They hatch the eggs of vipers and weave the spider's web; whoever eats their eggs dies, and if one is crushed, a viper breaks out.
Their webs will not become clothing, nor will they cover themselves with their works; their works are works of evil, and deeds of violence are in their hands.
Their feet run to evil, and they rush to shed innocent blood; their thoughts are thoughts of evil, ruin and wreckage are in their highways.
The way of peace they do not know, and there is no justice in their tracks; they have made their paths crooked, and no one who walks in them knows peace.
Therefore justice is far from us, and righteousness does not overtake us; we wait for light, and behold, darkness — for brightness, and we walk in gloom.
We grope for the wall like the blind, we grope like those with no eyes; we stumble at noon as in the twilight, among the vigorous we are like the dead.
We all growl like bears, and moan bitterly like doves; we wait for justice, and there is none — for salvation, and it is far from us.
For our rebellions are many before you, and our sins testify against us; our rebellions are with us, and our iniquities — we know them:
rebelling and denying YHWH, turning back from following our God, speaking oppression and revolt, conceiving and muttering lying words from the heart.
And justice is turned back, and righteousness stands far off; for truth has stumbled in the public square, and honesty cannot enter.
Truth is gone missing, and whoever turns from evil is plundered. YHWH saw it, and it was evil in his eyes that there was no justice.
He saw that there was no one, and was appalled that there was no one to intervene; so his own arm brought him salvation, and his own righteousness upheld him.
He put on righteousness like a breastplate, and a helmet of salvation on his head; he put on garments of vengeance for clothing, and wrapped himself in zeal like a cloak.
According to their deeds he will repay: wrath to his adversaries, recompense to his enemies; to the coastlands he will render their due.
So they shall fear the name of YHWH from the west, and his glory from the rising of the sun; for he will come like a pent-up river, driven by the breath of YHWH.
And a Redeemer will come to Zion, to those in Jacob who turn from rebellion, declares YHWH.

As for me, this is my covenant with them, says YHWH: My spirit that is upon you, and my words that I have put in your mouth, shall not depart from your mouth, or from the mouth of your offspring, or from the mouth of your offspring's offspring, says YHWH, from this time forth and forever.

isa.60.text: Arise, shine, for your light has come, and the glory of YHWH has risen upon you.
For behold, darkness shall cover the earth, and thick darkness the peoples; but upon you YHWH will rise, and his glory shall be seen upon you.
Nations shall come to your light, and kings to the brightness of your dawning.
Lift up your eyes all around and see: they all gather together, they come to you; your sons come from far away, and your daughters are carried on the hip.
Then you shall see and be radiant; your heart shall throb and swell, for the abundance of the sea shall be turned to you, the wealth of the nations shall come to you.
A flood of camels shall cover you, the young camels of Midian and Ephah; all those from Sheba shall come, bearing gold and frankincense, and proclaiming the praises of YHWH.
All the flocks of Kedar shall be gathered to you, the rams of Nebaioth shall serve you; they shall go up with favor on my altar, and I will glorify my glorious house.
Who are these that fly like a cloud, and like doves to their windows?

For the coastlands wait for me, the ships of Tarshish first, to bring your sons from far away, their silver and their gold with them, for the name of YHWH your God, for the Holy One of Israel, because he has glorified you.
Foreigners shall build up your walls, and their kings shall serve you; for in my wrath I struck you, but in my favor I have had mercy on you.
Your gates shall stand open continually — day and night they shall not be shut — to bring in to you the wealth of the nations, with their kings led in procession.
For the nation and kingdom that will not serve you shall perish; those nations shall be utterly laid waste.
The glory of Lebanon shall come to you, the cypress, the plane, and the pine together, to beautify the place of my sanctuary; and I will glorify the place of my feet.

The sons of those who afflicted you shall come bowing low to you, and all who despised you shall bow down at the soles of your feet; they shall call you the City of YHWH, the Zion of the Holy One of Israel.
Where once you were forsaken and hated, with no one passing through, I will make you an everlasting pride, a joy from generation to generation.
You shall drink the milk of nations, you shall nurse at the breast of kings; and you shall know that I, YHWH, am your Savior and your Redeemer, the Mighty One of Jacob.
Instead of bronze I will bring gold, instead of iron I will bring silver, instead of wood, bronze, and instead of stones, iron; I will make Peace your overseer and Righteousness your taskmaster.
No more shall violence be heard in your land, nor ruin and wreckage within your borders; you shall call your walls Salvation and your gates Praise.
The sun shall no longer be your light by day, nor the moon give you light by its brightness; but YHWH will be your everlasting light, and your God your glory.
Your sun shall no longer set, nor your moon withdraw; for YHWH will be your everlasting light, and the days of your mourning shall be ended.
Your people shall all be righteous; they shall possess the land forever — the shoot of my planting, the work of my hands, that I may be glorified.
The least of them shall become a thousand, and the smallest a mighty nation; I am YHWH — in its time I will hasten it.

isa.61.text: The Spirit of the Lord YHWH is upon me, because YHWH has anointed me to bring good news to the poor; he has sent me to bind up the brokenhearted, to proclaim liberty to the captives, and release to the prisoners;
to proclaim the year of YHWH's favor, and the day of vengeance of our God; to comfort all who mourn;

to grant to the mourners of Zion — to give them a crown of beauty instead of ashes, the oil of gladness instead of mourning, a mantle of praise instead of a faint spirit; so they shall be called oaks of righteousness, the planting of YHWH, that he may be glorified.
They shall rebuild the ancient ruins, they shall raise up the former devastations; they shall restore the ruined cities, the desolations of many generations.
Strangers shall stand and pasture your flocks, and foreigners shall be your plowmen and your vinedressers.
But you shall be called the priests of YHWH; they shall speak of you as the ministers of our God. You shall eat the wealth of the nations, and in their glory you shall boast.

Instead of your shame you shall have a double portion, and instead of disgrace they shall shout for joy over their inheritance; therefore in their land they shall possess a double portion, and everlasting joy shall be theirs.
For I, YHWH, love justice; I hate robbery and wrongdoing. I will faithfully give them their reward, and make an everlasting covenant with them.
Their offspring shall be known among the nations, and their descendants in the midst of the peoples; all who see them shall recognize them as the offspring YHWH has blessed.

I will greatly rejoice in YHWH, my soul shall exult in my God; for he has clothed me with garments of salvation, he has wrapped me in a robe of righteousness, as a bridegroom decks himself with a garland, and as a bride adorns herself with her jewels.
For as the earth brings forth its shoots, and as a garden makes its sowings spring up, so the Lord YHWH will make righteousness and praise spring up before all the nations.

isa.62.text: For Zion's sake I will not keep silent, and for Jerusalem's sake I will not rest, until her righteousness goes forth like brightness, and her salvation like a burning torch.
The nations shall see your righteousness, and all kings your glory; and you shall be called by a new name that the mouth of YHWH will assign.
You shall be a crown of beauty in the hand of YHWH, a royal diadem in the palm of your God.

No more shall you be called Forsaken, and no more shall your land be called Desolate; but you shall be called My Delight Is in Her, and your land Married; for YHWH delights in you, and your land shall be married.
For as a young man marries a virgin, so shall your sons marry you; and as the bridegroom rejoices over the bride, so shall your God rejoice over you.
On your walls, O Jerusalem, I have posted watchmen; all day and all night they shall never be silent. You who call YHWH to remembrance, take no rest,
and give him no rest until he establishes Jerusalem and makes it the praise of the earth.
YHWH has sworn by his right hand and by his mighty arm: Never again will I give your grain as food to your enemies, nor shall foreigners drink your new wine for which you have toiled;
but those who harvest it shall eat it and praise YHWH, and those who gather it shall drink it in the courts of my sanctuary.
Pass through, pass through the gates! Clear the way for the people! Build up, build up the highway, clear it of stones; lift up a banner over the peoples.
Behold, YHWH has proclaimed to the ends of the earth: Say to the daughter of Zion, "Behold, your salvation comes; behold, his reward is with him, and his recompense before him."
And they shall be called the Holy People, the Redeemed of YHWH; and you shall be called Sought Out, a City Not Forsaken.

isa.63.text: Who is this coming from Edom, in crimson-stained garments from Bozrah — this one so splendid in his robe, striding in the greatness of his strength? "It is I, speaking in righteousness, mighty to save."
Why is your robe red, and your garments like one who treads the winepress?

"I have trodden the winepress alone, and from the peoples no one was with me. I trod them in my anger and trampled them in my wrath; their lifeblood spattered my garments, and I stained all my clothing.
For the day of vengeance was in my heart, and the year of my redemption had come.
I looked, and there was no one to help; I was appalled that there was no one to uphold; so my own arm brought me salvation, and my own wrath upheld me.
I trampled peoples in my anger, I made them drunk in my wrath, and I poured out their lifeblood on the earth."

I will recount the steadfast love of YHWH, the praises of YHWH, for all that YHWH has done for us — the great goodness to the house of Israel that he granted them in his mercy, in the abundance of his steadfast love.
For he said, "Surely they are my people, children who will not be false"; and he became their Savior.
In all their affliction he was afflicted, and the angel of his presence saved them; in his love and his pity he redeemed them; he lifted them up and carried them all the days of old.
But they rebelled and grieved his Holy Spirit; so he turned to become their enemy, and he himself fought against them.
Then his people remembered the days of old, of Moses: Where is he who brought them up out of the sea with the shepherds of his flock? Where is he who put his Holy Spirit within him,
who sent his glorious arm to go at the right hand of Moses, who split the waters before them to make for himself an everlasting name,
who led them through the depths? Like a horse in the wilderness, they did not stumble.
Like cattle going down into the valley, the Spirit of YHWH gave them rest. So you led your people, to make for yourself a glorious name.
Look down from heaven and see, from your holy and glorious habitation. Where is your zeal and your might? The stirring of your inmost heart and your mercies are held back from me.
For you are our Father, though Abraham does not know us and Israel does not acknowledge us; you, YHWH, are our Father; Our Redeemer from of old is your name.
Why, YHWH, do you make us wander from your ways and harden our hearts, so we do not fear you? Return, for the sake of your servants, the tribes of your inheritance.
For a little while your holy people held possession; our foes have trampled your sanctuary.
We have become like those you never ruled, like those who were never called by your name. Oh, that you would tear open the heavens and come down, that the mountains would quake at your presence —

isa.64.text: as when fire kindles the brushwood and makes the waters boil, to make your name known to your foes, so that the nations tremble at your presence!
When you did awesome things we did not look for, you came down, and the mountains quaked at your presence.
