From of old no one has heard, no ear has perceived, no eye has seen any God but you, who acts for those who wait for him.
You meet the one who rejoices to do righteousness, those who remember you in your ways. Behold, you were angry, and we sinned; in our sins we have been a long time — and can we be saved?
We have all become like one unclean, and all our righteous deeds are like a filthy rag; we all wither like a leaf, and our iniquities carry us off like the wind.
There is no one who calls on your name, who rouses himself to take hold of you; for you have hidden your face from us, and melted us away by the hand of our iniquities.
Yet now, YHWH, you are our Father; we are the clay, and you are our potter; we are all the work of your hand.
Do not be angry beyond measure, YHWH, and do not remember iniquity forever. Behold, please look — we are all your people.
Your holy cities have become a wilderness; Zion has become a wilderness, Jerusalem a desolation.
Our holy and beautiful house, where our fathers praised you, has been burned with fire, and all that we treasured has become a ruin.
At these things will you restrain yourself, YHWH? Will you keep silent and afflict us beyond measure?

isa.65.text: I let myself be sought by those who did not ask; I let myself be found by those who did not seek me. I said, "Here I am, here I am," to a nation that did not call on my name.
All day long I stretched out my hands to a rebellious people, who walk in a way that is not good, after their own devices —
a people who provoke me to my face continually, sacrificing in gardens and burning incense on bricks;
who sit among the graves and spend the night in secret places, who eat the flesh of swine, with broth of foul things in their pots;
who say, "Keep to yourself, do not come near me, for I am holier than you." These are smoke in my nostrils, a fire that burns all day long.
Behold, it stands written before me: I will not keep silent, but I will repay; I will repay into their lap
your iniquities and the iniquities of your fathers together, says YHWH, who burned incense on the mountains and reviled me on the hills; I will measure their former deeds into their lap.
Thus says YHWH: As the new wine is found in the cluster, and one says, "Do not destroy it, for there is a blessing in it," so will I do for the sake of my servants, and not destroy them all.
I will bring forth offspring from Jacob, and from Judah an heir to my mountains; my chosen shall possess it, and my servants shall dwell there.
Sharon shall become a pasture for flocks, and the Valley of Achor a resting place for herds, for my people who have sought me.
But you who forsake YHWH, who forget my holy mountain, who set a table for Fortune and fill cups of mixed wine for Destiny —

I will destine you for the sword, and you shall all bow down to the slaughter; because I called and you did not answer, I spoke and you did not listen, but you did what was evil in my eyes and chose what did not please me.

Therefore thus says the Lord YHWH: Behold, my servants shall eat, but you shall go hungry; behold, my servants shall drink, but you shall go thirsty; behold, my servants shall rejoice, but you shall be put to shame;
behold, my servants shall sing for gladness of heart, but you shall cry out for pain of heart, and wail for brokenness of spirit.
You shall leave your name for a curse to my chosen, and the Lord YHWH will put you to death; but his servants he will call by another name.

So that whoever blesses himself in the land shall bless himself by the God of truth, and whoever swears in the land shall swear by the God of truth; for the former troubles are forgotten, and hidden from my eyes.
For behold, I am creating new heavens and a new earth; the former things shall not be remembered, nor come to mind.
But be glad and rejoice forever in what I am creating; for behold, I am creating Jerusalem a joy, and her people a gladness.
I will rejoice in Jerusalem and be glad in my people; no more shall be heard in her the sound of weeping or the cry of distress.

No more shall there be an infant who lives but a few days, or an old man who does not fill out his days; for the youth shall die a hundred years old, and the sinner a hundred years old shall be accursed.
They shall build houses and dwell in them; they shall plant vineyards and eat their fruit.
They shall not build for another to inhabit, nor plant for another to eat; for like the days of a tree shall be the days of my people, and my chosen shall long enjoy the work of their hands.
They shall not labor in vain, nor bear children for sudden terror; for they are the offspring of the blessed of YHWH, and their descendants with them.
Before they call, I will answer; while they are still speaking, I will hear.
The wolf and the lamb shall graze together, and the lion shall eat straw like the ox, and dust shall be the serpent's food. They shall not harm or destroy on all my holy mountain, says YHWH.

isa.66.text: Thus says YHWH: Heaven is my throne, and the earth is my footstool. Where is the house you would build for me, and where is the place of my rest?
All these things my hand has made, and so all these things came to be, declares YHWH. But this is the one to whom I will look: the afflicted, the broken in spirit, who trembles at my word.

Whoever slaughters an ox is like one who kills a man; whoever sacrifices a lamb, like one who breaks a dog's neck; whoever offers a grain offering, like one who offers swine's blood; whoever burns frankincense, like one who blesses an idol. Just as they have chosen their own ways, and their soul delights in their abominations,

so I will choose harsh treatment for them and bring their terrors upon them; because I called and no one answered, I spoke and they did not listen, but they did what was evil in my eyes and chose what did not please me.

Hear the word of YHWH, you who tremble at his word: Your own brothers who hate you, who cast you out for my name's sake, have said, "Let YHWH be glorified, that we may see your joy" — but it is they who shall be put to shame.
A sound of uproar from the city! A sound from the temple! The sound of YHWH rendering recompense to his enemies!
Before she was in labor she gave birth; before her pangs came upon her, she delivered a son.
Who has heard such a thing? Who has seen such things? Can a land be born in a single day? Can a nation be brought forth in one moment? Yet as soon as Zion was in labor she gave birth to her children.
Shall I bring to the moment of birth and not deliver? says YHWH. Shall I who deliver close the womb? says your God.
Rejoice with Jerusalem and be glad for her, all you who love her; rejoice with her in joy, all you who mourn over her —
that you may nurse and be satisfied from her comforting breast, that you may drink deeply and delight in the abundance of her glory.

For thus says YHWH: Behold, I will extend peace to her like a river, and the glory of the nations like an overflowing stream; you shall nurse, you shall be carried on the hip and dandled on the knees.
As a mother comforts her child, so will I comfort you; and in Jerusalem you shall be comforted.
You shall see, and your heart shall rejoice; your bones shall flourish like new grass; and the hand of YHWH shall be known to his servants, and his fury to his enemies.
For behold, YHWH will come with fire, and his chariots like the whirlwind, to render his anger in fury and his rebuke in flames of fire.
For with fire YHWH will execute judgment, and with his sword, upon all flesh; and those slain by YHWH shall be many.
Those who consecrate and purify themselves to enter the gardens, following one in the midst, who eat swine's flesh and vermin and mice — together they shall come to an end, declares YHWH.
And I, because of their works and their thoughts — the time is coming to gather all nations and tongues; and they shall come and see my glory.

I will set a sign among them, and I will send from them survivors to the nations — to Tarshish, Put, and Lud, who draw the bow, to Tubal and Javan, to the distant coastlands that have not heard my fame or seen my glory — and they shall declare my glory among the nations.

And they shall bring all your brothers from all the nations as an offering to YHWH — on horses, in chariots, in litters, on mules and dromedaries — to my holy mountain Jerusalem, says YHWH, just as the Israelites bring their offering in a clean vessel to the house of YHWH.
And from them also I will take some to be priests and Levites, says YHWH.
For as the new heavens and the new earth that I am making shall endure before me, declares YHWH, so shall your offspring and your name endure.
And it shall be that from new moon to new moon, and from Sabbath to Sabbath, all flesh shall come to worship before me, says YHWH.
And they shall go out and look on the corpses of the men who rebelled against me; for their worm shall not die, and their fire shall not be quenched, and they shall be an abhorrence to all flesh.

isa.1.text: The vision of Isaiah son of Amoz, which he saw concerning Judah and Jerusalem in the days of Uzziah, Jotham, Ahaz, and Hezekiah, kings of Judah.
Hear, O heavens, and give ear, O earth — for YHWH has spoken: "Children I have raised and brought up, and they have rebelled against me.
The ox knows its owner, and the donkey its master's feeding-trough; Israel does not know — my people does not understand."

Woe — sinning nation, people heavy with iniquity, offspring of evildoers, children who corrupt! They have forsaken YHWH; they have spurned the Holy One of Israel; they have turned their backs and gone.
Why be struck again? Why go on rebelling? The whole head is sick, and the whole heart faint.
From the sole of the foot to the head there is no soundness in it — wounds and bruises and raw sores, not pressed out, not bound up, not softened with oil.
Your land is a desolation; your cities are burned with fire. Your soil — before your eyes strangers devour it; a desolation, like an overthrow by strangers.
And the daughter of Zion is left like a booth in a vineyard, like a night-hut in a cucumber field — like a city besieged.
Had not YHWH of hosts left us a remnant, a few — we would have been like Sodom; we would have resembled Gomorrah.
Hear the word of YHWH, you rulers of Sodom! Give ear to the instruction of our God, you people of Gomorrah!
"What are your many sacrifices to me?" says YHWH. "I am sated with burnt offerings of rams and the fat of fattened beasts; the blood of bulls and lambs and goats I do not desire.
When you come to appear before my face — who has asked this from your hand, this trampling of my courts?
Bring no more worthless offerings; incense is an abomination to me. New moon and sabbath, the calling of assemblies — I cannot bear iniquity together with solemn gathering.
Your new moons and your appointed feasts my soul hates; they have become a burden upon me — I am weary of carrying them.
And when you spread out your hands, I will hide my eyes from you; even when you multiply prayer, I am not listening. Your hands are full of blood.
Wash yourselves; make yourselves clean. Remove the evil of your deeds from before my eyes. Cease to do evil;
learn to do good. Seek justice; set right the oppressor. Defend the orphan; plead the widow's cause."
"Come now, let us argue it out," says YHWH. "Though your sins be like scarlet, they shall be white as snow; though they be red like crimson, they shall be as wool.
If you are willing and you listen, you shall eat the good of the land;
but if you refuse and rebel, you shall be eaten by the sword — for the mouth of YHWH has spoken."
How the faithful city has become a harlot! She was full of justice; righteousness lodged in her — and now, murderers.
Your silver has become dross; your wine is cut with water.
Your princes are rebels and companions of thieves. Every one of them loves a bribe and chases after payoffs. They do not defend the orphan, and the widow's cause never comes before them.
Therefore — the word of the Lord, YHWH of hosts, the Mighty One of Israel: "Ah, I will relieve myself of my adversaries; I will avenge myself on my enemies.
And I will turn my hand against you: I will smelt away your dross as with lye, and remove all your alloy.
And I will restore your judges as at the first, and your counselors as at the beginning. Afterward you shall be called City of Righteousness, Faithful Town."
Zion shall be redeemed by justice, and those in her who return, by righteousness.
But rebels and sinners shall be broken together, and those who forsake YHWH shall be consumed.
For they shall be ashamed of the sacred oaks in which you delighted, and you shall blush for the gardens you have chosen.
For you shall be like an oak whose leaf withers, and like a garden that has no water.
And the strong shall become tinder, and his work a spark — and both shall burn together, with none to quench them.

isa.2.text: The word that Isaiah son of Amoz saw concerning Judah and Jerusalem.
And it shall come to pass in the last days: the mountain of the house of YHWH shall be established at the head of the mountains, and lifted above the hills — and all the nations shall stream to it.

And many peoples shall go and say, "Come, let us go up to the mountain of YHWH, to the house of the God of Jacob, that he may teach us his ways, and we may walk in his paths." For out of Zion shall go forth instruction, and the word of YHWH from Jerusalem.

And he shall judge between the nations and arbitrate for many peoples; and they shall beat their swords into plowshares, and their spears into pruning-hooks. Nation shall not lift sword against nation, and they shall learn war no more.
O house of Jacob — come, let us walk in the light of YHWH.
For you have abandoned your people, the house of Jacob — because they are full of the East, and of soothsayers like the Philistines, and they strike hands with the children of foreigners.
Their land is full of silver and gold, and there is no end to their treasures; their land is full of horses, and there is no end to their chariots.
And their land is full of idols: they bow down to the work of their own hands, to what their own fingers have made.
So humanity is bowed down, and each man brought low — do not forgive them!
Go into the rock; hide in the dust — from the terror of YHWH and from the splendor of his majesty.
The haughty eyes of humanity shall be brought low, and the loftiness of men bowed down — and YHWH alone shall be exalted on that day.
For YHWH of hosts has a day against all that is proud and lofty, against all that is lifted up — and it shall be brought low:
against all the cedars of Lebanon, lofty and lifted up, and against all the oaks of Bashan;
against all the high mountains and against all the uplifted hills;
against every high tower and against every fortified wall;
against all the ships of Tarshish and against all the treasured vessels of delight.
And the haughtiness of humanity shall be bowed down, and the loftiness of men brought low — and YHWH alone shall be exalted on that day.
And the idols shall vanish altogether.
And they shall enter the caves of the rocks and the holes of the dust, from the terror of YHWH and from the splendor of his majesty, when he rises to make the earth tremble.
On that day humanity shall fling away its idols of silver and its idols of gold, which they made for themselves to worship, to the burrowing rodents and to the bats —
to enter the crevices of the rocks and the clefts of the crags, from the terror of YHWH and from the splendor of his majesty, when he rises to make the earth tremble.
Cease from man, in whose nostrils is only breath — for of what account is he?

isa.3.text: For behold, the Lord, YHWH of hosts, is removing from Jerusalem and from Judah support and staff — every support of bread and every support of water:
warrior and man of war, judge and prophet, diviner and elder,
captain of fifty and man of rank, counselor, skilled craftsman, and expert in charms.
"And I will make boys their princes, and the capricious shall rule over them."
And the people shall oppress one another — man against man, each against his neighbor. The boy shall bully the elder, and the despised the honored.
When a man seizes his brother in his father's house: "You have a cloak — you shall be our chief, and this heap of ruins shall be under your hand,"
on that day he will protest: "I will not be a healer; in my house there is no bread and no cloak. You shall not make me chief of the people."
For Jerusalem has stumbled and Judah has fallen — because their tongue and their deeds are against YHWH, defying the eyes of his glory.
The look on their faces testifies against them; they declare their sin like Sodom — they do not hide it. Woe to their soul, for they have dealt out evil to themselves.
Say of the righteous one that it shall be well — for they shall eat the fruit of their deeds.
Woe to the wicked one — it shall go ill, for what his hands have dealt shall be done to him.
My people — its oppressors are children, and women rule over it. O my people, your guides lead you astray, and they have swallowed up the course of your paths.
YHWH has taken his stand to contend; he stands to judge peoples.
YHWH enters into judgment with the elders of his people and their princes: "It is you who have devoured the vineyard; the plunder of the poor is in your houses.
What do you mean by crushing my people, by grinding the faces of the poor?" — the word of the Lord, YHWH of hosts.
And YHWH said: Because the daughters of Zion are haughty, and walk with outstretched necks and darting eyes, walking with mincing steps, jingling with their anklets,
the Lord will strike the crowns of the daughters of Zion with scabs, and YHWH will lay bare their foreheads.
On that day the Lord will remove the splendor of the anklets, the headbands, and the crescents;
the pendants, the bracelets, and the veils;
the headdresses, the ankle-chains, the sashes, the perfume-boxes, and the amulets;
the rings and the nose-rings;
the festal robes, the mantles, the shawls, and the purses;
the mirrors, the linen garments, the turbans, and the cloaks.
And it shall be: instead of perfume — rot; instead of a sash — a rope; instead of well-set hair — baldness; instead of a rich robe — a girding of sackcloth; branding instead of beauty.
Your men shall fall by the sword, and your strength in battle.
And her gates shall lament and mourn — and stripped bare, she shall sit on the ground.

isa.4.text: And seven women shall take hold of one man on that day, saying: "We will eat our own bread and wear our own clothing — only let your name be called over us. Take away our disgrace!"
On that day the Branch of YHWH shall be for beauty and for glory, and the fruit of the land for pride and for splendor, for the survivors of Israel.
And it shall be that whoever is left in Zion and remains in Jerusalem shall be called holy — everyone written down for life in Jerusalem —
when the Lord has washed away the filth of the daughters of Zion, and rinsed the bloodstains of Jerusalem from her midst, by a spirit of judgment and a spirit of burning.

Then YHWH will create over the whole site of Mount Zion and over her assemblies a cloud by day, and smoke, and the brightness of flaming fire by night — for over all the glory there shall be a canopy.
And there shall be a booth for shade by day from the heat, and for refuge and shelter from storm and rain.

isa.5.text: Let me sing for my beloved my love-song about his vineyard: My beloved had a vineyard on a fertile hillside.

He dug it and cleared it of stones and planted it with choice vines; he built a watchtower in its midst and even hewed out a wine vat in it. And he hoped it would yield grapes — and it yielded stinking fruit.
And now, you who dwell in Jerusalem and you men of Judah — judge, I pray, between me and my vineyard.
What more was there to do for my vineyard that I have not done in it? Why, when I hoped it would yield grapes, did it yield stinking fruit?
And now let me tell you what I am doing to my vineyard: removing its hedge, that it may be devoured; breaching its wall, that it may be trampled.
I will make it a waste: it shall not be pruned or hoed, and briers and thorns shall come up — and I will command the clouds to rain no rain upon it.
For the vineyard of YHWH of hosts is the house of Israel, and the men of Judah are his delightful planting. And he hoped for justice — and behold, bloodshed; for righteousness — and behold, a cry!
Woe to those who join house to house, who add field to field, until there is no room left — and you are made to dwell alone in the midst of the land!
In my ears — YHWH of hosts: "Surely many houses shall become a desolation — great and fine ones, without inhabitant.
For ten acres of vineyard shall yield one bath, and a homer of seed shall yield an ephah."
Woe to those who rise early in the morning to chase strong drink, who linger into the evening till wine inflames them!
Lyre and harp, tambourine and flute and wine are at their feasts — but the work of YHWH they do not regard, and the deed of his hands they have not seen.
Therefore my people goes into exile for lack of knowledge; its nobility are men of hunger, and its multitude parched with thirst.
Therefore Sheol has widened its gullet and opened its mouth beyond measure — and down go her splendor and her multitude, her uproar and all who exult in her.
And humanity is bowed down, and each man brought low — and the eyes of the lofty are brought low.
But YHWH of hosts is exalted in justice, and the holy God shows himself holy in righteousness.
And lambs shall graze as in their own pasture, and among the ruins of the fat ones, strangers shall feed.
Woe to those who drag iniquity with cords of falsehood, and sin as with a cart rope —
who say: "Let him hurry, let him speed his work, that we may see it; let the counsel of the Holy One of Israel draw near and come, that we may know it!"
Woe to those who call evil good and good evil, who put darkness for light and light for darkness, who put bitter for sweet and sweet for bitter!
Woe to those who are wise in their own eyes, and discerning in their own sight!
Woe to those who are heroes — at drinking wine, and men of valor — at mixing strong drink,
who acquit the guilty for a bribe, and strip the righteous of their righteousness!

Therefore, as a tongue of fire devours stubble and dry grass collapses into flame, their root shall become rot and their blossom go up like dust — for they have rejected the instruction of YHWH of hosts and spurned the word of the Holy One of Israel.

Therefore the anger of YHWH burned against his people; he stretched out his hand against them and struck them. The mountains quaked, and their corpses were like refuse in the midst of the streets. For all this his anger has not turned back, and his hand is stretched out still.
And he will raise a banner for nations far away, and whistle for one from the end of the earth — and behold, swiftly, speedily, it comes!
None among them is weary, none stumbles; none slumbers, none sleeps. No belt is loosened at their waist; no sandal-thong is snapped.
Their arrows are sharpened, and all their bows are strung; their horses' hooves are reckoned like flint, and their wheels like the whirlwind.
Their roaring is like a lioness; they roar like young lions — growling, they seize the prey and carry it off, and there is none to rescue.
And they shall growl over it on that day like the growling of the sea. And if one looks to the land — behold, darkness and distress; and the light is darkened by its clouds.

isa.6.text: In the year that King Uzziah died, I saw the Lord sitting on a throne, high and lifted up — and the hem of his robe filled the temple.
Seraphim stood above him, each with six wings: with two he covered his face, with two he covered his feet, and with two he flew.
And one called to another and said: "Holy, holy, holy is YHWH of hosts! The fullness of all the earth is his glory!"
And the doorposts shook on their foundations at the voice of the one calling, and the house was filling with smoke.
And I said: "Woe is me — for I am undone! For I am a man of unclean lips, and I dwell among a people of unclean lips — for my eyes have seen the King, YHWH of hosts."
Then one of the seraphim flew to me, and in his hand a glowing coal, taken with tongs from the altar.
And he touched it to my mouth and said: "Behold, this has touched your lips — your iniquity is taken away, and your sin is atoned for."
And I heard the voice of the Lord saying: "Whom shall I send, and who will go for us?" And I said: "Here I am — send me."
And he said: "Go, and say to this people: 'Hear indeed — but do not understand; see indeed — but do not know.'
Make the heart of this people fat; make their ears heavy and smear over their eyes — lest they see with their eyes and hear with their ears and understand with their heart, and turn, and be healed."
And I said, "How long, my Lord?" And he said: "Until cities lie devastated without inhabitant, and houses without a person, and the soil is devastated into desolation —
and YHWH has sent everyone far away, and the forsaken places are many in the midst of the land.
And though a tenth remain in it, it shall be burned again — like a terebinth or an oak whose stump remains when it is felled. The holy seed is its stump."

isa.7.text: And it came to pass in the days of Ahaz son of Jotham, son of Uzziah, king of Judah, that Rezin king of Aram and Pekah son of Remaliah, king of Israel, came up against Jerusalem to make war on her — but he could not conquer her.
And it was told to the house of David: "Aram has settled upon Ephraim." And his heart shook, and the heart of his people, as the trees of the forest shake before the wind.
And YHWH said to Isaiah: "Go out now to meet Ahaz, you and Shear-jashub your son, at the end of the conduit of the upper pool, on the highway to the Fuller's Field,

and say to him: Take care and be quiet. Do not fear, and let not your heart grow faint before these two smoldering stubs of firebrands — before the burning anger of Rezin and Aram and the son of Remaliah.
Because Aram has plotted evil against you, with Ephraim and the son of Remaliah, saying,
'Let us go up against Judah and terrorize her; let us break her open for ourselves and set up a king within her — the son of Tabeel' —
thus says the Lord YHWH: It shall not stand, and it shall not come to be.
For the head of Aram is Damascus, and the head of Damascus is Rezin — and within sixty-five years Ephraim shall be shattered from being a people.
And the head of Ephraim is Samaria, and the head of Samaria is the son of Remaliah. If you do not stand firm in faith, you shall not stand at all."
And YHWH spoke again to Ahaz:
"Ask for yourself a sign from YHWH your God — make it deep as Sheol, or high as the heights above."
But Ahaz said, "I will not ask, and I will not test YHWH."
And he said: "Hear now, O house of David! Is it too little for you to weary men, that you weary my God as well?
Therefore the Lord himself will give you a sign: behold, the young woman is with child and shall bear a son, and she shall call his name Immanu-El — God-With-Us.
Curds and honey he shall eat, by the time he knows to refuse the evil and choose the good.
For before the boy knows to refuse the evil and choose the good, the land whose two kings you dread shall be abandoned.
YHWH will bring upon you and upon your people and upon your father's house days such as have not come since the day Ephraim broke away from Judah — the king of Assyria."
And it shall come to pass on that day: YHWH will whistle for the fly at the farthest streams of Egypt, and for the bee in the land of Assyria;
and they shall all come and settle in the steep ravines and in the clefts of the rocks, on all the thornbushes and at all the watering-places.
On that day the Lord will shave — with a razor hired beyond the River, with the king of Assyria — the head and the hair of the legs; and it shall sweep away the beard as well.
And it shall come to pass on that day: a man shall keep alive a young cow and two sheep,
and from the abundance of milk they give he shall eat curds — for curds and honey shall everyone eat who is left in the midst of the land.
And it shall come to pass on that day: every place where there were a thousand vines worth a thousand silver pieces shall become briers and thorns.
With arrows and bow one shall go there — for all the land shall be briers and thorns.
And as for all the hills once hoed with the hoe — you shall not go there, for fear of briers and thorns; they shall become a place for oxen to range and for sheep to trample.

isa.8.text: And YHWH said to me: "Take for yourself a large tablet, and write on it with an ordinary stylus: For Maher-shalal-hash-baz — Swift-Spoil-Speedy-Prey."
And I will call as faithful witnesses for myself Uriah the priest and Zechariah son of Jeberechiah.
And I drew near to the prophetess, and she conceived and bore a son. And YHWH said to me: "Call his name Maher-shalal-hash-baz.
For before the boy knows how to cry 'my father' and 'my mother,' the wealth of Damascus and the spoil of Samaria shall be carried away before the king of Assyria."
And YHWH spoke to me again:
"Because this people has rejected the waters of Shiloah that flow gently, and rejoices with Rezin and the son of Remaliah,

therefore, behold, the Lord is bringing up against them the waters of the River — mighty and many — the king of Assyria and all his glory. And it shall rise over all its channels and run over all its banks;
and it shall sweep on into Judah, flooding and passing through — it shall reach even to the neck. And its outspread wings shall fill the breadth of your land, O Immanu-El!"
Rage, O peoples — and be shattered! Give ear, all you far places of the earth! Gird yourselves — and be shattered! Gird yourselves — and be shattered!
Devise a plan — it shall be broken. Speak a word — it shall not stand. For God is with us.
For thus YHWH spoke to me, with a strong hand upon me, warning me not to walk in the way of this people:
"Do not call conspiracy everything this people calls conspiracy; do not fear what it fears, nor be in dread.
YHWH of hosts — him you shall sanctify. Let him be your fear; let him be your dread.
And he shall become a sanctuary — and a stone of striking and a rock of stumbling to the two houses of Israel, a trap and a snare to the inhabitant of Jerusalem.
And many among them shall stumble; they shall fall and be broken, be snared and be taken."
Bind up the testimony; seal the instruction among my disciples.
And I will wait for YHWH, who is hiding his face from the house of Jacob — I will hope in him.
Behold, I and the children YHWH has given me are signs and portents in Israel from YHWH of hosts, who dwells on Mount Zion.
And when they say to you, "Inquire of the ghosts and the familiar spirits that chirp and mutter" — should not a people inquire of its God? On behalf of the living, of the dead?
To the instruction and to the testimony! If they will not speak according to this word, it is because there is no dawn for them.
And they shall pass through the land, hard-pressed and hungry; and when they hunger, they shall rage and curse their king and their God, and turn their faces upward;
and they shall look to the earth — and behold, distress and darkness, the gloom of anguish; and they shall be thrust into thick darkness.

But there shall be no gloom for her who was in anguish. In the former time he brought contempt on the land of Zebulun and the land of Naphtali — but in the latter time he has made glorious the way of the sea, beyond the Jordan, Galilee of the nations.

isa.9.text: The people walking in darkness have seen a great light; those dwelling in the land of deep shadow — light has shone upon them.
You have multiplied the nation; you have made its joy great. They rejoice before you as with joy at the harvest, as men exult when they divide the spoil.
For the yoke of its burden, and the bar across its shoulders, the rod of its taskmaster — you have shattered, as on the day of Midian.
For every boot of the trampling warrior in the quake of battle, and every cloak rolled in blood, shall be for burning — food for the fire.
For a child has been born to us, a son has been given to us, and the dominion is upon his shoulder; and his name is called Wonder of a Counselor, Mighty God, Father Everlasting, Prince of Peace —

of the increase of dominion and of peace there shall be no end, upon the throne of David and over his kingdom, to establish it and uphold it with justice and with righteousness, from now and forever. The zeal of YHWH of hosts will do this.
The Lord has sent a word into Jacob, and it has fallen upon Israel.
And all the people shall know it — Ephraim and the inhabitant of Samaria — who say in pride and in arrogance of heart:
"Bricks have fallen — we will build with hewn stone; sycamores have been cut down — we will replace them with cedars."
So YHWH raised up the adversaries of Rezin against him, and spurred on his enemies:
Aram from the east and the Philistines from behind — and they devoured Israel with open mouth. For all this his anger has not turned back, and his hand is stretched out still.
But the people did not turn back to him who struck them, and YHWH of hosts they did not seek.
So YHWH cut off from Israel head and tail, palm-branch and reed, in one day.
The elder and the honored man — he is the head; and the prophet teaching lies — he is the tail.
And the guides of this people became misleaders, and those who were guided by them were swallowed up.

Therefore the Lord takes no joy in their young men, and on their orphans and widows he has no compassion; for every one of them is godless and an evildoer, and every mouth speaks folly. For all this his anger has not turned back, and his hand is stretched out still.
For wickedness burned like fire: it devours brier and thorn; it kindled the thickets of the forest, and they billowed upward in columns of smoke.
By the fury of YHWH of hosts the land was scorched, and the people became like food for the fire — no one spares his brother.
They carve on the right — and are hungry; they devour on the left — and are not satisfied; each devours the flesh of his own arm:
Manasseh devours Ephraim, and Ephraim Manasseh — together they are against Judah. For all this his anger has not turned back, and his hand is stretched out still.

isa.10.text: Woe to those who enact decrees of wickedness, and the scribes who keep writing oppression —
to turn the needy aside from justice and to rob the poor of my people of their right, so that widows become their spoil, and they plunder the orphans!
And what will you do on the day of visitation, in the ruin that comes from afar? To whom will you flee for help, and where will you leave your glory?
Nothing remains but to crouch among the prisoners or fall among the slain. For all this his anger has not turned back, and his hand is stretched out still.
Woe to Assyria, the rod of my anger — the staff in their hand is my fury!
Against a godless nation I send him; against the people of my wrath I command him — to take spoil and seize plunder, and to make them a trampling like the mire of the streets.
But he — not so does he intend, and not so does his heart reckon; for it is in his heart to destroy, and to cut off nations not a few.
For he says: "Are not my commanders all kings?
Is not Calno like Carchemish? Is not Hamath like Arpad? Is not Samaria like Damascus?
As my hand reached the kingdoms of the idols — whose images exceeded those of Jerusalem and Samaria —
shall I not do to Jerusalem and her images as I have done to Samaria and her idols?"

And it shall come to pass, when the Lord has finished all his work on Mount Zion and in Jerusalem: "I will punish the fruit of the arrogant heart of the king of Assyria, and the glory of his haughty eyes."

For he says: "By the strength of my hand I have done it, and by my wisdom — for I have understanding. I removed the boundaries of peoples and plundered their treasures; like a mighty one I brought down those enthroned.
My hand found the wealth of the peoples like a nest; and as one gathers abandoned eggs, I myself gathered all the earth — and there was none that fluttered a wing, or opened a mouth, or chirped."

Shall the axe vaunt itself over the one who hews with it? Shall the saw magnify itself over the one who wields it? — as if a rod should wield those who lift it, as if a staff should lift what is not wood!
Therefore the Lord, YHWH of hosts, will send a wasting sickness among his stout warriors; and beneath his glory a burning shall burn, like the burning of fire.
And the Light of Israel shall become a fire, and his Holy One a flame — and it shall burn and devour his thorns and his briers in one day.
And the glory of his forest and his garden-land it shall consume, soul and flesh alike — and it shall be as when a sick man wastes away.
And the remnant of the trees of his forest shall be so few that a child could write them down.

And it shall come to pass on that day: the remnant of Israel and the survivors of the house of Jacob shall no longer lean on the one who struck them, but shall lean on YHWH, the Holy One of Israel, in truth.
A remnant shall return — the remnant of Jacob — to the Mighty God.
For though your people Israel be like the sand of the sea, only a remnant of them shall return. Destruction is decreed, overflowing with righteousness;
for the Lord, YHWH of hosts, is carrying out a full and decisive end in the midst of all the earth.
Therefore thus says the Lord, YHWH of hosts: "Do not fear Assyria, O my people who dwell in Zion, though they strike you with the rod and lift their staff against you in the manner of Egypt.
For in a very little while the fury will be spent, and my anger will turn to their destruction."
And YHWH of hosts will brandish a whip against them, as when he struck Midian at the rock of Oreb; and his staff shall be over the sea, and he shall lift it in the manner of Egypt.
And it shall come to pass on that day: his burden shall depart from your shoulder, and his yoke from your neck — and the yoke shall be broken because of fatness.
He has come upon Aiath; he has passed through Migron; at Michmash he stores his baggage.
They have crossed the pass: "Geba is our lodging for the night." Ramah trembles; Gibeah of Saul has fled.
Cry shrill with your voice, O daughter of Gallim! Listen, O Laishah! Answer her, O Anathoth!
Madmenah is in flight; the inhabitants of Gebim take cover.
This very day he halts at Nob; he shakes his fist at the mountain of the daughter of Zion, the hill of Jerusalem.
Behold, the Lord, YHWH of hosts, is lopping the boughs with terrifying power: the tall of stature are hewn down, and the lofty are brought low.
He will cut down the thickets of the forest with iron — and Lebanon shall fall by the Majestic One.

isa.11.text: And a shoot shall come forth from the stump of Jesse, and a branch from his roots shall bear fruit.
And the spirit of YHWH shall rest upon him: a spirit of wisdom and understanding, a spirit of counsel and might, a spirit of knowledge and the fear of YHWH.
And his delight shall be in the fear of YHWH. He shall not judge by what his eyes see, nor decide by what his ears hear;

but with righteousness he shall judge the poor, and decide with equity for the humble of the earth. He shall strike the earth with the rod of his mouth, and with the breath of his lips he shall slay the wicked.
Righteousness shall be the belt of his waist, and faithfulness the belt of his loins.
And the wolf shall dwell with the lamb, and the leopard lie down with the kid; calf and young lion and fatling together — and a little child shall lead them.
Cow and bear shall graze; their young shall lie down together; and the lion shall eat straw like the ox.
The nursing infant shall play over the hole of the cobra, and the weaned child shall stretch out his hand over the viper's den.
They shall not hurt and they shall not destroy on all my holy mountain — for the earth shall be full of the knowledge of YHWH as the waters cover the sea.
And it shall come to pass on that day: the root of Jesse, standing as a banner for the peoples — to him shall the nations seek, and his resting place shall be glory.

And it shall come to pass on that day: the Lord will extend his hand a second time to recover the remnant of his people that is left — from Assyria and from Egypt, from Pathros and from Cush, from Elam and from Shinar, from Hamath and from the coastlands of the sea.
And he will raise a banner for the nations, and gather the outcasts of Israel; and the dispersed of Judah he will gather from the four corners of the earth.
And the jealousy of Ephraim shall depart, and the harassers of Judah shall be cut off: Ephraim shall not be jealous of Judah, and Judah shall not harass Ephraim.

But they shall swoop down on the shoulder of the Philistines toward the sea; together they shall plunder the children of the East. Edom and Moab shall be the reach of their hand, and the children of Ammon their subjects.
And YHWH will utterly destroy the tongue of the Sea of Egypt, and wave his hand over the River with his scorching wind; and he will strike it into seven streams, and let people cross in sandals.
And there shall be a highway for the remnant of his people that is left from Assyria — as there was for Israel on the day it came up from the land of Egypt.

isa.12.text: And you will say on that day: "I thank you, YHWH — for you were angry with me; may your anger turn back, and may you comfort me.
Behold, God is my salvation; I will trust and not be afraid. For Yah, YHWH, is my strength and my song — and he has become my salvation."
And you shall draw water with joy from the springs of salvation.
And you will say on that day: "Give thanks to YHWH; call on his name. Make his deeds known among the peoples; proclaim that his name is exalted.
Sing praises to YHWH, for he has done majestically — let this be known in all the earth.
Cry shrill and sing for joy, O inhabitant of Zion — for great in your midst is the Holy One of Israel!"

isa.28.text: Woe to the proud crown of the drunkards of Ephraim, and to the fading flower of its glorious beauty, set on the head of the rich valley of those laid low by wine!
Behold, the Lord has one strong and mighty — like a hailstorm, a destroying tempest, like a storm of mighty overflowing waters — he hurls it down to the earth with his hand.
Under feet it shall be trampled, the proud crown of the drunkards of Ephraim;
and the fading flower of its glorious beauty, set on the head of the rich valley, shall be like a first-ripe fig before summer: whoever sees it swallows it while it is still in his hand.
On that day YHWH of hosts shall be a crown of beauty and a diadem of glory to the remnant of his people —
and a spirit of justice to the one who sits in judgment, and strength to those who turn back the battle at the gate.

But these also reel with wine and stagger with strong drink: priest and prophet reel with strong drink; they are swallowed by wine; they stagger with strong drink; they reel in vision; they stumble in giving judgment.
For all the tables are full of vomit and filth — no place is clean.
"Whom will he teach knowledge? To whom will he explain the message? Those weaned from milk, just taken from the breast?
For it is: tsav to tsav, tsav to tsav; qav to qav, qav to qav — a little here, a little there."
Indeed, with stammering lips and another tongue he will speak to this people —
he who said to them, "This is the resting place: give rest to the weary; and this is the repose" — but they would not hear.

So the word of YHWH will be to them: tsav to tsav, tsav to tsav; qav to qav, qav to qav — a little here, a little there — that they may walk and stumble backward, and be broken, and snared, and taken.
Therefore hear the word of YHWH, you men of mockery, rulers of this people in Jerusalem.

Because you have said, "We have cut a covenant with Death, and with Sheol we have made a pact; when the overwhelming scourge passes through, it shall not come to us — for we have made the lie our refuge, and in falsehood we have hidden ourselves" —
therefore thus says the Lord YHWH: Behold, I am laying in Zion a stone — a tested stone, a precious cornerstone, a foundation founded firm. Whoever trusts shall not be put to flight.
And I will make justice the measuring line, and righteousness the plummet; and hail shall sweep away the refuge of the lie, and waters shall flood the hiding place.
Then your covenant with Death shall be annulled, and your pact with Sheol shall not stand; when the overwhelming scourge passes through, you shall be trampled by it.
As often as it passes through, it shall take you — for morning by morning it shall pass through, by day and by night; and it shall be sheer terror to understand the message.
For the bed is too short to stretch out on, and the covering too narrow to wrap oneself in.
For YHWH will rise up as on Mount Perazim; as in the valley at Gibeon he will rage — to do his deed (strange is his deed!) and to work his work (alien is his work!).
And now, do not mock — lest your bonds be made stronger; for a full and decisive end have I heard from the Lord, YHWH of hosts, upon all the earth.
Give ear and hear my voice; attend and hear my speech.
Does the plowman plow all day for sowing? Does he keep opening and harrowing his ground?
When he has leveled its surface, does he not scatter black cumin and sow cumin, and set wheat in rows and barley in its plot, and spelt at its border?
For he is instructed rightly — his God teaches him.
For black cumin is not threshed with a sledge, nor is a cart wheel rolled over cumin; but black cumin is beaten out with a staff, and cumin with a rod.
Grain for bread is crushed — but one does not thresh it forever; he drives his cart wheel over it, but with his horses he does not grind it fine.
This too comes forth from YHWH of hosts: wonderful in counsel, great in sound wisdom.

isa.29.text: Woe, Ariel, Ariel — city where David encamped! Add year to year; let the feasts run their round —
then I will press hard upon Ariel, and there shall be moaning and mourning; and she shall be to me like an ariel, an altar-hearth.
And I will encamp against you all around; I will besiege you with towers and raise siegeworks against you.
Brought low, you shall speak from the earth; from the dust your speech shall come muffled. Your voice shall be like a ghost's from the earth; from the dust your speech shall chirp.
But the horde of your strangers shall be like fine dust, and the horde of the ruthless like passing chaff — and it shall happen in an instant, suddenly:
from YHWH of hosts you shall be visited — with thunder and earthquake and great noise, whirlwind and tempest and the flame of devouring fire.
And the horde of all the nations warring against Ariel — all who war against her and her stronghold, and who press upon her — shall be like a dream, a vision of the night:

as when a hungry man dreams he is eating and wakes with his soul empty, or a thirsty man dreams he is drinking and wakes faint, his soul still craving — so shall be the horde of all the nations warring against Mount Zion.
Linger and be astounded! Blind yourselves and be blind! They are drunk — but not with wine; they stagger — but not with strong drink.
For YHWH has poured out on you a spirit of deep sleep: he has shut your eyes — the prophets — and covered your heads — the seers.
And the vision of all this has become to you like the words of a sealed book. They give it to one who can read, saying, "Read this, please" — and he says, "I cannot, for it is sealed."
And the book is given to one who cannot read, saying, "Read this, please" — and he says, "I do not know how to read."
And the Lord said: Because this people draws near with its mouth, and with its lips honors me, while its heart is far from me — and their fear of me is a commandment of men, learned by rote —
therefore, behold, I will again do wonders with this people, wonder upon wonder: the wisdom of its wise shall perish, and the discernment of its discerning shall hide itself.
Woe to those who go deep to hide their counsel from YHWH, whose deeds are done in the dark, who say, "Who sees us? Who knows us?"

How you turn things upside down! Shall the potter be reckoned as the clay — that the thing made should say of its maker, "He did not make me," and the thing formed say of its former, "He has no understanding"?
Is it not yet a very little while until Lebanon shall turn into a garden-land, and the garden-land be reckoned a forest?
On that day the deaf shall hear the words of a book, and out of gloom and darkness the eyes of the blind shall see.
And the lowly shall increase their joy in YHWH, and the poorest of humanity shall exult in the Holy One of Israel.
For the ruthless one shall be no more, the mocker shall be finished, and all who lie awake for evil shall be cut off —
those who make a man out to be a sinner with a word, who lay a snare for the one who argues a case in the gate, and with emptiness turn aside the righteous.
Therefore thus says YHWH — who redeemed Abraham — concerning the house of Jacob: Not now shall Jacob be ashamed; not now shall his face grow pale.
For when he sees his children, the work of my hands, in his midst, they shall sanctify my name: they shall sanctify the Holy One of Jacob, and stand in awe of the God of Israel.
And those whose spirit strayed shall come to understanding, and the grumblers shall learn instruction.

isa.30.text: "Woe to the rebellious children" — the word of YHWH — "who carry out a plan, but not mine; who pour out a libation, but not of my spirit — heaping sin upon sin;
who set out to go down to Egypt without asking my mouth, to take refuge in Pharaoh's stronghold and to shelter in the shadow of Egypt.
But Pharaoh's stronghold shall become your shame, and the shelter in Egypt's shadow your disgrace.
For though his princes are at Zoan and his envoys reach Hanes,
everyone comes to shame through a people that cannot profit them — not for help and not for profit, but for shame, and disgrace besides."

The burden of the beasts of the Negev: through a land of trouble and anguish, of lioness and lion, of viper and flying fiery serpent, they carry their riches on the backs of donkeys and their treasures on the humps of camels — to a people that cannot profit.
And Egypt — her help is vapor and emptiness. Therefore I have named her: Rahab-Who-Sits-Still.
Now go, write it on a tablet before them and inscribe it in a book, that it may be for a day to come, a witness forever:
that this is a rebellious people, lying children — children unwilling to hear the instruction of YHWH,
who say to the seers, "Do not see," and to the visionaries, "Do not envision for us what is right. Speak to us smooth things; envision illusions.
Leave the way; turn aside from the path; remove from before our faces the Holy One of Israel!"
Therefore thus says the Holy One of Israel: Because you have rejected this word, and trusted in oppression and crookedness, and leaned on them,
therefore this iniquity shall be for you like a breach about to fall, a bulge in a high wall, whose crash comes suddenly, in an instant;
and its breaking is like the breaking of a potters' jar, shattered so ruthlessly that among its fragments not a shard is found to scoop fire from the hearth or dip water from a cistern.
For thus said the Lord YHWH, the Holy One of Israel: In returning and rest you shall be saved; in quietness and trust shall be your strength — and you would not.
You said, "No — on horses we will flee!" Therefore you shall flee. And, "On swift steeds we will ride!" Therefore your pursuers shall be swift.
A thousand shall flee at the threat of one; at the threat of five you shall flee — until you are left like a flagstaff on a mountaintop, like a banner on a hill.
And therefore YHWH waits to be gracious to you; therefore he rises to show you compassion. For YHWH is a God of justice — happy are all who wait for him.
For, O people in Zion, dwellers in Jerusalem — you shall weep no more. He will surely be gracious to you at the sound of your cry; when he hears it, he will answer you.
Though the Lord give you the bread of adversity and the water of affliction, your Teacher shall no longer hide himself — your eyes shall see your Teacher,
and your ears shall hear a word behind you, saying, "This is the way — walk in it," whenever you turn to the right or turn to the left.
Then you will defile your silver-plated idols and your gold-clad images; you will scatter them like a menstruous rag — "Out!" you will say to it.
And he will give rain for your seed with which you sow the ground, and bread as the produce of the ground — and it shall be rich and fat. On that day your cattle shall graze in broad pastures;
and the oxen and the donkeys that work the ground shall eat seasoned fodder, winnowed with shovel and fork.
And on every high mountain and every lifted hill there shall be brooks running with water — on the day of the great slaughter, when the towers fall.

And the light of the moon shall be as the light of the sun, and the light of the sun shall be sevenfold, as the light of seven days — on the day YHWH binds up the breaking of his people and heals the wound of his blow.
Behold, the Name of YHWH comes from afar — his anger burning, his burden heavy; his lips are full of indignation, and his tongue is like devouring fire;
his breath like an overflowing torrent that reaches to the neck — to sift the nations in the sieve of destruction, with a misleading bridle on the jaws of the peoples.
But for you there shall be singing, as on a night when a feast is consecrated, and gladness of heart, as when one walks with the flute to come to the mountain of YHWH, to the Rock of Israel.
And YHWH will make heard the majesty of his voice, and show the descent of his arm — in raging anger and the flame of devouring fire, cloudburst and storm and hailstones.
For at the voice of YHWH, Assyria shall be shattered — with the rod he strikes.
And every stroke of the appointed staff that YHWH lays upon him shall be to tambourines and lyres — battling with brandished arm he battles against them.
For Topheth has long been prepared — for the king it is made ready — deep and wide, its pyre fire and wood in abundance; the breath of YHWH, like a stream of burning sulfur, sets it ablaze.

isa.31.text: Woe to those who go down to Egypt for help, who lean on horses, who trust in chariots because they are many, and in horsemen because they are very strong — but do not look to the Holy One of Israel, and do not seek YHWH!
Yet he too is wise: he brings disaster, and he does not take back his words; he will rise against the house of evildoers and against the helpers of those who work iniquity.
Egypt is man and not God; their horses are flesh and not spirit. When YHWH stretches out his hand, the helper shall stumble and the helped shall fall — and together all of them shall perish.

For thus YHWH said to me: As the lion growls — the young lion over its prey — and though a whole band of shepherds is called out against it, it is not terrified by their shouting nor cowed by their noise, so YHWH of hosts will come down to wage war upon Mount Zion and upon her hill.
Like birds hovering, so YHWH of hosts will shield Jerusalem — shielding and delivering, passing over and rescuing.
Return to him from whom you have so deeply revolted, O children of Israel!
For on that day everyone shall reject his idols of silver and his idols of gold, which your own hands made for you — a sin.
And Assyria shall fall by a sword not of man; a sword not of humankind shall devour him. He shall flee before the sword, and his young men shall be put to forced labor;
his rock shall pass away in terror, and his officers shall panic at the standard — the word of YHWH, whose fire is in Zion and whose furnace is in Jerusalem.

isa.32.text: Behold, a king shall reign in righteousness, and princes shall rule with justice.
And each shall be like a hiding place from the wind, a shelter from the storm — like streams of water in a dry place, like the shade of a great rock in a weary land.
Then the eyes of those who see shall not be smeared shut, and the ears of those who hear shall listen;
the heart of the hasty shall understand knowledge, and the tongue of stammerers shall hurry to speak clearly.
No longer shall the fool be called noble, nor the schemer be named honorable.
For the fool speaks folly, and his heart works iniquity — to practice godlessness and to speak error against YHWH, to leave the hungry soul empty and to deprive the thirsty of drink.
And the schemer — his schemes are evil: he devises plots to ruin the poor with lying words, even when the needy speaks what is right.
But the noble devises noble things — and on noble things he takes his stand.
Rise up, you women at ease; hear my voice! You confident daughters, give ear to my speech!
In little more than a year you shall shudder, you confident ones — for the vintage will fail; the ingathering will not come.
Tremble, you who are at ease; shudder, you confident ones! Strip, bare yourselves, gird sackcloth on your loins —
beating your breasts for the pleasant fields, for the fruitful vine,
for the soil of my people going up in thorns and briers — indeed for all the joyous houses of the exultant city.
For the palace is forsaken, the noisy city abandoned; hill and watchtower have become bare heights forever — the joy of wild donkeys, a pasture for flocks —
until a spirit is poured out upon us from on high, and the wilderness becomes a garden-land, and the garden-land is reckoned a forest.
Then justice shall dwell in the wilderness, and righteousness make her home in the garden-land.
And the work of righteousness shall be peace, and the service of righteousness quietness and trust forever.
And my people shall dwell in a habitation of peace, in dwellings of security, in untroubled resting places —
though hail flattens the forest, and the city is utterly laid low.
Happy are you who sow beside all waters, who let the ox and the donkey range free.

isa.33.text: Woe, destroyer — yourself undestroyed; betrayer — whom none have betrayed! When you have finished destroying, you shall be destroyed; when you have done with betraying, they shall betray you.
YHWH, be gracious to us — for you we wait. Be their arm every morning, our salvation also in the time of trouble.
At the sound of the tumult peoples flee; when you lift yourself up, nations scatter;
and your spoil is gathered as the caterpillar gathers — as locusts leap, men leap upon it.
YHWH is exalted, for he dwells on high; he has filled Zion with justice and righteousness.
And he shall be the steadfastness of your times, a wealth of salvations, wisdom and knowledge — the fear of YHWH is his treasure.
Behold, their heroes cry out in the streets; the messengers of peace weep bitterly.
The highways lie desolate; the wayfarer has ceased. He has broken the covenant, despised the cities; he has no regard for man.
The land mourns and languishes; Lebanon is shamed and withers; Sharon has become like a desert; Bashan and Carmel shake off their leaves.
"Now I will arise," says YHWH; "now I will lift myself up; now I will be exalted.
You conceive dry grass; you give birth to stubble; your own breath is a fire that will devour you.
And peoples shall be burnings of lime — thorns cut down, set ablaze in the fire."
Hear, you who are far off, what I have done; and know, you who are near, my might!
In Zion sinners are terrified; trembling has seized the godless: "Who among us can dwell with devouring fire? Who among us can dwell with everlasting burnings?"

The one who walks in righteousness and speaks uprightly, who rejects gain from extortion, who shakes his hands free of holding a bribe, who stops his ears against hearing of bloodshed and shuts his eyes against looking on evil —
he shall dwell on the heights; his stronghold shall be fortresses of rock; his bread shall be given him; his waters shall be sure.
Your eyes shall behold the king in his beauty; they shall see a land that stretches far.
Your heart shall muse on the terror: "Where is the one who counted? Where is the one who weighed? Where is the one who counted the towers?"
The insolent people you shall see no more — the people of speech too obscure to hear, of stammering tongue without understanding.

Gaze on Zion, the city of our appointed feasts! Your eyes shall see Jerusalem: an untroubled habitation, a tent that shall not be moved, whose pegs shall never be pulled up, and none of whose ropes shall be snapped.
But there YHWH in majesty shall be for us a place of rivers and broad streams — where no galley with oars shall go, and no majestic ship shall pass.
For YHWH is our judge; YHWH is our lawgiver; YHWH is our king — he will save us.
Your rigging hangs slack: it cannot hold the mast firm in its place, cannot keep the sail spread. Then prey and spoil in abundance shall be divided — even the lame shall seize plunder.
And no inhabitant shall say, "I am sick" — the people who dwell there shall be forgiven their iniquity.

isa.34.text: Draw near, O nations, to hear; O peoples, attend! Let the earth hear, and all that fills her — the world, and all that comes forth from her.
For YHWH has wrath against all the nations, and fury against all their host; he has devoted them to destruction; he has given them over to slaughter.
Their slain shall be flung out; the stench of their corpses shall rise — and the mountains shall melt with their blood.
And all the host of the heavens shall rot away, and the heavens shall be rolled up like a scroll; all their host shall wither — as a leaf withers from the vine, as a withering fig from the fig tree.
For my sword has drunk its fill in the heavens: behold, it descends upon Edom, upon the people I have devoted to judgment.
YHWH has a sword — full of blood, gorged with fat, with the blood of lambs and goats, with the kidney-fat of rams; for YHWH has a sacrifice in Bozrah, a great slaughter in the land of Edom.
Wild oxen shall go down with them, and young bulls with the mighty; their land shall be drenched with blood, and their soil gorged with fat.
For YHWH has a day of vengeance — a year of recompense for the cause of Zion.
And her streams shall be turned to pitch, and her soil to sulfur; her land shall become burning pitch.
Night and day it shall not be quenched; her smoke shall rise forever. From generation to generation she shall lie desolate; for age upon age none shall pass through her.
But the desert owl and the porcupine shall possess her; the great owl and the raven shall dwell in her. And he shall stretch over her the measuring line of chaos and the plumb-stones of emptiness.
Her nobles — there is no kingdom there to proclaim; and all her princes shall be nothing.
Thorns shall climb her palaces, nettles and thistles her fortresses; and she shall be a lair of jackals, a courtyard for the daughters of the ostrich.
Desert creatures shall meet with howling beasts, and the goat-demon shall call to its fellow; there also Lilith shall settle, and find for herself a resting place.
There the arrow-snake shall nest and lay, and hatch and brood in her shadow; there also the kites shall be gathered, each with her mate.
Search from the book of YHWH and read: not one of these is missing; none lacks her mate. For it is his mouth that has commanded, and his spirit that has gathered them.
And it is he who has cast the lot for them; his hand has portioned it out to them by line. They shall possess it forever; from generation to generation they shall dwell in it.

isa.35.text: The wilderness and the dry land shall be glad; the desert shall rejoice and blossom like the crocus.

It shall blossom abundantly and rejoice — with joy and singing! The glory of Lebanon shall be given to it, the splendor of Carmel and Sharon. They shall see the glory of YHWH, the splendor of our God.
Strengthen the weak hands; make firm the buckling knees.
Say to those whose hearts are racing: "Be strong — do not fear! Behold, your God comes with vengeance, with the recompense of God. He himself comes — and he will save you."
Then the eyes of the blind shall be opened, and the ears of the deaf unstopped;
then the lame shall leap like a deer, and the tongue of the mute sing for joy — for waters break forth in the wilderness, and streams in the desert.
The scorched mirage shall become a pool, and the thirsty ground springs of water; in the lair where jackals rested — grass, with reeds and rushes.
And a highway shall be there — a way — and it shall be called the Way of Holiness. The unclean shall not pass over it; it belongs to those who walk the way — and fools shall not stray onto it.
No lion shall be there; no ravening beast shall go up on it — none shall be found there. But the redeemed shall walk there;
and the ransomed of YHWH shall return and come to Zion with singing, everlasting joy upon their heads. Gladness and joy shall overtake them — and sorrow and sighing shall flee away.

isa.36.text: And it came to pass in the fourteenth year of King Hezekiah: Sennacherib king of Assyria came up against all the fortified cities of Judah and seized them.

And the king of Assyria sent the Rab-shakeh from Lachish to Jerusalem, to King Hezekiah, with a heavy force; and he took his stand by the conduit of the upper pool, on the highway to the Fuller's Field.
And there went out to him Eliakim son of Hilkiah, who was over the household, and Shebna the scribe, and Joah son of Asaph, the recorder.
And the Rab-shakeh said to them: "Say now to Hezekiah: Thus says the great king, the king of Assyria: What is this confidence in which you trust?
I say: mere lip-words are counsel and strength for war! Now — in whom do you trust, that you have rebelled against me?
Behold, you trust in this staff of crushed reed, in Egypt — which pierces the hand of any man who leans on it. Such is Pharaoh king of Egypt to all who trust in him.
And if you say to me, 'We trust in YHWH our God' — is it not he whose high places and altars Hezekiah removed, saying to Judah and Jerusalem, 'Before this altar you shall worship'?
Come now, make a wager with my lord the king of Assyria: I will give you two thousand horses — if you can put riders on them!
How then can you turn away the face of a single governor among the least of my lord's servants — while you trust in Egypt for chariots and horsemen?
And now — is it without YHWH that I have come up against this land to destroy it? YHWH said to me: Go up against this land and destroy it!"
And Eliakim and Shebna and Joah said to the Rab-shakeh: "Speak, please, to your servants in Aramaic, for we understand it; do not speak to us in Judean in the hearing of the people on the wall."

But the Rab-shakeh said: "Is it to your lord and to you that my lord sent me to speak these words? Was it not to the men sitting on the wall — doomed with you to eat their own dung and drink their own urine?"
Then the Rab-shakeh stood and called out with a great voice in Judean, and said: "Hear the words of the great king, the king of Assyria!
Thus says the king: Let not Hezekiah deceive you, for he cannot deliver you.
And let not Hezekiah make you trust in YHWH, saying, 'YHWH will surely deliver us; this city will not be given into the hand of the king of Assyria.'

Do not listen to Hezekiah! For thus says the king of Assyria: Make peace with me and come out to me — and eat, every one of you, from his own vine and his own fig tree, and drink, every one, the water of his own cistern,
until I come and take you to a land like your own land — a land of grain and new wine, a land of bread and vineyards.
Beware lest Hezekiah mislead you, saying, 'YHWH will deliver us.' Have the gods of the nations delivered — each one — his own land from the hand of the king of Assyria?
Where are the gods of Hamath and Arpad? Where are the gods of Sepharvaim? And did they deliver Samaria from my hand?
Who among all the gods of these lands has delivered his land from my hand — that YHWH should deliver Jerusalem from my hand?"
But they kept silent and answered him not a word — for the king's command was: "Do not answer him."

Then Eliakim son of Hilkiah, who was over the household, and Shebna the scribe, and Joah son of Asaph, the recorder, came to Hezekiah with their garments torn, and told him the words of the Rab-shakeh.

isa.37.text: And when King Hezekiah heard it, he tore his garments and covered himself with sackcloth, and went into the house of YHWH.
And he sent Eliakim, who was over the household, and Shebna the scribe, and the elders of the priests, covered in sackcloth, to Isaiah son of Amoz, the prophet.
And they said to him: "Thus says Hezekiah: This day is a day of distress and rebuke and disgrace — for children have come to the mouth of the womb, and there is no strength to give birth.

Perhaps YHWH your God will hear the words of the Rab-shakeh, whom his lord the king of Assyria sent to taunt the living God, and will rebuke the words YHWH your God has heard. Lift up a prayer for the remnant that is left."
And the servants of King Hezekiah came to Isaiah,
and Isaiah said to them: "Thus you shall say to your lord: Thus says YHWH: Do not fear the words you have heard, with which the underlings of the king of Assyria have blasphemed me.
Behold, I am putting a spirit in him: he shall hear a rumor and return to his own land — and I will cause him to fall by the sword in his own land."
And the Rab-shakeh returned and found the king of Assyria fighting against Libnah; for he had heard that he had pulled up from Lachish.
And he heard concerning Tirhakah king of Cush: "He has come out to fight with you." And when he heard it, he sent messengers to Hezekiah, saying:
"Thus shall you say to Hezekiah king of Judah: Do not let your God in whom you trust deceive you, saying: Jerusalem shall not be given into the hand of the king of Assyria.
Behold, you yourself have heard what the kings of Assyria have done to all the lands — devoting them to destruction. And you — will you be delivered?
Did the gods of the nations my fathers destroyed deliver them — Gozan and Haran and Rezeph, and the people of Eden who were in Telassar?
Where is the king of Hamath, and the king of Arpad, and the king of the city of Sepharvaim, of Hena and Ivvah?"
And Hezekiah took the letters from the hand of the messengers and read them; and Hezekiah went up to the house of YHWH and spread them out before YHWH.
And Hezekiah prayed to YHWH:
"YHWH of hosts, God of Israel, enthroned upon the cherubim — you alone are God over all the kingdoms of the earth; you made the heavens and the earth.
Incline your ear, YHWH, and hear! Open your eyes, YHWH, and see! Hear all the words of Sennacherib, which he has sent to taunt the living God.
Truly, YHWH, the kings of Assyria have laid waste all the lands, and their own land,
and have cast their gods into the fire — for they were no gods, but the work of human hands, wood and stone; so they destroyed them.
And now, YHWH our God, save us from his hand — that all the kingdoms of the earth may know that you alone are YHWH."
Then Isaiah son of Amoz sent to Hezekiah, saying: "Thus says YHWH, the God of Israel: Because you have prayed to me concerning Sennacherib king of Assyria —
this is the word YHWH has spoken against him: She despises you, she mocks you — the virgin daughter of Zion; behind you she tosses her head — the daughter of Jerusalem.
Whom have you taunted and blasphemed? Against whom have you raised your voice and lifted your eyes on high? Against the Holy One of Israel!

By the hand of your servants you have taunted the Lord, and said: 'With my many chariots I have climbed the heights of the mountains, the far recesses of Lebanon; I felled its tallest cedars, its choicest cypresses; I reached its remotest height, its garden forest.
I dug wells and drank waters; with the sole of my feet I dried up all the streams of Egypt.'
Have you not heard? Long ago I did it; from days of old I formed it — and now I have brought it to pass: that you should make fortified cities crash into heaps of ruins,
while their inhabitants, shorn of strength, were terrified and put to shame — became like plants of the field and green grass, like grass on the rooftops, blasted before it is grown.
But I know your sitting down, your going out and your coming in — and your raging against me.
Because your raging against me and your complacent ease have come up into my ears, I will put my hook in your nose and my bridle in your lips, and I will turn you back on the way by which you came.
And this shall be the sign for you: this year eat what grows of itself; in the second year what springs from that; and in the third year sow and reap, plant vineyards and eat their fruit.
And the surviving remnant of the house of Judah shall again take root downward and bear fruit upward;
for out of Jerusalem shall go forth a remnant, and survivors from Mount Zion. The zeal of YHWH of hosts will do this.

Therefore thus says YHWH concerning the king of Assyria: He shall not come into this city; he shall not shoot an arrow there; he shall not come before it with a shield, and he shall not heap up a siege-ramp against it.
By the way that he came, by the same he shall return — and into this city he shall not come: the word of YHWH.
For I will shield this city to save it — for my own sake, and for the sake of David my servant."
And the angel of YHWH went out and struck down in the camp of Assyria a hundred and eighty-five thousand; and when men rose early in the morning — behold, all of them were corpses, dead.
So Sennacherib king of Assyria pulled up and went and returned, and dwelt in Nineveh.

And as he was worshiping in the house of Nisroch his god, Adrammelech and Sharezer his sons struck him down with the sword and escaped to the land of Ararat. And Esarhaddon his son reigned in his place.

isa.38.text: In those days Hezekiah fell mortally ill; and Isaiah son of Amoz, the prophet, came to him and said to him: "Thus says YHWH: Set your house in order — for you are dying; you shall not live."
Then Hezekiah turned his face to the wall and prayed to YHWH,
and said: "Please, YHWH — remember how I have walked before you in truth and with a whole heart, and have done what is good in your eyes." And Hezekiah wept a great weeping.
And the word of YHWH came to Isaiah:
"Go and say to Hezekiah: Thus says YHWH, the God of David your father: I have heard your prayer; I have seen your tears. Behold, I am adding fifteen years to your days;
and from the palm of the king of Assyria I will deliver you and this city — and I will shield this city.
And this shall be the sign for you from YHWH, that YHWH will do this thing he has spoken:
behold, I am turning back the shadow of the steps, which has gone down on the stairway of Ahaz with the sun, ten steps backward." And the sun turned back ten steps on the stairway it had descended.
A writing of Hezekiah king of Judah, when he had been sick and recovered from his sickness:
I said: In the noonday-quiet of my days I must depart through the gates of Sheol; I am summoned away for the rest of my years.
I said: I shall not see Yah — Yah in the land of the living; I shall gaze on humankind no more, among the dwellers in the world of ceasing.
My dwelling is pulled up and carried off from me like a shepherd's tent; I have rolled up my life like a weaver — he cuts me from the loom. From day to night you bring me to my end.
I lay still until morning; like a lion, so he breaks all my bones. From day to night you bring me to my end.
Like a swift or a crane, so I twitter; I moan like the dove. My eyes grow weary looking upward: O Lord, I am oppressed — be my surety!
What shall I say? He has spoken to me, and he himself has done it. I will walk slowly all my years, because of the bitterness of my soul.
O Lord — by such things men live, and in all of them is the life of my spirit. Restore me to health; make me live!
Behold, for my peace I had bitterness — bitterness; but you have held back my soul in love from the pit of destruction, for you have cast all my sins behind your back.
For Sheol cannot thank you; Death cannot praise you; those who go down to the pit cannot hope for your faithfulness.
The living — the living, he thanks you, as I do this day; a father makes your faithfulness known to his children.
YHWH — to my rescue! And we will play my stringed songs all the days of our lives at the house of YHWH.
Now Isaiah had said, "Let them take a cake of figs and rub it on the boil, that he may recover."
And Hezekiah had said, "What is the sign that I shall go up to the house of YHWH?"

isa.39.text: At that time Merodach-baladan son of Baladan, king of Babylon, sent letters and a gift to Hezekiah — for he had heard that he had been sick and had grown strong.

And Hezekiah rejoiced over them, and showed them his treasure-house: the silver, the gold, the spices, the fine oil, his whole armory, and everything found among his treasures. There was nothing in his house or in all his dominion that Hezekiah did not show them.
Then Isaiah the prophet came to King Hezekiah and said to him: "What did these men say? And from where did they come to you?" And Hezekiah said, "From a far country they came to me — from Babylon."
And he said, "What have they seen in your house?" And Hezekiah said, "Everything in my house they have seen; there is nothing among my treasures I did not show them."
Then Isaiah said to Hezekiah: "Hear the word of YHWH of hosts:
Behold, days are coming when everything in your house, and all that your fathers have stored up to this day, shall be carried to Babylon. Nothing shall be left, says YHWH.
And some of your own sons, who shall issue from you, whom you shall father, shall be taken — and they shall be eunuchs in the palace of the king of Babylon."
And Hezekiah said to Isaiah, "The word of YHWH that you have spoken is good." For he said, "There shall be peace and steadfastness in my days."

ecc.1.text: The words of Qohelet, the Assembler, son of David, king in Jerusalem.
Vapor of vapors, says Qohelet — vapor of vapors: all is vapor.
What gain has a man from all his toil at which he toils under the sun?
A generation goes, and a generation comes — and the earth stands forever.
The sun rises and the sun sets — and pants back to its place, and rises there again.
Going to the south, circling to the north — circling, circling goes the wind, and on its circuits the wind returns.
All the rivers go to the sea, and the sea is not full; to the place where the rivers go, there they return to go again.
All things are weary — a man cannot say it. The eye is not satisfied with seeing, and the ear is not filled with hearing.
What has been is what shall be, and what has been done is what shall be done — and there is nothing at all new under the sun.
Is there a thing of which one says, "See this — it is new"? It already was, in the ages that were before us.
There is no remembrance of the former things; and even of the later things to come there will be no remembrance among those who come after.
I, Qohelet, was king over Israel in Jerusalem.
And I set my heart to search and probe with wisdom everything done under the heavens — a sorry business God has given the children of men to be busy with.
I have seen all the deeds done under the sun — and behold, all is vapor and a chasing after wind.
The crooked cannot be straightened, and what is missing cannot be counted.
