that Sanballat and Geshem sent to me, saying, "Come, let us meet together in the villages in the plain of Ono." But they were planning to do me harm.
So I sent messengers to them, saying: "I am doing a great work and cannot come down. Why should the work stop while I leave it and come down to you?"
And they sent to me in this way four times — and I answered them in the same way.
Then Sanballat sent his servant to me in the same way a fifth time, with an open letter in his hand.

In it was written: "It is reported among the nations — and Gashmu says it — that you and the Jews are planning to rebel; that is why you are building the wall. And you are to become their king, according to these reports;
and you have even set up prophets to proclaim about you in Jerusalem, 'There is a king in Judah!' And now these reports will be heard by the king. So come now, let us take counsel together."
And I sent to him, saying: "Nothing like these things you are saying has happened — you are inventing them out of your own heart."
For they were all trying to frighten us, thinking, "Their hands will drop from the work, and it will never be done." But now — strengthen my hands!

And I came to the house of Shemaiah son of Delaiah, son of Mehetabel — who was shut in — and he said: "Let us meet in the house of God, inside the temple, and shut the temple doors; for they are coming to kill you — by night they are coming to kill you."
But I said: "Should a man like me flee? And who is there like me who could enter the temple and live? I will not go in."
And I discerned — and behold, God had not sent him; he had spoken the prophecy against me because Tobiah and Sanballat had hired him.
He was hired so that I would be afraid and act accordingly and sin — and it would give them a bad name for me, so that they could taunt me.
Remember, O my God, Tobiah and Sanballat according to these their deeds — and also Noadiah the prophetess and the rest of the prophets who kept trying to frighten me.
So the wall was finished on the twenty-fifth of Elul — in fifty-two days.
And it came to pass, when all our enemies heard, and all the nations around us saw, that they fell greatly in their own eyes — for they knew that this work had been done by our God.
Also in those days the nobles of Judah were sending many letters to Tobiah, and Tobiah's letters were coming to them;
for many in Judah were bound to him by oath, because he was son-in-law to Shecaniah son of Arah, and his son Jehohanan had taken the daughter of Meshullam son of Berechiah.
They even kept speaking of his good deeds before me, and reporting my words to him. Tobiah sent letters to frighten me.

neh.7.text: And it came to pass, when the wall had been built and I had set up the doors, that the gatekeepers and the singers and the Levites were appointed.
And I put my brother Hanani, and Hananiah the commander of the citadel, in charge over Jerusalem — for he was a man of truth, and feared God more than most.

And I said to them: "The gates of Jerusalem shall not be opened until the sun is hot; and while the watchmen are still standing, let them shut and bar the doors. And station watches of the inhabitants of Jerusalem, each at his own watch, and each opposite his own house."
Now the city was wide and large, but the people within it were few, and no houses had been rebuilt.

And my God put it into my heart to gather the nobles and the officials and the people to be enrolled by genealogy. And I found the book of the genealogy of those who came up at the first — and I found written in it:

These are the people of the province who came up from the captivity of the exile, whom Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon had carried away, and who returned to Jerusalem and to Judah, each to his own town,
who came with Zerubbabel, Jeshua, Nehemiah, Azariah, Raamiah, Nahamani, Mordecai, Bilshan, Mispereth, Bigvai, Nehum, Baanah. The count of the men of the people of Israel:
the sons of Parosh — two thousand one hundred and seventy-two;
the sons of Shephatiah — three hundred and seventy-two;
the sons of Arah — six hundred and fifty-two;
the sons of Pahath-moab, of the sons of Jeshua and Joab — two thousand eight hundred and eighteen;
the sons of Elam — one thousand two hundred and fifty-four;
the sons of Zattu — eight hundred and forty-five;
the sons of Zaccai — seven hundred and sixty;
the sons of Binnui — six hundred and forty-eight;
the sons of Bebai — six hundred and twenty-eight;
the sons of Azgad — two thousand three hundred and twenty-two;
the sons of Adonikam — six hundred and sixty-seven;
the sons of Bigvai — two thousand and sixty-seven;
the sons of Adin — six hundred and fifty-five;
the sons of Ater, of Hezekiah — ninety-eight;
the sons of Hashum — three hundred and twenty-eight;
the sons of Bezai — three hundred and twenty-four;
the sons of Hariph — one hundred and twelve;
the sons of Gibeon — ninety-five;
the men of Bethlehem and Netophah — one hundred and eighty-eight;
the men of Anathoth — one hundred and twenty-eight;
the men of Beth-azmaveth — forty-two;
the men of Kiriath-jearim, Chephirah, and Beeroth — seven hundred and forty-three;
the men of Ramah and Geba — six hundred and twenty-one;
the men of Michmas — one hundred and twenty-two;
the men of Bethel and Ai — one hundred and twenty-three;
the men of the other Nebo — fifty-two;
the sons of the other Elam — one thousand two hundred and fifty-four;
the sons of Harim — three hundred and twenty;
the sons of Jericho — three hundred and forty-five;
the sons of Lod, Hadid, and Ono — seven hundred and twenty-one;
the sons of Senaah — three thousand nine hundred and thirty.
The priests: the sons of Jedaiah, of the house of Jeshua — nine hundred and seventy-three;
the sons of Immer — one thousand and fifty-two;
the sons of Pashhur — one thousand two hundred and forty-seven;
the sons of Harim — one thousand and seventeen.
The Levites: the sons of Jeshua, of Kadmiel, of the sons of Hodevah — seventy-four.
The singers: the sons of Asaph — one hundred and forty-eight.
The gatekeepers: the sons of Shallum, the sons of Ater, the sons of Talmon, the sons of Akkub, the sons of Hatita, the sons of Shobai — one hundred and thirty-eight.
The temple servants: the sons of Ziha, the sons of Hasupha, the sons of Tabbaoth,
the sons of Keros, the sons of Sia, the sons of Padon,
the sons of Lebana, the sons of Hagaba, the sons of Shalmai,
the sons of Hanan, the sons of Giddel, the sons of Gahar,
the sons of Reaiah, the sons of Rezin, the sons of Nekoda,
the sons of Gazzam, the sons of Uzza, the sons of Paseah,
the sons of Besai, the sons of Meunim, the sons of Nephushesim,
the sons of Bakbuk, the sons of Hakupha, the sons of Harhur,
the sons of Bazlith, the sons of Mehida, the sons of Harsha,
the sons of Barkos, the sons of Sisera, the sons of Temah,
the sons of Neziah, the sons of Hatipha.
The sons of Solomon's servants: the sons of Sotai, the sons of Sophereth, the sons of Perida,
the sons of Jaala, the sons of Darkon, the sons of Giddel,
the sons of Shephatiah, the sons of Hattil, the sons of Pochereth-hazzebaim, the sons of Amon.
All the temple servants and the sons of Solomon's servants — three hundred and ninety-two.
And these came up from Tel-melah, Tel-harsha, Cherub, Addon, and Immer — but they could not declare their fathers' houses or their descent, whether they were of Israel:
the sons of Delaiah, the sons of Tobiah, the sons of Nekoda — six hundred and forty-two.
And of the priests: the sons of Hobaiah, the sons of Hakkoz, the sons of Barzillai — who had taken a wife from the daughters of Barzillai the Gileadite and was called by their name.
These searched for their entries among those enrolled by genealogy, but it was not found; so they were excluded from the priesthood as defiled.
And the governor said to them that they were not to eat of the most holy things until the priest should stand with Urim and Thummim.
The whole assembly together: forty-two thousand three hundred and sixty,
besides their male and female servants — these were seven thousand three hundred and thirty-seven — and they had two hundred and forty-five singing men and singing women.
Their camels: four hundred and thirty-five; their donkeys: six thousand seven hundred and twenty.
And some of the heads of the fathers' houses gave to the work. The governor gave to the treasury a thousand darics of gold, fifty basins, five hundred and thirty priestly garments;
and some of the heads of the fathers' houses gave to the treasury of the work twenty thousand darics of gold and two thousand two hundred minas of silver;
and what the rest of the people gave: twenty thousand darics of gold, two thousand minas of silver, and sixty-seven priestly garments.

So the priests and the Levites and the gatekeepers and the singers, and some of the people, and the temple servants — all Israel — settled in their towns. And when the seventh month arrived, the children of Israel were in their towns.

neh.8.text: Then all the people gathered as one man into the square before the Water Gate; and they told Ezra the scribe to bring the book of the law of Moses, which YHWH had commanded Israel.
And Ezra the priest brought the law before the assembly — men and women, and all who could hear with understanding — on the first day of the seventh month;

and he read from it, facing the square before the Water Gate, from first light until midday, in front of the men and the women and those who could understand — and the ears of all the people were toward the book of the law.

And Ezra the scribe stood on a wooden platform made for the purpose; and beside him stood Mattithiah, Shema, Anaiah, Uriah, Hilkiah, and Maaseiah on his right, and on his left Pedaiah, Mishael, Malchijah, Hashum, Hashbaddanah, Zechariah, Meshullam.
And Ezra opened the book in the sight of all the people — for he was above all the people — and as he opened it, all the people stood.
And Ezra blessed YHWH, the great God; and all the people answered, "Amen! Amen!" with their hands lifted high; and they bowed and worshiped YHWH with faces to the ground.

And Jeshua, Bani, Sherebiah, Jamin, Akkub, Shabbethai, Hodiah, Maaseiah, Kelita, Azariah, Jozabad, Hanan, Pelaiah — the Levites — were making the people understand the law, while the people stood in their places;
and they read from the book, from the law of God, distinctly, giving the sense — so that they understood the reading.

And Nehemiah — that is, the governor — and Ezra the priest, the scribe, and the Levites who taught the people said to all the people: "This day is holy to YHWH your God. Do not mourn and do not weep." For all the people were weeping as they heard the words of the law.

And he said to them: "Go — eat the rich food and drink the sweet drinks, and send portions to anyone who has nothing prepared; for this day is holy to our Lord. And do not be grieved — for the joy of YHWH is your stronghold."
And the Levites quieted all the people, saying, "Hush — for the day is holy; do not be grieved."
And all the people went to eat and to drink and to send portions and to make great rejoicing — because they had understood the words that had been made known to them.
And on the second day the heads of the fathers' houses of all the people, the priests and the Levites, gathered to Ezra the scribe — to gain insight into the words of the law.
And they found written in the law, which YHWH had commanded by Moses, that the children of Israel should dwell in booths during the feast of the seventh month,

and that they should proclaim and send word through all their towns and through Jerusalem, saying: "Go out to the hill country and bring olive branches and wild-olive branches, myrtle branches, palm branches, and branches of leafy trees, to make booths, as it is written."

So the people went out and brought them, and made themselves booths — each on his roof, and in their courtyards, and in the courts of the house of God, and in the square of the Water Gate, and in the square of the Ephraim Gate.

And the whole assembly of those who had returned from the captivity made booths and lived in the booths — for the children of Israel had not done so from the days of Jeshua son of Nun until that day. And there was very great rejoicing.

And day by day, from the first day to the last day, he read from the book of the law of God. And they kept the feast seven days; and on the eighth day there was a solemn assembly, according to the ordinance.

neh.9.text: And on the twenty-fourth day of this month the children of Israel gathered with fasting, in sackcloth, with earth upon them;
and the seed of Israel separated themselves from all foreigners, and stood and confessed their sins and the iniquities of their fathers.
And they rose in their places and read from the book of the law of YHWH their God for a quarter of the day; and for another quarter they confessed and worshiped YHWH their God.
And on the stairs of the Levites stood Jeshua and Bani, Kadmiel, Shebaniah, Bunni, Sherebiah, Bani, Chenani — and they cried with a great voice to YHWH their God.

And the Levites — Jeshua and Kadmiel, Bani, Hashabneiah, Sherebiah, Hodiah, Shebaniah, Pethahiah — said: "Rise, bless YHWH your God from everlasting to everlasting! And may they bless your glorious name, exalted above all blessing and praise.

You are he, YHWH — you alone. You made the heavens, the heavens of the heavens, and all their host; the earth and all that is on her; the seas and all that is in them — and you give life to them all, and the host of the heavens bows down to you.
You are he, YHWH, the God who chose Abram and brought him out of Ur of the Chaldeans, and made his name Abraham;

and you found his heart faithful before you, and cut with him the covenant to give the land of the Canaanite, the Hittite, the Amorite and the Perizzite and the Jebusite and the Girgashite — to give it to his seed. And you have kept your words, for you are righteous.
And you saw the affliction of our fathers in Egypt, and heard their cry at the Sea of Reeds;

and you gave signs and wonders against Pharaoh and all his servants and all the people of his land — for you knew that they acted arrogantly against them; and you made yourself a name, as it is this day.
And you split the sea before them, and they passed through the midst of the sea on dry ground; and their pursuers you hurled into the depths — like a stone into mighty waters.
And by a pillar of cloud you led them by day, and by a pillar of fire by night, to light for them the way they should walk.
And on Mount Sinai you came down, and spoke with them from the heavens; and you gave them upright judgments and true laws, good statutes and commandments;
and your holy sabbath you made known to them, and commandments and statutes and law you commanded them by the hand of Moses your servant.

And bread from the heavens you gave them for their hunger, and water from the rock you brought out for their thirst; and you told them to go in and possess the land you had lifted your hand to give them.
But they — our fathers — acted arrogantly; they stiffened their necks and did not listen to your commandments.

They refused to listen, and did not remember your wonders that you did among them; they stiffened their necks and appointed a head to return to their slavery — in their rebellion. But you are a God of forgivenesses — gracious and compassionate, slow to anger and abounding in steadfast love — and you did not forsake them.
Even when they made themselves a molten calf and said, 'This is your god who brought you up from Egypt,' and committed great blasphemies —

you, in your abundant compassion, did not forsake them in the wilderness: the pillar of cloud did not depart from over them by day, to lead them on the way, nor the pillar of fire by night, to light for them the way they should walk.
And you gave your good spirit to instruct them; you did not withhold your manna from their mouths, and you gave them water for their thirst.
Forty years you sustained them in the wilderness — they lacked nothing; their garments did not wear out, and their feet did not swell.
And you gave them kingdoms and peoples, allotting them as a boundary-portion; and they possessed the land of Sihon — the land of the king of Heshbon — and the land of Og king of Bashan.
And their children you multiplied like the stars of the heavens, and brought them into the land you had told their fathers to enter and possess.

And the children went in and possessed the land; and you subdued before them the inhabitants of the land, the Canaanites, and gave them into their hand — with their kings and the peoples of the land — to do with them as they pleased.

And they captured fortified cities and rich soil, and took possession of houses full of every good thing, hewn cisterns, vineyards and olive groves and fruit trees in abundance; and they ate and were filled and grew fat — and luxuriated in your great goodness.
But they were defiant and rebelled against you; they cast your law behind their backs, and killed your prophets who had warned them, to turn them back to you — and they committed great blasphemies.

So you gave them into the hand of their adversaries, who oppressed them. And in the time of their distress they cried to you — and you heard from the heavens, and according to your abundant compassion you gave them deliverers, who delivered them from the hand of their adversaries.

But when they had rest, they returned to doing evil before you; and you abandoned them to the hand of their enemies, who ruled over them. Then they returned and cried out to you — and you heard from the heavens and rescued them, according to your compassion, many times.

And you warned them, to turn them back to your law; but they acted arrogantly and did not listen to your commandments, and sinned against your judgments — by which, if a man does them, he shall live. They gave a stubborn shoulder, stiffened their necks, and would not listen.
And you bore with them many years, and warned them by your spirit through your prophets — and they would not give ear. So you gave them into the hand of the peoples of the lands.
Yet in your abundant compassion you did not make an end of them, and you did not forsake them — for you are a gracious and compassionate God.

And now, our God — the great, the mighty, and the awesome God, keeping covenant and steadfast love — let not all the hardship seem small before you that has found us: our kings, our princes, our priests, our prophets, our fathers, and all your people, from the days of the kings of Assyria to this day.
And you are righteous in all that has come upon us — for you have acted in truth, and we have done wickedly.
And our kings, our princes, our priests, and our fathers have not done your law; they have not attended to your commandments and your testimonies with which you testified against them.
Even in their own kingdom, amid your great goodness that you gave them, in the broad and rich land you set before them, they did not serve you — and they did not turn from their evil deeds.
Behold — we are slaves today; and the land you gave our fathers, to eat its fruit and its goodness — behold, we are slaves upon it.
And its produce goes in abundance to the kings you have set over us because of our sins; they rule over our bodies and over our livestock as they please — and we are in great distress.

neh.10.text: Because of all this, we are cutting a firm agreement and writing it; and on the sealed document are our princes, our Levites, our priests.
And on the sealed documents: Nehemiah the governor, son of Hacaliah — and Zedekiah,
Maaziah, Bilgai, Shemaiah — these are the priests.
And the Levites: Jeshua son of Azaniah, Binnui of the sons of Henadad, Kadmiel;
and their brothers: Shebaniah, Hodiah, Kelita, Pelaiah, Hanan,
The heads of the people: Parosh, Pahath-moab, Elam, Zattu, Bani,

And the rest of the people — the priests, the Levites, the gatekeepers, the singers, the temple servants, and all who had separated themselves from the peoples of the lands to the law of God, their wives, their sons, and their daughters, everyone with knowledge and understanding —

join their brothers, their nobles, and enter into a curse and an oath: to walk in the law of God, given by the hand of Moses the servant of God, and to keep and do all the commandments of YHWH our Lord, and his judgments and his statutes;
and that we will not give our daughters to the peoples of the land, nor take their daughters for our sons;

and that when the peoples of the land bring wares or any grain to sell on the sabbath day, we will not buy from them on the sabbath or on a holy day; and that we will forgo the seventh year, and the exacting of every debt.
And we have laid on ourselves commandments: to charge ourselves a third of a shekel yearly for the service of the house of our God —

for the rows of bread and the regular grain offering and the regular burnt offering, for the sabbaths, the new moons, the appointed feasts; and for the holy things and the sin offerings to make atonement for Israel — and all the work of the house of our God.

And we have cast lots — the priests, the Levites, and the people — for the wood offering, to bring it to the house of our God, by our fathers' houses, at appointed times year by year, to burn on the altar of YHWH our God, as it is written in the law;
and to bring the firstfruits of our soil, and the firstfruits of all the fruit of every tree, year by year, to the house of YHWH;

and the firstborn of our sons and of our livestock, as it is written in the law — and the firstborn of our herds and our flocks — to bring to the house of our God, to the priests ministering in the house of our God;

and the first of our dough and our contributions, and the fruit of every tree, new wine and oil, we will bring to the priests, to the chambers of the house of our God — and the tithe of our soil to the Levites, for it is the Levites who collect the tithes in all the towns of our labor.

And the priest, the son of Aaron, shall be with the Levites when the Levites take the tithe; and the Levites shall bring up the tithe of the tithe to the house of our God, to the chambers of the treasure-house.

For to the chambers the children of Israel and the children of Levi shall bring the contribution of grain, new wine, and oil; there are the vessels of the sanctuary, and the ministering priests, and the gatekeepers and the singers. And we will not forsake the house of our God.

neh.11.text: And the princes of the people settled in Jerusalem; and the rest of the people cast lots to bring one out of ten to live in Jerusalem, the holy city, while the nine remaining parts stayed in the towns.
And the people blessed all the men who freely offered to live in Jerusalem.

And these are the heads of the province who settled in Jerusalem — while in the towns of Judah each lived on his own property, in their towns: Israel, the priests, the Levites, the temple servants, and the sons of Solomon's servants —

and in Jerusalem there settled some of the sons of Judah and some of the sons of Benjamin. Of the sons of Judah: Athaiah son of Uzziah, son of Zechariah, son of Amariah, son of Shephatiah, son of Mahalalel, of the sons of Perez;
and Maaseiah son of Baruch, son of Col-hozeh, son of Hazaiah, son of Adaiah, son of Joiarib, son of Zechariah, son of the Shilonite.
All the sons of Perez who settled in Jerusalem: four hundred and sixty-eight men of valor.
And these are the sons of Benjamin: Sallu son of Meshullam, son of Joed, son of Pedaiah, son of Kolaiah, son of Maaseiah, son of Ithiel, son of Jeshaiah;
and after him Gabbai, Sallai — nine hundred and twenty-eight.
And Joel son of Zichri was overseer over them, and Judah son of Hassenuah was second over the city.
Of the priests: Jedaiah son of Joiarib, Jachin,
Seraiah son of Hilkiah, son of Meshullam, son of Zadok, son of Meraioth, son of Ahitub — ruler of the house of God —
and their brothers who did the work of the house: eight hundred and twenty-two; and Adaiah son of Jeroham, son of Pelaliah, son of Amzi, son of Zechariah, son of Pashhur, son of Malchijah,
and his brothers, heads of fathers' houses: two hundred and forty-two; and Amashsai son of Azarel, son of Ahzai, son of Meshillemoth, son of Immer,
and their brothers, mighty men of valor: one hundred and twenty-eight; and their overseer was Zabdiel son of Haggedolim.
And of the Levites: Shemaiah son of Hasshub, son of Azrikam, son of Hashabiah, son of Bunni;
and Shabbethai and Jozabad, of the heads of the Levites, over the outside work of the house of God;

and Mattaniah son of Mica, son of Zabdi, son of Asaph — the leader who began the thanksgiving in prayer — and Bakbukiah, second among his brothers; and Abda son of Shammua, son of Galal, son of Jeduthun.
All the Levites in the holy city: two hundred and eighty-four.
And the gatekeepers — Akkub, Talmon, and their brothers who kept watch at the gates: one hundred and seventy-two.
And the rest of Israel, the priests, the Levites, were in all the towns of Judah, each on his own inheritance.
And the temple servants lived on the Ophel; and Ziha and Gishpa were over the temple servants.
And the overseer of the Levites in Jerusalem was Uzzi son of Bani, son of Hashabiah, son of Mattaniah, son of Mica, of the sons of Asaph, the singers — over the work of the house of God;
for there was a command of the king concerning them, and a fixed provision for the singers, each day's matter on its day.
And Pethahiah son of Meshezabel, of the sons of Zerah son of Judah, was at the king's hand in every matter concerning the people.
And as for the villages with their fields: some of the sons of Judah settled in Kiriath-arba and its daughter-towns, in Dibon and its daughter-towns, in Jekabzeel and its villages,
in Jeshua, in Moladah, in Beth-pelet,
in Hazar-shual, in Beersheba and its daughter-towns,
in Ziklag, in Meconah and its daughter-towns,
in En-rimmon, in Zorah, in Jarmuth,
Zanoah, Adullam and their villages, Lachish and its fields, Azekah and its daughter-towns. So they camped from Beersheba to the Valley of Hinnom.
And the sons of Benjamin from Geba onward: Michmash, Aija, Bethel and its daughter-towns,
Anathoth, Nob, Ananiah,
Hazor, Ramah, Gittaim,
Hadid, Zeboim, Neballat,
Lod, and Ono — the Valley of the Craftsmen.
And of the Levites, certain divisions of Judah were joined to Benjamin.

neh.12.text: And these are the priests and the Levites who came up with Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel, and Jeshua: Seraiah, Jeremiah, Ezra,
Amariah, Malluch, Hattush,
Shecaniah, Rehum, Meremoth,
Iddo, Ginnethoi, Abijah,
Mijamin, Maadiah, Bilgah,
Shemaiah, and Joiarib, Jedaiah,
Sallu, Amok, Hilkiah, Jedaiah. These were the heads of the priests and their brothers in the days of Jeshua.
And the Levites: Jeshua, Binnui, Kadmiel, Sherebiah, Judah, Mattaniah — who with his brothers was over the songs of thanksgiving;
and Bakbukiah and Unni, their brothers, stood opposite them in the watches.
And Jeshua fathered Joiakim, and Joiakim fathered Eliashib, and Eliashib — Joiada,
and Joiada fathered Jonathan, and Jonathan fathered Jaddua.
And in the days of Joiakim the priests, heads of fathers' houses, were: of Seraiah, Meraiah; of Jeremiah, Hananiah;
of Ezra, Meshullam; of Amariah, Jehohanan;
of Malluchi, Jonathan; of Shebaniah, Joseph;
of Harim, Adna; of Meraioth, Helkai;
of Iddo, Zechariah; of Ginnethon, Meshullam;
of Abijah, Zichri; of Miniamin, of Moadiah, Piltai;
of Bilgah, Shammua; of Shemaiah, Jehonathan;
and of Joiarib, Mattenai; of Jedaiah, Uzzi;
of Sallai, Kallai; of Amok, Eber;
of Hilkiah, Hashabiah; of Jedaiah, Nethanel.
The Levites in the days of Eliashib, Joiada, Johanan, and Jaddua were recorded as heads of fathers' houses — the priests too, down to the reign of Darius the Persian.
The sons of Levi, heads of the fathers' houses, were written in the book of the chronicles, down to the days of Johanan son of Eliashib.

And the heads of the Levites: Hashabiah, Sherebiah, and Jeshua son of Kadmiel, with their brothers opposite them, to praise and give thanks by the command of David the man of God — watch alongside watch:
Mattaniah and Bakbukiah, Obadiah, Meshullam, Talmon, Akkub — gatekeepers keeping the watch at the storehouses of the gates.
These were in the days of Joiakim son of Jeshua, son of Jozadak, and in the days of Nehemiah the governor and Ezra the priest, the scribe.

And at the dedication of the wall of Jerusalem they sought out the Levites from all their places, to bring them to Jerusalem to keep the dedication with gladness — with thanksgivings and with song, cymbals, harps, and lyres.
And the sons of the singers gathered — from the plain around Jerusalem, and from the villages of the Netophathites,
and from Beth-gilgal, and from the fields of Geba and Azmaveth; for the singers had built themselves villages around Jerusalem.
And the priests and the Levites purified themselves; and they purified the people, and the gates, and the wall.
Then I brought the princes of Judah up onto the wall, and I appointed two great thanksgiving choirs and processions. One went to the right, on top of the wall, toward the Dung Gate;
and after them went Hoshaiah and half the princes of Judah,
and Azariah, Ezra, and Meshullam,
Judah and Benjamin, and Shemaiah and Jeremiah;
and some of the sons of the priests with trumpets: Zechariah son of Jonathan, son of Shemaiah, son of Mattaniah, son of Micaiah, son of Zaccur, son of Asaph,
and his brothers — Shemaiah and Azarel, Milalai, Gilalai, Maai, Nethanel and Judah, Hanani — with the musical instruments of David the man of God; and Ezra the scribe went before them.
And at the Fountain Gate, straight ahead of them, they went up by the steps of the City of David, at the ascent of the wall, above the house of David, to the Water Gate on the east.
And the second thanksgiving choir went to the left, and I after it, with half the people — on top of the wall, above the Tower of the Ovens, to the Broad Wall,
and above the Ephraim Gate, and over the Old Gate and the Fish Gate, and the Tower of Hananel and the Tower of the Hundred, to the Sheep Gate; and they came to a halt at the Gate of the Guard.
So the two thanksgiving choirs stood in the house of God — and I, and half the officials with me,
and the priests — Eliakim, Maaseiah, Miniamin, Micaiah, Elioenai, Zechariah, Hananiah — with trumpets;
and Maaseiah and Shemaiah and Eleazar and Uzzi and Jehohanan and Malchijah and Elam and Ezer. And the singers sang out, with Jezrahiah the overseer.
And they offered great sacrifices that day and rejoiced — for God had made them rejoice with great joy; the women and the children rejoiced also — and the joy of Jerusalem was heard from far away.

And on that day men were appointed over the chambers for the stores, for the contributions, the firstfruits, and the tithes — to gather into them, from the fields of the towns, the portions required by the law for the priests and the Levites; for Judah rejoiced over the priests and the Levites who stood to serve.
And they kept the charge of their God and the charge of purification — and so did the singers and the gatekeepers — according to the command of David and of Solomon his son;
for long ago, in the days of David and Asaph, there were heads of the singers, and songs of praise and thanksgiving to God.

And all Israel, in the days of Zerubbabel and in the days of Nehemiah, gave the portions of the singers and the gatekeepers, each day's matter on its day; and they set apart what was holy for the Levites, and the Levites set it apart for the sons of Aaron.

neh.13.text: On that day the book of Moses was read in the ears of the people; and there was found written in it that no Ammonite or Moabite should ever enter the assembly of God —
because they had not met the children of Israel with bread and water, but had hired Balaam against them to curse them; and our God turned the curse into a blessing.
And when they heard the law, they separated all the mixed multitude from Israel.
Now before this, Eliashib the priest, appointed over the chambers of the house of our God, being related to Tobiah,

had prepared for him a large chamber — where formerly they had put the grain offering, the frankincense, and the vessels, and the tithes of grain, new wine, and oil, commanded for the Levites and the singers and the gatekeepers, and the contributions for the priests.
And during all this I was not in Jerusalem — for in the thirty-second year of Artaxerxes king of Babylon I had gone to the king; and at the end of some days I asked leave of the king,
and came to Jerusalem. And I discerned the evil that Eliashib had done for Tobiah — preparing him a chamber in the courts of the house of God.
And it grieved me exceedingly, and I threw all the household goods of Tobiah out of the chamber;
and I gave orders, and they purified the chambers, and I brought back there the vessels of the house of God, with the grain offering and the frankincense.
And I learned that the portions of the Levites had not been given — so that the Levites and the singers who did the work had fled, each to his own field.
And I contended with the officials and said, "Why is the house of God forsaken?" And I gathered them and set them at their posts;
and all Judah brought the tithe of grain and new wine and oil to the storehouses.

And I appointed as treasurers over the storehouses Shelemiah the priest and Zadok the scribe, and Pedaiah of the Levites, and at their hand Hanan son of Zaccur, son of Mattaniah — for they were counted faithful; and their charge was to distribute to their brothers.
Remember me, O my God, concerning this; and do not wipe out my loyal deeds that I have done for the house of my God and for its watches.

In those days I saw in Judah men treading winepresses on the sabbath, and bringing in heaps of grain and loading donkeys — and also wine, grapes, and figs, and every kind of load — and bringing them into Jerusalem on the sabbath day. And I warned them on the day they sold provisions.
And the Tyrians who lived there were bringing in fish and every kind of merchandise, and selling on the sabbath to the sons of Judah — and in Jerusalem!
And I contended with the nobles of Judah and said to them: "What is this evil thing you are doing — profaning the sabbath day?
Did not your fathers do just this — and did not our God bring all this evil upon us and upon this city? Yet you are adding burning wrath against Israel by profaning the sabbath!"

And it came to pass, when the gates of Jerusalem grew dark with shadow before the sabbath, that I gave orders and the doors were shut; and I ordered that they not be opened until after the sabbath. And I stationed some of my young men at the gates — no load was to come in on the sabbath day.
So the traders and sellers of every kind of merchandise spent the night outside Jerusalem — once and twice.
And I warned them and said to them: "Why are you spending the night in front of the wall? If you do it again, I will lay a hand on you!" From that time on they did not come on the sabbath.

And I told the Levites to purify themselves and come and keep the gates, to sanctify the sabbath day. This also remember for me, O my God — and spare me according to the abundance of your steadfast love.
Also in those days I saw the Jews who had married women of Ashdod, Ammon, and Moab;
and half their children spoke the language of Ashdod — they could not speak Judean, but only the tongue of each people.

And I contended with them and cursed them, and struck some of the men and pulled out their hair; and I made them swear by God: "You shall not give your daughters to their sons, nor take any of their daughters for your sons or for yourselves!

Did not Solomon king of Israel sin over such women? Among the many nations there was no king like him — beloved of his God, and God made him king over all Israel — yet even him the foreign women made to sin.
Shall we then listen to you, to do all this great evil, to be faithless to our God by marrying foreign women?"
And one of the sons of Joiada, son of Eliashib the high priest, was son-in-law to Sanballat the Horonite — and I chased him from me.
Remember them, O my God, for the defilings of the priesthood and of the covenant of the priesthood and the Levites!
So I purified them from everything foreign, and established watches for the priests and the Levites, each in his own work,
and for the wood offering at appointed times, and for the firstfruits. Remember me, O my God, for good.

1th.1.text: Paul and Silvanus and Timothy — to the assembly of the Thessalonians in God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ: grace to you, and peace.
We give thanks to God always for all of you, making mention of you in our prayers, unceasingly
remembering your work of faith and labor of love and endurance of hope in our Lord Jesus Christ, before our God and Father —
knowing, brothers beloved by God, your election;
because our good news came to you not in word only, but also in power and in the Holy Spirit and in full conviction — just as you know what kind of men we proved to be among you, for your sake.
And you became imitators of us and of the Lord, receiving the word in much affliction, with the joy of the Holy Spirit,
so that you became a pattern to all the believers in Macedonia and in Achaia.
For from you the word of the Lord has rung out — not only in Macedonia and Achaia; in every place your faith toward God has gone forth, so that we have no need to say anything.
For they themselves report about us what kind of entrance we had with you, and how you turned to God from the idols — to serve a living and true God,
and to await his Son from the heavens, whom he raised from the dead: Jesus, who rescues us from the coming wrath.

1th.2.text: For you yourselves know, brothers, our entrance among you — that it has not proved empty;
but having already suffered and been outrageously treated at Philippi, as you know, we spoke out boldly in our God to tell you the good news of God amid great struggle.
For our appeal does not come from error, nor from impurity, nor with deceit;
but just as we have been tested and approved by God to be entrusted with the good news, so we speak — not as pleasing men, but God, who tests our hearts.
For we never came with a word of flattery, as you know, nor with a pretext for greed — God is witness —
nor seeking glory from men, neither from you nor from others,
though we could have thrown our weight as apostles of Christ. Instead we became gentle in your midst — like a nursing mother cherishing her own children.
So, yearning for you, we were glad to share with you not only the good news of God but our very souls — because you had become beloved to us.
For you remember, brothers, our labor and toil: working night and day, so as not to burden any of you, we proclaimed to you the good news of God.
You are witnesses, and God also, how devoutly and justly and blamelessly we behaved toward you who believe —
just as you know how we treated each one of you, as a father his own children,
exhorting you and encouraging and testifying that you should walk worthily of God, who calls you into his own kingdom and glory.

And for this reason we too give thanks to God unceasingly: that when you received the word of God heard from us, you welcomed it not as a word of men but as what it truly is — the word of God, which is also at work in you who believe.
For you became imitators, brothers, of the assemblies of God in Judea that are in Christ Jesus — because you suffered the same things from your own countrymen as they did from the Judeans,
who killed both the Lord Jesus and the prophets, and drove us out; who do not please God, and are set against all humankind,
hindering us from speaking to the nations that they might be saved — so as always to fill up the measure of their sins. But the wrath has come upon them at last.
But we, brothers, orphaned from you for a short season — in face, not in heart — were all the more eager, with great longing, to see your face.
For we wanted to come to you — I, Paul, once and again — and Satan cut us off.
For what is our hope or joy or crown of boasting — is it not you? — before our Lord Jesus at his coming?
For you are our glory and our joy.

1th.3.text: Therefore, when we could bear it no longer, we were willing to be left behind in Athens alone,
and we sent Timothy, our brother and God's co-worker in the good news of Christ, to strengthen you and encourage you concerning your faith,
that no one be shaken in these afflictions. For you yourselves know that we are set for this;
for even when we were with you, we kept telling you beforehand that we were going to be afflicted — and so it happened, as you know.
For this reason, when I could bear it no longer, I sent to learn of your faith — lest somehow the tempter had tempted you, and our labor prove empty.
But now Timothy has come to us from you and brought us the good news of your faith and love — and that you always keep a good remembrance of us, longing to see us just as we long to see you.
For this reason, brothers, we were comforted about you, in all our distress and affliction, through your faith —
because now we live, if you stand firm in the Lord.
For what thanksgiving can we render to God for you, for all the joy with which we rejoice because of you before our God,
praying night and day, beyond all measure, that we may see your face and complete what is lacking in your faith?
Now may our God and Father himself, and our Lord Jesus, make straight our way to you;
and may the Lord make you increase and overflow in love for one another and for all — just as we do for you —
so as to strengthen your hearts, blameless in holiness before our God and Father, at the coming of our Lord Jesus with all his holy ones.

1th.4.text: Finally then, brothers, we ask you and urge you in the Lord Jesus: just as you received from us how you must walk and please God — as indeed you are walking — so overflow still more.
For you know what commands we gave you through the Lord Jesus.
For this is the will of God — your sanctification: that you keep away from sexual immorality;
that each of you know how to possess his own vessel in holiness and honor,
not in the passion of craving like the nations that do not know God;
that no one overstep and defraud his brother in this matter — because the Lord is an avenger in all these things, as we told you before and solemnly testified.
For God did not call us to impurity, but in holiness.
Therefore whoever rejects this rejects not man but God — the one who gives his Holy Spirit into you.
Now concerning brotherly love you have no need for anyone to write you, for you yourselves are God-taught to love one another —
and indeed you are doing it toward all the brothers in the whole of Macedonia. But we urge you, brothers, to overflow still more,
and to make it your ambition to live quietly, and to mind your own affairs, and to work with your hands, as we commanded you —
so that you may walk becomingly toward outsiders, and have need of nothing.
But we do not want you to be unknowing, brothers, about those who sleep — so that you do not grieve like the rest, who have no hope.
For if we believe that Jesus died and rose, so also God will bring with him those who have fallen asleep through Jesus.
For this we say to you by a word of the Lord: we the living, who remain until the coming of the Lord, shall by no means go ahead of those who have fallen asleep;
because the Lord himself — with a shout of command, with the voice of an archangel and with the trumpet of God — will come down from heaven, and the dead in Christ will rise first;
then we the living, who remain, will be caught up together with them in clouds to meet the Lord in the air — and so we shall always be with the Lord.
Therefore comfort one another with these words.

1th.5.text: Now concerning the times and the seasons, brothers, you have no need that anything be written to you;
for you yourselves know precisely that the day of the Lord comes like a thief in the night.
When they are saying, "Peace and security," then sudden destruction stands over them — like the birth-pang over a woman with child — and they shall by no means escape.
But you, brothers, are not in darkness, that the day should overtake you like a thief;
for you are all sons of light and sons of day. We do not belong to night or to darkness.
So then, let us not sleep like the rest, but stay awake and stay sober.
For those who sleep, sleep at night; and those who get drunk are drunk at night;
but we who belong to the day — let us be sober, having put on the breastplate of faith and love, and for a helmet the hope of salvation;
because God did not appoint us for wrath, but for the obtaining of salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ,
who died for us so that — whether we wake or sleep — we might live together with him.
Therefore comfort one another and build one another up, each the other — as indeed you are doing.
Now we ask you, brothers, to recognize those who labor among you and lead you in the Lord and admonish you,
and to esteem them beyond all measure, in love, because of their work. Be at peace among yourselves.
And we urge you, brothers: admonish the disorderly, encourage the fainthearted, hold fast the weak, be patient toward all.
See that no one pays back evil for evil to anyone; instead always pursue the good — toward one another, and toward all.
Rejoice always;
pray unceasingly;
in everything give thanks — for this is God's will in Christ Jesus for you.
Do not quench the Spirit;
do not despise prophecies —
but test everything; hold fast the good;
keep away from every form of evil.
And may the God of peace himself sanctify you wholly, complete; and may your spirit and soul and body be kept entire, blameless, at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.
Faithful is the one who calls you — and he will do it.
Brothers, pray for us.
Greet all the brothers with a holy kiss.
I put you on oath by the Lord: have this letter read to all the brothers.
The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.

2th.1.text: Paul and Silvanus and Timothy — to the assembly of the Thessalonians in God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ:
grace to you and peace from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
We ought to give thanks to God always for you, brothers, as is fitting — because your faith is growing beyond measure, and the love of each single one of you all for one another is increasing,
so that we ourselves boast of you in the assemblies of God, for your endurance and faith in all your persecutions and the afflictions you are bearing —
evidence of the just judgment of God, that you may be counted worthy of the kingdom of God, for which you are indeed suffering.
For it is just with God to repay affliction to those who afflict you,
and to you who are afflicted, rest with us — at the unveiling of the Lord Jesus from heaven with the angels of his power,
in a flame of fire, giving vengeance to those who do not know God and to those who do not obey the good news of our Lord Jesus —
who will pay the penalty of everlasting destruction, away from the face of the Lord and from the glory of his strength,
when he comes on that day to be glorified in his holy ones and marveled at among all who have believed — because our testimony to you was believed.
To this end we also pray always for you: that our God may count you worthy of the calling, and fulfill every good pleasure of goodness and every work of faith, in power,
so that the name of our Lord Jesus may be glorified in you, and you in him, according to the grace of our God and of the Lord Jesus Christ.

2th.2.text: Now we ask you, brothers, concerning the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ and our being gathered together to him:
do not be quickly shaken out of your mind or alarmed — whether by a spirit, or by a word, or by a letter purporting to be from us — as though the day of the Lord has already arrived.
Let no one deceive you in any way. For that day will not come unless the great falling-away comes first, and the man of lawlessness is unveiled — the son of destruction,
who opposes and exalts himself over everything called god or object of worship, so that he seats himself in the temple of God, exhibiting himself: that he is god.
Do you not remember that while I was still with you I told you these things?
And now you know what restrains him — that he may be unveiled in his own time.
For the mystery of lawlessness is already at work; only the one who now restrains — until he is out of the way.
And then the lawless one will be unveiled — whom the Lord Jesus will slay with the breath of his mouth and abolish by the appearing of his coming —
whose coming is by the working of Satan, in every power and in signs and lying wonders,
and in every deceit of unrighteousness for those who are perishing — because they did not receive the love of the truth so as to be saved.
And for this reason God sends them a working of delusion, so that they believe the lie —
that all may be judged who did not believe the truth but took pleasure in unrighteousness.
But we ought to give thanks to God always for you, brothers beloved by the Lord — because God chose you as firstfruits for salvation, in the sanctification of the Spirit and in faith in the truth;
to this he called you through our good news, for the obtaining of the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ.
So then, brothers, stand firm — and hold fast the traditions you were taught, whether by word or by our letter.
And may our Lord Jesus Christ himself, and God our Father, who loved us and gave everlasting comfort and good hope in grace,
comfort your hearts and strengthen them in every good work and word.

2th.3.text: Finally, brothers, pray for us — that the word of the Lord may run and be glorified, as it is with you,
and that we may be rescued from perverse and evil men; for faith does not belong to all.
But the Lord is faithful — he will strengthen you and guard you from the evil one.
And we are persuaded in the Lord about you, that the things we command you are both doing and will do.
And may the Lord direct your hearts into the love of God and into the endurance of Christ.
Now we command you, brothers, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ: keep away from every brother walking in disorder and not according to the tradition they received from us.
For you yourselves know how you must imitate us — because we were not disorderly among you,
nor did we eat anyone's bread without paying; rather, in labor and toil, night and day, we kept working, so as not to burden any of you —
not that we lack the right, but so as to give ourselves to you as a pattern to imitate.
For even when we were with you, we gave you this command: if anyone will not work, neither let him eat.
For we hear that some among you are walking in disorder — doing no work, but working everyone over.
To such people we command and appeal in the Lord Jesus Christ: let them work with quietness and eat their own bread.
But you, brothers — do not grow weary of doing good.
And if anyone does not obey our word through this letter, mark that man; do not mix with him, so that he may be turned back ashamed —
yet do not regard him as an enemy, but admonish him as a brother.
And may the Lord of peace himself give you peace, through everything, in every way. The Lord be with you all.
The greeting in my own hand — Paul's — which is the sign in every letter: this is how I write.
The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all.

jas.1.text: James, a slave of God and of the Lord Jesus Christ — to the twelve tribes in the dispersion: joy to you.
Count it all joy, my brothers, whenever you fall into trials of every kind,
knowing that the testing of your faith works endurance —
and let endurance have its complete work, so that you may be complete and entire, lacking in nothing.
But if any of you lacks wisdom, let him ask from God — who gives to all openhandedly and without reproaching — and it will be given him.
But let him ask in faith, doubting nothing; for the doubter is like a wave of the sea, wind-driven and tossed.
Let that man not suppose he will receive anything from the Lord —
a two-souled man, unstable in all his ways.
Let the lowly brother boast in his height,
and the rich man in his lowering — because like a flower of the grass he will pass away.
For the sun rises with its scorching heat and dries the grass, and its flower falls, and the beauty of its face perishes: so too the rich man, in the midst of his goings, will wither.
Happy is the man who endures trial — for having stood the test, he will receive the crown of life, which he has promised to those who love him.
Let no one being tested say, "I am being tempted by God" — for God cannot be tempted by evils, and he himself tempts no one.
But each is tempted by his own craving — dragged off and baited.
Then the craving, having conceived, gives birth to sin; and the sin, fully grown, births death.
Do not be deceived, my beloved brothers:
every good giving and every complete gift is from above, coming down from the Father of lights — with whom there is no variation, no shadow cast by turning.
By his own will he birthed us with the word of truth, that we should be a kind of firstfruits of his creatures.
Know this, my beloved brothers: let every person be quick to hear, slow to speak, slow to anger —
for a man's anger does not work the righteousness of God.
Therefore, putting off all filthiness and overflow of malice, receive with meekness the implanted word, which is able to save your souls.
And become doers of the word — not hearers only, reasoning yourselves astray.
For if anyone is a hearer of the word and not a doer, he is like a man studying the face of his birth in a mirror:
he studied himself, and has gone away — and immediately forgot what kind of man he was.
But the one who stoops to look into the complete law — the law of freedom — and remains beside it, becoming not a forgetful hearer but a doer of work: this one will be happy in his doing.
If anyone thinks he is religious while not bridling his tongue, but deceiving his own heart — this one's religion is empty.
Religion pure and undefiled before God the Father is this: to visit orphans and widows in their affliction, and to keep oneself unstained from the world.

jas.2.text: My brothers — do not hold the faith of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Lord of glory, with favoritism toward faces.
For if a man comes into your assembly gold-ringed, in splendid clothing, and a poor man also comes in, in filthy clothing,
and you look with favor on the one wearing the splendid clothing and say, "You — sit here, in the good place," and to the poor man you say, "You — stand there," or, "Sit under my footstool" —
have you not divided yourselves within, and become judges with evil reasonings?
Listen, my beloved brothers: has not God chosen the poor of the world — rich in faith, and heirs of the kingdom he promised to those who love him?
But you have dishonored the poor man. Is it not the rich who lord it over you — and they who drag you into courts?
Is it not they who blaspheme the beautiful name that was invoked over you?
If indeed you fulfill the royal law according to the scripture — "You shall love your neighbor as yourself" — you do well;
but if you show favoritism toward faces, you work sin — convicted by the law as transgressors.
For whoever keeps the whole law but stumbles at one point has become answerable for all of it;
for the one who said "Do not commit adultery" also said "Do not murder." And if you do not commit adultery but do murder, you have become a transgressor of the law.
So speak and so act as those about to be judged by the law of freedom;
for the judgment is merciless to the one who has shown no mercy. Mercy boasts over judgment.
What is the profit, my brothers, if someone says he has faith but has no works? Can that faith save him?
If a brother or sister is going naked and lacking daily food,
and one of you says to them, "Go in peace — be warmed and be filled," but you do not give them what the body needs — what is the profit?
So also faith, if it has no works, is dead — by itself.
But someone will say: "You have faith, and I have works." Show me your faith without the works — and I will show you, out of my works, the faith.
You believe that God is one? You do well. The demons also believe — and shudder.
But are you willing to learn, O empty man, that faith without works is idle?
Was not Abraham our father counted righteous out of works, when he offered up Isaac his son on the altar?
You see that faith was working together with his works — and out of the works, the faith was completed;
and the scripture was fulfilled that says, "And Abraham believed God, and it was counted to him as righteousness" — and he was called God's friend.
You see that a man is counted righteous out of works, and not out of faith alone.
And likewise also Rahab the prostitute — was she not counted righteous out of works, when she took in the messengers and sent them out by another way?
For just as the body without breath is dead, so also faith without works is dead.

jas.3.text: Not many of you should become teachers, my brothers — knowing that we will receive the heavier judgment;
for we all stumble in many ways. If anyone does not stumble in word, this is a complete man, able to bridle the whole body as well.
Now if we put bridles into the mouths of horses to make them obey us, we steer their whole body too.
Behold also the ships: so great, and driven by hard winds, they are steered by the smallest rudder, wherever the impulse of the helmsman intends.
So also the tongue is a small member — and boasts great things. Behold how small a fire kindles how great a forest!
And the tongue is a fire — the world of unrighteousness. The tongue is set among our members, staining the whole body, setting the wheel of birth ablaze — and set ablaze itself by Gehenna.
For every kind of beast and bird, of creeping thing and sea creature, is tamed and has been tamed by humankind;
but the tongue no one among men can tame — a restless evil, full of death-bearing venom.
With it we bless the Lord and Father, and with it we curse the human beings made in the likeness of God:
out of the same mouth come blessing and curse. My brothers, these things ought not to be so.
Does the spring pour out sweet and bitter from the same opening?
Can a fig tree, my brothers, bear olives, or a vine figs? Neither can salt water yield sweet.
Who is wise and understanding among you? Let him show his works out of a beautiful way of life — in the meekness of wisdom.
But if you hold bitter jealousy and self-seeking ambition in your heart, do not boast over the truth and lie against it.
This is not the wisdom coming down from above; it is earthly, soulish, demonic —
for where jealousy and self-seeking are, there is instability and every worthless practice.
But the wisdom from above is first pure, then peaceable, considerate, open to persuasion, full of mercy and good fruits, undivided, unhypocritical;
and the fruit of righteousness is sown in peace by those who make peace.

jas.4.text: From where do wars — and from where do battles among you — come? Is it not from here: from your pleasures at war in your members?
You crave, and do not have — so you murder. You are jealous, and cannot obtain — so you battle and make war. You do not have because you do not ask;
you ask and do not receive, because you ask badly — to spend it on your pleasures.
Adulteresses! Do you not know that friendship with the world is enmity with God? Whoever, then, intends to be a friend of the world makes himself an enemy of God.
Or do you suppose the scripture speaks emptily? He yearns jealously over the spirit he made to dwell in us —
but he gives greater grace. Therefore it says: "God sets himself against the proud, but gives grace to the lowly."
Submit, then, to God. Stand against the devil — and he will flee from you;
draw near to God — and he will draw near to you. Cleanse your hands, sinners; purify your hearts, you two-souled.
Be wretched, and mourn, and weep; let your laughter be turned to mourning, and your joy to downcast gloom.
Be humbled before the Lord — and he will lift you high.

Do not speak against one another, brothers. Whoever speaks against a brother or judges his brother speaks against the law and judges the law; and if you judge the law, you are not a doer of the law but a judge.
One is the lawgiver and judge — the one able to save and to destroy. But you — who are you, judging the neighbor?
Come now, you who say, "Today or tomorrow we will travel to this city and spend a year there and trade and make profit" —
you who do not know what tomorrow holds, what your life will be. For you are a mist, appearing for a little while and then vanishing.
Instead you should say: "If the Lord wills, we will both live and do this or that."
But as it is, you boast in your pretensions. All such boasting is evil.
So to one who knows the beautiful thing to do and does not do it — to him it is sin.

jas.5.text: Come now, you rich: weep, howling, over the miseries that are coming upon you.
Your wealth has rotted, and your garments have become moth-eaten;
your gold and your silver have corroded, and their corrosion will be a testimony against you, and will eat your flesh like fire. You have stored up treasure — in the last days!
Behold: the wages of the workers who mowed your fields — the pay you held back — cries out; and the shouts of the harvesters have entered the ears of the Lord of Hosts.
You have lived in luxury on the earth and indulged yourselves; you have fattened your hearts in a day of slaughter.
You have condemned — you have murdered — the righteous one. Does he not stand against you?
Be patient, then, brothers, until the coming of the Lord. Behold, the farmer waits for the precious fruit of the earth, patient over it until it receives the early and the late rain.
You also be patient; strengthen your hearts — for the coming of the Lord has drawn near.
Do not groan against one another, brothers, that you may not be judged. Behold — the judge stands before the doors.
Take as a pattern, brothers, of suffering and of patience the prophets who spoke in the name of the Lord.
Behold, we call happy those who endured. You have heard of the endurance of Job, and you have seen the end the Lord brought — that the Lord is deeply compassionate and merciful.
But above all things, my brothers, do not swear — neither by heaven nor by earth nor with any other oath. Let your Yes be yes and your No, no — so that you do not fall under judgment.
Is anyone among you suffering? Let him pray. Is anyone cheerful? Let him sing praise.
Is anyone among you sick? Let him call the elders of the assembly, and let them pray over him, anointing him with oil in the name of the Lord;
and the prayer of faith will save the one who is worn down, and the Lord will raise him up — and if he has committed sins, it will be forgiven him.
Confess your sins, then, to one another, and pray for one another, that you may be healed. The petition of a righteous person, set to work, has great strength.
Elijah was a human being of like feelings with us; and with prayer he prayed for it not to rain — and it did not rain on the earth for three years and six months;
and he prayed again — and the heaven gave rain, and the earth sprouted her fruit.
My brothers, if anyone among you wanders from the truth and someone turns him back,
let him know that the one who turns a sinner back from the wandering of his way will save his soul from death — and will cover a multitude of sins.

eph.1.text: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus through the will of God — to the holy ones who are [in Ephesus], the faithful in Christ Jesus:
grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who has blessed us with every spiritual blessing in the heavenly places in Christ —
just as he chose us in him before the founding of the world, that we should be holy and blameless before his face, in love,
having marked us out beforehand for adoption as sons through Jesus Christ to himself, according to the good pleasure of his will,
to the praise of the glory of his grace, with which he graced us in the Beloved —
in whom we have the ransom-release through his blood, the forgiveness of trespasses, according to the wealth of his grace,
which he lavished on us in all wisdom and understanding,
making known to us the mystery of his will, according to his good pleasure which he set forth in him
for the administration of the fullness of the times: to gather up all things under one head in the Christ — the things upon the heavens and the things upon the earth — in him,
in whom we were also made an inheritance, marked out beforehand according to the purpose of the one who works all things according to the counsel of his will,
that we who first hoped in the Christ should be to the praise of his glory —
in whom you also, having heard the word of truth, the good news of your salvation — in whom, having also believed, you were sealed with the Holy Spirit of the promise,
which is the down-payment of our inheritance, until the ransom-release of the possession — to the praise of his glory.
For this reason I too — having heard of the faith among you in the Lord Jesus and the love toward all the holy ones —
do not cease giving thanks for you, making mention in my prayers,
that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, may give you a spirit of wisdom and unveiling in the full knowledge of him —
the eyes of your heart enlightened, so that you may know what is the hope of his calling, what the wealth of the glory of his inheritance in the holy ones,
and what the surpassing greatness of his power toward us who believe, according to the working of the might of his strength,
which he worked in the Christ when he raised him from the dead — and seated him at his right hand in the heavenly places,
high above every ruler and authority and power and dominion, and every name that is named, not only in this age but also in the one to come;
and he set all things in order under his feet, and gave him as head over all things to the assembly,
which is his body — the fullness of the one who fills all things in all.

eph.2.text: And you — being dead in your trespasses and sins,
in which you once walked according to the age of this world, according to the ruler of the authority of the air, the spirit now working in the sons of disobedience —
among whom we all also once conducted ourselves, in the cravings of our flesh, doing the wishes of the flesh and of its thoughts; and we were by nature children of wrath, like the rest.
But God — being rich in mercy, because of his great love with which he loved us —
even when we were dead in trespasses, made us alive together with the Christ (by grace you have been saved!),
and raised us together and seated us together in the heavenly places in Christ Jesus,
so that in the coming ages he might display the surpassing wealth of his grace, in kindness toward us in Christ Jesus.
For by grace you have been saved, through faith — and this is not from yourselves; it is God's gift —
not from works, so that no one may boast.
For we are his workmanship, created in Christ Jesus for good works, which God prepared beforehand that we should walk in them.
Therefore remember that once you, the nations in flesh — called "foreskin" by the so-called "circumcision," made in flesh by hands —
remember that at that time you were without Christ, estranged from the commonwealth of Israel and strangers to the covenants of the promise, having no hope, and godless in the world.
But now, in Christ Jesus, you who once were far off have come to be near — in the blood of the Christ.
For he himself is our peace: the one who made the both one, and broke down the dividing wall of the fence — the enmity — in his flesh,
abolishing the law of commandments in decrees, so that in himself he might create the two into one new humanity, making peace,
and reconcile them both, in one body, to God through the cross — killing the enmity in it.
And coming, he brought the good news of peace to you who were far off, and peace to those near;
because through him we both have the way of approach, in one Spirit, to the Father.
So then you are no longer strangers and sojourners; you are fellow citizens of the holy ones and members of God's household,
built up on the foundation of the apostles and prophets — Christ Jesus himself being the cornerstone,
in whom the whole building, being fitted together, grows into a holy temple in the Lord;
in whom you also are being built together into a dwelling place of God in the Spirit.

eph.3.text: For this cause I, Paul, the prisoner of Christ Jesus for you, the nations —
if indeed you heard of the administration of the grace of God given to me for you:
that by unveiling the mystery was made known to me, as I wrote before in brief;
by reading it you can perceive my insight into the mystery of the Christ,
which in other generations was not made known to the sons of men as it has now been unveiled to his holy apostles and prophets in the Spirit:
that the nations are co-heirs, and co-body, and co-sharers of the promise in Christ Jesus through the good news —
of which I became a servant, according to the gift of God's grace given me, according to the working of his power.
To me — the very least of all the holy ones — this grace was given: to bring to the nations the good news of the untraceable wealth of the Christ,
and to bring to light for all what is the administration of the mystery hidden from the ages in God, who created all things —
so that now, through the assembly, the many-colored wisdom of God might be made known to the rulers and the authorities in the heavenly places,
according to the purpose of the ages, which he accomplished in the Christ, Jesus our Lord,
in whom we have the boldness and the way of approach, in confidence, through his faithfulness.
Therefore I ask you not to lose heart over my afflictions on your behalf — they are your glory.
For this cause I bend my knees before the Father,
from whom every family in the heavens and on earth is named,
that he may grant you — according to the wealth of his glory — to be strengthened with power through his Spirit into the inner human being;
that the Christ may dwell in your hearts through faith, in love — you having been rooted and founded —
so that you may have full strength to grasp, with all the holy ones, what is the breadth and length and height and depth,
and to know the love of the Christ that surpasses knowledge — that you may be filled unto all the fullness of God.
Now to the one able to do beyond all things — exceedingly beyond all that we ask or conceive — according to the power at work in us:
to him be the glory in the assembly and in Christ Jesus, to all the generations of the age of the ages. Amen.

eph.4.text: I urge you, then — I, the prisoner in the Lord — to walk worthily of the calling with which you were called:
with all lowliness of mind and meekness, with patience, bearing with one another in love,
eager to keep the oneness of the Spirit in the binding-band of peace.
One body and one Spirit — just as you were also called in one hope of your calling;
one Lord, one faith, one baptism;
one God and Father of all — who is over all and through all and in all.
But to each single one of us the grace was given according to the measure of the gift of the Christ.
Therefore it says: "Having gone up on high, he led captivity captive; he gave gifts to men."
Now this "he went up" — what is it, except that he also went down into the lower regions of the earth?
The one who went down is himself also the one who went up, high above all the heavens, that he might fill all things.
And he himself gave some as apostles, some as prophets, some as bringers of the good news, some as shepherds and teachers,
for the equipping of the holy ones for the work of service, for the building up of the body of the Christ —
until we all arrive at the oneness of the faith and of the full knowledge of the Son of God, at a complete man, at the measure of the stature of the fullness of the Christ;
so that we may no longer be infants — wave-tossed and carried around by every wind of teaching, in the dice-play of men, in craftiness toward the scheming of error —
but, speaking truth in love, may grow up in every way into him who is the head: Christ,

from whom the whole body — fitted together and knit together through every supplying joint, according to the working in measure of each single part — makes the body's growth, for the building up of itself in love.
This, then, I say and testify in the Lord: walk no longer as the nations walk, in the emptiness of their mind —
darkened in their thinking, estranged from the life of God because of the ignorance that is in them, because of the hardening of their heart;
who, having gone past feeling, gave themselves over to shamelessness, for the working of every impurity — with greed.
But you did not learn the Christ this way —
if indeed you heard him and were taught in him, just as truth is in Jesus:
to put off — as regards your former way of life — the old humanity, rotting away in the cravings of deceit;
and to be made new in the spirit of your mind;
and to put on the new humanity, created according to God in righteousness and holiness of the truth.
Therefore, having put off the lie, speak truth each one with his neighbor — because we are members of one another.
Be angry, and do not sin: let the sun not set on your provocation,
and do not give the devil a place.
Let the thief steal no longer; rather let him labor, working the good with his own hands, so that he may have something to share with the one who has need.
Let no rotten word go out of your mouth — but whatever is good for building up where it is needed, that it may give grace to those who hear.
And do not grieve the Holy Spirit of God, in whom you were sealed for the day of ransom-release.
Let all bitterness and rage and anger and shouting and slander be carried away from you, with all malice;
and become kind to one another, tender-hearted, freely forgiving each other — just as God in Christ freely forgave you.

eph.5.text: Become, then, imitators of God, as beloved children,
and walk in love — just as the Christ also loved us and handed himself over for us, an offering and sacrifice to God, for a fragrance of sweet smell.
But sexual immorality and impurity of every kind, or greed — let them not even be named among you, as is fitting for holy ones;
and no ugliness, no foolish talk or slick wit — things that have no place — but rather thanksgiving.
For this you know with certainty: no one immoral or impure or greedy — that is, an idolater — has an inheritance in the kingdom of the Christ and of God.
Let no one deceive you with empty words; for because of these things the wrath of God is coming on the sons of disobedience.
Do not, then, become co-sharers with them;
for you were once darkness, but now — light in the Lord. Walk as children of light
(for the fruit of the light is in all goodness and righteousness and truth),
testing what is well-pleasing to the Lord;
and do not share in the fruitless works of the darkness — rather, expose them;
for the things done by them in secret are shameful even to speak of.
But all things exposed by the light are made visible —
for everything made visible is light. Therefore it says: "Awake, O sleeper, and rise from the dead — and the Christ will dawn upon you."
Watch carefully, then, how you walk — not as unwise but as wise,
buying back the season, because the days are evil.
For this reason do not become senseless, but understand what the will of the Lord is.
And do not get drunk with wine — in which is dissipation — but be filled in the Spirit,
speaking to one another in psalms and hymns and spiritual songs, singing and making music in your heart to the Lord,
giving thanks always, for all things, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, to the God and Father,
submitting yourselves to one another in the fear of Christ:
the wives, to their own husbands, as to the Lord —
because a husband is head of the wife as the Christ also is head of the assembly, he himself savior of the body.
But as the assembly submits itself to the Christ, so also the wives to their husbands in everything.
Husbands, love your wives — just as the Christ also loved the assembly and handed himself over for her,
that he might sanctify her, having cleansed her by the washing of the water, in a word;
that he might present the assembly to himself in glory — having no stain or wrinkle or any such thing, but that she should be holy and blameless.
So too the husbands ought to love their own wives as their own bodies. He who loves his own wife loves himself;
for no one ever hated his own flesh — rather he nourishes and cherishes it, just as the Christ does the assembly,
because we are members of his body.
"For this cause a man shall leave father and mother and be joined to his wife, and the two shall become one flesh."
This mystery is great — but I am speaking of Christ and of the assembly.
In any case, you also, one by one: let each so love his own wife as himself — and the wife, let her stand in awe of her husband.

eph.6.text: Children, obey your parents in the Lord — for this is just.
"Honor your father and mother" — which is the first commandment with a promise —
"that it may go well with you, and you may be long-lived on the earth."
And fathers, do not provoke your children to anger; rather, nourish them in the discipline and instruction of the Lord.
Slaves, obey your masters according to the flesh with fear and trembling, in the singleness of your heart, as to the Christ —
not with eye-service, as pleasers of men, but as slaves of Christ, doing the will of God from the soul,
serving with goodwill, as to the Lord and not to men —
knowing that whatever good each one does, this he will receive back from the Lord, whether slave or free.
And masters, do the same toward them — letting go of the threat — knowing that both their Lord and yours is in the heavens, and there is no favoritism toward faces with him.
For the rest: be empowered in the Lord and in the might of his strength.
Put on the whole armor of God, so that you can stand against the schemings of the devil;

because our wrestling is not against blood and flesh, but against the rulers, against the authorities, against the world-powers of this darkness, against the spiritual forces of evil in the heavenly places.
For this reason take up the whole armor of God, so that you may be able to withstand in the evil day — and, having worked all things through, to stand.
Stand, then: your waist girded with truth, and wearing the breastplate of righteousness,
and your feet shod with the readiness of the good news of peace;
in everything taking up the great shield of faith, with which you will be able to quench all the fire-tipped arrows of the evil one;
and receive the helmet of salvation, and the sword of the Spirit — which is the spoken word of God —
through all prayer and petition, praying at every season in the Spirit, and staying awake for this very thing with all perseverance and petition for all the holy ones —
and for me: that a word may be given me at the opening of my mouth, to make known with boldness the mystery of the good news,
for which I am an ambassador in a chain — that in it I may speak boldly, as I must speak.
But so that you also may know my affairs — how I am doing — Tychicus, the beloved brother and faithful servant in the Lord, will make everything known to you;
