Give back to all what is owed: to whom the tax — the tax; to whom the toll — the toll; to whom the fear — the fear; to whom the honor — the honor.
Owe no one anything — except to love one another; for the one who loves the other has fulfilled the law.

For the "Do not commit adultery, Do not murder, Do not steal, Do not crave" — and if there is any other commandment — is summed up under one head, in this word: "You shall love your neighbor as yourself."
Love works no evil to the neighbor; love, therefore, is the fullness of the law.
And this — knowing the season: that it is already the hour for you to be woken out of sleep; for now our salvation is nearer than when we first believed.
The night is far along; the day has drawn near. Let us throw off, then, the works of the darkness — and let us put on the weapons of the light.
As in daylight, let us walk becomingly: not in carousings and drunkennesses, not in beddings and shameless acts, not in strife and jealousy —
rather, put on the Lord Jesus Christ; and make no forethought for the flesh, to feed its cravings.

rom.14.text: And the one who is weak in the faith — receive him, but not for passing judgment on his inner reasonings.
One believes he may eat everything; but the weak one eats garden plants.
Let the one who eats not despise the one who does not eat; and let the one who does not eat not judge the one who eats — for God has received him.
Who are you, judging another's household servant? To his own lord he stands or falls — and he shall be made to stand, for the Lord is able to make him stand.
One judges one day above another day; another judges every day alike. Let each be fully convinced in his own mind.

The one who minds the day, minds it to the Lord. And the one who eats, eats to the Lord — for he gives thanks to God; and the one who does not eat, to the Lord he does not eat — and he gives thanks to God.
For none of us lives to himself, and none dies to himself:
for if we live, we live to the Lord; and if we die, we die to the Lord. So whether we live or whether we die — we are the Lord's.
For to this end Christ died and lived: that he might be Lord of both the dead and the living.
But you — why do you judge your brother? Or you — why do you despise your brother? For we shall all stand before the judgment seat of God;
for it is written: "As I live, says the Lord: to me every knee shall bend, and every tongue shall confess to God."
So then, each of us will give an account concerning himself.
Let us, then, no longer judge one another; rather, judge this instead: not to set a stumbling-stone or a snare before the brother.
I know, and am persuaded in the Lord Jesus, that nothing is common in itself — except that to the one who counts something common, to that one it is common.
For if your brother is grieved because of food, you are no longer walking according to love. Do not, by your food, destroy that one for whom Christ died.
Let not your good, then, be spoken of as evil;
for the kingdom of God is not eating and drinking — it is righteousness and peace and joy in the Holy Spirit;
for the one who serves the Christ in this is well-pleasing to God and approved by men.
So then let us pursue the things of peace, and the things of building up one another.
Do not, for the sake of food, tear down the work of God. All things indeed are clean — but it is evil for the person who eats through stumbling.
It is beautiful not to eat meat, nor drink wine, nor do anything at which your brother stumbles or is snared or is weak.
The faith you have — keep it to yourself, before God. Happy is the one who does not judge himself in what he approves;
but the one who wavers is condemned if he eats, because it is not out of faith. And everything that is not out of faith is sin.

rom.15.text: Now we, the strong, ought to carry the weaknesses of those without strength — and not to please ourselves.
Let each of us please the neighbor, for the good, toward building up;
for the Christ also did not please himself — rather, as it is written: "The taunts of those who taunt you fell upon me."
For whatever was written before was written for our teaching — so that through the endurance and through the encouragement of the scriptures we might hold the hope.
And may the God of endurance and encouragement give you to set your minds the same among one another, according to Christ Jesus,
so that with one passion, with one mouth, you may glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.
Therefore receive one another — just as the Christ also received you, to the glory of God.
For I say that Christ has become a servant of the circumcision on behalf of the truth of God, to confirm the promises to the fathers,
and that the nations should glorify God on behalf of his mercy — just as it is written: "For this cause I will confess you among the nations, and to your name I will make music."
And again it says: "Rejoice, O nations, with his people."
And again: "Praise the Lord, all you nations; and let all the peoples praise him."
And again Isaiah says: "There shall be the root of Jesse — the one who rises to rule the nations; upon him nations shall hope."
And may the God of the hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing, so that you overflow in the hope, in the power of the Holy Spirit.
And I myself am persuaded about you, my brothers, that you yourselves are full of goodness, filled with all knowledge, able also to admonish one another.
But I have written to you rather boldly, in part, as one reminding you again — because of the grace given me by God:
that I should be a public minister of Christ Jesus to the nations, serving the good news of God as a priest, so that the offering of the nations may be welcome — made holy in the Holy Spirit.
I have, then, this boast in Christ Jesus, in the things toward God;
for I will not dare to speak of anything except what Christ has worked out through me, for the obedience of the nations — in word and work,
in the power of signs and wonders, in the power of the Spirit — so that from Jerusalem, and in a circuit round to Illyricum, I have brought the good news of the Christ to its fullness;
and so making it my ambition to bring the good news not where Christ has been named — lest I build on another's foundation —
but just as it is written: "Those to whom nothing was announced about him shall see; and those who have not heard shall understand."
For this reason also I was hindered these many times from coming to you;
but now, having no more place in these regions, and having a longing to come to you for many years now,
whenever I travel to Spain — for I hope, passing through, to see you, and to be sent onward there by you, once I have first been filled, in part, with your company.
But now I am traveling to Jerusalem, serving the holy ones;
for Macedonia and Achaia were pleased to make a certain sharing for the poor among the holy ones in Jerusalem.
They were pleased — and they are their debtors; for if the nations shared in their spiritual things, they owe it also to minister to them in the fleshly things.
Having finished this, then, and sealed this fruit to them, I will go away through you to Spain;
and I know that in coming to you, I shall come in the fullness of the blessing of Christ.
And I urge you, brothers — through our Lord Jesus Christ and through the love of the Spirit — to strive together with me in your prayers to God on my behalf:
that I may be rescued from the disobedient in Judea, and that my service for Jerusalem may be welcome to the holy ones;
so that, coming to you in joy through the will of God, I may be refreshed together with you.
And the God of peace be with you all. Amen.

rom.16.text: And I commend to you Phoebe our sister — who is also a servant of the assembly in Cenchreae —
that you may receive her in the Lord, worthily of the holy ones, and stand beside her in whatever matter she may need you; for she herself has been a protector of many — and of me myself.
Greet Prisca and Aquila, my co-workers in Christ Jesus,
who laid down their own necks for my soul — to whom not I alone give thanks, but also all the assemblies of the nations;
and greet the assembly at their house. Greet Epaenetus my beloved, who is the firstfruits of Asia for Christ.
Greet Mary, who labored much for you.
Greet Andronicus and Junia, my kin and my fellow prisoners — who are notable among the apostles, and who were in Christ before me.
Greet Ampliatus, my beloved in the Lord.
Greet Urbanus, our co-worker in Christ, and Stachys my beloved.
Greet Apelles, the tested-and-approved in Christ. Greet those of the household of Aristobulus.
Greet Herodion, my kinsman. Greet those of the household of Narcissus who are in the Lord.
Greet Tryphaena and Tryphosa, who labor in the Lord. Greet Persis the beloved, who labored much in the Lord.
Greet Rufus, the chosen in the Lord — and his mother, and mine.
Greet Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas — and the brothers with them.
Greet Philologus and Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas — and all the holy ones with them.
Greet one another with a holy kiss. All the assemblies of the Christ greet you.
And I urge you, brothers: keep your eye on those who make the divisions and the snares, against the teaching you learned — and turn away from them;
for such people do not serve our Lord Christ, but their own belly; and through smooth talk and fine words they deceive the hearts of the guileless.
For your obedience has reached everyone — so over you I rejoice; but I want you to be wise toward the good, and unmixed toward the evil.
And the God of peace will crush Satan under your feet — swiftly. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
Timothy my co-worker greets you; and Lucius and Jason and Sosipater, my kin.
I, Tertius — who wrote this letter down — greet you in the Lord.
Gaius — my host, and host of the whole assembly — greets you. Erastus the city treasurer greets you, and Quartus the brother.
The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.

rev.1.text: The Unveiling of Jesus Christ, which God gave him — to show his slaves the things that must come to pass swiftly; and he signified it, sending it through his angel to his slave John,
who bore witness to the word of God and the testimony of Jesus Christ — everything he saw.
Happy is the one who reads aloud, and those who hear the words of the prophecy and keep the things written in it — for the season is near.
John, to the seven assemblies in Asia: grace to you and peace from HE-WHO-IS and WHO-WAS and WHO-IS-COMING, and from the seven spirits before his throne,
and from Jesus Christ — the faithful witness, the firstborn of the dead, the ruler of the kings of the earth. To the one who loves us and has loosed us from our sins in his blood —
and made us a kingdom, priests to his God and Father — to him the glory and the might to the ages of the ages. Amen.
Behold, he is coming with the clouds — and every eye will see him, even those who pierced him through; and all the tribes of the earth will beat their breasts over him. Yes — Amen.
"I am the Alpha and the Omega," says the Lord God — HE-WHO-IS and WHO-WAS and WHO-IS-COMING, the All-Ruler.
I, John — your brother and co-sharer in the affliction and kingdom and endurance in Jesus — came to be on the island called Patmos, because of the word of God and the testimony of Jesus.
I came to be in the Spirit on the Lord's day, and I heard behind me a great voice, like a trumpet,
saying: "What you see, write into a scroll and send to the seven assemblies: to Ephesus, and to Smyrna, and to Pergamum, and to Thyatira, and to Sardis, and to Philadelphia, and to Laodicea."
And I turned to see the voice that was speaking with me; and turning, I saw seven golden lampstands,
and in the midst of the lampstands one like a son of man — clothed down to the feet, and girded across the chest with a golden band.
His head and his hair were white like white wool, like snow; and his eyes like a flame of fire;
and his feet like fine glowing bronze, as if fired in a furnace; and his voice like the voice of many waters;
and he held in his right hand seven stars, and out of his mouth a sharp two-mouthed sword going forth; and his face — like the sun shining in its power.
And when I saw him, I fell at his feet like a dead man. And he laid his right hand on me, saying: "Do not fear. I am the First and the Last,
and the Living One. And I became dead — and behold, I am living to the ages of the ages; and I hold the keys of Death and of Hades.
Write, then, the things you saw, and the things that are, and the things about to come to pass after these;
the mystery of the seven stars you saw on my right hand, and the seven golden lampstands: the seven stars are angels of the seven assemblies, and the seven lampstands are seven assemblies."

rev.2.text: "To the angel of the assembly in Ephesus write: These things says the one who holds the seven stars in his right hand, who walks in the midst of the seven golden lampstands:
I know your works — your labor and your endurance; and that you cannot bear the evil; and you tested those who call themselves apostles and are not, and found them liars;
and you have endurance, and have borne up for the sake of my name, and have not grown weary.
But I have this against you: you have left your first love.
Remember, then, from where you have fallen — and turn your mind around, and do the first works. But if not, I am coming to you, and I will move your lampstand out of its place — unless you turn.
Yet you have this: you hate the works of the Nicolaitans — which I also hate.
The one who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies. To the one conquering I will give to eat from the tree of life, which is in the paradise of God.
And to the angel of the assembly in Smyrna write: These things says the First and the Last, who became dead and lived:
I know your affliction and your poverty — but you are rich — and the slander from those who call themselves Jews and are not, but are a synagogue of Satan.

Fear nothing you are about to suffer. Behold, the devil is about to throw some of you into prison, that you may be tested — and you will have affliction ten days. Be faithful up to death, and I will give you the crown of life.
The one who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies. The one conquering shall by no means be harmed by the second death.
And to the angel of the assembly in Pergamum write: These things says the one who holds the sharp two-mouthed sword:

I know where you dwell — where the throne of Satan is; and you hold my name fast, and you did not deny my faith — even in the days of Antipas, my witness, my faithful one, who was killed among you, where Satan dwells.

But I have a few things against you: you have there some who hold the teaching of Balaam, who taught Balak to throw a snare before the sons of Israel — to eat idol-sacrifices and to commit immorality.
So you also have some who hold the teaching of the Nicolaitans, likewise.
Turn, then! But if not, I am coming to you swiftly — and I will make war with them, with the sword of my mouth.

The one who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies. To the one conquering I will give some of the hidden manna; and I will give him a white stone — and on the stone a new name written, which no one knows except the one who receives it.
And to the angel of the assembly in Thyatira write: These things says the Son of God — whose eyes are like a flame of fire, and whose feet are like fine glowing bronze:
I know your works — your love and faith and service and endurance; and your last works are more than the first.
But I have this against you: you tolerate the woman Jezebel — who calls herself a prophetess, and teaches and leads my slaves astray to commit immorality and to eat idol-sacrifices.
And I gave her time to turn — and she does not want to turn from her immorality.
Behold, I am throwing her onto a sickbed — and those who commit adultery with her into great affliction, unless they turn from her works;
and her children I will kill with death. And all the assemblies shall know that I am the one who searches kidneys and hearts — and I will give to each of you according to your works.
But to you I say — the rest in Thyatira, as many as do not hold this teaching, who have not known 'the deep things of Satan,' as they say — I am throwing on you no other weight;
only — what you have, hold fast until I come.
And the one conquering, and keeping my works up to the end — I will give him authority over the nations,
and he shall shepherd them with an iron rod, as the potter's vessels are shattered —
as I also have received from my Father. And I will give him the morning star.
The one who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies.

rev.3.text: And to the angel of the assembly in Sardis write: These things says the one who has the seven spirits of God and the seven stars: I know your works — that you have a name that you live. And you are dead.
Become watchful, and strengthen the things that remain, which were about to die — for I have not found your works filled full before my God.
Remember, then, how you received and heard; keep it, and turn. If then you do not watch, I will come like a thief — and you shall by no means know at what hour I will come upon you.
But you have a few names in Sardis that have not soiled their garments; and they shall walk with me in white — for they are worthy.
The one conquering shall be clothed thus, in white garments; and I shall by no means wipe his name out of the book of life; and I will confess his name before my Father and before his angels.
The one who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies.
And to the angel of the assembly in Philadelphia write: These things says the Holy One, the True One — who holds the key of David; who opens, and no one will shut; and who shuts, and no one opens:
I know your works. Behold — I have set before you an opened door, which no one can shut; because you have little power, and you kept my word, and did not deny my name.

Behold, I am giving some from the synagogue of Satan — those who call themselves Jews and are not, but lie — behold, I will make them come and bow down before your feet, and know that I have loved you.
Because you kept the word of my endurance, I also will keep you out of the hour of the testing that is about to come upon the whole inhabited world — to test those who dwell on the earth.
I am coming swiftly. Hold fast what you have — that no one take your crown.

The one conquering — I will make him a pillar in the temple of my God, and he shall never go outside again; and I will write on him the name of my God, and the name of the city of my God — the new Jerusalem, which comes down out of heaven from my God — and my new name.
The one who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies.
And to the angel of the assembly in Laodicea write: These things says the Amen, the faithful and true witness, the beginning of God's creation:
I know your works — that you are neither cold nor hot. Would that you were cold or hot!
So — because you are lukewarm, and neither hot nor cold, I am about to vomit you out of my mouth.
Because you say, 'I am rich; I have grown wealthy and have need of nothing' — and you do not know that you are the miserable one, and pitiable, and poor, and blind, and naked —

I counsel you to buy from me gold fired out of fire, so that you may be rich; and white garments, so that you may be clothed and the shame of your nakedness not be made visible; and eye-salve to anoint your eyes, so that you may see.
As many as I hold dear, I expose and I discipline. Be zealous, then — and turn.
Behold — I stand at the door and knock. If anyone hears my voice and opens the door, I will come in to him and dine with him, and he with me.
The one conquering — I will give him to sit with me on my throne, as I also conquered and sat with my Father on his throne.
The one who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies."

rev.4.text: After these things I saw — and behold: a door opened in heaven; and the first voice, which I had heard like a trumpet speaking with me, said: "Come up here, and I will show you the things that must come to pass after these."
Immediately I came to be in the Spirit — and behold: a throne stood in heaven, and on the throne, one sitting;
and the one sitting was like a jasper stone and a carnelian in appearance; and a rainbow encircled the throne, like an emerald in appearance.
And encircling the throne, twenty-four thrones; and on the thrones, twenty-four elders sitting, clothed in white garments, and on their heads golden crowns.
And out of the throne go forth lightnings and voices and thunders; and seven torches of fire burning before the throne — which are the seven spirits of God;
and before the throne, something like a sea of glass, like crystal. And in the midst of the throne, and in a circle around the throne, four living beings — filled with eyes in front and behind:
the first living being like a lion, and the second living being like a calf, and the third living being with the face as of a human, and the fourth living being like a flying eagle;

and the four living beings, each one of them with six wings apiece, are filled with eyes around and within. And they have no rest, day and night, saying: "Holy, holy, holy — the Lord God, the All-Ruler: WHO-WAS and WHO-IS and WHO-IS-COMING."
And whenever the living beings give glory and honor and thanksgiving to the one sitting on the throne — to the one living to the ages of the ages —
the twenty-four elders will fall down before the one sitting on the throne, and bow down to the one living to the ages of the ages — and they will throw their crowns before the throne, saying:
"Worthy are you, our Lord and God, to receive the glory and the honor and the power — because you created all things, and through your will they existed and were created."

rev.5.text: And I saw, on the right hand of the one sitting on the throne, a scroll written inside and on the back, sealed down with seven seals;
and I saw a strong angel proclaiming with a great voice: "Who is worthy to open the scroll and to loose its seals?"
And no one — in heaven, or on the earth, or under the earth — was able to open the scroll or to look at it.
And I wept much, because no one was found worthy to open the scroll or to look at it.
And one of the elders says to me: "Do not weep. Behold — the Lion from the tribe of Judah, the Root of David, has conquered: he can open the scroll and its seven seals."

And I saw, in the midst of the throne and the four living beings, and in the midst of the elders, a Lamb standing — as slaughtered — with seven horns and seven eyes, which are the seven spirits of God sent out into all the earth.
And he came, and he has taken it from the right hand of the one sitting on the throne.

And when he took the scroll, the four living beings and the twenty-four elders fell down before the Lamb — each holding a harp, and golden bowls full of incense, which are the prayers of the holy ones.

And they sing a new song, saying: "Worthy are you to take the scroll and to open its seals — because you were slaughtered, and with your blood you bought for God people out of every tribe and tongue and people and nation,
and made them a kingdom and priests to our God — and they reign upon the earth."
And I saw, and I heard the voice of many angels in a circle around the throne and the living beings and the elders — and their number was myriads of myriads and thousands of thousands —
saying with a great voice: "Worthy is the Lamb, the slaughtered one, to receive the power and wealth and wisdom and strength and honor and glory and blessing!"

And every created thing in heaven and on the earth and under the earth and on the sea — and all the things in them — I heard saying: "To the one sitting on the throne, and to the Lamb: the blessing and the honor and the glory and the might, to the ages of the ages!"
And the four living beings said: "Amen!" And the elders fell down and worshiped.

rev.6.text: And I saw when the Lamb opened one of the seven seals, and I heard one of the four living beings saying, like a voice of thunder: "Come!"
And I saw — and behold: a white horse; and the one sitting on it held a bow, and a crown was given him — and he went out conquering, and to conquer.
And when he opened the second seal, I heard the second living being saying: "Come!"
And another horse went out — fire-red; and to the one sitting on it, it was given to take the peace out of the earth, so that men should slaughter one another — and a great sword was given him.
And when he opened the third seal, I heard the third living being saying: "Come!" And I saw — and behold: a black horse; and the one sitting on it held a balance-scale in his hand.

And I heard something like a voice in the midst of the four living beings, saying: "A quart of wheat for a denarius — and three quarts of barley for a denarius; and the oil and the wine, do not harm."
And when he opened the fourth seal, I heard the voice of the fourth living being saying: "Come!"

And I saw — and behold: a pale-green horse; and the one sitting upon it — his name was Death, and Hades followed with him; and authority was given them over the fourth of the earth: to kill with sword and with famine and with death, and by the beasts of the earth.
And when he opened the fifth seal, I saw under the altar the souls of those slaughtered for the word of God, and for the testimony they held;
and they cried with a great voice, saying: "Until when, O Master — the holy and true — do you not judge and avenge our blood on those who dwell upon the earth?"

And to each of them was given a white robe; and it was said to them that they should rest yet a little time, until the number should be filled of their fellow slaves and their brothers who were about to be killed as they had been.
And I saw when he opened the sixth seal: a great earthquake came; and the sun became black as sackcloth of hair, and the whole moon became like blood,
and the stars of heaven fell to the earth — as a fig tree throws down her unripe figs when shaken by a great wind;
and the heaven was split away like a scroll rolling itself up, and every mountain and island was moved out of its place.
And the kings of the earth and the great men and the commanders and the rich and the strong, and every slave and free man, hid themselves in the caves and in the rocks of the mountains;
and they say to the mountains and to the rocks: "Fall on us — and hide us from the face of the one sitting on the throne, and from the wrath of the Lamb!
For the great day of their wrath has come — and who can stand?"

rev.7.text: After this I saw four angels standing at the four corners of the earth, holding fast the four winds of the earth, so that no wind should blow on the earth or on the sea or on any tree.

And I saw another angel coming up from the rising of the sun, holding the seal of the living God; and he cried with a great voice to the four angels to whom it had been given to harm the earth and the sea,
saying: "Do not harm the earth or the sea or the trees, until we seal the slaves of our God on their foreheads."
And I heard the number of the sealed: one hundred forty-four thousand, sealed out of every tribe of the sons of Israel:
of the tribe of Judah, twelve thousand sealed; of the tribe of Reuben, twelve thousand; of the tribe of Gad, twelve thousand;
of the tribe of Asher, twelve thousand; of the tribe of Naphtali, twelve thousand; of the tribe of Manasseh, twelve thousand;
of the tribe of Simeon, twelve thousand; of the tribe of Levi, twelve thousand; of the tribe of Issachar, twelve thousand;
of the tribe of Zebulun, twelve thousand; of the tribe of Joseph, twelve thousand; of the tribe of Benjamin, twelve thousand sealed.

After these things I saw — and behold: a great crowd that no one could number, out of every nation and tribes and peoples and tongues, standing before the throne and before the Lamb, clothed in white robes, with palm branches in their hands;
and they cry with a great voice, saying: "Salvation belongs to our God, the one sitting on the throne — and to the Lamb!"
And all the angels stood in a circle around the throne and the elders and the four living beings; and they fell before the throne on their faces and worshiped God,
saying: "Amen! The blessing and the glory and the wisdom and the thanksgiving and the honor and the power and the strength to our God, to the ages of the ages. Amen!"
And one of the elders answered, saying to me: "These, clothed in the white robes — who are they, and from where did they come?"
And I have said to him: "My lord — you know." And he said to me: "These are the ones coming out of the great affliction; and they washed their robes and whitened them in the blood of the Lamb.
For this reason they are before the throne of God, and they serve him day and night in his temple — and the one sitting on the throne will spread his tent over them.
They shall hunger no more, nor thirst any more; the sun shall by no means fall on them, nor any scorching heat —
because the Lamb at the center of the throne will shepherd them, and will guide them to springs of the waters of life; and God will wipe away every tear from their eyes."

rev.8.text: And when he opened the seventh seal, there came a silence in heaven — about half an hour.
And I saw the seven angels who stand before God; and seven trumpets were given them.
And another angel came and stood at the altar, holding a golden censer; and much incense was given him, to offer with the prayers of all the holy ones on the golden altar before the throne.
And the smoke of the incense, with the prayers of the holy ones, went up from the hand of the angel before God.
And the angel has taken the censer, and filled it from the fire of the altar, and threw it to the earth — and there came thunders and voices and lightnings and an earthquake.
And the seven angels holding the seven trumpets made themselves ready to trumpet.

And the first trumpeted: and there came hail and fire, mixed in blood, and it was thrown to the earth; and the third of the earth was burned down, and the third of the trees was burned down, and all green grass was burned down.
And the second angel trumpeted: and something like a great mountain, burning with fire, was thrown into the sea; and the third of the sea became blood,
and the third of the creatures in the sea — those having souls — died; and the third of the ships were destroyed.
And the third angel trumpeted: and a great star fell out of heaven, burning like a torch; and it fell on the third of the rivers and on the springs of the waters.
And the name of the star is called Wormwood. And the third of the waters became wormwood, and many of the people died from the waters — because they were made bitter.

And the fourth angel trumpeted: and the third of the sun was struck, and the third of the moon, and the third of the stars — so that the third of them was darkened, and the day did not shine for a third of it, and the night likewise.

And I saw, and I heard one eagle flying in mid-heaven, saying with a great voice: "Woe, woe, woe to those who dwell on the earth — because of the remaining voices of the trumpet of the three angels about to trumpet!"

rev.9.text: And the fifth angel trumpeted: and I saw a star fallen out of heaven to the earth; and the key of the shaft of the abyss was given to him,
and he opened the shaft of the abyss — and smoke came up out of the shaft like the smoke of a great furnace; and the sun and the air were darkened from the smoke of the shaft.
And out of the smoke, locusts went out onto the earth; and authority was given them, like the authority the scorpions of the earth have;
and it was said to them not to harm the grass of the earth, nor any green thing, nor any tree — only the human beings who do not have the seal of God on their foreheads.
And it was given them not to kill them, but that they should be tormented five months; and their torment is like the torment of a scorpion when it strikes a man.
And in those days people will seek death — and shall by no means find it; and they will long to die — and death flees from them.
And the likenesses of the locusts were like horses made ready for war; and on their heads, something like crowns similar to gold; and their faces like human faces;
and they had hair like women's hair; and their teeth were like lions';
and they had breastplates like breastplates of iron; and the sound of their wings was like the sound of chariots of many horses running into war;
and they have tails like scorpions, and stings — and in their tails is their authority to harm the human beings five months.
They have over them a king: the angel of the abyss. His name in Hebrew is Abaddon — and in the Greek he has the name Apollyon, the Destroyer.
The first woe has passed. Behold — two woes are still coming after these things.
And the sixth angel trumpeted: and I heard one voice out of the horns of the golden altar before God,
saying to the sixth angel — the one holding the trumpet: "Loose the four angels who are bound at the great river Euphrates."
And the four angels were loosed — those made ready for the hour and day and month and year — to kill the third of the human beings.
And the number of the armies of the cavalry: twice ten-thousand times ten-thousand — I heard their number.

And this is how I saw the horses in the vision, and those sitting on them: with breastplates fire-red and hyacinth-blue and sulfur-yellow; and the heads of the horses like heads of lions; and out of their mouths go forth fire and smoke and sulfur.
By these three plagues the third of the human beings were killed — by the fire and the smoke and the sulfur going out of their mouths;
for the authority of the horses is in their mouth and in their tails — for their tails are like snakes, having heads, and with them they do harm.

And the rest of the human beings, who were not killed in these plagues, did not turn from the works of their hands — from worshiping the demons and the idols of gold and silver and bronze and stone and wood, which can neither see nor hear nor walk;
and they did not turn from their murders, nor from their sorceries, nor from their immorality, nor from their thefts.

rev.10.text: And I saw another strong angel coming down out of heaven, clothed with a cloud, and the rainbow over his head; and his face like the sun, and his feet like pillars of fire;
and holding in his hand a little scroll, opened. And he set his right foot on the sea, and the left on the land,
and cried with a great voice, as a lion roars. And when he cried, the seven thunders spoke their own voices.
And when the seven thunders spoke, I was about to write; and I heard a voice out of heaven, saying: "Seal up what the seven thunders spoke — and do not write it."
And the angel whom I saw standing on the sea and on the land raised his right hand to heaven,

and swore by the one living to the ages of the ages — who created the heaven and the things in it, and the earth and the things in it, and the sea and the things in it — that there shall be no more delay;
but in the days of the voice of the seventh angel, when he is about to trumpet, the mystery of God is finished — as he brought the good news to his own slaves, the prophets.
And the voice I had heard out of heaven was speaking with me again, and saying: "Go — take the scroll that lies opened in the hand of the angel standing on the sea and on the land."
And I went off to the angel, telling him to give me the little scroll. And he says to me: "Take it and eat it down; and it will make your belly bitter — but in your mouth it will be sweet as honey."
And I took the little scroll out of the angel's hand and ate it down; and it was in my mouth sweet as honey — and when I had eaten it, my belly was made bitter.
And they say to me: "You must prophesy again — over many peoples and nations and tongues and kings."

rev.11.text: And a reed like a measuring rod was given to me, with the words: "Rise and measure the temple of God, and the altar, and those who worship in it;
but the court outside the temple — throw it out, and do not measure it, because it has been given to the nations; and they will trample the holy city forty-two months.
And I will give to my two witnesses, and they will prophesy one thousand two hundred sixty days, clothed in sackcloth."
These are the two olive trees and the two lampstands standing before the Lord of the earth.
And if anyone wants to harm them, fire goes out of their mouth and devours their enemies; and if anyone should want to harm them — thus must he be killed.

These hold the authority to shut the heaven, so that no rain may fall in the days of their prophesying; and they hold authority over the waters, to turn them into blood, and to strike the earth with every plague, as often as they will.
And when they finish their testimony, the beast coming up out of the abyss will make war with them, and will conquer them, and will kill them.
And their corpse lies on the street of the great city — which is called, spiritually, Sodom and Egypt — where also their Lord was crucified.
And some from the peoples and tribes and tongues and nations look at their corpse three days and a half; and they do not allow their corpses to be laid in a tomb.
And those who dwell on the earth rejoice over them and celebrate — and they will send gifts to one another, because these two prophets tormented those who dwell on the earth.
And after the three days and a half, a breath of life from God entered into them, and they stood on their feet — and great fear fell on those watching them.
And they heard a great voice out of heaven, saying to them: "Come up here." And they went up into heaven in the cloud — and their enemies watched them.

And in that hour there came a great earthquake, and the tenth of the city fell; and seven thousand names of human beings were killed in the earthquake. And the rest became terrified — and gave glory to the God of heaven.
The second woe has passed. Behold — the third woe is coming swiftly.
And the seventh angel trumpeted: and there came great voices in heaven, saying: "The kingdom of the world has become our Lord's — and his Christ's; and he shall reign to the ages of the ages!"
And the twenty-four elders, sitting before God on their thrones, fell on their faces and worshiped God,
saying: "We give you thanks, Lord God, the All-Ruler — WHO-IS and WHO-WAS — because you have taken your great power, and reigned.

And the nations raged — and your wrath came, and the season for the dead to be judged, and to give the reward to your slaves the prophets and to the holy ones and to those who fear your name, the small and the great — and to destroy those who destroy the earth."
And the temple of God in heaven was opened, and the ark of his covenant was seen in his temple; and there came lightnings and voices and thunders and an earthquake and great hail.

rev.12.text: And a great sign was seen in heaven: a woman clothed with the sun, and the moon under her feet, and on her head a crown of twelve stars;
and she has a child in the womb — and she cries out, in birth-pangs and tormented to give birth.
And another sign was seen in heaven — and behold: a great fire-red dragon, with seven heads and ten horns, and on his heads seven diadems;
and his tail drags the third of the stars of heaven — and he threw them to the earth. And the dragon stood before the woman about to give birth, so that when she bore her child he might devour it.
And she bore a son, a male, who is about to shepherd all the nations with an iron rod. And her child was snatched away to God and to his throne;
and the woman fled into the wilderness — where she has there a place prepared by God, that there they might nourish her one thousand two hundred sixty days.
And war came in heaven: Michael and his angels making war with the dragon. And the dragon made war, and his angels —
and he did not prevail, nor was a place found for them any longer in heaven.

And the great dragon was thrown down — the ancient snake, the one called Devil and Satan, the one leading the whole inhabited world astray — he was thrown to the earth, and his angels were thrown down with him.

And I heard a great voice in heaven, saying: "Now has come the salvation and the power and the kingdom of our God, and the authority of his Christ — because the accuser of our brothers has been thrown down, the one accusing them before our God day and night.
And they conquered him through the blood of the Lamb and through the word of their testimony — and they did not love their soul, even up to death.
For this reason celebrate, O heavens, and you who tent in them! Woe to the earth and the sea — because the devil has come down to you with great fury, knowing that he has little season left."
And when the dragon saw that he had been thrown to the earth, he pursued the woman who had borne the male child.

And to the woman were given the two wings of the great eagle, that she might fly into the wilderness, to her place — where she is nourished a season, and seasons, and half a season, away from the face of the snake.
And the snake threw water like a river out of his mouth after the woman, to make her one swept away by the river;
and the earth helped the woman: the earth opened her mouth and swallowed down the river that the dragon had thrown out of his mouth.
And the dragon was enraged over the woman, and went off to make war with the rest of her seed — those who keep the commandments of God and hold the testimony of Jesus.
And he stood on the sand of the sea.

rev.13.text: And I saw a beast coming up out of the sea, with ten horns and seven heads — and on its horns ten diadems, and on its heads names of blasphemy.
And the beast I saw was like a leopard, and its feet like a bear's, and its mouth like a lion's mouth. And the dragon gave it his power and his throne and great authority.
And one of its heads was as slaughtered to death — and its death-wound was healed. And the whole earth marveled after the beast;
and they worshiped the dragon, because he had given the authority to the beast; and they worshiped the beast, saying: "Who is like the beast — and who can make war with it?"
And a mouth was given it, speaking great things and blasphemies; and authority was given it to act forty-two months.
And it opened its mouth in blasphemies toward God: to blaspheme his name and his tent — those who tent in heaven.
And it was given to it to make war with the holy ones and to conquer them; and authority was given it over every tribe and people and tongue and nation.
And all who dwell on the earth will worship it — everyone whose name is not written in the book of life of the Lamb slaughtered from the founding of the world.
If anyone has an ear — let him hear:
if anyone is for captivity, into captivity he goes; if anyone is to be killed by the sword, by the sword he is to be killed. Here is the endurance and the faith of the holy ones.
And I saw another beast, coming up out of the earth; and it had two horns like a lamb — and it spoke like a dragon.
And it exercises all the authority of the first beast before it. And it makes the earth and those dwelling in it worship the first beast, whose death-wound was healed;
and it does great signs — even making fire come down out of heaven to the earth before the human beings;

and it leads astray those who dwell on the earth, through the signs it was given to do before the beast — telling those who dwell on the earth to make an image to the beast that has the wound of the sword, and lived.

And it was given to it to give breath to the image of the beast, so that the image of the beast should even speak — and cause that as many as would not worship the image of the beast should be killed.
And it makes all — the small and the great, and the rich and the poor, and the free and the slaves — that they give them a mark on their right hand or on their forehead,
so that no one can buy or sell except the one holding the mark: the name of the beast, or the number of its name.
Here is the wisdom. Let the one who has a mind calculate the number of the beast — for it is the number of a human being; and its number is six hundred sixty-six.

rev.14.text: And I saw — and behold: the Lamb, standing on Mount Zion, and with him one hundred forty-four thousand having his name and the name of his Father written on their foreheads.
And I heard a voice out of heaven, like the voice of many waters and like the voice of great thunder; and the voice I heard was like harpists harping on their harps.

And they sing something like a new song before the throne and before the four living beings and the elders; and no one could learn the song except the one hundred forty-four thousand — those bought from the earth.

These are those who were not defiled with women — for they are virgins. These are the ones following the Lamb wherever he goes. These were bought from among human beings — firstfruits to God and to the Lamb;
and in their mouth no lie was found: they are blameless.
And I saw another angel flying in mid-heaven, holding an everlasting good news to announce over those seated on the earth — over every nation and tribe and tongue and people —
saying with a great voice: "Fear God and give him glory — because the hour of his judgment has come; and worship the one who made the heaven and the earth and sea and springs of waters."
And another, a second angel, followed, saying: "Fallen, fallen is Babylon the great — she who has made all the nations drink from the wine of the fury of her immorality."
And another angel, a third, followed them, saying with a great voice: "If anyone worships the beast and its image, and receives a mark on his forehead or on his hand,
he also shall drink from the wine of the fury of God, mixed unwatered in the cup of his wrath — and he shall be tormented in fire and sulfur before holy angels and before the Lamb.
And the smoke of their torment goes up to ages of ages; and they have no rest day and night — those who worship the beast and its image, and anyone who receives the mark of its name.
Here is the endurance of the holy ones — those keeping the commandments of God and the faith of Jesus.

And I heard a voice out of heaven, saying: "Write: Happy are the dead who die in the Lord from now on. Yes, says the Spirit — that they may rest from their labors; for their works follow along with them."
And I saw — and behold: a white cloud, and on the cloud one sitting like a son of man, with a golden crown on his head and a sharp sickle in his hand.

And another angel came out of the temple, crying with a great voice to the one sitting on the cloud: "Send your sickle and reap — because the hour to reap has come; because the harvest of the earth is fully dry."
And the one sitting on the cloud threw his sickle over the earth — and the earth was reaped.
And another angel came out of the temple in heaven — he also holding a sharp sickle.

And another angel came from the altar — the one holding authority over the fire — and he called with a great voice to the one holding the sharp sickle: "Send your sharp sickle and gather the clusters of the vine of the earth — because her grapes are fully ripe."
And the angel threw his sickle to the earth and gathered the vine of the earth — and threw it into the great winepress of the fury of God.
And the winepress was trodden outside the city — and blood came out of the winepress, up to the bridles of the horses, for one thousand six hundred stadia.

rev.15.text: And I saw another sign in heaven, great and marvelous: seven angels holding seven plagues — the last ones, because in them the fury of God is finished.

And I saw something like a sea of glass mixed with fire; and those conquering — out of the beast and out of its image and out of the number of its name — standing on the sea of glass, holding the harps of God.

And they sing the song of Moses, the slave of God, and the song of the Lamb, saying: "Great and marvelous are your works, Lord God, the All-Ruler; righteous and true are your ways, O King of the ages!
Who shall not fear, O Lord, and glorify your name? For you alone are holy; for all the nations will come and worship before you — because your righteous acts have been made visible."
And after these things I saw: and the temple of the tent of the testimony in heaven was opened,
and the seven angels holding the seven plagues came out of the temple, clothed in clean bright linen, and girded around the chest with golden bands.
And one of the four living beings gave the seven angels seven golden bowls, full of the fury of God — the one living to the ages of the ages;
and the temple was filled with smoke from the glory of God and from his power — and no one could enter the temple until the seven plagues of the seven angels were finished.

rev.16.text: And I heard a great voice out of the temple, saying to the seven angels: "Go — and pour out the seven bowls of the fury of God into the earth."
And the first went off and poured out his bowl into the earth; and there came a bad and evil sore on the human beings holding the mark of the beast and worshiping its image.
And the second poured out his bowl into the sea; and it became blood, like a dead man's — and every soul of life died, the things in the sea.
And the third poured out his bowl into the rivers and the springs of the waters; and they became blood.
And I heard the angel of the waters saying: "Righteous are you — WHO-IS and WHO-WAS, the Holy One — because you have judged these things;
because they poured out the blood of holy ones and prophets — and you have given them blood to drink. They are worthy."
And I heard the altar saying: "Yes, Lord God, the All-Ruler — true and righteous are your judgments."
And the fourth poured out his bowl upon the sun; and it was given to it to scorch the human beings with fire —
and the human beings were scorched with a great scorching. And they blasphemed the name of God, who holds the authority over these plagues — and they did not turn to give him glory.
And the fifth poured out his bowl upon the throne of the beast; and its kingdom became darkened — and they kept chewing their tongues from the pain,
and they blasphemed the God of heaven for their pains and for their sores — and they did not turn from their works.
And the sixth poured out his bowl upon the great river Euphrates; and its water was dried up, so that the way of the kings from the rising of the sun might be made ready.
And I saw, out of the mouth of the dragon and out of the mouth of the beast and out of the mouth of the false prophet, three unclean spirits, like frogs —
for they are spirits of demons, doing signs — which go out to the kings of the whole inhabited world, to gather them for the war of the great day of God, the All-Ruler.
("Behold, I am coming like a thief. Happy is the one who stays awake and keeps his garments — so that he does not walk naked and they see his shame.")
And he gathered them into the place called, in Hebrew, Har-magedon.
And the seventh poured out his bowl upon the air — and a great voice came out of the temple, from the throne, saying: "It has come to pass!"
And there came lightnings and voices and thunders; and a great earthquake came — such as had not come since human beings came to be on the earth: so vast an earthquake, so great.
And the great city broke into three parts, and the cities of the nations fell. And Babylon the great was remembered before God — to give her the cup of the wine of the fury of his wrath;
and every island fled, and mountains were not found;
and great hail, like talent-weights, comes down out of heaven upon the human beings. And the human beings blasphemed God for the plague of the hail — because its plague is exceedingly great.

rev.17.text: And one of the seven angels holding the seven bowls came and spoke with me, saying: "Come — I will show you the judgment of the great prostitute who sits on many waters,
with whom the kings of the earth committed immorality — and those who dwell on the earth were made drunk from the wine of her immorality."
And he carried me away into a wilderness, in the Spirit. And I saw a woman sitting on a scarlet beast, teeming with names of blasphemy, having seven heads and ten horns.
And the woman was clothed in purple and scarlet, and gilded with gold and precious stone and pearls — holding in her hand a golden cup, full of abominations and the unclean things of her immorality;
and on her forehead a name written — a mystery: BABYLON THE GREAT, THE MOTHER OF THE PROSTITUTES AND OF THE ABOMINATIONS OF THE EARTH.
And I saw the woman — drunk from the blood of the holy ones and from the blood of the witnesses of Jesus. And seeing her, I marveled a great marvel.
And the angel said to me: "Why did you marvel? I will tell you the mystery of the woman, and of the beast that carries her — the one with the seven heads and the ten horns.

The beast you saw was, and is not — and is about to come up out of the abyss, and goes off into destruction. And those who dwell on the earth — whose names are not written on the book of life from the founding of the world — will marvel, seeing the beast: that it was, and is not, and shall be present.
Here is the mind that has wisdom: the seven heads are seven mountains, where the woman sits on them. And they are seven kings:
the five have fallen, the one is, the other has not yet come — and when he comes, he must remain a little while.
And the beast that was and is not — he is himself an eighth, and is of the seven, and goes off into destruction.
And the ten horns you saw are ten kings, who have not yet received a kingdom — but they receive authority as kings for one hour, together with the beast.
These hold one mind — and they give their power and authority to the beast.
These will make war with the Lamb — and the Lamb will conquer them, because he is Lord of lords and King of kings; and those with him are called and chosen and faithful."
And he says to me: "The waters you saw, where the prostitute sits — they are peoples and crowds and nations and tongues.
And the ten horns you saw, and the beast — these will hate the prostitute, and will make her desolated and naked, and will eat her flesh, and will burn her down with fire;
for God has put it into their hearts to do his mind: to act with one mind, and to give their kingdom to the beast — until the words of God are finished.
And the woman you saw is the great city that holds kingship over the kings of the earth."

rev.18.text: After these things I saw another angel coming down out of heaven, holding great authority — and the earth was lit up from his glory.

And he cried with a strong voice, saying: "Fallen, fallen is Babylon the great! And she has become a dwelling place of demons, and a prison of every unclean spirit, and a prison of every unclean bird, and a prison of every unclean and hated beast —

because from the wine of the fury of her immorality all the nations have fallen; and the kings of the earth committed immorality with her, and the merchants of the earth grew rich from the power of her luxury."
And I heard another voice out of heaven, saying: "Come out of her, my people — so that you do not share in her sins, and so that you do not receive of her plagues;
because her sins have been glued together up to heaven, and God has remembered her wrongs.
Give back to her as she also gave back; and double the double, according to her works. In the cup she mixed — mix her double;
as much as she glorified herself and lived in luxury, so much give her of torment and mourning. Because in her heart she says: 'I sit a queen; I am no widow; and mourning I shall never see' —
for this reason in one day her plagues will come: death and mourning and famine; and she will be burned down with fire — because strong is the Lord God who has judged her.
And the kings of the earth — those who committed immorality and lived in luxury with her — will weep and beat their breasts over her, when they see the smoke of her burning,
standing far off for fear of her torment, saying: "Woe, woe — the great city, Babylon, the strong city! For in one hour your judgment came."
And the merchants of the earth weep and mourn over her — because no one buys their cargo any more:

cargo of gold and silver and precious stone and pearls, and fine linen and purple and silk and scarlet; and every citron wood, and every ivory vessel, and every vessel of most precious wood and bronze and iron and marble;

and cinnamon and spice and incense and perfumed oil and frankincense, and wine and oil and fine flour and wheat, and cattle and sheep — and of horses and carriages and bodies; and souls of human beings.
"And the ripe fruit of your soul's craving has gone from you; and all the rich and the bright things have perished from you — and never, never shall they find them again."
The merchants of these things — who grew rich from her — will stand far off, for fear of her torment, weeping and mourning,
saying: "Woe, woe — the great city, clothed in fine linen and purple and scarlet, gilded with gold and precious stone and pearl!
For in one hour so great a wealth was made desolate." And every ship's pilot, and everyone sailing anywhere, and sailors, and as many as work the sea, stood far off
and cried out, watching the smoke of her burning, saying: "What city is like the great city?"

And they threw dust on their heads and cried out, weeping and mourning, saying: "Woe, woe — the great city, in which all who have ships on the sea grew rich from her costliness! For in one hour she was made desolate."
Celebrate over her, O heaven — and you holy ones and apostles and prophets — because God has judged your judgment out of her!
And one strong angel lifted a stone like a great millstone and threw it into the sea, saying: "So, with a rush, shall Babylon the great city be thrown down — and shall never be found again.

And the voice of harpists and musicians and flute-players and trumpeters shall never be heard in you again; and every craftsman of every craft shall never be found in you again; and the voice of a mill shall never be heard in you again;

and the light of a lamp shall never shine in you again; and the voice of bridegroom and bride shall never be heard in you again. Because your merchants were the great ones of the earth; because by your sorcery all the nations were led astray —
and in her was found the blood of prophets and of holy ones, and of all those slaughtered upon the earth."

rev.19.text: After these things I heard something like a great voice of a vast crowd in heaven, saying: "Hallelujah! The salvation and the glory and the power belong to our God —
because true and righteous are his judgments; because he judged the great prostitute who was corrupting the earth with her immorality — and he avenged the blood of his slaves from her hand."
And a second time they have said: "Hallelujah! And her smoke goes up to the ages of the ages."
And the twenty-four elders fell down, and the four living beings, and they worshiped God, the one sitting on the throne, saying: "Amen! Hallelujah!"
And a voice went out from the throne, saying: "Praise our God, all his slaves — you who fear him, the small and the great!"

And I heard something like the voice of a vast crowd, and like the voice of many waters, and like the voice of strong thunders, saying: "Hallelujah! For the Lord our God, the All-Ruler, has begun to reign!
Let us rejoice and exult and give the glory to him — because the wedding of the Lamb has come, and his wife has made herself ready;
and it was given her to be clothed in fine linen, bright, clean — for the fine linen is the righteous acts of the holy ones."
And he says to me: "Write: Happy are those called to the wedding supper of the Lamb." And he says to me: "These are the true words of God."

And I fell before his feet to worship him. And he says to me: "See that you do not! I am a fellow slave of yours and of your brothers who hold the testimony of Jesus. Worship God! For the testimony of Jesus is the spirit of the prophecy."
And I saw heaven opened — and behold: a white horse; and the one sitting on it called Faithful and True — and in righteousness he judges and makes war.
And his eyes are a flame of fire, and on his head many diadems — bearing a name written that no one knows except himself;
and he is clothed in a garment dipped in blood — and his name has been called: The Word of God.
And the armies in heaven were following him on white horses, clothed in fine linen, white, clean.

And out of his mouth goes forth a sharp sword, that with it he may strike the nations; and he himself will shepherd them with an iron rod; and he himself treads the winepress of the wine of the fury of the wrath of God, the All-Ruler.
And he has on his garment and on his thigh a name written: KING OF KINGS AND LORD OF LORDS.
And I saw one angel standing in the sun; and he cried with a great voice, saying to all the birds flying in mid-heaven: "Come — gather to the great supper of God,
to eat the flesh of kings and the flesh of commanders and the flesh of the strong, and the flesh of horses and of those sitting on them — and the flesh of all, free and slave, small and great."
And I saw the beast and the kings of the earth and their armies, gathered to make the war with the one sitting on the horse and with his army.

And the beast was seized — and with it the false prophet, the one who did the signs before it, by which he led astray those who received the mark of the beast and those worshiping its image. The two were thrown, living, into the lake of fire that burns with sulfur;
and the rest were killed with the sword of the one sitting on the horse — the sword that went out of his mouth; and all the birds were filled from their flesh.

rev.20.text: And I saw an angel coming down out of heaven, holding the key of the abyss and a great chain upon his hand.
And he seized the dragon — the ancient snake, who is the Devil and Satan — and bound him a thousand years,

and threw him into the abyss, and shut it and sealed it above him, so that he should lead the nations astray no longer until the thousand years were finished. After these things he must be loosed a little time.

And I saw thrones — and they sat on them, and judgment was given to them; and the souls of those beheaded for the testimony of Jesus and for the word of God, and those who had not worshiped the beast or its image and had not received the mark on their forehead and on their hand — and they lived, and reigned with the Christ a thousand years.
The rest of the dead did not live until the thousand years were finished. This is the first resurrection.

Happy and holy is the one who has a part in the first resurrection! Over these the second death has no authority; rather, they shall be priests of God and of the Christ, and shall reign with him a thousand years.
And when the thousand years are finished, Satan will be loosed out of his prison,
and will go out to lead astray the nations at the four corners of the earth — Gog and Magog — to gather them for the war; their number is like the sand of the sea.
And they went up over the breadth of the earth and encircled the camp of the holy ones and the beloved city. And fire came down out of heaven and devoured them;
and the devil, who led them astray, was thrown into the lake of fire and sulfur — where also the beast and the false prophet are; and they will be tormented day and night, to the ages of the ages.
And I saw a great white throne, and the one sitting on it — from whose face the earth and the heaven fled; and no place was found for them.

And I saw the dead — the great and the small — standing before the throne; and scrolls were opened. And another scroll was opened, which is the scroll of life. And the dead were judged out of the things written in the scrolls, according to their works.
And the sea gave up the dead in it, and Death and Hades gave up the dead in them; and they were judged, each one, according to their works.
And Death and Hades were thrown into the lake of fire. This is the second death — the lake of fire;
and if anyone was not found written in the scroll of life, he was thrown into the lake of fire.

rev.21.text: And I saw a new heaven and a new earth — for the first heaven and the first earth had gone away, and the sea is no more.
And the holy city, new Jerusalem, I saw coming down out of heaven from God, made ready like a bride adorned for her husband.
And I heard a great voice out of the throne, saying: "Behold — the tent of God is with human beings, and he will tent with them; and they shall be his peoples, and God himself shall be with them;
and he will wipe away every tear from their eyes. And death shall be no more; neither mourning nor crying nor pain shall be any more — the first things have gone away."
And the one sitting on the throne said: "Behold — I am making all things new." And he says: "Write — for these words are faithful and true."
And he said to me: "They have come to pass! I am the Alpha and the Omega, the beginning and the end. To the one who thirsts I will give from the spring of the water of life — as a free gift.
The one conquering shall inherit these things; and I will be his God, and he shall be my son.

But for the cowardly and unbelieving and abominable, and murderers and the immoral and sorcerers and idolaters and all the liars — their part is in the lake burning with fire and sulfur, which is the second death."
And one of the seven angels holding the seven bowls — full of the seven last plagues — came and spoke with me, saying: "Come, I will show you the bride, the wife of the Lamb."
And he carried me away in the Spirit onto a great and high mountain, and showed me the holy city Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God —
holding the glory of God; her radiance like a most precious stone, like a jasper stone, crystal-clear;
with a great and high wall; with twelve gates, and at the gates twelve angels, and names inscribed, which are the names of the twelve tribes of the sons of Israel:
from the east three gates, and from the north three gates, and from the south three gates, and from the west three gates;
and the wall of the city with twelve foundations — and on them twelve names, of the twelve apostles of the Lamb.
And the one speaking with me held a golden reed as a measure, to measure the city and her gates and her wall.
And the city lies foursquare: her length is as much as the width. And he measured the city with the reed: twelve thousand stadia — her length and width and height are equal.
And he measured her wall: one hundred forty-four cubits — a human measure, which is an angel's.
And the material of her wall is jasper; and the city, pure gold, like pure glass.
The foundations of the wall of the city are adorned with every precious stone: the first foundation jasper, the second sapphire, the third chalcedony, the fourth emerald,
the fifth sardonyx, the sixth carnelian, the seventh chrysolite, the eighth beryl, the ninth topaz, the tenth chrysoprase, the eleventh hyacinth, the twelfth amethyst;
and the twelve gates, twelve pearls — each single one of the gates was out of one pearl. And the street of the city: pure gold, like transparent glass.
And I saw no temple in her — for the Lord God, the All-Ruler, is her temple; and the Lamb.
And the city has no need of the sun or of the moon to shine on her — for the glory of God has lit her, and her lamp is the Lamb.
And the nations shall walk through her light; and the kings of the earth bring their glory into her;
and her gates shall by no means be shut by day — and night shall not be there;
and they shall carry the glory and the honor of the nations into her.
And there shall never enter into her anything common — no one who does abomination and lie; only those written in the Lamb's scroll of life.

rev.22.text: And he showed me a river of the water of life, bright as crystal, going forth out of the throne of God and of the Lamb,

in the middle of her street. And on this side of the river and on that: the tree of life, making twelve fruits, yielding its fruit each single month — and the leaves of the tree are for the healing of the nations.
And every curse shall be no more. And the throne of God and of the Lamb shall be in her, and his slaves will serve him;
and they shall see his face — and his name is on their foreheads.
And night shall be no more; and they have no need of lamplight or of sunlight — because the Lord God will shine on them; and they shall reign to the ages of the ages.
And he said to me: "These words are faithful and true. And the Lord, the God of the spirits of the prophets, sent his angel to show his slaves the things that must come to pass swiftly.
And behold — I am coming swiftly. Happy is the one who keeps the words of the prophecy of this scroll."
And I, John, am the one hearing and seeing these things. And when I heard and saw, I fell down to worship before the feet of the angel showing me these things.
And he says to me: "See that you do not! I am a fellow slave of yours and of your brothers the prophets, and of those who keep the words of this scroll. Worship God!"
And he says to me: "Do not seal the words of the prophecy of this scroll — for the season is near.
Let the one doing wrong do wrong still; and let the filthy one be filthy still; and let the righteous one do righteousness still; and let the holy one be made holy still."
"Behold — I am coming swiftly, and my reward is with me: to give back to each one as his work is.
I am the Alpha and the Omega, the First and the Last, the beginning and the end.
Happy are those washing their robes — that their right may be to the tree of life, and that they may enter the city by the gates.
Outside: the dogs and the sorcerers and the immoral and the murderers and the idolaters — and everyone loving and doing a lie.
I, Jesus, sent my angel to bear you this testimony over the assemblies. I am the Root and the Offspring of David — the bright star, the morning one."
And the Spirit and the bride say: "Come!" And let the one hearing say: "Come!" And let the one thirsting come; let the one who wills take the water of life — as a free gift.
I bear witness to everyone hearing the words of the prophecy of this scroll: if anyone adds to them, God will add to him the plagues written in this scroll;
and if anyone takes away from the words of the scroll of this prophecy, God will take away his part from the tree of life and out of the holy city — the things written in this scroll.
The one bearing witness to these things says: "Yes — I am coming swiftly." Amen. Come, Lord Jesus!
The grace of the Lord Jesus be with all.

2co.1.text: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus through the will of God, and Timothy the brother — to the assembly of God that is in Corinth, with all the holy ones in the whole of Achaia:
grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ — the Father of the compassions and God of all comfort,
who comforts us in all our affliction, so that we can comfort those in every affliction, through the comfort with which we ourselves are comforted by God.
Because just as the sufferings of the Christ overflow into us, so through the Christ our comfort also overflows.
And if we are afflicted — it is for your comfort and salvation; if we are comforted — it is for your comfort, which works itself out in the endurance of the same sufferings that we also suffer.
And our hope for you is firm — knowing that as you are sharers of the sufferings, so also of the comfort.
For we do not want you to be unknowing, brothers, about our affliction that came in Asia: that we were weighed down beyond all measure, beyond strength, so that we despaired even of living;
indeed, we ourselves held within ourselves the sentence of death — so that we should not be trusting in ourselves, but in God, who raises the dead;
who rescued us out of so great a death, and will rescue us; in whom we have set our hope that he will rescue us still —
you also joining in the work for us by your petition, so that from many faces, for the grace-gift given us through many, thanks may be given on our behalf.

For our boast is this — the testimony of our conscience: that in the holiness and unmixed sincerity of God — not in fleshly wisdom but in the grace of God — we conducted ourselves in the world, and all the more toward you.
For we write you nothing other than what you read — or indeed recognize; and I hope you will recognize it to the end,
just as you have recognized us in part: that we are your boast, just as you also are ours, in the day of our Lord Jesus.
And in this confidence I intended to come to you first, so that you might have a second grace:
to pass through you into Macedonia, and again from Macedonia to come to you, and by you to be sent onward to Judea.
Intending this, then — surely I did not act with lightness? Or the things I plan — do I plan them according to flesh, so that with me the "Yes, yes" is also "No, no"?
But God is faithful: our word to you is not "Yes and No."
For the Son of God, Jesus Christ — the one proclaimed among you through us, through me and Silvanus and Timothy — did not become "Yes and No"; in him it has become "Yes."
For as many as are the promises of God — in him is the "Yes." Therefore also through him is the "Amen," to God, for glory, through us.
And the one who makes us firm with you, into Christ — and who anointed us — is God,
who also sealed us, and gave the down-payment of the Spirit in our hearts.
And I call God as witness against my own soul: it was to spare you that I did not come again to Corinth.
Not that we lord it over your faith — rather we are co-workers of your joy; for by the faith you stand.

2co.2.text: For I decided this within myself: not to come to you again in grief.
For if I grieve you — then who cheers me, except the one grieved by me?
And I wrote this very thing so that, coming, I might not have grief from those who ought to make me rejoice — trusting in you all, that my joy is the joy of you all.
For out of much affliction and anguish of heart I wrote to you, through many tears — not that you should be grieved, but that you might know the love I have all the more abundantly for you.
But if anyone has caused grief, he has not grieved me — but in part (not to lay it on too heavily) all of you.
Sufficient for such a person is this punishment by the majority —
so that, on the contrary, you should rather freely forgive and comfort him, lest such a person be swallowed down by the overflowing grief.
Therefore I urge you: ratify your love toward him.
For to this end I also wrote: that I might know your tested character — whether you are obedient in everything.
And to whomever you freely forgive anything — I do too; for indeed what I have forgiven, if I have forgiven anything, is for your sake, in the face of Christ,
so that we may not be outmaneuvered by Satan — for we are not ignorant of his designs.
Now when I came to Troas for the good news of the Christ, and a door stood opened for me in the Lord,
I had no rest in my spirit, because I did not find Titus my brother; so, taking leave of them, I went out into Macedonia.
But thanks be to God — who always leads us in his triumph-procession in the Christ, and makes the fragrance of the knowledge of him visible through us in every place;
because we are the sweet smell of Christ, to God, among those being saved and among those perishing:
to the ones, a smell out of death, into death; to the others, a smell out of life, into life. And for these things — who is sufficient?
For we are not, like the many, peddling the word of God; rather, as out of unmixed sincerity — as out of God, before the face of God — in Christ we speak.

2co.3.text: Are we beginning again to commend ourselves? Or do we need, like some, letters of commendation to you — or from you?
You are our letter — written in our hearts, known and read by all human beings;
being made visible as a letter of Christ, served by us — written not with ink but with the Spirit of the living God; not on tablets of stone, but on tablets that are hearts of flesh.
And such confidence we have, through the Christ, toward God.
Not that we are sufficient from ourselves to count anything as coming out of ourselves; rather, our sufficiency is out of God,
who also made us sufficient as servants of a new covenant — not of letter but of Spirit; for the letter kills, but the Spirit makes alive.

Now if the service of death, in letters engraved on stones, came with glory — so that the sons of Israel could not gaze steadily at the face of Moses, because of the glory of his face, the glory being brought to nothing —
how shall the service of the Spirit not be all the more in glory?
For if there was glory for the service of condemnation, by much more does the service of righteousness overflow in glory.
Indeed, what was glorified has, in this respect, not even been glorified — on account of the surpassing glory;
for if what was being brought to nothing came through glory, by much more is what remains, in glory.
Having, then, such a hope, we use great open boldness —
and not as Moses used to put a veil over his face, so that the sons of Israel might not gaze at the end of what was being brought to nothing.
But their minds were hardened. For until this very day the same veil remains at the reading of the old covenant — not being unveiled, because in Christ it is brought to nothing;
but until today, whenever Moses is read, a veil lies over their heart —
and whenever one turns to the Lord, the veil is taken away.
Now the Lord is the Spirit; and where the Spirit of the Lord is — freedom.
And we all, with unveiled face, mirroring the glory of the Lord, are being transformed into the same image, from glory to glory — just as from the Lord, the Spirit.

2co.4.text: For this reason — having this service, according to the mercy shown us — we do not lose heart;

rather, we have renounced the hidden things of shame: not walking in craftiness, nor adulterating the word of God, but by making the truth visible, commending ourselves to every human conscience, before God.
And even if our good news is veiled — it is veiled among those who are perishing,
in whom the god of this age has blinded the minds of the unbelieving, so that the light-beam of the good news of the glory of the Christ — who is the image of God — should not dawn on them.
For we do not proclaim ourselves — we proclaim Christ Jesus as Lord, and ourselves your slaves for Jesus' sake.
Because the God who said, "Out of darkness light shall shine," is the one who shone in our hearts — for the light-beam of the knowledge of the glory of God in the face of Christ.
But we hold this treasure in clay vessels — so that the surpassing greatness of the power may be God's, and not out of us:
in everything afflicted, but not crushed into a corner; at a loss, but not utterly lost;
persecuted, but not abandoned; thrown down, but not destroyed —
always carrying around in the body the dying of Jesus, so that the life of Jesus may also be made visible in our body.
For we, the living, are always being handed over to death for Jesus' sake — so that the life of Jesus may also be made visible in our mortal flesh.
So death is at work in us — but life in you.
