And she wept over him the seven days their feast lasted; and it came to pass on the seventh day that he told her — because she had pressed him hard. And she told the riddle to the sons of her people.

And the men of the city said to him on the seventh day, before the sun went down: "What is sweeter than honey? And what is fiercer than a lion?" And he said to them: "If you had not plowed with my heifer, you would not have solved my riddle."

And the Spirit of YHWH rushed upon him, and he went down to Ashkelon and struck down thirty men of them, and took their gear, and gave the changes of clothing to those who had told the riddle. And his anger burned — and he went up to his father's house.
And Samson's wife was given to his companion — the one who had been his best man.

jdg.15.text: And it came to pass after some days, in the days of the wheat harvest, that Samson visited his wife with a young goat; and he said: "Let me go in to my wife, into the inner room." But her father would not let him go in.
And her father said: "I said to myself that you surely hated her, and I gave her to your companion. Is not her younger sister better than she? Let her be yours, please, instead."
And Samson said to them: "This time I am clear of guilt regarding the Philistines — when I do them harm."
And Samson went and caught three hundred foxes; and he took torches, and turned tail to tail, and set one torch between each two tails, in the middle;
and he set the torches on fire and let the foxes loose into the standing grain of the Philistines — and he burned everything, from stacked sheaves to standing grain, and even olive orchard.

And the Philistines said: "Who has done this?" And they said: "Samson, the son-in-law of the Timnite — because he took his wife and gave her to his companion." And the Philistines went up and burned her and her father with fire.
And Samson said to them: "If this is what you do — then I will be avenged on you, and only after that will I stop."
And he struck them, leg upon thigh — a great blow; and he went down and lived in the cleft of the rock of Etam.
And the Philistines came up and camped in Judah, and spread out at Lehi.
And the men of Judah said: "Why have you come up against us?" And they said: "We have come up to bind Samson — to do to him as he did to us."

And three thousand men of Judah went down to the cleft of the rock of Etam and said to Samson: "Do you not know that the Philistines rule over us? What is this you have done to us?" And he said to them: "As they did to me — so I did to them."
And they said to him: "We have come down to bind you, to give you into the hand of the Philistines." And Samson said to them: "Swear to me that you yourselves will not fall upon me."
And they spoke to him, saying: "No — we will only bind you and give you into their hand; we will surely not kill you." And they bound him with two new ropes and brought him up from the rock.

He had come as far as Lehi, and the Philistines came shouting to meet him — and the Spirit of YHWH rushed upon him; and the ropes on his arms became like flax burned with fire, and his bonds melted off his hands.
And he found a fresh jawbone of a donkey, and put out his hand and took it — and with it he struck down a thousand men.
And Samson said: "With the jawbone of a donkey — a heap, two heaps! With the jawbone of a donkey I have struck down a thousand men!"
And it came to pass, when he finished speaking, that he threw the jawbone from his hand; and he called that place Ramath-lehi — Jawbone Hill.

And he was very thirsty, and he called to YHWH and said: "You have given this great salvation by the hand of your servant — and now shall I die of thirst, and fall into the hand of the uncircumcised?"

And God split open the hollow that is at Lehi, and water came out of it; and he drank, and his spirit returned, and he revived. Therefore its name was called En-hakkore — the Spring of the Caller — which is at Lehi to this day.
And he judged Israel, in the days of the Philistines, twenty years.

jdg.17.text: And there was a man from the hill country of Ephraim, and his name was Micayehu.

And he said to his mother: "The eleven hundred pieces of silver that were taken from you — about which you uttered a curse, and even said it in my ears — behold, the silver is with me; I took it." And his mother said: "Blessed be my son by YHWH!"

And he returned the eleven hundred pieces of silver to his mother; and his mother said: "I solemnly consecrate the silver to YHWH, from my own hand, for my son — to make a carved image and a cast image. So now, I return it to you."

But he returned the silver to his mother; and his mother took two hundred pieces of silver and gave it to the silversmith, and he made it into a carved image and a cast image — and it was in the house of Micayehu.
And the man Micah had a house of gods; and he made an ephod and teraphim, and filled the hand of one of his sons — and he became his priest.
In those days there was no king in Israel; each man did what was right in his own eyes.
And there was a young man from Bethlehem of Judah, of the clan of Judah; and he was a Levite, and he was sojourning there.
And the man went from the city — from Bethlehem of Judah — to sojourn wherever he might find a place; and he came to the hill country of Ephraim, to the house of Micah, as he made his way.
And Micah said to him: "Where do you come from?" And he said to him: "I am a Levite from Bethlehem of Judah, and I am going to sojourn wherever I find a place."
And Micah said to him: "Stay with me, and be to me a father and a priest — and I will give you ten pieces of silver a year, and a set of clothes, and your keep." And the Levite went in.
And the Levite was content to stay with the man; and the young man became to him like one of his sons.
And Micah filled the hand of the Levite, and the young man became his priest — and he was in the house of Micah.
And Micah said: "Now I know that YHWH will do me good — because the Levite has become my priest."

jdg.18.text: In those days there was no king in Israel. And in those days the tribe of the Danites was seeking an inheritance for itself, to settle in — for up to that day nothing had fallen to it as an inheritance among the tribes of Israel.

And the sons of Dan sent from their clan five men, from their whole number — men of valor from Zorah and Eshtaol — to scout the land and search it out; and they said to them: "Go, search out the land." And they came to the hill country of Ephraim, to the house of Micah — and they lodged there.

While they were near the house of Micah, they recognized the voice of the young Levite; and they turned aside there and said to him: "Who brought you here? And what are you doing in this place? And what have you here?"
And he said to them: "Thus and so has Micah done for me: he has hired me, and I have become his priest."
And they said to him: "Inquire of God, please, that we may know whether our way — the one we are traveling — will prosper."
And the priest said to them: "Go in peace; the way you are traveling is straight before YHWH."

And the five men went, and came to Laish; and they saw the people in her midst, dwelling securely, after the manner of the Sidonians — quiet and trusting; and there was no one in the land putting anything to shame, no heir of restraint; and they were far from the Sidonians, and had no dealings with anyone.
And they came to their brothers at Zorah and Eshtaol; and their brothers said to them: "What do you report?"
And they said: "Rise — and let us go up against them! For we have seen the land, and behold: it is very good. And you sit silent? Do not be sluggish about going — about entering to possess the land.
When you come, you will come to a trusting people, and the land is wide on both hands — for God has given it into your hand: a place where there is no lack of anything that is in the earth."
And there set out from there, from the clan of the Danites, from Zorah and from Eshtaol, six hundred men girded with weapons of war.
And they went up and camped at Kiriath-jearim in Judah; therefore they called that place Mahaneh-dan — Camp of Dan — to this day; behold, it is behind Kiriath-jearim.
And they crossed from there to the hill country of Ephraim, and came to the house of Micah.

And the five men who had gone to scout the land of Laish answered and said to their brothers: "Do you know that in these houses there are an ephod and teraphim, and a carved image and a cast image? So now — know what you will do."
And they turned aside there and came to the house of the young Levite, at the house of Micah, and asked him of his welfare —
while the six hundred men girded with their weapons of war, who were of the sons of Dan, stood at the opening of the gate.

And the five men who had gone to scout the land went up, entered there, and took the carved image and the ephod and the teraphim and the cast image — while the priest stood at the opening of the gate with the six hundred men girded with weapons of war.
When these went into Micah's house and took the carved image, the ephod, and the teraphim, and the cast image, the priest said to them: "What are you doing?"

And they said to him: "Be quiet! Put your hand over your mouth and come with us, and be to us a father and a priest. Is it better for you to be priest to the house of one man — or to be priest to a tribe and a clan in Israel?"
And the priest's heart was glad; and he took the ephod and the teraphim and the carved image, and went into the midst of the people.
And they turned and went — and they put the little ones and the livestock and the valuables in front of them.
When they had gone some distance from the house of Micah, the men who were in the houses near Micah's house were called out, and they overtook the sons of Dan;
and they called to the sons of Dan, who turned their faces and said to Micah: "What is the matter with you, that you have been called out?"
And he said: "My gods that I made — you have taken them, and the priest, and gone away. And what do I have left? How can you say to me, 'What is the matter with you?'"
And the sons of Dan said to him: "Do not let your voice be heard among us — lest men bitter of soul fall upon you, and you gather in your own life and the lives of your household."
And the sons of Dan went on their way; and Micah saw that they were stronger than he, and he turned and went back to his house.

And they took what Micah had made, and the priest who had been his, and came against Laish — against a people quiet and trusting — and struck them with the mouth of the sword; and the city they burned with fire.
And there was no rescuer — for she was far from Sidon, and they had no dealings with anyone; and she lay in the valley that belongs to Beth-rehob. And they rebuilt the city and settled in it;
and they called the name of the city Dan, after the name of Dan their father, who was born to Israel — though at first Laish was the name of the city.
And the sons of Dan set up the carved image for themselves; and Jonathan son of Gershom, son of Moses, he and his sons were priests to the tribe of the Danites until the day of the exile of the land.
And they kept Micah's carved image, which he had made, all the days the house of God was in Shiloh.

jdg.19.text: And it came to pass in those days — and there was no king in Israel — that there was a Levite man sojourning in the far reaches of the hill country of Ephraim; and he took for himself a woman, a concubine, from Bethlehem of Judah.
And his concubine was unfaithful against him, and she went from him to her father's house, to Bethlehem of Judah — and she was there a stretch of four months.

And her husband rose and went after her, to speak to her heart and bring her back — his servant with him, and a pair of donkeys. And she brought him into her father's house; and when the young woman's father saw him, he rejoiced to meet him.
And his father-in-law, the young woman's father, held him fast, and he stayed with him three days; and they ate and drank, and lodged there.

And it came to pass on the fourth day that they rose early in the morning, and he got up to go; and the young woman's father said to his son-in-law: "Sustain your heart with a piece of bread — and afterward you may go."
So they sat, and the two of them ate together and drank; and the young woman's father said to the man: "Be willing, please — stay the night, and let your heart be glad."
And the man rose to go, but his father-in-law pressed him — and he turned back and lodged there.
And he rose early in the morning on the fifth day to go; and the young woman's father said: "Sustain your heart, please." And they lingered until the day declined — and the two of them ate.

And the man rose to go — he and his concubine and his servant — and his father-in-law, the young woman's father, said to him: "Behold now, the day has weakened toward evening. Stay the night, please. Behold, the day is making camp — lodge here, and let your heart be glad; and tomorrow rise early for your journey, and go to your tent."
But the man was not willing to stay the night; and he rose and went, and came as far as opposite Jebus — that is, Jerusalem — with a pair of saddled donkeys and his concubine with him.
They were near Jebus, and the day was far spent; and the servant said to his master: "Come, please — let us turn aside into this city of the Jebusites and lodge in it."
And his master said to him: "We will not turn aside into a city of foreigners who are not of the children of Israel; we will cross over as far as Gibeah."
And he said to his servant: "Come, let us draw near to one of the places, and lodge in Gibeah or in Ramah."
So they crossed over and went on; and the sun set on them beside Gibeah, which belongs to Benjamin.
And they turned aside there, to go in and lodge in Gibeah; and he went in and sat in the open square of the city — and there was no one gathering them into a house to lodge.

And behold — an old man coming from his work, from the field, in the evening. And the man was from the hill country of Ephraim, and he was sojourning in Gibeah; but the men of the place were Benjaminites.
And he lifted his eyes and saw the traveler in the open square of the city; and the old man said: "Where are you going, and where do you come from?"

And he said to him: "We are crossing from Bethlehem of Judah to the far reaches of the hill country of Ephraim — I am from there. I went to Bethlehem of Judah, and I am walking with the house of YHWH — and there is no one gathering me into a house.
And there is both straw and fodder for our donkeys, and there is also bread and wine for me and for your maidservant and for the young man with your servants. There is no lack of anything."
And the old man said: "Peace to you! Only — let all your needs be on me; only do not lodge in the square."
And he brought him into his house and mixed fodder for the donkeys; and they washed their feet, and ate and drank.

While they were making their hearts glad — behold, the men of the city, men of the sons of worthlessness, surrounded the house, beating on the door; and they said to the old man, the master of the house: "Bring out the man who came into your house — that we may know him!"
And the man, the master of the house, went out to them and said to them: "No, my brothers — do not act so wickedly, please! Since this man has come into my house, do not do this outrage.
Behold — my virgin daughter, and his concubine. Let me bring them out. Abuse them; do to them what is good in your eyes — but to this man do not do this outrageous thing."

But the men were not willing to listen to him. So the man seized his concubine and put her out to them, outside; and they knew her and abused her all night until morning — and they let her go at the rising of the dawn.
And the woman came, at the turning of the morning, and fell at the opening of the man's house where her master was — until it was light.

And her master rose in the morning and opened the doors of the house and went out to go on his way — and behold: the woman, his concubine, fallen at the opening of the house, her hands on the threshold.
And he said to her: "Rise — let us go." But there was no answer. And he took her up on the donkey; and the man rose and went to his place.
And he came to his house, and took the knife, and took hold of his concubine, and cut her, limb by limb, into twelve pieces — and sent her through all the territory of Israel.

And it came to pass that all who saw it said: "Nothing like this has ever happened or been seen from the day the children of Israel came up out of the land of Egypt to this day. Set your minds on it, take counsel, and speak!"

jdg.20.text: And all the children of Israel went out, and the congregation assembled as one man — from Dan to Beersheba, and the land of Gilead — to YHWH at Mizpah.
And the chiefs of all the people, all the tribes of Israel, took their stand in the assembly of the people of God: four hundred thousand men on foot, drawing the sword.
And the sons of Benjamin heard that the children of Israel had gone up to Mizpah. And the children of Israel said: "Speak — how did this evil come to be?"
And the Levite man, the husband of the murdered woman, answered and said: "I came to Gibeah, which belongs to Benjamin — I and my concubine — to spend the night.
And the lords of Gibeah rose against me and surrounded the house against me by night. Me they intended to kill; and my concubine they abused — and she died.
And I took hold of my concubine and cut her in pieces and sent her through all the countryside of Israel's inheritance — for they have committed depravity and outrage in Israel.
Behold, all of you are children of Israel: give here your word and counsel!"
And all the people rose as one man, saying: "None of us will go to his tent, and none of us will turn aside to his house.
And now, this is the thing we will do to Gibeah: against her by lot!

And we will take ten men of a hundred from all the tribes of Israel — and a hundred of a thousand, and a thousand of ten thousand — to take provisions for the people, so that when they come to Geba of Benjamin they may do according to all the outrage it has done in Israel."
So all the men of Israel were gathered against the city, as one man, joined together.
And the tribes of Israel sent men through the whole tribe of Benjamin, saying: "What is this evil that has come to be among you?

So now — hand over the men, the sons of worthlessness, who are in Gibeah, that we may put them to death and burn away the evil from Israel." But Benjamin was not willing to listen to the voice of their brothers, the children of Israel;
and the sons of Benjamin gathered from the cities to Gibeah, to go out to battle with the children of Israel.
And the sons of Benjamin mustered on that day, from the cities, twenty-six thousand men drawing the sword — apart from the inhabitants of Gibeah, who mustered seven hundred chosen men.
Of all this people, seven hundred chosen men were bound in their right hand — left-handed; every one of them could sling a stone at a hair and not miss.
And the men of Israel mustered, apart from Benjamin, four hundred thousand men drawing the sword — every one of them a man of war.
And they rose and went up to Bethel and inquired of God; and the children of Israel said: "Who shall go up first for us, to the battle with the sons of Benjamin?" And YHWH said: "Judah first."
And the children of Israel rose in the morning and camped against Gibeah.
And the men of Israel went out to battle with Benjamin; and the men of Israel drew up in battle order against them at Gibeah.
And the sons of Benjamin came out from Gibeah — and they destroyed down to the ground, of Israel on that day, twenty-two thousand men.
But the people, the men of Israel, strengthened themselves and drew up battle again in the place where they had drawn up on the first day.

And the children of Israel went up and wept before YHWH until the evening, and inquired of YHWH: "Shall I again draw near for battle with the sons of Benjamin, my brother?" And YHWH said: "Go up against him."
And the children of Israel drew near to the sons of Benjamin on the second day;
and Benjamin came out to meet them from Gibeah on the second day and destroyed down to the ground, of the children of Israel, eighteen thousand men more — all of these drawing the sword.

Then all the children of Israel, and all the people, went up and came to Bethel; and they wept and sat there before YHWH, and fasted that day until evening, and offered up burnt offerings and peace offerings before YHWH.
And the children of Israel inquired of YHWH — for the ark of the covenant of God was there in those days,

and Phinehas son of Eleazar, son of Aaron, stood before it in those days — saying: "Shall I once more go out to battle with the sons of Benjamin, my brother — or shall I stop?" And YHWH said: "Go up — for tomorrow I will give him into your hand."
And Israel set ambushers against Gibeah, all around.
And the children of Israel went up against the sons of Benjamin on the third day, and drew up against Gibeah as at the other times.

And the sons of Benjamin came out to meet the people — they were drawn away from the city — and they began to strike down some of the people, slain as at the other times, on the highways (of which one goes up to Bethel and the other to Gibeah), in the field: about thirty men of Israel.
And the sons of Benjamin said: "They are struck down before us, as at the first." But the children of Israel said: "Let us flee — and draw them away from the city onto the highways."
And all the men of Israel rose from their place and drew up at Baal-tamar; and the ambush of Israel burst out from its place, from the bare ground of Geba.
And ten thousand chosen men from all Israel came in front of Gibeah, and the battle was heavy — and they did not know that the evil was touching them.
And YHWH struck Benjamin down before Israel; and the children of Israel destroyed in Benjamin on that day twenty-five thousand one hundred men — all of these drawing the sword.
And the sons of Benjamin saw that they were struck down. Now the men of Israel had given ground to Benjamin, because they trusted the ambush they had set against Gibeah;
and the ambush hurried and rushed on Gibeah, and the ambush drew out and struck the whole city with the mouth of the sword.
Now the appointed sign for the men of Israel with the ambush was that they should send up a great cloud of smoke from the city;

then the men of Israel would turn in the battle. And Benjamin had begun to strike down slain among the men of Israel — about thirty men — for they said: "Surely he is utterly struck down before us, as in the first battle."
But the cloud began to go up from the city — a pillar of smoke; and Benjamin turned and looked behind — and behold: the whole city going up to the heavens!
And the men of Israel turned — and the men of Benjamin were terrified, for they saw that the evil had touched them;
and they turned before the men of Israel toward the way of the wilderness — but the battle clung to them; and those from the cities were destroying them in the midst of it.
They encircled Benjamin, pursued him, trod him down at his resting place — as far as opposite Gibeah, toward the sunrise.
And there fell of Benjamin eighteen thousand men — all of these men of valor.

And they turned and fled to the wilderness, to the rock of Rimmon; and they gleaned of them on the highways five thousand men, and clung after them as far as Gidom, and struck down of them two thousand men.
So all who fell of Benjamin that day were twenty-five thousand men drawing the sword — all of these men of valor.
But six hundred men turned and fled to the wilderness, to the rock of Rimmon; and they stayed at the rock of Rimmon four months.

And the men of Israel turned back upon the sons of Benjamin and struck them with the mouth of the sword — from the whole city to the beasts, to all that was found; and all the cities that were found they set on fire.

jdg.21.text: Now the men of Israel had sworn at Mizpah: "No man of us shall give his daughter to Benjamin as a wife."
And the people came to Bethel and sat there until evening before God; and they lifted their voice and wept a great weeping,
and said: "Why, YHWH, God of Israel, has this come to pass in Israel — that today one tribe should be missing from Israel?"
And it came to pass the next day that the people rose early and built an altar there, and offered up burnt offerings and peace offerings.

And the children of Israel said: "Who is there, from all the tribes of Israel, who did not come up in the assembly to YHWH?" For the great oath had been sworn concerning whoever did not come up to YHWH at Mizpah: "He shall surely be put to death."
And the children of Israel felt sorrow for Benjamin their brother, and said: "Today one tribe has been hacked off from Israel.
What shall we do for wives for those who are left — since we have sworn by YHWH not to give them any of our daughters as wives?"
And they said: "Which one of the tribes of Israel did not come up to YHWH at Mizpah?" And behold — no man had come to the camp from Jabesh-gilead, to the assembly;
for the people were mustered — and behold, not a man was there of the inhabitants of Jabesh-gilead.

And the congregation sent there twelve thousand men of the sons of valor, and commanded them: "Go, and strike the inhabitants of Jabesh-gilead with the mouth of the sword — the women and the little ones too.
And this is the thing you shall do: every male, and every woman who has known the bed of a male, you shall devote to destruction."

And they found among the inhabitants of Jabesh-gilead four hundred young virgin women who had not known a man by the bed of a male; and they brought them to the camp at Shiloh, which is in the land of Canaan.
And the whole congregation sent word and spoke to the sons of Benjamin who were at the rock of Rimmon — and they proclaimed peace to them.
And Benjamin returned at that time; and they gave them the women they had kept alive from the women of Jabesh-gilead — but they did not find enough for them so.
And the people felt sorrow for Benjamin — for YHWH had made a breach in the tribes of Israel.
And the elders of the congregation said: "What shall we do for wives for those who are left — since women have been destroyed out of Benjamin?"
And they said: "There must be an inheritance for the survivors of Benjamin — that a tribe not be wiped out of Israel;
but we cannot give them wives from our daughters." For the children of Israel had sworn: "Cursed be the one who gives a wife to Benjamin."
And they said: "Behold — the festival of YHWH at Shiloh, from year to year — which is north of Bethel, toward the sunrise from the highway that goes up from Bethel to Shechem, and south of Lebonah."
And they commanded the sons of Benjamin: "Go, and lie in wait in the vineyards;

and watch — and behold, if the daughters of Shiloh come out to whirl in the dances, then come out of the vineyards, and snatch — each man his wife — from the daughters of Shiloh, and go to the land of Benjamin.

And it shall be, when their fathers or their brothers come to contend with us, that we will say to them: 'Be gracious to us regarding them — for we did not take each man his wife in the battle; and since you did not give them yourselves, at this time you bear no guilt.'"

And the sons of Benjamin did so, and carried off wives, according to their number, from the dancers whom they seized; and they went and returned to their inheritance, and built the cities, and settled in them.
And the children of Israel walked away from there at that time, each man to his tribe and to his clan; and they went out from there, each man to his inheritance.
In those days there was no king in Israel; each man did what was right in his own eyes.

jos.1.text: And it came to pass, after the death of Moses the servant of YHWH, that YHWH said to Joshua son of Nun, Moses' attendant:
"Moses my servant is dead. So now — rise, cross this Jordan, you and all this people, into the land that I am giving to them, to the children of Israel.
Every place the sole of your foot treads — to you I have given it, just as I spoke to Moses:
from the wilderness, and this Lebanon, as far as the great river, the river Euphrates — all the land of the Hittites — and to the Great Sea, toward the going down of the sun, shall be your territory.
No man shall stand before you all the days of your life. As I was with Moses, I will be with you; I will not let you drop, and I will not abandon you.
Be strong and courageous — for you shall give this people the land I swore to their fathers to give them, as an inheritance.
Only be strong and very courageous, keeping to do according to all the teaching that Moses my servant commanded you. Do not turn from it, right or left — so that you may act wisely everywhere you go.

This scroll of the teaching shall not move from your mouth: murmur it day and night, so that you keep to do according to all that is written in it — for then you shall make your way prosper, and then you shall act wisely.
Have I not commanded you? Be strong and courageous! Do not tremble and do not be shattered — for YHWH your God is with you everywhere you go."
And Joshua commanded the officers of the people:

"Cross through the midst of the camp and command the people: 'Prepare provisions for yourselves — for in three more days you cross this Jordan, to go in and possess the land that YHWH your God is giving you to possess.'"
And to the Reubenite and the Gadite and the half-tribe of Manasseh, Joshua said:
"Remember the word Moses the servant of YHWH commanded you: 'YHWH your God is giving you rest, and has given you this land.'
Your wives, your little ones, and your livestock shall stay in the land Moses gave you beyond the Jordan; but you shall cross armed before your brothers — all the mighty men of valor — and help them,

until YHWH gives rest to your brothers as to you, and they too possess the land YHWH your God is giving them. Then you shall return to the land of your possession and possess it — the land Moses the servant of YHWH gave you beyond the Jordan, toward the sunrise."
And they answered Joshua: "All that you have commanded us, we will do; and wherever you send us, we will go.
Just as we listened to Moses in everything, so will we listen to you. Only — may YHWH your God be with you, as he was with Moses.
Any man who rebels against your mouth and does not listen to your words, in all you command him — he shall be put to death. Only be strong and courageous!"

jos.2.text: And Joshua son of Nun sent from Shittim two men — spies — secretly, saying: "Go, see the land — and Jericho." And they went, and came into the house of a prostitute woman whose name was Rahab; and they lay down there.
And it was told to the king of Jericho: "Behold — men have come here tonight, from the children of Israel, to dig out the land."
And the king of Jericho sent to Rahab: "Bring out the men who came to you — who came into your house — for they have come to dig out the whole land!"
But the woman had taken the two men and hidden them; and she said: "True — the men came to me, but I did not know where they were from;
and as the gate was about to close, at dark, the men went out. I do not know where the men went. Chase after them quickly — for you will overtake them."
But she had brought them up to the roof and hidden them in the stalks of flax laid out for her on the roof.
And the men chased after them on the Jordan road, toward the fords; and the gate was shut as soon as the pursuers had gone out after them.
And before the two lay down to sleep, she came up to them on the roof;
and she said to the men: "I know that YHWH has given you the land — and that the dread of you has fallen on us, and that all the inhabitants of the land melt away before you.

For we heard how YHWH dried up the waters of the Sea of Reeds before you when you came out of Egypt, and what you did to the two kings of the Amorites beyond the Jordan — to Sihon and to Og — whom you devoted to destruction;
and we heard, and our heart melted, and no spirit rose any more in any man because of you. For YHWH your God — he is God in the heavens above and on the earth beneath.
So now — swear to me, please, by YHWH: since I have shown you steadfast love, you also will show steadfast love to my father's house; and give me a true sign,
and keep alive my father and my mother, and my brothers and my sisters, and all who belong to them — and rescue our lives from death."
And the men said to her: "Our life for yours — to the death! — if you do not tell this business of ours. And it shall be, when YHWH gives us the land, that we will show you steadfast love and truth."
Then she let them down by the rope through the window — for her house was in the wall of the city rampart; in the rampart itself she lived.
And she said to them: "Go to the hill country, lest the pursuers strike upon you; and hide yourselves there three days, until the pursuers return — and afterward go on your way."
And the men said to her: "We will be clear of this oath of yours which you have made us swear —

behold: when we come into the land, tie this cord of scarlet thread in the window through which you let us down; and gather to yourself, into the house, your father and your mother and your brothers and all your father's house.

And it shall be: anyone who goes out of the doors of your house into the street — his blood on his own head, and we are clear; but anyone who is with you in the house — his blood on our head, if a hand is laid on him.
But if you tell this business of ours — then we will be clear of your oath which you made us swear."
And she said: "According to your words, so be it." And she sent them away, and they went; and she tied the scarlet cord in the window.
And they went and came to the hill country, and stayed there three days, until the pursuers returned; and the pursuers searched all along the road — and did not find them.
And the two men returned and came down from the hill country, and crossed over and came to Joshua son of Nun; and they recounted to him everything that had befallen them.
And they said to Joshua: "Indeed YHWH has given the whole land into our hand — and indeed all the inhabitants of the land melt away before us."

jos.3.text: And Joshua rose early in the morning, and they set out from Shittim and came to the Jordan — he and all the children of Israel; and they lodged there before crossing.
And it came to pass, at the end of three days, that the officers crossed through the midst of the camp,
and commanded the people: "When you see the ark of the covenant of YHWH your God, with the levitical priests carrying it, then you shall set out from your place and walk after it —
only let there be a distance between you and it of about two thousand cubits by measure. Do not come near it — so that you may know the way you must walk, for you have not crossed this way before."
And Joshua said to the people: "Consecrate yourselves — for tomorrow YHWH will do wonders in your midst."
And Joshua said to the priests: "Lift the ark of the covenant, and cross before the people." And they lifted the ark of the covenant and walked before the people.
And YHWH said to Joshua: "This day I begin to make you great in the eyes of all Israel — so that they may know that as I was with Moses, I will be with you.
And you — command the priests carrying the ark of the covenant: 'When you come to the edge of the waters of the Jordan, in the Jordan you shall stand.'"
And Joshua said to the children of Israel: "Draw near here, and hear the words of YHWH your God."

And Joshua said: "By this you shall know that a living God is in your midst — and that he will surely dispossess before you the Canaanite and the Hittite and the Hivite and the Perizzite and the Girgashite and the Amorite and the Jebusite:
behold — the ark of the covenant of the Lord of all the earth crossing before you into the Jordan.
So now, take for yourselves twelve men from the tribes of Israel — one man, one man, per tribe.

And it shall be: when the soles of the feet of the priests carrying the ark of YHWH, the Lord of all the earth, come to rest in the waters of the Jordan, the waters of the Jordan will be cut off — the waters coming down from above — and they will stand as one heap."
And it came to pass, when the people set out from their tents to cross the Jordan, with the priests carrying the ark of the covenant before the people,

and when the carriers of the ark came to the Jordan, and the feet of the priests carrying the ark dipped into the edge of the waters — now the Jordan runs full over all its banks all the days of harvest —

that the waters coming down from above stood still: they rose up as one heap, very far away, at Adam, the city beside Zarethan; and the waters going down to the sea of the Arabah — the Salt Sea — were completely cut off. And the people crossed opposite Jericho.

And the priests carrying the ark of the covenant of YHWH stood firm on dry ground in the middle of the Jordan, while all Israel crossed on dry ground — until the whole nation had finished crossing the Jordan.

jos.4.text: And it came to pass, when the whole nation had finished crossing the Jordan, that YHWH said to Joshua:
"Take for yourselves from the people twelve men — one man, one man, per tribe —

and command them: 'Lift for yourselves, from here, from the middle of the Jordan — from the standing place of the priests' feet — twelve stones, made ready; and carry them across with you, and set them down at the lodging place where you lodge tonight.'"
And Joshua called the twelve men whom he had appointed from the children of Israel — one man, one man, per tribe;

and Joshua said to them: "Cross before the ark of YHWH your God into the middle of the Jordan, and each of you raise one stone onto his shoulder, according to the number of the tribes of the children of Israel —
so that this may be a sign in your midst. When your children ask, in time to come, 'What are these stones to you?' —

you shall say to them: 'That the waters of the Jordan were cut off before the ark of the covenant of YHWH; when it crossed into the Jordan, the waters of the Jordan were cut off.' And these stones shall be a memorial to the children of Israel — to the age."

And the children of Israel did so, just as Joshua commanded: they lifted twelve stones from the middle of the Jordan, as YHWH had spoken to Joshua, according to the number of the tribes of the children of Israel; and they carried them across with them to the lodging place and set them down there.
And twelve stones Joshua set up in the middle of the Jordan, under the standing place of the feet of the priests carrying the ark of the covenant — and they are there to this day.

And the priests carrying the ark stood in the middle of the Jordan until every word was finished that YHWH commanded Joshua to speak to the people — according to all that Moses had commanded Joshua; and the people hurried and crossed.
And it came to pass, when all the people had finished crossing, that the ark of YHWH crossed — with the priests — before the people.
And the sons of Reuben and the sons of Gad and the half-tribe of Manasseh crossed armed before the children of Israel, as Moses had spoken to them:
about forty thousand equipped for war crossed before YHWH, for battle, to the plains of Jericho.
On that day YHWH made Joshua great in the eyes of all Israel; and they held him in awe, as they had held Moses in awe, all the days of his life.
And YHWH said to Joshua:
"Command the priests carrying the ark of the testimony — and let them come up from the Jordan."
And Joshua commanded the priests: "Come up from the Jordan."

And it came to pass, when the priests carrying the ark of the covenant of YHWH came up from the middle of the Jordan — the soles of the priests' feet were drawn up onto the dry ground — that the waters of the Jordan returned to their place and ran, as before, over all its banks.
And the people came up from the Jordan on the tenth of the first month, and camped at Gilgal, at the eastern edge of Jericho.
And these twelve stones, which they took from the Jordan, Joshua set up at Gilgal.
And he said to the children of Israel: "When your children ask their fathers, in time to come, 'What are these stones?' —
you shall let your children know: 'On dry land Israel crossed this Jordan' —
for YHWH your God dried up the waters of the Jordan before you until you had crossed, just as YHWH your God did to the Sea of Reeds, which he dried up before us until we had crossed —
so that all the peoples of the earth may know the hand of YHWH, that it is strong; so that you may fear YHWH your God all the days."

jos.5.text: And it came to pass, when all the kings of the Amorites beyond the Jordan to the west, and all the kings of the Canaanites by the sea, heard how YHWH had dried up the waters of the Jordan before the children of Israel until they had crossed, that their heart melted, and there was no spirit in them any more, because of the children of Israel.
At that time YHWH said to Joshua: "Make yourself knives of flint, and again circumcise the children of Israel — a second time."
And Joshua made himself knives of flint and circumcised the children of Israel at the Hill of the Foreskins.
And this is the reason Joshua circumcised: all the people who came out of Egypt — the males, all the men of war — had died in the wilderness, on the way, after they came out of Egypt.
For all the people who came out had been circumcised; but all the people born in the wilderness, on the way, after they came out of Egypt — they had not been circumcised.

For forty years the children of Israel walked in the wilderness, until the whole nation — the men of war who came out of Egypt — was finished, because they did not listen to the voice of YHWH; to whom YHWH swore that he would not let them see the land that YHWH had sworn to their fathers to give us — a land flowing with milk and honey.
And their sons he raised up in their place: these Joshua circumcised — for they were uncircumcised, since they had not been circumcised on the way.
And it came to pass, when the whole nation had finished being circumcised, that they stayed in their places in the camp until they were healed.
And YHWH said to Joshua: "Today I have rolled away the reproach of Egypt from upon you." And the name of that place was called Gilgal — Rolling — to this day.
And the children of Israel camped at Gilgal; and they kept the Passover on the fourteenth day of the month, in the evening, on the plains of Jericho.
And they ate from the produce of the land on the day after the Passover — unleavened bread and roasted grain — on that very day.
And the manna ceased on the next day, when they ate from the produce of the land; and there was no more manna for the children of Israel — and they ate of the yield of the land of Canaan that year.

And it came to pass, when Joshua was by Jericho, that he lifted his eyes and looked — and behold: a man standing opposite him, his sword drawn in his hand. And Joshua went to him and said to him: "Are you for us — or for our adversaries?"
And he said: "No — for I am the commander of the army of YHWH; now I have come." And Joshua fell on his face to the ground and worshiped, and said to him: "What does my lord speak to his servant?"
And the commander of the army of YHWH said to Joshua: "Pull your sandal off your foot — for the place on which you are standing is holy." And Joshua did so.

jos.6.text: Now Jericho was shut up tight because of the children of Israel: none going out, and none coming in.
And YHWH said to Joshua: "See — I have given into your hand Jericho and her king, the mighty men of valor.
And you shall circle the city — all the men of war — going around the city once. So you shall do six days;
and seven priests shall carry seven ram's-horn shofars before the ark. And on the seventh day you shall circle the city seven times, and the priests shall blow the shofars.

And it shall be: at the long blast on the ram's horn — when you hear the sound of the shofar — all the people shall shout a great shout; and the wall of the city will fall flat beneath itself, and the people shall go up, each man straight ahead."
And Joshua son of Nun called the priests and said to them: "Lift the ark of the covenant, and let seven priests carry seven ram's-horn shofars before the ark of YHWH."
And he said to the people: "Cross over and circle the city — and let the armed men cross before the ark of YHWH."

And it came to pass, when Joshua had spoken to the people, that the seven priests carrying the seven ram's-horn shofars before YHWH crossed over and blew the shofars — and the ark of the covenant of YHWH walked behind them,
and the armed men walked before the priests blowing the shofars, and the rear guard walked behind the ark — walking on, and blowing the shofars.
And Joshua commanded the people: "You shall not shout, and you shall not let your voice be heard, and no word shall go out of your mouth — until the day I say to you, 'Shout!' Then you shall shout."
So the ark of YHWH circled the city, going around once; and they came into the camp, and lodged in the camp.
And Joshua rose early in the morning, and the priests lifted the ark of YHWH;

and the seven priests carrying the seven ram's-horn shofars before the ark of YHWH walked on and on, and blew the shofars — with the armed men walking before them, and the rear guard walking behind the ark of YHWH, walking on, and blowing the shofars.
And they circled the city on the second day, once, and returned to the camp. So they did six days.
And it came to pass on the seventh day that they rose early, at the going up of the dawn, and circled the city in this same manner seven times — only on that day they circled the city seven times.
And it came to pass at the seventh time, when the priests blew the shofars, that Joshua said to the people: "Shout! For YHWH has given you the city.

And the city shall be devoted to destruction — it and all that is in it — to YHWH. Only Rahab the prostitute shall live, she and all who are with her in the house, because she hid the messengers we sent.

But you — only keep yourselves from the devoted things, lest you devote yourselves to destruction by taking from the devoted things, and make the camp of Israel devoted to destruction, and trouble it.
And all silver and gold, and vessels of bronze and iron — they are holy to YHWH; they shall go into the treasury of YHWH."

And the people shouted, and they blew the shofars. And it came to pass, when the people heard the sound of the shofar, that the people shouted a great shout — and the wall fell flat beneath itself; and the people went up into the city, each man straight ahead — and they captured the city.
And they devoted to destruction all that was in the city — from man to woman, from young to old, and to ox and sheep and donkey — with the mouth of the sword.
And to the two men who had spied out the land Joshua said: "Go into the house of the prostitute woman, and bring out from there the woman and all who belong to her, as you swore to her."

And the young men, the spies, went in and brought out Rahab and her father and her mother and her brothers and all who belonged to her — all her family they brought out; and they set them outside the camp of Israel.
And the city they burned with fire, and all that was in it; only the silver and the gold, and the vessels of bronze and iron, they gave to the treasury of the house of YHWH.

But Rahab the prostitute, and her father's house, and all who belonged to her, Joshua kept alive; and she has lived in the midst of Israel to this day — because she hid the messengers Joshua sent to spy out Jericho.

And Joshua swore an oath at that time: "Cursed before YHWH be the man who rises and builds this city — Jericho: at the cost of his firstborn he shall lay her foundation, and at the cost of his youngest he shall set up her doors."
And YHWH was with Joshua — and the report of him went through all the land.

jos.7.text: But the children of Israel broke faith with the devoted things: Achan son of Carmi, son of Zabdi, son of Zerah, of the tribe of Judah, took from the devoted things — and the anger of YHWH burned against the children of Israel.
And Joshua sent men from Jericho to Ai, which is beside Beth-aven, east of Bethel; and he said to them: "Go up and spy out the land." And the men went up and spied out Ai.

And they returned to Joshua and said to him: "Do not let all the people go up. Let about two thousand men, or about three thousand men, go up and strike Ai. Do not weary all the people there — for they are few."
So about three thousand men of the people went up there — and they fled before the men of Ai;

and the men of Ai struck down about thirty-six men of them, and chased them from before the gate as far as the quarries, and struck them down on the slope. And the heart of the people melted and became water.
And Joshua tore his garments and fell on his face to the ground before the ark of YHWH until the evening — he and the elders of Israel — and they put dust on their heads.
And Joshua said: "Ah, Lord YHWH! Why did you ever bring this people across the Jordan — to give us into the hand of the Amorites, to destroy us? If only we had been content to stay beyond the Jordan!
Please, my Lord — what can I say, now that Israel has turned its neck before its enemies?
The Canaanites and all the inhabitants of the land will hear, and will surround us and cut off our name from the earth. And what will you do for your great name?"
And YHWH said to Joshua: "Get up! Why is this — that you are fallen on your face?

Israel has sinned. Yes — they have crossed my covenant which I commanded them; yes — they have taken from the devoted things; yes — they have stolen; yes — they have lied; yes — they have put it among their own gear.

And the children of Israel cannot stand before their enemies; they turn their neck before their enemies — because they have become a thing devoted to destruction. I will not be with you any more unless you destroy the devoted thing from your midst.

Rise, consecrate the people, and say: 'Consecrate yourselves for tomorrow. For thus says YHWH, the God of Israel: A devoted thing is in your midst, Israel! You cannot stand before your enemies until you remove the devoted thing from your midst.

And you shall be brought near in the morning by your tribes; and it shall be: the tribe YHWH takes shall come near by clans, and the clan YHWH takes shall come near by households, and the household YHWH takes shall come near man by man.
And the one taken with the devoted thing shall be burned with fire — he and all that belongs to him — because he crossed the covenant of YHWH, and because he committed an outrage in Israel.'"
And Joshua rose early in the morning and brought Israel near by its tribes — and the tribe of Judah was taken;
and he brought near the clans of Judah, and took the clan of the Zerahites; and he brought near the clan of the Zerahites man by man — and Zabdi was taken;
and he brought near his household man by man — and Achan son of Carmi, son of Zabdi, son of Zerah, of the tribe of Judah, was taken.
And Joshua said to Achan: "My son — give glory, please, to YHWH the God of Israel, and give him praise; and tell me, please, what you have done. Do not hide it from me."
And Achan answered Joshua and said: "Truly — I have sinned against YHWH, the God of Israel; and thus and so I did:

I saw among the spoil one beautiful mantle of Shinar, and two hundred shekels of silver, and one tongue of gold weighing fifty shekels — and I coveted them, and took them; and behold, they are hidden in the ground inside my tent, with the silver underneath."
And Joshua sent messengers, and they ran to the tent — and behold: hidden in his tent, with the silver underneath.
And they took them from inside the tent and brought them to Joshua and to all the children of Israel — and they poured them out before YHWH.

And Joshua — and all Israel with him — took Achan son of Zerah, and the silver and the mantle and the tongue of gold, and his sons and his daughters, and his ox and his donkey and his flock, and his tent and all that belonged to him; and they brought them up to the Valley of Achor.
And Joshua said: "Why have you troubled us? YHWH will trouble you this day." And all Israel stoned him with stone; and they burned them with fire, and heaped stones over them.

And they raised over him a great mound of stones — to this day; and YHWH turned from the burning of his anger. Therefore the name of that place is called the Valley of Achor — the Valley of Trouble — to this day.

jos.8.text: And YHWH said to Joshua: "Do not fear, and do not be shattered. Take with you all the people of war, and rise — go up to Ai. See: I have given into your hand the king of Ai and his people and his city and his land.
And you shall do to Ai and her king as you did to Jericho and her king — only its spoil and its livestock you may plunder for yourselves. Set yourself an ambush against the city, behind it."
And Joshua rose, and all the people of war, to go up to Ai; and Joshua chose thirty thousand men, mighty men of valor, and sent them out by night.
And he commanded them: "See — you are to lie in ambush against the city, behind the city. Do not go very far from the city; and all of you be ready.
And I, and all the people with me, will draw near to the city; and it shall be, when they come out to meet us as at the first, that we will flee before them.
And they will come out after us until we have drawn them away from the city — for they will say, 'They are fleeing before us as at the first' — and we will flee before them.
Then you shall rise from the ambush and take possession of the city; and YHWH your God will give it into your hand.
And it shall be, when you seize the city, that you shall set the city on fire. According to the word of YHWH you shall do. See — I have commanded you."
And Joshua sent them out, and they went to the place of ambush and stayed between Bethel and Ai, west of Ai; and Joshua lodged that night in the midst of the people.
And Joshua rose early in the morning and mustered the people; and he went up — he and the elders of Israel — before the people, to Ai.
And all the people of war who were with him went up and drew near, and came opposite the city; and they camped north of Ai — with the ravine between him and Ai.
And he took about five thousand men and set them as an ambush between Bethel and Ai, west of the city.
So the people set all the camp that was north of the city, and its heel — its rear guard — west of the city; and Joshua went that night into the middle of the valley.

And it came to pass, when the king of Ai saw it, that the men of the city hurried and rose early and went out to meet Israel for battle — he and all his people — at the appointed place before the Arabah; and he did not know that an ambush lay against him behind the city.
And Joshua and all Israel let themselves be struck before them — and they fled by the way of the wilderness;
and all the people who were in the city were called out to pursue after them; and they pursued after Joshua — and were drawn away from the city.
And not a man was left in Ai or Bethel who did not go out after Israel; and they left the city open, and pursued after Israel.
And YHWH said to Joshua: "Stretch out the javelin in your hand toward Ai — for into your hand I will give her." And Joshua stretched out the javelin in his hand toward the city;
and the ambush rose quickly from its place, and they ran as soon as he stretched out his hand, and entered the city and captured it; and they hurried and set the city on fire.

And the men of Ai turned and looked behind them — and behold: the smoke of the city going up to the heavens; and there was no strength in them to flee this way or that — for the people fleeing to the wilderness had turned on the pursuer.
And Joshua and all Israel saw that the ambush had captured the city, and that the smoke of the city was going up — and they turned back and struck the men of Ai;

and the others came out of the city to meet them, so that they were in the middle of Israel — some on this side, some on that; and they struck them down until no survivor or fugitive was left to them.
But the king of Ai they seized alive, and brought him near to Joshua.

And it came to pass, when Israel had finished killing all the inhabitants of Ai in the field, in the wilderness where they had pursued them — and they all fell by the mouth of the sword until they were finished — that all Israel returned to Ai and struck it with the mouth of the sword.
And all who fell that day, from man to woman, were twelve thousand — all the people of Ai.
And Joshua did not draw back his hand, which he had stretched out with the javelin, until he had devoted all the inhabitants of Ai to destruction.
Only the livestock and the spoil of that city Israel plundered for themselves, according to the word of YHWH that he had commanded Joshua.
And Joshua burned Ai, and made it a mound for the age — a desolation to this day.

And the king of Ai he hanged on a tree until evening time; and at sundown Joshua gave command, and they took his corpse down from the tree and threw it at the opening of the city gate — and raised over it a great mound of stones, to this day.
Then Joshua built an altar to YHWH, the God of Israel, on Mount Ebal —

as Moses the servant of YHWH had commanded the children of Israel, as it is written in the scroll of the teaching of Moses: an altar of whole stones, over which no iron had been swung; and they offered up on it burnt offerings to YHWH, and sacrificed peace offerings.
And he wrote there, on the stones, a copy of the teaching of Moses, which he had written before the children of Israel.

And all Israel — its elders and officers and judges — stood on this side and that of the ark, facing the levitical priests carrying the ark of the covenant of YHWH — sojourner and native alike — half of them facing Mount Gerizim and half facing Mount Ebal, as Moses the servant of YHWH had commanded, to bless the people Israel first of all.
And afterward he read out all the words of the teaching — the blessing and the curse — according to all that is written in the scroll of the teaching.

There was not a word of all that Moses had commanded that Joshua did not read out in front of the whole assembly of Israel — with the women and the little ones, and the sojourner walking in their midst.

jos.9.text: And it came to pass, when all the kings beyond the Jordan heard — in the hill country and in the lowland and along all the shore of the Great Sea toward Lebanon: the Hittite and the Amorite, the Canaanite, the Perizzite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite —
that they gathered together to fight with Joshua and with Israel — with one mouth.
But the inhabitants of Gibeon heard what Joshua had done to Jericho and to Ai;
and they, too, acted — with cunning: they went and disguised themselves as envoys, and took worn-out sacks for their donkeys, and wineskins worn out and split and tied up,
and worn-out, patched sandals on their feet, and worn-out garments on themselves; and all the bread of their provisions was dry — it had become crumbs.
And they went to Joshua, to the camp at Gilgal, and said to him and to the men of Israel: "From a far country we have come. So now — cut a covenant with us."
And the men of Israel said to the Hivites: "Perhaps you live in our midst — how then can we cut a covenant with you?"
And they said to Joshua: "We are your servants." And Joshua said to them: "Who are you — and where do you come from?"
And they said to him: "From a very far country your servants have come, for the name of YHWH your God — for we have heard the report of him, and all that he did in Egypt,
and all that he did to the two kings of the Amorites beyond the Jordan: to Sihon king of Heshbon, and to Og king of Bashan, who was in Ashtaroth.

And our elders and all the inhabitants of our land said to us: 'Take provisions in your hand for the road, and go to meet them, and say to them: We are your servants. So now — cut a covenant with us.'
This bread of ours — we took it hot as provision from our houses on the day we set out to come to you; and now, behold: it is dry, and has become crumbs.
And these wineskins, which we filled new — behold, they are split; and these garments of ours, and our sandals, are worn out from the very great length of the road."
And the men took some of their provisions — but the mouth of YHWH they did not ask.
And Joshua made peace with them, and cut a covenant with them to let them live; and the leaders of the congregation swore to them.
And it came to pass, at the end of three days after they had cut the covenant with them, that they heard that they were near neighbors — living in their very midst.
And the children of Israel set out and came to their cities on the third day; and their cities were Gibeon and Chephirah and Beeroth and Kiriath-jearim.
And the children of Israel did not strike them — because the leaders of the congregation had sworn to them by YHWH, the God of Israel; and the whole congregation grumbled against the leaders.
And all the leaders said to the whole congregation: "We have sworn to them by YHWH, the God of Israel — and now we cannot touch them.
This is what we will do to them: let them live — so that wrath not come upon us over the oath we swore to them."
And the leaders said to them: "Let them live." And they became woodcutters and water-drawers for the whole congregation, as the leaders had spoken concerning them.
And Joshua called them and spoke to them: "Why did you deceive us, saying, 'We are very far from you' — when you live in our very midst?
So now — cursed are you: there shall never be cut off from you slave, woodcutter, and water-drawer for the house of my God."

And they answered Joshua and said: "Because it was clearly told to your servants that YHWH your God had commanded Moses his servant to give you all the land, and to destroy all the inhabitants of the land before you — and we feared greatly for our lives because of you; and so we did this thing.
And now — behold, we are in your hand: do to us whatever is good and right in your eyes to do."
And he did so to them: he rescued them from the hand of the children of Israel, and they did not kill them.
And Joshua made them, on that day, woodcutters and water-drawers for the congregation — and for the altar of YHWH, to this day, at the place he would choose.

jos.10.text: And it came to pass, when Adoni-zedek king of Jerusalem heard that Joshua had captured Ai and devoted it to destruction — that as he had done to Jericho and her king, so he did to Ai and her king — and that the inhabitants of Gibeon had made peace with Israel and were in their midst,
that they feared greatly; for Gibeon was a great city, like one of the royal cities — indeed, greater than Ai — and all her men were mighty.
And Adoni-zedek king of Jerusalem sent to Hoham king of Hebron, and to Piram king of Jarmuth, and to Japhia king of Lachish, and to Debir king of Eglon:
"Come up to me and help me, and let us strike Gibeon — for it has made peace with Joshua and with the children of Israel."

And the five kings of the Amorites gathered and went up — the king of Jerusalem, the king of Hebron, the king of Jarmuth, the king of Lachish, the king of Eglon — they and all their camps; and they camped against Gibeon, and made war on her.

And the men of Gibeon sent to Joshua, to the camp at Gilgal: "Do not let your hands drop from your servants! Come up to us quickly, and save us, and help us — for all the kings of the Amorites who live in the hill country have gathered against us."
And Joshua went up from Gilgal — he and all the people of war with him, all the mighty men of valor.
And YHWH said to Joshua: "Do not fear them — for into your hand I have given them; not a man of them shall stand before you."
And Joshua came upon them suddenly — all night he had marched up from Gilgal;
and YHWH threw them into panic before Israel, and struck them a great blow at Gibeon, and pursued them by the way of the ascent of Beth-horon, and struck them as far as Azekah and as far as Makkedah.

And it came to pass, as they fled before Israel — they were on the descent of Beth-horon — that YHWH threw down on them great stones from the heavens, as far as Azekah, and they died; more died by the hailstones than the children of Israel killed with the sword.

Then Joshua spoke to YHWH, on the day YHWH gave the Amorites over before the children of Israel; and he said, in the eyes of Israel: "Sun — stand still at Gibeon! And moon — in the Valley of Aijalon!"

And the sun stood still, and the moon stood, until the nation took vengeance on its enemies. Is it not written in the Scroll of the Upright? And the sun stood in the middle of the heavens, and did not hurry to set, for about a whole day.
And there has been no day like that one, before it or after it — YHWH listening to the voice of a man; for YHWH fought for Israel.
And Joshua returned, and all Israel with him, to the camp at Gilgal.
But those five kings fled, and hid themselves in the cave at Makkedah.
And it was told to Joshua: "The five kings have been found — hidden in the cave at Makkedah."
And Joshua said: "Roll great stones against the mouth of the cave, and appoint men over it to guard them.
And you — do not stand still! Pursue after your enemies and strike their rear. Do not let them enter their cities — for YHWH your God has given them into your hand."

And it came to pass, when Joshua and the children of Israel had finished striking them with a very great blow, until they were finished — though the survivors of them survived and entered the fortified cities —
that all the people returned to the camp, to Joshua at Makkedah, in peace. No one so much as sharpened his tongue against the children of Israel — against a single man.
And Joshua said: "Open the mouth of the cave, and bring out to me these five kings from the cave."
And they did so, and brought out to him these five kings from the cave: the king of Jerusalem, the king of Hebron, the king of Jarmuth, the king of Lachish, the king of Eglon.

And it came to pass, when they had brought these kings out to Joshua, that Joshua called all the men of Israel, and said to the commanders of the men of war who had gone with him: "Come near — set your feet on the necks of these kings." And they came near and set their feet on their necks.
And Joshua said to them: "Do not fear, and do not be shattered. Be strong and courageous — for thus will YHWH do to all your enemies with whom you fight."
And Joshua struck them afterward and put them to death, and hanged them on five trees; and they hung on the trees until the evening.

And it came to pass, at the time of sundown, that Joshua gave command, and they took them down from the trees and threw them into the cave where they had hidden; and they set great stones against the mouth of the cave — there to this very day.

And Makkedah Joshua captured on that day, and struck it with the mouth of the sword — and her king; he devoted them to destruction, with every soul in it. He left no survivor; and he did to the king of Makkedah as he had done to the king of Jericho.
And Joshua crossed over — and all Israel with him — from Makkedah to Libnah, and fought with Libnah;

and YHWH gave it also into the hand of Israel, with her king; and he struck it with the mouth of the sword, and every soul in it — he left in it no survivor; and he did to her king as he had done to the king of Jericho.
And Joshua crossed over — and all Israel with him — from Libnah to Lachish; and he camped against it and fought against it;
and YHWH gave Lachish into the hand of Israel, and he captured it on the second day, and struck it with the mouth of the sword, and every soul in it — according to all he had done to Libnah.
Then Horam king of Gezer came up to help Lachish; and Joshua struck him and his people, until no survivor was left to him.
And Joshua crossed over — and all Israel with him — from Lachish to Eglon; and they camped against it and fought against it;
and they captured it on that day and struck it with the mouth of the sword; and every soul in it he devoted to destruction on that day — according to all he had done to Lachish.
And Joshua went up — and all Israel with him — from Eglon to Hebron; and they fought against it,

and captured it, and struck it with the mouth of the sword — and her king and all her towns and every soul in it. He left no survivor, according to all he had done to Eglon; and he devoted it to destruction, with every soul in it.
And Joshua turned back — and all Israel with him — to Debir, and fought against it;

and he captured it, and her king, and all her towns; and they struck them with the mouth of the sword and devoted to destruction every soul in it — he left no survivor. As he had done to Hebron, so he did to Debir and her king — and as he had done to Libnah and her king.

So Joshua struck the whole land — the hill country and the Negev and the lowland and the slopes — and all their kings. He left no survivor; and every breathing thing he devoted to destruction, as YHWH the God of Israel had commanded.
And Joshua struck them from Kadesh-barnea as far as Gaza, and all the land of Goshen as far as Gibeon.
And all these kings and their land Joshua captured at one time — for YHWH, the God of Israel, fought for Israel.
And Joshua returned, and all Israel with him, to the camp at Gilgal.

jos.11.text: And it came to pass, when Jabin king of Hazor heard, that he sent to Jobab king of Madon, and to the king of Shimron, and to the king of Achshaph,
and to the kings who were in the north — in the hill country, and in the Arabah south of Chinneroth, and in the lowland, and in the heights of Dor on the west:
the Canaanite in the east and the west, and the Amorite and the Hittite and the Perizzite and the Jebusite in the hill country, and the Hivite below Hermon, in the land of Mizpah.
And they went out — they and all their camps with them — a people many as the sand on the seashore in multitude, with very many horses and chariots;
and all these kings met by appointment, and came and camped together at the waters of Merom, to fight with Israel.

And YHWH said to Joshua: "Do not fear before them — for tomorrow at this time I am giving all of them over, slain, before Israel. Their horses you shall hamstring, and their chariots you shall burn with fire."
And Joshua came upon them — and all the people of war with him — at the waters of Merom, suddenly; and they fell on them.

And YHWH gave them into the hand of Israel, and they struck them and pursued them as far as Great Sidon and as far as Misrephoth-maim, and as far as the Valley of Mizpeh eastward; and they struck them until no survivor was left to them.
And Joshua did to them as YHWH had said to him: their horses he hamstrung, and their chariots he burned with fire.
And Joshua turned back at that time and captured Hazor, and struck her king with the sword — for Hazor was formerly the head of all these kingdoms.
And they struck every soul in it with the mouth of the sword, devoting them to destruction — not a breathing thing was left; and Hazor he burned with fire.
And all the cities of these kings, with all their kings, Joshua captured and struck with the mouth of the sword — he devoted them to destruction, as Moses the servant of YHWH had commanded.
Only — all the cities standing on their mounds Israel did not burn; except Hazor alone: her Joshua burned.

And all the spoil of these cities, and the livestock, the children of Israel plundered for themselves; only every human they struck with the mouth of the sword, until they had destroyed them — they left not a breathing thing.
As YHWH had commanded Moses his servant, so Moses commanded Joshua; and so Joshua did — he set aside nothing of all that YHWH had commanded Moses.
So Joshua took all this land: the hill country and all the Negev and all the land of Goshen and the lowland and the Arabah, and the hill country of Israel and its lowland —
from Mount Halak, which rises toward Seir, as far as Baal-gad in the Valley of Lebanon below Mount Hermon; and all their kings he captured, and struck them, and put them to death.
Many days Joshua made war with all these kings.
There was not a city that made peace with the children of Israel except the Hivites, the inhabitants of Gibeon — everything they took in battle;

for it was from YHWH — to harden their hearts to meet Israel in battle, so that he might devote them to destruction, so that they should find no favor, so that he might destroy them, as YHWH had commanded Moses.

And Joshua came at that time and cut off the Anakim from the hill country — from Hebron, from Debir, from Anab, and from all the hill country of Judah, and from all the hill country of Israel; with their cities Joshua devoted them to destruction.
No Anakim were left in the land of the children of Israel — only in Gaza, in Gath, and in Ashdod did any remain.
So Joshua took the whole land, according to all that YHWH had spoken to Moses; and Joshua gave it as an inheritance to Israel, by their divisions, by their tribes. And the land grew quiet from war.

jos.12.text: And these are the kings of the land whom the children of Israel struck, and whose land they possessed beyond the Jordan toward the sunrise — from the wadi Arnon to Mount Hermon, and all the eastern Arabah:

Sihon king of the Amorites, who lived in Heshbon — ruling from Aroer, which is on the edge of the wadi Arnon, and the middle of the wadi, and half of Gilead, as far as the wadi Jabbok, the border of the sons of Ammon;
and the Arabah as far as the Sea of Chinneroth eastward, and as far as the sea of the Arabah — the Salt Sea — eastward, by the road to Beth-jeshimoth; and from the south, below the slopes of Pisgah;
and the territory of Og king of Bashan, of the remnant of the Rephaim, who lived in Ashtaroth and in Edrei,
and ruled in Mount Hermon and in Salecah and in all Bashan, to the border of the Geshurite and the Maacathite, and half of Gilead — the border of Sihon king of Heshbon.
Moses the servant of YHWH, and the children of Israel, struck them; and Moses the servant of YHWH gave it as a possession to the Reubenite and the Gadite and the half-tribe of Manasseh.

And these are the kings of the land whom Joshua and the children of Israel struck beyond the Jordan to the west — from Baal-gad in the Valley of Lebanon as far as Mount Halak, which rises toward Seir; and Joshua gave it to the tribes of Israel as a possession, by their divisions:

in the hill country and in the lowland and in the Arabah and on the slopes and in the wilderness and in the Negev — the Hittite, the Amorite, and the Canaanite, the Perizzite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite:
the king of Jericho, one; the king of Ai, which is beside Bethel, one;
the king of Jerusalem, one; the king of Hebron, one;
the king of Jarmuth, one; the king of Lachish, one;
the king of Eglon, one; the king of Gezer, one;
the king of Debir, one; the king of Geder, one;
the king of Hormah, one; the king of Arad, one;
the king of Libnah, one; the king of Adullam, one;
the king of Makkedah, one; the king of Bethel, one;
the king of Tappuah, one; the king of Hepher, one;
the king of Aphek, one; the king of Lasharon, one;
the king of Madon, one; the king of Hazor, one;
the king of Shimron-meron, one; the king of Achshaph, one;
the king of Taanach, one; the king of Megiddo, one;
the king of Kedesh, one; the king of Jokneam in Carmel, one;
the king of Dor in the heights of Dor, one; the king of Goiim in Gilgal, one;
the king of Tirzah, one. All the kings: thirty-one.

jos.13.text: Now Joshua was old, come along in days; and YHWH said to him: "You have grown old — you have come along in days; and very much of the land still remains to be possessed.
This is the land that remains: all the districts of the Philistines, and all the Geshurite —

from the Shihor, which faces Egypt, to the border of Ekron northward, counted as Canaanite: the five lords of the Philistines — the Gazite and the Ashdodite, the Ashkelonite, the Gittite, and the Ekronite — and the Avvim;
from the south, all the land of the Canaanites, and Mearah, which belongs to the Sidonians, as far as Aphek, to the border of the Amorites;
and the land of the Gebalite, and all Lebanon toward the sunrise, from Baal-gad below Mount Hermon to Lebo-hamath.

All the inhabitants of the hill country from Lebanon to Misrephoth-maim — all the Sidonians — I myself will dispossess them before the children of Israel. Only allot it to Israel as an inheritance, as I have commanded you.
So now — divide this land as an inheritance to the nine tribes and the half-tribe of Manasseh."
With the other half, the Reubenite and the Gadite took their inheritance, which Moses gave them beyond the Jordan eastward — as Moses the servant of YHWH gave them:
from Aroer, which is on the edge of the wadi Arnon, and the city that is in the middle of the wadi, and all the tableland from Medeba to Dibon;
and all the cities of Sihon king of the Amorites, who reigned in Heshbon, as far as the border of the sons of Ammon;
and Gilead, and the territory of the Geshurite and the Maacathite, and all Mount Hermon, and all Bashan as far as Salecah;
all the kingdom of Og in Bashan, who reigned in Ashtaroth and in Edrei — he was left of the remnant of the Rephaim; and Moses struck them and dispossessed them.
But the children of Israel did not dispossess the Geshurite and the Maacathite; and Geshur and Maacath have lived in the midst of Israel to this day.
Only to the tribe of Levi he gave no inheritance: the fire-offerings of YHWH, the God of Israel — that is its inheritance, as he spoke to it.
And Moses gave to the tribe of the sons of Reuben, by their clans;
and their territory was from Aroer, which is on the edge of the wadi Arnon, and the city in the middle of the wadi, and all the tableland by Medeba:
Heshbon and all her towns in the tableland; Dibon and Bamoth-baal and Beth-baal-meon,
and Jahaz and Kedemoth and Mephaath,
and Kiriathaim and Sibmah and Zereth-shahar on the hill of the valley,
and Beth-peor and the slopes of Pisgah and Beth-jeshimoth —

and all the cities of the tableland, and all the kingdom of Sihon king of the Amorites, who reigned in Heshbon, whom Moses struck — him and the leaders of Midian: Evi and Rekem and Zur and Hur and Reba, the princes of Sihon who lived in the land.
And Balaam son of Beor, the diviner, the children of Israel killed with the sword, among their slain.
And the border of the sons of Reuben was the Jordan, as a boundary. This is the inheritance of the sons of Reuben by their clans — the cities and their villages.
And Moses gave to the tribe of Gad — to the sons of Gad — by their clans;
and their territory was Jazer, and all the cities of Gilead, and half the land of the sons of Ammon, as far as Aroer, which faces Rabbah;
and from Heshbon to Ramath-mizpeh and Betonim, and from Mahanaim to the border of Lidebir;

and in the valley: Beth-haram and Beth-nimrah and Succoth and Zaphon — the rest of the kingdom of Sihon king of Heshbon — with the Jordan as a boundary, to the end of the Sea of Chinnereth, beyond the Jordan eastward.
This is the inheritance of the sons of Gad by their clans — the cities and their villages.
And Moses gave to the half-tribe of Manasseh; and it belonged to the half-tribe of the sons of Manasseh, by their clans;
