and their territory was from Mahanaim: all Bashan — all the kingdom of Og king of Bashan — and all the Havvoth-jair in Bashan: sixty cities;
and half of Gilead, and Ashtaroth and Edrei — the royal cities of Og in Bashan — to the sons of Machir son of Manasseh: to half of the sons of Machir, by their clans.
These are what Moses gave as inheritance in the plains of Moab, beyond the Jordan at Jericho, eastward.
But to the tribe of Levi Moses gave no inheritance: YHWH, the God of Israel — he is their inheritance, as he spoke to them.

jos.14.text: And these are what the children of Israel inherited in the land of Canaan — which Eleazar the priest, and Joshua son of Nun, and the heads of the fathers' houses of the tribes of the children of Israel gave them to inherit,
by the lot of their inheritance, as YHWH had commanded by the hand of Moses, for the nine tribes and the half-tribe.
For Moses had given the inheritance of the two tribes and the half-tribe beyond the Jordan; but to the Levites he gave no inheritance among them.

For the sons of Joseph were two tribes — Manasseh and Ephraim; and they gave the Levites no portion in the land, except cities to live in, with their pasturelands for their livestock and their property.
As YHWH had commanded Moses, so the children of Israel did — and they divided the land.

And the sons of Judah drew near to Joshua at Gilgal; and Caleb son of Jephunneh the Kenizzite said to him: "You know the word that YHWH spoke to Moses, the man of God, about me and about you at Kadesh-barnea.
Forty years old I was when Moses the servant of YHWH sent me from Kadesh-barnea to spy out the land — and I brought back word to him just as it was with my heart;
but my brothers who went up with me made the heart of the people melt. And I — I followed fully after YHWH my God.
And Moses swore on that day: 'Surely the land your foot has trodden shall be an inheritance for you and for your sons to the age — because you have followed fully after YHWH my God.'

And now — behold, YHWH has kept me alive, as he spoke, these forty-five years since YHWH spoke this word to Moses, while Israel walked in the wilderness. And now — behold, I am today eighty-five years old.
I am still as strong today as on the day Moses sent me: as my strength was then, so my strength is now — for battle, and for going out and coming in.

So now — give me this hill country of which YHWH spoke on that day. For you yourself heard on that day that Anakim are there, and great fortified cities. Perhaps YHWH will be with me — and I shall dispossess them, as YHWH spoke."
And Joshua blessed him; and he gave Hebron to Caleb son of Jephunneh as an inheritance.
Therefore Hebron has belonged to Caleb son of Jephunneh the Kenizzite as an inheritance to this day — because he followed fully after YHWH, the God of Israel.
And the name of Hebron was formerly Kiriath-arba — Arba was the greatest man among the Anakim. And the land grew quiet from war.

jos.15.text: And the lot for the tribe of the sons of Judah, by their clans, reached to the border of Edom — the wilderness of Zin, southward, at the far edge of the south.
And their southern border was from the end of the Salt Sea, from the tongue that faces southward;
and it went out south of the Ascent of Akrabbim, and crossed to Zin, and went up south of Kadesh-barnea, and crossed Hezron, and went up to Addar, and turned to Karka;
and it crossed to Azmon and went out at the Brook of Egypt; and the outgoings of the border were at the sea. This shall be your southern border.
And the eastern border: the Salt Sea, to the end of the Jordan. And the border on the north side: from the tongue of the sea, at the end of the Jordan;
and the border went up to Beth-hoglah and crossed north of Beth-arabah; and the border went up to the Stone of Bohan son of Reuben;

and the border went up to Debir from the Valley of Achor, and northward, turning toward Gilgal, which faces the Ascent of Adummim, which is south of the wadi; and the border crossed to the waters of En-shemesh, and its outgoings were at En-rogel;

and the border went up the Valley of Ben-hinnom to the southern shoulder of the Jebusite — that is, Jerusalem; and the border went up to the top of the mountain that faces the Valley of Hinnom on the west, at the north end of the Valley of Rephaim;

and the border was drawn from the top of the mountain to the spring of the waters of Nephtoah, and went out to the cities of Mount Ephron; and the border was drawn to Baalah — that is, Kiriath-jearim;
and the border circled from Baalah westward to Mount Seir, and crossed to the northern shoulder of Mount Jearim — that is, Chesalon — and went down to Beth-shemesh, and crossed Timnah;
and the border went out to the northern shoulder of Ekron; and the border was drawn to Shikkeron, and crossed Mount Baalah, and went out at Jabneel; and the outgoings of the border were at the sea.
And the western border: the Great Sea, as a boundary. This is the border of the sons of Judah all around, by their clans.
And to Caleb son of Jephunneh he gave a portion in the midst of the sons of Judah, according to the mouth of YHWH to Joshua: Kiriath-arba — Arba was the father of Anak — that is, Hebron.
And Caleb dispossessed from there the three sons of Anak: Sheshai and Ahiman and Talmai, children of Anak.
And he went up from there against the inhabitants of Debir; and the name of Debir was formerly Kiriath-sepher — Scroll Town.
And Caleb said: "Whoever strikes Kiriath-sepher and captures it — I will give him Achsah my daughter as a wife."
And Othniel son of Kenaz, Caleb's brother, captured it; and he gave him Achsah his daughter as a wife.
And it came to pass, when she came, that she urged him to ask her father for a field; and she slipped down from the donkey, and Caleb said to her: "What is it?"
And she said: "Give me a blessing! Since you have given me away as Negev-land — dry southland — give me also basins of water." And he gave her the upper basins and the lower basins.
This is the inheritance of the tribe of the sons of Judah, by their clans.
And the cities at the far edge of the tribe of the sons of Judah, toward the border of Edom in the Negev, were: Kabzeel and Eder and Jagur,
and Kinah and Dimonah and Adadah,
and Kedesh and Hazor and Ithnan,
Ziph and Telem and Bealoth,
and Hazor-hadattah and Kerioth-hezron — that is, Hazor —
Amam and Shema and Moladah,
and Hazar-gaddah and Heshmon and Beth-pelet,
and Hazar-shual and Beersheba and Biziothiah,
Baalah and Iim and Ezem,
and Eltolad and Chesil and Hormah,
and Ziklag and Madmannah and Sansannah,
and Lebaoth and Shilhim and Ain and Rimmon. All the cities: twenty-nine, with their villages.
In the lowland: Eshtaol and Zorah and Ashnah,
and Zanoah and En-gannim, Tappuah and Enam,
Jarmuth and Adullam, Socoh and Azekah,
and Shaaraim and Adithaim and Gederah and Gederothaim: fourteen cities with their villages.
Zenan and Hadashah and Migdal-gad,
and Dilean and Mizpeh and Joktheel,
Lachish and Bozkath and Eglon,
and Cabbon and Lahmas and Chitlish,
and Gederoth, Beth-dagon and Naamah and Makkedah: sixteen cities with their villages.
Libnah and Ether and Ashan,
and Iphtah and Ashnah and Nezib,
and Keilah and Achzib and Mareshah: nine cities with their villages.
Ekron, with her daughter-towns and her villages;
from Ekron and westward, all that lay alongside Ashdod, with their villages;
Ashdod, her daughter-towns and her villages; Gaza, her daughter-towns and her villages — as far as the Brook of Egypt, and the Great Sea as a boundary.
And in the hill country: Shamir and Jattir and Socoh,
and Dannah and Kiriath-sannah — that is, Debir —
and Anab and Eshtemoh and Anim,
and Goshen and Holon and Giloh: eleven cities with their villages.
Arab and Rumah and Eshan,
and Janim and Beth-tappuah and Aphekah,
and Humtah and Kiriath-arba — that is, Hebron — and Zior: nine cities with their villages.
Maon, Carmel and Ziph and Juttah,
and Jezreel and Jokdeam and Zanoah,
Kain, Gibeah and Timnah: ten cities with their villages.
Halhul, Beth-zur and Gedor,
and Maarath and Beth-anoth and Eltekon: six cities with their villages.
Kiriath-baal — that is, Kiriath-jearim — and Rabbah: two cities with their villages.
In the wilderness: Beth-arabah, Middin and Secacah,
and Nibshan and the City of Salt and En-gedi: six cities with their villages.
But the Jebusites, the inhabitants of Jerusalem — the sons of Judah could not dispossess them; and the Jebusites have lived with the sons of Judah in Jerusalem to this day.

jos.16.text: And the lot went out for the sons of Joseph: from the Jordan at Jericho, to the waters of Jericho on the east — the wilderness going up from Jericho into the hill country to Bethel;
and it went out from Bethel to Luz, and crossed to the border of the Archites at Ataroth,
and went down westward to the border of the Japhletites, as far as the border of Lower Beth-horon, and as far as Gezer; and its outgoings were at the sea.
And the sons of Joseph — Manasseh and Ephraim — took their inheritance.
And the border of the sons of Ephraim, by their clans, was: the border of their inheritance on the east was Ataroth-addar, as far as Upper Beth-horon;
and the border went out westward — Michmethath on the north; and the border circled eastward to Taanath-shiloh, and passed by it on the east to Janoah;
and it went down from Janoah to Ataroth and Naarah, and touched Jericho, and went out at the Jordan.
From Tappuah the border went westward to the wadi Kanah, and its outgoings were at the sea. This is the inheritance of the tribe of the sons of Ephraim, by their clans —
together with the cities set apart for the sons of Ephraim in the midst of the inheritance of the sons of Manasseh: all the cities with their villages.
But they did not dispossess the Canaanites living in Gezer; and the Canaanites have lived in the midst of Ephraim to this day — and became forced labor, serving.

jos.17.text: And the lot fell to the tribe of Manasseh — for he was Joseph's firstborn. To Machir, Manasseh's firstborn, the father of Gilead — because he was a man of war — went Gilead and Bashan.

And it fell to the rest of the sons of Manasseh, by their clans: to the sons of Abiezer, and to the sons of Helek, and to the sons of Asriel, and to the sons of Shechem, and to the sons of Hepher, and to the sons of Shemida — these were the male sons of Manasseh son of Joseph, by their clans.
But Zelophehad son of Hepher, son of Gilead, son of Machir, son of Manasseh, had no sons — only daughters; and these are the names of his daughters: Mahlah and Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah.

And they came near before Eleazar the priest, and before Joshua son of Nun, and before the leaders: "YHWH commanded Moses to give us an inheritance among our brothers." So, according to the mouth of YHWH, he gave them an inheritance among their father's brothers.
And ten portions fell to Manasseh, apart from the land of Gilead and Bashan, which is beyond the Jordan —
because the daughters of Manasseh inherited an inheritance among his sons; and the land of Gilead belonged to the rest of the sons of Manasseh.
And the border of Manasseh was from Asher to Michmethath, which faces Shechem; and the border went to the right, to the inhabitants of En-tappuah.
The land of Tappuah belonged to Manasseh — but Tappuah itself, on the border of Manasseh, belonged to the sons of Ephraim.

And the border went down to the wadi Kanah, south of the wadi — these cities belonged to Ephraim, in the midst of the cities of Manasseh — and the border of Manasseh was north of the wadi; and its outgoings were at the sea:
southward Ephraim's, and northward Manasseh's, with the sea as his boundary; and they touch Asher on the north and Issachar on the east.

And Manasseh had, within Issachar and within Asher: Beth-shean and her daughter-towns, and Ibleam and her daughter-towns, and the inhabitants of Dor and her daughter-towns, and the inhabitants of En-dor and her daughter-towns, and the inhabitants of Taanach and her daughter-towns, and the inhabitants of Megiddo and her daughter-towns — the three heights.
But the sons of Manasseh could not dispossess these cities; and the Canaanites were determined to stay in this land.
And it came to pass, when the children of Israel grew strong, that they put the Canaanites to forced labor — but they did not dispossess them at all.
And the sons of Joseph spoke to Joshua: "Why have you given me one lot and one portion as an inheritance — when I am a numerous people, since YHWH has blessed me this far?"

And Joshua said to them: "If you are a numerous people — go up to the forest, and clear ground for yourself there in the land of the Perizzites and the Rephaim, since the hill country of Ephraim is too cramped for you."

And the sons of Joseph said: "The hill country is not enough for us; and there are iron chariots among all the Canaanites who live in the valley land — those in Beth-shean and her daughter-towns, and those in the Valley of Jezreel."
And Joshua said to the house of Joseph — to Ephraim and to Manasseh: "You are a numerous people, and you have great strength. You shall not have one lot only —

for the hill country shall be yours. It is forest — clear it, and its farthest reaches shall be yours; for you shall dispossess the Canaanites, though they have iron chariots, though they are strong."

jos.18.text: And the whole congregation of the children of Israel assembled at Shiloh, and set up the tent of meeting there; and the land lay subdued before them.
And there remained among the children of Israel seven tribes that had not yet divided their inheritance.
And Joshua said to the children of Israel: "How long will you slack off from going in to possess the land that YHWH, the God of your fathers, has given you?
Provide yourselves three men per tribe, and I will send them; and let them rise and walk through the land and write it up according to their inheritance — and come back to me.
And let them divide it into seven portions: Judah shall stand on its border in the south, and the house of Joseph shall stand on their border in the north.
And you shall write up the land in seven portions and bring it here to me; and I will cast the lot for you here, before YHWH our God.

For the Levites have no portion among you — for the priesthood of YHWH is their inheritance; and Gad and Reuben and the half-tribe of Manasseh have taken their inheritance beyond the Jordan eastward, which Moses the servant of YHWH gave them."

And the men rose and went; and Joshua commanded those going to write up the land: "Go, walk through the land, and write it up, and return to me; and here I will throw the lot for you, before YHWH, at Shiloh."
And the men went and crossed through the land, and wrote it up by cities, in seven portions, on a scroll; and they came to Joshua, to the camp at Shiloh.
And Joshua threw the lot for them at Shiloh, before YHWH; and there Joshua divided the land to the children of Israel according to their divisions.
And the lot of the tribe of the sons of Benjamin came up, by their clans; and the territory of their lot went out between the sons of Judah and the sons of Joseph.

And their border on the north side was from the Jordan; and the border went up to the shoulder of Jericho on the north, and went up through the hill country westward; and its outgoings were at the wilderness of Beth-aven;
and the border crossed from there to Luz, to the shoulder of Luz southward — that is, Bethel; and the border went down to Ataroth-addar, on the mountain that is south of Lower Beth-horon;

and the border was drawn and circled, on the west side, southward from the mountain that faces Beth-horon on the south; and its outgoings were at Kiriath-baal — that is, Kiriath-jearim — a city of the sons of Judah. This is the west side.
And the south side: from the edge of Kiriath-jearim; and the border went out westward and went out to the spring of the waters of Nephtoah;

and the border went down to the edge of the mountain that faces the Valley of Ben-hinnom, which is in the Valley of Rephaim to the north; and it went down the Valley of Hinnom to the shoulder of the Jebusite southward, and went down to En-rogel;
and it was drawn on the north and went out at En-shemesh, and went out to Geliloth, which faces the Ascent of Adummim; and it went down to the Stone of Bohan son of Reuben;
and it crossed to the shoulder facing the Arabah northward, and went down to the Arabah;

and the border crossed to the shoulder of Beth-hoglah northward; and the outgoings of the border were at the northern tongue of the Salt Sea, at the southern end of the Jordan. This is the southern border.
And the Jordan bounds it on the east side. This is the inheritance of the sons of Benjamin, by its borders all around, by their clans.
And the cities of the tribe of the sons of Benjamin, by their clans, were: Jericho and Beth-hoglah and Emek-keziz,
and Beth-arabah and Zemaraim and Bethel,
and Avvim and Parah and Ophrah,
and Chephar-ammoni and Ophni and Geba: twelve cities with their villages.
Gibeon and Ramah and Beeroth,
and Mizpeh and Chephirah and Mozah,
and Rekem and Irpeel and Taralah,
and Zela, Haeleph, and the Jebusite — that is, Jerusalem — Gibeath, Kiriath: fourteen cities with their villages. This is the inheritance of the sons of Benjamin, by their clans.

jos.19.text: And the second lot went out for Simeon — for the tribe of the sons of Simeon, by their clans; and their inheritance was in the midst of the inheritance of the sons of Judah.
And they had in their inheritance: Beersheba and Sheba and Moladah,
and Hazar-shual and Balah and Ezem,
and Eltolad and Bethul and Hormah,
and Ziklag and Beth-marcaboth and Hazar-susah,
and Beth-lebaoth and Sharuhen: thirteen cities with their villages;
Ain, Rimmon, and Ether and Ashan: four cities with their villages —
together with all the villages that were around these cities as far as Baalath-beer, Ramah of the Negev. This is the inheritance of the tribe of the sons of Simeon, by their clans.

Out of the allotted portion of the sons of Judah came the inheritance of the sons of Simeon — for the portion of the sons of Judah was too large for them; so the sons of Simeon inherited in the midst of their inheritance.
And the third lot came up for the sons of Zebulun, by their clans; and the border of their inheritance reached as far as Sarid;
and their border went up westward, to Maralah, and touched Dabbesheth, and touched the wadi that faces Jokneam;
and it turned back from Sarid eastward, toward the sunrise, to the border of Chisloth-tabor, and went out to Daberath, and went up to Japhia;
and from there it crossed eastward, toward the sunrise, to Gath-hepher, to Eth-kazin, and went out at Rimmon, bending toward Neah;
and the border circled around it on the north to Hannathon; and its outgoings were at the Valley of Iphtah-el;
and Kattath and Nahalal and Shimron and Idalah and Bethlehem: twelve cities with their villages.
This is the inheritance of the sons of Zebulun by their clans — these cities with their villages.
For Issachar the fourth lot went out — for the sons of Issachar, by their clans;
and their border was: Jezreel and Chesulloth and Shunem,
and Hapharaim and Shion and Anaharath,
and Rabbith and Kishion and Ebez,
and Remeth and En-gannim and En-haddah and Beth-pazzez;
and the border touched Tabor and Shahazumah and Beth-shemesh; and the outgoings of their border were at the Jordan: sixteen cities with their villages.
This is the inheritance of the tribe of the sons of Issachar, by their clans — the cities with their villages.
And the fifth lot went out for the tribe of the sons of Asher, by their clans;
and their border was: Helkath and Hali and Beten and Achshaph,
and Allammelech and Amad and Mishal; and it touched Carmel on the west, and Shihor-libnath;
and it turned back toward the sunrise to Beth-dagon, and touched Zebulun and the Valley of Iphtah-el northward — Beth-emek and Neiel — and went out to Cabul on the left,
and Ebron and Rehob and Hammon and Kanah, as far as Great Sidon;
and the border turned back to Ramah, and as far as the fortified city of Tyre; and the border turned back to Hosah, and its outgoings were at the sea, in the region of Achzib;
and Ummah and Aphek and Rehob: twenty-two cities with their villages.
This is the inheritance of the tribe of the sons of Asher, by their clans — these cities with their villages.
For the sons of Naphtali the sixth lot went out — for the sons of Naphtali, by their clans;
and their border was from Heleph, from the oak at Zaanannim, and Adami-nekeb and Jabneel, as far as Lakkum; and its outgoings were at the Jordan;
and the border turned back westward to Aznoth-tabor, and went out from there to Hukkok; and it touched Zebulun on the south, and touched Asher on the west, and Judah at the Jordan toward the sunrise.
And the fortified cities: Ziddim, Zer, and Hammath, Rakkath, and Chinnereth,
and Adamah and Ramah and Hazor,
and Kedesh and Edrei and En-hazor,
and Iron and Migdal-el, Horem and Beth-anath and Beth-shemesh: nineteen cities with their villages.
This is the inheritance of the tribe of the sons of Naphtali, by their clans — the cities with their villages.
For the tribe of the sons of Dan, by their clans, the seventh lot went out;
and the border of their inheritance was: Zorah and Eshtaol and Ir-shemesh,
and Shaalabbin and Aijalon and Ithlah,
and Elon and Timnah and Ekron,
and Eltekeh and Gibbethon and Baalath,
and Jehud and Bene-berak and Gath-rimmon,
and the waters of the Jarkon, and Rakkon, with the territory facing Joppa.

And the territory of the sons of Dan went out from their grasp; and the sons of Dan went up and fought with Leshem, and captured it, and struck it with the mouth of the sword, and possessed it, and settled in it; and they called Leshem, Dan — after the name of Dan their father.
This is the inheritance of the tribe of the sons of Dan, by their clans — these cities with their villages.
And when they had finished distributing the land as inheritance, by its borders, the children of Israel gave an inheritance to Joshua son of Nun in their midst:
according to the mouth of YHWH they gave him the city he asked for — Timnath-serah, in the hill country of Ephraim; and he built the city and settled in it.

These are the inheritances that Eleazar the priest, and Joshua son of Nun, and the heads of the fathers' houses of the tribes of the children of Israel distributed by lot at Shiloh, before YHWH, at the opening of the tent of meeting. And they finished dividing the land.

jos.20.text: "Speak to the children of Israel: Give yourselves the cities of refuge of which I spoke to you by the hand of Moses —
for a killer to flee there, one who strikes down a soul in error, without knowing; and they shall be for you a refuge from the redeemer of blood.

And he shall flee to one of these cities, and stand at the opening of the city gate, and speak his words in the ears of the elders of that city; and they shall gather him into the city, to themselves, and give him a place — and he shall live with them.
And if the redeemer of blood pursues after him, they shall not shut the killer into his hand — for without knowing he struck his fellow, and he did not hate him before.

And he shall stay in that city until he stands before the congregation for judgment — until the death of the high priest who is in those days; then the killer shall return and come to his own city and his own house, to the city from which he fled."
And they set apart Kedesh in Galilee, in the hill country of Naphtali; and Shechem, in the hill country of Ephraim; and Kiriath-arba — that is, Hebron — in the hill country of Judah.

And beyond the Jordan at Jericho, eastward, they gave Bezer in the wilderness, on the tableland, from the tribe of Reuben; and Ramoth in Gilead, from the tribe of Gad; and Golan in Bashan, from the tribe of Manasseh.

These were the appointed cities for all the children of Israel, and for the sojourner sojourning in their midst — for anyone who strikes down a soul in error to flee there, so that he not die by the hand of the redeemer of blood before standing in front of the congregation.

jos.21.text: And the heads of the fathers' houses of the Levites drew near to Eleazar the priest, and to Joshua son of Nun, and to the heads of the fathers' houses of the tribes of the children of Israel;
and they spoke to them at Shiloh, in the land of Canaan: "YHWH commanded by the hand of Moses that we be given cities to live in, with their pasturelands for our livestock."
And the children of Israel gave the Levites, from their inheritance, according to the mouth of YHWH, these cities with their pasturelands.

And the lot went out for the clans of the Kohathites; and it fell to the sons of Aaron the priest, of the Levites, from the tribe of Judah and from the tribe of the Simeonites and from the tribe of Benjamin, by lot: thirteen cities.
And to the rest of the sons of Kohath, from the clans of the tribe of Ephraim and from the tribe of Dan and from the half-tribe of Manasseh, by lot: ten cities.
And to the sons of Gershon, from the clans of the tribe of Issachar and from the tribe of Asher and from the tribe of Naphtali and from the half-tribe of Manasseh in Bashan, by lot: thirteen cities.
To the sons of Merari, by their clans, from the tribe of Reuben and from the tribe of Gad and from the tribe of Zebulun: twelve cities.
So the children of Israel gave the Levites these cities with their pasturelands, as YHWH had commanded by the hand of Moses — by lot.
And they gave, from the tribe of the sons of Judah and from the tribe of the sons of Simeon, these cities that are called by name;
and they fell to the sons of Aaron, of the clans of the Kohathites, of the sons of Levi — for the lot was theirs first;
and they gave them Kiriath-arba — Arba was the father of Anak — that is, Hebron, in the hill country of Judah, with its pasturelands around it.
But the fields of the city, and its villages, they gave to Caleb son of Jephunneh as his holding.
And to the sons of Aaron the priest they gave the killer's city of refuge, Hebron, with its pastureland; and Libnah with its pastureland,
and Jattir with its pastureland, and Eshtemoa with its pastureland,
and Holon with its pastureland, and Debir with its pastureland,
and Ain with its pastureland, and Juttah with its pastureland, Beth-shemesh with its pastureland: nine cities from these two tribes.
And from the tribe of Benjamin: Gibeon with its pastureland, Geba with its pastureland,
Anathoth with its pastureland, and Almon with its pastureland: four cities.
All the cities of the sons of Aaron, the priests: thirteen cities with their pasturelands.
And as for the clans of the sons of Kohath, the Levites — the rest of the sons of Kohath — the cities of their lot were from the tribe of Ephraim;
and they gave them the killer's city of refuge, Shechem, with its pastureland, in the hill country of Ephraim; and Gezer with its pastureland,
and Kibzaim with its pastureland, and Beth-horon with its pastureland: four cities.
And from the tribe of Dan: Elteke with its pastureland, Gibbethon with its pastureland,
Aijalon with its pastureland, Gath-rimmon with its pastureland: four cities.
And from the half-tribe of Manasseh: Taanach with its pastureland, and Gath-rimmon with its pastureland: two cities.
All the cities for the clans of the rest of the sons of Kohath: ten, with their pasturelands.

And to the sons of Gershon, of the clans of the Levites: from the half-tribe of Manasseh, the killer's city of refuge, Golan in Bashan, with its pastureland; and Beeshterah with its pastureland: two cities.
And from the tribe of Issachar: Kishion with its pastureland, Daberath with its pastureland,
Jarmuth with its pastureland, En-gannim with its pastureland: four cities.
And from the tribe of Asher: Mishal with its pastureland, Abdon with its pastureland,
Helkath with its pastureland, and Rehob with its pastureland: four cities.
And from the tribe of Naphtali: the killer's city of refuge, Kedesh in Galilee, with its pastureland; and Hammoth-dor with its pastureland, and Kartan with its pastureland: three cities.
All the cities of the Gershonites, by their clans: thirteen cities with their pasturelands.
And to the clans of the sons of Merari — the rest of the Levites: from the tribe of Zebulun, Jokneam with its pastureland, Kartah with its pastureland,
Dimnah with its pastureland, Nahalal with its pastureland: four cities.
And from the tribe of Reuben: Bezer with its pastureland, and Jahaz with its pastureland,
Kedemoth with its pastureland, and Mephaath with its pastureland: four cities.
And from the tribe of Gad: the killer's city of refuge, Ramoth in Gilead, with its pastureland; and Mahanaim with its pastureland,
Heshbon with its pastureland, Jazer with its pastureland: four cities in all.
All the cities for the sons of Merari, by their clans — the rest of the clans of the Levites: their lot was twelve cities.
All the cities of the Levites, in the midst of the holding of the children of Israel: forty-eight cities with their pasturelands.
These cities were each city with its pasturelands around it — so for all these cities.
So YHWH gave Israel all the land he had sworn to give their fathers; and they possessed it and settled in it.
And YHWH gave them rest all around, according to all he had sworn to their fathers; and not a man of all their enemies stood before them — all their enemies YHWH gave into their hand.
Not a word fell of all the good word that YHWH had spoken to the house of Israel. Everything came to pass.

jos.22.text: Then Joshua called the Reubenite and the Gadite and the half-tribe of Manasseh,
and said to them: "You have kept all that Moses the servant of YHWH commanded you, and you have listened to my voice in all that I commanded you.
You have not abandoned your brothers these many days, up to this day; and you have kept the charge — the commandment of YHWH your God.

And now YHWH your God has given rest to your brothers, as he spoke to them. So now — turn and go to your tents, to the land of your holding, which Moses the servant of YHWH gave you beyond the Jordan.

Only keep carefully to do the commandment and the teaching that Moses the servant of YHWH commanded you: to love YHWH your God, and to walk in all his ways, and to keep his commandments, and to cling to him, and to serve him with all your heart and with all your soul."
And Joshua blessed them and sent them away; and they went to their tents.

Now to one half of the tribe of Manasseh Moses had given land in Bashan; and to its other half Joshua gave land with their brothers on the west side of the Jordan. And when Joshua sent them away to their tents, he blessed them,

and said to them: "Return to your tents with great riches, and with very much livestock — with silver and with gold and with bronze and with iron, and with very many garments. Divide the spoil of your enemies with your brothers."

And the sons of Reuben and the sons of Gad and the half-tribe of Manasseh returned; and they went from the children of Israel, from Shiloh in the land of Canaan, to go to the land of Gilead — the land of their holding, in which they had taken holdings according to the mouth of YHWH by the hand of Moses.

And they came to the districts of the Jordan that are in the land of Canaan; and the sons of Reuben and the sons of Gad and the half-tribe of Manasseh built an altar there by the Jordan — an altar great to look at.

And the children of Israel heard it said: "Behold — the sons of Reuben and the sons of Gad and the half-tribe of Manasseh have built the altar facing the land of Canaan, at the districts of the Jordan, on the side belonging to the children of Israel."
And the children of Israel heard — and the whole congregation of the children of Israel assembled at Shiloh, to go up against them for war.
And the children of Israel sent to the sons of Reuben and to the sons of Gad and to the half-tribe of Manasseh, into the land of Gilead, Phinehas son of Eleazar the priest,
and ten leaders with him — one leader, one leader per father's house, for all the tribes of Israel; each of them was head of his father's house, among the thousands of Israel.
And they came to the sons of Reuben and the sons of Gad and the half-tribe of Manasseh, into the land of Gilead, and spoke with them:

"Thus says the whole congregation of YHWH: What is this breaking of faith you have committed against the God of Israel — to turn back today from following YHWH, by building yourselves an altar, rebelling today against YHWH?
Was the guilt of Peor too small for us — from which we have not cleansed ourselves to this day, though the plague came on the congregation of YHWH —
that you turn back today from following YHWH? And it shall be: you rebel today against YHWH — and tomorrow he will be furious with the whole congregation of Israel.

But if the land of your holding is unclean — cross over into the land of YHWH's holding, where the dwelling of YHWH has settled, and take holdings among us. But do not rebel against YHWH, and do not make us rebels, by building yourselves an altar besides the altar of YHWH our God.
Did not Achan son of Zerah break faith with the devoted things — and wrath came on the whole congregation of Israel? And he was one man: he did not perish alone in his guilt."
And the sons of Reuben and the sons of Gad and the half-tribe of Manasseh answered and spoke with the heads of the thousands of Israel:
"El! God! YHWH! El! God! YHWH! — He knows, and Israel itself shall know: if it was in rebellion or in breaking faith with YHWH — do not save us this day! —
building ourselves an altar to turn back from following YHWH; or if it was to offer burnt offering and grain offering on it, or to make peace offerings on it — let YHWH himself seek it out.
No — rather, out of anxiety, for a reason, we did this. We said: In time to come your sons may say to our sons, 'What have you to do with YHWH, the God of Israel?
YHWH has set a border between us and you, sons of Reuben and sons of Gad — the Jordan. You have no portion in YHWH!' And so your sons would make our sons cease from fearing YHWH.
So we said: Let us act for ourselves — let us build the altar; not for burnt offering, and not for sacrifice,

but as a witness between us and you, and between our generations after us, that we do perform the service of YHWH before him with our burnt offerings and our sacrifices and our peace offerings — so that your sons will not say to our sons in time to come, 'You have no portion in YHWH.'

And we said: And it shall be, if they say this to us or to our generations in time to come, that we will say: 'See the pattern of the altar of YHWH that our fathers made — not for burnt offering, and not for sacrifice — but it is a witness between us and you.'

Far be it from us to rebel against YHWH, and to turn back today from following YHWH by building an altar for burnt offering, for grain offering, and for sacrifice — besides the altar of YHWH our God that stands before his dwelling!"

And Phinehas the priest, and the leaders of the congregation, and the heads of the thousands of Israel who were with him, heard the words the sons of Reuben and the sons of Gad and the sons of Manasseh spoke — and it was good in their eyes.

And Phinehas son of Eleazar the priest said to the sons of Reuben and the sons of Gad and the sons of Manasseh: "Today we know that YHWH is in our midst — because you have not broken this faith with YHWH. Now you have rescued the children of Israel from the hand of YHWH."

And Phinehas son of Eleazar the priest, and the leaders, returned from the sons of Reuben and from the sons of Gad, from the land of Gilead to the land of Canaan, to the children of Israel — and brought them back word;

and the word was good in the eyes of the children of Israel, and the children of Israel blessed God, and spoke no more of going up against them for war, to destroy the land in which the sons of Reuben and the sons of Gad were living.
And the sons of Reuben and the sons of Gad gave the altar a name: "For it is a Witness between us that YHWH is God."

jos.23.text: And it came to pass, many days after YHWH had given Israel rest from all their enemies all around — and Joshua was old, come along in days —
that Joshua called all Israel — its elders and its heads and its judges and its officers — and said to them: "I have grown old; I have come along in days.
And you have seen all that YHWH your God did to all these nations because of you — for YHWH your God, he is the one who fought for you.
See — I have allotted to you these remaining nations as an inheritance for your tribes, from the Jordan — with all the nations I have cut off — to the Great Sea, toward the going down of the sun.
And YHWH your God — he will push them out before you and dispossess them from before you; and you shall possess their land, as YHWH your God spoke to you.
So be very strong, to keep and to do all that is written in the scroll of the teaching of Moses — without turning from it, right or left,

without going in among these nations that remain with you. And the name of their gods you shall not invoke, and you shall not swear by them, and you shall not serve them, and you shall not bow down to them —
but to YHWH your God you shall cling, as you have done to this day.
And YHWH has dispossessed before you nations great and mighty; and as for you — not a man has stood before you, to this day.
One man of you chases a thousand — for YHWH your God, he is the one fighting for you, as he spoke to you.
So keep careful watch over your souls: to love YHWH your God.
But if you turn — you surely turn back — and cling to the rest of these nations, these remaining ones among you, and intermarry with them, and go in among them, and they among you:

know surely that YHWH your God will not continue to dispossess these nations from before you; and they shall become for you a trap and a snare, and a whip on your sides, and thorns in your eyes — until you perish from off this good soil that YHWH your God has given you.

And behold — I am walking today the way of all the earth. And you know, with all your heart and with all your soul, that not one word has fallen of all the good words that YHWH your God spoke concerning you: all have come to pass for you — not one word of them has fallen.

And it shall be: just as every good word that YHWH your God spoke to you has come upon you, so will YHWH bring upon you every evil word — until he has destroyed you from off this good soil that YHWH your God has given you,

when you cross the covenant of YHWH your God, which he commanded you, and go and serve other gods and bow down to them. Then the anger of YHWH will burn against you, and you will perish quickly from off the good land he has given you."

jos.24.text: And Joshua gathered all the tribes of Israel to Shechem; and he called the elders of Israel and its heads and its judges and its officers — and they took their stand before God.

And Joshua said to all the people: "Thus says YHWH, the God of Israel: Beyond the River your fathers lived long ago — Terah, the father of Abraham and the father of Nahor — and they served other gods.
And I took your father Abraham from beyond the River, and led him through all the land of Canaan, and multiplied his seed; and I gave him Isaac.
And to Isaac I gave Jacob and Esau; and to Esau I gave Mount Seir to possess — but Jacob and his sons went down to Egypt.
And I sent Moses and Aaron, and I struck Egypt with plagues, according to what I did in its midst — and afterward I brought you out.
And I brought your fathers out of Egypt, and you came to the sea; and the Egyptians pursued after your fathers with chariots and horsemen to the Sea of Reeds.

And they cried out to YHWH, and he set thick darkness between you and the Egyptians, and brought the sea over them and covered them — and your own eyes saw what I did in Egypt. And you lived in the wilderness many days.
And I brought you to the land of the Amorites who lived beyond the Jordan, and they fought with you; and I gave them into your hand, and you possessed their land — and I destroyed them before you.
And Balak son of Zippor, king of Moab, rose and fought against Israel; and he sent and called Balaam son of Beor to curse you —
but I was not willing to listen to Balaam, and he blessed you — blessed you indeed; and I rescued you from his hand.

And you crossed the Jordan and came to Jericho; and the lords of Jericho fought against you — the Amorite and the Perizzite and the Canaanite and the Hittite and the Girgashite, the Hivite and the Jebusite — and I gave them into your hand.
And I sent the hornet before you, and it drove them out before you — the two kings of the Amorites; not by your sword and not by your bow.
And I gave you a land in which you had not labored, and cities you did not build — and you have settled in them; vineyards and olive groves you did not plant — you are eating from them.
So now: fear YHWH, and serve him in integrity and in truth; and remove the gods your fathers served beyond the River and in Egypt — and serve YHWH.

And if it is evil in your eyes to serve YHWH — choose for yourselves today whom you will serve: whether the gods your fathers served beyond the River, or the gods of the Amorites in whose land you are living. But as for me and my house — we will serve YHWH."
And the people answered and said: "Far be it from us to abandon YHWH, to serve other gods!

For YHWH our God — he is the one who brought us and our fathers up from the land of Egypt, from the house of slaves, and who did these great signs before our eyes, and kept us all along the way we walked, and among all the peoples through whose midst we passed;
and YHWH drove out before us all the peoples — the Amorites who lived in the land. We also will serve YHWH — for he is our God."
And Joshua said to the people: "You are not able to serve YHWH — for he is a holy God; he is a jealous God; he will not carry away your rebellion and your sins.
If you abandon YHWH and serve foreign gods, then he will turn and do you harm, and finish you — after having done you good."
And the people said to Joshua: "No — for YHWH we will serve!"
And Joshua said to the people: "You are witnesses against yourselves that you yourselves have chosen YHWH, to serve him." And they said: "Witnesses!"
"So now — remove the foreign gods that are in your midst, and bend your heart toward YHWH, the God of Israel."
And the people said to Joshua: "YHWH our God we will serve — and to his voice we will listen."
So Joshua cut a covenant for the people on that day, and set them a statute and a judgment at Shechem.
And Joshua wrote these words in the scroll of the teaching of God; and he took a great stone and set it up there, under the oak that was by the sanctuary of YHWH.

And Joshua said to all the people: "Behold — this stone shall be a witness against us; for it has heard all the sayings of YHWH that he spoke with us. And it shall be a witness against you — lest you deny your God."
And Joshua sent the people away, each man to his inheritance.
And it came to pass, after these things, that Joshua son of Nun, the servant of YHWH, died — a hundred and ten years old;
and they buried him in the territory of his inheritance, at Timnath-serah, which is in the hill country of Ephraim, north of Mount Gaash.
And Israel served YHWH all the days of Joshua, and all the days of the elders who lived on after Joshua — and who had known all the work of YHWH that he did for Israel.

And the bones of Joseph, which the children of Israel had brought up from Egypt, they buried at Shechem, in the piece of field Jacob had bought from the sons of Hamor, the father of Shechem, for a hundred kesitahs — and they became an inheritance for the sons of Joseph.
And Eleazar son of Aaron died; and they buried him at Gibeah — the hill of Phinehas his son — which had been given him in the hill country of Ephraim.

1sa.1.text: And there was one man from Ramathaim-zophim, from the hill country of Ephraim, and his name was Elkanah son of Jeroham, son of Elihu, son of Tohu, son of Zuph — an Ephrathite.
And he had two wives: the name of one was Hannah, and the name of the second Peninnah. And Peninnah had children — but Hannah had no children.
And that man went up from his city year by year to worship and to sacrifice to YHWH of Armies at Shiloh; and there the two sons of Eli — Hophni and Phinehas — were priests to YHWH.
And the day came when Elkanah sacrificed; and he gave portions to Peninnah his wife and to all her sons and daughters —
but to Hannah he gave one double portion, for he loved Hannah; but YHWH had shut her womb.
And her rival would vex her with vexation upon vexation, to make her thunder inside — because YHWH had shut up her womb.
And so it went, year by year: whenever she went up to the house of YHWH, so she would vex her; and she wept, and would not eat.
And Elkanah her husband said to her: "Hannah, why do you weep? And why do you not eat? And why is your heart grieved? Am I not better to you than ten sons?"
And Hannah rose, after the eating at Shiloh and after the drinking; and Eli the priest was sitting on the seat by the doorpost of the temple of YHWH.
And she — bitter of soul — prayed to YHWH, and wept and wept.

And she vowed a vow and said: "YHWH of Armies — if you will truly look on the affliction of your maidservant, and remember me, and not forget your maidservant, and give your maidservant seed of men — then I will give him to YHWH all the days of his life, and no razor shall come up on his head."
And it came to pass, as she prayed long before YHWH, that Eli was watching her mouth.
Now Hannah — she was speaking upon her heart: only her lips moved, and her voice was not heard. And Eli took her for a drunkard;
and Eli said to her: "How long will you keep making yourself drunk? Put away your wine from you."
And Hannah answered and said: "No, my lord — I am a woman hard-pressed in spirit. Wine and strong drink I have not drunk; but I have been pouring out my soul before YHWH.
Do not set your maidservant down as a daughter of worthlessness — for out of the abundance of my anguish and my vexation I have spoken until now."
And Eli answered and said: "Go in peace — and may the God of Israel grant your asking, which you have asked of him."
And she said: "May your servant find favor in your eyes." And the woman went her way, and ate — and her face was hers no longer as before.
And they rose early in the morning and worshiped before YHWH, and returned and came to their house at Ramah. And Elkanah knew Hannah his wife — and YHWH remembered her.
And it came to pass, at the turning of the days, that Hannah conceived and bore a son; and she called his name Samuel — "for from YHWH I asked him."
And the man Elkanah went up with all his house to sacrifice to YHWH the yearly sacrifice, and his vow;
but Hannah did not go up — for she said to her husband: "When the boy is weaned, I will bring him, and he shall appear before the face of YHWH, and stay there to the age."
And Elkanah her husband said to her: "Do what is good in your eyes; stay until you have weaned him — only may YHWH establish his word." So the woman stayed and nursed her son until she weaned him.
And she brought him up with her, when she had weaned him, with three bulls and one ephah of flour and a skin of wine; and she brought him to the house of YHWH at Shiloh — and the boy was young.
And they slaughtered the bull, and they brought the boy to Eli.
And she said: "Please, my lord — as your soul lives, my lord: I am the woman who stood with you here, praying to YHWH.
For this boy I prayed; and YHWH has given me my asking, which I asked of him.
And I also have lent him to YHWH: all the days that he lives, he is lent to YHWH." And he worshiped YHWH there.

1sa.2.text: And Hannah prayed, and said: "My heart exults in YHWH; my horn is lifted high in YHWH; my mouth is opened wide over my enemies — for I rejoice in your salvation.
There is none holy like YHWH — for there is none besides you; and there is no rock like our God.
Do not multiply speaking so high, so high; let arrogance go out of your mouth no more — for YHWH is a God of knowings, and by him deeds are weighed.
The bow of the mighty is shattered — and the stumbling have girded on strength.
The full have hired themselves out for bread — and the hungry have ceased to hunger; even the barren has borne seven — and she of many sons has withered.
YHWH kills, and makes alive; he brings down to Sheol — and brings up.
YHWH dispossesses, and makes rich; he brings low — he also lifts high.

He raises the poor from the dust; from the ash heap he lifts the needy — to seat them with nobles, and he gives them a throne of glory as inheritance. For to YHWH belong the pillars of the earth, and he has set the world upon them.
He guards the feet of his devoted ones — but the wicked are silenced in darkness; for not by strength does a man prevail.

YHWH — those who contend with him are shattered; against them he thunders in the heavens. YHWH judges the ends of the earth; and he gives strength to his king, and lifts high the horn of his anointed."
And Elkanah went to Ramah, to his house; and the boy was serving YHWH before the face of Eli the priest.
Now the sons of Eli were sons of worthlessness: they did not know YHWH.
And this was the priests' custom with the people: whenever any man sacrificed a sacrifice, the priest's servant would come, while the meat was boiling, with the three-pronged fork in his hand,
and thrust it into the basin or the kettle or the cauldron or the pot; whatever the fork brought up, the priest would take with it. So they did to all Israel who came there, at Shiloh.
Even before they burned the fat, the priest's servant would come and say to the man sacrificing: "Give meat for the priest to roast! He will not take boiled meat from you — only raw."
And if the man said to him: "Let them surely burn the fat first, as is due today — then take for yourself whatever your soul craves," he would say: "No — give it now; and if not, I take it by force."
And the sin of the young men was very great before the face of YHWH — for the men treated the offering of YHWH with contempt.
But Samuel was serving before the face of YHWH — a boy, girded with a linen ephod;
and his mother would make him a little robe and bring it up to him year by year, when she went up with her husband to sacrifice the yearly sacrifice.
And Eli would bless Elkanah and his wife and say: "May YHWH set seed for you from this woman, in place of the asking that was asked for YHWH." And they would go to his place.
For YHWH visited Hannah, and she conceived and bore three sons and two daughters. And the boy Samuel grew up with YHWH.
Now Eli was very old; and he heard all that his sons were doing to all Israel — and that they lay with the women who served at the opening of the tent of meeting.
And he said to them: "Why do you do things like these? For I hear of your evil dealings from all this people.
No, my sons — for it is not a good report that I hear the people of YHWH spreading.

If a man sins against a man, God may mediate for him; but if a man sins against YHWH — who shall intercede for him?" But they did not listen to the voice of their father — for YHWH desired to put them to death.
And the boy Samuel went on growing, and in favor — both with YHWH and with men.
And a man of God came to Eli and said to him: "Thus says YHWH: Did I not reveal myself — reveal myself indeed — to your father's house when they were in Egypt, belonging to the house of Pharaoh?

And I chose him out of all the tribes of Israel to be my priest — to go up on my altar, to burn incense, to carry an ephod before me; and I gave to your father's house all the fire-offerings of the children of Israel.
Why do you kick at my sacrifice and my offering, which I commanded in my dwelling — and honor your sons above me, fattening yourselves on the first of every offering of Israel, my people?

Therefore — an utterance of YHWH, the God of Israel: I said indeed that your house and your father's house would walk before me to the age. But now — an utterance of YHWH — far be it from me! For those who honor me I will honor; and those who despise me shall be counted small.
Behold, days are coming when I will hack off your arm and the arm of your father's house, so that there will be no old man in your house;
and you will look with narrowed eye on my dwelling, amid all the good done to Israel; and there shall not be an old man in your house, all the days.
And the man of yours I do not cut off from my altar shall be left to wear out your eyes and to grieve your soul; and all the increase of your house shall die as men in their prime.
And this shall be the sign for you, which will come upon your two sons, upon Hophni and Phinehas: in one day the two of them shall die.
And I will raise up for myself a faithful priest: as it is in my heart and in my soul, he will do; and I will build him a faithful house, and he shall walk before my anointed all the days.

And it shall be: everyone left in your house shall come to bow down to him for a small coin of silver and a round of bread, and shall say: 'Attach me, please, to one of the priestly offices — to eat a piece of bread.'"

1sa.3.text: And the boy Samuel was serving YHWH before the face of Eli. And the word of YHWH was rare in those days; no vision broke through.
And it came to pass at that time — Eli lying in his place, and his eyes had begun to grow dim; he could not see —
and the lamp of God had not yet gone out, and Samuel was lying in the temple of YHWH, where the ark of God was —
that YHWH called to Samuel; and he said: "Here I am!"
And he ran to Eli and said: "Here I am — for you called me." And he said: "I did not call. Go back; lie down." And he went and lay down.
And YHWH called yet again: "Samuel!" And Samuel rose and went to Eli and said: "Here I am — for you called me." And he said: "I did not call, my son. Go back; lie down."
Now Samuel did not yet know YHWH; the word of YHWH had not yet been revealed to him.
And YHWH called Samuel again, a third time; and he rose and went to Eli and said: "Here I am — for you called me." Then Eli understood that YHWH was calling the boy;
and Eli said to Samuel: "Go, lie down. And it shall be, if he calls to you, that you shall say: 'Speak, YHWH — for your servant is listening.'" And Samuel went and lay down in his place.
And YHWH came and took his stand, and called as at the other times: "Samuel! Samuel!" And Samuel said: "Speak — for your servant is listening."
And YHWH said to Samuel: "Behold — I am doing a thing in Israel at which the two ears of everyone who hears it will ring.
On that day I will raise up against Eli all that I have spoken against his house — beginning, and finishing.
And I have told him that I am judging his house to the age, for the guilt he knew of — for his sons were bringing a curse on themselves, and he did not restrain them.
And therefore I have sworn to the house of Eli: the guilt of the house of Eli shall never be covered over by sacrifice or by offering — to the age."
And Samuel lay until the morning; then he opened the doors of the house of YHWH. And Samuel was afraid to tell the vision to Eli;
but Eli called Samuel and said: "Samuel, my son." And he said: "Here I am."
And he said: "What is the word he spoke to you? Do not hide it from me, please. So may God do to you, and so may he add, if you hide from me a word of all the word he spoke to you."
So Samuel told him all the words, and hid nothing from him. And he said: "He is YHWH. What is good in his eyes, let him do."
And Samuel grew, and YHWH was with him — and he let none of his words fall to the ground.
And all Israel, from Dan to Beersheba, knew that Samuel stood confirmed as a prophet to YHWH.
And YHWH continued to appear at Shiloh — for YHWH revealed himself to Samuel at Shiloh, by the word of YHWH.

1sa.4.text: And the word of Samuel came to all Israel. And Israel went out to meet the Philistines for battle; and they camped by Ebenezer, and the Philistines camped at Aphek.
And the Philistines drew up to meet Israel; and the battle spread, and Israel was struck down before the Philistines — and they struck down in the battle line, in the field, about four thousand men.

And the people came into the camp, and the elders of Israel said: "Why has YHWH struck us down today before the Philistines? Let us take to ourselves, from Shiloh, the ark of the covenant of YHWH — that it may come into our midst and save us from the palm of our enemies."

And the people sent to Shiloh, and they carried from there the ark of the covenant of YHWH of Armies, who sits enthroned on the cherubim; and the two sons of Eli — Hophni and Phinehas — were there with the ark of the covenant of God.
And it came to pass, as the ark of the covenant of YHWH came into the camp, that all Israel shouted a great shout — and the earth resounded.
And the Philistines heard the sound of the shouting and said: "What is the sound of this great shouting in the camp of the Hebrews?" And they learned that the ark of YHWH had come into the camp.
And the Philistines were afraid — for they said, "God has come into the camp" — and they said: "Woe to us! For nothing like this has happened before.
Woe to us! Who will rescue us from the hand of these majestic gods? These are the gods who struck Egypt with every kind of blow, in the wilderness!
Strengthen yourselves and be men, Philistines — lest you serve the Hebrews as they have served you. Be men — and fight!"
And the Philistines fought, and Israel was struck down, and they fled, each man to his tents; and the blow was very great — thirty thousand foot soldiers of Israel fell.
And the ark of God was taken; and the two sons of Eli died — Hophni and Phinehas.
And a man of Benjamin ran from the battle line and came to Shiloh that same day — his garments torn, and soil on his head.
And he came — and behold: Eli sitting on his seat by the roadside, watching; for his heart was trembling for the ark of God. And the man came to tell it in the city — and the whole city cried out.
And Eli heard the sound of the outcry and said: "What is the sound of this uproar?" And the man hurried and came and told Eli.
Now Eli was ninety-eight years old, and his eyes were set — he could not see.
And the man said to Eli: "I am the one who has come from the battle line — I fled from the battle line today." And he said: "What was the word, my son?"

And the bringer of news answered and said: "Israel has fled before the Philistines, and there has also been a great slaughter among the people; and your two sons also are dead — Hophni and Phinehas — and the ark of God has been taken."

And it came to pass, at his mentioning the ark of God, that he fell backward off the seat, beside the gate; and his neck broke, and he died — for the man was old, and heavy. And he had judged Israel forty years.

And his daughter-in-law, the wife of Phinehas, was pregnant, near to giving birth; and she heard the report — that the ark of God was taken, and that her father-in-law and her husband were dead — and she crouched down and gave birth, for her pains turned upon her.
And about the time of her death, the women standing over her spoke: "Do not fear — for you have borne a son." But she did not answer, and did not set her heart to it.
And she called the boy Ichabod — Where-Is-the-Glory — saying: "The glory has gone into exile from Israel" — because of the taking of the ark of God, and because of her father-in-law and her husband.
And she said: "The glory has gone into exile from Israel — for the ark of God has been taken."

1sa.5.text: And the Philistines took the ark of God and brought it from Ebenezer to Ashdod;
and the Philistines took the ark of God and brought it into the house of Dagon, and set it up beside Dagon.
And the Ashdodites rose early the next day — and behold: Dagon, fallen on his face to the ground before the ark of YHWH. And they took Dagon and returned him to his place.

And they rose early in the morning the next day — and behold: Dagon, fallen on his face to the ground before the ark of YHWH; and the head of Dagon and the two palms of his hands were cut off, on the threshold — only the trunk of Dagon was left of him.
Therefore the priests of Dagon, and all who enter the house of Dagon, do not tread on the threshold of Dagon in Ashdod to this day.
And the hand of YHWH was heavy on the Ashdodites, and he devastated them; and he struck them with tumors — Ashdod and its territories.
And the men of Ashdod saw that it was so, and they said: "The ark of the God of Israel shall not stay with us — for his hand is hard on us, and on Dagon our god."

And they sent and gathered all the lords of the Philistines to them, and said: "What shall we do with the ark of the God of Israel?" And they said: "Let the ark of the God of Israel be moved around to Gath." And they moved the ark of the God of Israel around.

And it came to pass, after they had moved it around, that the hand of YHWH was against the city — a very great panic; and he struck the men of the city, from small to great, and tumors broke out on them.

And they sent the ark of God to Ekron. And it came to pass, as the ark of God came to Ekron, that the Ekronites cried out: "They have moved the ark of the God of Israel around to me — to kill me and my people!"

And they sent and gathered all the lords of the Philistines and said: "Send the ark of the God of Israel away — let it return to its own place, and not kill me and my people." For there was a deathly panic through the whole city; the hand of God was very heavy there,
and the men who did not die were struck with the tumors — and the cry of the city went up to the heavens.

1sa.6.text: And the ark of YHWH was in the countryside of the Philistines seven months.
And the Philistines called for the priests and the diviners: "What shall we do with the ark of YHWH? Let us know how we should send it back to its place."

And they said: "If you are sending away the ark of the God of Israel, do not send it away empty — but surely return to him a guilt-payment. Then you will be healed, and it will be made known to you why his hand does not turn away from you."

And they said: "What is the guilt-payment we should return to him?" And they said: "By the number of the lords of the Philistines: five tumors of gold and five mice of gold — for one plague was on all of you, and on your lords.

And you shall make images of your tumors and images of your mice that are ruining the land, and give glory to the God of Israel. Perhaps he will lighten his hand from upon you and your gods and your land.
And why should you make your heart heavy, as Egypt and Pharaoh made their heart heavy? When he had dealt severely with them, did they not send them away — and they went?
So now — take and prepare one new cart, and two nursing cows on which no yoke has come up; and harness the cows to the cart, and bring their calves back home, away from them.
And take the ark of YHWH and set it on the cart; and the objects of gold you are returning to him as a guilt-payment, put in a box at its side. And send it off — and let it go.

And watch: if it goes up by the road of its own territory, to Beth-shemesh — it was he who did us this great evil. But if not, then we shall know that it was not his hand that touched us; it was chance that happened to us."
And the men did so: they took two nursing cows and harnessed them to the cart, and their calves they shut up at home;
and they set the ark of YHWH on the cart, with the box and the mice of gold and the images of their tumors.

And the cows went straight along the road, on the road to Beth-shemesh — on one highway they walked, walking and lowing, and did not turn aside right or left; and the lords of the Philistines walked behind them, as far as the border of Beth-shemesh.
Now Beth-shemesh was reaping the wheat harvest in the valley; and they lifted their eyes and saw the ark — and rejoiced to see it.
And the cart came into the field of Joshua of Beth-shemesh and stood there; and a great stone was there. And they split the wood of the cart, and the cows they offered up as a burnt offering to YHWH.

And the Levites took down the ark of YHWH, and the box that was with it, in which were the objects of gold, and set them on the great stone; and the men of Beth-shemesh offered up burnt offerings and sacrificed sacrifices on that day to YHWH.
And the five lords of the Philistines saw it — and they returned to Ekron on that day.
And these are the tumors of gold that the Philistines returned as a guilt-payment to YHWH: for Ashdod one, for Gaza one, for Ashkelon one, for Gath one, for Ekron one;

and the mice of gold were by the number of all the cities of the Philistines belonging to the five lords — from fortified city to unwalled village. And the great stone on which they set the ark of YHWH is a witness to this day, in the field of Joshua of Beth-shemesh.

And he struck some of the men of Beth-shemesh, because they looked into the ark of YHWH; and he struck of the people seventy men — fifty thousand men. And the people mourned, because YHWH had struck the people with a great blow.
And the men of Beth-shemesh said: "Who is able to stand before YHWH — this holy God? And to whom shall he go up from us?"
And they sent messengers to the inhabitants of Kiriath-jearim: "The Philistines have returned the ark of YHWH. Come down — bring it up to yourselves."

1sa.7.text: And the men of Kiriath-jearim came and brought up the ark of YHWH, and brought it into the house of Abinadab on the hill; and Eleazar his son they consecrated to guard the ark of YHWH.
And it came to pass, from the day the ark settled at Kiriath-jearim, that the days multiplied and became twenty years — and the whole house of Israel lamented after YHWH.

And Samuel said to the whole house of Israel: "If with all your heart you are returning to YHWH — remove the foreign gods from your midst, and the Ashtaroth; and set your heart firm toward YHWH, and serve him alone — and he will rescue you from the hand of the Philistines."
And the children of Israel removed the Baals and the Ashtaroth, and served YHWH alone.
And Samuel said: "Gather all Israel to Mizpah — and I will pray for you to YHWH."

And they gathered to Mizpah, and drew water and poured it out before YHWH; and they fasted on that day, and said there: "We have sinned against YHWH." And Samuel judged the children of Israel at Mizpah.

And the Philistines heard that the children of Israel had gathered to Mizpah, and the lords of the Philistines went up against Israel; and the children of Israel heard, and were afraid before the Philistines.
And the children of Israel said to Samuel: "Do not be silent toward us — do not stop crying out to YHWH our God, that he may save us from the hand of the Philistines."
And Samuel took one nursing lamb and offered it up as a whole burnt offering to YHWH; and Samuel cried out to YHWH for Israel — and YHWH answered him.

And it came to pass, while Samuel was offering up the burnt offering, that the Philistines drew near for battle against Israel; and YHWH thundered with a great voice on that day against the Philistines, and threw them into panic — and they were struck down before Israel.
And the men of Israel went out from Mizpah and pursued the Philistines, and struck them down as far as below Beth-car.
And Samuel took one stone and set it between Mizpah and Shen, and called its name Ebenezer — Stone of Help — and said: "Thus far YHWH has helped us."
So the Philistines were subdued, and they did not come again into the territory of Israel; and the hand of YHWH was against the Philistines all the days of Samuel.

And the cities the Philistines had taken from Israel were returned to Israel, from Ekron to Gath; and their territory Israel rescued from the hand of the Philistines. And there was peace between Israel and the Amorites.
And Samuel judged Israel all the days of his life;
and he went year by year on a circuit — Bethel and Gilgal and Mizpah — and judged Israel at all these places;
and his return was to Ramah, for there was his house, and there he judged Israel; and he built there an altar to YHWH.

1sa.8.text: And it came to pass, when Samuel grew old, that he set his sons as judges for Israel.
And the name of his firstborn son was Joel, and the name of his second Abijah — judges at Beersheba.
But his sons did not walk in his ways: they bent aside after unjust gain, and took bribes — and bent justice.
And all the elders of Israel gathered themselves and came to Samuel at Ramah;
and they said to him: "Behold — you have grown old, and your sons do not walk in your ways. Now — set a king over us to judge us, like all the nations."
And the thing was evil in the eyes of Samuel when they said, "Give us a king to judge us." And Samuel prayed to YHWH.
And YHWH said to Samuel: "Listen to the voice of the people in all they say to you — for it is not you they have rejected; it is me they have rejected from being king over them.
Like all the deeds they have done from the day I brought them up from Egypt until this day — abandoning me and serving other gods — so they are doing to you also.
So now, listen to their voice. Only — warn them solemnly, and tell them the custom of the king who will reign over them."
And Samuel spoke all the words of YHWH to the people who were asking a king from him.
And he said: "This will be the custom of the king who will reign over you: your sons he will take, and set for himself in his chariots and among his horsemen — and they will run before his chariot;
and he will set for himself commanders of thousands and commanders of fifties; and they will plow his plowing and reap his harvest, and make his weapons of war and the gear of his chariots.
And your daughters he will take — as perfumers and as cooks and as bakers.
And your fields and your vineyards and your best olive groves he will take — and give to his servants.
And your grain and your vineyards he will tithe — and give to his officers and his servants.
And your male servants and your female servants and your best young men and your donkeys he will take — and put to his own work.
Your flocks he will tithe. And you — you will be his slaves.
And you will cry out on that day because of your king, whom you chose for yourselves — and YHWH will not answer you on that day."
But the people refused to listen to the voice of Samuel; and they said: "No! There shall be a king over us —
and we too shall be like all the nations: our king shall judge us, and go out before us, and fight our battles."
And Samuel heard all the words of the people — and he spoke them in the ears of YHWH.
And YHWH said to Samuel: "Listen to their voice — and make them a king." And Samuel said to the men of Israel: "Go, each man to his city."

1sa.9.text: And there was a man of Benjamin, and his name was Kish son of Abiel, son of Zeror, son of Becorath, son of Aphiah — a Benjaminite, a mighty man of substance.
And he had a son whose name was Saul — a choice young man, and good; and there was no man of the children of Israel better than he: from his shoulders upward he was taller than all the people.
And the donkeys of Kish, Saul's father, were lost; and Kish said to Saul his son: "Take, please, one of the servants with you, and rise — go seek the donkeys."

And he crossed the hill country of Ephraim, and crossed the land of Shalishah — and they did not find them; and they crossed the land of Shaalim — and nothing; and he crossed the land of the Benjaminites — and they did not find them.
They had come into the land of Zuph when Saul said to his servant who was with him: "Come, let us return — lest my father leave off caring about the donkeys and worry about us."

And he said to him: "Behold now — there is a man of God in this city, and the man is held in honor; all that he speaks surely comes to pass. Now let us go there. Perhaps he will tell us our way — the one we should walk."
And Saul said to his servant: "But behold — if we go, what shall we bring the man? For the bread is gone from our vessels, and there is no gift to bring the man of God. What do we have?"
And the servant answered Saul again and said: "Behold — found in my hand: a quarter shekel of silver. I will give it to the man of God — and he will tell us our way."
(Formerly in Israel, thus said a man when he went to inquire of God: "Come, let us go to the seer" — for the one called a prophet today was formerly called a seer.)
And Saul said to his servant: "Your word is good. Come — let us go." And they went to the city where the man of God was.
As they were going up the ascent of the city, they found young women coming out to draw water; and they said to them: "Is the seer here?"
And they answered them and said: "He is. Behold — ahead of you. Hurry now, for today he has come to the city, because the people have a sacrifice today at the high place.

As you come into the city, you will find him at once, before he goes up to the high place to eat; for the people will not eat until he comes — for he blesses the sacrifice, and afterward the invited eat. So go up now — for him — today you will find him."
And they went up to the city. As they were coming into the middle of the city — behold: Samuel coming out toward them, to go up to the high place.
Now YHWH had uncovered the ear of Samuel one day before Saul's coming:

"About this time tomorrow I will send you a man from the land of Benjamin, and you shall anoint him as leader over my people Israel; and he shall save my people from the hand of the Philistines — for I have seen my people, for their cry has come to me."
And Samuel saw Saul; and YHWH answered him: "Behold the man of whom I spoke to you. This one shall restrain my people."
And Saul drew near to Samuel in the middle of the gate and said: "Tell me, please — where is the house of the seer?"
And Samuel answered Saul and said: "I am the seer. Go up before me to the high place, and eat with me today; and in the morning I will send you off — and all that is in your heart I will tell you.

And as for the donkeys lost to you three days ago — do not set your heart on them, for they have been found. And to whom belongs all that is desirable in Israel? Is it not to you — and to all your father's house?"

And Saul answered and said: "Am I not a Benjaminite — from the smallest of the tribes of Israel? And my clan the least of all the clans of the tribe of Benjamin? Why then have you spoken to me a word like this?"
And Samuel took Saul and his servant and brought them into the hall, and gave them a place at the head of the invited — and they were about thirty men.
And Samuel said to the cook: "Give the portion I gave you — the one I told you: 'Set it aside with you.'"

And the cook lifted up the thigh and what was on it, and set it before Saul. And he said: "Behold what was kept — set it before you; eat. For it was kept for you for this appointed time, when I said: 'I have invited the people.'" So Saul ate with Samuel on that day.
And they came down from the high place into the city; and he spoke with Saul on the roof.

And they rose early; and it came to pass, at the going up of the dawn, that Samuel called to Saul on the roof: "Rise — and I will send you off." And Saul rose, and the two of them — he and Samuel — went outside.

As they were going down at the edge of the city, Samuel said to Saul: "Tell the servant to cross on ahead of us" — and he crossed on — "but you, stand still now, and I will let you hear the word of God."

1sa.10.text: And Samuel took the flask of oil and poured it on his head, and kissed him; and he said: "Has not YHWH anointed you as leader over his inheritance?

When you go from me today, you will find two men by Rachel's tomb, in the territory of Benjamin, at Zelzah; and they will say to you: 'The donkeys you went to seek have been found. And behold, your father has dropped the matter of the donkeys and is worrying about you, saying: What shall I do for my son?'

And you shall pass on from there and beyond, and come to the oak of Tabor; and three men will find you there, going up to God at Bethel — one carrying three young goats, and one carrying three rounds of bread, and one carrying a skin of wine;
and they will ask you of your welfare, and give you two loaves of bread — and you shall take it from their hand.

After that you will come to the Hill of God, where the garrisons of the Philistines are; and it shall be, as you come there to the city, that you will meet a band of prophets coming down from the high place — with harp and tambourine and flute and lyre before them, and they prophesying;
and the Spirit of YHWH will rush upon you, and you will prophesy with them — and you will be turned into another man.
And it shall be, when these signs come to you: do for yourself whatever your hand finds — for God is with you.

And you shall go down before me to Gilgal; and behold — I am coming down to you, to offer up burnt offerings, to sacrifice peace offerings. Seven days you shall wait, until I come to you and make known to you what you shall do."
And it came to pass, as he turned his shoulder to go from Samuel, that God turned him another heart; and all these signs came to pass on that day.
And they came there, to the hill — and behold: a band of prophets to meet him; and the Spirit of God rushed upon him, and he prophesied in their midst.

And it came to pass, when all who knew him from before saw — and behold, he prophesied with the prophets — that the people said, each man to his fellow: "What is this that has happened to the son of Kish? Is Saul also among the prophets?"
And a man from there answered and said: "And who is their father?" Therefore it became a proverb: "Is Saul also among the prophets?"
And when he had finished prophesying, he came to the high place.
And Saul's uncle said to him and to his servant: "Where did you go?" And he said: "To seek the donkeys; and when we saw they were nowhere, we went to Samuel."
And Saul's uncle said: "Tell me, please — what did Samuel say to you?"
And Saul said to his uncle: "He told us plainly that the donkeys had been found." But the matter of the kingship, of which Samuel had spoken, he did not tell him.
And Samuel summoned the people to YHWH at Mizpah;

and he said to the children of Israel: "Thus says YHWH, the God of Israel: I brought Israel up from Egypt, and I rescued you from the hand of Egypt and from the hand of all the kingdoms oppressing you.

But you — today you have rejected your God, who himself saves you from all your evils and your distresses; and you have said to him: 'No — set a king over us.' So now: take your stand before YHWH, by your tribes and by your thousands."
And Samuel brought near all the tribes of Israel — and the tribe of Benjamin was taken;
and he brought near the tribe of Benjamin by its clans — and the clan of the Matrites was taken; and Saul son of Kish was taken. And they sought him — and he was not found.
And they asked again of YHWH: "Has the man come here yet?" And YHWH said: "Behold — he has hidden himself among the baggage."
And they ran and took him from there; and he took his stand in the midst of the people — and he was taller than all the people, from his shoulders upward.
And Samuel said to all the people: "Do you see him whom YHWH has chosen? For there is none like him in all the people!" And all the people shouted and said: "Long live the king!"
And Samuel spoke to the people the custom of the kingship, and wrote it in the scroll, and laid it down before YHWH. And Samuel sent all the people away, each man to his house.
