And Saul too went to his house, to Gibeah; and there went with him the men of valor whose hearts God had touched.
But sons of worthlessness said: "How shall this one save us?" And they despised him, and brought him no gift. But he was as one keeping silent.

1sa.11.text: And Nahash the Ammonite came up and camped against Jabesh-gilead; and all the men of Jabesh said to Nahash: "Cut a covenant with us — and we will serve you."
And Nahash the Ammonite said to them: "On this condition I will cut it with you: that I gouge out every right eye of yours — and lay it as a reproach on all Israel."
And the elders of Jabesh said to him: "Let us alone seven days, that we may send messengers through all the territory of Israel; and if there is no one to save us — we will come out to you."
And the messengers came to Gibeah of Saul and spoke the words in the ears of the people — and all the people lifted their voice and wept.
And behold — Saul coming behind the oxen from the field. And Saul said: "What is the matter with the people, that they weep?" And they recounted to him the words of the men of Jabesh.
And the Spirit of God rushed upon Saul when he heard these words — and his anger burned exceedingly.

And he took a yoke of oxen and cut them in pieces, and sent them through all the territory of Israel by the hand of the messengers, saying: "Whoever does not come out after Saul and after Samuel — so shall it be done to his oxen!" And the dread of YHWH fell on the people — and they came out as one man.
And he mustered them at Bezek; and the children of Israel were three hundred thousand, and the men of Judah thirty thousand.

And they said to the messengers who had come: "Thus shall you say to the men of Jabesh-gilead: Tomorrow, by the heat of the sun, you shall have deliverance." And the messengers came and told the men of Jabesh — and they rejoiced;
and the men of Jabesh said: "Tomorrow we will come out to you — and you may do to us whatever is good in your eyes."

And it came to pass the next day that Saul set the people in three companies; and they came into the middle of the camp at the morning watch, and struck Ammon until the heat of the day. And those who were left were scattered — no two of them were left together.
And the people said to Samuel: "Who was it that said, 'Shall Saul reign over us?' Hand the men over — and we will put them to death!"
But Saul said: "No man shall be put to death this day — for today YHWH has worked deliverance in Israel."
And Samuel said to the people: "Come, let us go to Gilgal — and renew the kingship there."

And all the people went to Gilgal, and there they made Saul king before YHWH at Gilgal; and there they sacrificed peace offerings before YHWH — and there Saul rejoiced, and all the men of Israel, exceedingly.

1sa.12.text: And Samuel said to all Israel: "Behold — I have listened to your voice in all you said to me, and I have set a king over you.
And now — behold, the king walking before you. And I have grown old and gray, and my sons — behold, they are with you; and I have walked before you from my youth until this day.

Here I am. Testify against me before YHWH and before his anointed: Whose ox have I taken? And whose donkey have I taken? And whom have I oppressed? Whom have I crushed? And from whose hand have I taken a bribe, to shut my eyes with it? — and I will restore it to you."
And they said: "You have not oppressed us and you have not crushed us, and you have taken nothing from any man's hand."
And he said to them: "YHWH is witness against you, and his anointed is witness this day, that you have found nothing in my hand." And they said: "Witness!"
And Samuel said to the people: "YHWH — who made Moses and Aaron, and who brought your fathers up from the land of Egypt.
And now, take your stand, and I will enter into judgment with you before YHWH, concerning all the righteous acts of YHWH that he did for you and your fathers.
When Jacob came to Egypt, and your fathers cried out to YHWH, YHWH sent Moses and Aaron, and they brought your fathers out of Egypt, and settled them in this place.

But they forgot YHWH their God; and he sold them into the hand of Sisera, commander of the army of Hazor, and into the hand of the Philistines, and into the hand of the king of Moab — and they fought against them.
And they cried out to YHWH and said: 'We have sinned — for we abandoned YHWH and served the Baals and the Ashtaroth. But now — rescue us from the hand of our enemies, and we will serve you.'
And YHWH sent Jerubbaal and Bedan and Jephthah and Samuel, and rescued you from the hand of your enemies all around — and you lived in security.
But when you saw that Nahash king of the sons of Ammon was coming against you, you said to me: 'No — a king shall reign over us!' — when YHWH your God was your king.
So now — behold the king you have chosen, whom you asked for. And behold: YHWH has set a king over you.
If you fear YHWH and serve him and listen to his voice, and do not rebel against the mouth of YHWH — then both you and the king who reigns over you will be followers after YHWH your God.
But if you do not listen to the voice of YHWH, and you rebel against the mouth of YHWH — then the hand of YHWH will be against you, as it was against your fathers.
Even now — take your stand and see this great thing that YHWH is doing before your eyes.

Is it not wheat harvest today? I will call to YHWH, and he will give thunders and rain — and know and see that your evil is great, which you have done in the eyes of YHWH, in asking a king for yourselves."
And Samuel called to YHWH, and YHWH gave thunders and rain on that day — and all the people greatly feared YHWH and Samuel.
And all the people said to Samuel: "Pray for your servants to YHWH your God, that we may not die — for we have added to all our sins an evil: to ask a king for ourselves."
And Samuel said to the people: "Do not fear. You have done all this evil — only do not turn aside from following YHWH; and serve YHWH with all your heart.
And do not turn aside — for that would be after the empty things that cannot profit and cannot rescue, because they are emptiness.
For YHWH will not cast off his people, for the sake of his great name — because YHWH has been pleased to make you a people for himself.
As for me also — far be it from me to sin against YHWH by ceasing to pray for you; and I will teach you the good and straight way.
Only fear YHWH, and serve him in truth, with all your heart — for see what great things he has done with you.
But if you surely do evil — both you and your king will be swept away."

1sa.13.text: Saul was a year old when he became king — and two years he reigned over Israel.

And Saul chose himself three thousand from Israel: two thousand were with Saul at Michmash and in the hill country of Bethel, and a thousand were with Jonathan at Gibeah of Benjamin; and the rest of the people he sent away, each man to his tents.
And Jonathan struck the garrison of the Philistines that was at Geba — and the Philistines heard. And Saul blew the shofar through all the land, saying: "Let the Hebrews hear!"
And all Israel heard it said: "Saul has struck the garrison of the Philistines — and Israel has also made itself a stench to the Philistines." And the people were summoned after Saul to Gilgal.

And the Philistines gathered to fight with Israel: thirty thousand chariots, and six thousand horsemen, and people like the sand on the seashore in multitude; and they came up and camped at Michmash, east of Beth-aven.

And the men of Israel saw that they were in a tight place — for the people were hard pressed; and the people hid themselves in the caves and in the thickets and in the crags and in the vaults and in the pits;
and Hebrews crossed the Jordan, to the land of Gad and Gilead. But Saul was still at Gilgal — and all the people trembled behind him.
And he waited seven days, to the appointed time Samuel had set; but Samuel did not come to Gilgal — and the people began scattering from him.
And Saul said: "Bring to me the burnt offering and the peace offerings." And he offered up the burnt offering.
And it came to pass, as he finished offering up the burnt offering — behold: Samuel coming. And Saul went out to meet him, to bless him.

And Samuel said: "What have you done?" And Saul said: "Because I saw that the people were scattering from me, and you had not come at the appointed days, and the Philistines were gathering at Michmash,
I said: 'Now the Philistines will come down on me at Gilgal — and I have not softened the face of YHWH.' So I forced myself, and offered up the burnt offering."

And Samuel said to Saul: "You have acted the fool. You have not kept the commandment of YHWH your God, which he commanded you. For just now YHWH would have established your kingdom over Israel to the age;
but now your kingdom shall not stand. YHWH has sought for himself a man after his own heart, and YHWH has appointed him as leader over his people — because you have not kept what YHWH commanded you."
And Samuel rose and went up from Gilgal to Gibeah of Benjamin. And Saul mustered the people found with him: about six hundred men.
And Saul and Jonathan his son, and the people found with them, were staying in Geba of Benjamin — and the Philistines camped at Michmash.
And the raiding force went out of the camp of the Philistines in three companies: one company turned toward the Ophrah road, to the land of Shual;
and one company turned toward the Beth-horon road; and one company turned toward the border road overlooking the Valley of the Hyenas, toward the wilderness.
Now no smith was to be found in all the land of Israel — for the Philistines had said: "Lest the Hebrews make sword or spear."
So all Israel went down to the Philistines, each man to sharpen his plowshare and his mattock and his axe and his sickle;
and the charge was a pim for the plowshares and the mattocks and the three-pronged forks and the axes — and for setting the goads.
And it came to pass, on the day of battle, that neither sword nor spear was found in the hand of any of the people with Saul and Jonathan — but they were found for Saul and for Jonathan his son.
And a garrison of the Philistines went out to the pass of Michmash.

1sa.14.text: And the day came when Jonathan son of Saul said to the young man carrying his armor: "Come — let us cross over to the Philistine garrison on the other side there." But he did not tell his father.
And Saul was sitting at the edge of Gibeah, under the pomegranate tree at Migron; and the people with him were about six hundred men —
and Ahijah son of Ahitub — brother of Ichabod, son of Phinehas, son of Eli, the priest of YHWH at Shiloh — was carrying an ephod. And the people did not know that Jonathan had gone.

And between the passes by which Jonathan sought to cross to the Philistine garrison there was a tooth of rock on one side and a tooth of rock on the other; the name of one was Bozez, and the name of the other Seneh —
the one tooth a pillar on the north, opposite Michmash, and the other on the south, opposite Geba.

And Jonathan said to the young man carrying his armor: "Come — let us cross over to the garrison of these uncircumcised. Perhaps YHWH will act for us — for there is no restraint on YHWH from saving by many or by few."
And his armor-bearer said to him: "Do all that is in your heart. Turn where you will — behold, I am with you, heart to your heart."
And Jonathan said: "Behold — we are crossing over to the men, and we will show ourselves to them.
If they say to us, 'Stand still until we reach you' — then we will stand in our place and not go up to them.
But if they say, 'Come up to us' — then we will go up, for YHWH has given them into our hand; and this shall be the sign for us."
And the two of them showed themselves to the Philistine garrison; and the Philistines said: "Behold — Hebrews coming out of the holes where they hid themselves!"

And the men of the garrison answered Jonathan and his armor-bearer and said: "Come up to us — and we will teach you a thing!" And Jonathan said to his armor-bearer: "Come up behind me — for YHWH has given them into the hand of Israel."
And Jonathan climbed up on his hands and on his feet, with his armor-bearer behind him; and they fell before Jonathan — and his armor-bearer kept killing behind him.
And the first blow that Jonathan and his armor-bearer struck was about twenty men — within about half a furrow's length of a yoke of field.
And there was trembling in the camp, in the field, and among all the people; the garrison and the raiders trembled — they too; and the earth quaked, and it became a trembling of God.
And Saul's watchmen at Gibeah of Benjamin looked — and behold: the multitude melting away, going this way and that.
And Saul said to the people with him: "Muster now — and see who has gone from us." And they mustered — and behold: Jonathan and his armor-bearer were missing.
And Saul said to Ahijah: "Bring near the ark of God." (For the ark of God was on that day with the children of Israel.)
And it came to pass, while Saul was speaking to the priest, that the uproar in the camp of the Philistines went on and grew; and Saul said to the priest: "Withdraw your hand."
And Saul and all the people with him were called together, and they came to the battle — and behold: every man's sword was against his fellow — a very great panic.
And the Hebrews who had been with the Philistines before, who had gone up with them into the camp all around — they too turned, to be with Israel, with Saul and Jonathan;
and all the men of Israel who had hidden themselves in the hill country of Ephraim heard that the Philistines had fled — and they too clung after them in the battle.
So YHWH saved Israel on that day; and the battle crossed over past Beth-aven.

And the men of Israel were hard pressed on that day; and Saul laid an oath on the people: "Cursed be the man who eats bread before evening, before I am avenged on my enemies!" And none of the people tasted bread.
And all the land came into the forest — and there was honey on the face of the field;
and the people came into the forest — and behold: a flow of honey. But no one brought his hand to his mouth, for the people feared the oath.

But Jonathan had not heard when his father put the people under oath; and he stretched out the tip of the staff in his hand and dipped it in the honeycomb, and brought his hand back to his mouth — and his eyes lit up.
And a man of the people answered and said: "Your father put the people under a strict oath: 'Cursed be the man who eats bread today.'" And the people were faint.
And Jonathan said: "My father has troubled the land. See, please, how my eyes have lit up — because I tasted a little of this honey.
How much more, if the people had eaten freely today from the spoil of their enemies which they found — would not the blow against the Philistines now have been greater?"
And they struck the Philistines that day from Michmash to Aijalon — and the people were very faint;
and the people pounced on the spoil, and took sheep and oxen and calves and slaughtered them on the ground — and the people ate with the blood.
And they told Saul: "Behold — the people are sinning against YHWH, eating with the blood." And he said: "You have dealt treacherously. Roll a great stone to me today."

And Saul said: "Scatter among the people and say to them: 'Bring to me, each man his ox and each man his sheep, and slaughter them here and eat — and do not sin against YHWH by eating with the blood.'" And all the people brought, each man his ox in his hand that night, and slaughtered them there.
And Saul built an altar to YHWH; it was the first altar he built to YHWH.

And Saul said: "Let us go down after the Philistines by night and plunder them until the morning light, and let us not leave a man among them." And they said: "Do whatever is good in your eyes." But the priest said: "Let us draw near here — to God."
And Saul asked of God: "Shall I go down after the Philistines? Will you give them into the hand of Israel?" But he did not answer him on that day.
And Saul said: "Draw near here, all you chiefs of the people — and know and see wherein this sin has come to pass today.
For as YHWH lives, who saves Israel — even if it is in Jonathan my son, he shall surely die." And not one of all the people answered him.
And he said to all Israel: "You be on one side, and I and Jonathan my son will be on the other side." And the people said to Saul: "Do what is good in your eyes."
And Saul said to YHWH, the God of Israel: "Give a perfect lot." And Jonathan and Saul were taken — and the people went free.
And Saul said: "Cast between me and Jonathan my son." And Jonathan was taken.
And Saul said to Jonathan: "Tell me what you have done." And Jonathan told him and said: "I did indeed taste a little honey with the tip of the staff in my hand. Here I am — I will die."
And Saul said: "So may God do, and so may he add — you shall surely die, Jonathan."

But the people said to Saul: "Shall Jonathan die — who has worked this great salvation in Israel? Far be it! As YHWH lives, not a hair of his head shall fall to the ground — for with God he has worked this day." So the people ransomed Jonathan, and he did not die.
And Saul went up from pursuing the Philistines; and the Philistines went to their own place.

And Saul took hold of the kingship over Israel; and he fought all around against all his enemies — against Moab, and against the sons of Ammon, and against Edom, and against the kings of Zobah, and against the Philistines; and wherever he turned, he brought punishment.
And he acted with valor, and struck Amalek — and rescued Israel from the hand of its plunderer.
And the sons of Saul were Jonathan and Ishvi and Malchi-shua; and the names of his two daughters: the name of the firstborn Merab, and the name of the younger Michal.
And the name of Saul's wife was Ahinoam daughter of Ahimaaz; and the name of the commander of his army was Abner son of Ner, Saul's uncle;
and Kish, Saul's father, and Ner, Abner's father, were sons of Abiel.
And the war against the Philistines was hard all the days of Saul; and whenever Saul saw any mighty man or any son of valor — he gathered him to himself.

1sa.15.text: And Samuel said to Saul: "It was me YHWH sent to anoint you as king over his people, over Israel. So now — listen to the voice of the words of YHWH.
Thus says YHWH of Armies: I have taken account of what Amalek did to Israel — how he set himself against him on the road, when he came up from Egypt.
Now go, and strike Amalek, and devote to destruction all that he has; and do not spare him — but put to death from man to woman, from child to nursing infant, from ox to sheep, from camel to donkey."
And Saul summoned the people and mustered them at Telaim: two hundred thousand on foot, and ten thousand men of Judah.
And Saul came to the city of Amalek, and lay in wait in the wadi.

And Saul said to the Kenites: "Go, turn aside, come down from among the Amalekites — lest I sweep you away with them; for you showed steadfast love to all the children of Israel when they came up from Egypt." And the Kenites turned aside from among Amalek.
And Saul struck Amalek, from Havilah as far as the approach to Shur, which faces Egypt;
and he seized Agag king of Amalek alive — and all the people he devoted to destruction with the mouth of the sword.

But Saul and the people spared Agag, and the best of the flock and the herd — the fatlings and the lambs — and all that was good; and they were not willing to devote them to destruction. But everything despised and worthless — that they devoted to destruction.
And the word of YHWH came to Samuel:
"I regret that I made Saul king — for he has turned back from following me, and my words he has not carried out." And it burned in Samuel, and he cried out to YHWH all night.

And Samuel rose early to meet Saul in the morning; and it was told to Samuel: "Saul came to Carmel — and behold, he is setting up a monument for himself; and he turned and crossed over and went down to Gilgal."
And Samuel came to Saul; and Saul said to him: "Blessed are you to YHWH! I have carried out the word of YHWH."
And Samuel said: "Then what is this sound of sheep in my ears — and the sound of oxen that I hear?"
And Saul said: "From the Amalekites they brought them — because the people spared the best of the sheep and the oxen, to sacrifice to YHWH your God; and the rest we devoted to destruction."
And Samuel said to Saul: "Stop! — and I will tell you what YHWH spoke to me this night." And he said to him: "Speak."
And Samuel said: "Though you are small in your own eyes — are you not head of the tribes of Israel? And YHWH anointed you king over Israel.
And YHWH sent you on a road and said: 'Go, and devote to destruction the sinners — Amalek — and fight against him until they are finished.'
Why then did you not listen to the voice of YHWH — but pounced on the spoil, and did the evil in the eyes of YHWH?"
And Saul said to Samuel: "But I did listen to the voice of YHWH — and I went on the road YHWH sent me, and I brought Agag king of Amalek, and Amalek I devoted to destruction;
and the people took from the spoil sheep and oxen, the first of the devoted things, to sacrifice to YHWH your God at Gilgal."
And Samuel said: "Does YHWH delight in burnt offerings and sacrifices as in listening to the voice of YHWH? Behold — to listen is better than sacrifice; to pay heed, than the fat of rams.
For rebellion is the sin of divination; and stubbornness is wickedness and household idols. Because you have rejected the word of YHWH — he has rejected you from being king."
And Saul said to Samuel: "I have sinned — for I have crossed the mouth of YHWH, and your words; because I feared the people, and listened to their voice.
And now — carry away my sin, please, and return with me, and I will bow down to YHWH."
And Samuel said to Saul: "I will not return with you — for you have rejected the word of YHWH, and YHWH has rejected you from being king over Israel."
And Samuel turned to go — and he seized the corner of his robe, and it tore.
And Samuel said to him: "YHWH has torn the kingdom of Israel from you this day — and given it to your fellow, who is better than you.
And also — the Everlasting One of Israel does not lie and does not change his mind; for he is not a human being, that he should change his mind."
And he said: "I have sinned. Now — honor me, please, before the elders of my people and before Israel; and return with me, and I will bow down to YHWH your God."
So Samuel returned after Saul; and Saul bowed down to YHWH.
And Samuel said: "Bring Agag king of Amalek near to me." And Agag came to him in fetters; and Agag said: "Surely the bitterness of death has turned aside."
And Samuel said: "As your sword has made women childless, so shall your mother be childless among women." And Samuel hewed Agag in pieces before YHWH at Gilgal.
And Samuel went to Ramah; and Saul went up to his house, at Gibeah of Saul.
And Samuel did not see Saul again until the day of his death — for Samuel mourned over Saul; and YHWH regretted that he had made Saul king over Israel.

1sa.16.text: And YHWH said to Samuel: "How long will you mourn over Saul, when I have rejected him from reigning over Israel? Fill your horn with oil, and go — I am sending you to Jesse the Bethlehemite; for I have seen among his sons a king for myself."
And Samuel said: "How can I go? Saul will hear of it and kill me." And YHWH said: "Take a heifer of the herd in your hand, and say: 'I have come to sacrifice to YHWH.'
And call Jesse to the sacrifice; and I myself will make known to you what you shall do — and you shall anoint for me the one I say to you."
And Samuel did what YHWH spoke, and came to Bethlehem; and the elders of the city trembled to meet him, and one said: "Is your coming peace?"
And he said: "Peace. I have come to sacrifice to YHWH. Consecrate yourselves, and come with me to the sacrifice." And he consecrated Jesse and his sons, and called them to the sacrifice.
And it came to pass, as they came, that he saw Eliab and said: "Surely — before YHWH stands his anointed."

But YHWH said to Samuel: "Do not look at his appearance, or at the height of his stature — for I have rejected him. For it is not what a human sees: the human sees with the eyes — but YHWH sees into the heart."
And Jesse called Abinadab and made him pass before Samuel; and he said: "Neither has YHWH chosen this one."
And Jesse made Shammah pass by; and he said: "Neither has YHWH chosen this one."
And Jesse made seven of his sons pass before Samuel; and Samuel said to Jesse: "YHWH has not chosen these."

And Samuel said to Jesse: "Are the young men all here?" And he said: "There still remains the youngest — and behold, he is shepherding the flock." And Samuel said to Jesse: "Send and fetch him — for we will not sit down to table until he comes here."
And he sent and brought him in. And he was ruddy, with beautiful eyes, and good to look at. And YHWH said: "Rise, anoint him — for this is he."
And Samuel took the horn of oil and anointed him in the midst of his brothers; and the Spirit of YHWH rushed upon David from that day forward. And Samuel rose and went to Ramah.
And the Spirit of YHWH turned aside from Saul — and an evil spirit from YHWH terrified him.
And Saul's servants said to him: "Behold now — an evil spirit of God is terrifying you.

Let our lord speak: your servants are before you. Let them seek a man who knows how to play the lyre; and it shall be, when the evil spirit of God is upon you, that he will play with his hand — and it will be well with you."
And Saul said to his servants: "See out for me, please, a man who plays well — and bring him to me."

And one of the young men answered and said: "Behold — I have seen a son of Jesse the Bethlehemite who knows how to play; a mighty man of valor, and a man of war, and discerning in speech, and a man of good form — and YHWH is with him."
And Saul sent messengers to Jesse and said: "Send me David your son, who is with the flock."
And Jesse took a donkey-load of bread, and a skin of wine, and one young goat, and sent them by the hand of David his son to Saul.
And David came to Saul and stood before him; and he loved him greatly — and he became his armor-bearer.
And Saul sent to Jesse: "Let David stand before me, please — for he has found favor in my eyes."

And it came to pass, whenever the spirit of God was upon Saul, that David took the lyre and played with his hand; and Saul found relief, and it was well with him — and the evil spirit turned aside from him.

1sa.17.text: And the Philistines gathered their camps for battle; and they gathered at Socoh, which belongs to Judah, and camped between Socoh and Azekah, at Ephes-dammim.
And Saul and the men of Israel gathered and camped in the Valley of Elah; and they drew up battle order to meet the Philistines.
And the Philistines were standing on the mountain on one side, and Israel standing on the mountain on the other side — with the ravine between them.
And the man of the space-between came out from the camps of the Philistines — Goliath his name, from Gath: his height six cubits and a span;
and a helmet of bronze on his head, and armor of scales he wore — and the weight of the armor was five thousand shekels of bronze;
and greaves of bronze on his legs, and a javelin of bronze between his shoulders;
and the shaft of his spear was like a weavers' beam, and the blade of his spear six hundred shekels of iron — and the shield-carrier walked before him.

And he stood and called to the battle lines of Israel and said to them: "Why do you come out to draw up for battle? Am I not the Philistine, and you servants of Saul? Choose yourselves a man — and let him come down to me.
If he is able to fight with me and strikes me down — then we will be your servants; but if I prevail over him and strike him down — then you shall be our servants, and serve us."
And the Philistine said: "I defy the battle lines of Israel this day! Give me a man — and let us fight together."
And Saul and all Israel heard these words of the Philistine — and they were shattered, and feared greatly.
Now David was the son of that Ephrathite man from Bethlehem of Judah whose name was Jesse; and he had eight sons. And in the days of Saul the man was old, come along among men.

And the three oldest sons of Jesse had gone — they went after Saul to the battle; and the names of his three sons who went to the battle: Eliab the firstborn, and his second Abinadab, and the third Shammah.
And David — he was the youngest; and the three oldest went after Saul.
And David went back and forth from Saul, to shepherd his father's flock at Bethlehem.
And the Philistine drew near morning and evening, and took his stand — forty days.
And Jesse said to David his son: "Take, please, for your brothers, an ephah of this roasted grain and these ten loaves of bread, and run them to the camp, to your brothers;
and these ten cuts of cheese bring to the commander of the thousand; and look in on your brothers' welfare, and take their pledge."
Now Saul and they and all the men of Israel were in the Valley of Elah, fighting with the Philistines.

And David rose early in the morning and left the flock with a keeper, and lifted his load and went, as Jesse had commanded him; and he came to the wagon-circle as the army was going out to the battle line, shouting the war cry —
and Israel and the Philistines drew up, battle line to meet battle line.
And David left the things off him in the care of the keeper of the baggage, and ran to the battle line; and he came and asked his brothers of their welfare.

And as he was speaking with them — behold: the man of the space-between coming up, Goliath the Philistine by name, from Gath, from the ranks of the Philistines; and he spoke the same words — and David heard.
And all the men of Israel, when they saw the man, fled from before him and feared greatly.

And the men of Israel said: "Have you seen this man coming up? It is to defy Israel that he comes up. And it shall be: the man who strikes him down — the king will enrich him with great riches, and give him his daughter, and make his father's house free in Israel."

And David said to the men standing with him: "What shall be done for the man who strikes down this Philistine and turns aside the reproach from Israel? For who is this uncircumcised Philistine — that he defies the battle lines of the living God?"
And the people told him the same word: "So shall it be done for the man who strikes him down."

And Eliab his oldest brother heard him speaking to the men; and Eliab's anger burned against David, and he said: "Why have you come down? And with whom did you leave those few sheep in the wilderness? I know your insolence and the evil of your heart — for you have come down to watch the battle!"
And David said: "What have I done now? Was it not only a word?"
And he turned away from him toward another and said the same thing; and the people gave him back word, as the first word.
And the words David spoke were heard, and they told them before Saul — and he had him fetched.
And David said to Saul: "Let no man's heart fall because of him. Your servant will go and fight with this Philistine."
And Saul said to David: "You are not able to go against this Philistine, to fight with him — for you are a boy, and he a man of war from his youth."
And David said to Saul: "Your servant was shepherding for his father among the flock; and the lion came — and the bear — and carried off a sheep from the herd;
and I went out after it and struck it, and rescued the sheep from its mouth; and when it rose against me, I seized it by its beard and struck it — and killed it.
Both the lion and the bear your servant has struck down; and this uncircumcised Philistine shall be like one of them — for he has defied the battle lines of the living God."

And David said: "YHWH, who rescued me from the paw of the lion and from the paw of the bear — he will rescue me from the hand of this Philistine." And Saul said to David: "Go — and may YHWH be with you."
And Saul dressed David in his own gear, and set a helmet of bronze on his head, and dressed him in armor.
And David girded on his sword over his gear, and tried to walk — for he had not tested them; and David said to Saul: "I cannot walk in these — for I have not tested them." And David took them off.

And he took his staff in his hand, and chose himself five smooth stones from the wadi, and put them in the shepherds' bag he had — in the pouch — with his sling in his hand; and he drew near to the Philistine.
And the Philistine came on, coming nearer and nearer to David, with the man carrying the shield before him.
And the Philistine looked, and saw David — and despised him; for he was a boy, ruddy, with a beautiful face.
And the Philistine said to David: "Am I a dog — that you come at me with sticks?" And the Philistine cursed David by his gods.
And the Philistine said to David: "Come to me — and I will give your flesh to the birds of the heavens and the beasts of the field."

And David said to the Philistine: "You come at me with sword and with spear and with javelin — but I come at you in the name of YHWH of Armies, the God of the battle lines of Israel, whom you have defied.

This day YHWH will shut you into my hand, and I will strike you down and take your head off you; and I will give the corpses of the camp of the Philistines this day to the birds of the heavens and the wild animals of the earth — and all the earth shall know that Israel has a God;
and all this assembly shall know that not by sword and spear does YHWH save — for the battle belongs to YHWH, and he will give you into our hand."
And it came to pass, when the Philistine rose and came and drew near to meet David, that David hurried and ran toward the battle line to meet the Philistine;
and David put his hand into the bag and took from it a stone, and slung it — and struck the Philistine in his forehead; and the stone sank into his forehead, and he fell on his face to the ground.
So David prevailed over the Philistine with the sling and with the stone; and he struck the Philistine down and killed him — and there was no sword in David's hand.

And David ran and stood over the Philistine, and took his sword and drew it from its sheath, and finished him — and cut off his head with it. And the Philistines saw that their mighty one was dead — and they fled.

And the men of Israel and Judah rose and shouted, and pursued the Philistines as far as the approach to the ravine, and to the gates of Ekron; and the slain of the Philistines fell along the Shaaraim road, as far as Gath and as far as Ekron.
And the children of Israel returned from hotly pursuing the Philistines — and plundered their camps.
And David took the head of the Philistine and brought it to Jerusalem; and his weapons he put in his own tent.
And when Saul saw David going out to meet the Philistine, he said to Abner, the commander of the army: "Whose son is this boy, Abner?" And Abner said: "As your soul lives, O king — I do not know."
And the king said: "You ask whose son the young man is."
And as David returned from striking down the Philistine, Abner took him and brought him before Saul — with the head of the Philistine in his hand.
And Saul said to him: "Whose son are you, boy?" And David said: "The son of your servant Jesse the Bethlehemite."

1sa.18.text: And it came to pass, when he had finished speaking to Saul, that the soul of Jonathan was bound up with the soul of David — and Jonathan loved him as his own soul.
And Saul took him that day, and did not let him return to his father's house.
And Jonathan and David cut a covenant — because he loved him as his own soul;
and Jonathan stripped off the robe that was on him and gave it to David — and his gear, even to his sword and to his bow and to his belt.
And David went out wherever Saul sent him, and succeeded; and Saul set him over the men of war. And it was good in the eyes of all the people — and also in the eyes of Saul's servants.

And it came to pass, as they came in — as David returned from striking down the Philistine — that the women came out from all the cities of Israel, singing and dancing, to meet Saul the king, with tambourines, with joy, and with triangles.
And the women making merry sang out, and said: "Saul has struck down his thousands — and David his ten thousands!"

And it burned in Saul exceedingly, and this word was evil in his eyes; and he said: "To David they have given ten thousands, and to me they have given the thousands — all he lacks now is the kingship!"
And Saul kept a jealous eye on David from that day forward.
And it came to pass the next day that an evil spirit of God rushed upon Saul, and he raved inside the house — while David was playing with his hand, as day by day; and the spear was in Saul's hand.
And Saul hurled the spear, and said: "I will pin David to the wall!" But David turned aside from before him — twice.
And Saul was afraid of David — because YHWH was with him, and had turned aside from Saul.
So Saul removed him from his presence and set him as a commander of a thousand; and he went out and came in before the people.
And David succeeded in all his ways — and YHWH was with him.
And Saul saw that he was succeeding greatly — and he dreaded him.
But all Israel and Judah loved David — for it was he who went out and came in before them.

And Saul said to David: "Behold my older daughter Merab — her I will give you as a wife; only be a son of valor for me, and fight the battles of YHWH." For Saul said to himself: "Let not my hand be on him — let the hand of the Philistines be on him."
And David said to Saul: "Who am I — and what is my life, my father's clan in Israel — that I should be son-in-law to the king?"
But it came to pass, at the time for giving Merab, Saul's daughter, to David, that she was given to Adriel the Meholathite as a wife.
And Michal, Saul's daughter, loved David; and they told Saul — and the thing was right in his eyes.
And Saul said: "I will give her to him — and let her be a snare to him, and let the hand of the Philistines be on him." And Saul said to David: "By the second you shall become my son-in-law today."
And Saul commanded his servants: "Speak to David quietly: 'Behold — the king delights in you, and all his servants love you. So now — become son-in-law to the king.'"
And Saul's servants spoke these words in David's ears; and David said: "Is it a light thing in your eyes to become son-in-law to the king — when I am a poor man, and lightly esteemed?"
And Saul's servants told him: "Words like these David spoke."

And Saul said: "Thus shall you say to David: 'The king has no delight in bride-price — but in a hundred foreskins of the Philistines, to be avenged on the king's enemies.'" For Saul planned to make David fall by the hand of the Philistines.
And his servants told David these words; and the thing was right in David's eyes, to become son-in-law to the king. And the days were not yet full

when David rose and went — he and his men — and struck down among the Philistines two hundred men; and David brought their foreskins, and they counted them out in full to the king, that he might become the king's son-in-law. And Saul gave him Michal his daughter as a wife.
And Saul saw and knew that YHWH was with David — and Michal, Saul's daughter, loved him;
and Saul was still more afraid of David. And Saul was David's enemy all the days.
And the commanders of the Philistines came out; and it came to pass, as often as they came out, that David succeeded more than all the servants of Saul — and his name became very precious.

1sa.19.text: And Saul spoke to Jonathan his son and to all his servants about putting David to death; but Jonathan, Saul's son, delighted greatly in David.
And Jonathan told David: "Saul my father is seeking to put you to death. So now — be on guard, please, in the morning; and stay in a secret place, and hide yourself.
And I will go out and stand beside my father in the field where you are, and I will speak of you to my father; and whatever I see, I will tell you."

And Jonathan spoke good of David to Saul his father, and said to him: "Let not the king sin against his servant, against David — for he has not sinned against you, and his deeds have been very good for you:

he put his life in his palm and struck down the Philistine, and YHWH worked a great salvation for all Israel. You saw it, and you rejoiced. Why then would you sin against innocent blood, putting David to death for nothing?"
And Saul listened to the voice of Jonathan; and Saul swore: "As YHWH lives — he shall not be put to death."
And Jonathan called David, and Jonathan told him all these words; and Jonathan brought David to Saul, and he was in his presence as before.
And the war went on again; and David went out and fought against the Philistines, and struck them a great blow — and they fled before him.
And an evil spirit of YHWH came upon Saul as he sat in his house with his spear in his hand — and David was playing with his hand;
and Saul sought to pin David to the wall with the spear, but he slipped away from before Saul, and he struck the spear into the wall. And David fled and escaped that night.
And Saul sent messengers to David's house to watch it and to put him to death in the morning; and Michal his wife told David: "If you do not save your life tonight — tomorrow you are put to death."
And Michal let David down through the window; and he went, and fled, and escaped.
And Michal took the household idol and laid it on the bed, and put the quilt of goats' hair at its head, and covered it with the garment.
And Saul sent messengers to take David; and she said: "He is sick."
And Saul sent the messengers back to see David: "Bring him up to me in the bed — to put him to death."
And the messengers came in — and behold: the household idol on the bed, with the quilt of goats' hair at its head.
And Saul said to Michal: "Why have you deceived me like this, and sent away my enemy so that he escaped?" And Michal said to Saul: "He said to me, 'Send me away — why should I kill you?'"
And David fled and escaped, and came to Samuel at Ramah, and told him all that Saul had done to him; and he went — he and Samuel — and they stayed at Naioth.
And it was told to Saul: "Behold — David is at Naioth in Ramah."

And Saul sent messengers to take David; and they saw the company of the prophets prophesying, with Samuel standing as head over them — and the Spirit of God came upon Saul's messengers, and they also prophesied.
And they told Saul, and he sent other messengers — and they also prophesied; and Saul sent messengers again, a third set — and they also prophesied.
Then he went himself to Ramah, and came to the great cistern at Secu; and he asked: "Where are Samuel and David?" And one said: "Behold — at Naioth in Ramah."
And he went there, toward Naioth in Ramah; and the Spirit of God came upon him also — and he walked on, prophesying, until he came to Naioth in Ramah.
And he too stripped off his garments, and he too prophesied before Samuel; and he lay naked all that day and all that night. Therefore they say: "Is Saul also among the prophets?"

1sa.20.text: And David fled from Naioth in Ramah, and came and said before Jonathan: "What have I done? What is my guilt, and what my sin before your father — that he seeks my life?"
And he said to him: "Far be it! You shall not die. Behold — my father does nothing great or small without uncovering my ear. Why would my father hide this thing from me? It is not so."

But David swore again and said: "Your father knows well that I have found favor in your eyes; and he says: 'Let Jonathan not know this, lest he be grieved.' But truly — as YHWH lives, and as your soul lives: there is but a step between me and death."
And Jonathan said to David: "Whatever your soul says — I will do for you."
And David said to Jonathan: "Behold — tomorrow is the new moon, and I should surely sit with the king to eat. Send me away, and I will hide in the field until the third evening.
If your father misses me at all, then say: 'David asked urgent leave of me to run to Bethlehem his city — for the yearly sacrifice is there for the whole clan.'
If he says, 'Good' — peace to your servant; but if it truly burns in him — know that the evil is settled with him.

And show steadfast love to your servant — for into a covenant of YHWH you have brought your servant with you. But if there is guilt in me — put me to death yourself; why should you bring me to your father?"
And Jonathan said: "Far be it from you! For if I knew for certain that the evil was settled with my father, to come upon you — would I not tell it to you?"
And David said to Jonathan: "Who will tell me — if your father answers you harshly?"
And Jonathan said to David: "Come — let us go out to the field." And the two of them went out to the field.

And Jonathan said to David: "By YHWH, the God of Israel! When I have sounded out my father about this time tomorrow, or the third day — and behold, it is good toward David — will I not then send to you and uncover your ear?

So may YHWH do to Jonathan, and so may he add: if it pleases my father to bring the evil upon you, I will uncover your ear and send you away, and you shall go in peace. And may YHWH be with you, as he was with my father.
And will you not, while I still live — will you not show me the steadfast love of YHWH, that I not die?
And do not cut off your steadfast love from my house — to the age; not even when YHWH cuts off the enemies of David, each one from the face of the ground."
So Jonathan cut a covenant with the house of David: "And may YHWH seek it from the hand of David's enemies."
And Jonathan made David swear again, by his love for him — for with the love of his own soul he loved him.
And Jonathan said to him: "Tomorrow is the new moon; and you will be missed — for your seat will be empty.
And on the third day go far down, and come to the place where you hid on the day of the deed, and stay beside the stone Ezel.
And I will shoot three arrows to its side, as though I shot at a target;

and behold — I will send the boy: 'Go, find the arrows.' If I say plainly to the boy, 'Behold — the arrows are on this side of you; take them' — then come, for there is peace for you and nothing wrong, as YHWH lives.
But if I say this to the young man: 'Behold — the arrows are beyond you' — go, for YHWH has sent you away.
And as for the word we have spoken, I and you — behold: YHWH is between me and you, to the age."
So David hid in the field. And the new moon came, and the king sat down to the food, to eat;
and the king sat on his seat, as at other times — on the seat by the wall; and Jonathan rose, and Abner sat at Saul's side — and David's place was empty.
And Saul said nothing that day; for he said: "Something has happened to him; he is not clean — surely he is not clean."

And it came to pass on the next day, the second of the new moon, that David's place was empty; and Saul said to Jonathan his son: "Why has the son of Jesse not come to the food — either yesterday or today?"
And Jonathan answered Saul: "David asked urgent leave of me, to go to Bethlehem;

and he said: 'Send me away, please, for our clan has a sacrifice in the city, and my brother himself has commanded me. So now — if I have found favor in your eyes, let me slip away, please, and see my brothers.' Therefore he has not come to the king's table."

And Saul's anger burned against Jonathan, and he said to him: "You son of a twisted rebellious woman! Do I not know that you are choosing the son of Jesse — to your own shame, and to the shame of your mother's nakedness?
For all the days the son of Jesse lives on the ground, you and your kingship will not be established. So now — send and fetch him to me, for he is a son of death!"
And Jonathan answered Saul his father and said to him: "Why should he be put to death? What has he done?"
And Saul hurled the spear at him, to strike him. And Jonathan knew that it was settled with his father to put David to death;
and Jonathan rose from the table in burning anger, and ate no food on the second day of the new moon — for he was grieved over David, because his father had humiliated him.
And it came to pass in the morning that Jonathan went out to the field, to the appointment with David — and a little boy with him.
And he said to his boy: "Run — find, please, the arrows I shoot." The boy ran — and he shot the arrow beyond him.
And the boy came to the place of the arrow Jonathan had shot; and Jonathan called after the boy and said: "Is not the arrow beyond you?"
And Jonathan called after the boy: "Quick! Hurry — do not stand still!" And Jonathan's boy gathered up the arrows and came to his master.
And the boy knew nothing; only Jonathan and David knew the matter.
And Jonathan gave his weapons to the boy who was his, and said to him: "Go — carry them into the city."
The boy went — and David rose from beside the south stone, and fell on his face to the ground, and bowed three times; and they kissed each other, and wept with each other — David the more.
And Jonathan said to David: "Go in peace — since the two of us have sworn, in the name of YHWH: YHWH shall be between me and you, and between my seed and your seed, to the age."

1sa.21.text: And he rose and went; and Jonathan came into the city.
And David came to Nob, to Ahimelech the priest; and Ahimelech trembled to meet David and said to him: "Why are you alone, and no man with you?"

And David said to Ahimelech the priest: "The king has commanded me a matter, and said to me: 'Let no man know anything of the business on which I send you and which I have commanded you.' And the young men I have directed to such-and-such a place.
So now — what do you have at hand? Five loaves of bread — give them into my hand; or whatever can be found."
And the priest answered David and said: "There is no common bread at hand — but there is holy bread; if only the young men have kept themselves from women."

And David answered the priest and said to him: "Indeed — women have been kept from us, as before, whenever I set out; and the vessels of the young men are holy, even when it is a common journey — how much more today, when there will be holiness in the vessel."
So the priest gave him holy bread — for there was no bread there except the Bread of the Presence, which is removed from before YHWH, to set hot bread in its place on the day it is taken away.
Now a man of Saul's servants was there that day, detained before YHWH; and his name was Doeg the Edomite, chief of the herdsmen who belonged to Saul.
And David said to Ahimelech: "And is there not here, at hand, a spear or a sword? For neither my sword nor my weapons did I take in my hand — because the king's business was urgent."

And the priest said: "The sword of Goliath the Philistine, whom you struck down in the Valley of Elah — behold, it is wrapped in a cloth behind the ephod. If you will take it for yourself, take it — for there is no other but it here." And David said: "There is none like it. Give it to me."
And David rose and fled that day from before Saul, and came to Achish king of Gath.
And the servants of Achish said to him: "Is this not David, the king of the land? Is it not for this one they sang in the dances: 'Saul has struck down his thousands — and David his ten thousands'?"
And David laid these words on his heart, and feared greatly before Achish king of Gath;
and he changed his behavior in their eyes, and played the madman in their hands — scratching marks on the doors of the gate and letting his spittle run down onto his beard.
And Achish said to his servants: "Look — you see a man raving mad. Why do you bring him to me?
Am I short of madmen, that you bring this one to rave for me? Shall this one come into my house?"

1sa.22.text: And David went from there and escaped to the cave of Adullam; and his brothers and all his father's house heard, and went down to him there.
And there gathered to him every man in hardship, and every man who had a creditor, and every man bitter of soul; and he became commander over them — and there were with him about four hundred men.
And David went from there to Mizpeh of Moab; and he said to the king of Moab: "Let my father and my mother come out, please, to be with you — until I know what God will do for me."
And he led them into the presence of the king of Moab; and they stayed with him all the days David was in the stronghold.
And Gad the prophet said to David: "Do not stay in the stronghold. Go — get yourself into the land of Judah." And David went, and came into the forest of Hereth.

And Saul heard that David and the men with him had been discovered. Now Saul was sitting at Gibeah, under the tamarisk on the height, with his spear in his hand, and all his servants standing around him;

and Saul said to his servants standing around him: "Hear now, Benjaminites! Will the son of Jesse give all of you fields and vineyards? Will he make all of you commanders of thousands and commanders of hundreds —

that all of you have conspired against me? And no one uncovers my ear when my son cuts a covenant with the son of Jesse; and none of you is sick over me, or uncovers my ear that my son has raised up my own servant against me as an ambusher, as at this day."
And Doeg the Edomite, who was stationed over the servants of Saul, answered and said: "I saw the son of Jesse coming to Nob, to Ahimelech son of Ahitub;
and he inquired of YHWH for him, and gave him provisions — and the sword of Goliath the Philistine he gave him."
And the king sent to call Ahimelech son of Ahitub the priest, and all his father's house, the priests who were at Nob; and they all came to the king.
And Saul said: "Hear now, son of Ahitub." And he said: "Here I am, my lord."

And Saul said to him: "Why have you conspired against me — you and the son of Jesse — in your giving him bread and a sword, and inquiring of God for him, so that he rises against me as an ambusher, as at this day?"
And Ahimelech answered the king and said: "And who among all your servants is like David — faithful, and the king's son-in-law, and captain over your bodyguard, and honored in your house?

Did I begin today to inquire of God for him? Far be it from me! Let not the king lay anything against his servant, or against all my father's house — for your servant knew nothing of all this, small or great."
And the king said: "You shall surely die, Ahimelech — you and all your father's house!"

And the king said to the runners stationed around him: "Turn and put to death the priests of YHWH — for their hand also is with David, and because they knew he was fleeing and did not uncover my ear." But the servants of the king were not willing to stretch out their hand to fall on the priests of YHWH.
And the king said to Doeg: "You turn — and fall on the priests." And Doeg the Edomite turned, and he fell on the priests; and he put to death on that day eighty-five men who wore the linen ephod.
And Nob, the city of the priests, he struck with the mouth of the sword: from man to woman, from child to nursing infant — and ox and donkey and sheep, with the mouth of the sword.
But one son of Ahimelech son of Ahitub escaped — and his name was Abiathar; and he fled after David.
And Abiathar told David that Saul had killed the priests of YHWH.
And David said to Abiathar: "I knew that day — because Doeg the Edomite was there — that he would surely tell Saul. I myself have turned against every soul of your father's house.
Stay with me. Do not fear — for whoever seeks my life seeks your life; for with me you are under guard."

1sa.23.text: And they told David: "Behold — the Philistines are fighting against Keilah, and they are plundering the threshing floors."
And David inquired of YHWH: "Shall I go and strike these Philistines?" And YHWH said to David: "Go, and strike the Philistines — and save Keilah."
And David's men said to him: "Behold — here in Judah we are afraid; how much more if we go to Keilah, against the battle lines of the Philistines!"
So David inquired of YHWH yet again; and YHWH answered him and said: "Rise — go down to Keilah, for I am giving the Philistines into your hand."
And David went with his men to Keilah, and fought against the Philistines, and drove off their livestock, and struck them a great blow. So David saved the inhabitants of Keilah.
And it came to pass, when Abiathar son of Ahimelech fled to David at Keilah, that an ephod came down in his hand.
And it was told to Saul that David had come to Keilah; and Saul said: "God has handed him over into my hand — for he has shut himself in, entering a city of doors and bar."
And Saul summoned all the people to war, to go down to Keilah — to besiege David and his men.
And David knew that Saul was crafting the evil against him; and he said to Abiathar the priest: "Bring the ephod near."
And David said: "YHWH, God of Israel — your servant has clearly heard that Saul is seeking to come to Keilah, to ruin the city because of me.
Will the lords of Keilah shut me into his hand? Will Saul come down, as your servant has heard? YHWH, God of Israel — tell your servant, please." And YHWH said: "He will come down."
And David said: "Will the lords of Keilah shut me and my men into the hand of Saul?" And YHWH said: "They will shut you in."

And David rose — and his men, about six hundred — and they went out of Keilah, and walked wherever they could walk. And it was told to Saul that David had escaped from Keilah — and he stopped going out.
And David stayed in the wilderness, in the strongholds; and he stayed in the hill country, in the wilderness of Ziph. And Saul sought him all the days — but God did not give him into his hand.
And David saw that Saul had come out to seek his life; and David was in the wilderness of Ziph, at Horesh.
And Jonathan son of Saul rose and went to David at Horesh — and strengthened his hand in God;
and he said to him: "Do not fear — for the hand of Saul my father will not find you; and you shall be king over Israel, and I shall be second to you. And Saul my father also knows it is so."
And the two of them cut a covenant before YHWH; and David stayed at Horesh, and Jonathan went to his house.
And the Ziphites went up to Saul at Gibeah: "Is not David hiding among us, in the strongholds at Horesh, on the hill of Hachilah, south of Jeshimon?
So now, O king — whenever your soul desires to come down, come down; and it will be ours to shut him into the hand of the king."
And Saul said: "Blessed are you to YHWH — for you have had compassion on me.
Go, please — make more certain still. Know and see the place where his foot is — who has seen him there? For it has been said to me: he is very cunning.

And see and know all the hiding places where he hides; and return to me with certainty, and I will go with you. And it shall be, if he is in the land, that I will search him out among all the thousands of Judah."
And they rose and went to Ziph, ahead of Saul. Now David and his men were in the wilderness of Maon, in the Arabah, south of Jeshimon;
and Saul and his men went to seek him. And they told David, and he went down to the rock and stayed in the wilderness of Maon; and Saul heard, and pursued after David into the wilderness of Maon.

And Saul went on this side of the mountain, and David and his men on that side of the mountain; and David was hurrying to get away from before Saul, while Saul and his men were encircling David and his men to seize them —
when a messenger came to Saul: "Hurry and come — for the Philistines have raided the land!"
So Saul turned back from pursuing after David and went to meet the Philistines. Therefore they called that place the Rock of Partings.

1sa.24.text: And David went up from there, and stayed in the strongholds of En-gedi.
And it came to pass, when Saul returned from pursuing the Philistines, that they told him: "Behold — David is in the wilderness of En-gedi."
And Saul took three thousand chosen men from all Israel, and went to seek David and his men on the face of the Rocks of the Wild Goats.
And he came to the sheepfolds along the road, and there was a cave there; and Saul went in to relieve himself — and David and his men were sitting in the far reaches of the cave.

And David's men said to him: "Behold — the day of which YHWH said to you: 'Behold, I am giving your enemy into your hand, and you shall do to him as is good in your eyes.'" And David rose and cut off the corner of Saul's robe — quietly.
And it came to pass afterward that David's heart struck him, because he had cut off the corner that was Saul's;
and he said to his men: "Far be it from me, by YHWH, that I should do this thing to my lord, to the anointed of YHWH — to stretch out my hand against him; for he is the anointed of YHWH."
And David tore into his men with words, and did not let them rise against Saul. And Saul rose from the cave and went on the road.
And David rose afterward and went out of the cave, and called after Saul: "My lord the king!" And Saul looked behind him — and David bowed, face to the ground, and prostrated himself.
And David said to Saul: "Why do you listen to the words of men who say: 'Behold — David seeks your harm'?

Behold — this day your own eyes have seen that YHWH gave you today into my hand, in the cave; and one said to kill you — but my eye spared you, and I said: 'I will not stretch out my hand against my lord, for he is the anointed of YHWH.'

And my father — see! Yes, see the corner of your robe in my hand. For in that I cut off the corner of your robe and did not kill you — know and see that there is no evil or rebellion in my hand, and I have not sinned against you; yet you are hunting my life, to take it.
May YHWH judge between me and you, and may YHWH avenge me on you — but my hand shall not be against you.
As the proverb of the ancients says: 'Out of the wicked comes wickedness' — but my hand shall not be against you.
After whom has the king of Israel come out? After whom are you chasing? After a dead dog! After a single flea!
So may YHWH be the arbiter, and judge between me and you; and may he see, and plead my cause, and give me justice out of your hand."
And it came to pass, when David had finished speaking these words to Saul, that Saul said: "Is this your voice, my son David?" And Saul lifted his voice and wept.
And he said to David: "You are more righteous than I — for you have repaid me good, and I have repaid you evil.
And you have declared today the good you have done me: that YHWH shut me into your hand — and you did not kill me.
For if a man finds his enemy, does he send him off on a good road? May YHWH repay you good for what you have done for me this day.
And now — behold, I know that you shall surely be king, and that the kingdom of Israel shall stand in your hand.
So now — swear to me by YHWH that you will not cut off my seed after me, and that you will not wipe out my name from my father's house."
And David swore to Saul. And Saul went to his house — and David and his men went up to the stronghold.

1sa.25.text: And Samuel died; and all Israel gathered and mourned for him, and they buried him at his house in Ramah. And David rose and went down to the wilderness of Paran.
Now there was a man in Maon whose business was in Carmel; and the man was very great, and he had three thousand sheep and a thousand goats — and he was shearing his sheep in Carmel.

And the name of the man was Nabal, and the name of his wife Abigail; and the woman was good in understanding and beautiful in form — but the man was hard and evil in his dealings; and he was a Calebite.
And David heard in the wilderness that Nabal was shearing his sheep;
and David sent ten young men, and David said to the young men: "Go up to Carmel and come to Nabal, and ask him, in my name, of his welfare;
and say this: 'To life! Peace to you, and peace to your house, and peace to all that is yours!
And now — I have heard that you have shearers. Now, your shepherds were with us; we did not shame them, and nothing of theirs went missing, all the days they were in Carmel.

Ask your young men, and they will tell you. So let the young men find favor in your eyes — for we have come on a good day. Give, please, whatever your hand finds, to your servants and to your son, to David.'"
And David's young men came and spoke to Nabal all these words, in David's name — and they waited.
And Nabal answered David's servants and said: "Who is David? And who is the son of Jesse? These days many are the servants breaking away, each from his master!
Shall I take my bread and my water, and my slaughtered meat that I slaughtered for my shearers, and give it to men who come from I know not where?"
And David's young men turned back on their way, and returned and came and told him all these words.

And David said to his men: "Gird on, each man, his sword!" And they girded on, each man his sword, and David also girded on his sword; and about four hundred men went up behind David, and two hundred stayed with the baggage.
But one of the young men told Abigail, Nabal's wife: "Behold — David sent messengers from the wilderness to bless our master, and he screamed insults at them.
And the men were very good to us: we were not shamed, and we missed nothing all the days we went about with them, when we were in the field.
They were a wall around us, both night and day, all the days we were with them shepherding the flock.
So now — know and see what you will do; for the evil is settled against our master and against all his house. And he is such a son of worthlessness that no one can speak to him."

And Abigail hurried, and took two hundred loaves of bread, and two skins of wine, and five sheep prepared, and five seahs of roasted grain, and a hundred clusters of raisins, and two hundred cakes of pressed figs — and set them on the donkeys;
and she said to her young men: "Cross on ahead of me — behold, I am coming behind you." But to her husband Nabal she said nothing.
And it came to pass, as she rode on the donkey, coming down under cover of the mountain — behold: David and his men coming down toward her; and she met them.
Now David had said: "Surely for nothing I guarded all this fellow has in the wilderness, so that nothing of all his went missing — and he has returned me evil for good.
So may God do to the enemies of David, and so may he add — if by morning I leave of all that is his so much as one who urinates against a wall!"
And Abigail saw David, and hurried and got down from the donkey; and she fell before David on her face, and bowed to the ground.
And she fell at his feet and said: "On me, my lord — on me be the guilt! And let your maidservant speak, please, in your ears; and hear the words of your maidservant.

Let my lord not set his heart, please, on this man of worthlessness, on Nabal; for as his name is, so is he: Nabal — Fool — is his name, and folly is with him. But I, your maidservant, did not see my lord's young men whom you sent.

And now, my lord — as YHWH lives, and as your soul lives — since YHWH has held you back from coming into blood-guilt and from saving yourself with your own hand: now, let your enemies be like Nabal, and all who seek evil against my lord.
And now — this blessing-gift your servant has brought to my lord: let it be given to the young men who walk at my lord's feet.
Carry away, please, the rebellion of your maidservant. For YHWH will surely make my lord a faithful house — for my lord fights the battles of YHWH, and evil shall not be found in you all your days.

And should a man rise to pursue you and to seek your life — the soul of my lord shall be bound up in the bundle of the living, with YHWH your God; but the soul of your enemies he shall sling away, from inside the hollow of the sling.
And it shall be, when YHWH does for my lord according to all the good he has spoken concerning you, and appoints you as leader over Israel,

that this shall not be for you a staggering — a stumbling of heart for my lord: to have poured out blood for nothing, and for my lord to have saved himself with his own hand. And when YHWH does good to my lord — remember your maidservant."
And David said to Abigail: "Blessed be YHWH, the God of Israel, who sent you this day to meet me!
And blessed be your good sense, and blessed be you — who have kept me this day from coming into blood-guilt, and from saving myself with my own hand.

But truly — as YHWH lives, the God of Israel, who has held me back from harming you: had you not hurried and come to meet me, there would not have been left to Nabal by morning light one who urinates against a wall."
And David took from her hand what she had brought him; and to her he said: "Go up in peace to your house. See — I have listened to your voice, and I have lifted your face."

And Abigail came to Nabal — and behold: he had a feast in his house like the feast of a king; and Nabal's heart was merry within him, and he was drunk to excess. So she told him nothing, small or great, until the morning light.
And it came to pass in the morning, when the wine had gone out of Nabal, that his wife told him these things — and his heart died within him, and he became like stone.
And it came to pass, about ten days later, that YHWH struck Nabal — and he died.

And David heard that Nabal was dead, and he said: "Blessed be YHWH, who has pleaded the cause of my reproach from the hand of Nabal, and has held back his servant from evil; and the evil of Nabal YHWH has returned on his own head." And David sent and spoke for Abigail, to take her as his wife.
And David's servants came to Abigail at Carmel and spoke to her: "David has sent us to you, to take you to him as a wife."
And she rose and bowed, face to the ground, and said: "Behold — your maidservant is a servant, to wash the feet of the servants of my lord."
And Abigail hurried and rose, and rode on the donkey — with her five young women walking at her feet; and she went after David's messengers, and became his wife.
And Ahinoam David took from Jezreel; and the two of them, both, became his wives.
But Saul had given Michal his daughter, David's wife, to Palti son of Laish, who was from Gallim.

1sa.26.text: And the Ziphites came to Saul at Gibeah: "Is not David hiding on the hill of Hachilah, facing Jeshimon?"
And Saul rose and went down to the wilderness of Ziph — and with him three thousand chosen men of Israel — to seek David in the wilderness of Ziph.
And Saul camped on the hill of Hachilah, which faces Jeshimon, by the road. And David was staying in the wilderness; and he saw that Saul had come after him into the wilderness,
and David sent spies, and knew that Saul had indeed come.

And David rose and came to the place where Saul was camped; and David saw the place where Saul lay, with Abner son of Ner, the commander of his army — Saul lying inside the wagon-circle, with the people camped around him.
And David answered and said to Ahimelech the Hittite and to Abishai son of Zeruiah, Joab's brother: "Who will go down with me to Saul, to the camp?" And Abishai said: "I will go down with you."
So David and Abishai came to the people by night — and behold: Saul lying asleep inside the wagon-circle, with his spear stuck in the ground at his head, and Abner and the people lying around him.
And Abishai said to David: "God has shut your enemy into your hand today. So now — let me strike him, please, with the spear into the ground, one stroke; I will not need a second."
But David said to Abishai: "Do not destroy him! For who has stretched out his hand against the anointed of YHWH and been held clean?"
And David said: "As YHWH lives — surely YHWH himself will strike him; or his day will come and he will die; or he will go down into battle and be swept away.
Far be it from me, by YHWH, to stretch out my hand against the anointed of YHWH! So now — take, please, the spear at his head, and the jar of water; and let us go."

So David took the spear and the jar of water from beside Saul's head, and they went their way. And no one saw, and no one knew, and no one awoke — for they were all asleep, because a deep sleep from YHWH had fallen on them.
And David crossed to the other side and stood on the top of the mountain, far off — a great space between them;
and David called to the people and to Abner son of Ner: "Will you not answer, Abner?" And Abner answered and said: "Who are you, calling to the king?"
And David said to Abner: "Are you not a man? And who is like you in Israel? Then why have you not kept watch over your lord the king? For one of the people came in to destroy the king your lord.

This thing you have done is not good. As YHWH lives — you are sons of death, because you have not kept watch over your lord, over the anointed of YHWH. And now — see where the king's spear is, and the jar of water that was at his head!"
And Saul recognized David's voice and said: "Is this your voice, my son David?" And David said: "My voice, my lord the king."
And he said: "Why is this — my lord pursuing after his servant? For what have I done? And what evil is in my hand?

So now — let my lord the king hear, please, the words of his servant. If it is YHWH who has incited you against me — let him smell an offering. But if it is human beings — cursed are they before YHWH; for they have driven me out today from being joined to the inheritance of YHWH, saying: 'Go — serve other gods.'
And now — let not my blood fall to the ground away from the face of YHWH; for the king of Israel has come out to seek a single flea — as one hunts a partridge in the mountains."

And Saul said: "I have sinned. Return, my son David — for I will not harm you again, because my life was precious in your eyes this day. Behold — I have played the fool, and have gone very far astray."
And David answered and said: "Behold the king's spear! Let one of the young men cross over and take it.
And YHWH will return to each man his righteousness and his faithfulness — for YHWH gave you today into my hand, and I was not willing to stretch out my hand against the anointed of YHWH.
And behold — as your life was great this day in my eyes, so may my life be great in the eyes of YHWH; and may he rescue me from every distress."
And Saul said to David: "Blessed are you, my son David. You will surely do great things — and surely prevail." And David went his way, and Saul returned to his place.

1sa.27.text: And David said in his heart: "Now I shall be swept away one day by the hand of Saul. There is nothing better for me than to escape — escape to the land of the Philistines; and Saul will despair of me, of seeking me any more in all the territory of Israel — and I shall escape out of his hand."
And David rose and crossed over — he and the six hundred men with him — to Achish son of Maoch, king of Gath.
And David stayed with Achish at Gath — he and his men, each man with his household; David with his two wives: Ahinoam the Jezreelite and Abigail, Nabal's widow, the Carmelite.
And it was told to Saul that David had fled to Gath — and he did not seek him any more.

And David said to Achish: "If now I have found favor in your eyes, let them give me a place in one of the country towns, and let me stay there. Why should your servant stay in the royal city with you?"
And Achish gave him Ziklag on that day; therefore Ziklag has belonged to the kings of Judah to this day.
And the number of the days David stayed in the countryside of the Philistines was a year and four months.

And David went up with his men, and they raided the Geshurites and the Girzites and the Amalekites — for these were the inhabitants of the land from of old, from the approach to Shur as far as the land of Egypt.
And David struck the land and left alive neither man nor woman; and he took sheep and oxen and donkeys and camels and garments, and returned and came to Achish.
And Achish said: "Where did you raid today?" And David said: "Against the Negev of Judah" — or "Against the Negev of the Jerahmeelites" — or "Against the Negev of the Kenites."
And David left alive neither man nor woman to bring to Gath, saying: "Lest they tell on us: 'Thus David did.'" And such was his practice all the days he stayed in the countryside of the Philistines.
And Achish trusted David, saying: "He has made himself an utter stench to his own people, to Israel — and he shall be my servant to the age."

1sa.28.text: And it came to pass in those days that the Philistines gathered their camps for war, to fight against Israel; and Achish said to David: "Know surely that you shall go out with me in the camp — you and your men."
And David said to Achish: "Then you yourself shall know what your servant can do." And Achish said to David: "Then I will make you keeper of my head all the days."
Now Samuel was dead, and all Israel had mourned for him and buried him at Ramah, his own city. And Saul had removed the mediums and the spiritists from the land.
And the Philistines gathered and came and camped at Shunem; and Saul gathered all Israel, and they camped at Gilboa.
And Saul saw the camp of the Philistines — and he was afraid, and his heart trembled greatly.
And Saul inquired of YHWH — but YHWH did not answer him: not by dreams, not by the Urim, not by the prophets.

And Saul said to his servants: "Seek me a woman who is mistress of a spirit, and I will go to her and inquire through her." And his servants said to him: "Behold — there is a woman, mistress of a spirit, at En-dor."

And Saul disguised himself and put on other garments; and he went — he and two men with him — and they came to the woman by night. And he said: "Divine for me, please, by the spirit — and bring up for me the one I name to you."
And the woman said to him: "Behold — you know what Saul has done: how he has cut off the mediums and the spiritists from the land. Why then are you laying a snare for my life, to put me to death?"
And Saul swore to her by YHWH: "As YHWH lives — no guilt shall come upon you in this matter."
And the woman said: "Whom shall I bring up for you?" And he said: "Bring up Samuel for me."
And the woman saw Samuel — and she cried out with a great voice; and the woman said to Saul: "Why have you deceived me? You are Saul!"
And the king said to her: "Do not fear. What do you see?" And the woman said to Saul: "A god I see — coming up out of the earth."
And he said to her: "What is his form?" And she said: "An old man coming up — and he is wrapped in a robe." And Saul knew that it was Samuel; and he bowed, face to the ground, and prostrated himself.

And Samuel said to Saul: "Why have you disturbed me, bringing me up?" And Saul said: "I am in very hard straits: the Philistines are fighting against me, and God has turned aside from me and answers me no more — not by the hand of the prophets, not by dreams. So I have called you, to make known to me what I should do."
And Samuel said: "And why do you ask me — when YHWH has turned aside from you and become your adversary?
YHWH has done for himself just as he spoke by my hand: YHWH has torn the kingdom out of your hand and given it to your fellow — to David.
Because you did not listen to the voice of YHWH, and did not carry out his burning anger against Amalek — therefore YHWH has done this thing to you this day.
And YHWH will give Israel also, with you, into the hand of the Philistines; and tomorrow — you and your sons will be with me. Also the camp of Israel YHWH will give into the hand of the Philistines."
And Saul instantly fell full length to the ground, and was greatly afraid at the words of Samuel; and there was no strength in him — for he had eaten no food all that day and all that night.
