and all the people were arguing through all the tribes of Israel, saying: "The king rescued us from the palm of our enemies, and he delivered us from the palm of the Philistines — and now he has fled from the land, from Absalom;
and Absalom, whom we anointed over us, has died in the battle. So now — why do you say nothing about bringing the king back?"

And King David sent to Zadok and to Abiathar the priests: "Speak to the elders of Judah: 'Why should you be the last to bring the king back to his house — when the word of all Israel has come to the king, to his house?
You are my brothers; you are my bone and my flesh. Why should you be the last to bring back the king?'
And say to Amasa: 'Are you not my bone and my flesh? So may God do to me, and so may he add — if you shall not be commander of the army before me all the days, in place of Joab.'"
And he bent the heart of every man of Judah as one man; and they sent to the king: "Return — you and all your servants."
And the king returned and came as far as the Jordan; and Judah came to Gilgal, to go to meet the king — to bring the king across the Jordan.
And Shimei son of Gera, the Benjaminite from Bahurim, hurried and came down with the men of Judah to meet King David —
and a thousand men from Benjamin with him; and Ziba, the servant of the house of Saul, with his fifteen sons and his twenty servants with him — and they rushed down to the Jordan before the king,
and crossed the ford to bring the king's household across, and to do what was good in his eyes. And Shimei son of Gera fell before the king as he was about to cross the Jordan,

and said to the king: "Let not my lord count guilt against me, and do not remember how your servant went crooked on the day my lord the king went out from Jerusalem — let the king not take it to his heart.
For your servant knows that I — I have sinned; and behold, I have come today, first of all the house of Joseph, to come down to meet my lord the king."
And Abishai son of Zeruiah answered and said: "Shall Shimei not be put to death for this — that he cursed the anointed of YHWH?"

And David said: "What is there between me and you, sons of Zeruiah, that today you should become an adversary to me? Shall any man be put to death in Israel today? For do I not know that today I am king over Israel?"
And the king said to Shimei: "You shall not die." And the king swore it to him.

And Mephibosheth, Saul's grandson, came down to meet the king. He had not tended his feet, and had not trimmed his mustache, and had not washed his garments, from the day the king went away until the day he came back in peace.
And it came to pass, when he came to Jerusalem to meet the king, that the king said to him: "Why did you not go with me, Mephibosheth?"
And he said: "My lord the king — my servant deceived me. For your servant said: 'Let me saddle the donkey for myself and ride on it and go with the king' — for your servant is lame.
And he slandered your servant to my lord the king. But my lord the king is like an angel of God — so do what is good in your eyes.

For all my father's house were nothing but men of death before my lord the king — and yet you set your servant among those who eat at your table. What right do I still have, to cry out any more to the king?"
And the king said to him: "Why do you go on speaking of your affairs? I have said: you and Ziba shall divide the field."
And Mephibosheth said to the king: "Let him even take the whole — now that my lord the king has come in peace to his own house."
And Barzillai the Gileadite had come down from Rogelim, and he crossed the Jordan with the king, to escort him across the Jordan.
Now Barzillai was very old — eighty years old; and he had sustained the king during his stay at Mahanaim, for he was a very great man.
And the king said to Barzillai: "Cross over with me, and I will sustain you with me in Jerusalem."
And Barzillai said to the king: "How many are the days of the years of my life, that I should go up with the king to Jerusalem?

I am eighty years old today. Can I tell between good and bad? Can your servant taste what I eat and what I drink? Can I still hear the voice of singing men and singing women? Why should your servant be a burden any longer to my lord the king?
Your servant will just cross the Jordan with the king a little way — and why should the king repay me with such a reward?

Let your servant return, please, and let me die in my own city, by the grave of my father and my mother. But behold your servant Chimham — let him cross over with my lord the king; and do for him what is good in your eyes."
And the king said: "Chimham shall cross over with me, and I will do for him what is good in your eyes; and all that you choose to lay on me, I will do for you."
And all the people crossed the Jordan, and the king crossed; and the king kissed Barzillai and blessed him — and he returned to his place.
And the king crossed over to Gilgal, and Chimham crossed with him; and all the people of Judah brought the king across — and also half the people of Israel.

And behold — all the men of Israel coming to the king; and they said to the king: "Why have our brothers, the men of Judah, stolen you away, and brought the king and his household across the Jordan — and all David's men with him?"

And every man of Judah answered the men of Israel: "Because the king is near of kin to me. Why then does this burn in you? Have we eaten anything at all at the king's expense? Or has anything been carried off for us?"

And the men of Israel answered the men of Judah and said: "I have ten shares in the king — and in David, too, I am more than you. Why then have you made light of me? Was not my word first, about bringing back my king?" But the word of the men of Judah was harder than the word of the men of Israel.

2sa.20.text: And there happened to be there a man of worthlessness, whose name was Sheba son of Bichri, a Benjaminite; and he blew the shofar and said: "We have no share in David, and no inheritance in the son of Jesse! Every man to his tents, O Israel!"
And every man of Israel went up from following David, to follow Sheba son of Bichri; but the men of Judah clung to their king, from the Jordan to Jerusalem.

And David came to his house at Jerusalem; and the king took the ten women — the concubines he had left to keep the house — and put them in a guarded house and sustained them, but he did not go in to them. And they were shut up until the day of their death — a widowhood of the living.
And the king said to Amasa: "Summon the men of Judah to me within three days — and you, take your stand here."
And Amasa went to summon Judah — but he delayed past the set time appointed him.

And David said to Abishai: "Now Sheba son of Bichri will do us more harm than Absalom. You — take your lord's servants and pursue after him, lest he find himself fortified cities and tear away out of our sight."
And Joab's men went out after him — with the Cherethites and the Pelethites and all the mighty men; and they went out of Jerusalem to pursue after Sheba son of Bichri.

They were beside the great stone that is at Gibeon when Amasa came before them. Now Joab was girded with his military coat as his garment, and over it a belt with a sword strapped on his hips in its sheath; and as he stepped forward, it fell out.
And Joab said to Amasa: "Is it well with you, my brother?" And Joab's right hand took hold of Amasa's beard, to kiss him;

and Amasa was not on guard against the sword in Joab's hand — and he struck him with it into the belly, and poured his inner parts out on the ground; and he did not strike him a second time — and he died. And Joab and Abishai his brother pursued after Sheba son of Bichri.
And a man stood over him, one of Joab's young men, and said: "Whoever favors Joab, and whoever is for David — after Joab!"

And Amasa was wallowing in blood in the middle of the highway; and the man saw that all the people stood still, and he moved Amasa off the highway into the field and threw a garment over him — because he saw that everyone who came upon him stood still.
When he was removed from the highway, every man passed on after Joab, to pursue after Sheba son of Bichri.
And he crossed through all the tribes of Israel to Abel and Beth-maacah, and all the Berites; and they assembled and came in after him too.

And they came and besieged him in Abel of Beth-maacah, and heaped up a siege ramp against the city, and it stood against the rampart; and all the people with Joab were battering, to bring down the wall.
And a wise woman called from the city: "Listen! Listen! Say, please, to Joab: 'Come near here — and let me speak to you.'"
And he came near to her; and the woman said: "Are you Joab?" And he said: "I am." And she said to him: "Hear the words of your maidservant." And he said: "I am listening."
And she said: "In old times they would surely say: 'Let them but ask at Abel' — and so they settled a matter.
I am of the peaceable, faithful ones of Israel. You are seeking to put to death a city — a mother in Israel. Why would you swallow up the inheritance of YHWH?"
And Joab answered and said: "Far be it — far be it from me to swallow up or to destroy!

The matter is not so. But a man from the hill country of Ephraim — Sheba son of Bichri is his name — has lifted his hand against the king, against David. Give him up — him alone — and I will go away from the city." And the woman said to Joab: "Behold — his head shall be thrown to you over the wall."

And the woman went to all the people in her wisdom; and they cut off the head of Sheba son of Bichri and threw it to Joab. And he blew the shofar, and they scattered from the city, each man to his tents; and Joab returned to Jerusalem, to the king.
Now Joab was over the whole army of Israel; and Benaiah son of Jehoiada was over the Cherethites and over the Pelethites;
and Adoram was over the forced labor; and Jehoshaphat son of Ahilud was recorder;
and Sheva was scribe; and Zadok and Abiathar were priests;
and Ira the Jairite also was priest to David.

2sa.21.text: And there was a famine in the days of David, three years, year after year; and David sought the face of YHWH. And YHWH said: "It is on account of Saul and the house of blood — because he put the Gibeonites to death."

And the king called the Gibeonites and spoke to them. (Now the Gibeonites were not of the children of Israel, but of the remnant of the Amorites; and the children of Israel had sworn to them — but Saul had sought to strike them down in his zeal for the children of Israel and Judah.)
And David said to the Gibeonites: "What shall I do for you? And with what shall I make atonement — so that you bless the inheritance of YHWH?"

And the Gibeonites said to him: "It is not a matter of silver or gold between us and Saul and his house; and it is not for us to put any man to death in Israel." And he said: "Whatever you say — I will do for you."
And they said to the king: "The man who consumed us, and who schemed against us so that we were destroyed from taking our stand anywhere in the territory of Israel —
let seven men of his sons be given to us, and we will impale them before YHWH at Gibeah of Saul, the chosen of YHWH." And the king said: "I will give them."
But the king spared Mephibosheth son of Jonathan, son of Saul — because of the oath of YHWH between them, between David and Jonathan son of Saul.

And the king took the two sons of Rizpah daughter of Aiah, whom she had borne to Saul — Armoni and Mephibosheth — and the five sons of Michal daughter of Saul, whom she had borne to Adriel son of Barzillai the Meholathite;

and he gave them into the hand of the Gibeonites, and they impaled them on the mountain before YHWH — and the seven fell together. And they were put to death in the first days of harvest, at the beginning of the barley harvest.

And Rizpah daughter of Aiah took sackcloth and spread it out for herself on the rock, from the beginning of harvest until water poured down on them from the heavens; and she did not let the birds of the heavens rest on them by day, nor the wild animals of the field by night.
And it was told to David what Rizpah daughter of Aiah, Saul's concubine, had done.

And David went and took the bones of Saul, and the bones of Jonathan his son, from the lords of Jabesh-gilead — who had stolen them from the open square of Beth-shan, where the Philistines had hanged them on the day the Philistines struck Saul down at Gilboa;
and he brought up from there the bones of Saul and the bones of Jonathan his son; and they gathered the bones of those who had been impaled,

and buried the bones of Saul and Jonathan his son in the land of Benjamin, at Zela, in the tomb of Kish his father. And they did all that the king commanded — and after that, God was entreated for the land.
And there was war again between the Philistines and Israel; and David went down, and his servants with him, and they fought the Philistines — and David grew weary.
And Ishbi-benob, who was among the descendants of the Rapha — the weight of his spearhead three hundred shekels of bronze, and he girded with new armor — said he would strike David down.

But Abishai son of Zeruiah came to his help, and struck the Philistine and killed him. Then David's men swore to him: "You shall not go out with us to battle again — that you not quench the lamp of Israel."
And it came to pass afterward that there was war again with the Philistines, at Gob; then Sibbecai the Hushathite struck down Saph, who was among the descendants of the Rapha.
And there was war again with the Philistines at Gob; and Elhanan son of Jaare-oregim the Bethlehemite struck down Goliath the Gittite — the shaft of whose spear was like a weavers' beam.
And there was war again, at Gath; and there was a man of huge stature, with six fingers on each hand and six toes on each foot — twenty-four in number; and he too was born to the Rapha.
And he defied Israel — and Jonathan son of Shimei, David's brother, struck him down.
These four were born to the Rapha in Gath — and they fell by the hand of David and by the hand of his servants.

2sa.22.text: And David spoke to YHWH the words of this song, on the day YHWH rescued him from the palm of all his enemies, and from the palm of Saul.
And he said: "YHWH is my crag and my stronghold — my deliverer, mine.
God, my rock — I take refuge in him: my shield and the horn of my salvation, my high fortress and my flight; my savior — you save me from violence.
Worthy of praise! — I call YHWH; and from my enemies I am saved.
For the breakers of death wrapped around me; the torrents of destruction terrified me;
the cords of Sheol encircled me; the snares of death confronted me.
In my distress I call YHWH — to my God I call; and he hears my voice from his temple; my cry is in his ears.
Then the earth heaved and quaked; the foundations of the heavens shuddered — they heaved, because it burned in him.
Smoke went up from his nostrils, and fire from his mouth devoured; coals blazed out from him.
And he bent the heavens and came down — with thick darkness under his feet;
and he rode on a cherub and flew; he was seen on the wings of the wind.
And he set darkness around him as shelters — a massing of waters, thick clouds of the skies;
out of the brightness before him blazed coals of fire.
YHWH thundered from the heavens; the Most High gave his voice.
And he sent out arrows and scattered them — lightning, and threw them into panic.
And the channels of the sea were seen; the foundations of the world were laid bare — at the rebuke of YHWH, at the blast of the breath of his nostrils.
He reaches from on high; he takes me — he draws me out of many waters.
He rescues me from my strong enemy, from those who hate me — for they were mightier than I.
They confronted me on the day of my calamity — but YHWH became my support;
and he brought me out into the broad place; he set me free — because he delighted in me.
YHWH repays me according to my righteousness; according to the cleanness of my hands he returns to me.
For I have kept the ways of YHWH, and have not done wickedness against my God;
for all his judgments are before me — and from his statutes I do not turn aside.
And I was whole toward him, and kept myself from my guilt;
and YHWH has returned to me according to my righteousness — according to my cleanness before his eyes.
With the loyal, you show yourself loyal; with the blameless warrior, you show yourself blameless;
with the pure, you show yourself pure — and with the crooked, you show yourself twisting.
And an afflicted people you save; but your eyes are on the exalted — you bring them low.
For you are my lamp, YHWH; and YHWH lights up my darkness.
For by you I run at a raiding band; by my God I leap a wall.
This God — his way is whole; the saying of YHWH is refined; he is a shield to all who take refuge in him.
For who is God besides YHWH? And who is a rock besides our God?
This God is my strong fortress; and he sets the whole one free on his way.
He makes my feet like the deer's — and stands me on my high places.
He trains my hands for battle — so that my arms bend a bow of bronze.
And you have given me the shield of your salvation — and your answering makes me great.
You widen my steps under me — and my ankles do not give way.
I pursue my enemies and destroy them; and I do not turn back until they are finished.
And I finish them and shatter them, and they do not rise — they fall under my feet.
And you gird me with strength for battle; you bring down beneath me those who rise against me.
And my enemies — you give me their necks; those who hate me — I silence them.
They look — and there is no savior; to YHWH — and he does not answer them.
And I grind them like the dust of the earth; like the mud of the streets I crush them, I trample them flat.
And you set me free from the strivings of my people; you keep me as head of nations — a people I did not know serves me.
Sons of the foreign land come cringing to me; at the mere hearing of the ear, they obey me.
Sons of the foreign land wilt away — and come trembling out of their strongholds.
YHWH lives! And blessed be my rock! And exalted be God, the rock of my salvation —
the God who gives me acts of vengeance, and brings peoples down beneath me;
who brings me out from my enemies. Above those who rise against me you lift me high; from the man of violence you rescue me.
Therefore I will thank you, YHWH, among the nations — and to your name I will make music:
a tower of salvations for his king — showing steadfast love to his anointed, to David and to his seed, to the age."

2sa.23.text: And these are the last words of David: An utterance of David son of Jesse; an utterance of the man raised on high — the anointed of the God of Jacob, the sweet singer of the songs of Israel:
"The Spirit of YHWH has spoken in me, and his word is on my tongue.
The God of Israel has said; the Rock of Israel has spoken to me: One who rules over humankind justly — who rules in the fear of God —
is like the light of morning when the sun rises: a morning without clouds; from its brightness after rain, fresh grass out of the earth.
For is not my house so with God? For he has set for me a covenant of the age — arranged in everything, and kept. For all my salvation and all my desire — will he not make it sprout?
But the worthless — all of them are like thorns blown aside; for they cannot be taken in the hand:
the man who touches them arms himself with iron and the shaft of a spear — and with fire they are burned, burned up, where they lie."
These are the names of the mighty men who belonged to David: Josheb-basshebeth the Tahchemonite, head of the Three — he was Adino the Eznite: against eight hundred slain at one time.
And after him: Eleazar son of Dodo, son of Ahohi — among the three mighty men with David when they defied the Philistines gathered there for battle, and the men of Israel had gone up.

He rose and struck the Philistines until his hand grew weary and his hand stuck fast to the sword; and YHWH worked a great deliverance on that day — and the people came back after him, only to strip the slain.
And after him: Shammah son of Agee the Hararite. The Philistines gathered as a raiding company, and there was a plot of field there full of lentils, and the people fled before the Philistines;
but he took his stand in the middle of the plot and rescued it and struck the Philistines — and YHWH worked a great deliverance.
And three of the thirty chiefs went down and came at harvest time to David, to the cave of Adullam, while a company of Philistines was camped in the Valley of Rephaim.
Now David was then in the stronghold, and a garrison of the Philistines was then at Bethlehem.
And David felt a craving and said: "Who will give me a drink of water from the cistern of Bethlehem, which is by the gate!"

And the three mighty men broke through the camp of the Philistines and drew water from the cistern of Bethlehem, which is by the gate, and carried it and brought it to David. But he was not willing to drink it — and he poured it out to YHWH,
and said: "Far be it from me, YHWH, to do this! Is this not the blood of the men who went at the risk of their lives?" And he was not willing to drink it. These things the three mighty men did.
And Abishai, Joab's brother, son of Zeruiah — he was head of the Three; and he swung his spear against three hundred slain, and won a name among the Three.
Of the Three he was the most honored, and he became their commander — but to the first Three he did not attain.

And Benaiah son of Jehoiada, son of a man of valor, great in deeds, from Kabzeel — he struck down the two lion-heroes of Moab; and he went down and struck the lion inside the cistern on a day of snow;

and he struck down an Egyptian, an impressive man; and in the Egyptian's hand was a spear — but he went down to him with a staff, and tore the spear from the Egyptian's hand, and killed him with his own spear.
These things Benaiah son of Jehoiada did — and he won a name among the three mighty men.
He was honored above the Thirty, but to the Three he did not attain; and David set him over his bodyguard.
Asahel, Joab's brother, was among the Thirty; Elhanan son of Dodo, of Bethlehem;
Shammah the Harodite; Elika the Harodite;
Helez the Paltite; Ira son of Ikkesh the Tekoite;
Abiezer the Anathothite; Mebunnai the Hushathite;
Zalmon the Ahohite; Maharai the Netophathite;
Heleb son of Baanah the Netophathite; Ittai son of Ribai, from Gibeah of the sons of Benjamin;
Benaiah the Pirathonite; Hiddai from the wadis of Gaash;
Abi-albon the Arbathite; Azmaveth the Barhumite;
Eliahba the Shaalbonite; the sons of Jashen; Jonathan;
Shammah the Hararite; Ahiam son of Sharar the Ararite;
Eliphelet son of Ahasbai, son of the Maacathite; Eliam son of Ahithophel the Gilonite;
Hezro the Carmelite; Paarai the Arbite;
Igal son of Nathan, from Zobah; Bani the Gadite;
Zelek the Ammonite; Naharai the Beerothite, armor-bearer of Joab son of Zeruiah;
Ira the Ithrite; Gareb the Ithrite;
Uriah the Hittite. Thirty-seven in all.

2sa.24.text: And the anger of YHWH burned again against Israel; and he incited David against them: "Go — count Israel and Judah."

And the king said to Joab, the commander of the army who was with him: "Range, please, through all the tribes of Israel, from Dan to Beersheba, and muster the people — that I may know the number of the people."

And Joab said to the king: "May YHWH your God add to the people a hundred times as many as they are, while the eyes of my lord the king still see it! But my lord the king — why does he delight in this thing?"
But the king's word prevailed against Joab and against the commanders of the army; and Joab and the commanders of the army went out from before the king, to muster the people — Israel.
And they crossed the Jordan and camped at Aroer, south of the city that is in the middle of the wadi, toward Gad, and on to Jazer;
and they came to Gilead and to the land of Tahtim-hodshi; and they came to Dan-jaan and around toward Sidon;
and they came to the fortress of Tyre and all the cities of the Hivites and the Canaanites; and they went out to the Negev of Judah, at Beersheba.
And they ranged through the whole land and came, at the end of nine months and twenty days, to Jerusalem.
And Joab gave the number of the muster of the people to the king: and Israel was eight hundred thousand men of valor drawing the sword, and the men of Judah five hundred thousand men.

And David's heart struck him after he had counted the people; and David said to YHWH: "I have sinned greatly in what I have done. And now, YHWH — carry over, please, the guilt of your servant; for I have acted very foolishly."
And David rose in the morning — and the word of YHWH had come to Gad the prophet, David's seer:
"Go and speak to David: Thus says YHWH: Three things I am holding over you. Choose yourself one of them — and I will do it to you."

And Gad came to David and told him; and he said to him: "Shall seven years of famine come to you in your land? Or three months of your fleeing before your adversaries, as they pursue you? Or shall there be three days of plague in your land? Now — know and see what answer I shall return to the one who sent me."
And David said to Gad: "I am in very hard straits. Let us fall, please, into the hand of YHWH — for his compassions are many; but into the hand of humankind let me not fall."
So YHWH gave a plague in Israel from the morning until the appointed time; and there died of the people, from Dan to Beersheba, seventy thousand men.

And the angel stretched out his hand toward Jerusalem, to ruin it; but YHWH relented concerning the evil, and said to the angel destroying among the people: "Enough. Now — let your hand drop." And the angel of YHWH was by the threshing floor of Araunah the Jebusite.

And David said to YHWH, when he saw the angel striking among the people — he said: "Behold — it is I who have sinned, and I who have gone crooked; but these — the flock — what have they done? Let your hand, please, be against me and against my father's house."
And Gad came to David on that day and said to him: "Go up — raise an altar to YHWH on the threshing floor of Araunah the Jebusite."
And David went up, according to the word of Gad, as YHWH had commanded.
And Araunah looked down and saw the king and his servants crossing over toward him; and Araunah went out and bowed to the king, his face to the ground.
And Araunah said: "Why has my lord the king come to his servant?" And David said: "To buy the threshing floor from you, to build an altar to YHWH — that the plague may be held back from the people."
And Araunah said to David: "Let my lord the king take and offer up whatever is good in his eyes. See — the oxen for the burnt offering, and the threshing sledges and the oxen's yokes for the wood.
All of it Araunah gives, O king, to the king." And Araunah said to the king: "May YHWH your God accept you."

But the king said to Araunah: "No — I will surely buy it from you at a price; I will not offer up to YHWH my God burnt offerings that cost nothing." So David bought the threshing floor and the oxen for fifty shekels of silver.
And David built there an altar to YHWH, and offered up burnt offerings and peace offerings. And YHWH was entreated for the land — and the plague was held back from Israel.

1ki.1.text: Now King David was old, come along in days; and they covered him with garments — but he could not get warm.

And his servants said to him: "Let them seek for my lord the king a young virgin woman, and let her stand before the king and be his attendant; and let her lie in your lap — and my lord the king will be warm."
And they sought a beautiful young woman through all the territory of Israel; and they found Abishag the Shunammite, and brought her to the king.
And the young woman was exceedingly beautiful; and she became the king's attendant and served him — but the king did not know her.
Now Adonijah son of Haggith was exalting himself, saying: "I will be king!" And he got himself chariots and horsemen, and fifty men to run before him.
And his father had never pained him in all his days by saying, "Why have you done so?" And he too was very good-looking; and she had borne him after Absalom.
And his dealings were with Joab son of Zeruiah and with Abiathar the priest — and they gave their help behind Adonijah;
but Zadok the priest, and Benaiah son of Jehoiada, and Nathan the prophet, and Shimei and Rei, and the mighty men who belonged to David — they were not with Adonijah.

And Adonijah sacrificed sheep and oxen and fatlings by the Stone of Zoheleth, which is beside En-rogel; and he invited all his brothers, the king's sons, and all the men of Judah, the king's servants —
but Nathan the prophet, and Benaiah, and the mighty men, and Solomon his brother, he did not invite.
And Nathan said to Bathsheba, Solomon's mother: "Have you not heard that Adonijah son of Haggith has become king — and our lord David does not know?
So now, come — let me counsel you, please, with counsel: save your own life, and the life of your son Solomon.

Go, and come in to King David, and say to him: 'Did you not, my lord the king, swear to your maidservant: Surely Solomon your son shall reign after me, and he shall sit on my throne? Why then has Adonijah become king?'
Behold — while you are still speaking there with the king, I will come in after you and fill out your words."
And Bathsheba came in to the king, into the inner room. Now the king was very old, and Abishag the Shunammite was serving the king.
And Bathsheba bowed and prostrated herself to the king; and the king said: "What is it?"
And she said to him: "My lord — you swore by YHWH your God to your maidservant: 'Surely Solomon your son shall reign after me, and he shall sit on my throne.'
And now — behold, Adonijah has become king; and now, my lord the king, you do not know it.

And he has sacrificed ox and fatling and sheep in abundance, and has invited all the king's sons, and Abiathar the priest, and Joab the commander of the army — but Solomon your servant he did not invite.
And you, my lord the king — the eyes of all Israel are on you, to tell them who shall sit on the throne of my lord the king after him.
Otherwise it shall be, when my lord the king lies down with his fathers, that I and my son Solomon will be counted offenders."
And behold — while she was still speaking with the king, Nathan the prophet came in.
And they told the king: "Behold — Nathan the prophet." And he came in before the king and prostrated himself to the king, his face to the ground.
And Nathan said: "My lord the king — did you yourself say: 'Adonijah shall reign after me, and he shall sit on my throne'?

For he has gone down today and sacrificed ox and fatling and sheep in abundance, and has invited all the king's sons, and the commanders of the army, and Abiathar the priest; and behold — they are eating and drinking before him, and saying: 'Long live King Adonijah!'
But me — me, your servant — and Zadok the priest, and Benaiah son of Jehoiada, and Solomon your servant, he did not invite.
If this thing has come from my lord the king — then you have not let your servants know who should sit on the throne of my lord the king after him."
And King David answered and said: "Call Bathsheba to me." And she came in before the king and stood before the king.
And the king swore and said: "As YHWH lives, who has ransomed my life from every distress:
just as I swore to you by YHWH, the God of Israel — 'Surely Solomon your son shall reign after me, and he shall sit on my throne in my place' — so I will do this very day."
And Bathsheba bowed, face to the earth, and prostrated herself to the king, and said: "May my lord King David live to the age!"
And King David said: "Call me Zadok the priest, and Nathan the prophet, and Benaiah son of Jehoiada." And they came in before the king.
And the king said to them: "Take with you the servants of your lord, and mount Solomon my son on my own mule, and bring him down to Gihon;
and there let Zadok the priest and Nathan the prophet anoint him king over Israel. And blow the shofar and say: 'Long live King Solomon!'
Then come up after him, and he shall come and sit on my throne — and he shall reign in my place; for him I have appointed to be leader over Israel and over Judah."
And Benaiah son of Jehoiada answered the king and said: "Amen! So may YHWH, the God of my lord the king, say.
As YHWH has been with my lord the king, so may he be with Solomon — and may he make his throne greater than the throne of my lord King David."
And Zadok the priest and Nathan the prophet and Benaiah son of Jehoiada went down, with the Cherethites and the Pelethites, and they mounted Solomon on King David's mule and led him to Gihon.
And Zadok the priest took the horn of oil from the tent and anointed Solomon; and they blew the shofar, and all the people said: "Long live King Solomon!"
And all the people came up after him, the people playing flutes and rejoicing with great joy — and the earth split open at their sound.
And Adonijah heard it, and all the invited guests with him — they had just finished eating; and Joab heard the sound of the shofar and said: "Why is the sound of the city in an uproar?"
While he was still speaking — behold: Jonathan son of Abiathar the priest came; and Adonijah said: "Come in — for you are a man of valor, and you bring good news."
And Jonathan answered and said to Adonijah: "On the contrary! Our lord King David has made Solomon king.
And the king sent with him Zadok the priest and Nathan the prophet and Benaiah son of Jehoiada, with the Cherethites and the Pelethites; and they mounted him on the king's mule,
and Zadok the priest and Nathan the prophet anointed him king at Gihon; and they came up from there rejoicing, and the city is in an uproar. That is the sound you heard.
And moreover — Solomon has sat down on the throne of the kingship.

And moreover — the king's servants have come to bless our lord King David: 'May your God make the name of Solomon better than your name, and his throne greater than your throne!' And the king bowed in worship on his bed.
And moreover, thus said the king: 'Blessed be YHWH, the God of Israel, who has given today one to sit on my throne — with my own eyes seeing it.'"
And all the invited guests of Adonijah trembled, and rose — and went, each man his own way.
And Adonijah was afraid before Solomon; and he rose and went and took hold of the horns of the altar.

And it was told to Solomon: "Behold — Adonijah is afraid of King Solomon; and behold, he has seized the horns of the altar, saying: 'Let King Solomon swear to me today that he will not put his servant to death with the sword.'"
And Solomon said: "If he proves a son of valor — not a hair of him shall fall to the ground; but if evil is found in him — he shall die."
And King Solomon sent, and they brought him down from the altar; and he came and prostrated himself to King Solomon. And Solomon said to him: "Go to your house."

1ki.2.text: And the days of David drew near to die; and he charged Solomon his son:
"I am walking the way of all the earth. Be strong — and be a man.

And keep the charge of YHWH your God: to walk in his ways, to keep his statutes, his commandments and his judgments and his testimonies, as it is written in the teaching of Moses — so that you may act wisely in all you do, and everywhere you turn;

so that YHWH may raise up his word that he spoke concerning me: 'If your sons keep their way, to walk before me in truth, with all their heart and with all their soul — there shall not be cut off for you a man from the throne of Israel.'

And you also know what Joab son of Zeruiah did to me — what he did to the two commanders of the armies of Israel, to Abner son of Ner and to Amasa son of Jether: he killed them, and set the blood of war in peacetime, and put the blood of war on the belt around his hips and on the sandal on his feet.
So act according to your wisdom — and do not let his gray head go down to Sheol in peace.
But to the sons of Barzillai the Gileadite show steadfast love, and let them be among those who eat at your table — for so they drew near to me when I fled from Absalom your brother.

And behold — there is with you Shimei son of Gera, the Benjaminite from Bahurim; and he cursed me with a grievous curse on the day I went to Mahanaim. But he came down to meet me at the Jordan, and I swore to him by YHWH: 'I will not put you to death with the sword.'
But now — do not hold him clean; for you are a wise man, and you will know what to do to him: bring his gray head down to Sheol in blood."
And David lay down with his fathers, and was buried in the City of David.
And the days that David reigned over Israel were forty years: at Hebron he reigned seven years, and at Jerusalem he reigned thirty-three years.
And Solomon sat on the throne of David his father — and his kingship was firmly established.
And Adonijah son of Haggith came to Bathsheba, Solomon's mother; and she said: "Is your coming peace?" And he said: "Peace."
And he said: "I have a word for you." And she said: "Speak."
And he said: "You yourself know that the kingship was mine, and all Israel set their faces on me to reign; but the kingship turned around and became my brother's — for from YHWH it was his.
So now — one request I ask of you; do not turn my face away." And she said to him: "Speak."
And he said: "Say, please, to Solomon the king — for he will not turn your face away — that he give me Abishag the Shunammite as a wife."
And Bathsheba said: "Good. I myself will speak to the king for you."

And Bathsheba came to King Solomon to speak to him for Adonijah; and the king rose to meet her and bowed to her, and sat on his throne, and set a throne for the king's mother — and she sat at his right hand.
And she said: "One small request I ask of you; do not turn my face away." And the king said to her: "Ask, my mother — for I will not turn your face away."
And she said: "Let Abishag the Shunammite be given to Adonijah your brother as a wife."

And King Solomon answered and said to his mother: "And why do you ask Abishag the Shunammite for Adonijah? Ask the kingship for him too — for he is my older brother — for him, and for Abiathar the priest, and for Joab son of Zeruiah!"
And King Solomon swore by YHWH: "So may God do to me, and so may he add — for against his own life Adonijah has spoken this word.
And now — as YHWH lives, who has established me and seated me on the throne of David my father, and who has made me a house as he promised: today Adonijah shall be put to death."
And King Solomon sent by the hand of Benaiah son of Jehoiada; and he fell on him — and he died.

And to Abiathar the priest the king said: "Go to Anathoth, to your own fields — for you are a man of death; but this day I will not put you to death, because you carried the ark of the Lord YHWH before David my father, and because you were afflicted in all that my father was afflicted."
So Solomon drove out Abiathar from being priest to YHWH — fulfilling the word of YHWH that he had spoken concerning the house of Eli at Shiloh.
And the report came to Joab — for Joab had leaned after Adonijah, though he had not leaned after Absalom — and Joab fled to the tent of YHWH and took hold of the horns of the altar.
And it was told to King Solomon that Joab had fled to the tent of YHWH — and behold, he was beside the altar; and Solomon sent Benaiah son of Jehoiada: "Go — fall on him."

And Benaiah came to the tent of YHWH and said to him: "Thus says the king: Come out." And he said: "No — for here I will die." And Benaiah brought word back to the king: "Thus Joab spoke, and thus he answered me."
And the king said to him: "Do as he has spoken: fall on him, and bury him — and remove from me, and from my father's house, the blood that Joab poured out without cause.

And may YHWH return his blood on his own head — for he fell on two men more righteous and better than he, and killed them with the sword, and my father David did not know it: Abner son of Ner, commander of the army of Israel, and Amasa son of Jether, commander of the army of Judah.
May their blood return on the head of Joab and on the head of his seed, to the age; but for David, and for his seed, and for his house, and for his throne — may there be peace to the age, from YHWH."
And Benaiah son of Jehoiada went up and fell on him and put him to death; and he was buried at his own house, in the wilderness.
And the king set Benaiah son of Jehoiada over the army in his place; and Zadok the priest the king set in place of Abiathar.
And the king sent and called Shimei, and said to him: "Build yourself a house in Jerusalem, and live there — and do not go out from there, this way or that.
For it shall be: on the day you go out and cross the wadi Kidron — know surely that you shall surely die; your blood will be on your own head."
And Shimei said to the king: "The word is good. As my lord the king has spoken, so your servant will do." And Shimei lived in Jerusalem many days.
And it came to pass, at the end of three years, that two of Shimei's servants fled to Achish son of Maacah, king of Gath; and they told Shimei: "Behold — your servants are in Gath."
And Shimei rose and saddled his donkey and went to Gath, to Achish, to seek his servants; and Shimei went and brought his servants from Gath.
And it was told to Solomon that Shimei had gone from Jerusalem to Gath and returned.

And the king sent and called Shimei and said to him: "Did I not make you swear by YHWH, and warn you: 'On the day you go out and walk this way or that — know surely that you shall surely die'? And you said to me: 'The word is good; I have heard.'
Why then have you not kept the oath of YHWH, and the command I laid on you?"
And the king said to Shimei: "You yourself know all the evil — your own heart knows it — that you did to David my father; and YHWH has returned your evil on your own head.
But King Solomon is blessed — and the throne of David shall stand established before YHWH, to the age."
And the king commanded Benaiah son of Jehoiada; and he went out and fell on him — and he died. And the kingdom stood established in the hand of Solomon.

1ki.3.text: And Solomon allied himself by marriage with Pharaoh king of Egypt; and he took Pharaoh's daughter and brought her to the City of David, until he finished building his own house, and the house of YHWH, and the wall of Jerusalem all around.
Only — the people were sacrificing at the high places; for no house had yet been built for the name of YHWH, until those days.
And Solomon loved YHWH, walking in the statutes of David his father — only, at the high places he was sacrificing and burning incense.
And the king went to Gibeon to sacrifice there, for that was the great high place: a thousand burnt offerings Solomon would offer up on that altar.
At Gibeon YHWH appeared to Solomon in a dream of the night; and God said: "Ask — what shall I give you?"

And Solomon said: "You showed your servant David my father great steadfast love, as he walked before you in truth and in righteousness and in uprightness of heart with you; and you kept for him this great steadfast love, and gave him a son sitting on his throne, as at this day.
And now, YHWH my God — you have made your servant king in place of David my father; and I am a little boy: I do not know how to go out or come in.
And your servant is in the midst of your people whom you have chosen — a people so many they cannot be counted or numbered for multitude.
So give your servant a listening heart, to judge your people — to discern between good and evil; for who is able to judge this weighty people of yours?"
And the word was good in the eyes of the Lord — that Solomon had asked this thing.

And God said to him: "Because you asked this thing, and did not ask for yourself many days, and did not ask riches for yourself, and did not ask the life of your enemies — but asked for yourself discernment, to hear justice:
behold, I have done according to your words. Behold — I have given you a heart wise and discerning, such that there has been none like you before you, and after you none shall rise like you.
And even what you did not ask I have given you — both riches and honor — such that no man among the kings shall be like you, all your days.
And if you walk in my ways, keeping my statutes and my commandments, as David your father walked — then I will lengthen your days."

And Solomon awoke — and behold: a dream. And he came to Jerusalem and stood before the ark of the covenant of the Lord, and offered up burnt offerings and made peace offerings, and made a feast for all his servants.
Then two women — prostitutes — came to the king, and stood before him.
And the one woman said: "Please, my lord — I and this woman live in one house; and I gave birth, with her in the house.
And it came to pass on the third day after I gave birth that this woman also gave birth; and we were together — no stranger with us in the house — only the two of us in the house.
And this woman's son died in the night — because she lay on him.
And she rose in the middle of the night and took my son from beside me — your maidservant being asleep — and laid him in her lap; and her dead son she laid in my lap.
And I rose in the morning to nurse my son — and behold: dead. But when I looked closely at him in the morning — behold: it was not my son, whom I had borne."

And the other woman said: "No! For my son is the living one, and your son is the dead one." And this one kept saying: "No! For your son is the dead one, and my son is the living one." So they spoke before the king.
And the king said: "This one says, 'This is my son — the living one — and your son is the dead one'; and this one says, 'No! For your son is the dead one, and my son is the living one.'"
And the king said: "Fetch me a sword." And they brought the sword before the king.
And the king said: "Cut the living child in two — and give half to one, and half to the other."

And the woman whose son was the living one said to the king — for her compassion burned hot for her son — she said: "Please, my lord! Give her the living child — only do not put him to death!" But the other said: "Neither mine nor yours shall he be. Cut!"
And the king answered and said: "Give her the living child — and do not put him to death. She is his mother."
And all Israel heard of the judgment the king had judged; and they stood in awe before the king — for they saw that the wisdom of God was within him, to do justice.

1ki.4.text: And King Solomon was king over all Israel.
And these were the officials he had: Azariah son of Zadok, the priest;
Elihoreph and Ahijah, sons of Shisha, scribes; Jehoshaphat son of Ahilud, recorder;
and Benaiah son of Jehoiada over the army; and Zadok and Abiathar, priests;
and Azariah son of Nathan over the district officers; and Zabud son of Nathan, a priest, the king's friend;
and Ahishar over the household; and Adoniram son of Abda over the forced labor.
And Solomon had twelve district officers over all Israel, and they provisioned the king and his household — each one would provision one month in the year.
And these are their names: Ben-hur, in the hill country of Ephraim;
Ben-deker, in Makaz and in Shaalbim and Beth-shemesh and Elon-beth-hanan;
Ben-hesed, in Arubboth — to him belonged Socoh and all the land of Hepher;
Ben-abinadab, all the heights of Dor — Taphath, Solomon's daughter, was his wife;
Baana son of Ahilud: Taanach and Megiddo, and all Beth-shean, which is beside Zarethan below Jezreel, from Beth-shean to Abel-meholah, as far as beyond Jokmeam;

Ben-geber, in Ramoth-gilead — to him belonged the Havvoth-jair of Jair son of Manasseh, which are in Gilead; to him the region of Argob, which is in Bashan: sixty great cities with wall and bronze bar;
Ahinadab son of Iddo, at Mahanaim;
Ahimaaz, in Naphtali — he too took a daughter of Solomon, Basemath, as a wife;
Baana son of Hushai, in Asher and Bealoth;
Jehoshaphat son of Paruah, in Issachar;
Shimei son of Ela, in Benjamin;
Geber son of Uri, in the land of Gilead — the land of Sihon king of the Amorites and of Og king of Bashan; and one officer who was in the land.
Judah and Israel were many — like the sand by the sea in multitude — eating and drinking and rejoicing.

1ki.5.text: And Solomon was ruling over all the kingdoms, from the River to the land of the Philistines and to the border of Egypt — bringing tribute and serving Solomon all the days of his life.
And Solomon's food for one day was thirty kors of fine flour and sixty kors of meal,
ten fattened oxen and twenty pasture oxen and a hundred sheep — besides deer and gazelle and roebuck and fattened fowl.
For he held dominion over everything Beyond-the-River, from Tiphsah to Gaza — over all the kings of Beyond-the-River; and he had peace on all sides, all around.
And Judah and Israel lived in security, each man under his own vine and under his own fig tree, from Dan to Beersheba, all the days of Solomon.
And Solomon had forty thousand stalls of horses for his chariotry, and twelve thousand horsemen.
And those district officers provisioned King Solomon, and everyone who drew near King Solomon's table — each his month; they let nothing be lacking.
And the barley and the straw for the horses and the swift steeds they would bring to the place where he might be — each according to his charge.
And God gave Solomon wisdom and understanding in very great measure, and breadth of heart like the sand on the seashore;
and the wisdom of Solomon was greater than the wisdom of all the sons of the east, and than all the wisdom of Egypt.
And he was wiser than any human — than Ethan the Ezrahite, and Heman and Calcol and Darda, the sons of Mahol; and his name was in all the nations round about.
And he spoke three thousand proverbs; and his songs were a thousand and five.
And he spoke of the trees — from the cedar in Lebanon to the hyssop that comes out of the wall; and he spoke of the beasts and of the birds and of the creeping things and of the fish.
And they came from all the peoples to hear the wisdom of Solomon — from all the kings of the earth who had heard of his wisdom.
And Hiram king of Tyre sent his servants to Solomon, for he had heard that they had anointed him king in place of his father — for Hiram had been a lover of David all the days.
And Solomon sent to Hiram:
"You know David my father — that he could not build a house for the name of YHWH his God, because of the war that surrounded him, until YHWH put them under the soles of his feet.
But now YHWH my God has given me rest all around: there is no adversary, and no evil strike.
And behold — I intend to build a house for the name of YHWH my God, as YHWH spoke to David my father: 'Your son, whom I will set in your place on your throne — he shall build the house for my name.'

So now — give command, and let them cut me cedars from Lebanon; and my servants will be with your servants, and I will give you the wages of your servants according to all you say. For you know that there is no one among us who knows how to cut timber like the Sidonians."
And it came to pass, when Hiram heard the words of Solomon, that he rejoiced greatly; and he said: "Blessed be YHWH this day, who has given David a wise son over this numerous people!"
And Hiram sent to Solomon: "I have heard what you sent me. I will do all your desire in cedar timber and in cypress timber.

My servants will bring them down from Lebanon to the sea; and I will make them into rafts on the sea, to the place you direct me, and break them apart there — and you shall carry them. And you shall do my desire, by giving food for my household."
So Hiram kept giving Solomon cedar timber and cypress timber — all his desire;
and Solomon gave Hiram twenty thousand kors of wheat as food for his household, and twenty kors of beaten oil. Thus Solomon would give Hiram year by year.
And YHWH gave wisdom to Solomon, as he had promised him; and there was peace between Hiram and Solomon — and the two of them cut a covenant.
And King Solomon raised a levy of forced labor out of all Israel; and the levy was thirty thousand men.
And he sent them to Lebanon, ten thousand a month in shifts: a month they would be in Lebanon, two months at home. And Adoniram was over the levy.
And Solomon had seventy thousand burden-bearers, and eighty thousand stonecutters in the hill country —
besides Solomon's chief officers who were over the work: three thousand three hundred, directing the people who did the work.
And the king commanded, and they quarried out great stones — costly stones — to lay the foundation of the house with dressed stones.
And Solomon's builders and Hiram's builders, and the men of Gebal, shaped them; and they prepared the timber and the stones to build the house.

1ki.6.text: And it came to pass in the four hundred and eightieth year after the children of Israel came out of the land of Egypt — in the fourth year, in the month of Ziv, which is the second month, of Solomon's reign over Israel — that he built the house for YHWH.
And the house that King Solomon built for YHWH: sixty cubits its length, and twenty its width, and thirty cubits its height.
And the porch in front of the main hall of the house: twenty cubits its length, across the width of the house; ten cubits its depth, in front of the house.
And he made for the house windows with recessed frames.

And he built against the wall of the house a side structure all around — against the walls of the house all around, for the main hall and for the inner sanctuary — and he made side chambers all around:

the lowest story five cubits in width, and the middle six cubits in width, and the third seven cubits in width; for he set ledges around the outside of the house, so as not to grip into the walls of the house.
And the house, when it was being built, was built of stone finished at the quarry; and hammers and the axe — any tool of iron — were not heard in the house while it was being built.
The entrance to the middle chamber was on the south shoulder of the house; and by winding stairs they went up to the middle story, and from the middle to the third.
And he built the house and finished it; and he roofed the house with beams and rows of cedar.
And he built the side structure against the whole house, five cubits its height; and it gripped the house with cedar timbers.
And the word of YHWH came to Solomon:

"This house that you are building — if you walk in my statutes and do my judgments, and keep all my commandments, to walk in them: then I will raise up my word with you, which I spoke to David your father;
and I will dwell in the midst of the children of Israel — and I will not abandon my people Israel."
And Solomon built the house and finished it.

And he built the walls of the house on the inside with boards of cedar — from the floor of the house up to the walls of the ceiling he overlaid it with wood on the inside; and he overlaid the floor of the house with boards of cypress.
And he built the twenty cubits at the rear of the house with boards of cedar, from the floor up to the walls; and he built it within as an inner sanctuary — as the Holy of Holies.
And the house — that is, the main hall in front — was forty cubits.
And the cedar inside the house was carved with gourds and open flowers; all was cedar — no stone was seen.
And he prepared an inner sanctuary in the midst of the house, within — to set there the ark of the covenant of YHWH.
And the front of the inner sanctuary: twenty cubits in length and twenty cubits in width and twenty cubits its height; and he overlaid it with closed gold; and he overlaid the altar with cedar.
And Solomon overlaid the house within with closed gold; and he drew chains of gold across in front of the inner sanctuary, and overlaid it with gold.
And the whole house he overlaid with gold, until all the house was finished; and the whole altar that belonged to the inner sanctuary he overlaid with gold.
And in the inner sanctuary he made two cherubim of olive wood, each ten cubits in height:
five cubits the one wing of the cherub, and five cubits the second wing of the cherub — ten cubits from the tips of its wings to the tips of its wings;
and ten cubits the second cherub: one measure and one shape for the two cherubim —
the height of the one cherub ten cubits, and so the second cherub.

And he set the cherubim inside the inner house; and the wings of the cherubim were spread out, so that the wing of the one touched the wall, and the wing of the second cherub touched the second wall — and their wings toward the middle of the house touched, wing to wing.
And he overlaid the cherubim with gold.
And all the walls of the house, around, he carved with engraved carvings of cherubim and palm trees and open flowers — in the inner room and the outer.
And the floor of the house he overlaid with gold — inner room and outer.
And for the entrance of the inner sanctuary he made doors of olive wood; the lintel and doorposts were a fifth of the wall.

And the two doors were of olive wood; and he carved on them carvings of cherubim and palm trees and open flowers, and overlaid them with gold — hammering the gold down onto the cherubim and onto the palm trees.
And so he made for the entrance of the main hall doorposts of olive wood, a fourth of the wall,
and two doors of cypress wood: the two leaves of the one door folding, and the two leaves of the second door folding.
And he carved cherubim and palm trees and open flowers, and overlaid them with gold laid evenly over the carved work.
And he built the inner court: three courses of dressed stone, and a course of cut cedar beams.
In the fourth year the foundation of the house of YHWH was laid, in the month of Ziv;
and in the eleventh year, in the month of Bul — which is the eighth month — the house was finished in all its details and all its specifications. So he built it in seven years.

1ki.7.text: And his own house Solomon built in thirteen years — and he finished all his house.

And he built the House of the Forest of Lebanon: a hundred cubits its length, and fifty cubits its width, and thirty cubits its height — on four rows of cedar pillars, with cut cedar beams on the pillars;
and it was roofed with cedar above, over the side chambers that rested on the pillars — forty-five, fifteen to a row;
and there were window frames in three rows, and opening faced opening, three times;
and all the doorways and doorposts were squared in frame — and opening faced opening, three times.
And he made the Hall of Pillars: fifty cubits its length and thirty cubits its width; and a porch in front of them, with pillars and a canopy in front of them.
And he made the Hall of the Throne, where he would judge — the Hall of Judgment; and it was paneled with cedar from floor to floor.
And his house where he would live, in the other court, set back from the hall, was of like workmanship; and he made a house like this hall for Pharaoh's daughter, whom Solomon had taken.
All these were of costly stones, cut to measure, sawed with saws inside and out — from the foundation to the coping, and outside as far as the great court.
And the foundation was of costly stones — great stones: stones of ten cubits and stones of eight cubits;
and above were costly stones, cut to measure, and cedar.
And the great court all around had three courses of dressed stone and a course of cut cedar beams — as did the inner court of the house of YHWH, and the porch of the house.
And King Solomon sent and fetched Hiram from Tyre.

He was the son of a widow woman of the tribe of Naphtali, and his father was a man of Tyre, a worker in bronze; and he was filled with the wisdom and the understanding and the knowledge to do every work in bronze. And he came to King Solomon and did all his work.
And he formed the two pillars of bronze: eighteen cubits the height of the one pillar, and a line of twelve cubits would go around the second pillar.
And he made two capitals to set on the tops of the pillars, cast in bronze: five cubits the height of the one capital, and five cubits the height of the second capital;
lattices of lattice-work, twisted threads of chain-work, for the capitals on the top of the pillars: seven for the one capital and seven for the second capital.
And he made the pillars with two rows around on the one lattice, to cover the capitals that were on the top, above the pomegranates; and so he did for the second capital.
And the capitals on the tops of the pillars in the porch were of lily-work — four cubits.
And there were capitals on the two pillars also above, close beside the bulge that was next to the lattice; and the pomegranates were two hundred, in rows around, on the second capital.

And he raised up the pillars for the porch of the main hall: he raised the right pillar and called its name Jachin — He-Establishes; and he raised the left pillar and called its name Boaz — In-Him-Is-Strength.
And on the top of the pillars was lily-work. So the work of the pillars was finished.
And he made the Sea, cast: ten cubits from brim to brim, circular all around, and five cubits its height; and a line of thirty cubits would circle it all around.
And gourds were under its brim all around, encircling it — ten to a cubit, surrounding the Sea all around: two rows of gourds, cast in its casting.
It stood on twelve oxen: three facing north, and three facing west, and three facing south, and three facing east; and the Sea was on them, above — and all their hindquarters were inward.
And its thickness was a handbreadth, and its brim was made like the brim of a cup — like a lily blossom. Two thousand baths it would hold.
And he made the ten stands of bronze: four cubits the length of the one stand, and four cubits its width, and three cubits its height.
And this was the workmanship of the stand: they had panels, and panels between the crossbars;
and on the panels between the crossbars were lions, oxen, and cherubim — and on the crossbars likewise, above; and below the lions and the oxen were wreaths of hanging work.
And each stand had four bronze wheels and axles of bronze; and its four feet had shoulder-pieces: beneath the basin the shoulder-pieces were cast, with wreaths at the side of each.

And its mouth, inside the crown and upward, was a cubit; and its mouth was round, made like a pedestal, a cubit and a half; and also on its mouth were carvings — but their panels were square, not round.
And the four wheels were underneath the panels, and the axles of the wheels were in the stand; and the height of each wheel was a cubit and half a cubit.
And the workmanship of the wheels was like the workmanship of a chariot wheel: their axles and their rims and their spokes and their hubs — all cast.
And there were four shoulder-pieces at the four corners of each stand; the shoulder-pieces were of one piece with the stand.
And at the top of the stand was a round band, half a cubit high; and on the top of the stand its stays and its panels were of one piece with it.
And he engraved on the plates of its stays and on its panels cherubim, lions, and palm trees — according to the clear space on each — with wreaths all around.
In this way he made the ten stands: one casting, one measure, one shape for all of them.
And he made ten basins of bronze: each basin would hold forty baths; each basin was four cubits; one basin on each stand, for the ten stands.
And he set the stands, five on the right side of the house and five on the left side of the house; and the Sea he set on the right side of the house, to the east, toward the south.
And Hiram made the basins and the shovels and the sprinkling bowls. So Hiram finished doing all the work that he did for King Solomon on the house of YHWH:
two pillars; and the bowls of the capitals that were on the top of the pillars, two; and the two lattices to cover the two bowls of the capitals on the top of the pillars;
and the four hundred pomegranates for the two lattices — two rows of pomegranates for each lattice, to cover the two bowls of the capitals on the face of the pillars;
and the ten stands, and the ten basins on the stands;
and the one Sea, and the twelve oxen under the Sea;
and the pots and the shovels and the sprinkling bowls. And all these vessels, which Hiram made for King Solomon for the house of YHWH, were of burnished bronze.
In the plain of the Jordan the king cast them, in the thick clay ground between Succoth and Zarethan.
And Solomon left all the vessels unweighed, because of their very great abundance; the weight of the bronze was never determined.
And Solomon made all the vessels that were in the house of YHWH: the altar of gold; and the table on which was the Bread of the Presence, of gold;
and the lampstands — five on the right and five on the left, before the inner sanctuary — of closed gold; and the flowers and the lamps and the tongs, of gold;

and the cups and the snuffers and the sprinkling bowls and the ladles and the fire-pans, of closed gold; and the hinges for the doors of the inner house — the Holy of Holies — and for the doors of the house — the main hall — of gold.

So all the work that King Solomon did on the house of YHWH was completed; and Solomon brought in the holy things of David his father — the silver and the gold and the vessels — and put them in the treasuries of the house of YHWH.

1ki.8.text: Then Solomon assembled the elders of Israel — all the heads of the tribes, the leaders of the fathers' houses of the children of Israel — to King Solomon at Jerusalem, to bring up the ark of the covenant of YHWH from the City of David, which is Zion.
And all the men of Israel assembled to King Solomon at the festival, in the month of Ethanim — which is the seventh month.
And all the elders of Israel came; and the priests lifted the ark,
and they brought up the ark of YHWH, and the tent of meeting, and all the holy vessels that were in the tent — the priests and the Levites brought them up.
And King Solomon, and the whole congregation of Israel who had assembled to him, were with him before the ark — sacrificing sheep and oxen that could not be counted or numbered for multitude.
And the priests brought the ark of the covenant of YHWH to its place, into the inner sanctuary of the house — into the Holy of Holies, under the wings of the cherubim;
for the cherubim spread out their wings over the place of the ark, and the cherubim made a covering over the ark and over its poles, from above.
And the poles were so long that the ends of the poles could be seen from the holy place, in front of the inner sanctuary — but they could not be seen from outside. And they are there to this day.
There was nothing in the ark except the two tablets of stone that Moses had placed there at Horeb — where YHWH cut a covenant with the children of Israel when they came out of the land of Egypt.
And it came to pass, as the priests came out of the holy place, that the cloud filled the house of YHWH;
and the priests could not stand to serve because of the cloud — for the glory of YHWH filled the house of YHWH.
Then Solomon said: "YHWH has said he would dwell in the thick darkness.
I have surely built you a house of lofty dwelling — a fixed place for your sitting, for the ages."
And the king turned his face and blessed the whole assembly of Israel — with the whole assembly of Israel standing —
and he said: "Blessed be YHWH, the God of Israel, who spoke with his own mouth to David my father — and with his own hand has fulfilled it, saying:
'From the day I brought my people Israel out of Egypt, I chose no city out of all the tribes of Israel to build a house, for my name to be there; but I chose David, to be over my people Israel.'
And it was with the heart of David my father to build a house for the name of YHWH, the God of Israel;
but YHWH said to David my father: 'Because it was with your heart to build a house for my name — you did well, that it was with your heart.
Only — you shall not build the house; rather your son, who comes out of your loins: he shall build the house for my name.'

And YHWH has raised up his word that he spoke: I have risen in place of David my father, and I sit on the throne of Israel, as YHWH spoke, and I have built the house for the name of YHWH, the God of Israel;
and I have set there a place for the ark, in which is the covenant of YHWH that he cut with our fathers when he brought them out of the land of Egypt."
And Solomon stood before the altar of YHWH, in front of the whole assembly of Israel, and spread out his palms toward the heavens;

and he said: "YHWH, God of Israel — there is no God like you, in the heavens above or on the earth beneath: keeping the covenant and the steadfast love for your servants who walk before you with all their heart;
who have kept for your servant David my father what you spoke to him: you spoke with your mouth, and with your hand you have fulfilled it — as at this day.

And now, YHWH, God of Israel — keep for your servant David my father what you spoke to him: 'There shall not be cut off for you a man from before me, sitting on the throne of Israel — if only your sons keep their way, to walk before me as you have walked before me.'
And now, God of Israel — let your word be confirmed, please, which you spoke to your servant David my father.
But will God really dwell on the earth? Behold — the heavens, and the heavens of the heavens, cannot contain you; how much less this house that I have built!
Yet turn to the prayer of your servant and to his plea, YHWH my God — to hear the cry and the prayer your servant prays before you today:
that your eyes may be open toward this house night and day — toward the place of which you said, 'My name shall be there' — to hear the prayer your servant prays toward this place.
And hear the plea of your servant, and of your people Israel, when they pray toward this place; and you — hear in the place of your dwelling, in the heavens. Hear — and forgive.
If a man sins against his fellow, and an oath is laid on him to bind him by oath, and he comes and swears before your altar in this house —

then you — hear in the heavens, and act, and judge your servants: condemning the wicked, to bring his way down on his own head; and vindicating the righteous, to give him according to his righteousness.
When your people Israel are struck down before an enemy because they have sinned against you, and they return to you and confess your name, and pray and plead with you in this house —
then you — hear in the heavens, and forgive the sin of your people Israel, and bring them back to the soil you gave to their fathers.
When the heavens are shut up and there is no rain, because they have sinned against you, and they pray toward this place and confess your name, and turn back from their sin because you afflict them —

then you — hear in the heavens, and forgive the sin of your servants, and of your people Israel — for you teach them the good way in which they should walk — and give rain on your land, which you gave to your people as an inheritance.
If there is famine in the land; if there is plague; if there is blight, mildew, locust, devouring locust; if their enemy besieges them in the land of their gates — any strike, any sickness —
any prayer, any plea, that any person of all your people Israel makes — each knowing the strike of his own heart — spreading out his palms toward this house:

then you — hear in the heavens, the fixed place of your dwelling, and forgive, and act; and give to each according to all his ways, as you know his heart — for you alone know the heart of all the sons of humankind —
so that they may fear you all the days that they live on the face of the soil that you gave to our fathers.
And also the foreigner, who is not of your people Israel, but comes from a far country for the sake of your name —
for they shall hear of your great name and of your strong hand and your outstretched arm — when he comes and prays toward this house:

you — hear in the heavens, the fixed place of your dwelling, and act according to all the foreigner calls to you for — so that all the peoples of the earth may know your name, to fear you as your people Israel do, and may know that your name is called over this house that I have built.
When your people go out to battle against their enemy, on the way you send them, and they pray to YHWH toward the city you have chosen and the house I have built for your name —
then hear in the heavens their prayer and their plea, and uphold their cause.

When they sin against you — for there is no human who does not sin — and you are angry with them and give them over before an enemy, and their captors carry them captive to the enemy's land, far or near;

and they turn it back to their heart in the land where they are held captive, and turn, and plead with you in the land of their captors, saying: 'We have sinned, and gone crooked; we have done wickedly';

and they return to you with all their heart and with all their soul, in the land of their enemies who carried them captive, and pray to you toward their land, which you gave to their fathers — the city you have chosen, and the house I have built for your name:
then hear in the heavens, the fixed place of your dwelling, their prayer and their plea, and uphold their cause;
and forgive your people who have sinned against you — all their rebellions by which they rebelled against you; and give them compassion before their captors, that they may have compassion on them.
For they are your people and your inheritance, whom you brought out of Egypt, from inside the iron furnace.
May your eyes be open to the plea of your servant and to the plea of your people Israel — to listen to them whenever they call to you.

For you yourself set them apart for yourself as an inheritance, out of all the peoples of the earth — as you spoke by the hand of Moses your servant, when you brought our fathers out of Egypt, Lord YHWH."

And it came to pass, when Solomon had finished praying to YHWH all this prayer and plea, that he rose from before the altar of YHWH, from kneeling on his knees with his palms spread toward the heavens;
and he stood and blessed the whole assembly of Israel with a great voice:
"Blessed be YHWH, who has given rest to his people Israel, according to all he spoke. Not one word has fallen of all his good word, which he spoke by the hand of Moses his servant.
May YHWH our God be with us, as he was with our fathers; may he not abandon us and not cast us off —
bending our hearts toward him: to walk in all his ways, and to keep his commandments and his statutes and his judgments, which he commanded our fathers.

And may these words of mine, with which I have pleaded before YHWH, be near to YHWH our God day and night — to uphold the cause of his servant and the cause of his people Israel, each day's matter in its day —
so that all the peoples of the earth may know that YHWH — he is God. There is none else.
And let your heart be whole with YHWH our God — to walk in his statutes and to keep his commandments, as at this day."
And the king, and all Israel with him, were sacrificing sacrifice before YHWH.

And Solomon sacrificed the peace offerings that he sacrificed to YHWH: twenty-two thousand oxen and a hundred and twenty thousand sheep. So the king and all the children of Israel dedicated the house of YHWH.

On that day the king consecrated the middle of the court in front of the house of YHWH; for there he made the burnt offering and the grain offering and the fat of the peace offerings — because the bronze altar before YHWH was too small to hold the burnt offering and the grain offering and the fat of the peace offerings.
And Solomon held the festival at that time — and all Israel with him, a great assembly, from Lebo-hamath to the Brook of Egypt — before YHWH our God, seven days and seven days: fourteen days.

On the eighth day he sent the people away; and they blessed the king, and went to their tents rejoicing and glad of heart over all the good that YHWH had done for David his servant and for Israel his people.

1ki.9.text: And it came to pass, when Solomon had finished building the house of YHWH and the king's house, and every desire of Solomon that he delighted to do,
that YHWH appeared to Solomon a second time, as he had appeared to him at Gibeon.

And YHWH said to him: "I have heard your prayer and your plea, which you pleaded before me. I have consecrated this house that you built, to set my name there to the age; and my eyes and my heart shall be there all the days.
And you — if you walk before me as David your father walked, in wholeness of heart and in uprightness, doing according to all I have commanded you, keeping my statutes and my judgments:
then I will raise up the throne of your kingdom over Israel to the age, as I spoke to David your father: 'There shall not be cut off for you a man from the throne of Israel.'
But if you turn — you or your sons — clean away from following me, and do not keep my commandments and my statutes which I have set before you, and you go and serve other gods and bow down to them:

then I will cut Israel off from the face of the soil I have given them; and the house I have consecrated for my name I will send away from before my face — and Israel shall become a proverb and a taunt among all the peoples.
And this house shall become a heap of ruins; everyone passing by it will be appalled, and will hiss, and they will say: 'Why has YHWH done such a thing to this land and to this house?'

And they will say: 'Because they abandoned YHWH their God, who brought their fathers out of the land of Egypt, and took hold of other gods and bowed down to them and served them — therefore YHWH has brought on them all this evil.'"
And it came to pass, at the end of the twenty years in which Solomon built the two houses — the house of YHWH and the king's house —
Hiram king of Tyre having supplied Solomon with cedar timber and cypress timber and gold, as much as he desired — that then King Solomon gave Hiram twenty cities in the land of Galilee.
And Hiram came out from Tyre to see the cities Solomon had given him — and they were not right in his eyes;
and he said: "What are these cities you have given me, my brother?" And he called them the land of Cabul — Good-for-Nothing — to this day.
And Hiram had sent the king a hundred and twenty talents of gold.
And this is the account of the forced labor that King Solomon raised — to build the house of YHWH, and his own house, and the Millo, and the wall of Jerusalem, and Hazor and Megiddo and Gezer.
(Pharaoh king of Egypt had gone up and captured Gezer and burned it with fire; and the Canaanites living in the city he had killed — and he gave it as a dowry to his daughter, Solomon's wife.)
And Solomon built Gezer, and Lower Beth-horon,
and Baalath, and Tamar in the wilderness, in the land;

and all the storage cities Solomon had, and the chariot cities and the horsemen's cities — and every desire of Solomon that he desired to build, in Jerusalem and in Lebanon and in all the land of his dominion.
All the people who were left of the Amorites, the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites — who were not of the children of Israel —
their descendants who were left after them in the land, whom the children of Israel had not been able to devote to destruction: these Solomon raised as a levy of forced labor, to this day.
But of the children of Israel Solomon made no slave; for they were the men of war, and his servants and his commanders and his officers, and the commanders of his chariotry and his horsemen.
These were the chief officers over Solomon's work: five hundred and fifty, directing the people who did the work.
