As soon as Pharaoh's daughter came up from the City of David to her house that he had built for her — then he built the Millo.
And Solomon would offer up, three times a year, burnt offerings and peace offerings on the altar he had built for YHWH, burning incense with them before YHWH. So he completed the house.
And King Solomon made a fleet at Ezion-geber, which is near Eloth, on the shore of the Sea of Reeds, in the land of Edom;
and Hiram sent his servants with the fleet — men of ships, who knew the sea — together with the servants of Solomon;
and they came to Ophir and took from there gold — four hundred and twenty talents — and brought it to King Solomon.

1ki.10.text: And the queen of Sheba heard the report of Solomon — for the name of YHWH — and she came to test him with riddles.

And she came to Jerusalem with a very weighty retinue: camels carrying spices, and gold in great abundance, and precious stones. And she came to Solomon and spoke with him all that was with her heart;
and Solomon answered all her questions — there was nothing hidden from the king that he did not tell her.
And the queen of Sheba saw all the wisdom of Solomon, and the house he had built,

and the food of his table, and the seating of his servants, and the attendance of his ministers and their clothing, and his cupbearers, and his burnt offering that he offered up at the house of YHWH — and there was no more spirit in her.
And she said to the king: "True was the word I heard in my own land about your words and about your wisdom.
And I did not believe the words until I came and my own eyes saw — and behold: not the half was told me. You exceed in wisdom and goodness the report I heard.
Happy are your men! Happy are these your servants, who stand before you continually, hearing your wisdom!
May YHWH your God be blessed, who delighted in you, to set you on the throne of Israel! Because YHWH loves Israel to the age, he has made you king — to do justice and righteousness."

And she gave the king a hundred and twenty talents of gold, and spices in very great abundance, and precious stones; never again did such an abundance of spice come in as that which the queen of Sheba gave King Solomon.
And also the fleet of Hiram, which carried gold from Ophir, brought from Ophir almug wood in very great abundance, and precious stones;
and the king made of the almug wood supports for the house of YHWH and for the king's house, and lyres and harps for the singers. No such almug wood has come or been seen to this day.

And King Solomon gave the queen of Sheba all her desire, whatever she asked — besides what he gave her according to the hand of King Solomon. And she turned and went to her own land, she and her servants.
And the weight of the gold that came to Solomon in one year was six hundred sixty-six talents of gold —
besides that from the traveling merchants, and the trade of the traders, and all the kings of Arabia, and the governors of the land.
And King Solomon made two hundred large shields of beaten gold — six hundred shekels of gold going into each shield —
and three hundred small shields of beaten gold — three minas of gold going into each shield; and the king put them in the House of the Forest of Lebanon.
And the king made a great throne of ivory, and overlaid it with refined gold:
six steps to the throne, and a rounded top to the throne behind it, and armrests on this side and that of the seat, with two lions standing beside the armrests;
and twelve lions standing there on the six steps, on this side and that. Nothing like it was ever made for any kingdom.
And all King Solomon's drinking vessels were gold, and all the vessels of the House of the Forest of Lebanon were closed gold. There was no silver — it was counted as nothing in the days of Solomon.
For the king had a fleet of Tarshish at sea with Hiram's fleet: once every three years the fleet of Tarshish would come, carrying gold and silver, ivory, and apes, and peacocks.
So King Solomon grew greater than all the kings of the earth, in riches and in wisdom;
and all the earth sought the face of Solomon, to hear his wisdom, which God had set in his heart;
and they kept bringing, each man his gift — vessels of silver and vessels of gold, and garments and weapons and spices, horses and mules — year by year.
And Solomon gathered chariots and horsemen: he had a thousand four hundred chariots and twelve thousand horsemen; and he stationed them in the chariot cities, and with the king at Jerusalem.
And the king made silver in Jerusalem like stones, and cedars he made like the sycamores in the lowland, for abundance.
And the source of Solomon's horses was Egypt — and Kue: the king's merchants would take them from Kue at a price;

and a chariot would come up and go out of Egypt for six hundred pieces of silver, and a horse for a hundred and fifty; and so, by their hand, they exported them to all the kings of the Hittites and the kings of Aram.

1ki.11.text: But King Solomon loved many foreign women — besides Pharaoh's daughter: Moabite, Ammonite, Edomite, Sidonian, Hittite women —

from the nations of which YHWH had said to the children of Israel: "You shall not go in among them, and they shall not come in among you; surely they will bend your heart after their gods." To these Solomon clung in love.
And he had seven hundred wives — princesses — and three hundred concubines; and his wives bent his heart.
And it came to pass, at the time of Solomon's old age, that his wives bent his heart after other gods; and his heart was not whole with YHWH his God, like the heart of David his father.
And Solomon went after Ashtoreth, the goddess of the Sidonians, and after Milcom, the abomination of the Ammonites.
And Solomon did the evil in the eyes of YHWH, and did not follow fully after YHWH like David his father.
Then Solomon built a high place for Chemosh, the abomination of Moab, on the mountain that faces Jerusalem — and for Molech, the abomination of the sons of Ammon.
And so he did for all his foreign wives, who burned incense and sacrificed to their gods.
And YHWH was angry with Solomon, because his heart had bent away from YHWH, the God of Israel, who had appeared to him twice,
and had commanded him concerning this very thing — not to go after other gods; but he did not keep what YHWH commanded.

And YHWH said to Solomon: "Because this has been with you, and you have not kept my covenant and my statutes which I commanded you — I will surely tear the kingdom from you and give it to your servant.
Only, in your days I will not do it — for the sake of David your father; out of the hand of your son I will tear it.
Yet the whole kingdom I will not tear away: one tribe I will give to your son — for the sake of David my servant, and for the sake of Jerusalem, which I have chosen."
And YHWH raised up an adversary against Solomon: Hadad the Edomite; he was of the royal seed in Edom.
For it came to pass, when David was in Edom — when Joab the commander of the army went up to bury the slain — that he struck down every male in Edom;
for Joab stayed there six months, with all Israel, until he had cut off every male in Edom.
But Hadad fled — he and Edomite men of his father's servants with him — to go to Egypt; and Hadad was a small boy.
And they rose out of Midian and came to Paran, and took men with them from Paran, and came to Egypt, to Pharaoh king of Egypt; and he gave him a house, and assigned him food, and gave him land.
And Hadad found great favor in the eyes of Pharaoh, and he gave him as a wife the sister of his own wife — the sister of Tahpenes the queen mother.
And the sister of Tahpenes bore him Genubath his son; and Tahpenes weaned him inside Pharaoh's house — and Genubath was in Pharaoh's house among the sons of Pharaoh.
And Hadad heard in Egypt that David lay down with his fathers, and that Joab the commander of the army was dead; and Hadad said to Pharaoh: "Send me off — and let me go to my own land."
And Pharaoh said to him: "But what do you lack with me, that behold — you are seeking to go to your own land?" And he said: "Nothing. But you must surely send me off."
And God raised up against him another adversary: Rezon son of Eliada, who had fled from his lord Hadadezer king of Zobah;
and he gathered men around him and became commander of a raiding band, when David killed them; and they went to Damascus and settled in it — and they reigned in Damascus.
And he was an adversary to Israel all the days of Solomon — this besides the evil of Hadad; and he loathed Israel, and reigned over Aram.
And Jeroboam son of Nebat — an Ephraimite from Zeredah, whose mother's name was Zeruah, a widow woman — a servant of Solomon: he too lifted a hand against the king.
And this is the matter for which he lifted a hand against the king: Solomon built the Millo; he closed the breach of the City of David his father.
Now the man Jeroboam was a mighty man of valor; and Solomon saw that the young man was a doer of work, and appointed him over all the carrying-labor of the house of Joseph.

And it came to pass at that time, when Jeroboam went out of Jerusalem, that Ahijah the Shilonite, the prophet, found him on the road — he wrapped in a new cloak, and the two of them alone in the open field.
And Ahijah took hold of the new cloak that was on him, and tore it into twelve pieces;
and he said to Jeroboam: "Take ten pieces for yourself. For thus says YHWH, the God of Israel: Behold — I am tearing the kingdom out of the hand of Solomon, and I will give you the ten tribes.
But the one tribe shall remain his — for the sake of my servant David, and for the sake of Jerusalem, the city I have chosen out of all the tribes of Israel —

because they have abandoned me, and bowed down to Ashtoreth the goddess of the Sidonians, to Chemosh the god of Moab, and to Milcom the god of the sons of Ammon; and they have not walked in my ways, doing what is right in my eyes — my statutes and my judgments — like David his father.

"Yet I will not take the whole kingdom out of his hand; for I will keep him as ruler all the days of his life — for the sake of David my servant, whom I chose, who kept my commandments and my statutes.
But I will take the kingship out of the hand of his son and give it to you — the ten tribes;
and to his son I will give one tribe, so that David my servant may have a lamp before me all the days, in Jerusalem, the city I have chosen for myself, to set my name there.
And you I will take, and you shall reign over all that your soul desires — and you shall be king over Israel.

And it shall be: if you listen to all I command you, and walk in my ways, and do what is right in my eyes, keeping my statutes and my commandments, as David my servant did — then I will be with you, and I will build you a faithful house, as I built for David, and I will give Israel to you.
And I will humble the seed of David because of this — but not all the days."
And Solomon sought to put Jeroboam to death; but Jeroboam rose and fled to Egypt, to Shishak king of Egypt — and he was in Egypt until the death of Solomon.
And the rest of the acts of Solomon, and all that he did, and his wisdom — are they not written in the Scroll of the Acts of Solomon?
And the days Solomon reigned in Jerusalem over all Israel were forty years.
And Solomon lay down with his fathers and was buried in the city of David his father; and Rehoboam his son reigned in his place.

1ki.12.text: And Rehoboam went to Shechem — for all Israel had come to Shechem to make him king.
And it came to pass, when Jeroboam son of Nebat heard — he was still in Egypt, where he had fled from King Solomon — that Jeroboam stayed in Egypt;
and they sent and called him. And Jeroboam and the whole assembly of Israel came, and spoke to Rehoboam:
"Your father made our yoke hard. Now — lighten the hard service of your father, and his heavy yoke that he laid on us — and we will serve you."
And he said to them: "Go away for three more days — then return to me." And the people went.
And King Rehoboam took counsel with the elders who had stood before Solomon his father while he lived: "How do you counsel me to answer this people?"
And they spoke to him: "If today you will be a servant to this people, and serve them, and answer them and speak good words to them — then they will be your servants all the days."
But he abandoned the counsel of the elders which they had counseled him, and took counsel with the young men who had grown up with him, who stood before him;
and he said to them: "What do you counsel, that we answer this people, who have spoken to me, saying: 'Lighten the yoke your father laid on us'?"

And the young men who had grown up with him spoke to him: "Thus shall you say to this people who spoke to you, saying, 'Your father made our yoke heavy — you lighten it for us': thus shall you speak to them: 'My little finger is thicker than my father's loins!
So now — my father loaded a heavy yoke on you, and I will add to your yoke; my father disciplined you with whips — and I will discipline you with scorpions.'"
And Jeroboam and all the people came to Rehoboam on the third day, as the king had spoken: "Return to me on the third day."
And the king answered the people harshly; and he abandoned the counsel of the elders which they had counseled him,

and spoke to them according to the counsel of the young men: "My father made your yoke heavy, and I will add to your yoke; my father disciplined you with whips — and I will discipline you with scorpions."
So the king did not listen to the people — for it was a turn of events from YHWH, in order to raise up his word that YHWH had spoken by the hand of Ahijah the Shilonite to Jeroboam son of Nebat.

And all Israel saw that the king had not listened to them; and the people returned the king an answer: "What share do we have in David? No inheritance in the son of Jesse! To your tents, Israel! Now see to your own house, David!" And Israel went to its tents.
But as for the children of Israel living in the cities of Judah — Rehoboam reigned over them.
And King Rehoboam sent Adoram, who was over the forced labor; and all Israel stoned him with stone, and he died. And King Rehoboam managed to get up into the chariot, to flee to Jerusalem.
So Israel has been in rebellion against the house of David to this day.

And it came to pass, when all Israel heard that Jeroboam had returned, that they sent and called him to the congregation, and made him king over all Israel. None followed the house of David except the tribe of Judah alone.

And Rehoboam came to Jerusalem and assembled all the house of Judah and the tribe of Benjamin — a hundred and eighty thousand chosen warriors — to fight against the house of Israel, to bring the kingship back to Rehoboam son of Solomon.
But the word of God came to Shemaiah, the man of God:
"Say to Rehoboam son of Solomon, king of Judah, and to all the house of Judah and Benjamin, and the rest of the people:

Thus says YHWH: You shall not go up, and you shall not fight with your brothers, the children of Israel. Return, each man to his house — for from me this thing has come." And they listened to the word of YHWH, and turned back to go, according to the word of YHWH.
And Jeroboam built Shechem in the hill country of Ephraim and lived in it; and he went out from there and built Penuel.
And Jeroboam said in his heart: "Now the kingdom will return to the house of David.

If this people goes up to make sacrifices in the house of YHWH in Jerusalem, then the heart of this people will return to their lord — to Rehoboam king of Judah; and they will kill me, and return to Rehoboam king of Judah."
And the king took counsel, and made two calves of gold; and he said to them: "You have gone up to Jerusalem long enough! Behold your gods, Israel, who brought you up from the land of Egypt!"
And he set the one in Bethel, and the other he put in Dan.
And this thing became a sin — and the people went before the one, as far as Dan.
And he made houses on high places, and made priests from the whole range of the people, who were not of the sons of Levi.

And Jeroboam made a festival in the eighth month, on the fifteenth day of the month, like the festival that was in Judah, and he went up onto the altar. So he did in Bethel, sacrificing to the calves he had made; and he stationed in Bethel the priests of the high places he had made.

And he went up onto the altar he had made in Bethel, on the fifteenth day of the eighth month — in the month he had devised out of his own heart; and he made a festival for the children of Israel, and went up onto the altar to burn incense.

1ki.13.text: And behold — a man of God came from Judah, by the word of YHWH, to Bethel, as Jeroboam was standing on the altar to burn incense;

and he called out against the altar by the word of YHWH and said: "Altar! Altar! Thus says YHWH: Behold — a son shall be born to the house of David, Josiah his name; and he shall sacrifice on you the priests of the high places who burn incense on you — and human bones shall be burned on you."
And he gave a sign that day: "This is the sign that YHWH has spoken: behold — the altar shall be torn apart, and the ashes on it shall be poured out."

And it came to pass, when the king heard the word of the man of God, which he called out against the altar at Bethel, that Jeroboam stretched out his hand from the altar: "Seize him!" And his hand, which he stretched out against him, dried up — and he could not draw it back to himself.
And the altar was torn apart, and the ashes poured out from the altar — according to the sign the man of God had given by the word of YHWH.

And the king answered and said to the man of God: "Soften, please, the face of YHWH your God, and pray for me — that my hand may return to me." And the man of God softened the face of YHWH; and the king's hand returned to him, and became as before.
And the king spoke to the man of God: "Come home with me and refresh yourself — and let me give you a gift."
And the man of God said to the king: "If you gave me half your house, I would not go in with you; and I will not eat bread and will not drink water in this place.
For so it was commanded me by the word of YHWH: 'You shall not eat bread, and you shall not drink water, and you shall not return by the road you went.'"
And he went by another road, and did not return by the road on which he had come to Bethel.

Now a certain old prophet was living in Bethel; and his son came and recounted to him all the deed the man of God had done that day in Bethel — the words he had spoken to the king; and they recounted them to their father.
And their father spoke to them: "Which way did he go?" And his sons had seen the road by which the man of God who came from Judah had gone.
And he said to his sons: "Saddle the donkey for me." And they saddled the donkey for him, and he rode on it,
and went after the man of God, and found him sitting under the terebinth; and he said to him: "Are you the man of God who came from Judah?" And he said: "I am."
And he said to him: "Come home with me — and eat bread."
And he said: "I cannot turn back with you or go in with you; and I will not eat bread, and I will not drink water with you in this place.
For a word came to me by the word of YHWH: 'You shall not eat bread and you shall not drink water there; you shall not turn back to go by the road you went.'"
And he said to him: "I too am a prophet like you; and an angel spoke to me by the word of YHWH: 'Bring him back with you to your house, and let him eat bread and drink water.'" He lied to him.
So he turned back with him, and ate bread in his house, and drank water.
And it came to pass, as they were sitting at the table, that the word of YHWH came to the prophet who had brought him back;
and he called out to the man of God who had come from Judah: "Thus says YHWH: Because you have rebelled against the mouth of YHWH, and have not kept the commandment that YHWH your God commanded you,
but turned back and ate bread and drank water in the place of which he spoke to you, 'Do not eat bread and do not drink water' — your corpse shall not come to the tomb of your fathers."
And it came to pass, after he had eaten bread and after he had drunk, that he saddled the donkey for him — for the prophet whom he had brought back;
and he went — and a lion found him on the road and killed him. And his corpse lay thrown down on the road, with the donkey standing beside it, and the lion standing beside the corpse.
And behold — men passing by saw the corpse thrown down on the road, and the lion standing beside the corpse; and they came and told it in the city where the old prophet lived.

And the prophet who had brought him back from the road heard, and said: "It is the man of God who rebelled against the mouth of YHWH; and YHWH has given him to the lion, and it has broken him and killed him — according to the word of YHWH that he spoke to him."
And he spoke to his sons: "Saddle the donkey for me." And they saddled it.
And he went and found his corpse thrown down on the road, with the donkey and the lion standing beside the corpse; the lion had not eaten the corpse and had not broken the donkey.
And the prophet lifted the corpse of the man of God and laid it on the donkey and brought it back; and he came to the city of the old prophet, to lament and to bury him.
And he laid his corpse in his own grave; and they lamented over him: "Alas, my brother!"
And it came to pass, after he had buried him, that he said to his sons: "When I die, bury me in the grave where the man of God is buried; beside his bones lay my bones.
For the word he called out by the word of YHWH against the altar in Bethel, and against all the houses of the high places in the cities of Samaria — it shall surely come to pass."

After this thing, Jeroboam did not turn back from his evil way; and again he made priests for the high places from the whole range of the people — whoever desired it, he would fill his hand, and he became one of the priests of the high places.
And in this thing there was sin for the house of Jeroboam — to blot it out and destroy it from the face of the ground.

1ki.14.text: At that time Abijah son of Jeroboam fell sick.

And Jeroboam said to his wife: "Rise, please, and disguise yourself, so they will not know that you are the wife of Jeroboam; and go to Shiloh. Behold — Ahijah the prophet is there, the one who spoke of me becoming king over this people.
And take in your hand ten loaves of bread, and cakes, and a jar of honey, and go to him. He will tell you what will happen to the boy."
And Jeroboam's wife did so: she rose and went to Shiloh, and came to the house of Ahijah. Now Ahijah could not see — for his eyes were set because of his old age.

But YHWH had said to Ahijah: "Behold — the wife of Jeroboam is coming to seek a word from you about her son, for he is sick. Thus and so shall you speak to her. And when she comes, she will be pretending to be a stranger."

And it came to pass, when Ahijah heard the sound of her feet coming in at the door, that he said: "Come in, wife of Jeroboam. Why is this — you pretending to be a stranger? For I am sent to you with something hard.
Go, say to Jeroboam: Thus says YHWH, the God of Israel: Because I lifted you up from among the people and appointed you leader over my people Israel,

and tore the kingdom from the house of David and gave it to you — yet you have not been like my servant David, who kept my commandments and walked after me with all his heart, doing only what is right in my eyes,
but you have done more evil than all who were before you: you went and made yourself other gods, and cast images, to provoke me — and me you have thrown behind your back:

therefore — behold, I am bringing evil on the house of Jeroboam, and I will cut off from Jeroboam every last male, bond or free, in Israel; and I will burn away what is left of the house of Jeroboam as one burns away dung — until it is gone.
He of Jeroboam who dies in the city, the dogs shall eat; and he who dies in the field, the birds of the heavens shall eat — for YHWH has spoken.
And you — rise, go to your house. As your feet enter the city, the child will die.

And all Israel will lament for him and bury him — for he alone of Jeroboam's line shall come to a grave, because in him alone, of the house of Jeroboam, something good toward YHWH, the God of Israel, was found.
And YHWH will raise up for himself a king over Israel who will cut off the house of Jeroboam — this is the day. And what? — even now.

And YHWH will strike Israel as a reed sways in the water; and he will uproot Israel from this good soil that he gave to their fathers, and scatter them beyond the River — because they made their Asherah poles, provoking YHWH.
And he will give Israel up — on account of the sins of Jeroboam, which he sinned, and which he made Israel sin."
And Jeroboam's wife rose and went, and came to Tirzah. As she came to the threshold of the house — the boy died.
And they buried him, and all Israel lamented for him — according to the word of YHWH, which he spoke by the hand of his servant Ahijah the prophet.
And the rest of the acts of Jeroboam — how he fought and how he reigned — behold, they are written in the Scroll of the Days of the Kings of Israel.
And the days Jeroboam reigned were twenty-two years; and he lay down with his fathers — and Nadab his son reigned in his place.

And Rehoboam son of Solomon reigned in Judah. Rehoboam was forty-one years old when he became king, and seventeen years he reigned in Jerusalem — the city YHWH had chosen out of all the tribes of Israel, to set his name there. And his mother's name was Naamah the Ammonite.
And Judah did the evil in the eyes of YHWH; and by the sins they sinned they made him more jealous than all their fathers had done.
For they too built themselves high places and standing-stones and Asherah poles, on every high hill and under every leafy tree;
and there were even male cult prostitutes in the land. They acted according to all the abominations of the nations that YHWH had dispossessed before the children of Israel.
And it came to pass in the fifth year of King Rehoboam that Shishak king of Egypt came up against Jerusalem;
and he took the treasures of the house of YHWH and the treasures of the king's house — he took everything; and he took all the shields of gold that Solomon had made.
And King Rehoboam made in their place shields of bronze, and committed them into the hand of the commanders of the runners, who guarded the entrance of the king's house;
and whenever the king went into the house of YHWH, the runners would carry them — and return them to the runners' guardroom.
And the rest of the acts of Rehoboam, and all he did — are they not written in the Scroll of the Days of the Kings of Judah?
And there was war between Rehoboam and Jeroboam all the days.
And Rehoboam lay down with his fathers, and was buried with his fathers in the City of David. And his mother's name was Naamah the Ammonite. And Abijam his son reigned in his place.

1ki.15.text: And in the eighteenth year of King Jeroboam son of Nebat, Abijam became king over Judah.
Three years he reigned in Jerusalem; and his mother's name was Maacah daughter of Abishalom.
And he walked in all the sins of his father, which he had done before him; and his heart was not whole with YHWH his God, like the heart of David his father.
But for David's sake YHWH his God gave him a lamp in Jerusalem — raising up his son after him, and keeping Jerusalem standing;
because David did what was right in the eyes of YHWH, and did not turn aside from anything he commanded him all the days of his life — except in the matter of Uriah the Hittite.
And there was war between Rehoboam and Jeroboam all the days of his life.
And the rest of the acts of Abijam, and all he did — are they not written in the Scroll of the Days of the Kings of Judah? And there was war between Abijam and Jeroboam.
And Abijam lay down with his fathers, and they buried him in the City of David; and Asa his son reigned in his place.
And in the twentieth year of Jeroboam king of Israel, Asa became king of Judah;
and forty-one years he reigned in Jerusalem. And his grandmother's name was Maacah daughter of Abishalom.
And Asa did what was right in the eyes of YHWH, like David his father:
he expelled the male cult prostitutes from the land, and removed all the idols his fathers had made;
and even Maacah his grandmother — he removed her from being queen mother, because she had made a monstrous thing for Asherah; and Asa cut down her monstrous thing and burned it in the wadi Kidron.
But the high places were not removed. Only — the heart of Asa was whole with YHWH all his days.
And he brought into the house of YHWH the holy things of his father, and his own holy things: silver and gold and vessels.
And there was war between Asa and Baasha king of Israel all their days.
And Baasha king of Israel came up against Judah and built Ramah — to keep anyone from going out or coming in to Asa king of Judah.

And Asa took all the silver and the gold left in the treasuries of the house of YHWH and the treasuries of the king's house, and put them into the hand of his servants; and King Asa sent them to Ben-hadad son of Tabrimmon, son of Hezion, king of Aram, who lived in Damascus, saying:

"A covenant between me and you — between my father and your father! Behold, I have sent you a gift of silver and gold. Go — break your covenant with Baasha king of Israel, that he may go up away from me."

And Ben-hadad listened to King Asa, and sent the commanders of his forces against the cities of Israel; and he struck Ijon and Dan and Abel-beth-maacah, and all Chinneroth, together with all the land of Naphtali.
And it came to pass, when Baasha heard, that he stopped building Ramah — and stayed in Tirzah.

And King Asa summoned all Judah — no one exempt — and they carried off the stones of Ramah and its timber, with which Baasha had been building; and with them King Asa built Geba of Benjamin, and Mizpah.

And the rest of all the acts of Asa, and all his might, and all he did, and the cities he built — are they not written in the Scroll of the Days of the Kings of Judah? Only, in the time of his old age he was diseased in his feet.
And Asa lay down with his fathers, and was buried with his fathers in the city of David his father; and Jehoshaphat his son reigned in his place.
And Nadab son of Jeroboam became king over Israel in the second year of Asa king of Judah; and he reigned over Israel two years.
And he did the evil in the eyes of YHWH, and walked in the way of his father, and in his sin by which he made Israel sin.

And Baasha son of Ahijah, of the house of Issachar, conspired against him; and Baasha struck him down at Gibbethon, which belonged to the Philistines, while Nadab and all Israel were besieging Gibbethon.
And Baasha put him to death in the third year of Asa king of Judah — and reigned in his place.

And it came to pass, as soon as he became king, that he struck down the whole house of Jeroboam; he left to Jeroboam not a breathing soul, until he had destroyed him — according to the word of YHWH, which he spoke by the hand of his servant Ahijah the Shilonite —
for the sins of Jeroboam, which he sinned and which he made Israel sin — by his provocation with which he provoked YHWH, the God of Israel.
And the rest of the acts of Nadab, and all he did — are they not written in the Scroll of the Days of the Kings of Israel?
And there was war between Asa and Baasha king of Israel all their days.
In the third year of Asa king of Judah, Baasha son of Ahijah became king over all Israel, at Tirzah — twenty-four years.
And he did the evil in the eyes of YHWH, and walked in the way of Jeroboam, and in his sin by which he made Israel sin.

1ki.16.text: And the word of YHWH came to Jehu son of Hanani, against Baasha:
"Because I lifted you up from the dust and appointed you leader over my people Israel — and you have walked in the way of Jeroboam, and have made my people Israel sin, provoking me by their sins:
"Behold — I am sweeping away Baasha and his house; and I will make your house like the house of Jeroboam son of Nebat.
He of Baasha who dies in the city, the dogs shall eat; and he of his who dies in the field, the birds of the heavens shall eat."
And the rest of the acts of Baasha, and what he did, and his might — are they not written in the Scroll of the Days of the Kings of Israel?
And Baasha lay down with his fathers, and was buried at Tirzah; and Elah his son reigned in his place.

And moreover, by the hand of Jehu son of Hanani the prophet, the word of YHWH had come against Baasha and against his house — both for all the evil he did in the eyes of YHWH, provoking him with the work of his hands, becoming like the house of Jeroboam, and because he had struck it down.
In the twenty-sixth year of Asa king of Judah, Elah son of Baasha became king over Israel, at Tirzah — two years.
And his servant Zimri, commander of half the chariotry, conspired against him — while he was in Tirzah drinking himself drunk in the house of Arza, who was over the household at Tirzah.
And Zimri came in and struck him and put him to death, in the twenty-seventh year of Asa king of Judah — and reigned in his place.
And it came to pass, when he became king, as soon as he sat on his throne, that he struck down the whole house of Baasha; he left him no male — neither his kinsman-redeemers nor his friends.
So Zimri destroyed the whole house of Baasha — according to the word of YHWH that he spoke against Baasha by the hand of Jehu the prophet —
for all the sins of Baasha and the sins of Elah his son, which they sinned and which they made Israel sin, provoking YHWH, the God of Israel, with their empty idols.
And the rest of the acts of Elah, and all he did — are they not written in the Scroll of the Days of the Kings of Israel?
In the twenty-seventh year of Asa king of Judah, Zimri reigned seven days, at Tirzah — while the people were camped against Gibbethon, which belonged to the Philistines.
And the people in the camp heard it said: "Zimri has conspired, and has even struck down the king!" And all Israel made Omri, commander of the army, king over Israel that day, in the camp.
And Omri went up from Gibbethon, and all Israel with him — and they besieged Tirzah.
And it came to pass, when Zimri saw that the city was captured, that he went into the citadel of the king's house — and burned the king's house down over himself with fire, and died:
for his sins that he sinned, doing the evil in the eyes of YHWH — walking in the way of Jeroboam, and in his sin that he committed, making Israel sin.
And the rest of the acts of Zimri, and the conspiracy that he conspired — are they not written in the Scroll of the Days of the Kings of Israel?
Then the people Israel divided in half: half the people followed Tibni son of Ginath, to make him king, and half followed Omri.
But the people who followed Omri were stronger than the people who followed Tibni son of Ginath; and Tibni died — and Omri became king.
In the thirty-first year of Asa king of Judah, Omri became king over Israel — twelve years; at Tirzah he reigned six years.
And he bought the hill of Samaria from Shemer for two talents of silver; and he built up the hill, and called the name of the city he built Samaria — after the name of Shemer, the lord of the hill.
And Omri did the evil in the eyes of YHWH — and did worse than all who were before him;
and he walked in all the way of Jeroboam son of Nebat, and in his sins by which he made Israel sin — provoking YHWH, the God of Israel, with their empty idols.
And the rest of the acts of Omri that he did, and the might that he showed — are they not written in the Scroll of the Days of the Kings of Israel?
And Omri lay down with his fathers, and was buried in Samaria; and Ahab his son reigned in his place.
And Ahab son of Omri became king over Israel in the thirty-eighth year of Asa king of Judah; and Ahab son of Omri reigned over Israel, in Samaria, twenty-two years.
And Ahab son of Omri did the evil in the eyes of YHWH — more than all who were before him.

And it came to pass — as if walking in the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat were a light thing — that he took as a wife Jezebel daughter of Ethbaal, king of the Sidonians; and he went and served Baal, and bowed down to him.
And he raised an altar for Baal in the house of Baal, which he built in Samaria.
And Ahab made the Asherah pole; and Ahab went on doing more to provoke YHWH, the God of Israel, than all the kings of Israel who were before him.

In his days Hiel of Bethel built Jericho: at the cost of Abiram his firstborn he laid its foundation, and at the cost of Segub his youngest he set up its doors — according to the word of YHWH, which he spoke by the hand of Joshua son of Nun.

1ki.17.text: And Elijah the Tishbite, of the settlers of Gilead, said to Ahab: "As YHWH lives, the God of Israel, before whom I stand: there shall be neither dew nor rain these years — except by the mouth of my word."
And the word of YHWH came to him:
"Go from here, and turn eastward, and hide yourself by the wadi Cherith, which faces the Jordan.
And it shall be that you drink from the wadi; and I have commanded the ravens to sustain you there."
And he went and did according to the word of YHWH: he went and stayed by the wadi Cherith, which faces the Jordan.
And the ravens kept bringing him bread and meat in the morning, and bread and meat in the evening; and from the wadi he drank.
And it came to pass, at the end of some days, that the wadi dried up — for there was no rain in the land.
And the word of YHWH came to him:
"Rise, go to Zarephath, which belongs to Sidon, and stay there. Behold — I have commanded a widow woman there to sustain you."

And he rose and went to Zarephath, and came to the entrance of the city — and behold: a widow woman there, gathering sticks. And he called to her and said: "Fetch me, please, a little water in a vessel, that I may drink."
And she went to fetch it; and he called to her and said: "Bring me, please, a piece of bread in your hand."

And she said: "As YHWH your God lives — I have nothing baked; only a handful of flour in the jar, and a little oil in the flask. And behold, I am gathering two sticks, and I will go in and make it for me and my son — and we will eat it, and die."
And Elijah said to her: "Do not fear. Go in, do as you have said — only make me a little round cake from it first, and bring it out to me; and for yourself and your son make something afterward.
For thus says YHWH, the God of Israel: The jar of flour shall not run out, and the flask of oil shall not fail — until the day YHWH gives rain on the face of the ground."
And she went and did according to the word of Elijah; and she ate — she and he, and her household — for many days.
The jar of flour did not run out, and the flask of oil did not fail — according to the word of YHWH, which he spoke by the hand of Elijah.
And it came to pass, after these things, that the son of the woman, the mistress of the house, fell sick; and his sickness grew very severe — until no breath was left in him.
And she said to Elijah: "What is there between me and you, man of God? You have come to me to bring my guilt to remembrance — and to put my son to death!"
And he said to her: "Give me your son." And he took him from her lap and carried him up to the upper room where he was staying, and laid him on his own bed.
And he called to YHWH and said: "YHWH my God — have you brought evil even on the widow with whom I am sojourning, by putting her son to death?"
And he stretched himself out over the child three times, and called to YHWH and said: "YHWH my God — let the life of this child return, please, into him!"
And YHWH listened to the voice of Elijah; and the life of the child returned into him — and he lived.
And Elijah took the child and brought him down from the upper room into the house and gave him to his mother; and Elijah said: "See — your son lives."
And the woman said to Elijah: "Now — this I know: that you are a man of God, and the word of YHWH in your mouth is truth."

1ki.18.text: And it came to pass, after many days, that the word of YHWH came to Elijah in the third year: "Go, show yourself to Ahab — and I will give rain on the face of the ground."
And Elijah went to show himself to Ahab. Now the famine was severe in Samaria;
and Ahab called Obadiah, who was over the household. Now Obadiah greatly feared YHWH;
and it had come to pass, when Jezebel was cutting off the prophets of YHWH, that Obadiah took a hundred prophets and hid them, fifty men to a cave, and sustained them with bread and water.

And Ahab said to Obadiah: "Go through the land, to all the springs of water and to all the wadis. Perhaps we may find grass, and keep horse and mule alive, and not have to cut off some of the livestock."
And they divided the land between them, to pass through it: Ahab went one way by himself, and Obadiah went another way by himself.
And as Obadiah was on the road — behold: Elijah, coming to meet him. And he recognized him and fell on his face and said: "Is it you — my lord Elijah?"
And he said to him: "It is I. Go, say to your lord: 'Behold — Elijah.'"
And he said: "How have I sinned, that you are giving your servant into the hand of Ahab, to put me to death?

As YHWH your God lives — there is no nation or kingdom where my lord has not sent to seek you; and when they said, 'He is not here,' he made the kingdom and the nation swear that they could not find you.
And now you say: 'Go, say to your lord: Behold — Elijah'!

And it will come to pass, when I go from you, that the Spirit of YHWH will carry you off to I know not where; and I will come to tell Ahab, and he will not find you — and he will kill me. And your servant has feared YHWH from my youth.

Has it not been told to my lord what I did when Jezebel killed the prophets of YHWH — how I hid a hundred men of the prophets of YHWH, fifty and fifty to a cave, and sustained them with bread and water?
And now you say: 'Go, say to your lord: Behold — Elijah.' And he will kill me!"
And Elijah said: "As YHWH of Armies lives, before whom I stand — today I will show myself to him."
And Obadiah went to meet Ahab and told him; and Ahab went to meet Elijah.
And it came to pass, when Ahab saw Elijah, that Ahab said to him: "Is that you — you troubler of Israel?"
And he said: "I have not troubled Israel — but you, and your father's house: by your abandoning the commandments of YHWH, and by your going after the Baals.
So now — send, gather all Israel to me, to Mount Carmel; and the prophets of Baal, four hundred and fifty, and the prophets of Asherah, four hundred — the eaters at Jezebel's table."
And Ahab sent through all the children of Israel, and gathered the prophets to Mount Carmel.

And Elijah drew near to all the people and said: "How long will you keep hopping between the two branches? If YHWH is God — go after him; and if Baal — go after him." And the people answered him not a word.
And Elijah said to the people: "I alone am left a prophet of YHWH — and the prophets of Baal are four hundred and fifty men.

Let them give us two bulls; and let them choose one bull for themselves and cut it in pieces and lay it on the wood — but set no fire; and I will prepare the other bull and set it on the wood — and set no fire.
Then call on the name of your god, and I will call on the name of YHWH; and the god who answers with fire — he is God." And all the people answered and said: "The word is good."
And Elijah said to the prophets of Baal: "Choose one bull for yourselves and prepare it first — for you are the many; and call on the name of your god — but set no fire."

And they took the bull that was given them and prepared it, and called on the name of Baal from the morning until noon: "Baal — answer us!" But there was no voice, and no one answering. And they hopped about the altar that had been made.

And it came to pass at noon that Elijah mocked them and said: "Call with a great voice — for he is a god! Perhaps he is musing, or has stepped aside, or is on a journey. Perhaps he is asleep — and must be woken!"
And they called with a great voice, and gashed themselves, as was their custom, with swords and with spears — until blood poured out over them.
And it came to pass, as noon passed, that they raved on until the offering-up of the evening offering — and there was no voice, and no one answering, and no one paying attention.
And Elijah said to all the people: "Draw near to me." And all the people drew near to him; and he repaired the altar of YHWH that had been torn down.
And Elijah took twelve stones, according to the number of the tribes of the sons of Jacob — to whom the word of YHWH had come: "Israel shall be your name" —
and with the stones he built an altar in the name of YHWH; and he made a trench around the altar, big enough to hold two seahs of seed.
And he arranged the wood, and cut the bull in pieces and laid it on the wood.

And he said: "Fill four jars with water, and pour it on the burnt offering and on the wood." And he said: "Do it a second time" — and they did it a second time. And he said: "Do it a third time" — and they did it a third time;
and the water ran all around the altar — and the trench, too, he filled with water.

And it came to pass, at the offering-up of the evening offering, that Elijah the prophet drew near and said: "YHWH, God of Abraham, Isaac, and Israel — today let it be known that you are God in Israel, and that I am your servant, and that by your word I have done all these things.
Answer me, YHWH — answer me! — that this people may know that you, YHWH, are God; and that you yourself have turned their heart back again."
And the fire of YHWH fell, and devoured the burnt offering and the wood and the stones and the dust — and the water in the trench it licked up.
And all the people saw, and fell on their faces, and said: "YHWH — he is God! YHWH — he is God!"
And Elijah said to them: "Seize the prophets of Baal! Let not a man of them escape!" And they seized them; and Elijah brought them down to the wadi Kishon — and slaughtered them there.
And Elijah said to Ahab: "Go up — eat and drink; for there is the sound of the roar of rain."
And Ahab went up to eat and to drink; and Elijah went up to the top of Carmel, and crouched to the ground, and put his face between his knees.
And he said to his servant: "Go up, please — look toward the sea." And he went up and looked, and said: "There is nothing." And he said: "Go back" — seven times.

And it came to pass at the seventh time that he said: "Behold — a little cloud, like a man's palm, rising from the sea." And he said: "Go up, say to Ahab: 'Harness and go down — that the rain not stop you.'"
And it came to pass, in a little while, that the heavens grew black with clouds and wind, and there was a great rain; and Ahab rode off and went to Jezreel.
And the hand of YHWH came on Elijah; and he belted up his waist and ran before Ahab — all the way to the approach of Jezreel.

1ki.19.text: And Ahab told Jezebel all that Elijah had done — and all about how he had killed all the prophets with the sword.
And Jezebel sent a messenger to Elijah: "So may the gods do, and so may they add — if by this time tomorrow I have not made your life like the life of one of them!"
And he saw, and rose, and went for his life; and he came to Beersheba, which belongs to Judah, and left his servant there.

And he himself went a day's journey into the wilderness; and he came and sat under a single broom tree. And he asked for his life to die, and said: "Enough! Now, YHWH — take my life; for I am no better than my fathers."
And he lay down and slept under the one broom tree — and behold: an angel touching him; and he said to him: "Rise — eat."
And he looked — and behold: at his head, a round cake baked on hot stones, and a flask of water. And he ate and drank, and lay down again.
And the angel of YHWH came back a second time and touched him and said: "Rise — eat; for the journey is too much for you."
And he rose and ate and drank; and he walked, in the strength of that food, forty days and forty nights — to the mountain of God, Horeb.
And he came there to the cave, and spent the night there. And behold — the word of YHWH came to him, and said to him: "What are you doing here, Elijah?"

And he said: "I have been zealous — zealous for YHWH, the God of Armies; for the children of Israel have abandoned your covenant, torn down your altars, and killed your prophets with the sword. And I am left — I alone; and they seek my life, to take it."

And he said: "Go out and stand on the mountain before YHWH." And behold — YHWH passing by: and a great and strong wind, tearing apart mountains and shattering crags before YHWH — YHWH was not in the wind. And after the wind, a quaking — YHWH was not in the quaking.
And after the quaking, fire — YHWH was not in the fire. And after the fire — a voice of thin stillness.

And it came to pass, when Elijah heard it, that he wrapped his face in his cloak, and went out and stood at the opening of the cave. And behold — a voice to him, and it said: "What are you doing here, Elijah?"

And he said: "I have been zealous — zealous for YHWH, the God of Armies; for the children of Israel have abandoned your covenant, torn down your altars, and killed your prophets with the sword. And I am left — I alone; and they seek my life, to take it."
And YHWH said to him: "Go — return on your way, to the wilderness of Damascus; and when you arrive, anoint Hazael as king over Aram.
And Jehu son of Nimshi you shall anoint as king over Israel; and Elisha son of Shaphat, from Abel-meholah, you shall anoint as prophet in your place.
And it shall be: whoever escapes the sword of Hazael, Jehu will put to death; and whoever escapes the sword of Jehu, Elisha will put to death.
But I will leave in Israel seven thousand — all the knees that have not bowed to Baal, and every mouth that has not kissed him."
And he went from there and found Elisha son of Shaphat — he plowing, with twelve yoke of oxen before him, and he with the twelfth. And Elijah crossed over to him and threw his cloak onto him.
And he left the oxen and ran after Elijah, and said: "Let me kiss my father and my mother, please — and I will follow you." And he said to him: "Go, turn back — for what have I done to you?"

And he turned back from following him, and took the yoke of oxen and sacrificed them; and with the oxen's gear he boiled their flesh, and gave it to the people, and they ate. Then he rose and followed Elijah — and served him.

1ki.20.text: And Ben-hadad king of Aram gathered all his forces — thirty-two kings with him, and horses and chariots; and he went up and besieged Samaria, and fought against it.
And he sent messengers to Ahab king of Israel, into the city,
and said to him: "Thus says Ben-hadad: Your silver and your gold — they are mine; and your wives and your best sons — they are mine."
And the king of Israel answered and said: "According to your word, my lord the king: I am yours — and all that I have."
And the messengers came back and said: "Thus speaks Ben-hadad: Indeed I sent to you saying, 'Your silver and your gold and your wives and your sons you shall give me' —

but this time tomorrow I will send my servants to you, and they shall search your house and the houses of your servants; and it shall be: everything desirable to your eyes they shall put in their hand and take."

And the king of Israel called all the elders of the land and said: "Know, please, and see, that this man is seeking evil; for he sent to me for my wives and my sons and my silver and my gold — and I did not withhold them from him."
And all the elders and all the people said to him: "Do not listen — and do not consent."

And he said to the messengers of Ben-hadad: "Say to my lord the king: All that you first sent to your servant, I will do; but this thing I cannot do." And the messengers went, and brought back word to him.
And Ben-hadad sent to him and said: "So may the gods do to me, and so may they add — if the dust of Samaria will suffice for handfuls for all the people at my feet!"
And the king of Israel answered and said: "Speak this: Let not the one girding on his armor boast like the one taking it off."
And it came to pass, when he heard this word — he was drinking, he and the kings, in the field-shelters — that he said to his servants: "Take positions!" And they took positions against the city.

And behold — one prophet drew near to Ahab king of Israel and said: "Thus says YHWH: Have you seen all this great multitude? Behold — I am giving it into your hand today; and you shall know that I am YHWH."
And Ahab said: "By whom?" And he said: "Thus says YHWH: By the young men of the district commanders." And he said: "Who shall open the battle?" And he said: "You."
And he mustered the young men of the district commanders, and they were two hundred and thirty-two; and after them he mustered all the people — all the children of Israel: seven thousand.
And they went out at noon, while Ben-hadad was drinking himself drunk in the field-shelters — he and the kings, the thirty-two kings helping him.
And the young men of the district commanders went out first; and Ben-hadad sent out scouts, and they told him: "Men have come out of Samaria."
And he said: "If they have come out for peace — seize them alive; and if they have come out for battle — alive seize them."
So these went out of the city — the young men of the district commanders, and the army behind them;
and each struck down his man. And Aram fled, and Israel pursued them; and Ben-hadad king of Aram escaped on a horse, with horsemen.
And the king of Israel went out and struck the horses and the chariots — and struck Aram a great blow.
And the prophet drew near to the king of Israel and said to him: "Go, strengthen yourself; and know and see what you must do — for at the turn of the year the king of Aram is coming up against you."

And the servants of the king of Aram said to him: "Their gods are gods of the mountains — that is why they were stronger than we. But let us fight them on the level ground — surely we shall be stronger than they.
And do this thing: remove the kings, each from his post, and set governors in their place;

and muster for yourself an army like the army you lost — horse for horse and chariot for chariot — and let us fight them on the level ground; surely we shall be stronger than they." And he listened to their voice, and did so.
And it came to pass at the turn of the year that Ben-hadad mustered Aram and went up to Aphek, for battle with Israel.
And the children of Israel were mustered and provisioned, and went to meet them; and the children of Israel camped opposite them like two little flocks of goats — while Aram filled the land.

And the man of God drew near and spoke to the king of Israel and said: "Thus says YHWH: Because Aram has said, 'YHWH is a god of mountains, and not a god of valleys' — I will give all this great multitude into your hand; and you shall know that I am YHWH."

And they camped opposite each other seven days. And it came to pass on the seventh day that the battle was joined; and the children of Israel struck down of Aram a hundred thousand foot soldiers in one day;

and those who were left fled to Aphek, into the city — and the wall fell on twenty-seven thousand of the men who were left. And Ben-hadad fled and came into the city, into an inner room within a room.

And his servants said to him: "Behold now — we have heard that the kings of the house of Israel are kings of steadfast love. Let us put, please, sackcloth on our waists and ropes on our heads, and go out to the king of Israel. Perhaps he will let your life live."

So they girded sackcloth on their waists and ropes on their heads, and came to the king of Israel and said: "Your servant Ben-hadad says: 'Let my life live, please.'" And he said: "Is he still alive? He is my brother."

Now the men were watching for an omen, and they quickly seized on it from him, and said: "Your brother — Ben-hadad!" And he said: "Go, fetch him." And Ben-hadad came out to him — and he brought him up onto the chariot.

And he said to him: "The cities my father took from your father I will return; and you may set up market streets for yourself in Damascus, as my father set up in Samaria." "And I — with a covenant I will send you away." So he cut a covenant with him, and sent him away.
And a certain man of the sons of the prophets said to his fellow, by the word of YHWH: "Strike me, please." But the man refused to strike him.
And he said to him: "Because you have not listened to the voice of YHWH — behold, as you go from me, the lion will strike you." And he went from beside him, and the lion found him — and struck him.
And he found another man and said: "Strike me, please." And the man struck him — striking and wounding.
And the prophet went and stood waiting for the king by the road, disguised with a bandage over his eyes.

And it came to pass, as the king passed by, that he cried out to the king and said: "Your servant went out into the middle of the battle — and behold, a man turned aside and brought me a man and said: 'Guard this man. If he is missing — found missing — then your life shall be for his life, or you shall weigh out a talent of silver.'
And as your servant was busy here and there — he was gone." And the king of Israel said to him: "So is your judgment; you yourself have decided it."
And he quickly removed the bandage from his eyes; and the king of Israel recognized him — that he was one of the prophets.
And he said to him: "Thus says YHWH: Because you have let go out of your hand the man I devoted to destruction — your life shall be for his life, and your people for his people."
And the king of Israel went to his house sullen and raging — and came to Samaria.

1ki.21.text: And it came to pass after these things: Naboth the Jezreelite had a vineyard in Jezreel, beside the palace of Ahab, king of Samaria.

And Ahab spoke to Naboth: "Give me your vineyard, and let it be my vegetable garden — for it is close beside my house; and I will give you in its place a better vineyard than it. Or if it is good in your eyes, I will give you silver — its price."
And Naboth said to Ahab: "Far be it from me, by YHWH, to give you the inheritance of my fathers!"

And Ahab came into his house sullen and raging over the word Naboth the Jezreelite had spoken to him — "I will not give you the inheritance of my fathers"; and he lay down on his bed and turned his face away, and would not eat food.
And Jezebel his wife came to him and spoke to him: "What is this — your spirit so sullen, and you not eating food?"

And he spoke to her: "Because I spoke to Naboth the Jezreelite and said to him: 'Give me your vineyard for silver — or, if you prefer, I will give you a vineyard in its place'; and he said: 'I will not give you my vineyard.'"
And Jezebel his wife said to him: "Is it you who now exercise kingship over Israel? Rise, eat food, and let your heart be glad. I myself will give you the vineyard of Naboth the Jezreelite."
And she wrote letters in Ahab's name, and sealed them with his seal, and sent the letters to the elders and the nobles who were in his city, who lived with Naboth.
And she wrote in the letters: "Proclaim a fast — and seat Naboth at the head of the people;
and seat two men, sons of worthlessness, opposite him, and let them testify against him: 'You cursed God and king!' Then take him out and stone him — and let him die."
And the men of his city — the elders and the nobles who lived in his city — did as Jezebel had sent to them, as was written in the letters she sent them:
they proclaimed a fast, and seated Naboth at the head of the people;

and the two men — sons of worthlessness — came and sat opposite him; and the men of worthlessness testified against him, against Naboth, in front of the people: "Naboth cursed God and king!" And they took him outside the city and stoned him with stones — and he died.
And they sent to Jezebel: "Naboth has been stoned — and is dead."

And it came to pass, when Jezebel heard that Naboth had been stoned and was dead, that Jezebel said to Ahab: "Rise — take possession of the vineyard of Naboth the Jezreelite, which he refused to give you for silver; for Naboth is not alive, but dead."
And it came to pass, when Ahab heard that Naboth was dead, that Ahab rose to go down to the vineyard of Naboth the Jezreelite — to take possession of it.
And the word of YHWH came to Elijah the Tishbite:
"Rise, go down to meet Ahab king of Israel, who is in Samaria. Behold — he is in the vineyard of Naboth, where he has gone down to take possession of it.

And speak to him: Thus says YHWH: Have you murdered — and also taken possession? And speak to him: Thus says YHWH: In the place where the dogs licked up the blood of Naboth, the dogs shall lick up your blood — yours also."
And Ahab said to Elijah: "So you have found me, my enemy!" And he said: "I have found you — because you have sold yourself to do the evil in the eyes of YHWH.
Behold — I am bringing evil on you, and I will sweep away what comes after you; and I will cut off from Ahab every male, bond or free, in Israel;
and I will make your house like the house of Jeroboam son of Nebat, and like the house of Baasha son of Ahijah — for the provocation with which you provoked me, and because you made Israel sin.
And of Jezebel also YHWH has spoken: The dogs shall eat Jezebel by the rampart of Jezreel.
He of Ahab who dies in the city, the dogs shall eat; and he who dies in the field, the birds of the heavens shall eat."
(Truly there was never anyone like Ahab, who sold himself to do the evil in the eyes of YHWH — whom Jezebel his wife incited;
and he acted most abominably in going after the idols, according to all that the Amorites had done, whom YHWH dispossessed before the children of Israel.)
And it came to pass, when Ahab heard these words, that he tore his garments and put sackcloth on his flesh, and fasted; and he lay in the sackcloth, and walked about softly.
And the word of YHWH came to Elijah the Tishbite:
"Have you seen how Ahab has humbled himself before me? Because he has humbled himself before me, I will not bring the evil in his days; in the days of his son I will bring the evil on his house."

1ki.22.text: And they stayed quiet three years — no war between Aram and Israel.
And it came to pass in the third year that Jehoshaphat king of Judah came down to the king of Israel.
And the king of Israel said to his servants: "Do you know that Ramoth-gilead is ours — and we sit doing nothing about taking it out of the hand of the king of Aram?"
And he said to Jehoshaphat: "Will you go with me to battle at Ramoth-gilead?" And Jehoshaphat said to the king of Israel: "I am as you are — my people as your people, my horses as your horses."
And Jehoshaphat said to the king of Israel: "Seek first, please, this day, the word of YHWH."

And the king of Israel gathered the prophets — about four hundred men — and said to them: "Shall I go against Ramoth-gilead to battle, or shall I hold back?" And they said: "Go up — and the Lord will give it into the hand of the king."
And Jehoshaphat said: "Is there not here still a prophet of YHWH, that we might seek from him?"

And the king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat: "There is still one man through whom to seek YHWH — but I hate him, for he does not prophesy good about me, only evil: Micaiah son of Imlah." And Jehoshaphat said: "Let not the king say so."
And the king of Israel called a certain officer and said: "Quickly — Micaiah son of Imlah."

And the king of Israel and Jehoshaphat king of Judah were sitting, each on his throne, dressed in robes, at the threshing floor at the entrance of the gate of Samaria — and all the prophets prophesying before them.
And Zedekiah son of Chenaanah made himself horns of iron, and said: "Thus says YHWH: With these you shall gore Aram — until they are finished."
And all the prophets were prophesying the same: "Go up to Ramoth-gilead and succeed — and YHWH will give it into the hand of the king."

And the messenger who went to call Micaiah spoke to him: "Behold now — the words of the prophets, with one mouth, are good toward the king. Let your word, please, be like the word of one of them — and speak good."
And Micaiah said: "As YHWH lives — what YHWH says to me, that I will speak."

And he came to the king; and the king said to him: "Micaiah — shall we go against Ramoth-gilead to battle, or shall we hold back?" And he said to him: "Go up and succeed — and YHWH will give it into the hand of the king."
And the king said to him: "How many times must I make you swear that you will speak to me nothing but truth, in the name of YHWH?"
And he said: "I saw all Israel scattered on the mountains, like sheep that have no shepherd. And YHWH said: 'These have no masters. Let them return, each man to his house, in peace.'"
And the king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat: "Did I not say to you: he will not prophesy good about me — only evil?"
And he said: "Therefore — hear the word of YHWH: I saw YHWH sitting on his throne, and all the host of the heavens standing by him, on his right and on his left.
And YHWH said: 'Who will lure Ahab, so that he goes up and falls at Ramoth-gilead?' And this one said one thing, and that one said another.
And the spirit came out and stood before YHWH and said: 'I will lure him.' And YHWH said to him: 'By what means?'
And he said: 'I will go out and be a lying spirit in the mouth of all his prophets.' And he said: 'You shall lure him — and you shall also prevail. Go out and do so.'
So now — behold: YHWH has put a lying spirit in the mouth of all these your prophets; and YHWH has spoken evil concerning you."
And Zedekiah son of Chenaanah drew near and struck Micaiah on the cheek, and said: "Which way did the Spirit of YHWH pass from me, to speak with you?"
And Micaiah said: "Behold — you shall see, on that day when you go into an inner room within a room to hide yourself."
And the king of Israel said: "Take Micaiah and return him to Amon the governor of the city, and to Joash the king's son;
and say: Thus says the king: Put this man in the prison house, and feed him bread of scarcity and water of scarcity — until I come in peace."
And Micaiah said: "If you indeed return in peace — YHWH has not spoken by me." And he said: "Hear, peoples — all of you!"
And the king of Israel and Jehoshaphat king of Judah went up to Ramoth-gilead.
And the king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat: "I will disguise myself and go into the battle — but you, wear your own robes." And the king of Israel disguised himself and went into the battle.
Now the king of Aram had commanded his thirty-two chariot commanders: "Fight with neither small nor great — only with the king of Israel alone."
And it came to pass, when the chariot commanders saw Jehoshaphat, that they said: "Surely he is the king of Israel!" And they turned against him to fight — and Jehoshaphat cried out.
And it came to pass, when the chariot commanders saw that he was not the king of Israel, that they turned back from following him.

And a man drew his bow in his innocence — and struck the king of Israel between the joints and the armor. And he said to his charioteer: "Turn your hand and take me out of the camp — for I am wounded."
And the battle mounted that day; and the king was propped up in the chariot, facing Aram — and he died in the evening; and the blood of the wound ran out into the hollow of the chariot.
And the shout passed through the camp at the going down of the sun: "Each man to his city, and each man to his land!"
So the king died, and came to Samaria; and they buried the king in Samaria.
And they washed out the chariot at the pool of Samaria; and the dogs licked up his blood — where the prostitutes bathed — according to the word of YHWH that he had spoken.
And the rest of the acts of Ahab, and all he did, and the ivory house he built, and all the cities he built — are they not written in the Scroll of the Days of the Kings of Israel?
And Ahab lay down with his fathers; and Ahaziah his son reigned in his place.
And Jehoshaphat son of Asa became king over Judah, in the fourth year of Ahab king of Israel.
Jehoshaphat was thirty-five years old when he became king, and twenty-five years he reigned in Jerusalem; and his mother's name was Azubah daughter of Shilhi.
And he walked in all the way of Asa his father — he did not turn aside from it — doing what was right in the eyes of YHWH.
Only the high places were not removed; the people were still sacrificing and burning incense on the high places.
And Jehoshaphat made peace with the king of Israel.
And the rest of the acts of Jehoshaphat, and the might he showed, and how he fought — are they not written in the Scroll of the Days of the Kings of Judah?
And the remnant of the male cult prostitutes, who were left in the days of Asa his father, he swept out of the land.
And there was no king in Edom; a deputy was king.
Jehoshaphat made ships of Tarshish to go to Ophir for gold — but they did not go, for the ships were wrecked at Ezion-geber.
Then Ahaziah son of Ahab said to Jehoshaphat: "Let my servants go with your servants in the ships." But Jehoshaphat was not willing.
And Jehoshaphat lay down with his fathers, and was buried with his fathers in the city of David his father; and Jehoram his son reigned in his place.
Ahaziah son of Ahab became king over Israel, in Samaria, in the seventeenth year of Jehoshaphat king of Judah; and he reigned over Israel two years.
And he did the evil in the eyes of YHWH, and walked in the way of his father and in the way of his mother, and in the way of Jeroboam son of Nebat, who made Israel sin;
and he served Baal and bowed down to him, and provoked YHWH, the God of Israel — according to all that his father had done.

2ki.1.text: And Moab rebelled against Israel after the death of Ahab.

And Ahaziah fell through the lattice of his upper room in Samaria, and was injured; and he sent messengers and said to them: "Go, inquire of Baal-zebub, the god of Ekron, whether I shall recover from this injury."

But the angel of YHWH spoke to Elijah the Tishbite: "Rise, go up to meet the messengers of the king of Samaria, and speak to them: Is it because there is no God in Israel that you are going to inquire of Baal-zebub, the god of Ekron?
Therefore thus says YHWH: The bed you have climbed onto — you shall not come down from it; for you shall surely die." And Elijah went.
And the messengers returned to him; and he said to them: "Why is this — you have returned?"

And they said to him: "A man came up to meet us and said to us: 'Go, return to the king who sent you, and speak to him: Thus says YHWH: Is it because there is no God in Israel that you are sending to inquire of Baal-zebub, the god of Ekron? Therefore the bed you have climbed onto — you shall not come down from it; for you shall surely die.'"
And he spoke to them: "What was the manner of the man who came up to meet you and spoke these words to you?"
And they said to him: "A man — an owner of hair, with a leather belt girded on his waist." And he said: "It is Elijah the Tishbite."
And he sent to him a commander of fifty with his fifty; and he went up to him — and behold, he was sitting on the top of the hill — and he spoke to him: "Man of God — the king has spoken: Come down!"

And Elijah answered and spoke to the commander of the fifty: "And if I am a man of God — let fire come down from the heavens and devour you and your fifty!" And fire came down from the heavens and devoured him and his fifty.
And again he sent to him another commander of fifty with his fifty; and he answered and spoke to him: "Man of God — thus says the king: Quickly, come down!"

And Elijah answered and spoke to them: "If I am a man of God — let fire come down from the heavens and devour you and your fifty!" And the fire of God came down from the heavens and devoured him and his fifty.

And again he sent a third commander of fifty with his fifty. And the third commander of fifty went up and came and bowed on his knees in front of Elijah, and pleaded with him and spoke to him: "Man of God — let my life, please, and the life of these your fifty servants, be precious in your eyes.
Behold — fire came down from the heavens and devoured the two former commanders of fifty and their fifties; but now — let my life be precious in your eyes."
And the angel of YHWH spoke to Elijah: "Go down with him. Do not fear before him." And he rose and went down with him to the king.

And he spoke to him: "Thus says YHWH: Because you sent messengers to inquire of Baal-zebub, the god of Ekron — is it because there is no God in Israel to inquire of his word? — therefore the bed you have climbed onto — you shall not come down from it; for you shall surely die."
And he died, according to the word of YHWH that Elijah had spoken. And Jehoram became king in his place, in the second year of Jehoram son of Jehoshaphat, king of Judah — for he had no son.
And the rest of the acts of Ahaziah, which he did — are they not written in the Scroll of the Days of the Kings of Israel?

2ki.2.text: And it came to pass, when YHWH was about to bring Elijah up to the heavens in the storm-wind, that Elijah and Elisha went from Gilgal.
And Elijah said to Elisha: "Stay here, please — for YHWH has sent me as far as Bethel." And Elisha said: "As YHWH lives, and as your soul lives — I will not leave you." And they went down to Bethel.
And the sons of the prophets who were at Bethel came out to Elisha and said to him: "Do you know that today YHWH is taking your lord from over your head?" And he said: "I too know. Be silent."
And Elijah said to him: "Elisha — stay here, please, for YHWH has sent me to Jericho." And he said: "As YHWH lives, and as your soul lives — I will not leave you." And they came to Jericho.
And the sons of the prophets who were at Jericho drew near to Elisha and said to him: "Do you know that today YHWH is taking your lord from over your head?" And he said: "I too know. Be silent."
And Elijah said to him: "Stay here, please — for YHWH has sent me to the Jordan." And he said: "As YHWH lives, and as your soul lives — I will not leave you." And the two of them went on.
And fifty men of the sons of the prophets went and stood opposite, at a distance; and the two of them stood by the Jordan.
And Elijah took his cloak and rolled it up and struck the water; and it divided this way and that — and the two of them crossed over on dry ground.

And it came to pass, as they crossed, that Elijah said to Elisha: "Ask — what shall I do for you before I am taken from you?" And Elisha said: "Let a double portion of your spirit, please, be upon me."
And he said: "You have asked a hard thing. If you see me being taken from you — it shall be so for you; and if not — it shall not be."

And it came to pass, as they were walking along, walking and talking — behold: a chariot of fire and horses of fire; and they separated between the two of them — and Elijah went up in the storm-wind to the heavens.

And Elisha was watching, and he was crying out: "My father! My father! The chariot of Israel and its horsemen!" And he saw him no more. And he took hold of his own garments and tore them into two pieces.
And he picked up the cloak of Elijah, which had fallen from him, and went back and stood on the bank of the Jordan;

and he took the cloak of Elijah, which had fallen from him, and struck the water, and said: "Where is YHWH, the God of Elijah — even he?" And he struck the water, and it divided this way and that — and Elisha crossed over.
And the sons of the prophets who were at Jericho, opposite, saw him and said: "The spirit of Elijah has settled on Elisha!" And they came to meet him, and bowed down to him to the ground.

And they said to him: "Behold now — there are with your servants fifty men, sons of valor. Let them go, please, and seek your lord; lest the Spirit of YHWH has lifted him and thrown him onto one of the mountains, or into one of the valleys." And he said: "You shall not send."
But they pressed him until he was ashamed, and he said: "Send." And they sent fifty men, and they searched three days — and did not find him.
And they returned to him — he was staying at Jericho — and he said to them: "Did I not say to you: Do not go?"
And the men of the city said to Elisha: "Behold now — the site of the city is good, as my lord sees; but the water is bad, and the land bereaves."
And he said: "Fetch me a new bowl, and put salt in it." And they fetched it to him.
And he went out to the spring of the water and threw salt in there, and said: "Thus says YHWH: I have healed this water; there shall no longer be death and bereaving from there."
And the water has been healed to this day — according to the word of Elisha that he spoke.
And he went up from there to Bethel; and as he was going up on the road, small boys came out of the city and jeered at him, and said to him: "Go up, baldhead! Go up, baldhead!"
And he turned around and saw them, and cursed them in the name of YHWH; and two she-bears came out of the forest — and tore open forty-two of the children.
And he went from there to Mount Carmel; and from there he returned to Samaria.

2ki.3.text: And Jehoram son of Ahab became king over Israel, in Samaria, in the eighteenth year of Jehoshaphat king of Judah; and he reigned twelve years.
And he did the evil in the eyes of YHWH — only not like his father and his mother; for he removed the pillar of Baal that his father had made.
Only — he clung to the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, which he made Israel sin; he did not turn from them.
Now Mesha king of Moab was a sheep-breeder; and he would render to the king of Israel a hundred thousand lambs, and a hundred thousand rams' worth of wool.
And it came to pass, when Ahab died, that the king of Moab rebelled against the king of Israel.
And King Jehoram went out that day from Samaria, and mustered all Israel;

and he went and sent to Jehoshaphat king of Judah: "The king of Moab has rebelled against me. Will you go with me against Moab, to battle?" And he said: "I will go up. I am as you are — my people as your people, my horses as your horses."
And he said: "By which way shall we go up?" And he said: "By the way of the wilderness of Edom."
So the king of Israel went, and the king of Judah, and the king of Edom; and they made a circuit of seven days' journey — and there was no water for the camp or for the animals at their feet.
And the king of Israel said: "Alas! For YHWH has called these three kings to give them into the hand of Moab!"

And Jehoshaphat said: "Is there not here a prophet of YHWH, that we may seek YHWH from him?" And one of the servants of the king of Israel answered and said: "Here is Elisha son of Shaphat, who poured water on the hands of Elijah."
And Jehoshaphat said: "The word of YHWH is with him." And the king of Israel and Jehoshaphat and the king of Edom went down to him.

And Elisha said to the king of Israel: "What is there between me and you? Go to the prophets of your father and the prophets of your mother!" And the king of Israel said to him: "No — for YHWH has called these three kings to give them into the hand of Moab."
And Elisha said: "As YHWH of Armies lives, before whom I stand: were it not that I regard the face of Jehoshaphat king of Judah, I would not look at you or see you.
But now — fetch me a musician." And it came to pass, as the musician played, that the hand of YHWH came upon him;
and he said: "Thus says YHWH: Make this wadi full of ditches — ditches upon ditches.
