For thus says YHWH: You shall not see wind, and you shall not see rain; yet that wadi shall be filled with water — and you shall drink, you and your herds and your animals.
And this is a light thing in the eyes of YHWH: he will also give Moab into your hand;
and you shall strike every fortified city and every choice city, and fell every good tree, and stop up all the springs of water, and ruin every good plot of land with stones."
And it came to pass in the morning, at the offering-up of the offering — behold: water coming from the direction of Edom; and the land was filled with the water.
And all Moab had heard that the kings had come up to fight against them; and they were called out — everyone able to gird on a belt and upward — and they stood at the border.
And they rose early in the morning, and the sun shone on the water; and Moab saw the water opposite them red as blood,
and they said: "This is blood! The kings have surely fought each other, and each has struck down his fellow. So now — to the plunder, Moab!"
And they came to the camp of Israel; and Israel rose and struck Moab, and they fled before them; and they came on into the land, striking Moab.

And the cities they tore down; and on every good plot of land each man threw his stone until they had filled it; and every spring of water they stopped up, and every good tree they felled — until only the stones of Kir-hareseth were left standing; and the slingers surrounded it and struck it.
And the king of Moab saw that the battle was too strong for him; and he took with him seven hundred men who drew the sword, to break through to the king of Edom — but they could not.

And he took his firstborn son, who was to reign in his place, and offered him up as a burnt offering on the wall. And great wrath came upon Israel; and they pulled away from him and returned to their land.

2ki.4.text: And a certain woman, of the wives of the sons of the prophets, cried out to Elisha: "Your servant my husband is dead; and you know that your servant feared YHWH. And the creditor has come to take my two children for himself as slaves!"
And Elisha said to her: "What shall I do for you? Tell me — what do you have in the house?" And she said: "Your maidservant has nothing at all in the house — except a jar of oil."
And he said: "Go, borrow vessels for yourself from outside, from all your neighbors — empty vessels; do not get few.
Then go in and shut the door behind you and behind your sons, and pour into all these vessels — and set aside each one that is full."
And she went from him and shut the door behind her and behind her sons: they kept bringing vessels to her, and she kept pouring.
And it came to pass, when the vessels were full, that she said to her son: "Bring me one more vessel." And he said to her: "There is no other vessel." And the oil stopped.
And she came and told the man of God; and he said: "Go, sell the oil and pay your debt — and you and your sons live on what is left."

And it came to pass one day that Elisha passed through to Shunem; and there was a prominent woman there, and she pressed him to eat food. And it came to pass, as often as he passed through, he would turn aside there to eat food.
And she said to her husband: "Behold now — I know that he is a holy man of God, who passes by us continually.
Let us make, please, a little walled upper room, and put there for him a bed and a table and a chair and a lampstand; and it shall be, when he comes to us, he can turn aside there."
And it came to pass one day that he came there; and he turned aside to the upper room and lay down there.
And he said to Gehazi his servant: "Call this Shunammite." And he called her, and she stood before him.

And he said to him: "Say to her, please: Behold — you have gone to all this trouble for us. What can be done for you? Is there something to speak for you to the king, or to the commander of the army?" And she said: "I dwell among my own people."
And he said: "Then what can be done for her?" And Gehazi said: "Truly — she has no son, and her husband is old."
And he said: "Call her." And he called her, and she stood in the doorway.
And he said: "At this appointed time, at the time of life returning, you will be embracing a son." And she said: "No, my lord, man of God — do not lie to your maidservant."
And the woman conceived, and bore a son at that appointed time, at the time of life returning — as Elisha had spoken to her.
And the child grew. And it came to pass one day that he went out to his father, to the reapers;
and he said to his father: "My head! My head!" And he said to the servant: "Carry him to his mother."
And he carried him and brought him to his mother; and he sat on her knees until noon — and died.
And she went up and laid him on the bed of the man of God, and shut the door behind him, and went out.
And she called to her husband and said: "Send me, please, one of the servants and one of the donkeys, and let me run to the man of God and return."
And he said: "Why are you going to him today? It is not new moon and not sabbath." And she said: "Peace."
And she saddled the donkey and said to her servant: "Drive on — go! Do not slow the riding for me unless I tell you."
And she went, and came to the man of God, to Mount Carmel. And it came to pass, when the man of God saw her from opposite, that he said to Gehazi his servant: "Behold — that Shunammite there.
Now — run, please, to meet her, and say to her: Is it peace with you? Is it peace with your husband? Is it peace with the child?" And she said: "Peace."

And she came to the man of God, to the mountain, and took hold of his feet; and Gehazi drew near to push her away. But the man of God said: "Let her be — for her soul is bitter within her; and YHWH has hidden it from me, and has not told me."
And she said: "Did I ask a son from my lord? Did I not say: Do not deceive me?"

And he said to Gehazi: "Gird your waist and take my staff in your hand and go. If you meet a man, do not greet him; and if a man greets you, do not answer him. And lay my staff on the face of the boy."
And the mother of the boy said: "As YHWH lives, and as your soul lives — I will not leave you." And he rose and went after her.
And Gehazi passed on ahead of them and laid the staff on the face of the boy — but there was no voice and no attention. And he went back to meet him, and told him: "The boy has not awakened."
And Elisha came into the house — and behold: the boy dead, laid out on his bed.
And he went in and shut the door behind the two of them, and prayed to YHWH.
And he got up and lay on the child, and put his mouth on his mouth, and his eyes on his eyes, and his palms on his palms, and stretched himself out over him — and the flesh of the child grew warm.
And he came back and walked in the house, once this way and once that way; and he went up and stretched himself out over him — and the boy sneezed as many as seven times, and the boy opened his eyes.
And he called to Gehazi and said: "Call this Shunammite." And he called her, and she came to him; and he said: "Lift up your son."
And she came and fell at his feet and bowed down to the ground; and she lifted up her son — and went out.

And Elisha returned to Gilgal. Now the famine was in the land, and the sons of the prophets were sitting before him; and he said to his servant: "Set the great pot and boil stew for the sons of the prophets."

And one went out to the field to gather herbs, and found a wild vine, and gathered from it wild gourds — the fill of his garment; and he came and sliced them into the pot of stew — for they did not know them.
And they poured it out for the men to eat. And it came to pass, as they were eating from the stew, that they cried out and said: "Death in the pot, man of God!" And they could not eat.
And he said: "Then fetch flour." And he threw it into the pot, and said: "Pour it out for the people, and let them eat." And there was nothing harmful in the pot.

And a man came from Baal-shalishah, and brought the man of God bread of firstfruits — twenty loaves of barley bread, and fresh grain in his sack; and he said: "Give it to the people, and let them eat."
And his attendant said: "How can I set this before a hundred men?" And he said: "Give it to the people, and let them eat; for thus says YHWH: Eat — and have some left over."
And he set it before them; and they ate, and had some left over — according to the word of YHWH.

2ki.5.text: Now Naaman, commander of the army of the king of Aram, was a great man before his lord, and highly regarded — for through him YHWH had given victory to Aram; and the man was a mighty warrior — with a skin-disease.
And Aram had gone out in raiding bands, and had carried captive from the land of Israel a little girl; and she was in the service of Naaman's wife.
And she said to her mistress: "If only my lord were before the prophet who is in Samaria! Then he would gather him in from his skin-disease."
And he came and told his lord: "Thus and so spoke the girl who is from the land of Israel."

And the king of Aram said: "Go, come — and let me send a letter to the king of Israel." And he went, and took in his hand ten talents of silver, and six thousand pieces of gold, and ten changes of garments.
And he brought the letter to the king of Israel: "And now, when this letter comes to you — behold, I have sent to you Naaman my servant; and you shall gather him in from his skin-disease."

And it came to pass, when the king of Israel read the letter, that he tore his garments and said: "Am I God — to put to death and to make alive — that this man sends to me to gather a man in from his skin-disease? Only know, please, and see: he is seeking a quarrel with me!"

And it came to pass, when Elisha the man of God heard that the king of Israel had torn his garments, that he sent to the king: "Why have you torn your garments? Let him come, please, to me — and he shall know that there is a prophet in Israel."
And Naaman came with his horses and his chariot, and stood at the entrance of the house of Elisha.
And Elisha sent a messenger to him: "Go and wash seven times in the Jordan — and your flesh shall return to you, and be clean."

And Naaman was furious, and went off, and said: "Behold — I said to myself: He will surely come out, and stand, and call on the name of YHWH his God, and wave his hand over the place — and gather in the diseased skin!
Are not Abana and Pharpar, the rivers of Damascus, better than all the waters of Israel? Could I not wash in them and be clean?" And he turned and went off in a rage.
And his servants drew near and spoke to him, and said: "My father — had the prophet spoken a great thing to you, would you not do it? How much more when he says to you: Wash — and be clean."
And he went down and dipped in the Jordan seven times, according to the word of the man of God; and his flesh returned like the flesh of a little boy — and he was clean.

And he returned to the man of God — he and all his camp — and came and stood before him and said: "Behold now — I know that there is no God in all the earth except in Israel. And now — take, please, a blessing-gift from your servant."
And he said: "As YHWH lives, before whom I stand — I will not take." And he pressed him to take — but he refused.

And Naaman said: "If not — then let there be given to your servant, please, a load of earth for a pair of mules; for your servant will no longer make burnt offering or sacrifice to other gods — but only to YHWH.

In this matter may YHWH forgive your servant: when my lord goes into the house of Rimmon to bow down there, and he leans on my hand, and I bow down in the house of Rimmon — when I bow down in the house of Rimmon, may YHWH forgive your servant in this matter."
And he said to him: "Go in peace." And he went from him some distance of land.

And Gehazi, the servant of Elisha the man of God, said: "Behold — my lord has spared this Naaman the Aramean, not taking from his hand what he brought. As YHWH lives — I will run after him and take something from him!"
And Gehazi pursued after Naaman. And Naaman saw someone running after him, and jumped down from the chariot to meet him, and said: "Is it peace?"

And he said: "Peace. My lord has sent me, saying: 'Behold — just now two young men of the sons of the prophets have come to me from the hill country of Ephraim. Give them, please, a talent of silver and two changes of garments.'"

And Naaman said: "Be pleased — take two talents." And he urged him, and tied up two talents of silver in two bags, with two changes of garments, and gave them to two of his young men — and they carried them before him.
And he came to the citadel-hill, and took them from their hand and stowed them in the house; and he sent the men off, and they went.
And he came in and stood by his lord. And Elisha said to him: "From where, Gehazi?" And he said: "Your servant has gone neither here nor there."

And he said to him: "Did my heart not go along when the man turned from his chariot to meet you? Is this a time to take silver, and to take garments — and olive groves and vineyards, and sheep and oxen, and male and female slaves?
The skin-disease of Naaman shall cling to you and to your seed forever." And he went out from before him — diseased, like snow.

2ki.10.text: Now Ahab had seventy sons in Samaria. And Jehu wrote letters and sent them to Samaria — to the officials of Jezreel, the elders, and the guardians of Ahab's sons:
"And now, when this letter comes to you — with you are your lord's sons, and with you the chariots and the horses, and a fortified city, and the weapons:
look for the best and most upright of your lord's sons and set him on his father's throne — and fight for your lord's house."
And they were very greatly afraid, and said: "Behold — the two kings did not stand before him; and how shall we stand?"

And he who was over the household, and he who was over the city, and the elders, and the guardians sent to Jehu: "We are your servants; and all you say to us we will do. We will make no man king. Do what is good in your eyes."

And he wrote them a second letter: "If you are mine, and you listen to my voice — take the heads of the men, your lord's sons, and come to me at this time tomorrow to Jezreel." Now the king's sons — seventy men — were with the great men of the city, who were raising them.
And it came to pass, when the letter came to them, that they took the king's sons and slaughtered them — seventy men — and put their heads in baskets, and sent them to him at Jezreel.
And the messenger came and told him: "They have brought the heads of the king's sons." And he said: "Set them in two heaps at the entrance of the gate — until morning."
And it came to pass in the morning that he went out and stood, and said to all the people: "You are righteous. Behold — I conspired against my lord and killed him. But who struck down all these?
Know, then, that nothing of the word of YHWH shall fall to the ground — that YHWH spoke against the house of Ahab; and YHWH has done what he spoke by the hand of his servant Elijah."
And Jehu struck down all who were left of the house of Ahab in Jezreel — and all his great men and his familiar friends and his priests — until he left him no survivor.
And he rose and came and went toward Samaria. On the road, at Beth-eked of the Shepherds,

Jehu found the brothers of Ahaziah king of Judah, and said: "Who are you?" And they said: "We are the brothers of Ahaziah; and we have come down to greet the sons of the king and the sons of the queen mother."
And he said: "Seize them alive!" And they seized them alive, and slaughtered them at the pit of Beth-eked — forty-two men; and he left not a man of them.

And he went from there and found Jehonadab son of Rechab coming to meet him; and he blessed him, and said to him: "Is your heart upright with mine, as my heart is with your heart?" And Jehonadab said: "It is — and it is!" "Then give your hand." And he gave his hand — and he brought him up to him onto the chariot.
And he said: "Come with me — and see my zeal for YHWH." And they had him ride in his chariot.
And he came to Samaria and struck down all who were left to Ahab in Samaria, until he had destroyed him — according to the word of YHWH, which he spoke to Elijah.
And Jehu gathered all the people and said to them: "Ahab served Baal a little — Jehu will serve him much!

So now — all the prophets of Baal, all his servants, and all his priests: call them to me; let not a man be missing. For I have a great sacrifice for Baal; whoever is missing shall not live." But Jehu was acting with cunning — in order to destroy the servants of Baal.
And Jehu said: "Consecrate a solemn assembly for Baal." And they proclaimed it.

And Jehu sent through all Israel; and all the servants of Baal came — there was not a man left who did not come. And they came into the house of Baal; and the house of Baal was filled, mouth to mouth.
And he said to him who was over the wardrobe: "Bring out vestments for all the servants of Baal." And he brought out the attire for them.

And Jehu came, with Jehonadab son of Rechab, into the house of Baal; and he said to the servants of Baal: "Search, and see that there is not here with you any of the servants of YHWH — but only the servants of Baal alone."

And they went in to make sacrifices and burnt offerings. Now Jehu had stationed for himself eighty men outside, and said: "The man who lets escape any of the men I am bringing into your hands — his life for his life."

And it came to pass, when he had finished making the burnt offering, that Jehu said to the runners and the officers: "Come in — strike them down; let not a man come out!" And they struck them with the edge of the sword; and the runners and the officers threw them out — and went as far as the city of the house of Baal.
And they brought out the pillars of the house of Baal — and burned them;
and they tore down the pillar of Baal; and they tore down the house of Baal, and made it a latrine — to this day.
So Jehu destroyed Baal out of Israel.
Only — the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, which he made Israel sin, Jehu did not turn away from following: the calves of gold that were at Bethel and at Dan.

And YHWH said to Jehu: "Because you have done well in doing what is right in my eyes — according to all that was in my heart you have done to the house of Ahab — sons of the fourth generation shall sit for you on the throne of Israel."
But Jehu did not keep watch to walk in the law of YHWH, the God of Israel, with all his heart; he did not turn from the sins of Jeroboam, which he made Israel sin.
In those days YHWH began to cut Israel short; and Hazael struck them in all the territory of Israel:
from the Jordan eastward — all the land of Gilead, the Gadites and the Reubenites and the Manassites — from Aroer, which is by the wadi Arnon, both Gilead and Bashan.
And the rest of the acts of Jehu, and all he did, and all his might — are they not written in the Scroll of the Days of the Kings of Israel?
And Jehu lay down with his fathers, and they buried him in Samaria; and Jehoahaz his son reigned in his place.
And the days that Jehu reigned over Israel were twenty-eight years, in Samaria.

2ki.11.text: And Athaliah, the mother of Ahaziah, saw that her son was dead; and she rose and destroyed all the royal seed.

But Jehosheba, daughter of King Joram, sister of Ahaziah, took Joash son of Ahaziah and stole him away from among the king's sons who were being put to death — him and his nurse — into the bedchamber; and they hid him from Athaliah, and he was not put to death.
And he was with her in the house of YHWH, hiding, six years — while Athaliah was reigning over the land.

And in the seventh year Jehoiada sent and took the commanders of hundreds — of the Carites and of the runners — and brought them to himself in the house of YHWH; and he cut a covenant with them and made them swear in the house of YHWH, and showed them the king's son.
And he commanded them: "This is the thing you shall do: a third of you, coming on duty on the sabbath, keeping watch over the king's house;
and a third at the gate Sur; and a third at the gate behind the runners — and you shall keep watch over the house, for defense.
And the two divisions of you — all who go off duty on the sabbath — shall keep watch over the house of YHWH, around the king.

And you shall surround the king on every side, each man with his weapons in his hand; and whoever comes within the ranks shall be put to death. And be with the king when he goes out and when he comes in."

And the commanders of hundreds did according to all that Jehoiada the priest commanded: each took his men — those coming on duty on the sabbath with those going off duty on the sabbath — and they came to Jehoiada the priest.
And the priest gave the commanders of hundreds the spears and the shields that had belonged to King David, which were in the house of YHWH.
And the runners stood, each man with his weapons in his hand, from the south side of the house to the north side of the house, by the altar and by the house — around the king, on every side.
And he brought out the king's son, and put on him the crown and the testimony; and they made him king and anointed him; and they clapped their hands and said: "Long live the king!"
And Athaliah heard the sound of the runners — the people — and came to the people at the house of YHWH.

And she looked — and behold: the king standing by the pillar, according to the custom, and the commanders and the trumpets beside the king, and all the people of the land rejoicing and blowing trumpets. And Athaliah tore her garments and cried: "Conspiracy! Conspiracy!"

And Jehoiada the priest commanded the commanders of hundreds, those appointed over the force, and said to them: "Bring her out between the ranks; and whoever follows her — put to death with the sword." For the priest had said: "Let her not be put to death in the house of YHWH."
So they laid hands on her; and she went by the way of the horses' entrance to the king's house — and there she was put to death.
And Jehoiada cut the covenant between YHWH and the king and the people, to be a people for YHWH — and between the king and the people.

And all the people of the land came to the house of Baal and tore it down: his altars and his images they shattered thoroughly, and Mattan the priest of Baal they killed before the altars. And the priest set watchmen over the house of YHWH.

And he took the commanders of hundreds, and the Carites, and the runners, and all the people of the land; and they brought the king down from the house of YHWH, and came by way of the gate of the runners to the king's house — and he sat on the throne of the kings.
And all the people of the land rejoiced, and the city was quiet; and Athaliah they had put to death with the sword at the king's house.

2ki.12.text: Jehoash was seven years old when he became king.
In the seventh year of Jehu, Jehoash became king; and forty years he reigned in Jerusalem. And his mother's name was Zibiah, from Beersheba.
And Jehoash did what was right in the eyes of YHWH all his days — as Jehoiada the priest instructed him.
Only the high places were not removed; the people were still sacrificing and burning incense on the high places.

And Jehoash said to the priests: "All the silver of the holy gifts that is brought into the house of YHWH — current silver: each man's silver of assessment for persons, and all silver that comes up on a man's heart to bring into the house of YHWH —
let the priests take it for themselves, each from his acquaintance; and they shall repair the breaches of the house, wherever a breach is found."
And it came to pass, in the twenty-third year of King Jehoash, that the priests had not repaired the breaches of the house.

And King Jehoash called Jehoiada the priest and the priests, and said to them: "Why are you not repairing the breaches of the house? So now — take no more silver from your acquaintances; but hand it over for the breaches of the house."
And the priests consented to take no more silver from the people — and not to repair the breaches of the house themselves.

And Jehoiada the priest took a chest and bored a hole in its lid, and set it beside the altar, on the right as one comes into the house of YHWH; and the priests who kept the threshold would put into it all the silver brought into the house of YHWH.

And it came to pass, when they saw that there was much silver in the chest, that the king's scribe and the high priest would come up, and they would tie it up and count the silver found in the house of YHWH;
and they would give the weighed-out silver into the hands of the workmen appointed over the house of YHWH; and they would pay it out to the carpenters and the builders working on the house of YHWH,
and to the masons and the stonecutters — and for buying timber and quarried stone to repair the breaches of the house of YHWH, and for everything spent on the house to repair it.
But there were not made for the house of YHWH silver cups, snuffers, sprinkling bowls, trumpets — any vessel of gold or vessel of silver — from the silver brought into the house of YHWH;
for they gave it to the workmen, and with it they repaired the house of YHWH.
And they did not keep accounts with the men into whose hand they gave the silver to pay the workmen — for they were dealing in faithfulness.
The silver of guilt offerings and the silver of sin offerings was not brought into the house of YHWH; it belonged to the priests.
Then Hazael king of Aram went up and fought against Gath, and captured it; and Hazael set his face to go up against Jerusalem.

And Jehoash king of Judah took all the holy gifts that Jehoshaphat and Jehoram and Ahaziah, his fathers, the kings of Judah, had consecrated, and his own holy gifts, and all the gold found in the treasuries of the house of YHWH and the king's house — and sent them to Hazael king of Aram; and he went up away from Jerusalem.
And the rest of the acts of Joash, and all he did — are they not written in the Scroll of the Days of the Kings of Judah?
And his servants rose and conspired a conspiracy, and struck down Joash at Beth-millo, on the way down to Silla —
Jozacar son of Shimeath and Jehozabad son of Shomer, his servants, struck him, and he died; and they buried him with his fathers in the City of David. And Amaziah his son reigned in his place.

2ki.13.text: In the twenty-third year of Joash son of Ahaziah, king of Judah, Jehoahaz son of Jehu became king over Israel, in Samaria — seventeen years.
And he did the evil in the eyes of YHWH, and went after the sin of Jeroboam son of Nebat, which he made Israel sin; he did not turn from it.
And the anger of YHWH burned against Israel; and he gave them into the hand of Hazael king of Aram, and into the hand of Ben-hadad son of Hazael — all the days.
And Jehoahaz softened the face of YHWH; and YHWH listened to him — for he saw the oppression of Israel, how the king of Aram oppressed them.
And YHWH gave Israel a deliverer, and they came out from under the hand of Aram; and the children of Israel lived in their tents as before.
Yet they did not turn from the sins of the house of Jeroboam, which he made Israel sin; in them they walked — and even the Asherah pole was left standing in Samaria.
For he had left Jehoahaz no army — except fifty horsemen and ten chariots and ten thousand foot soldiers; for the king of Aram had destroyed them, and made them like dust for threshing.
And the rest of the acts of Jehoahaz, and all he did, and his might — are they not written in the Scroll of the Days of the Kings of Israel?
And Jehoahaz lay down with his fathers, and they buried him in Samaria; and Joash his son reigned in his place.
In the thirty-seventh year of Joash king of Judah, Jehoash son of Jehoahaz became king over Israel, in Samaria — sixteen years.
And he did the evil in the eyes of YHWH; he did not turn from all the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, which he made Israel sin; in them he walked.
And the rest of the acts of Joash, and all he did, and his might with which he fought against Amaziah king of Judah — are they not written in the Scroll of the Days of the Kings of Israel?
And Joash lay down with his fathers, and Jeroboam sat on his throne; and Joash was buried in Samaria with the kings of Israel.

Now Elisha had fallen sick with the sickness of which he would die; and Joash king of Israel came down to him and wept over his face, and said: "My father! My father! The chariot of Israel and its horsemen!"
And Elisha said to him: "Take a bow and arrows." And he took to himself a bow and arrows.
And he said to the king of Israel: "Set your hand on the bow." And he set his hand; and Elisha laid his hands on the king's hands,

and said: "Open the window eastward." And he opened it. And Elisha said: "Shoot!" And he shot. And he said: "An arrow of victory for YHWH — an arrow of victory over Aram! You shall strike Aram at Aphek — until finishing them."
And he said: "Take the arrows." And he took them. And he said to the king of Israel: "Strike the ground." And he struck three times — and stopped.
And the man of God was angry with him, and said: "You should have struck five or six times — then you would have struck Aram until finishing them. But now — three times you shall strike Aram."
And Elisha died, and they buried him. Now raiding bands of Moab would come into the land at the coming of the year.

And it came to pass, as some men were burying a man, that behold — they saw the raiding band; and they threw the man into the grave of Elisha. And as the man went in and touched the bones of Elisha — he came to life, and stood up on his feet.
And Hazael king of Aram had oppressed Israel all the days of Jehoahaz.

But YHWH was gracious to them and had compassion on them and turned toward them — for the sake of his covenant with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob; and he was not willing to destroy them, and did not throw them away from before his face — until now.
And Hazael king of Aram died; and Ben-hadad his son reigned in his place.

And Jehoash son of Jehoahaz took back from the hand of Ben-hadad son of Hazael the cities that he had taken from the hand of Jehoahaz his father in war. Three times Joash struck him — and he recovered the cities of Israel.

2ki.14.text: In the second year of Joash son of Joahaz, king of Israel, Amaziah son of Joash, king of Judah, became king.
He was twenty-five years old when he became king, and twenty-nine years he reigned in Jerusalem; and his mother's name was Jehoaddin, from Jerusalem.
And he did what was right in the eyes of YHWH — only not like David his father; according to all that Joash his father had done, he did.
Only the high places were not removed; the people were still sacrificing and burning incense on the high places.
And it came to pass, as soon as the kingdom was firm in his hand, that he struck down his servants who had struck down the king his father;

but the sons of the strikers he did not put to death — as it is written in the scroll of the law of Moses, where YHWH commanded: "Fathers shall not be put to death for sons, and sons shall not be put to death for fathers; but each man shall die for his own sin."
He struck Edom in the Valley of Salt — ten thousand — and took Sela in the battle, and called its name Joktheel, to this day.
Then Amaziah sent messengers to Jehoash son of Jehoahaz, son of Jehu, king of Israel: "Come — let us look each other in the face!"

And Jehoash king of Israel sent to Amaziah king of Judah: "The thornbush that was in Lebanon sent to the cedar that was in Lebanon: 'Give your daughter to my son as a wife.' And a wild beast that was in Lebanon passed by — and trampled the thornbush.
You have surely struck Edom, and your heart has lifted you up. Be honored — and stay in your own house. Why should you provoke evil, so that you fall — you, and Judah with you?"
But Amaziah would not listen. And Jehoash king of Israel went up; and they looked each other in the face — he and Amaziah king of Judah — at Beth-shemesh, which belongs to Judah.
And Judah was struck down before Israel; and they fled, each man to his tent.

And Jehoash king of Israel captured Amaziah king of Judah, son of Jehoash son of Ahaziah, at Beth-shemesh; and he came to Jerusalem and broke down the wall of Jerusalem from the Gate of Ephraim to the Corner Gate — four hundred cubits.
And he took all the gold and the silver, and all the vessels found in the house of YHWH and in the treasuries of the king's house, and hostages — and returned to Samaria.
And the rest of the acts of Jehoash, which he did, and his might, and how he fought with Amaziah king of Judah — are they not written in the Scroll of the Days of the Kings of Israel?
And Jehoash lay down with his fathers, and was buried in Samaria with the kings of Israel; and Jeroboam his son reigned in his place.
And Amaziah son of Joash, king of Judah, lived after the death of Jehoash son of Jehoahaz, king of Israel, fifteen years.
And the rest of the acts of Amaziah — are they not written in the Scroll of the Days of the Kings of Judah?
And they conspired a conspiracy against him in Jerusalem, and he fled to Lachish; and they sent after him to Lachish — and put him to death there.
And they carried him on the horses; and he was buried in Jerusalem with his fathers, in the City of David.
And all the people of Judah took Azariah — he was sixteen years old — and made him king in place of his father Amaziah.
He built Elath and restored it to Judah — after the king lay down with his fathers.
In the fifteenth year of Amaziah son of Joash, king of Judah, Jeroboam son of Joash, king of Israel, became king in Samaria — forty-one years.
And he did the evil in the eyes of YHWH; he did not turn from all the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, which he made Israel sin.

He restored the border of Israel, from Lebo-hamath to the Sea of the Arabah — according to the word of YHWH, the God of Israel, which he spoke by the hand of his servant Jonah son of Amittai, the prophet, who was from Gath-hepher.
For YHWH saw the affliction of Israel — very bitter: none bond, none free, and no helper for Israel.
And YHWH had not said he would wipe out the name of Israel from under the heavens; and he saved them by the hand of Jeroboam son of Joash.

And the rest of the acts of Jeroboam, and all he did, and his might — how he fought, and how he recovered Damascus and Hamath for Judah in Israel — are they not written in the Scroll of the Days of the Kings of Israel?
And Jeroboam lay down with his fathers, with the kings of Israel; and Zechariah his son reigned in his place.

2ki.15.text: In the twenty-seventh year of Jeroboam king of Israel, Azariah son of Amaziah, king of Judah, became king.
He was sixteen years old when he became king, and fifty-two years he reigned in Jerusalem; and his mother's name was Jecoliah, from Jerusalem.
And he did what was right in the eyes of YHWH — according to all that Amaziah his father had done.
Only the high places were not removed; the people were still sacrificing and burning incense on the high places.
And YHWH struck the king, and he had a skin-disease until the day of his death; and he lived in a separate house. And Jotham the king's son was over the household, judging the people of the land.
And the rest of the acts of Azariah, and all he did — are they not written in the Scroll of the Days of the Kings of Judah?
And Azariah lay down with his fathers, and they buried him with his fathers in the City of David; and Jotham his son reigned in his place.
In the thirty-eighth year of Azariah king of Judah, Zechariah son of Jeroboam became king over Israel, in Samaria — six months.
And he did the evil in the eyes of YHWH, as his fathers had done; he did not turn from the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, which he made Israel sin.
And Shallum son of Jabesh conspired against him, and struck him down before the people, and put him to death — and reigned in his place.
And the rest of the acts of Zechariah — behold, they are written in the Scroll of the Days of the Kings of Israel.
This was the word of YHWH that he spoke to Jehu: "Sons of the fourth generation shall sit for you on the throne of Israel." And so it was.
Shallum son of Jabesh became king in the thirty-ninth year of Uzziah king of Judah; and he reigned a month of days in Samaria.
And Menahem son of Gadi went up from Tirzah and came to Samaria, and struck down Shallum son of Jabesh in Samaria, and put him to death — and reigned in his place.
And the rest of the acts of Shallum, and the conspiracy that he conspired — behold, they are written in the Scroll of the Days of the Kings of Israel.
Then Menahem struck Tiphsah, and all who were in it, and its territory, from Tirzah — because it did not open to him; and he struck it, and all its pregnant women he ripped open.
In the thirty-ninth year of Azariah king of Judah, Menahem son of Gadi became king over Israel — ten years, in Samaria.
And he did the evil in the eyes of YHWH; he did not turn from the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, which he made Israel sin — all his days.
Pul king of Assyria came against the land; and Menahem gave Pul a thousand talents of silver, that his hands might be with him, to strengthen the kingdom in his hand.

And Menahem exacted the silver from Israel — from all the mighty men of wealth — to give to the king of Assyria: fifty shekels of silver for each man. And the king of Assyria turned back, and did not stay there in the land.
And the rest of the acts of Menahem, and all he did — are they not written in the Scroll of the Days of the Kings of Israel?
And Menahem lay down with his fathers; and Pekahiah his son reigned in his place.
In the fiftieth year of Azariah king of Judah, Pekahiah son of Menahem became king over Israel, in Samaria — two years.
And he did the evil in the eyes of YHWH; he did not turn from the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, which he made Israel sin.

And Pekah son of Remaliah, his officer, conspired against him, and struck him down in Samaria, in the citadel of the king's house — with Argob and Arieh, and with him fifty men of the sons of the Gileadites; and he put him to death — and reigned in his place.
And the rest of the acts of Pekahiah, and all he did — behold, they are written in the Scroll of the Days of the Kings of Israel.
In the fifty-second year of Azariah king of Judah, Pekah son of Remaliah became king over Israel, in Samaria — twenty years.
And he did the evil in the eyes of YHWH; he did not turn from the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, which he made Israel sin.

In the days of Pekah king of Israel, Tiglath-pileser king of Assyria came and took Ijon, and Abel-beth-maacah, and Janoah, and Kedesh, and Hazor, and Gilead, and Galilee — all the land of Naphtali — and carried them into exile to Assyria.
And Hoshea son of Elah conspired a conspiracy against Pekah son of Remaliah, and struck him down and put him to death — and reigned in his place, in the twentieth year of Jotham son of Uzziah.
And the rest of the acts of Pekah, and all he did — behold, they are written in the Scroll of the Days of the Kings of Israel.
In the second year of Pekah son of Remaliah, king of Israel, Jotham son of Uzziah, king of Judah, became king.
He was twenty-five years old when he became king, and sixteen years he reigned in Jerusalem; and his mother's name was Jerusha daughter of Zadok.
And he did what was right in the eyes of YHWH — according to all that Uzziah his father had done, he did.
Only the high places were not removed; the people were still sacrificing and burning incense on the high places. He built the Upper Gate of the house of YHWH.
And the rest of the acts of Jotham, which he did — are they not written in the Scroll of the Days of the Kings of Judah?
In those days YHWH began to send against Judah Rezin king of Aram, and Pekah son of Remaliah.
And Jotham lay down with his fathers, and was buried with his fathers in the city of David his father; and Ahaz his son reigned in his place.

2ki.16.text: In the seventeenth year of Pekah son of Remaliah, Ahaz son of Jotham, king of Judah, became king.
Ahaz was twenty years old when he became king, and sixteen years he reigned in Jerusalem; and he did not do what was right in the eyes of YHWH his God, like David his father.

And he walked in the way of the kings of Israel; and he even made his own son pass through the fire — according to the abominations of the nations that YHWH had dispossessed before the children of Israel.
And he sacrificed and burned incense on the high places and on the hills — and under every leafy tree.
Then Rezin king of Aram and Pekah son of Remaliah, king of Israel, came up to Jerusalem for battle; and they besieged Ahaz — but could not overcome him in battle.
At that time Rezin king of Aram recovered Elath for Aram, and drove the Judahites out of Elath; and Edomites came to Elath, and have lived there to this day.

And Ahaz sent messengers to Tiglath-pileser king of Assyria: "I am your servant and your son. Come up and save me from the palm of the king of Aram, and from the palm of the king of Israel, who are rising against me."
And Ahaz took the silver and the gold found in the house of YHWH and in the treasuries of the king's house, and sent it to the king of Assyria as a bribe.
And the king of Assyria listened to him; and the king of Assyria went up against Damascus and seized it, and carried it into exile to Kir — and Rezin he put to death.

And King Ahaz went to Damascus to meet Tiglath-pileser king of Assyria; and he saw the altar that was in Damascus. And King Ahaz sent Uriah the priest the likeness of the altar and its pattern, according to all its workmanship.
And Uriah the priest built the altar; according to all that King Ahaz had sent from Damascus, so Uriah the priest made it — before King Ahaz came from Damascus.
And the king came from Damascus; and the king saw the altar, and the king drew near to the altar and went up on it,
and burned his burnt offering and his grain offering, and poured out his drink offering, and splashed the blood of his peace offerings against the altar.
And the bronze altar that was before YHWH — he moved it from the front of the house, from between his altar and the house of YHWH, and put it on the north side of his altar.

And King Ahaz commanded Uriah the priest: "On the great altar burn the morning burnt offering and the evening grain offering, and the king's burnt offering and his grain offering, and the burnt offering of all the people of the land, and their grain offering and their drink offerings; and all the blood of burnt offering and all the blood of sacrifice you shall splash against it. And the bronze altar shall be for me — to inquire by."
And Uriah the priest did according to all that King Ahaz commanded.
And King Ahaz cut off the panels of the stands and removed the basin from on them; and he took down the Sea from the bronze oxen that were under it, and set it on a pavement of stones.
And the sabbath canopy that had been built in the house, and the king's outer entrance, he turned aside from the house of YHWH — because of the king of Assyria.
And the rest of the acts of Ahaz, which he did — are they not written in the Scroll of the Days of the Kings of Judah?
And Ahaz lay down with his fathers, and was buried with his fathers in the City of David; and Hezekiah his son reigned in his place.

2ki.17.text: In the twelfth year of Ahaz king of Judah, Hoshea son of Elah became king in Samaria over Israel — nine years.
And he did the evil in the eyes of YHWH — only not like the kings of Israel who were before him.
Against him came up Shalmaneser king of Assyria; and Hoshea became his servant, and rendered him tribute.

But the king of Assyria found conspiracy in Hoshea — for he had sent messengers to So, king of Egypt, and had not brought up tribute to the king of Assyria as year by year; and the king of Assyria shut him up, and bound him in a prison house.
And the king of Assyria came up against the whole land; and he came up against Samaria, and besieged it three years.

In the ninth year of Hoshea, the king of Assyria captured Samaria, and carried Israel into exile to Assyria; and he settled them in Halah, and on the Habor, the river of Gozan, and in the cities of the Medes.

And it came to pass — because the children of Israel had sinned against YHWH their God, who brought them up out of the land of Egypt, from under the hand of Pharaoh king of Egypt; and they feared other gods,
and walked in the statutes of the nations that YHWH had dispossessed before the children of Israel, and in those the kings of Israel had made.
And the children of Israel did secretly things that were not right against YHWH their God; and they built themselves high places in all their cities — from watchtower to fortified city;
and they set up for themselves standing-stones and Asherah poles on every high hill and under every leafy tree;
and they burned incense there, on all the high places, like the nations that YHWH had exiled before them; and they did evil things, provoking YHWH;
and they served the idols — of which YHWH had said to them: "You shall not do this thing."

And YHWH warned Israel and Judah, by the hand of every prophet and every seer: "Turn back from your evil ways, and keep my commandments, my statutes — according to all the law that I commanded your fathers, and that I sent to you by the hand of my servants the prophets."
But they did not listen; and they stiffened their neck like the neck of their fathers, who did not trust in YHWH their God.

And they rejected his statutes, and his covenant that he cut with their fathers, and his testimonies that he testified against them; and they went after the empty nothing — and became nothing themselves — and after the nations that surrounded them, of whom YHWH had commanded them not to do as they did.
And they abandoned all the commandments of YHWH their God, and made themselves a cast image — two calves — and made an Asherah pole, and bowed down to all the host of the heavens, and served Baal;
and they made their sons and their daughters pass through the fire, and practiced divinations and read omens, and sold themselves to do the evil in the eyes of YHWH — provoking him.
And YHWH was very angry with Israel, and removed them from before his face; none was left — only the tribe of Judah alone.
Even Judah did not keep the commandments of YHWH their God; and they walked in the statutes of Israel, which they had made.
And YHWH rejected all the seed of Israel, and afflicted them, and gave them into the hand of plunderers — until he had thrown them away from before his face.
For he tore Israel from the house of David, and they made Jeroboam son of Nebat king; and Jeroboam drove Israel away from following YHWH — and made them sin a great sin.
And the children of Israel walked in all the sins of Jeroboam, which he did; they did not turn from them —
until YHWH removed Israel from before his face, as he had spoken by the hand of all his servants the prophets. And Israel was exiled from its own soil to Assyria — to this day.

And the king of Assyria brought people from Babylon and from Cuthah and from Avva and from Hamath and Sepharvaim, and settled them in the cities of Samaria in place of the children of Israel; and they took possession of Samaria and lived in its cities.
And it came to pass, at the beginning of their dwelling there, that they did not fear YHWH; and YHWH sent lions among them — and they kept killing among them.

And they said to the king of Assyria: "The nations you exiled and settled in the cities of Samaria do not know the custom of the god of the land; and he has sent lions among them, and behold — they are killing them, because they do not know the custom of the god of the land."
And the king of Assyria commanded: "Take there one of the priests you exiled from there; and let them go and live there — and let him teach them the custom of the god of the land."
And one of the priests they had exiled from Samaria came and lived in Bethel; and he was teaching them how they should fear YHWH.
But each nation went on making its own gods, and set them in the houses of the high places the Samaritans had made — each nation in its cities where they lived:
the men of Babylon made Succoth-benoth, and the men of Cuth made Nergal, and the men of Hamath made Ashima,
and the Avvites made Nibhaz and Tartak; and the Sepharvites were burning their sons in the fire to Adrammelech and Anammelech, the gods of Sepharvaim.
So they were fearing YHWH — and also making for themselves, from among their whole range, priests of the high places, who kept officiating for them in the houses of the high places.
YHWH they were fearing — and their own gods they were serving, according to the custom of the nations from which they had been exiled.

To this day they act according to the former customs: they are not fearing YHWH, and they are not acting according to their statutes and their custom, or according to the law and the commandment that YHWH commanded the sons of Jacob — whose name he made Israel.
And YHWH had cut a covenant with them and commanded them: "You shall not fear other gods; and you shall not bow down to them, and you shall not serve them, and you shall not sacrifice to them —
but YHWH, who brought you up out of the land of Egypt with great power and an outstretched arm: him you shall fear, and to him you shall bow down, and to him you shall sacrifice.
And the statutes and the judgments, and the law and the commandment, which he wrote for you, you shall keep watch to do all the days — and you shall not fear other gods.
And the covenant I have cut with you, you shall not forget — and you shall not fear other gods.
But YHWH your God you shall fear — and he will deliver you from the hand of all your enemies."
But they did not listen; rather, according to their former custom they went on acting.
So these nations were fearing YHWH — and serving their carved images; their children too, and their children's children: as their fathers did, so they do — to this day.

2ki.18.text: And it came to pass in the third year of Hoshea son of Elah, king of Israel, that Hezekiah son of Ahaz, king of Judah, became king.
He was twenty-five years old when he became king, and twenty-nine years he reigned in Jerusalem; and his mother's name was Abi daughter of Zechariah.
And he did what was right in the eyes of YHWH — according to all that David his father had done.

He removed the high places, and shattered the standing-stones, and cut down the Asherah pole; and he crushed to pieces the bronze serpent that Moses had made — for until those days the children of Israel had been burning incense to it; and it was called Nehushtan — Bronze-Thing.
In YHWH, the God of Israel, he trusted; and after him there was none like him among all the kings of Judah — nor among those before him.
And he clung to YHWH; he did not turn from following him; and he kept his commandments, which YHWH had commanded Moses.
And YHWH was with him; wherever he went out, he prospered. And he rebelled against the king of Assyria, and did not serve him.
He struck the Philistines as far as Gaza and its territory — from watchtower to fortified city.
And it came to pass in the fourth year of King Hezekiah — which was the seventh year of Hoshea son of Elah, king of Israel — that Shalmaneser king of Assyria came up against Samaria and besieged it;
and they captured it at the end of three years. In the sixth year of Hezekiah — which was the ninth year of Hoshea king of Israel — Samaria was captured.
And the king of Assyria carried Israel into exile to Assyria, and led them to Halah, and to the Habor, the river of Gozan, and the cities of the Medes —
because they did not listen to the voice of YHWH their God, but transgressed his covenant: all that Moses the servant of YHWH had commanded. They neither listened nor did it.
And in the fourteenth year of King Hezekiah, Sennacherib king of Assyria came up against all the fortified cities of Judah — and seized them.

And Hezekiah king of Judah sent to the king of Assyria at Lachish: "I have sinned. Turn back from me; whatever you lay on me I will bear." And the king of Assyria laid on Hezekiah king of Judah three hundred talents of silver and thirty talents of gold.
And Hezekiah gave all the silver found in the house of YHWH and in the treasuries of the king's house.
At that time Hezekiah cut off the doors of the temple of YHWH, and the doorposts that Hezekiah king of Judah had overlaid — and gave them to the king of Assyria.

And the king of Assyria sent the Tartan and the Rab-saris and the Rab-shakeh from Lachish to King Hezekiah, with a heavy force, to Jerusalem. And they went up and came to Jerusalem; and they went up and came and stood by the channel of the upper pool, which is on the highway of the Fuller's Field.
And they called for the king; and there came out to them Eliakim son of Hilkiah, who was over the household, and Shebnah the scribe, and Joah son of Asaph, the recorder.
And the Rab-shakeh said to them: "Say, please, to Hezekiah: Thus says the great king, the king of Assyria: What is this trust in which you have trusted?
You have said — mere word of lips! — 'There is counsel and strength for the war.' Now — on whom have you trusted, that you have rebelled against me?

Now behold — you have trusted for yourself on the staff of this crushed reed, on Egypt — which, if a man leans on it, goes into his palm and pierces it. Such is Pharaoh king of Egypt to all who trust in him.

And if you say to me: 'In YHWH our God we trust' — is he not the one whose high places and altars Hezekiah has removed, saying to Judah and to Jerusalem: 'Before this altar you shall bow down, in Jerusalem'?
So now — make a wager, please, with my lord the king of Assyria: I will give you two thousand horses — if you are able to put riders on them!
And how could you turn back the face of a single governor among the least of my lord's servants — while you trust for yourself on Egypt for chariots and for horsemen?
Now — is it apart from YHWH that I have come up against this place to destroy it? YHWH said to me: 'Go up against this land — and destroy it.'"

And Eliakim son of Hilkiah, and Shebnah, and Joah said to the Rab-shakeh: "Speak, please, to your servants in Aramaic — for we understand it; and do not speak with us in Judahite, in the ears of the people who are on the wall."

And the Rab-shakeh said to them: "Is it to your lord and to you that my lord has sent me to speak these words? Is it not to the men sitting on the wall — doomed with you to eat their own dung and drink their own urine?"
And the Rab-shakeh stood and called out with a great voice in Judahite; and he spoke and said: "Hear the word of the great king, the king of Assyria!
Thus says the king: Let not Hezekiah deceive you — for he cannot deliver you from his hand.
And let not Hezekiah make you trust in YHWH, saying: 'YHWH will surely deliver us; and this city will not be given into the hand of the king of Assyria.'

Do not listen to Hezekiah! For thus says the king of Assyria: Make a blessing-peace with me and come out to me — and eat, each of his own vine and each of his own fig tree, and drink, each the water of his own cistern —

until I come and take you to a land like your own land: a land of grain and new wine, a land of bread and vineyards, a land of olive oil and honey — and live, and do not die! And do not listen to Hezekiah, for he misleads you, saying: 'YHWH will deliver us.'
Have the gods of the nations really delivered — each his own land — from the hand of the king of Assyria?
Where are the gods of Hamath and Arpad? Where are the gods of Sepharvaim, Hena, and Ivvah? Did they deliver Samaria from my hand?
Who among all the gods of the lands have delivered their land from my hand — that YHWH should deliver Jerusalem from my hand?"
But the people kept silent and did not answer him a word — for it was the king's command: "Do not answer him."
And Eliakim son of Hilkiah, who was over the household, and Shebna the scribe, and Joah son of Asaph, the recorder, came to Hezekiah with garments torn; and they told him the words of the Rab-shakeh.

2ki.19.text: And it came to pass, when King Hezekiah heard, that he tore his garments and covered himself with sackcloth, and went into the house of YHWH.
And he sent Eliakim, who was over the household, and Shebna the scribe, and the elders of the priests, covered with sackcloth, to Isaiah the prophet, son of Amoz.
And they said to him: "Thus says Hezekiah: A day of distress and rebuke and contempt is this day; for children have come to the point of birth — and there is no strength to bring forth.

Perhaps YHWH your God will hear all the words of the Rab-shakeh, whom the king of Assyria his lord sent to taunt the living God, and will rebuke the words that YHWH your God has heard. So lift up a prayer for the remnant that is found."
And the servants of King Hezekiah came to Isaiah;
and Isaiah said to them: "Thus shall you say to your lord: Thus says YHWH: Do not fear because of the words you have heard, with which the underlings of the king of Assyria have blasphemed me.
Behold — I am putting a spirit in him; and he shall hear a report and return to his own land; and I will make him fall by the sword in his own land."
And the Rab-shakeh returned, and found the king of Assyria fighting against Libnah — for he had heard that he had pulled up from Lachish.
And he heard concerning Tirhakah king of Cush: "Behold — he has come out to fight with you." And again he sent messengers to Hezekiah:
"Thus shall you say to Hezekiah king of Judah: Let not your God, in whom you trust, deceive you, saying: 'Jerusalem will not be given into the hand of the king of Assyria.'
Behold — you have heard what the kings of Assyria have done to all the lands, devoting them to destruction. And you — you will be delivered?
Did the gods of the nations that my fathers destroyed deliver them — Gozan and Haran and Rezeph, and the sons of Eden who were in Telassar?
Where is the king of Hamath, and the king of Arpad, and the king of the city of Sepharvaim, of Hena and Ivvah?"
And Hezekiah took the letters from the hand of the messengers and read them; and he went up to the house of YHWH — and Hezekiah spread it out before YHWH.
And Hezekiah prayed before YHWH and said: "YHWH, God of Israel, enthroned on the cherubim — you are God, you alone, over all the kingdoms of the earth; you made the heavens and the earth.
Bend your ear, YHWH, and hear; open your eyes, YHWH, and see. And hear the words of Sennacherib, which he sent to taunt the living God.
Truly, YHWH, the kings of Assyria have laid waste the nations and their land,
and have given their gods to the fire — for they were no gods, but the work of human hands, wood and stone; so they destroyed them.
And now, YHWH our God — save us, please, from his hand, that all the kingdoms of the earth may know that you, YHWH, are God — you alone."
And Isaiah son of Amoz sent to Hezekiah: "Thus says YHWH, the God of Israel: What you have prayed to me concerning Sennacherib king of Assyria — I have heard.
This is the word that YHWH has spoken about him: She despises you, she mocks you — the virgin daughter of Zion; behind you she shakes her head — the daughter of Jerusalem.
Whom have you taunted and blasphemed? Against whom have you raised your voice, and lifted your eyes on high? Against the Holy One of Israel!

By the hand of your messengers you have taunted the Lord, and said: 'With the multitude of my chariots I have gone up the heights of the mountains, the far reaches of Lebanon; and I cut down its tallest cedars, the choicest of its cypresses; and I came to its farthest lodging, its garden forest.
I dug and drank foreign waters; and I dried up with the sole of my feet all the streams of Egypt.'
Have you not heard? From long ago I did it; from days of old I shaped it — now I have brought it to pass: that you should turn fortified cities into crashed heaps of ruins,
while their inhabitants, short of hand, were shattered and put to shame — they became grass of the field and green sprouting, grass of the rooftops, grain blighted before it stands.
But your sitting down, and your going out and your coming in — I know them; and your raging against me.

Because your raging against me, and your complacent roar, have come up into my ears — I will put my hook in your nose and my bridle in your lips, and I will turn you back by the road on which you came.

And this shall be the sign for you: this year, eat what grows of itself; and in the second year, what springs up from that; and in the third year — sow and reap, and plant vineyards and eat their fruit.
And the escaped remnant of the house of Judah shall again take root downward — and bear fruit upward.
For from Jerusalem shall go out a remnant, and an escaped band from Mount Zion. The zeal of YHWH will do this.

Therefore thus says YHWH concerning the king of Assyria: He shall not come into this city, and he shall not shoot an arrow there; and he shall not come before it with a shield, and he shall not pour out a siege-ramp against it.
By the road on which he came, he shall return; and into this city he shall not come — declares YHWH.
And I will shield this city, to save it — for my own sake, and for the sake of David my servant."

And it came to pass that night that the angel of YHWH went out and struck down in the camp of Assyria a hundred eighty-five thousand; and when they rose early in the morning — behold: all of them dead corpses.
And Sennacherib king of Assyria pulled up, and went, and returned — and stayed in Nineveh.

And it came to pass, as he was bowing down in the house of Nisroch his god, that Adrammelech and Sharezer struck him down with the sword; and they escaped into the land of Ararat. And Esarhaddon his son reigned in his place.

2ki.20.text: In those days Hezekiah fell sick to the point of death; and Isaiah son of Amoz, the prophet, came to him and said to him: "Thus says YHWH: Set your house in order — for you are dying, and you shall not live."
And he turned his face to the wall, and prayed to YHWH:
"Ah, YHWH — remember, please, how I have walked before you in truth and with a whole heart, and have done what is good in your eyes." And Hezekiah wept a great weeping.
And it came to pass, before Isaiah had gone out of the middle court, that the word of YHWH came to him:

"Turn back, and say to Hezekiah, the leader of my people: Thus says YHWH, the God of David your father: I have heard your prayer; I have seen your tears. Behold — I am healing you. On the third day you shall go up to the house of YHWH.

And I will add to your days fifteen years; and from the palm of the king of Assyria I will deliver you, and this city; and I will shield this city — for my own sake, and for the sake of David my servant."
And Isaiah said: "Fetch a cake of figs." And they fetched it and laid it on the boil — and he recovered.
And Hezekiah said to Isaiah: "What is the sign that YHWH will heal me, and that I shall go up on the third day to the house of YHWH?"
And Isaiah said: "This is the sign for you from YHWH, that YHWH will do the thing he has spoken: shall the shadow go forward ten steps — or shall it go back ten steps?"
And Hezekiah said: "It is a light thing for the shadow to stretch forward ten steps. No — let the shadow turn backward ten steps."
And Isaiah the prophet called to YHWH; and he brought the shadow back on the steps where it had gone down — on the steps of Ahaz — backward ten steps.
At that time Berodach-baladan son of Baladan, king of Babylon, sent letters and a gift to Hezekiah — for he had heard that Hezekiah had been sick.

And Hezekiah listened to them, and showed them his whole treasure house: the silver and the gold and the spices and the fine oil, and his armory, and all that was found in his treasuries. There was nothing that Hezekiah did not show them, in his house and in all his dominion.
And Isaiah the prophet came to King Hezekiah and said to him: "What did these men say — and from where do they come to you?" And Hezekiah said: "From a far country they have come — from Babylon."
And he said: "What have they seen in your house?" And Hezekiah said: "Everything in my house they have seen. There is nothing I did not show them in my treasuries."
And Isaiah said to Hezekiah: "Hear the word of YHWH:
Behold — days are coming when everything in your house, and what your fathers have stored up to this day, shall be carried to Babylon. Nothing shall be left — says YHWH.
And some of your sons who go out from you, whom you will father, shall be taken — and they shall be officials in the palace of the king of Babylon."
And Hezekiah said to Isaiah: "The word of YHWH that you have spoken is good." For he said: "Is it not so — if there is peace and truth in my days?"

And the rest of the acts of Hezekiah, and all his might, and how he made the pool and the channel and brought the water into the city — are they not written in the Scroll of the Days of the Kings of Judah?
And Hezekiah lay down with his fathers; and Manasseh his son reigned in his place.

2ki.21.text: Manasseh was twelve years old when he became king, and fifty-five years he reigned in Jerusalem; and his mother's name was Hephzibah.
And he did the evil in the eyes of YHWH — according to the abominations of the nations that YHWH had dispossessed before the children of Israel.

And he built again the high places that Hezekiah his father had destroyed; and he raised altars for Baal and made an Asherah pole, as Ahab king of Israel had done; and he bowed down to all the host of the heavens, and served them.
And he built altars in the house of YHWH — of which YHWH had said: "In Jerusalem I will set my name."
And he built altars for all the host of the heavens, in the two courts of the house of YHWH.
And he made his son pass through the fire, and practiced soothsaying and read omens, and dealt with ghost-mediums and familiar spirits. He did much evil in the eyes of YHWH — provoking him.

And he set the carved image of Asherah that he had made in the house of which YHWH had said to David and to Solomon his son: "In this house, and in Jerusalem, which I have chosen out of all the tribes of Israel, I will set my name forever;

and I will not again make the foot of Israel wander from the soil I gave to their fathers — if only they keep watch to do according to all I have commanded them, and according to all the law that my servant Moses commanded them."
But they did not listen; and Manasseh led them astray, to do more evil than the nations that YHWH had destroyed before the children of Israel.
And YHWH spoke by the hand of his servants the prophets:
"Because Manasseh king of Judah has done these abominations — doing worse than all that the Amorites did who were before him — and has made even Judah sin with his idols:
therefore thus says YHWH, the God of Israel: Behold — I am bringing evil on Jerusalem and Judah such that whoever hears of it — both his ears shall ring.

And I will stretch over Jerusalem the measuring line of Samaria, and the plumb-weight of the house of Ahab; and I will wipe Jerusalem as one wipes the dish — wiping it and turning it over on its face.
And I will forsake the remnant of my inheritance, and give them into the hand of their enemies; and they shall become plunder and spoil to all their enemies —
because they have done the evil in my eyes, and have been provoking me, from the day their fathers came out of Egypt to this very day."

And moreover, Manasseh shed innocent blood in very great abundance — until he had filled Jerusalem from mouth to mouth — apart from his sin by which he made Judah sin, doing the evil in the eyes of YHWH.
And the rest of the acts of Manasseh, and all he did, and the sin that he sinned — are they not written in the Scroll of the Days of the Kings of Judah?
And Manasseh lay down with his fathers, and was buried in the garden of his house — in the garden of Uzza; and Amon his son reigned in his place.
Amon was twenty-two years old when he became king, and two years he reigned in Jerusalem; and his mother's name was Meshullemeth daughter of Haruz, from Jotbah.
And he did the evil in the eyes of YHWH, as Manasseh his father had done;
and he walked in all the way his father had walked, and served the idols his father had served, and bowed down to them;
and he abandoned YHWH, the God of his fathers, and did not walk in the way of YHWH.
And the servants of Amon conspired against him, and put the king to death in his own house.
And the people of the land struck down all who had conspired against King Amon; and the people of the land made Josiah his son king in his place.
And the rest of the acts of Amon, which he did — are they not written in the Scroll of the Days of the Kings of Judah?
And he was buried in his tomb in the garden of Uzza; and Josiah his son reigned in his place.

2ki.22.text: Josiah was eight years old when he became king, and thirty-one years he reigned in Jerusalem; and his mother's name was Jedidah daughter of Adaiah, from Bozkath.
And he did what was right in the eyes of YHWH, and walked in all the way of David his father; he did not turn aside to the right or the left.
And it came to pass in the eighteenth year of King Josiah that the king sent Shaphan son of Azaliah, son of Meshullam, the scribe, to the house of YHWH:
"Go up to Hilkiah the high priest, and let him total up the silver brought into the house of YHWH, which the keepers of the threshold have gathered from the people;
and let them give it into the hand of the workmen appointed over the house of YHWH; and let them give it to the workmen who are in the house of YHWH, to repair the breaches of the house —
to the carpenters and the builders and the masons — and for buying timber and quarried stone to repair the house.
Only, no accounting shall be kept with them for the silver given into their hand — for they are dealing in faithfulness."
And Hilkiah the high priest said to Shaphan the scribe: "I have found the scroll of the law in the house of YHWH." And Hilkiah gave the scroll to Shaphan — and he read it.

And Shaphan the scribe came to the king and brought the king back word, and said: "Your servants have poured out the silver found in the house, and have given it into the hand of the workmen appointed over the house of YHWH."
And Shaphan the scribe told the king: "Hilkiah the priest has given me a scroll." And Shaphan read it before the king.
And it came to pass, when the king heard the words of the scroll of the law, that he tore his garments.
And the king commanded Hilkiah the priest, and Ahikam son of Shaphan, and Achbor son of Micaiah, and Shaphan the scribe, and Asaiah the king's servant:

"Go, seek YHWH on my behalf, and on behalf of the people, and on behalf of all Judah, concerning the words of this scroll that has been found; for great is the wrath of YHWH that is kindled against us — because our fathers did not listen to the words of this scroll, to do according to all that is written concerning us."

So Hilkiah the priest, and Ahikam, and Achbor, and Shaphan, and Asaiah went to Huldah the prophetess, wife of Shallum son of Tikvah, son of Harhas, keeper of the garments — she was living in Jerusalem, in the Second Quarter — and they spoke with her.
And she said to them: "Thus says YHWH, the God of Israel: Say to the man who sent you to me:
Thus says YHWH: Behold — I am bringing evil on this place and on its inhabitants: all the words of the scroll that the king of Judah has read —
because they have abandoned me, and burned incense to other gods, in order to provoke me with all the work of their hands; and my wrath is kindled against this place — and it shall not be quenched.
But to the king of Judah, who sent you to seek YHWH — thus shall you say to him: Thus says YHWH, the God of Israel, concerning the words you have heard:

Because your heart was soft, and you humbled yourself before YHWH when you heard what I spoke against this place and against its inhabitants — that they would become a desolation and a curse — and you tore your garments and wept before me: I also have heard — declares YHWH.

Therefore — behold, I will gather you to your fathers, and you shall be gathered to your grave in peace; and your eyes shall not see all the evil I am bringing on this place." And they brought the king back word.

2ki.23.text: And the king sent, and they gathered to him all the elders of Judah and Jerusalem.

And the king went up to the house of YHWH — and every man of Judah, and all the inhabitants of Jerusalem with him, and the priests and the prophets, and all the people, from small to great; and he read in their ears all the words of the scroll of the covenant, which had been found in the house of YHWH.

And the king stood by the pillar and cut the covenant before YHWH: to walk after YHWH and to keep his commandments and his testimonies and his statutes, with all the heart and with all the soul — to raise up the words of this covenant, written on this scroll. And all the people stood in the covenant.

And the king commanded Hilkiah the high priest and the priests of the second rank and the keepers of the threshold to bring out of the temple of YHWH all the vessels made for Baal and for Asherah and for all the host of the heavens; and he burned them outside Jerusalem, in the fields of the Kidron — and carried their ashes to Bethel.

And he put down the idol-priests whom the kings of Judah had installed, who burned incense on the high places in the cities of Judah and the surroundings of Jerusalem — and those burning incense to Baal, to the sun and to the moon and to the constellations, and to all the host of the heavens.

And he brought the Asherah pole out of the house of YHWH, outside Jerusalem, to the wadi Kidron, and burned it in the wadi Kidron, and crushed it to dust — and threw its dust on the graves of the common people.
And he tore down the houses of the male cult prostitutes that were in the house of YHWH — where the women wove hangings for Asherah.

And he brought all the priests out of the cities of Judah, and defiled the high places where the priests had burned incense, from Geba to Beersheba; and he tore down the high places of the gates — at the entrance of the Gate of Joshua, the governor of the city, which was on a man's left at the city gate.
Only, the priests of the high places did not come up to the altar of YHWH in Jerusalem — but they ate unleavened bread in the midst of their brothers.
And he defiled the Topheth, which is in the Valley of the Sons of Hinnom — so that no one might make his son or his daughter pass through the fire to Molech.

And he put away the horses that the kings of Judah had dedicated to the sun, at the entrance of the house of YHWH, by the chamber of Nathan-melech the official, which was in the colonnades; and the chariots of the sun he burned with fire.

And the altars on the roof of Ahaz's upper chamber, which the kings of Judah had made, and the altars Manasseh had made in the two courts of the house of YHWH — the king tore down; and he ran from there, and threw their dust into the wadi Kidron.

And the high places facing Jerusalem, to the south of the Mountain of Corruption — which Solomon king of Israel had built for Ashtoreth the abomination of the Sidonians, and for Chemosh the abomination of Moab, and for Milcom the detestation of the sons of Ammon — the king defiled;
and he shattered the standing-stones and cut down the Asherah poles — and filled their places with human bones.

And also the altar at Bethel — the high place made by Jeroboam son of Nebat, who made Israel sin — even that altar and the high place he tore down; and he burned the high place, crushed it to dust, and burned the Asherah pole.

And Josiah turned and saw the graves that were there on the hill; and he sent and took the bones from the graves, and burned them on the altar and defiled it — according to the word of YHWH that the man of God had called out, who called out these very things.

And he said: "What is that marker I see?" And the men of the city said to him: "It is the grave of the man of God who came from Judah — who called out these things you have done against the altar of Bethel."
And he said: "Let him rest. Let no man disturb his bones." So they left his bones untouched — with the bones of the prophet who came from Samaria.

And also all the houses of the high places that were in the cities of Samaria, which the kings of Israel had made to provoke — Josiah removed; and he did to them according to all the deeds he had done at Bethel.
And he slaughtered all the priests of the high places who were there, on the altars — and burned human bones on them. And he returned to Jerusalem.
And the king commanded all the people: "Keep a Passover to YHWH your God — as it is written on this scroll of the covenant."
For no Passover like this one had been kept since the days of the judges who judged Israel — nor all the days of the kings of Israel and the kings of Judah;
but in the eighteenth year of King Josiah, this Passover was kept to YHWH, in Jerusalem.

And also the ghost-mediums and the familiar spirits, and the household gods and the idols, and all the detestable things that were seen in the land of Judah and in Jerusalem — Josiah swept away, in order to raise up the words of the law written on the scroll that Hilkiah the priest had found in the house of YHWH.
And like him there was no king before him, who turned to YHWH with all his heart and with all his soul and with all his might, according to all the law of Moses; and after him none arose like him.
Yet YHWH did not turn from the burning of his great anger, with which his anger burned against Judah — over all the provocations with which Manasseh had provoked him.
And YHWH said: "Even Judah I will remove from before my face, as I removed Israel; and I will reject this city that I chose — Jerusalem — and the house of which I said: My name shall be there."
And the rest of the acts of Josiah, and all he did — are they not written in the Scroll of the Days of the Kings of Judah?
In his days Pharaoh Neco king of Egypt went up toward the king of Assyria, to the river Euphrates; and King Josiah went out to meet him — and he put him to death at Megiddo, as soon as he saw him.

And his servants carried him dead in a chariot from Megiddo, and brought him to Jerusalem, and buried him in his own tomb. And the people of the land took Jehoahaz son of Josiah, and anointed him, and made him king in place of his father.
Jehoahaz was twenty-three years old when he became king, and three months he reigned in Jerusalem; and his mother's name was Hamutal daughter of Jeremiah, from Libnah.
And he did the evil in the eyes of YHWH — according to all that his fathers had done.
And Pharaoh Neco bound him at Riblah, in the land of Hamath, from reigning in Jerusalem; and he laid a fine on the land: a hundred talents of silver and a talent of gold.
And Pharaoh Neco made Eliakim son of Josiah king in place of Josiah his father, and changed his name to Jehoiakim; and Jehoahaz he took — and he came to Egypt, and died there.

And the silver and the gold Jehoiakim gave to Pharaoh; only, he taxed the land to give the silver at Pharaoh's command: each man according to his assessment, he exacted the silver and the gold from the people of the land, to give to Pharaoh Neco.
Jehoiakim was twenty-five years old when he became king, and eleven years he reigned in Jerusalem; and his mother's name was Zebidah daughter of Pedaiah, from Rumah.
And he did the evil in the eyes of YHWH — according to all that his fathers had done.
